messages.Tooltip.ComponentBase`1.persistent: The <mark=#ffffff10>persistent</mark> field determines whether this component will be saved, for example to the Inventory. If this field is set to <mark=#ffffff10>false</mark>, the component will not be persisted when saved.
messages.Tooltip.ComponentBase`1.UpdateOrder:
Controls the order in which this component updates, relative to all other components in the World. Components with a higher value will update after components with a lower value.

Many components do not implement their functionality via the common updating system. This field has no effect on such components.
messages.Tooltip.ComponentBase`1.EnabledField:
Controls whether this component is "enabled". Generally, components that are disabled (this field set to false) should do nothing.

Not all components respect this property. This field has no effect on such components.
messages.Tooltip.AssetProxy`1: Asset Proxy is a component that allows for access to an asset through a slot when being grabbed by the user.
messages.Tooltip.AssetProxy`1.AssetReference: The asset to expose to asset ref fields when grabbing the object root of the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopTextureProvider: The DesktopTextureProvider component only works in user space. This component is an ITexture2D and can be used as a texture. This texture is able to display what is on any particular monitor on a user's machine. This component is commonly used in the desktop tab on a user's Dash.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopTextureProvider.DisplayIn: What monitor to get image data for.
messages.Tooltip.FontAtlasTexture: The FontAtlasTexture component makes a GlyphAtlas containing an atlas of Glyphs representing characters into one large texture that can be used. The internal mechanisms of this component are not assignable directly.
messages.Tooltip.LocalMeshProvider: The Local Mesh Provider is a component that provides a local mesh. This is used internally to render things like UIX and particles.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicAssetProvider`1.HighPrior: If true, integrating this asset (e.g. processing procedural assets) gets higher priority than assets with this flag off. An example is user laser procedural meshes.
messages.Tooltip.LocalPointRenderBufferProvider: Local Point Render Buffer Provider component as seen in the Scene Inspector
messages.Tooltip.DebugMaterial: The DebugMaterial component is used to display mesh data in the form of a color map.
messages.Tooltip.DebugMaterial.Scale: How much to scale the positions of the displayed color data pixels.
messages.Tooltip.DebugMaterial.Offset: How much to offset the positions of the displayed color data pixels.
messages.Tooltip.DebugMaterial.Visualize: What kind of data to visualize for the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.DebugMaterial.Normalize: Enable keeping visualized mesh data within a 0-1 range for all color values.
messages.Tooltip.DebugMaterial.RenderQueue: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.DepthProjectionMaterial: The DepthProjectionMaterial is commonly used when importing depth videos and acts as a material that displaces vertices in 3D.
messages.Tooltip.DepthProjectionMaterial.Color: The surface color texture of the material.
messages.Tooltip.DepthProjectionMaterial.Depth: the texture used to displace vertices based on intensity.
messages.Tooltip.DepthProjectionMaterial.DepthEnc: How the depth data is encoded into <mark=#ffffff10>Depth</mark>
messages.Tooltip.DepthProjectionMaterial.FieldOfV: the field of view of the <mark=#ffffff10>Depth</mark> data.
messages.Tooltip.DepthProjectionMaterial.NearClip: the near clip of the <mark=#ffffff10>Depth</mark> data.
messages.Tooltip.DepthProjectionMaterial.FarClip: the far clip of the <mark=#ffffff10>Depth</mark> data.
messages.Tooltip.DepthProjectionMaterial.DiscardT: how strong the depth data can be till its cut off.
messages.Tooltip.DepthProjectionMaterial.BlendMod: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.DepthProjectionMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.BlurMaterial: The BlurMaterial Component is used to blur objects rendered behind it, by first rendering everything behind it using z-test and then grabbing the pixels, then creating spread transparent ghost images of the original pixels to give the illusion of blur.
messages.Tooltip.UI_StencilMaterial.RectClip: Toggles if the material should use <mark=#ffffff10>Rect</mark>
messages.Tooltip.UI_StencilMaterial.ColorMask: What colors behind the material should make it through the filter.
messages.Tooltip.UI_StencilMaterial.StencilCompar: See Type:StencilComparison for an in depth explanation on what this does.
messages.Tooltip.UI_StencilMaterial.StencilOperat: See Type:StencilOperation for an in depth explanation on what this does.
messages.Tooltip.UI_StencilMaterial.StencilID: The Stencil ID of this material. This is sometimes written to the frame buffer's Stencil mask, or used to determine whether this material should render for a particular pixel.
messages.Tooltip.UI_StencilMaterial.StencilWriteM: does a Bitwise AND with this number for every pixel in the frame buffer this is rendering on top of when this object is drawn, after reading from the buffer.
messages.Tooltip.UI_StencilMaterial.StencilReadMa: is bitwise ANDed with the Stencil in the frame buffer before the test compares them.
messages.Tooltip.UI_StencilMaterial.RenderQueue: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.BlurMaterial.Iterations: How many ghost images to make in order to incur the blurring effect
messages.Tooltip.BlurMaterial.Spread: How much the ghost images spread from the center real image to incur the blurring effect
messages.Tooltip.BlurMaterial.SpreadMagnitudeText: A texture to use that affects the strength of the blur effect.
messages.Tooltip.BlurMaterial.UsePoissonDisc: Use the poisson disk blur algorithm
messages.Tooltip.BlurMaterial.DepthFadeDivisor: This setting uses the depth of the scene as a scaling factor for how blurry the filter is based on this depth, so further out objects get more blurry.
messages.Tooltip.BlurMaterial.Refract: Whether this material should also do refraction effects.
messages.Tooltip.BlurMaterial.NormalMap: The normal map is used as a way to change the appearance of a surface when shined on by lights, to give the illusion of a raised surface.
messages.Tooltip.BlurMaterial.PerObject: Whether to do the effect per object rather than to the entire image grab pass done by the shader by default.
messages.Tooltip.BlurMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.BlurMaterial.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.BlurMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.BlurMaterial.ZTest: Determines whether this object should render when it is in front of or behind other objects that respect depth from the camera
messages.Tooltip.ChannelMatrixMaterial: The ChannelMatrixMaterial component can be used to make meshes render the colors behind them differently by mixing around the color channels of colors behind them.
messages.Tooltip.ChannelMatrixMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.ChannelMatrixMaterial.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.ChannelMatrixMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.ChannelMatrixMaterial.ZTest: Determines whether this object should render when it is in front of or behind other objects that respect depth from the camera
messages.Tooltip.DepthMaterial: The DepthMaterial component renders everything behind it in black and white depending on how far away it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.DepthMaterial.Multiply: how much to multiply the resulting color.
messages.Tooltip.DepthMaterial.Offset: How much to offset the positions of the displayed color data pixels.
messages.Tooltip.DepthMaterial.Clip: whether to clip the depth range.
messages.Tooltip.DepthMaterial.ClipStart: the depth start value, which is the minimum distance from the camera which will be black.
messages.Tooltip.DepthMaterial.ClipEnd: the depth end value, which is the maximum distance from the camera, which will be white.
messages.Tooltip.DepthMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.DepthMaterial.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.DepthMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.DepthMaterial.ZTest: Determines whether this object should render when it is in front of or behind other objects that respect depth from the camera
messages.Tooltip.GammaMaterial: The GammaMaterial material modifies the gamma of colors it renders on top of.
messages.Tooltip.GammaMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.GammaMaterial.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.GammaMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.GammaMaterial.ZTest: Determines whether this object should render when it is in front of or behind other objects that respect depth from the camera
messages.Tooltip.GrayscaleMaterial: The GrayscaleMaterial component is a material that allows the mapping of grayscale values from 0 to 1 to any other value. This is analogous to the [https://docs.blender.org/manual/en/latest/render/shader_nodes/converter/color_ramp.html Color Ramp] node in Blender. A common misconception is that this can only produce black and white values, however that is only the default gradient, and a user-provided Gradient texture allows for various colored effects.
messages.Tooltip.GrayscaleMaterial.RatioRed: The proportion of red to use for grayscale computation
messages.Tooltip.GrayscaleMaterial.RatioGreen: The proportion of green to use for grayscale computation
messages.Tooltip.GrayscaleMaterial.RatioBlue: The proportion of blue to use for grayscale computation
messages.Tooltip.GrayscaleMaterial.Lerp: The value to use to transition between the first and second texture and value sets in absence of <mark=#ffffff10>LerpTexture</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.GrayscaleMaterial.Gradient: A texture which maps black values on its far left and white values on its far right
messages.Tooltip.GrayscaleMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.GrayscaleMaterial.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.GrayscaleMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.GrayscaleMaterial.ZTest: Determines whether this object should render when it is in front of or behind other objects that respect depth from the camera
messages.Tooltip.HSV_Material: The HSV_Material is used to change the hue, staturation, and colors of objects being rendered behind it.
messages.Tooltip.HSV_Material.HSV_Offset: How much to add to the hue, saturation, and value of colors behind the material
messages.Tooltip.HSV_Material.HSV_Multiply: How much to multiply the hue, saturation, and value of colors behind the material
messages.Tooltip.HSV_Material.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.HSV_Material.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.HSV_Material.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.HSV_Material.ZTest: Determines whether this object should render when it is in front of or behind other objects that respect depth from the camera
messages.Tooltip.InvertMaterial: The invert material inverts the colors of things behind it
messages.Tooltip.InvertMaterial.Lerp: The value to use to transition between the first and second texture and value sets in absence of <mark=#ffffff10>LerpTexture</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.InvertMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.InvertMaterial.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.InvertMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.InvertMaterial.ZTest: Determines whether this object should render when it is in front of or behind other objects that respect depth from the camera
messages.Tooltip.LUT_Material: The mapping function of an LUT takes an input RGB value and then samples the 3D Texture at that corresponding coordinate to find the output RGB value, using the 3D Texture's Filter Mode and Wrap Mode to resolve how intermediate values work.
messages.Tooltip.LUT_Material.LUT: The 3D texture to use as a lookup table
messages.Tooltip.LUT_Material.SecondaryLUT: A secondary 3D texture to use to lerp between
messages.Tooltip.LUT_Material.UseSRGB: Whether to use the SRGB color space when spitting out colors.
messages.Tooltip.LUT_Material.Lerp: The value to use to transition between the first and second texture and value sets in absence of <mark=#ffffff10>LerpTexture</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.LUT_Material.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.LUT_Material.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.LUT_Material.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.LUT_Material.ZTest: Determines whether this object should render when it is in front of or behind other objects that respect depth from the camera
messages.Tooltip.PixelateMaterial: The Pixelate Material component point filters the colors behind it using a spaced out grid of points. It then uses the colors to make a new pixel grid which makes up the pixelated effect it generates.
messages.Tooltip.PixelateMaterial.PerObject: Whether to do the effect per object rather than to the entire image grab pass done by the shader by default.
messages.Tooltip.PixelateMaterial.Resolution: The resolution of the pixel effect.
messages.Tooltip.PixelateMaterial.ResolutionMagni: The texture to determine the resolution of certain areas of the surface.
messages.Tooltip.PixelateMaterial.ResolutionTextu: How big to scale the <mark=#ffffff10>ResolutionMagnitudeTexture</mark> along the surface.
How much to offset the <mark=#ffffff10>ResolutionMagnitudeTexture</mark> along the surface.
messages.Tooltip.PixelateMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.PixelateMaterial.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.PixelateMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.PixelateMaterial.ZTest: Determines whether this object should render when it is in front of or behind other objects that respect depth from the camera
messages.Tooltip.PosterizeMaterial: The PosterizeMaterial material reduces the amount of colors in the color pallet of things behind it. This can help make the illusion of a comic book effect.
messages.Tooltip.PosterizeMaterial.Levels: How many different levels of color there should be per color channel.
messages.Tooltip.PosterizeMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.PosterizeMaterial.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.PosterizeMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.PosterizeMaterial.ZTest: Determines whether this object should render when it is in front of or behind other objects that respect depth from the camera
messages.Tooltip.RefractMaterial: The RefractMaterial component can be used to create distortion or bending effects on the objects and visuals rendered behind it.
messages.Tooltip.RefractMaterial.DepthBias: The bias for the depth buffer affecting the refraction strength.
messages.Tooltip.RefractMaterial.DepthFadeDivisor: This setting uses the depth of the scene as a scaling factor for how blurry the filter is based on this depth, so further out objects get more blurry.
messages.Tooltip.RefractMaterial.NormalMap: The normal map is used as a way to change the appearance of a surface when shined on by lights, to give the illusion of a raised surface.
messages.Tooltip.RefractMaterial.PerObject: Whether to do the effect per object rather than to the entire image grab pass done by the shader by default.
messages.Tooltip.RefractMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.RefractMaterial.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.RefractMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.RefractMaterial.ZTest: Determines whether this object should render when it is in front of or behind other objects that respect depth from the camera
messages.Tooltip.ThresholdMaterial: The ThresholdMaterial component is a material that shows black and white. Pixels behind need to be above a certain luminance to be white and anything below will show black.
messages.Tooltip.ThresholdMaterial.Threshold: How much brightness is needed to pass the filter.
messages.Tooltip.ThresholdMaterial.Transition: How much of the color to transition across at the threshold limit.
messages.Tooltip.ThresholdMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.ThresholdMaterial.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.ThresholdMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.ThresholdMaterial.ZTest: Determines whether this object should render when it is in front of or behind other objects that respect depth from the camera
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial: The FlatLitToonMaterial is a material that used to be used before the xiexe toon material.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.MainTexture: The main color map of the material.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.ColorMask: What colors behind the material should make it through the filter.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.EmissionMap: The glow or emission color map.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.NormalMap: The normal map is used as a way to change the appearance of a surface when shined on by lights, to give the illusion of a raised surface.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.MainTextureS: The UV scaling of the main texture.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.MainTextureO: The UV offset of the main texture.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.ColorMaskSca: The UV scaling of the color mask texture.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.ColorMaskOff: The UV offset of the color mask texture.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.EmissionMapS: The UV scaling of the emission texture.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.EmissionMapO: The UV offset of the emission texture.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.NormalMapSca: The UV scaling of the normal map texture.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.NormalMapOff: The UV offset of the normal texture.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.AlphaCutoff: If <mark=#ffffff10>BlendMode</mark> is set to cutout, discard rendering of pixels for the surface that fall below this alpha threshold.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.Color: The surface color texture of the material.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.EmissionColo: The tint of the emission color.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.Shadow: The strength of shadow on the toon material.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.OutlineWidth: The thickness of the outline around the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.OutlineColor: The color of the outline around the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.OutlineTint: How much to tint the outline around the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.OffsetFactor: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), adjusted for the polygon's slope relative to the camera's near and far planes. This can be used to sort overlapping geometry that would otherwise Z-fight.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.OffsetUnits: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), in units equal to the smallest possible difference for the rendering device being used.
messages.Tooltip.FlatLitToonMaterial.RenderQueue: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial: The FresnelLerpMaterial component is a material that can be used to make a fresnel effect that can be lerped between two different preset frensel settings.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.Lerp: The value to use to transition between the first and second texture and value sets in absence of <mark=#ffffff10>LerpTexture</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.LerpTexture: The texture to use to blend linearly between the first and second texture and value sets.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.LerpTextureS: The UV scale of the lerp texture.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.LerpTextureO: The UV offset of the lerp texture.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.LerpTextureP: Whether to UV map the lerp texture using polar UVs.
The polar UV power of the lerp texture UV when using polar UVs.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.Exponent0: The sharpness of the fresnel effect for the first lerp value set.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.Exponent1: The sharpness of the fresnel effect for the second lerp value set.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.GammaCurve: The gamma curve of the material colors.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.FarColor0: The far color tint for the first lerp set.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.NearColor0: The near color tint for the first lerp set.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.FarColor1: The Far color tint for the second lerp set.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.NearColor1: The near color tint for the second lerp set.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.FarTexture0: The Far texture for the first lerp texture set.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.NearTexture0: The near texture for the first lerp texture set.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.FarTexture1: The Far texture for the second lerp texture set.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.NearTexture1: The near texture for the second lerp texture set.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.NormalMap0: The normal map for the first lerp texture set.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.NormalMap1: The normal map for the second lerp texture set.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.OffsetFactor: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), adjusted for the polygon's slope relative to the camera's near and far planes. This can be used to sort overlapping geometry that would otherwise Z-fight.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.OffsetUnits: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), in units equal to the smallest possible difference for the rendering device being used.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelLerpMaterial.RenderQueue: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial: The FresnelLerpMaterial component is a material that can be used to make a fresnel effect.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.Exponent: The sharpness of the fresnel effect.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.GammaCurve: The gamma curve of the material colors.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.FarColor: The color tint of geometry facing away from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.NearColor: The color tint of geometry facing towards the camera.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.FarTexture: The texture for the geometry facing away from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.NearTexture: The texture for geometry facing towards the camera.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.FarTextureScale: The UV scale of the far texture.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.FarTextureOffset: The UV offset of the far texture.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.NearTextureScale: The UV scale of the near texture.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.NearTextureOffse: The UV offset of the near texture.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.NormalMap: The normal map is used as a way to change the appearance of a surface when shined on by lights, to give the illusion of a raised surface.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.NormalScale: How much to scale up the effect of the normal map.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.UseVertexColors: Whether the material should use vertex colors from the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.VertexColorInter: How to interpolate vertex colors on the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.AlphaCutoff: If <mark=#ffffff10>BlendMode</mark> is set to cutout, discard rendering of pixels for the surface that fall below this alpha threshold.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.MaskScale: The UV scale of the mask texture.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.MaskOffset: The UV offset of the mask texture.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.MaskMode: How to apply the mask texture to the material.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.ZTest: Determines whether this object should render when it is in front of or behind other objects that respect depth from the camera
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.OffsetFactor: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), adjusted for the polygon's slope relative to the camera's near and far planes. This can be used to sort overlapping geometry that would otherwise Z-fight.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.OffsetUnits: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), in units equal to the smallest possible difference for the rendering device being used.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.RenderQueue: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.PolarUVmapping: Whether to use polar UV unwrapping.
messages.Tooltip.FresnelMaterial.PolarPower: The power of the polar UV unwrapping.
messages.Tooltip.FurMaterial: The FurMaterial component is used to make multi layered geometry out of a mesh's original geometry that has a specified distance between layers. The number of layers is a constant 20. Combined with a map to create holes in the layers via alpha, this creates the illusion of depth and fur like properties. The geometry layers are instanced on the GPU.
messages.Tooltip.FurMaterial.Color: The surface color texture of the material.
messages.Tooltip.FurMaterial.SpecularColor: Specular Tint. Behaves like PBS Specular Tinting
messages.Tooltip.FurMaterial.FurLength: Length of the fur.  Always applied in the force direction.
messages.Tooltip.FurMaterial.FurHardness: How Stiff the fur is. Biases fur to point in the mesh normal direction instead of the force direction.
messages.Tooltip.FurMaterial.FurThinness: How many times to re-iterate the fur noise map. Behaves similarly to texture scaling.
messages.Tooltip.FurMaterial.FurShading: How much fake shading should be applied to the fur.
messages.Tooltip.FurMaterial.FurColoring: How much the color of the fur should take precedent over shading.
messages.Tooltip.FurMaterial.NormalMap: The normal map is used as a way to change the appearance of a surface when shined on by lights, to give the illusion of a raised surface.
messages.Tooltip.FurMaterial.Noise: Noise texture. This should always be on the alpha channel. This determines the pattern of the fur.
messages.Tooltip.FurMaterial.TextureScale: How much to scale up or down the different texture maps.
messages.Tooltip.FurMaterial.TextureOffset: How much to shift around the position of the different texture maps.
messages.Tooltip.FurMaterial.AlphaCutoff: If <mark=#ffffff10>BlendMode</mark> is set to cutout, discard rendering of pixels for the surface that fall below this alpha threshold.
messages.Tooltip.FurMaterial.ForceGlobal: The amount of displacement to apply in the world coordinate system. The W value corresponds to a proportional affinity to point towards world origin.
messages.Tooltip.FurMaterial.ForceLocal: The amount of displacement to apply in the object's local coordinate system.
messages.Tooltip.FurMaterial.RenderQueue: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.LegacyCircleSegmentMaterial: The LegacyCircleSegmentMaterial component is a leftover Component from content migrated from other platforms. It should not be used, and should be replaced whenever possible.
messages.Tooltip.MatcapMaterial: The MatcapMaterial component is used to make fake reflections on a surface using a matcap texture.
messages.Tooltip.MatcapMaterial.Matcap: A texture that looks like a sphere with reflection shading on it. Used to make the fake reflection map.
messages.Tooltip.MatcapMaterial.NormalMap: The normal map is used as a way to change the appearance of a surface when shined on by lights, to give the illusion of a raised surface.
messages.Tooltip.MatcapMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.MatcapMaterial.RenderQueue: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.OverlayFresnelMaterial: The Overlay Fresnel Material functions similar to a Fresnel Material with the added attribute of displaying through objects.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayFresnelMaterial.Exponent: The sharpness of the fresnel effect.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayFresnelMaterial.GammaCurv: The gamma curve of the material colors.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayFresnelMaterial.BehindFar: The far color tint when the material is behind things and being rendered on top.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayFresnelMaterial.BehindNea: The near color tint when the material is behind things and being rendered on top.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayFresnelMaterial.FrontFarC: The far color tint when the material is within view.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayFresnelMaterial.FrontNear: The near color tint when the material is within view.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayFresnelMaterial.BehindFar: The far texture when the material is behind things and being rendered on top.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayFresnelMaterial.BehindNea: The near texture when the material is behind things and being rendered on top.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayFresnelMaterial.FrontFarT: The far texture when the material is within view.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayFresnelMaterial.FrontNear: The near texture when the material is within view.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayFresnelMaterial.NormalMap: The normal map is used as a way to change the appearance of a surface when shined on by lights, to give the illusion of a raised surface.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayFresnelMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayFresnelMaterial.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayFresnelMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayFresnelMaterial.RenderQue: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.OverlayFresnelMaterial.OffsetFac: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), adjusted for the polygon's slope relative to the camera's near and far planes. This can be used to sort overlapping geometry that would otherwise Z-fight.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayFresnelMaterial.OffsetUni: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), in units equal to the smallest possible difference for the rendering device being used.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayFresnelMaterial.PolarUVma: Whether to use polar UV unwrapping.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayFresnelMaterial.PolarPowe: The power of the polar UV unwrapping.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayUnlitMaterial: The OverlayUnlitMaterial component is a material that is similar to a Component:UnlitMaterial except it renders differently when in front or behind an object.

messages.Tooltip.OverlayUnlitMaterial.FrontTintCo: The color tint when the material is in front of an object.

messages.Tooltip.OverlayUnlitMaterial.FrontTextur: The texture to use when the material is in front of an object.

messages.Tooltip.OverlayUnlitMaterial.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.

messages.Tooltip.OverlayUnlitMaterial.PolarUVmapp: Whether to use polar UV unwrapping.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayUnlitMaterial.PolarPower: The power of the polar UV unwrapping.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayUnlitMaterial.OffsetFacto: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), adjusted for the polygon's slope relative to the camera's near and far planes. This can be used to sort overlapping geometry that would otherwise Z-fight.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayUnlitMaterial.OffsetUnits: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), in units equal to the smallest possible difference for the rendering device being used.

messages.Tooltip.PBS_ColorMaskSpecular: The ColorMask texture is a color mask that gets mapped to the four albedo/emissive channels to change individual tints for certain parts of the material.

messages.Tooltip.PBS_Material.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Material.AlphaCutoff: If <mark=#ffffff10>BlendMode</mark> is set to cutout, discard rendering of pixels for the surface that fall below this alpha threshold.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Material.OffsetFactor: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), adjusted for the polygon's slope relative to the camera's near and far planes. This can be used to sort overlapping geometry that would otherwise Z-fight.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Material.OffsetUnits: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), in units equal to the smallest possible difference for the rendering device being used.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Material.RenderQueue: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Specular.SpecularColor: Specular Tint. Behaves like PBS Specular Tinting
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Metallic: PBS Metallic is a [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Physically_based_rendering physically-based] material that uses a Metallic-Smoothness (MS) map to store data about the metallicity and smoothness of an object. It is based on the Unity 5 Standard PBR Setup.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Metallic.Metallic: How much metallicness the surface should have if <mark=#ffffff10>MetallicMap</mark> is not specified.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Metallic.Smoothness: How much smoothness the surface should have if <mark=#ffffff10>MetallicMap</mark> is not specified.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Specular: The PBS_MultiUV_Specular component is used to create a multiple image layered effect (pairs) except for a lack of a second Specular map image. When this material's AlphaHandling mode is set to AlphaBlend, it behaves as if if were Opaque, which is a known bug.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.AlbedoScale: How much to scale up or down the albedo texture maps.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.AlbedoOffse: How much to push around the position of the albedo texture maps.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.AlbedoUV: The UV map index Albedo map should use.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.EmissionMap: The UV scaling of the emission texture.
The UV offset of the emission texture.
What UV map data index the emission map can use
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.NormalMapSc: The UV scaling of the normal map texture.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.NormalMapOf: The UV offset of the normal texture.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.NormalMapUV: What UV Map data index on the mesh the normal maps should use.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.OcclusionMa: What UV map data index in the mesh data the occlusion maps should use.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.SecondaryAl: The amount to scale up or down the UV of the secondary albedo texture map.
How much to push around or position the UV of the secondary albedo texture.
The UV map to use for the secondary albedo texture on this material.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.SecondaryEm: The scaling of the UV maps on the second emissive map for this material.
The offset or positioning of the UVs for the secondary emission map of this material.
The UV map index to use for mesh UV data for this material's secondary emission map.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.AlbedoColor: The color to multiply the texture of the albedo (base color) with. Basically a tint. Default white.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.AlbedoTextu: The texture to use as the color of the surface.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.EmissiveCol: The color to multiply the texture of the emission (glowly color) with. Basically a tint. Default white.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.EmissiveMap: The texture to use as the glowing (emission) color of the surface.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.NormalMap: The normal map is used as a way to change the appearance of a surface when shined on by lights, to give the illusion of a raised surface.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.NormalScale: How much to scale up the effect of the normal map.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.OcclusionMa: Used to specify surface parts in eternal shadow due to being close to other mesh parts. (Green channel for PBS materials, Red channel for Xiexe)
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.SecondaryAl: The texture image to use for the secondary albedo map for this material. This determines a multiplicative secondary base color texture.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.SecondaryEm: The emissive color tint (multiplicative) for the secondary emission map for this material.
The secondary multiplicative emission map texture for this material.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.Culling: Whether to show the material on the front, back, or both sides
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.AlphaHandli: How to handle alpha values in pixels on the <mark=#ffffff10>AlbedoTexture</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.AlphaClip: Any alpha value below this amount is not rendered for any given pixel when cutout is enabled.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.OffsetFacto: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), adjusted for the polygon's slope relative to the camera's near and far planes. This can be used to sort overlapping geometry that would otherwise Z-fight.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.OffsetUnits: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), in units equal to the smallest possible difference for the rendering device being used.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Material.RenderQueue: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Specular.SpecularCol: Specular Tint. Behaves like PBS Specular Tinting
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Specular.SpecularMap: The scaling of the UV map of the Specular map for the material.
The offset or positioning of the Specular map on the surface.
The Specular map image for defining smoothness/Specular on different parts of the material surface.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Metallic: The PBS_MultiUVMetallic component is used as a material that can stack two different sets of material data except for shine maps. When this material's AlphaHandling mode is set to AlphaBlend, it behaves as if if were Opaque, which is a known bug.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Metallic.Metallic: The metallicness of the material, multiplied by the red channel of <mark=#ffffff10>MetallicGlossMap</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Metallic.Smoothness: The Smoothness value in the absence of the Metallic+Smoothness image map.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_MultiUV_Metallic.MetallicMap: The scale up or down factor of the UVs for the Metallic map on this material.
The position or offset for UVs for the Metallic map for this material.
The UV map index to get UV data from for the mesh this material is using for the Metallic map.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_RimSpecular: The PBS_RimSpecular material is used in away materials, and can be used to make a normal PBS material with an emissive rim from camera view.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_RimMaterial.TextureScale: How much to scale up or down the different texture maps.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_RimMaterial.TextureOffset: How much to shift around the position of the different texture maps.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_RimMaterial.AlbedoColor: The color to multiply the texture of the albedo (base color) with. Basically a tint. Default white.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_RimMaterial.AlbedoTexture: The texture to use as the color of the surface.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_RimMaterial.EmissiveColor: The color to multiply the texture of the emission (glowly color) with. Basically a tint. Default white.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_RimMaterial.EmissiveMap: The texture to use as the glowing (emission) color of the surface.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_RimMaterial.NormalMap: The normal map is used as a way to change the appearance of a surface when shined on by lights, to give the illusion of a raised surface.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_RimMaterial.NormalScale: How much to scale up the effect of the normal map.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_RimMaterial.OcclusionMap: Used to specify surface parts in eternal shadow due to being close to other mesh parts. (Green channel for PBS materials, Red channel for Xiexe)
messages.Tooltip.PBS_RimMaterial.GammaCurve: The gamma curve of the material colors.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_RimMaterial.ForceZWrite: Whether to enforce writing to the Z-buffer.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_RimMaterial.OffsetFactor: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), adjusted for the polygon's slope relative to the camera's near and far planes. This can be used to sort overlapping geometry that would otherwise Z-fight.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_RimMaterial.OffsetUnits: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), in units equal to the smallest possible difference for the rendering device being used.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_RimMaterial.RenderQueue: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.PBS_RimSpecular.SpecularColor: Specular Tint. Behaves like PBS Specular Tinting
messages.Tooltip.PBS_RimMetallic: The PBS_RimMetallic material is used in away materials, and can be used to make a normal PBS material with an emissive rim from camera view.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_RimMetallic.Metallic: The metallicness of the material, multiplied by the red channel of <mark=#ffffff10>MetallicGlossMap</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_RimMetallic.Smoothness: The Smoothness value in the absence of the Metallic+Smoothness image map.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_SliceSpecular: A PBS Slice (Metallic & Specular) shader: * Supports up to 8 plane slicers, cutting a mesh * Supports slice fade effect along the edge (albedo & emissive) * Is dual-sided, rendering interior (mesh will look as a shell though) * Adds a GenericSlicer component, which allows to set the slicing plane visually. To create/edit the material, click "Create Slicer" in the inspector.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.TextureScale: How much to scale up or down the different texture maps.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.TextureOffset: How much to shift around the position of the different texture maps.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.AlbedoColor: The color to multiply the texture of the albedo (base color) with. Basically a tint. Default white.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.EdgeColor: The color of the material edge where it is being sliced.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.AlbedoTexture: The texture to use as the color of the surface.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.EmissiveColor: The color to multiply the texture of the emission (glowly color) with. Basically a tint. Default white.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.EdgeEmissiveColor: The emission color of the edge of the material where it is being sliced.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.EmissiveMap: The texture to use as the glowing (emission) color of the surface.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.NormalMap: The normal map is used as a way to change the appearance of a surface when shined on by lights, to give the illusion of a raised surface.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.NormalScale: How much to scale up the effect of the normal map.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.OcclusionMap: Used to specify surface parts in eternal shadow due to being close to other mesh parts. (Green channel for PBS materials, Red channel for Xiexe)
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.DetailTextureScale: How much to scale up or down the different detail texture maps.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.DetailTextureOffset: How much to shift around the position of the different detail texture maps.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.DetailAlbedoTexture: A multiplied layer of albedo on top of the default <mark=#ffffff10>AlbedoTexture</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.DetailNormalMap: A layered normal map on top of the default <mark=#ffffff10>NormalMap</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.DetailNormalScale: How much to scale up the effect of the detail normal map.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.Culling: Whether to show the material on the front, back, or both sides
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.HideSlicers: Whether to hide the visuals of the planes for the different slicers.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.Slicers: A list of slicers (up to 8) that slice this material and create edges. These have a normal and a position in global or local space.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.LocalSpace: Whether to calculate <mark=#ffffff10>Distance</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>Point</mark> in local space.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.EdgeTransitionStart: The place at which the material color at the slicing edge should start transition from 100% slice properties to 0% slice properties to normal material.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.EdgeTransitionEnd: The place at which the material color at the slicing edge should end transitioning from 100% slice properties to 0% slice properties to normal material.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.AlphaHandling: How to handle alpha values in pixels on the <mark=#ffffff10>AlbedoTexture</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice._useAlphaClip: Whether to respect the value for <mark=#ffffff10>AlphaClip</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.AlphaClip: Any alpha value below this amount is not rendered for any given pixel when cutout is enabled.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.OffsetFactor: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), adjusted for the polygon's slope relative to the camera's near and far planes. This can be used to sort overlapping geometry that would otherwise Z-fight.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.OffsetUnits: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), in units equal to the smallest possible difference for the rendering device being used.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.RenderQueue: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.PBS_SliceSpecular.SpecularColor: Specular Tint. Behaves like PBS Specular Tinting
messages.Tooltip.PBS_SliceMetallic: A PBS Slice (Metallic & Specular) shader: * Supports up to 8 plane slicers, cutting a mesh * Supports slice fade effect along the edge (albedo & emissive) * Is dual-sided, rendering interior (mesh will look as a shell though) * Adds a GenericSlicer component, which allows to set the slicing plane visually. To create/edit the material, click "Create Slicer" in the inspector.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_SliceMetallic.Metallic: The metallicness of the material, multiplied by the red channel of <mark=#ffffff10>MetallicGlossMap</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_SliceMetallic.Smoothness: The Smoothness value in the absence of the Metallic+Smoothness image map.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterialSpecular: The PBS_StencilMaterialSpecular component is used as a material for meshes to make them render differently when in front of other stencil materials.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterial.TextureScale: How much to scale up or down the different texture maps.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterial.TextureOffse: How much to shift around the position of the different texture maps.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterial.AlbedoColor: The color to multiply the texture of the albedo (base color) with. Basically a tint. Default white.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterial.AlbedoTextur: The texture to use as the color of the surface.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterial.EmissiveColo: The color to multiply the texture of the emission (glowly color) with. Basically a tint. Default white.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterial.EmissiveMap: The texture to use as the glowing (emission) color of the surface.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterial.NormalMap: The normal map is used as a way to change the appearance of a surface when shined on by lights, to give the illusion of a raised surface.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterial.NormalScale: How much to scale up the effect of the normal map.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterial.OcclusionMap: Used to specify surface parts in eternal shadow due to being close to other mesh parts. (Green channel for PBS materials, Red channel for Xiexe)
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterial.ColorMask: What colors behind the material should make it through the filter.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterial.StencilCompa: See Type:StencilComparison for an in depth explanation on what this does.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterial.StencilOpera: See Type:StencilOperation for an in depth explanation on what this does.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterial.StencilID: The Stencil ID of this material. This is sometimes written to the frame buffer's Stencil mask, or used to determine whether this material should render for a particular pixel.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterial.StencilWrite: does a Bitwise AND with this number for every pixel in the frame buffer this is rendering on top of when this object is drawn, after reading from the buffer.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterial.StencilReadM: is bitwise ANDed with the Stencil in the frame buffer before the test compares them.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterial.OffsetFactor: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), adjusted for the polygon's slope relative to the camera's near and far planes. This can be used to sort overlapping geometry that would otherwise Z-fight.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterial.OffsetUnits: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), in units equal to the smallest possible difference for the rendering device being used.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterial.RenderQueue: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterialSpecular.Spec: Specular Tint. Behaves like PBS Specular Tinting
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterialMetallic: The PBS_StencilMaterialMetallic component is used as a material for meshes to make them render differently when in front of other stencil materials.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterialMetallic.Meta: The metallicness of the material, multiplied by the red channel of <mark=#ffffff10>MetallicGlossMap</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_StencilMaterialMetallic.Smoo: The Smoothness value in the absence of the Metallic+Smoothness image map.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_TriplanarSpecular: A triplanar shader generates UVs and samples a texture by projecting in world space. The input texture is sampled 3 times, once in each of the world x, y and z axes, and the resulting information is planar projected onto the model, blended by the normal.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_TriplanarMaterial.TextureSca: How much to scale up or down the different texture maps.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_TriplanarMaterial.TextureOff: How much to shift around the position of the different texture maps.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_TriplanarMaterial.AlbedoColo: The color to multiply the texture of the albedo (base color) with. Basically a tint. Default white.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_TriplanarMaterial.AlbedoText: The texture to use as the color of the surface.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_TriplanarMaterial.EmissiveCo: The color to multiply the texture of the emission (glowly color) with. Basically a tint. Default white.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_TriplanarMaterial.EmissiveMa: The texture to use as the glowing (emission) color of the surface.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_TriplanarMaterial.NormalMap: The normal map is used as a way to change the appearance of a surface when shined on by lights, to give the illusion of a raised surface.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_TriplanarMaterial.NormalScal: How much to scale up the effect of the normal map.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_TriplanarMaterial.OcclusionM: Used to specify surface parts in eternal shadow due to being close to other mesh parts. (Green channel for PBS materials, Red channel for Xiexe)
messages.Tooltip.PBS_TriplanarMaterial.TriplanarB: How sharp the transition is between different directions of the triplanar texture on the material.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_TriplanarMaterial.ObjectSpac: Whether the effects of this material happen in global or local space. Object space is local space (the transform space of the mesh this is on)
messages.Tooltip.PBS_TriplanarMaterial.OffsetFact: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), adjusted for the polygon's slope relative to the camera's near and far planes. This can be used to sort overlapping geometry that would otherwise Z-fight.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_TriplanarMaterial.OffsetUnit: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), in units equal to the smallest possible difference for the rendering device being used.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_TriplanarMaterial.Culling: Whether to show the material on the front, back, or both sides
messages.Tooltip.PBS_TriplanarMaterial.RenderQueu: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.PBS_TriplanarSpecular.SpecularCo: Specular Tint. Behaves like PBS Specular Tinting
messages.Tooltip.PBS_TriplanarMetallic: A triplanar shader generates UVs and samples a texture by projecting in world space. The input texture is sampled 3 times, once in each of the world x, y and z axes, and the resulting information is planar projected onto the model, blended by the normal.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_TriplanarMetallic.Metallic: The metallicness of the material, multiplied by the red channel of <mark=#ffffff10>MetallicGlossMap</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_TriplanarMetallic.Smoothness: The Smoothness value in the absence of the Metallic+Smoothness image map.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColorSpecular: The PBS_VertexColorSpecular component is a Component:PBS_Specular component except that it allows for the use of vertex colors as a multiplier for different colors/properties of the material.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColor.TextureScale: How much to scale up or down the different texture maps.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColor.TextureOffset: How much to shift around the position of the different texture maps.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColor.AlbedoColor: The color to multiply the texture of the albedo (base color) with. Basically a tint. Default white.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColor.AlbedoTexture: The texture to use as the color of the surface.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColor.EmissiveColor: The color to multiply the texture of the emission (glowly color) with. Basically a tint. Default white.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColor.EmissiveMap: The texture to use as the glowing (emission) color of the surface.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColor.NormalMap: The normal map is used as a way to change the appearance of a surface when shined on by lights, to give the illusion of a raised surface.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColor.NormalScale: How much to scale up the effect of the normal map.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColor.OcclusionMap: Used to specify surface parts in eternal shadow due to being close to other mesh parts. (Green channel for PBS materials, Red channel for Xiexe)
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColor.VertexColorInter: How to interpolate vertex colors on the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColor.Culling: Whether to show the material on the front, back, or both sides
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColor.AlphaHandling: How to handle alpha values in pixels on the <mark=#ffffff10>AlbedoTexture</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColor.AlphaClip: Any alpha value below this amount is not rendered for any given pixel when cutout is enabled.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColor.OffsetFactor: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), adjusted for the polygon's slope relative to the camera's near and far planes. This can be used to sort overlapping geometry that would otherwise Z-fight.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColor.OffsetUnits: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), in units equal to the smallest possible difference for the rendering device being used.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColor.RenderQueue: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColorSpecular.Specular: Specular Tint. Behaves like PBS Specular Tinting
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColorSpecular.VertexCo: The material map type that vertex colors should be applied to.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColorMetallic: The PBS_VertexColorMetallic component is a Component:PBS_Metallic component except that it allows for the use of vertex colors as a multiplier for different colors/properties of the material.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColorMetallic.Metallic: The metallicness of the material, multiplied by the red channel of <mark=#ffffff10>MetallicGlossMap</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColorMetallic.Smoothne: The Smoothness value in the absence of the Metallic+Smoothness image map.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VertexColorMetallic.VertexCo: The material map type that vertex colors should be applied to.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VoronoiCrystal: The PBS_VoronoiCrystal component is generated by making a random grid of points, then doing a gradient based on the distance from a point to the midway point to another point on the random point grid. this makes a set of "cells" which can be used to create stars or other effects for use on surfaces.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VoronoiCrystal.NoiseScale: how scaled up or down the noise pattern is.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VoronoiCrystal.NoiseAnimatio: How offset the noise animation is. Allows for animating the material over time using a time driver Component/ProtoFlux.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VoronoiCrystal.CellColorTint: The tint color to multiply different cells generated by as part of the voronoi texture.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VoronoiCrystal.ColorGradient: The tint color of the cells generated towards their center as a sideways pixel line image.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VoronoiCrystal.EmissionColor: The tint of the emission color.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VoronoiCrystal.EmissionGradi: The emission/glow of the cells generated towards their center as a sideways pixel line image.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VoronoiCrystal.SmoothnessGra: The smoothness of the cells generated towards their center as a sideways pixel line image.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VoronoiCrystal.CellSmoothnes: The smoothness to multiply different cells generated by as part of the voronoi texture.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VoronoiCrystal.CellMetallic: The metallicness to multiply the existing metallic gradient for different cells for the voronoi texture.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VoronoiCrystal.NormalMap: The normal map is used as a way to change the appearance of a surface when shined on by lights, to give the illusion of a raised surface.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VoronoiCrystal.NormalStrengt: How much to scale up or down the effect of the normals.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VoronoiCrystal.EdgeThickness: The thickness of the edges between cells before they advance along the gradient texture towards the center.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VoronoiCrystal.EdgeColor: The color of the material edge where it is being sliced.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VoronoiCrystal.EdgeEmission: the emission color of the edges of the voronoi effect.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VoronoiCrystal.EdgeSmoothnes: the smoothness of the edges of the voronoi cells.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VoronoiCrystal.EdgeMetallic: the metallicness of the edges of the voronoi cells.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_VoronoiCrystal.EdgeNormalStr: the normal strength of the edges on the voronoi cells.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.Texture: The primary texture to use for the skybox when <mark=#ffffff10>TextureLerp</mark> is at 0.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.SecondaryT: The secondary texture to use for the skybox when <mark=#ffffff10>TextureLerp</mark> is at 1.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.Cubemap: A cubemap override to use to make the skybox when <mark=#ffffff10>TextureLerp</mark> is at 0.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.SecondaryC: A cubemap override to use to make the skybox when <mark=#ffffff10>TextureLerp</mark> is at 1.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.CubemapLOD: which LOD to use of the Cubemap
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.TextureLer: How much to lerp between set 1 or 2 for this material.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.Projection: How to handle projection of the skybox texture for this skybox.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.FieldOfVie: What area of the texture to sample of the skybox in terms of FOV.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.AngleOffse: how much to shift the skybox sample area by in degrees.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.Perspectiv: What area of the texture to sample of the skybox in terms of perspective FOV.
how much to shift the skybox sample area by in perspective degrees.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.Tint: What color to multiply the skybox with.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.Exposure: How much exposure or brightness should be given to the sky.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.TintTextur: A texture to multiply the colors of the material by
how much to scale up or down the uvs of the tint texture on the material
how much to shift the uvs of the tint texture on the material
The Projection360+TintMode to use with this material.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.Tint0: The Tint to use when <mark=#ffffff10>TextureLerp</mark> is at 0.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.Tint1: The Tint to use when <mark=#ffffff10>TextureLerp</mark> is at 1.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.OutsideMod: The Projection360+OutsideMode to use with this material.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.OutsideCol: The Color to use when viewing outside the skybox
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.TextureOff: How much to shift around the position of the different texture maps.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.TextureSca: How much to scale up or down the different texture maps.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.StereoText: Whether a texture on the surface should appear different in the right eye.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.RightEyeTe: The override for texture in the right eye for offset.
The override for texture in the right eye for scale.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.ZTest: Determines whether this object should render when it is in front of or behind other objects that respect depth from the camera
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.OffsetFact: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), adjusted for the polygon's slope relative to the camera's near and far planes. This can be used to sort overlapping geometry that would otherwise Z-fight.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.OffsetUnit: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), in units equal to the smallest possible difference for the rendering device being used.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.MaxIntensi: The max intensity of the brightness of the material
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.RectClip: Toggles if the material should use <mark=#ffffff10>Rect</mark>
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.ColorMask: What colors behind the material should make it through the filter.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.OffsetText: The texture used to offset the positions of pixels.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.OffsetMask: The mask to use for material offset effects before applying the actual offset effect.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.OffsetText: How much to offset the detail of the <mark=#ffffff10>OffsetTexture</mark>
How much to scale the detail of the <mark=#ffffff10>OffsetTexture</mark>
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.OffsetMagn: How much to amplify the offset effect.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.StencilCom: See Type:StencilComparison for an in depth explanation on what this does.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.StencilOpe: See Type:StencilOperation for an in depth explanation on what this does.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.StencilID: The Stencil ID of this material. This is sometimes written to the frame buffer's Stencil mask, or used to determine whether this material should render for a particular pixel.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.StencilWri: does a Bitwise AND with this number for every pixel in the frame buffer this is rendering on top of when this object is drawn, after reading from the buffer.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.StencilRea: is bitwise ANDed with the Stencil in the frame buffer before the test compares them.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360Material.RenderQueu: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.Projection360PropertyBlock: See Material Property Block for a vastly detailed explaination.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360PropertyBlock.Textu: The texture to override the <mark=#ffffff10>Texture</mark> field of a Projection360Material for a renderer.
messages.Tooltip.Projection360PropertyBlock.Persp: The value to override the <mark=#ffffff10>PerspectiveFieldOfView</mark> field of a Projection360Material for a renderer.
The value to override the <mark=#ffffff10>PerspectiveAngleOffset</mark> field of a Projection360Material for a renderer.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionMaterial: Used with Camera Portals to render what the component sees into a visual result. Like mirrors, portals, a Tardis, gateways, and Non-Euclidean tunnels.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionMaterial.ReflectionTex: A texture usually generated by a Camera Portal or previously rendered by a Camera Portal.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionMaterial.NormalMap: The normal map is used as a way to change the appearance of a surface when shined on by lights, to give the illusion of a raised surface.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionMaterial.NormalMapScal: The UV scaling of the normal map texture.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionMaterial.NormalMapOffs: The UV offset of the normal texture.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionMaterial.Distort: How much effect the normal map should have on the reflection.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionMaterial.TintColor: What color to tint the projected image.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionMaterial.AlphaCutoff: If <mark=#ffffff10>BlendMode</mark> is set to cutout, discard rendering of pixels for the surface that fall below this alpha threshold.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionMaterial.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionMaterial.OffsetFactor: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), adjusted for the polygon's slope relative to the camera's near and far planes. This can be used to sort overlapping geometry that would otherwise Z-fight.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionMaterial.OffsetUnits: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), in units equal to the smallest possible difference for the rendering device being used.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionMaterial.RenderQueue: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.GradientSkyMaterial: The GradientSkyMaterial component is used in Component:Skybox to make a gradient type sky for use in worlds.
messages.Tooltip.GradientSkyMaterial.BaseColor: Starting color for the fog volume
messages.Tooltip.GradientSkyMaterial._gradients: Applies a list of Gradients to the sky.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralSkyMaterial: The Procedural Sky Material component is used to make a on-the-fly generated skybox using a light object and some values for atmosphere and sun size.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralSkyMaterial.SunQuality: The quality of the sun visual in the sky if any.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralSkyMaterial.SunSize: The radius of the sun in the sky.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralSkyMaterial.Sun: The light being used for the sun position and color.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralSkyMaterial.Atmosphere: The thickness of the atmosphere, which determines how high up the "blue" refraction effect seen in atmospheres. if this value is low, the blue of the sky becomes a thin band on the horizon that is faint. this would give the illusion of a thin atmosphere.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralSkyMaterial.SkyTint: The color of the atmosphere in the sky. for earth this is blue.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralSkyMaterial.GroundColo: The color of the sky below the horizon.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralSkyMaterial.Exposure: How much exposure or brightness should be given to the sky.
messages.Tooltip.TextUnlitMaterial: The TextUnlitMaterial component is a material used to render text glyphs. It does so via meshes that automatically generates UV maps that map to each character on a font map. Such as Component:Text and Component:TextRenderer.
messages.Tooltip.TextUnlitMaterial.TintColor: What color to tint the projected image.
messages.Tooltip.TextUnlitMaterial.OutlineColor: The color of the outline around the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.TextUnlitMaterial.BackgroundColo: The color of behind each text glyph
messages.Tooltip.TextUnlitMaterial.AutoBackground: Whether to automatically generate the background text color.
messages.Tooltip.TextUnlitMaterial.GlyphRenderMet: How to render each glyph or text character.
messages.Tooltip.TextUnlitMaterial.PixelRange: Sets the distance field range in output pixels.
messages.Tooltip.TextUnlitMaterial.FaceDilate: How fat to make the letters like bolding them.
messages.Tooltip.TextUnlitMaterial.OutlineThickne: The thickness of the outline on text for colored outlines.
messages.Tooltip.TextUnlitMaterial.FaceSoftness: How much blurring to do on the edges of the text.
messages.Tooltip.TextUnlitMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.TextUnlitMaterial.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.TextUnlitMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.TextUnlitMaterial.ZTest: Determines whether this object should render when it is in front of or behind other objects that respect depth from the camera
messages.Tooltip.TextUnlitMaterial.OffsetFactor: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), adjusted for the polygon's slope relative to the camera's near and far planes. This can be used to sort overlapping geometry that would otherwise Z-fight.
messages.Tooltip.TextUnlitMaterial.OffsetUnits: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), in units equal to the smallest possible difference for the rendering device being used.
messages.Tooltip.TextUnlitMaterial.RenderQueue: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.TextureDebugMaterial: The TextureDebugMaterial component shows a grayscale of a texture channel. Used for debugging.
messages.Tooltip.TextureDebugMaterial.Texture: The texture to debug data for.
messages.Tooltip.TextureDebugMaterial.TextureChan: The channel to display from <mark=#ffffff10>Texture</mark> as the surface.
messages.Tooltip.MainAndMaskTexturePropertyBlock: The MainAndMaskTexturePropertyBlock component is used to modify material properties on mesh renderers and skinned mesh renderers as part of their material Property blocks. This is useful in instances where having lots of extra materials is less performant than modifying a material using a Property Block.
The texture to replace the Main texture on a material with.
The texture to replace the Mask texture on a material with.
messages.Tooltip.MainTexturePropertyBlock: This component allows one to swap out what a material considers its main texture, such as the albedo texture, with the given Texture.
messages.Tooltip.MainTexturePropertyBlock.Texture: Texture to swap out the main texture with.
messages.Tooltip.UI_CircleSegment: The UI_CircleSegment material is used to render a curved circle onto a quad. This component only functions properly when rendered on a Component:CircleSegmentShaderMesh as it relies on the UV data generated by that mesh to create it's visuals.
messages.Tooltip.UI_CircleSegment.FillTint: The center color of the circle arc
messages.Tooltip.UI_CircleSegment.OutlineTint: How much to tint the outline around the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.UI_CircleSegment.Overlay: Whether this material should render on top of everything
messages.Tooltip.UI_CircleSegment.OverlayTint: what to tint the entire material as.
messages.Tooltip.UI_CircleSegment.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.UI_CircleSegment.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.UI_CircleSegment.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.UI_CircleSegment.ZTest: Determines whether this object should render when it is in front of or behind other objects that respect depth from the camera

This can be used to sort overlapping geometry that would otherwise Z-fight.
messages.Tooltip.UI_CircleSegment.OffsetUnits: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), in units equal to the smallest possible difference for the rendering device being used.
messages.Tooltip.UI_TextUnlitMaterial: The UI_TextUnlitMaterial component is a material used to render text glyphs on a UIX. It does so via meshes that automatically generates UV maps that map to each character on a font map. Such as Component:Text and Component:TextRenderer.
messages.Tooltip.UI_TextUnlitMaterial.Overlay: Whether this material should render on top of everything
messages.Tooltip.UI_TextUnlitMaterial.OverlayTint: what to tint the entire material as.

messages.Tooltip.UI_TextUnlitMaterial.StencilOper: See Type:StencilOperation for an in depth explanation on what this does.
messages.Tooltip.UI_TextUnlitMaterial.StencilID: The Stencil ID of this material. This is sometimes written to the frame buffer's Stencil mask, or used to determine whether this material should render for a particular pixel.

messages.Tooltip.UI_UnlitMaterial: The UI_UnlitMaterial component is used to show UIX. This material works best when rendered on a base, which the sample UIX made through the dev create new menu has by default.
messages.Tooltip.UI_UnlitMaterial.Texture: The texture to debug data for.
messages.Tooltip.UI_UnlitMaterial.TextureScale: How much to scale up or down the different texture maps.
messages.Tooltip.UI_UnlitMaterial.TextureOffset: How much to shift around the position of the different texture maps.
messages.Tooltip.UI_UnlitMaterial.Tint: The color to multiply or tint by for the entire material.
messages.Tooltip.UI_UnlitMaterial.Overlay: Whether this material should render on top of everything
messages.Tooltip.UI_UnlitMaterial.OverlayTint: what to tint the entire material as.
messages.Tooltip.UI_UnlitMaterial.AlphaCutoff: If <mark=#ffffff10>BlendMode</mark> is set to cutout, discard rendering of pixels for the surface that fall below this alpha threshold.
messages.Tooltip.UI_UnlitMaterial.AlphaClip: Any alpha value below this amount is not rendered for any given pixel when cutout is enabled.
messages.Tooltip.UI_UnlitMaterial.MaskScale: The UV scale of the mask texture.
messages.Tooltip.UI_UnlitMaterial.MaskOffset: The UV offset of the mask texture.
messages.Tooltip.UI_UnlitMaterial.MaskMode: How to apply the mask texture to the material.
messages.Tooltip.UI_UnlitMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.UI_UnlitMaterial.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.UI_UnlitMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.UI_UnlitMaterial.ZTest: Determines whether this object should render when it is in front of or behind other objects that respect depth from the camera
