This can be used to sort overlapping geometry that would otherwise Z-fight.
messages.Tooltip.UI_UnlitMaterial.OffsetUnits: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), in units equal to the smallest possible difference for the rendering device being used.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitDistanceLerpMaterial: The UnlitDistanceLerpMaterial component changes it's surface depending on how close the material's surface is to a given Point.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitDistanceLerpMaterial.LocalS: Whether to calculate <mark=#ffffff10>Distance</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>Point</mark> in local space.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitDistanceLerpMaterial.Distan: The distance where the transition from near to far starts.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitDistanceLerpMaterial.Transi: How much distance from near, far is.

messages.Tooltip.UnlitDistanceLerpMaterial.FarTex: The texture for the geometry facing away from the camera.

The UV offset of the far texture.

messages.Tooltip.UnlitDistanceLerpMaterial.FarCol: The color tint of geometry facing away from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitDistanceLerpMaterial.UseVer: Whether the material should use vertex colors from the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitDistanceLerpMaterial.Vertex: How to interpolate vertex colors on the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitDistanceLerpMaterial.BlendM: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitDistanceLerpMaterial.AlphaC: If <mark=#ffffff10>BlendMode</mark> is set to cutout, discard rendering of pixels for the surface that fall below this alpha threshold.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitDistanceLerpMaterial.Sidedn: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitDistanceLerpMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitDistanceLerpMaterial.Offset: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), adjusted for the polygon's slope relative to the camera's near and far planes. This can be used to sort overlapping geometry that would otherwise Z-fight.
How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), in units equal to the smallest possible difference for the rendering device being used.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitDistanceLerpMaterial.Render: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial: The UnlitMaterial component is a material that can render meshes without lighting at full bright. Optionally can render each vertex like a square that faces the user or a direction. It can also optionally render differently depending on which eye it is viewed from. This is used in the left and right cat item, and for 3D photos.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.TintColor: What color to tint the projected image.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.Texture: The texture to debug data for.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.TextureScale: How much to scale up or down the different texture maps.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.TextureOffset: How much to shift around the position of the different texture maps.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.MaskScale: The UV scale of the mask texture.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.MaskOffset: The UV offset of the mask texture.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.MaskMode: How to apply the mask texture to the material.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.AlphaCutoff: If <mark=#ffffff10>BlendMode</mark> is set to cutout, discard rendering of pixels for the surface that fall below this alpha threshold.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.UseVertexColors: Whether the material should use vertex colors from the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.VertexColorInterpo: How to interpolate vertex colors on the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.OffsetTexture: The texture used to offset the positions of pixels.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.OffsetMagnitude: How much to amplify the offset effect.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.OffsetTextureScale: How much to scale the detail of the <mark=#ffffff10>OffsetTexture</mark>
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.OffsetTextureOffse: How much to offset the detail of the <mark=#ffffff10>OffsetTexture</mark>
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.PolarUVmapping: Whether to use polar UV unwrapping.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.PolarPower: The power of the polar UV unwrapping.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.StereoTextureTrans: Whether a texture on the surface should appear different in the right eye.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.RightEyeTextureSca: The override for texture in the right eye for scale.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.RightEyeTextureOff: The override for texture in the right eye for offset.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.DecodeAsNormalMap: Whether to decode the textures as a normal map.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.UseBillboardGeomet: Whether to not render the surface, and instead render each vertex like a Particle with this material and texture.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.UsePerBillboardSca: Whether scaling of vertex points should be used for Billboard geometry positioning.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.UsePerBillboardRot: Whether rotation of vertex points should be used for Billboard geometry positioning.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.UsePerBillboardUV: Whether uv data of vertex points should be used for Billboard geometry positioning.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.BillboardSize: The base size for Billboard geometry.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.OffsetFactor: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), adjusted for the polygon's slope relative to the camera's near and far planes. This can be used to sort overlapping geometry that would otherwise Z-fight.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.OffsetUnits: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), in units equal to the smallest possible difference for the rendering device being used.
messages.Tooltip.UnlitMaterial.RenderQueue: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.UV_RectMaterial: The UV_RectMaterial component is a material that shows where a rect (rectangle) would be on a material surface.
messages.Tooltip.UV_RectMaterial.OuterColor: The color for the rest of the material.
messages.Tooltip.UV_RectMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.UV_RectMaterial.Sidedness: Render on both sides of the mesh, front, or back.
messages.Tooltip.UV_RectMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.UV_RectMaterial.ZTest: Determines whether this object should render when it is in front of or behind other objects that respect depth from the camera
messages.Tooltip.UV_RectMaterial.OffsetFactor: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), adjusted for the polygon's slope relative to the camera's near and far planes. This can be used to sort overlapping geometry that would otherwise Z-fight.

messages.Tooltip.FogBoxVolumeMaterial: The FogBoxVolumeMaterial is a material used to create cube-shaped volumetric fog on a mesh, such as a Texture3D. Using this material on non-cube-like meshes may result in visual artifacts and/or denser fog in places where the corners of the cube would be.
messages.Tooltip.FogBoxVolumeMaterial.BaseColor: Starting color for the fog volume
messages.Tooltip.FogBoxVolumeMaterial.Accumulatio: Color to use for a Constant fog
Color at the end of a Vertical Gradient fog
Color at the start of a Vertical Gradient fog
messages.Tooltip.FogBoxVolumeMaterial.GammaCurve: The gamma curve of the material colors.
messages.Tooltip.FogBoxVolumeMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.FogBoxVolumeMaterial.RenderQueue: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.VolumeUnlitMaterial: The VolumeUnlitMaterial component is used to display 3D Textures like Xrays, sunbeams, MRIs, CT scans, or any other 3D texture in a volumetric 3D cube that can optionally be sliced. Can also be used for volumetric lighting, but high texture resolutions can be large and cause considerable render lag.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeUnlitMaterial.Mode: How to display the texture in 3D space.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeUnlitMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeUnlitMaterial.RenderQueue: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.VolumeUnlitMaterial.Volume: The 3D texture this material should display.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeUnlitMaterial.StepSize: How many cell details there should be.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeUnlitMaterial.Gain: How much to gain up the color.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeUnlitMaterial.Exp: What exponent to raise colors to.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeUnlitMaterial.Accumulation: if color goes beyond this brightness, don't render it.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeUnlitMaterial.HitThreshold: tells what value to consider a hit. Any value above this is drawn, and any other value below this is culled.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeUnlitMaterial.InputRange: What range duration within 0<->1 do the colors in the 3d image have? Useful for isolating particular ranges for densities in a CT scan.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeUnlitMaterial.InputOffset: at what point does the range start?
messages.Tooltip.VolumeUnlitMaterial.UseAlphaChan: Whether to use the alpha channel in the pixel data of <mark=#ffffff10>Volume</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeUnlitMaterial.Slices: Slice planes that cut the volume along local positions and directions.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeUnlitMaterial.Highlights: A list of planes with offsets which define a region of the volume to override the color of.
messages.Tooltip.WireframeMaterial: Wireframe material is a material that can be used to see the edges of the polygons in a mesh. This does not show the polygons of a text renderer.
messages.Tooltip.WireframeMaterial.Thickness: the thickness of the lines in the polygons
messages.Tooltip.WireframeMaterial.ScreenSpace: whether the thickness should stay constant in width on the screen regardless of distance
messages.Tooltip.WireframeMaterial.LineColor: the color of this material's lines
messages.Tooltip.WireframeMaterial.FillColor: the color that should fill the center of each polygon.
messages.Tooltip.WireframeMaterial.InnerLineColor: The color of the lines on the inner frensel.
messages.Tooltip.WireframeMaterial.InnerFillColor: The color of the fill in the inner frensel.
messages.Tooltip.WireframeMaterial.UseFresnel: Whether to use the frensel effect with the normal and inner line and fill colors.
messages.Tooltip.WireframeMaterial.LineFarColor: what the color of the lines should be when this material is rendered further away zbuffer wise.
messages.Tooltip.WireframeMaterial.FillFarColor: what the color of the fill should be when this material is rendered further away zbuffer wise.
messages.Tooltip.WireframeMaterial.InnerLineFarCo: what the color of the lines on the inner frensel should be when this material is rendered further away zbuffer wise.
messages.Tooltip.WireframeMaterial.InnerFillFarCo: what the color of the fill on the inner frensel should be when this material is rendered further away zbuffer wise.
messages.Tooltip.WireframeMaterial.Exp: What exponent to raise colors to.
messages.Tooltip.WireframeMaterial.Texture: The texture to debug data for.
messages.Tooltip.WireframeMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.WireframeMaterial.DoubleSided: Whether to render this material on both sides.
messages.Tooltip.WireframeMaterial.OffsetFactor: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), adjusted for the polygon's slope relative to the camera's near and far planes. This can be used to sort overlapping geometry that would otherwise Z-fight.
messages.Tooltip.WireframeMaterial.OffsetUnits: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), in units equal to the smallest possible difference for the rendering device being used.
messages.Tooltip.WireframeMaterial.RenderQueue: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial: The XiexeToonMaterial component was originally made to be a toon material Component, but is widely considered a robust material.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.MainTexture: The main color map of the material.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.Color: The surface color texture of the material.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.UseVertexColor: Whether the material should use vertex colors from the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.VertexColorInt: How to interpolate vertex colors on the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.BlendMode: How to blend this material's colors vs what it rendered on top of.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.ZWrite: whether this material should respect the distance it is from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.AlphaClip: Any alpha value below this amount is not rendered for any given pixel when cutout is enabled.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.MainTextureSca: The UV scaling of the main texture.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.MainTextureOff: The UV offset of the main texture.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.NormalMap: The normal map is used as a way to change the appearance of a surface when shined on by lights, to give the illusion of a raised surface.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.NormalMapScale: The UV scaling of the normal map texture.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.NormalMapOffse: The UV offset of the normal texture.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.NormalScale: How much to scale up the effect of the normal map.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.Metallic: The metallicness of the material, multiplied by the red channel of <mark=#ffffff10>MetallicGlossMap</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.Glossiness: The glossiness of the material, multiplied by the alpha channel of <mark=#ffffff10>MetallicGlossMap</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.Reflectivity: The reflectivity of the material in the absence of <mark=#ffffff10>MetallicGlossMap</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.MetallicGlossM: A packed channel texture for Metallic, glossyness, and reflectivity.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.EmissionMap: The glow or emission color map.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.EmissionMapSca: The UV scaling of the emission texture.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.EmissionMapOff: The UV offset of the emission texture.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.RimAlbedoTint: How much the rim lighting effects albedo tint.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.RimAttenuation: The strength of the rim light
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.RimRange: Size of light area of the shadowramp
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.SpecularIntens: Controls the intensity of specular effects. A value of 100 will make the material decently shiny, while a value of 0 will turn off specular entirely.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.SpecularArea: Controls how spread out specular effects are. A value of 0.1 would be very spread out, like a piece of paper, while 0.9 would be very focused, like metal.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.Matcap: A texture that looks like a sphere with reflection shading on it. Used to make the fake reflection map.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.MatcapTint: The color tint to apply to <mark=#ffffff10>Matcap</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.OcclusionMap: Used to specify surface parts in eternal shadow due to being close to other mesh parts. (Green channel for PBS materials, Red channel for Xiexe)
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.OcclusionColor: The color of the occlusion effect.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.OutlineWidth: The thickness of the outline around the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.OutlineColor: The color of the outline around the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.OutlineAlbedoT: Whether the outline is tinted by albedo.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.OutlineMask: Grayscale mask that determines relative thickeness of outline. Black is no outline. White is 100% of the OutlineWidth value.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.ShadowRamp: The shadow ramp used to influence what shadows look like on a model.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.ShadowRampMask: Grayscale mask that selects which pixel row of the Shadow Ramp texture to use for a given texel.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.ShadowRim: Color of the dark rim light
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.ShadowSharpnes: Definition of all shadows applied to the material
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.ShadowRimRange: Size of the dark section of the shadowramp
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.ShadowRimThres: Size of the dark section of the shadowramp
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.ShadowRimSharp: Definition of the dark section of the shadowramp
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.ShadowRimAlbed: How much the albedo color influences the dark section of the shadowramp
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.ThicknessMap: The thickness map of the surface. Used to make subsurface effects.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.SubsurfaceColo: The color of the subsurface effect.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.OffsetFactor: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), adjusted for the polygon's slope relative to the camera's near and far planes. This can be used to sort overlapping geometry that would otherwise Z-fight.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.OffsetUnits: How much this material should be pushed forwards or backwards on the depth buffer (negative is closer), in units equal to the smallest possible difference for the rendering device being used.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.Culling: Whether to show the material on the front, back, or both sides
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.ColorMask: What colors behind the material should make it through the filter.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.AlbedoUV: The UV map index Albedo map should use.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.NormalUV: The UV map index normal map should use.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.MetallicUV: The UV map index metallic map should use.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.ThicknessUV: The UV map index Thickness map should use.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.OcclusionUV: The UV map index occlusion map should use.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.EmissionUV: The UV map index emission map should use.
messages.Tooltip.XiexeToonMaterial.RenderQueue: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.DynamicSubtitleProvider: The DynamicSubtitleProvider component is used to provide subtitles on videos that are playing in world.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicSubtitleProvider.AssetURL: The URI to the subtitle file.
messages.Tooltip.SineWaveClip: The SineWaveClip component is an audio clip that can be used to get a whistling like tone with a frequency and loudness.
messages.Tooltip.SquareWaveClip: The SquareWaveClip component makes a kind of buzzing noise that can be given a pitch and a loudness.
messages.Tooltip.SawtoothWaveClip: The SawtoothWaveClip component is used to get a buzzing type frequency of audio that can have a different pitch or loudness.
messages.Tooltip.TriangleWaveClip: The TriangleWaveClip component is used to make a buzzing like repeating sound clip, which can be used to make audio clips that make a repeating noise.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexNoiseClip: The SimplexNoiseClip component samples from simplex noise in order to get a pseudo-random frequency chart. Like a constantly random smooth changing whistle pitch.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexNoiseClip.Duration: How long the clip is and its duration of how much of simplex noise it samples.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexNoiseClip.Offset: Where in the simplex noise algorithm this clip starts sampling from.
messages.Tooltip.ValueNoiseClip: The ValueNoiseClip component takes completely random noise and turns it into audio data. The best way to describe the noise would be like TV static.
messages.Tooltip.ValueNoiseClip.Seed: The seed for the random audio noise.
messages.Tooltip.CheckerboardCubemap: The CheckerboardCubemap component procedurally generates a cubemap for Projection 360 Materials that is a checkerboard with different pairs of colors for each of the 6 sides.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralCubemapBase.FilterMode: How to handle the interpolation between pixels.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralCubemapBase.Anisotropi: The levels of Anisotropic filtering distances when using Anisotropic for <mark=#ffffff10>FilterMode</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralCubemapBase.MipmapBias: Whether to see lower resolution versions of the texture closer up or not (MipMaps).
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralCubemapBase.Profile: The color profile to use for this texture's rendering.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralCubemap.Size: The size of each side of the cubemap in pixels.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralCubemap.Mipmaps: Whether to enable the rendering of mipmaps, which are lower res textures at further distances from the camera to improve performance.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralCubemap.Format: TextureFormat describes how Texture's pixel are stored in the VRAM.
messages.Tooltip.CheckerboardCubemap.CheckerSize: The size in pixels for 1 square of the checkerboard pattern.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexCubemap.Background: The color for the simplex's background.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexCubemap.Foreground: The color for the simplex's foreground.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexCubemap.Scale: The scale of the simplex algorithm.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexCubemap.Use4D: whether to use 4D to allow animation or shifting of the texture.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexCubemap.WOffset: The offset of the texture in 4D.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicSpriteFont: The DynamicSpriteFont component is used to construct a font asset that can be used to create custom fonts character by character.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicSpriteFont.MaxSize: The max size of a glyph in the font sheet.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicSpriteFont.Glyphs: A list of Font characters that should make up this dynamic font.
messages.Tooltip.DiagnosticGaussianSplat: The Diagnostic Gaussian Splat component is used to render diagnostic info on Gaussian Splats.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralGaussianSplat.Override: Force the bounding box calculated from this component to use <mark=#ffffff10>OverridenBoundingBox</mark> instead of calculating when requested.

the bounding box this component should say it has when <mark=#ffffff10>OverrideBoundingBox</mark> is enabled. Useful for bounding box calculations with Flux, or changing the selection box for this component when rendered.
messages.Tooltip.DiagnosticGaussianSplat.SplatCol: The profile that the vertex colors for this mesh should be displayed in.
messages.Tooltip.DiagnosticGaussianSplat.Splats: A list of splats to render diagnostics for.
messages.Tooltip.ArrowMesh: Arrow Mesh is a procedural Asset mesh that generates a cylinder with a cone on the end.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralMesh.OverrideBoundingB: Force the bounding box calculated from this component to use <mark=#ffffff10>OverridenBoundingBox</mark> instead of calculating when requested.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralMesh.OverridenBounding: the bounding box this component should say it has when <mark=#ffffff10>OverrideBoundingBox</mark> is enabled. Useful for bounding box calculations with Flux, or changing the selection box for this component when rendered.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralMesh.Profile: The profile that the vertex colors for this mesh should be displayed in.
messages.Tooltip.ArrowMesh.Vector: the direction in local space of the mesh render rendering this arrow that the arrow should point towards. Also defines the length. For more information on Local Space, see Coordinate  Spaces
messages.Tooltip.ArrowMesh.Sides: how many sides the cone and cylinder of this arrow should have.
messages.Tooltip.ArrowMesh.BodyRadius: Determines the thickness of the base under the arrow.
messages.Tooltip.ArrowMesh.HeadRadius: Determines the radius of the arrows head's base.
messages.Tooltip.ArrowMesh.HeadLength: Determines the length of the arrow's head.
messages.Tooltip.ArrowMesh.MinimalBodyLength: Determines the arrow's length at minimum. In case the provided <mark=#ffffff10>Vector</mark>'s magnitude is less than this number.
messages.Tooltip.ArrowMesh.SphereOnZero: whether the arrow should render as a sphere instead when the <mark=#ffffff10>Vector</mark> is (0,0,0)
messages.Tooltip.ArrowMesh.BodyUVScale: the scale multiplier of the uvs on the cylinder body. Uvs of the cylinder is the same as a procedural cylinder.
messages.Tooltip.ArrowMesh.BodyUVOffset: the shifted offset of the uvs for the cylinder body in 2d space
messages.Tooltip.ArrowMesh.HeadUVScale: the scale multiplier of the uvs on the cone head. Uv of the cone is the same as a procedural cone.
messages.Tooltip.ArrowMesh.HeadUVOffset: the shifted offset of the uvs for the cone head in 2d space
messages.Tooltip.ArrowMesh.BaseColor: Color of the vertices of the bottom edge of the arrow.
messages.Tooltip.ArrowMesh.TopColor: Color of the vertices of the top edge of the arrow.
messages.Tooltip.ArrowMesh.HeadColor: Color of the vertices of the head of the arrow.
messages.Tooltip.BallisticPathMesh: The ballistics path mesh is a component that generates a stripe or tube mesh that traces the future path of a virtual thrown object.
messages.Tooltip.BallisticPathMesh.InitialPositio: The initial position of the ballistics path.
messages.Tooltip.BallisticPathMesh.InitialVelocit: The initial velocity of the ballistics path.
messages.Tooltip.BallisticPathMesh.Gravity: The gravity affecting the ballistics path (m/s^2)
messages.Tooltip.BallisticPathMesh.Drag: the resistance through the air of the generated ballistics path
messages.Tooltip.BallisticPathMesh.StepSize: How many units maximum every bend/segment is in length (the last segment may be shorter).
messages.Tooltip.BallisticPathMesh.TotalUnits: How many segments the path mesh will have give or take 1 or 2.
messages.Tooltip.BallisticPathMesh.Shape: What shape the arc should be rendered as.
messages.Tooltip.BallisticPathMesh.Size: How big to scale this ballisitics path
messages.Tooltip.BallisticPathMesh.Points: how many sides the tube mesh uses. Only accepts values greater than or equal to 3.
messages.Tooltip.BallisticPathMesh.DualSided: whether the generated mesh should be dual sided.
messages.Tooltip.BallisticPathMesh.Up: The up direction against gravity for the generated mesh (local space)
messages.Tooltip.BallisticPathMesh.DistanceSizeGr: How much to make the width/radius of the path mesh grow as it progresses along the arc.
messages.Tooltip.BallisticPathMesh.MinGrownSize: How skinny at minimum the Ballistic Path Mesh will get from applying <mark=#ffffff10>DistanceSizeGrowth</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.BallisticPathMesh.MaxGrownSize: How wide at maximum the Ballistic Path Mesh will get from applying <mark=#ffffff10>DistanceSizeGrowth</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.BallisticPathMesh.UseLastSegment: Whether to add one final segment that goes to <codr>LastSegmentPosition</mark>
messages.Tooltip.BallisticPathMesh.LastSegmentPos: The local position to place the end of the last segment at if <mark=#ffffff10>UseLastSegment</mark> is enabled.
messages.Tooltip.BentTubeMesh: Bent tube mesh is a component that makes a tube structure.
messages.Tooltip.BentTubeMesh.Radius: The radius of the tube (from center to outside).
messages.Tooltip.BentTubeMesh.Sides: How many sides the tube should have around it
messages.Tooltip.BentTubeMesh.Segments: how many bends from end to end the tube should have
messages.Tooltip.BentTubeMesh.StartPoint: Where the tube should start at in local space. Also known as P0 on a quadratic bezier curve.
messages.Tooltip.BentTubeMesh.DirectTargetPoint: P1 on the quadratic bezier curve in local space.
messages.Tooltip.BentTubeMesh.ActualTargetPoint: P2 on the quadratic bezier curve in local space.
messages.Tooltip.BentTubeMesh.StartPointColor: What the color of the tube should be at the start if using a material that supports vertex colors.
messages.Tooltip.BentTubeMesh.EndPointColor: What the color of the tube should be at the end if using a material that supports vertex colors.
messages.Tooltip.BentTubeMesh.Ends: How to handle the geometry of the ends of the tube.
messages.Tooltip.BentTubeMesh.Shading: Toggle this mesh being smooth shaded
messages.Tooltip.BevelBoxMesh: The BevelBoxMesh component can be placed in a MeshRenderer to create a box with beveled edges.
messages.Tooltip.BevelBoxMesh.UVScale: The inverse of the size the material should appear on the surface as.
messages.Tooltip.BevelBoxMesh.ScaleUVWithSize: Change uv scale visually with the value of <mark=#ffffff10>Size</mark>
messages.Tooltip.BezierTubeMesh: Bezier tube mesh is a component that allows for making a continuous tube of geometry that is a Bezier curve shape defined by points.
messages.Tooltip.BezierTubeMesh.Radius: The radius of the tube (from center to outside).
messages.Tooltip.BezierTubeMesh.Points: Curve tangent point/handle array used for the generation of this mesh. Editing this via Mods produces undesirable results where the curve has really sharp bends.
messages.Tooltip.BezierTubeMesh.SegmentPoints: How many sides the tube mesh has around it's mid section.
messages.Tooltip.BezierTubeMesh.StepsPerUnitLengt: How many bends from point to point the tube should have.
messages.Tooltip.BoxMesh: The BoxMesh component procedurally generates a cube mesh for use with a MeshRenderer, according to the parameters provided.
messages.Tooltip.BoxMesh.UVScale: The inverse of the size the material should appear on the surface as.
messages.Tooltip.BoxMesh.ScaleUVWithSize: Change uv scale visually with the value of <mark=#ffffff10>Size</mark>
messages.Tooltip.BoxMeshGizmo: The BoxMeshGizmo component is used to resize Component:BoxMeshs in a visual manner using the DevTip.
messages.Tooltip.BoxMeshGizmo._target: The box Mesh this component is targeting for editing.
messages.Tooltip.BoxMeshGizmo._cubeGizmo: The visual for this component which is used to edit <mark=#ffffff10>_target</mark> in a visual manner.
messages.Tooltip.CameraFrustumMesh: The Camera Frustum Mesh is a component that can be used in a Mesh Renderer to visualize the limits and view cone of a Camera
messages.Tooltip.CameraFrustumMesh.Near: Slices the mesh to demonstrate camera nearclip
messages.Tooltip.CameraFrustumMesh.Far: Expands the mesh to demonstrate camera farclip.
messages.Tooltip.CameraFrustumMesh.HorizontalAngl: The horizontal FOV of the camera represented by this mesh.
messages.Tooltip.CameraFrustumMesh.VerticalAngle: The Vertical FOV of the camera represented by this mesh.
messages.Tooltip.CameraFrustumMesh.DualSided: Enables the mesh to be dual sided.
messages.Tooltip.CameraFrustumMesh.NearCap: Whether or not the end closest to the camera should be capped with a quad.
messages.Tooltip.CameraFrustumMesh.FarCap: Whether or not the end furthest from the camera should be capped with a quad.
messages.Tooltip.CapsuleMesh: Creates a mesh that is a cylinder with rounded ends instead of flat ends.
messages.Tooltip.CapsuleMesh.Radius: Determines the roundness of the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.CapsuleMesh.Height: Determines how tall the mesh is.
messages.Tooltip.CapsuleMesh.Rings: Determines the smoothness of the top & bottom of the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.CapsuleMesh.UVScale: The inverse of the size the material should appear on the surface as.
messages.Tooltip.CircleMesh: Attach this component to a slot, and then put it inside of a Mesh Renderer component to view what it looks like.
messages.Tooltip.CircleMesh.Rotation: How much to rotate the circle mesh from it's rest position before rendering
messages.Tooltip.CircleMesh.Segments: How many sides the circle has.
messages.Tooltip.CircleMesh.Radius: How big the circle's radius is.
messages.Tooltip.CircleMesh.Arc: How much out of 360 degrees the circle should take up of a full circle.
messages.Tooltip.CircleMesh.UVScale: The scale of the texture detail of the material rendered on this mesh.
messages.Tooltip.CircleMesh.ScaleUVWithSize: Whether to make the detail of the material independent of the size of circle in global space.
messages.Tooltip.CircleMesh.TriangleFan: Whether to make the circle a bunch of triangles that meet in the middle or not.
messages.Tooltip.ConeMesh: The ConeMesh component acts as a source of geometry for rendering a cone with x sides with an optionally flat end.
messages.Tooltip.ConeMesh.Height: Determines the height of the cone.
messages.Tooltip.ConeMesh.RadiusBase: Determines the width of the cone's base.
messages.Tooltip.ConeMesh.RadiusTop: Determines the width from the top of the cone.
messages.Tooltip.ConeMesh.Sides: How smooth the roundness of the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.ConeMesh.Caps: Whether to cap the ends with geometry or leave them open.
messages.Tooltip.ConeMesh.UVScale: The scale of the procedural UV map. Larger values make textures appear smaller on the mesh's surface, and vice versa.
messages.Tooltip.ConstrainedDelaunayMesh.Vertices: The vertices that make up the outside edge of the plane.
messages.Tooltip.ConstrainedDelaunayMesh.Holes: The holes cut into the main mesh.
messages.Tooltip.ConstrainedDelaunayMesh.AutoTria: Whether to auto generate the triangulation plane value.
messages.Tooltip.ConstrainedDelaunayMesh.Triangul: Where to attempt to center the triangulation.
The plane to align the triangulation calculation with for best fit.
messages.Tooltip.ConvexHullMesh: The ConvexHullMesh component is used to generate a Convex Hull Mesh using a series of Points to wrap around.
messages.Tooltip.ConvexHullMesh.Points: The points in local space this mesh is trying to shrink wrap around.
messages.Tooltip.ConvexHullMesh.FlatShading: Whether the geometry should be not smooth.
messages.Tooltip.ConvexHullMesh.MinVertexDistance: the distance by minimum 2 <mark=#ffffff10>Points</mark> can be before they're automatically merged.
messages.Tooltip.CrossMesh: The CrossMesh component is a 3D mesh made of 3 crossing boxes of equal thickness and length. It can be rotated, and the bars can be lengthened and thickened/thinned.
messages.Tooltip.CrossMesh.Size: The size of the cross from end to end in local space.
messages.Tooltip.CrossMesh.BarRatio: This multiplied by size is how thick each bar is in local space.
messages.Tooltip.CurvedPlaneMesh: Generates a plane mesh that is curved vertically. A picture in this page of it can be seen.
messages.Tooltip.CurvedPlaneMesh.Size: How big the curve plane mesh is, mostly a ratio
messages.Tooltip.CurvedPlaneMesh.Curvature: How much to bend the curve plane mesh by
messages.Tooltip.CurvedPlaneMesh.TiltAngle: How much to "rotate" the curve plane mesh length wise.
messages.Tooltip.CurvedPlaneMesh.AspectRatioCompe: How to compensate for stretching due to curving the plane mesh backwards.
messages.Tooltip.CurvedPlaneMesh.UVScale: The inverse of the size the material should appear on the surface as.
messages.Tooltip.CurvedPlaneMesh.UVOffset: Shift the visual detail of the material around.
messages.Tooltip.CurvedPlaneMesh.Segments: How many bends/angles the plane should have in it's curve
messages.Tooltip.CylinderMesh: The CylinderMesh component is used to provide a mesh to render. The mesh that it provides is a procedural 3D cylinder with a number of sides and a radius.
messages.Tooltip.CylinderMesh.Height: The height of the cylinder in meters in local space.
messages.Tooltip.CylinderMesh.Radius: The radius of the cylinder in meters in local space.
messages.Tooltip.CylinderMesh.Sides: How many sides the cylinder should have around it's midsection.
messages.Tooltip.CylinderMesh.Caps: Whether the cylinder should be a tube or not.
messages.Tooltip.CylinderMesh.UVScale: The inverse of the size the material should appear on the surface as.
messages.Tooltip.DebugTestMeshThroughput: The DebugTestMeshThroughput component is used in code by parallel jamming quads into a MeshX object via multithreading to test how much can be done at once.
messages.Tooltip.DebugTestMeshThroughput.Count: How many mesh quads to parrallel jam into the meshx data.
messages.Tooltip.DebugTestMeshThroughput.Progress: How much to offset the quads by in rotation.
messages.Tooltip.DebugTestMeshThroughput.UpdateTi: How long it took for the Asset to update.
messages.Tooltip.LightningMesh: The LightingMesh component generates geometry of a procedural lighting bolt for use with a Component:MeshRenderer.
messages.Tooltip.LightningMesh.Seed: Used to generate a pseudo-random variant of a lighting bolt mesh
messages.Tooltip.LightningMesh.Points: The shading for the lighting bolt geometry. Like smooth or flat for example.
messages.Tooltip.LightningMesh.Ends: how to handle the geometry at the beginning and end of lines in the lighting bolt
messages.Tooltip.LightningMesh.DualSided: whether the lighting bolt is visible from the inside.
messages.Tooltip.LightningMesh.Point0: the start point of the lightning bolt
messages.Tooltip.LightningMesh.Point1: the end point of the lightning bolt
messages.Tooltip.LightningMesh.StrikeLevels: A list of different branch variants and bends in the lighting bolt.
messages.Tooltip.FrameMesh: The FrameMesh component generates a procedural mesh that is like a flat polygon 2d frame for use with Mesh Renderers.
messages.Tooltip.FrameMesh.ContentSize: The size of interior object. usually driven by Asset Frames
messages.Tooltip.FrameMesh.Thickness: How thick the frame should be, expanding outwards from the center content.
messages.Tooltip.FrameMesh.UVScale: How big the procedural UV map should be, which influences material detail and color map scales.
messages.Tooltip.GridMesh: The GridMesh component generates a procedural mesh for use with Mesh Renderers that is like a Quad Mesh but has multiple squares instead of being one big square.
messages.Tooltip.GridMesh.Points: how many vertex points should be used on each axis to make the grid of quads
messages.Tooltip.GridMesh.Size: how big the grid is scale wise.
messages.Tooltip.GridMesh.DisplacementMagnitude: How far to displace the grid texture using <mark=#ffffff10>DisplacementTexture</mark>
messages.Tooltip.GridMesh.DisplacementTexture: The texture to use for displacement. must be set to Uncompressed.
messages.Tooltip.HollowConeMesh: The HollowConeMesh component is used to generate geometry for use with a Component:MeshRenderer. This component generates a cone with a hollow center with internal geometry and a thickness.
messages.Tooltip.HollowConeMesh.Height: how tall the hollow cone should be starting from the center
messages.Tooltip.HollowConeMesh.OuterRadiusBase: the radius of the bottom ring that makes up the outer tube
messages.Tooltip.HollowConeMesh.InnerRadiusBase: the radius if the bottom ring that makes up the inner tube
messages.Tooltip.HollowConeMesh.OuterRadiusTop: the radius of the top ring that makes up the outer tube
messages.Tooltip.HollowConeMesh.InnerRadiusTop: the radius of the top ring that makes up the inner tube
messages.Tooltip.HollowConeMesh.Segments: how many faces make up each cylinder for the outside and inside.
messages.Tooltip.HollowConeMesh.UVScale: how much to multiply the scale of the UVs for the procedural mesh.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMesh: The IcoSphereMesh component procedurally generates a icosahedron mesh for use with a MeshRenderer, according to the parameters provided.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMesh.Radius: The radius of the ico sphere visual.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMesh.Subdivisions: Number of IcoSphere subdivisions — Values are clamped in the range [0,8]
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMesh.FlatShading: Use flat/faceted shading instead of smooth shading.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMesh.FlatFaceExtrude: Distance to extrude faces after being generated. Creates an "Exploded" appearance
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMesh.FlatFaceScale: Scale of individual faces, relative to their base size.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMeshGizmo: The IcoSphereMeshGizmo component is a Gizmo component used to edit ico sphere mesh properties via the dev tool.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMeshGizmo._target: The ico sphere mesh we are editing and targeting.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMeshGizmo._sphereGizmo: The Sphere gizmo to use as a way of editing <mark=#ffffff10>_target</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMeshSH1: The Ico Sphere Mesh SH1 component creates Ico Sphere data using Sphercal harmonics.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMeshSH1.Colors: The spherical harmonic colors to use.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMeshSH1.RadiusMultiplie: The spherical harmonics to use to influence the mesh radius at different spots.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMeshSH1.NegativeRadiusI: Whether negative colors invert the mesh geometry.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMeshSH2: The Ico Sphere Mesh SH2 component creates Ico Sphere data using Sphercal harmonics.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMeshSH2.Colors: The spherical harmonic colors to use.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMeshSH2.RadiusMultiplie: The spherical harmonics to use to influence the mesh radius at different spots.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMeshSH2.NegativeRadiusI: Whether negative colors invert the mesh geometry.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMeshSH3: The Ico Sphere Mesh SH3 component creates Ico Sphere data using Sphercal harmonics.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMeshSH3.Colors: The spherical harmonic colors to use.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMeshSH3.RadiusMultiplie: The spherical harmonics to use to influence the mesh radius at different spots.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMeshSH3.NegativeRadiusI: Whether negative colors invert the mesh geometry.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMeshSH4: The Ico Sphere Mesh SH4 component creates Ico Sphere data using Sphercal harmonics.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMeshSH4.Colors: The spherical harmonic colors to use.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMeshSH4.RadiusMultiplie: The spherical harmonics to use to influence the mesh radius at different spots.
messages.Tooltip.IcoSphereMeshSH4.NegativeRadiusI: Whether negative colors invert the mesh geometry.
messages.Tooltip.LabelPointerMesh: The LabelPointerMesh component is used by the labeler tool to point to a position and underline some generated text elements.
messages.Tooltip.LabelPointerMesh.LabelPoint: Where the label should render in local space.
messages.Tooltip.LabelPointerMesh.TargetPoint: Where the line from the label should point to.
messages.Tooltip.LabelPointerMesh.LabelRotation: The rotation of the label visual.
messages.Tooltip.LabelPointerMesh.LabelWidth: The width of the label visual.
messages.Tooltip.LabelPointerMesh.Width: The width of the line going from the label to <mark=#ffffff10>TargetPoint</mark>
messages.Tooltip.LabelPointerMesh.ExpandLerp: How long the label underline should be.
messages.Tooltip.LabelPointerMesh.DualSided: Whether the mesh should have duplicate geometry with the fronts facing the opposite direction.
messages.Tooltip.MultiLineMesh: The MultiLineMesh component generates geometry for use with a Component:MeshRenderer. For now, this component is unusable without Mods due to having a list type object.
messages.Tooltip.MultiLineMesh.Lines: A set of lines that make up the source of the data for the geometry of this mesh.
messages.Tooltip.MultiSegmentMesh: Multi line segment mesh is a component that generates mesh data for use with a SkinnedMeshRenderer. It uses a list of lines to generate lines of geometry with varying thickness.
messages.Tooltip.MultiSegmentMesh.Sides: how many faces around each line (circle wise) that the lines have.
messages.Tooltip.MultiSegmentMesh.FlipNormalDirec: Flips the normals on the generated mesh data.
messages.Tooltip.MultiSegmentMesh.Segments: Different lines that make up the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.BoxArrayMesh: Box Array Mesh is a component that is generated by brush tools and can be a cloud of boxes with points, sizes, and rotations.
messages.Tooltip.BoxArrayMesh.Points: List of positions that there should be boxes
messages.Tooltip.BoxArrayMesh.Sizes: List of sizes for each box.
messages.Tooltip.BoxArrayMesh.Rotations: List of rotations for each box.
messages.Tooltip.BoxArrayMesh.Colors: List of colors for each box.
messages.Tooltip.BoxArrayMesh.ColorsProfile: The profile that the vertex colors for this mesh should be displayed in.. However, this is for every box in the array.
messages.Tooltip.BoxArrayMesh.UV_Scales: List of UV scales for each box, scaling the material surface detail bigger or smaller.
messages.Tooltip.ColorDepthPointCloud: The Color Depth Point Cloud component.
messages.Tooltip.ImageColorDistributionGraph: The ImageColorDistributionGraph component is used to make mesh data for a set of points with colors and sizes based on a provided image's pixels. The mesh needs to be used with Billboard geometry to be viewed properly.
messages.Tooltip.ImageColorDistributionGraph.Text: The texture to analyze and use to generate the vertex graph for this mesh.
messages.Tooltip.ImageColorDistributionGraph.Colo: The color space to use for X, Y, and Z coordinates on the graph for.
messages.Tooltip.ImageColorDistributionGraph.MaxT: The max size of the image on one axis that this image will use when making the graph. The image gets resized internally to fit this size.
messages.Tooltip.ImageColorDistributionGraph.Base: The size of any color point on the graph at minimum.
messages.Tooltip.ImageColorDistributionGraph.Accu: How much to add to the size of a graph point if a color is repeated with the same RGB values.
messages.Tooltip.ImageColorDistributionGraph.MaxS: The maximum size a color point on the graph can be.
messages.Tooltip.ImageColorDistributionGraph.Scal: How much to scale the graph up or down.
messages.Tooltip.ImageColorDistributionGraph.Alph: if alpha is below this threshold, then don't add the color to the graph.
messages.Tooltip.PointMesh: Point mesh makes geometry data of a cloud of points that have UVs, scales, colors, and more. These points can be displayed via a Mesh Renderer using a Unlit Material with at least it's UseBillboardGeometry field set to true.
messages.Tooltip.PointMesh.Points: The positions of each vertex point.
messages.Tooltip.PointMesh.Sizes: The sizes of each vertex point.
messages.Tooltip.PointMesh.Rotations: The rotations of each vertex point.
messages.Tooltip.PointMesh.Colors: The vertex color of each vertex point.
messages.Tooltip.PointMesh.ColorsProfile: The color profile of each vertex point.
messages.Tooltip.PointMesh.UVs: The UV center and quad scales of each vertex point.
messages.Tooltip.PointMesh.Sort: Enable sorting the mesh vertex indices by distance from <mark=#ffffff10>SortPoint</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.PointMesh.SortPoint: The point to sort by distance from.
messages.Tooltip.QuadArrayMesh: Quad array mesh makes a bunch of quads with defined positions, rotations, vertex colors, and UV coordinates which are rendered as one mesh. This is made from Quad brushes which can be found in the resonite essentials folder.
messages.Tooltip.QuadArrayMesh.Points: An array inaccessible without Mods that defines where each quad for this mesh should be in local space.
messages.Tooltip.QuadArrayMesh.Sizes: An array inaccessible without Mods that defines the scale for each quad for this mesh in local space.
messages.Tooltip.QuadArrayMesh.Rotations: An array inaccessible without Mods that defines the rotation for each quad for this mesh in local space.
messages.Tooltip.QuadArrayMesh.Colors: An array inaccessible without Mods that defines the vertex colors for each quad for this mesh.
messages.Tooltip.QuadArrayMesh.ColorsProfile: The color profile of each quad.
messages.Tooltip.QuadArrayMesh.UVs: An array inaccessible without Mods xy of each element defines the center of each quad and zw defines the uv scales of each quad on a texture/material.
messages.Tooltip.QuadMesh: Quad mesh is a component that generates mesh data procedurally that makes a flat 2D square.
messages.Tooltip.QuadMesh.Rotation: How to rotate the quad in local space.
messages.Tooltip.QuadMesh.Size: The size of the quad in local space.
messages.Tooltip.QuadMesh.UVOffset: The added offset to the UVs.
messages.Tooltip.QuadMesh.UVScale: The scale of the UVs from the bottom left.
messages.Tooltip.QuadMesh.ScaleUVWithSize: Whether to scale uv mapping with <mark=#ffffff10>Size</mark>
messages.Tooltip.QuadMesh.DualSided: Whether to make a second pair of triangles that are visible from the opposite side.
messages.Tooltip.QuadMesh.UseVertexColors: Whether to use vertex colors which can be read by materials.
messages.Tooltip.RampMesh: The RampMesh component procedurally generates mesh data for a ramp shaped mesh.
messages.Tooltip.RampMesh.UVScale: Changes the UV scale of the procedural mesh.
messages.Tooltip.StandaloneRectMesh`1: The StandaloneRectMesh is a procedural wrapper to allow mesh renderer usable mesh data of any RectMeshSource. The list of RectMeshSources this component can use for M is generated inside the component selector list when attaching.
messages.Tooltip.StandaloneRectMesh`1.Rect: The part of the generated waveform to render.
messages.Tooltip.StandaloneRectMesh`1.Mesh: The mesh parameters used to render this rect mesh. Usually takes an audio clip.
messages.Tooltip.RigBoneMesh: Procedural mesh that generates a typical rig bone visual used in 3D software.
messages.Tooltip.RigBoneMesh.Length: Determines the total length of the shape.
messages.Tooltip.RigBoneMesh.Width: Determines the width of the widest point.
messages.Tooltip.RigBoneMesh.NormalizedSplitPosit: How close to the 'bottom' the widest point of the shape is. 0.2 is default.
messages.Tooltip.RingMesh: A ring mesh is a component that generates a ring of quads that has an InnerRadius, OuterRadius, and resolution which is the Segments field.
messages.Tooltip.RingMesh.Rotation: Rotate the entire mesh by this.
messages.Tooltip.RingMesh.Segments: how sub divisions length wise this ring mesh has (smoothness of the arc)
messages.Tooltip.RingMesh.Arc: how much of a circle from 0<->360 this mesh should take up.
messages.Tooltip.RingMesh.InnerRadius: The radius of the inner circle of this ring mesh.
messages.Tooltip.RingMesh.OuterRadius: The radius of the outer circle of this ring mesh.
messages.Tooltip.RingMesh.UVScale: The scale of material details and colors on the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.SegmentMesh: The SegmentMesh component is an evolved form of Component:SlotSegmentMesh the behavior is identical, except when not provided slots it will use local coordinate points instead.
messages.Tooltip.SegmentMesh.Radius: The radius of the line from <mark=#ffffff10>PointA</mark>/<mark=#ffffff10>SlotA</mark> to <mark=#ffffff10>PointB</mark>/<mark=#ffffff10>SlotB</mark>
messages.Tooltip.SegmentMesh.PointA: The starting point of the generated line in local space.
messages.Tooltip.SegmentMesh.PointB: The ending point of the generated line in local space.
messages.Tooltip.SegmentMesh.SlotA: The starting point of the generated line. This will be used instead of <mark=#ffffff10>PointA</mark> if provided.
messages.Tooltip.SegmentMesh.SlotB: The ending point of the generated line. This will be used instead of <mark=#ffffff10>PointB</mark> if provided.
messages.Tooltip.SegmentMesh.Shading: Whether to use different shading like smooth or flat.
messages.Tooltip.SlotSegmentMesh: The SlotSegmentMesh component generates mesh data of a line between PointA and PointB. This is to be used with a Component:MeshRenderer.
messages.Tooltip.SlotSegmentMesh.Radius: The radius of the line from <mark=#ffffff10>PointA</mark> to <mark=#ffffff10>PointB</mark>
messages.Tooltip.SlotSegmentMesh.PointA: The starting point of the generated line.
messages.Tooltip.SlotSegmentMesh.PointB: The ending point of the generated line.
messages.Tooltip.SphereMesh: The SphereMesh component generates a sphere mesh for use with a MeshRenderer.
messages.Tooltip.SphereMesh.Radius: The radius of the sphere mesh.
messages.Tooltip.SphereMesh.Segments: how many vertical lined edges the sphere has
messages.Tooltip.SphereMesh.Rings: how many horizontal lined edges the sphere has.
messages.Tooltip.SphereMesh.Shading: Whether to have smooth or flat shading
messages.Tooltip.SphereMesh.UVScale: the scale of material detail on the surface
messages.Tooltip.SphereMesh.DualSided: whether to make a second set of polygons that shows the mesh when viewed from the inside.
messages.Tooltip.SphereMeshGizmo: The SphereMeshGizmo component is used to adjust the radius of a sphere mesh.
messages.Tooltip.SphereMeshGizmo._sphereGizmo: The gizmo to use to edit the sphere mesh size.
messages.Tooltip.StripeMesh: The StripeMesh component generates mesh data that is used with a Component:MeshRenderer.
messages.Tooltip.StripeMesh.Points: The points used to generate the geometry for this mesh.
messages.Tooltip.StripeMesh.SegmentPoints: How many cuts/bends are between each point. Higher means more detail
messages.Tooltip.StripeMesh.DualSided: Whether this mesh is visible from the back and front.
messages.Tooltip.StripeWireMesh: The StripeWireMesh component is used primarily in ProtoFlux wires, and generates mesh data for use with a Component:MeshRenderer.
messages.Tooltip.WireMeshBase.Point0: The start point in local space for this mesh
messages.Tooltip.WireMeshBase.Point1: The end point for this mesh in local space.
messages.Tooltip.WireMeshBase.Tangent0: the direction the mesh should bend after exiting start point
messages.Tooltip.WireMeshBase.Tangent1: the direction the mesh should bend while entering exit point.
messages.Tooltip.WireMeshBase.Orientation0: How much to rotate the starting point edge by after applying <mark=#ffffff10>Tangent0</mark>
messages.Tooltip.WireMeshBase.Orientation1: How much to rotate the end point edge by after applying <mark=#ffffff10>Tangent1</mark>
messages.Tooltip.WireMeshBase.Steps: how many segments the mesh should have between <mark=#ffffff10>Point0</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>Point1</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.WireMeshBase.Exp: how much the mesh should be forced in the direction of the start and end tangents when starting and ending it's path.
messages.Tooltip.WireMeshBase.Color0: The vertex color to give the part of the mesh starting at <mark=#ffffff10>Point0</mark>. Is interpolated along the mesh to <mark=#ffffff10>Point1</mark> to color <mark=#ffffff10>Color1</mark>
messages.Tooltip.WireMeshBase.Color1: The vertex color for <mark=#ffffff10>Point1</mark> to interpolate towards.
messages.Tooltip.WireMeshBase.UVScale: The scale of the texture detail on this mesh's surface.
messages.Tooltip.WireMeshBase.UVOffset: The offset of the texture detail on this mesh's surface.
messages.Tooltip.StripeWireMesh.Width0: the width of the mesh in local space at <mark=#ffffff10>Point0</mark> on the mesh. Is interpolated along the mesh to <mark=#ffffff10>Point1</mark> to width <mark=#ffffff10>Width1</mark>
messages.Tooltip.StripeWireMesh.Width1: The width in local space for <mark=#ffffff10>Point1</mark> to interpolate towards.
messages.Tooltip.TorusMesh: The TorusMesh component generates procedural geometry that is used with Component:MeshRenderer.
messages.Tooltip.TorusMesh.MinorSegments: The amount of detail/cuts along the circle of the torus. Making this 4 will make the torus a square instead of a circle shape.
messages.Tooltip.TorusMesh.MajorSegments: The amount of detail/cuts going around and through the center along the outside and inside.
messages.Tooltip.TorusMesh.MinorRadius: the fatness/thickness of the torus. If this is too big than the hole in the center will seal up.
messages.Tooltip.TorusMesh.UVScale: the scale of the material detail on the surface of the torus.
messages.Tooltip.TriangleDiagnosticMesh: The TriangleDiagnosticMeshes component is used to show where a triangle is in the world. This is usually used as a debug tool.
messages.Tooltip.TriangleDiagnosticMesh.TriangleI: The index of the triangle on the mesh to make a diagnostic for.
messages.Tooltip.TriangleDiagnosticMesh.Vertex0Co: The vertex color of vertex 0 for this diagnostic mesh.
messages.Tooltip.TriangleDiagnosticMesh.Vertex1Co: The vertex color of vertex 1 for this diagnostic mesh.
messages.Tooltip.TriangleDiagnosticMesh.Vertex2Co: The vertex color of vertex 2 for this diagnostic mesh.
messages.Tooltip.TriangleDiagnosticMesh.VertexCol: The color profile of the input Colors of <mark=#ffffff10>Vertex0Color</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>Vertex1Color</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>Vertex2Color</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.TriangleDiagnosticMesh.Displace: How much to displace the triangle's position by it's normal (front face direction)
messages.Tooltip.TriangleDiagnosticMesh.Mesh: The mesh to make a diagnostic for.
messages.Tooltip.TriangleMesh: The TriangeMesh component is used to generate mesh data which is a triangle for use with a Component:MeshRenderer.
messages.Tooltip.TriangleMesh.Vertex0: The first vertex point of the triangle.
messages.Tooltip.TriangleMesh.Vertex1: The second vertex point of the triangle.
messages.Tooltip.TriangleMesh.Vertex2: The third vertex point of the triangle.
messages.Tooltip.TriangleMesh.AutoNormals: whether to automatically generate the triangle normals
messages.Tooltip.TriangleMesh.AutoTangents: whether to automatically generate the triangle tangents
messages.Tooltip.TriangleMesh.DualSided: whether to make this visible from the back.
messages.Tooltip.TubeBoxMesh: The TubeBoxMesh component is used to generate geometry that is used with a Component:MeshRenderer.
messages.Tooltip.TubeBoxMesh.SegmentPoints: The divisions in geometry/edges in the tube's circular direction.
messages.Tooltip.TubeBoxMesh.UVScale: The scale of material detail on the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.TubeBoxMesh.UVOffset: The offset of material detail on the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.TubeMesh: The TubeMesh component is used to generate a procedural tube mesh for use with a Component:MeshRenderer.
messages.Tooltip.TubeMesh.Points: A list of points that the tube should follow in order.
messages.Tooltip.TubeMesh.SegmentPoints: How many cuts/bend points there should be between each TubePoint in <mark=#ffffff10>Points</mark>
messages.Tooltip.FieldAdapter`1.Target: The target field is driven to <mark=#ffffff10>Value</mark>'s rgb colors inverted.
messages.Tooltip.FieldAdapter`1.Value: The field being driven by a system using the legacy audio curve settings from before Awwdio.
messages.Tooltip.TubeSpiralMesh: The TubeSpiralMesh component is a procedural Mesh that looks like a corkscrew often seen on the end of a wine bottle opener.
messages.Tooltip.TubeSpiralMesh.Steps: How many length wise subdivisions the tube mesh has.
messages.Tooltip.TubeSpiralMesh.MaximumDistanceBe: The distance allowed between each segment before the amount of steps are reduced.
messages.Tooltip.TubeSpiralMesh.CoilCount: How many winds around the center the coil should have.
messages.Tooltip.TubeSpiralMesh.ScaleCoilCountByL: Whether the distance between coils stays consistent as the length grows by adding more coils.
messages.Tooltip.TubeSpiralMesh.CoilPhase: How much to offset the coil starting angle.
messages.Tooltip.TubeSpiralMesh.StartPoint: The center point position of what the Spiral is spiraling around at the beginning.
messages.Tooltip.TubeSpiralMesh.StartTangent: The bend towards bias position of the Spiral at the beginning. Makes the Spiral sheer or warp towards this point at the beginning.
messages.Tooltip.TubeSpiralMesh.EndPoint: The center point position of what the Spiral is spiraling around at the end.
messages.Tooltip.TubeSpiralMesh.EndTangent: The bend towards bias position of the Spiral at the end. Makes the Spiral sheer or warp towards this point at the end. lerps from beginning.
messages.Tooltip.TubeSpiralMesh.StartSpiralRadius: The distance from the center to the Spiral tube center at the beginning.
messages.Tooltip.TubeSpiralMesh.EndSpiralRadius: The distance from the center to the Spiral tube center at the end.
messages.Tooltip.TubeSpiralMesh.StartSpiralOrient: The rotation of each segment ring at the start.
messages.Tooltip.TubeSpiralMesh.EndSpiralOrientat: The rotation of each segment ring at the end. Lerps from start.
messages.Tooltip.TubeSpiralMesh.Ends: How the ends should be sealed if at all on the corkscrew.
messages.Tooltip.TubeSpiralMesh.Shading: The Shading of the screw geometry.
messages.Tooltip.TubeSpiralMesh.StartTubeRadius: The radius of each segment at the start.
messages.Tooltip.TubeSpiralMesh.EndTubeRadius: The radius of each segment at the end. Lerps from start.
messages.Tooltip.TubeSpiralMesh.TubePoints: How many sides the tube has circumference wise on each segment.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyTubeSpiralMeshAdapter: The Legacy Tube Spiral Mesh Adapter component is used to convert legacy content for tube spiral meshes into current content.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyTubeSpiralMeshAdapter.Upwa: The original float value for UpwardTrend.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyTubeSpiralMeshAdapter.EndP: The value field for the EndPoint of the Component:TubeSpiralMesh.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyTubeSpiralMeshAdapter.Coil: The value field for the CoilCount of the Component:TubeSpiralMesh.
messages.Tooltip.TubeWireMesh: This creates a procedural  mesh like the Component:StripeWireMesh but is a tube instead.
messages.Tooltip.TubeWireMesh.Radius0: The starting radius of the tube.
messages.Tooltip.TubeWireMesh.Radius1: The ending radius of the tube.
messages.Tooltip.TubeWireMesh.Ends: How to seal the ends of the mesh, if at all.
messages.Tooltip.TubeWireMesh.Points: How many subdivisions circumference wise the tube has.
messages.Tooltip.WrapperMesh: The WrapperMesh is a procedural mesh that copies an existing mesh, making it act like a procedural mesh instead through this component.
messages.Tooltip.MultiChannelSimplexTexture3D: The MultiChannelSimplexTexture3D component creates a monochrome noise channel for each RGBA channel with their own settings. It is also considered a ITexture3D element.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralTexture3DBase.FilterMo: How to interpolate enlarged pixels when rendering the texture.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralTexture3DBase.Anisotro: The levels of Anisotropic filtering distances when using Anisotropic for <mark=#ffffff10>FilterMode</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralTexture3DBase.WrapMode: How to handle wrapping for UVW sampling positions beyond the 0 to 1 range for U.
How to handle wrapping for UVW sampling positions beyond the 0 to 1 range for V.
How to handle wrapping for UVW sampling positions beyond the 0 to 1 range for W.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralTexture3DBase.Profile: The color profile to use for this texture's rendering.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralTexture3D.Size: The size of the procedural texture in pixels.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralTexture3D.Format: TextureFormat describes how Texture's pixel are stored in the VRAM.
messages.Tooltip.MultiChannelSimplexTexture3D.R: The settings to use when generating the noise for the red channel.
messages.Tooltip.MultiChannelSimplexTexture3D.G: The settings to use when generating the noise for the green channel.
messages.Tooltip.MultiChannelSimplexTexture3D.B: The settings to use when generating the noise for the blue channel.
messages.Tooltip.MultiChannelSimplexTexture3D.A: The settings to use when generating the noise for the alpha channel.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTexture3D: The SimplexTexture3D component makes a procedural noise in 3D similar to the StaticTexture3D component, as a Texture3D.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTexture3D.Offset: How much to shift the texture from 0-1 on each axis.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTexture3D.Scale: How much to scale up or down the noise texture.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTexture3D.Background: The background color of the noise generation.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTexture3D.Foreground: The foreground color of the noise generation.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTexture3D.Use4D: Whether or not the noise texture should have a 4 spatial dimension element to it.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTexture3D.WOffset: The offset of the texture in the 4th spatial dimension/
messages.Tooltip.UVW_ProceduralTexture3D: The UVWProceduralTexture3D component is used to create a default RGB cube that any XYZ coordinate corresponds to the same RGB color, as a Texture3D. This works as long as if ValueOffset is left at 0 and ValueMultiplier is left at 1. This means if it is plugged into a LUT Material, it will not modify the colors seen through the material.
messages.Tooltip.UVW_ProceduralTexture3D.ValueOff: How much to add to the colors in the cube by, which shifts all colors towards the +X,+Y,+Z direction.
messages.Tooltip.UVW_ProceduralTexture3D.ValueMul: How much to multiply the colors in the cube by, which scales all colors towards the +X,+Y,+Z direction.
messages.Tooltip.AudioWaveformTexture: The AudioWaveformTexture component is used to generate a texture of a line with a visual representation of the volume and pitch of an audio over it's duration. For use with a material.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralTextureBase.FilterMode: How to handle the interpolation between pixels.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralTextureBase.Anisotropi: The levels of Anisotropic filtering distances when using Anisotropic for <mark=#ffffff10>FilterMode</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralTextureBase.MipmapBias: Whether to see lower resolution versions of the texture closer up or not (MipMaps).
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralTextureBase.Profile: The color profile to use for this texture's rendering.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralTexture.Size: The size of the procedural texture in pixels.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralTexture.Mipmaps: Whether to enable the rendering of mipmaps, which are lower res textures at further distances from the camera to improve performance.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralTexture.Format: TextureFormat describes how Texture's pixel are stored in the VRAM.
messages.Tooltip.AudioWaveformTexture.Clip: The audio clip to pull data from for generating the wave form.
messages.Tooltip.AudioWaveformTexture.ForegroundC: The color of the waveform itself.
messages.Tooltip.DocumentPageTexture: The DocumentPageTexture component generates texture data using a document file like a PDF which can be displayed using a material in a Component:MeshRenderer or UIX.
messages.Tooltip.DocumentPageTexture.Size: The size of the procedural texture in pixels.
messages.Tooltip.DocumentPageTexture.Mipmaps: Whether to use mip maps or not.
messages.Tooltip.DocumentPageTexture.Document: The document to display using this texture.
messages.Tooltip.DocumentPageTexture.PageIndex: The page in <mark=#ffffff10>Document</mark> to display as the texture data.
messages.Tooltip.DocumentPageTexture.PageRegion: The area of the in <mark=#ffffff10>Document</mark> to display on page <mark=#ffffff10>PageIndex</mark>
messages.Tooltip.GradientStripTexture: The GradientStripTexture is a component that generates a texture with a gradient defined by Gradient
messages.Tooltip.GradientStripTexture.Gradient: A list of gradients that this texture uses to generate the final result. This requires Mods to edit.
messages.Tooltip.GradientStripTexture.Exp: The sharpness of the transition between each <mark=#ffffff10>Gradient</mark>
messages.Tooltip.GradientStripTexture._orientatio: The direction to generate the gradient strip towards
messages.Tooltip.GridTexture: The GridTexture component is used to generate a grid of lines with a background color to use in materials.
messages.Tooltip.GridTexture.BackgroundColor: The color of the background before <mark=#ffffff10>Grids</mark> are layered on top.
messages.Tooltip.GridTexture.Grids: A list of Grids of lines that get layered on top of each other in order.
messages.Tooltip.NoiseTexture: Noise texture is a component that procedurally generates a 2D texture with a random noise pattern like TV static.
messages.Tooltip.NoiseTexture.Seed: The number to use to seed the generation of the noise for the texture.
messages.Tooltip.NoiseTexture.Monochrome: Whether to make the noise generation use a mix of 2 colors rather than random colors.
messages.Tooltip.NoiseTexture.MonochromeMax: Color 1 to use for the noise texture when <mark=#ffffff10>Monochrome</mark> is enabled.
messages.Tooltip.NoiseTexture.MonochromeMin: Color 2 to use for the noise texture when <mark=#ffffff10>Monochrome</mark> is enabled.
messages.Tooltip.NoiseTexture.Bias: The bias to use more of <mark=#ffffff10>MonochromeMax</mark> or <mark=#ffffff10>MonochromeMin</mark>
messages.Tooltip.NoiseTexture.Gain: How much to brighten the entire texture.
messages.Tooltip.NoiseTexture.Clamp: Whether to keep color ranges for the output from going beyond 1 and causing bloom.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTexture: The SimplexTexture component is used to generate procedural noise to use in any material.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTexture.Offset: How much to shift the procedural noise.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTexture.Scale: scales the pattern up or down inside the image.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTexture.Background: The background color of the noise.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTexture.Foreground: The color of the noise itself.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTexture.Use3D: Whether to allow <mark=#ffffff10>ZOffset</mark> to affect the texture, and generates 3D noise.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTexture.ZOffset: The offset in the 3D noise, which takes a slice of the 3D noise for the texture.
messages.Tooltip.SolidColorTexture: The SolidColorTexture component Creates a Texture2D of a solid color.
messages.Tooltip.SolidColorTexture.Color: The solid color to use for the texture.
messages.Tooltip.StringQRCodeTexture: The StringQRCodeTexture Component is used to encode string data like URLs and text into a QR code texture.
messages.Tooltip.StringQRCodeTexture.Format: TextureFormat describes how Texture's pixel are stored in the VRAM.
messages.Tooltip.StringQRCodeTexture.Payload: The string to encode into the QR code.
messages.Tooltip.StringQRCodeTexture.ECCLevel: The level of error correction to encode (for use with a dirty QR code for higher values)
messages.Tooltip.StringQRCodeTexture.Color0: The background color of the QR code
messages.Tooltip.StringQRCodeTexture.Color1: The color of the usually black squares of the QRCode
messages.Tooltip.UVTexture: The UVTexture component is used to procedurally generate texture data for a debug uv texture image that is colored red and green based on the x and y coordinates at any given point on an image.
messages.Tooltip.RenderTextureProvider: Attach to a slot and use as a render texture for any component that accepts it.
messages.Tooltip.RenderTextureProvider.Size: The size the camera renders in <mark=#ffffff10>width</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>height</mark>
messages.Tooltip.RenderTextureProvider.Depth: Precision, in bits, of the depth buffer used for rendering. Possibly values are 0, 16, 24 and 32.
messages.Tooltip.RenderTextureProvider.FilterMode: How to handle the interpolation between pixels.
messages.Tooltip.RenderTextureProvider.Anisotropi: The levels of Anisotropic filtering distances when using Anisotropic for <mark=#ffffff10>FilterMode</mark>
messages.Tooltip.SpriteProvider: A SpriteProvider defines a slice of a Texture2D as a Sprite that may be used by other components.
messages.Tooltip.SpriteProvider.Rect: The rectangle within the sprite to render. Note that the second x and y are actually the width and height of the Rect. The values are proportions of the width and height of the image in the Texture property.
messages.Tooltip.SpriteProvider.Borders: Border widths for 9-slice scaling. The XYZW components set the left, bottom, right, and top borders respectively.
messages.Tooltip.SpriteProvider.Scale: Effects the center size when 9 slicing.
messages.Tooltip.SpriteProvider.FixedSize: Effects the center size when 9 slicing.
messages.Tooltip.NullTextureProvider: Null texture providers are automatically added to the Root as a way of telling the render engine of FrooxEngine what to use as a null texture when a material is missing. This texture is a procedural gray and dark gray checkerboard texture.
messages.Tooltip.StaticAnimationProvider: Static Animation Provider is a Component that is generated when importing AnimJ's or files with animation data.
messages.Tooltip.StaticAssetProvider`3.URL: The URI to the FrooxEngine approved shader generated by the Rendering team of YellowDogManStudios.
messages.Tooltip.StaticAudioClip: The StaticAudioClip component is what stores pre recorded audio data for components that play audio like Component:AudioClipPlayer. This component is auto generated when a sound file is imported into the game as part of an audio clip object.
messages.Tooltip.StaticAudioClip.LoadMode: How and when to load the audio
messages.Tooltip.StaticAudioClip.SampleRateMode: How to sample the audio's audio data.
messages.Tooltip.StaticBinary: This component can be combined with the BinaryExportable component to allow for exporting the Slot as a binary file on your device.
messages.Tooltip.StaticCubemap: The StaticCubemap is a component that is used to store data for a 6 sided texture for use with skyboxes.
messages.Tooltip.StaticTextureProvider`4.FilterMo: How to handle the interpolation between pixels.
messages.Tooltip.StaticTextureProvider`4.Anisotro: The levels of Anisotropic filtering distances when using Anisotropic for <mark=#ffffff10>FilterMode</mark>
messages.Tooltip.StaticTextureProvider`4.Uncompre: Whether to not compress the texture's size before loading into ram/vram. doesn't affect cloud size.
messages.Tooltip.StaticTextureProvider`4.DirectLo: Whether to load the cubemap directly from it's URI source and to not cache it locally.
messages.Tooltip.StaticTextureProvider`4.ForceExa: Whether to not generate variants for this texture and force a certain texture type
messages.Tooltip.StaticTextureProvider`4.Preferre: The format to use for texture compression rather than the auto picked one. See Texture Compression Formats for more details.
The color profile to use rather than the auto picked one. (usually linear)
messages.Tooltip.StaticTextureProvider`4.MipMapBi: Whether to see lower resolution versions of the texture closer up or not (MipMaps).
messages.Tooltip.StaticCubemap.MaxSize: The maxiumum size to show to the user, which scaled versions are generated by the cloud on the fly from the original and sent as part of the Asset Variant System
messages.Tooltip.StaticDocument: Static document is a component that is used to store the data of a text document (Usually a PDF but never text files) for usage in a document viewer favorite-d by the user.
messages.Tooltip.StaticFont: This component is auto generated when importing new font files.
messages.Tooltip.StaticFont.Padding: How many pixels of padding should be used between letters in the font.
messages.Tooltip.StaticFont.PixelRange: Sets the distance field range in output pixels.
messages.Tooltip.StaticFont.GlyphEmSize: Sets the size of the glyphs in the atlas in pixels per em.
messages.Tooltip.StaticFont.MipMaps: Whether to display lower res versions of the font when it is further away to save on performance.
messages.Tooltip.StaticFont.MipMapFiltering: How to filter transitions between mipmaps when it is viewed at incremental distances further away.
messages.Tooltip.StaticFont.LODBias: Whether to see lower res versions at smaller distances to save performance.
messages.Tooltip.StaticGaussianSplat: The Static Gaussian Splat component stores data for the Gaussian Splat asset itself.
messages.Tooltip.StaticLocaleProvider: The StaticLocaleProvider component gets the list of locale data for all languages.
messages.Tooltip.StaticLocaleProvider.OverrideLoc: The locale to use other than the user selected one.
messages.Tooltip.StaticMesh: Attach to a slot, provide a URL and insert into a Component:SkinnedMeshRenderer or Component:MeshRenderer.
messages.Tooltip.StaticMesh.Readable: Whether this component's data can be read via protoflux.
messages.Tooltip.StaticSavedObjectProvider.PreGat: Whether to pregather the object's assets or not.
messages.Tooltip.StaticShader: The StaticShader component is used to handle the shaders often seen in Materials.
messages.Tooltip.StaticTexture2D: The StaticTexture2D Component stores a reference to a static asset representing a 2D Texture.
messages.Tooltip.StaticTexture2D.IsNormalMap: This texture should be interpreted as a Normal Map
messages.Tooltip.StaticTexture2D.PowerOfTwoAlignT: The threshold for pixel count from range [0.0,1.0] before scaling both axes to a power of 2.
messages.Tooltip.StaticTexture2D.CrunchCompressed: Whether the texture should be lossily compressed using [https://github.com/Unity-Technologies/crunch Crunch]. This goes on top of the compression set in PreferredFormat and doesn't decrease VRAM usage, only download size.
messages.Tooltip.StaticTexture2D.MinSize: The miniumum size to show to the user, which scaled versions are generated by the cloud on the fly from the original and sent as part of the Asset Variant System
messages.Tooltip.StaticTexture2D.MaxSize: The maxiumum size to show to the user, which scaled versions are generated by the cloud on the fly from the original and sent as part of the Asset Variant System
messages.Tooltip.StaticTexture2D.MipMaps: Whether to enable the rendering of mipmaps, which are lower res textures at further distances from the camera to improve performance.
messages.Tooltip.StaticTexture2D.KeepOriginalMipM: Whether to use the original mipmaps that came with the image data (yes you could have rainbow mipmaps at further distances) or not.
messages.Tooltip.StaticTexture2D.MipMapFilter: How to apply filtering to the image's mipmaps. please see Filtering!
messages.Tooltip.StaticTexture2D.Readable: Whether this can be read per pixel via things like ProtoFlux
messages.Tooltip.StaticTexture3D: The StaticTexture3D component represents a bunch of textures stacked on top of each other to make a 3D grid of pixel values, as a Texture3D. These textures can be displayed in 3D, or sampled via 3D positions in a few different components or in ProtoFlux Nodes. When importing a Texture3D, this component is usually applied after the import.
messages.Tooltip.StaticTexture3D.Readable: Whether or not the texture can be sampled via texture sampling ProtoFlux nodes
messages.Tooltip.AudioClipAssetMetadata: Audio Clip Asset Metadata is a component that will populate itself with different small bits of information about an audio clip, also known as metadata. The metadata will be the default values until an audio clip is provided, which will then populate the data.
messages.Tooltip.AudioClipAssetMetadata.AudioClip: The audio clip to read metadata from
messages.Tooltip.AudioClipAssetMetadata.SampleRat: The Hz the audio is sampled at.
messages.Tooltip.AudioClipAssetMetadata.Channels: The audio channel configuration the audio was recorded in.
messages.Tooltip.AudioClipAssetMetadata.ChannelCo: How many channels are in the audio recording. usually 1 channel for mono or 2 channels for L and R Stereo.
messages.Tooltip.AudioClipAssetMetadata.SampleCou: how many total samples the audio clip has
messages.Tooltip.AudioClipAssetMetadata.Duration: How long the audio clip is in seconds.
messages.Tooltip.AudioClipAssetMetadata.Extension: the extension of the file type the audio was recorded in.
