messages.Tooltip.AudioClipAssetMetadata.CodecInfo: The codec the audio is written in like WAV or FLAC and the quality of the audio.
messages.Tooltip.AudioClipAssetMetadata.FullyDeco: If the audio is loaded or not and decoded (Waveform visible)
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamMetadata`1: Audio Stream Metadata is a component that reads the running tallies of the different samples being sent and received by an AudioStream`1.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamMetadata`1.Stream: The audio source to get data from
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamMetadata`1.UnreadSamp: The amount of samples yet to be played
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamMetadata`1.TotalMisse: Samples missed due to lag or other reasons.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamMetadata`1.LastMissed: The last time samples were missed instead of being read from the audio source.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamMetadata`1.BufferLeng: How big the sample buffer is.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamMetadata`1.AverageRea: How many samples per second on a running average are being processed.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamMetadata`1.AverageWri: The many samples per second on a running average are being written to the buffer per second.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamMetadata`1.GlobalInde: The audio source's index in the pool of audio players playing sounds.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamMetadata`1.SamplesAva: Samples currently ready to be encoded to a format supported by FrooxEngine's audio system.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamMetadata`1.FrameSize: The size of audio data coming in, which is in bytes and is influenced by data and channel count.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamMetadata`1.MaxFrameSi: The max size of audio data coming in, which is in bytes and is influenced by data and channel count.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamMetadata`1.EncodedSam: how fast encoded samples are being processed.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamMetadata`1.TotalPacke: How many audio packets have been networked by the audio source so far.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamMetadata`1.TotalLostP: How many packets of the network that were not received from the audio source.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamMetadata`1.LastLostPa: The last time packets were lost by the audio source.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamMetadata`1.PacketLoss: On average, the percentage of audio packets from the network that are being lost.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamMetadata`1.AverageCod: The average amount of samples incoming in encoded form.
messages.Tooltip.BitmapAssetMetadata: The BitmapAssetMetadata component is used to get pixel data information on a given image or texture.
messages.Tooltip.BitmapAssetMetadata.Asset: The image to get data from.
messages.Tooltip.BitmapAssetMetadata.BitsPerPixel: How many bits are used per pixel to store color data in <mark=#ffffff10>Asset</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.BitmapAssetMetadata.ChannelCount: How many channels <mark=#ffffff10>Asset</mark> has (RGBA for example is 4, but some images may have more or less.)
messages.Tooltip.BitmapAssetMetadata.AverageHSV: <mark=#ffffff10>Asset</mark>'s average HSV color, visible ot not.
messages.Tooltip.BitmapAssetMetadata.InvalidPixel: how many pixels in <mark=#ffffff10>Asset</mark> that have NAN values in their color values.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyLinearFloatColorFieldAdapt: The LegacyLinearFloatColorFieldAdapter component is used in converted legacy particle systems that were converted to PhotonDust as part of The Performance Updates.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyColorAsLinearAdapter: The LegacyColorAsLinearAdapter component is used to convert old colors from migrated content into the linear color space for use in modern content.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyColorToLinearReinterpret_s: The LegacyColorToLinearReinterpret_sRGB_Adapter component is used to convert colors from legacy colors to modern content with different color spaces.
messages.Tooltip.AdjustHDRInverseGamma22: Drives a target field by applying a fixed inverse 2.2 HDR gamma from to an input value.
messages.Tooltip.CubemapAssetMetadata: The CubemapAssetMetadata component is used to display info about any particular cube map provided.
messages.Tooltip.CubemapAssetMetadata.Size: The size in pixels of the width or height of any 1 of the 6 sides. (The sides are the same size)
messages.Tooltip.CubemapAssetMetadata.HasMipMaps: Whether the cubemap has mipmap data.
messages.Tooltip.CubemapAssetMetadata.MipMapCount: How many mip map resolution variants the cubemap has.
messages.Tooltip.CubemapAssetMetadata.MemoryBytes: How much memory the cubemap takes up.
messages.Tooltip.CubemapAssetMetadata.FormattedMe: The bytes formatted into a readable string.
messages.Tooltip.CubemapAssetMetadata.Format: The texture format of the cube map.
messages.Tooltip.CubemapAssetMetadata.ActualLoade: The Asset variant Type currently loaded.
messages.Tooltip.CubemapAssetMetadata.Profile: The color profile being used for this texture when rendering it.
messages.Tooltip.DocumentAssetMetadata: The DocumentAssetMetadata component gets data about a provided document asset.
messages.Tooltip.DocumentAssetMetadata.Document: The document (usually PDF) that info is being acquired from.
messages.Tooltip.DocumentAssetMetadata.PageCount: The page count of the document.
messages.Tooltip.DocumentPageMetadata: The DocumentPageMetadata component is used to give Metadata about a particular page on a document (Usually a PDF)
messages.Tooltip.DocumentPageMetadata.Document: The document to get Metadata for.
messages.Tooltip.DocumentPageMetadata.PageIndex: The page in <mark=#ffffff10>Document</mark> to get metadata for.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatAssetMetadata: The Gaussian Splat Asset Metadata component gives statistics on Gaussian Splats seen in inspectors for components that generate or provide such data.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatAssetMetadata.Splat: The splat to get data on.
The number of splats in the Gaussian splat.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatAssetMetadata.Memor: The amount of memory in bytes the Gaussian splat takes up.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatAssetMetadata.Forma: The amount of memory in bytes the Gaussian splat takes up when formatted.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatAssetMetadata.Actua: The variant of the Gaussian splat that is loaded from the cloud.
messages.Tooltip.ITexture2DAssetMetadata: This component gives the info on the size of a Texture2D.
messages.Tooltip.ITexture2DAssetMetadata.Texture: The texture to read data for
messages.Tooltip.MaterialAssetMetadata: The MaterialAssetMetadata component gives variant info about materials in the form of usable values. These values are usually listed at the bottom of a material inspector but are unusable in that state.
messages.Tooltip.MaterialAssetMetadata.Material: The material to get Metadata on.
messages.Tooltip.MaterialAssetMetadata.VariantInd: The material variant defined by GPU definitions on the shader. For example, a reflection material has a second variant for having a normal map vs without.
messages.Tooltip.MaterialAssetMetadata.VariantID: The ID of the material variant (can be seen at the bottom of a material in the Inspector too.
messages.Tooltip.MaterialAssetMetadata.WaitingFor: Whether this material is waiting to be applied in the render scene due to missing variants or otherwise.
messages.Tooltip.MeshAssetMetadata: The MeshAssetMetadata component gives info about the data on a mesh component.
messages.Tooltip.MeshAssetMetadata.Mesh: The mesh to get meta data on.
messages.Tooltip.MeshAssetMetadata.PointCount: the amount of vertices with no connected faces in <mark=#ffffff10>Mesh</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.MeshAssetMetadata.SubmeshCount: How many sub meshes are in the main <mark=#ffffff10>Mesh</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.MeshAssetMetadata.HasUV0s: Whether <mark=#ffffff10>Mesh</mark> has data for the first UV map.
messages.Tooltip.MeshAssetMetadata.HasUV1s: Whether <mark=#ffffff10>Mesh</mark> has data for the second UV map.
messages.Tooltip.MeshAssetMetadata.HasUV2s: Whether <mark=#ffffff10>Mesh</mark> has data for the third UV map.
messages.Tooltip.MeshAssetMetadata.HasUV3s: Whether <mark=#ffffff10>Mesh</mark> has data for the fourth UV map.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralAssetMetadata`1: The ProceduralAssetMetadata component is shown in the inspector for procedural meshes and textures.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralAssetMetadata`1.Asset: The procedural asset to get data on.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralAssetMetadata`1.Update: How many times <mark=#ffffff10>Asset</mark> has been updated since generation.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralAssetMetadata`1.Error: Whether <mark=#ffffff10>Asset</mark> ran into an error when generating.
messages.Tooltip.Texture2DAssetMetadata: The Texture2DAssetMetadata component converts the metadata often seen on texture components in an inspector into data that can be pulled into other systems as actual numbers and values.
messages.Tooltip.Texture2DAssetMetadata.Texture: The texture to get metadata on.
messages.Tooltip.Texture2DAssetMetadata.Size: How big <mark=#ffffff10>Texture</mark> is in pixels as a width height pair.
messages.Tooltip.Texture2DAssetMetadata.MemoryByt: How many bytes of memory <mark=#ffffff10>Texture</mark> takes up.
messages.Tooltip.Texture2DAssetMetadata.Formatted: The bytes of memory <mark=#ffffff10>Texture</mark> takes up formatted into an easy to read format.
messages.Tooltip.Texture2DAssetMetadata.ActualLoa: The actual variant of <mark=#ffffff10>Texture</mark> that is loaded on the client.
messages.Tooltip.Texture2DAssetMetadata.Profile: The color profile <mark=#ffffff10>Texture</mark> is being rendered in.
messages.Tooltip.Texture3DAssetMetadata: The Texture3DAssetMetadata component is able to retrieve the 3D texture data like size, memory, and format usually seen at the bottom of an inspector on a particular component as usable values.
messages.Tooltip.Texture3DAssetMetadata.Texture: The texture to get Asset Metadata for.
messages.Tooltip.Texture3DAssetMetadata.Depth: The depth or Z of <mark=#ffffff10>Texture</mark> in pixels.
messages.Tooltip.Texture3DAssetMetadata.MemoryByt: How many bytes of memory <mark=#ffffff10>Texture</mark> takes up.
messages.Tooltip.Texture3DAssetMetadata.Formatted: The bytes that <mark=#ffffff10>Texture</mark> uses in easy to read human format.
messages.Tooltip.Texture3DAssetMetadata.Format: The format that <mark=#ffffff10>Texture</mark> is kept in.
messages.Tooltip.Texture3DAssetMetadata.ActualLoa: The variant of <mark=#ffffff10>Texture</mark> that has been loaded, which can be different on a client due to graphics settings or variants are being calculated on the cloud.
messages.Tooltip.Texture3DAssetMetadata.Profile: The color space <mark=#ffffff10>Texture</mark> is rendered in.
messages.Tooltip.VideoTextureAssetMetadata: The VideoTextureAssetMetadata component turns the data at the bottom of an inspector on a video asset Type component into usable values which can be used elsewhere.
messages.Tooltip.VideoTextureAssetMetadata.Textur: The video texture to get information on.
messages.Tooltip.VideoTextureAssetMetadata.Size: The size of the <mark=#ffffff10>Texture</mark> in pixels.
messages.Tooltip.VideoTextureProvider: The VideoTextureProvider component is used to display and play video. This component importantly implements both ITexture2D and IAudioSource which means it can be used as both a texture and a source of audio like a StaticAudioClip
messages.Tooltip.VideoTextureProvider.Playback: controls the playback of both the audio and visual elements of this component at the same time.
messages.Tooltip.VideoTextureProvider.URL: The website or file source of the video. Like a youtube video or a file on a machine.
messages.Tooltip.VideoTextureProvider.Stream: Whether this video should stream the data or cache it
messages.Tooltip.VideoTextureProvider.Volume: The volume of the video when being used as an IAudioSource in an audio player.
messages.Tooltip.VideoTextureProvider.ForcePlayba: The engine to use for playback like UnityPlaybackEngine or LibVLC.
messages.Tooltip.VideoTextureProvider.ForceVideoS: Whether to force parsing a streaming service like Twitch.
messages.Tooltip.VideoTextureProvider.VideoTitle: The field to populate with the name of the video that has been loaded.
messages.Tooltip.VideoTextureProvider.CurrentPlay: The current engine being used for playback
messages.Tooltip.VideoTextureProvider.CurrentCloc: The amount of seconds the player may have in error due to lag or otherwise.
messages.Tooltip.VideoTextureProvider.FilterMode: How to handle the interpolation between pixels.
messages.Tooltip.VideoTextureProvider.Anisotropic: The levels of Anisotropic filtering distances when using Anisotropic for <mark=#ffffff10>FilterMode</mark>
messages.Tooltip.VideoTextureProvider.AudioTrackI: the audio track that the video player should use to play
messages.Tooltip.VideoTextureProvider.PreferAudio: whether to parse the video source as audio only rather than video and audio. An example of an audio only is an MP3 file type.
messages.Tooltip.VideoTextureProvider.MaxWidth: The max pixels on the x axis that will be rendered when using this as a texture.
messages.Tooltip.VideoTextureProvider.MaxHeight: The max pixels on the y axis that will be rendered when using this as a texture.
messages.Tooltip.AssetLoader`1: AssetLoader is a Component that is used to tell the game that an Asset is still being used. This is helpful for when an asset is referenced in a way that deletes it during asset cleanup and saving.
messages.Tooltip.AudioExportable: Is used to make an item export an audio clip when a user tries to export the root object.
messages.Tooltip.AudioExportable.Audio: The audio clip to be exported.
messages.Tooltip.BinaryExportable: The BinaryExportable component takes in a Binary asset and allows the Slot to be exportable as a binary file on your device.
messages.Tooltip.ExportDialog.ExportName: The name of the Export file.
messages.Tooltip.ExportDialog._targetFolder: The target folder location to Export to.
messages.Tooltip.ExportDialog.EditEnabled: Whether this component is edit enabled.
messages.Tooltip.ExportDialog._cancel: The button for canceling the export.
messages.Tooltip.ExportDialog._export: The button to start the export.
messages.Tooltip.ExportDialog._exportOptions: The kinds of Export options available.
messages.Tooltip.ExportDialog.FileName: The name of the file when exporting.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatExportable: The Gaussian Splat Exportable component allows for making a slot give an option to export as a Gaussian Splat.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatExportable.Splat: The splat to add as an option for export.
messages.Tooltip.ModelExportable: The ModelExportable component is used to mark a slot hierarchy as a model that can be exported to a user's local machine. This will export static and skinned meshes, but is subject to the same restrictions normal model exporting has.
messages.Tooltip.ModelExportable.Root: The slot to treat as a model that can be exported.
messages.Tooltip.ModelExportable.OnlyComponents: What components should only be exported, rather than all mesh data components in the hiearchy. If this list is blank, it will not have any affect.
messages.Tooltip.PackageExportable: The PackageExportable component takes in any Slot hierarchy as a field, and when the slot that has this exportable component on it gets exported, it will actually save a file on your device of the target slot hierarchy provided (and not the item that your exporting from unless it is also under that hierarchy).
messages.Tooltip.PackageExportable.Root: The slot hierarchy to be exported.
messages.Tooltip.TextExportable: The TextExportable component allows the user to take a Slot and export it as a text file on their device.
messages.Tooltip.TextExportable.TextSource: Anything that is a string, such as textfields, contents, etc.
messages.Tooltip.TextExportable.Description: The description of what this text export is. Will be shown during the export process.
messages.Tooltip.TextureExportable: The TextureExportable allows you to make a StaticTexture2D exportable through the files page in the dash menu.
messages.Tooltip.VideoExportable: Using this will allow you to export videos.
messages.Tooltip.VideoExportable.Video: The video asset to be exported.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeExportable: The VolumeExportable component is used to export 3D textures (or 2D images that a layered on top of each other) as a .cube file format.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionAnimationBodyCollider: The LocomotionAnimationBodyCollider component is required for any colliders to be considered for self-collision for the hands.
Whether this collider is ignored by the player's left hand during locomotion.
Whether this collider is ignored by the player's right hand during locomotion.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionAnimationColliderBlock: This component can be used in combination with the LocomotionAnimationBodyCollider component to control which colliders on an avatar should be avoided by the avatar's hands.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionAnimationConfiguration: The LocomotionAnimationConfiguration component is used to customize the user's locomotion animations using a list of gaits and a global settings menu as well.
The settings to use for all gaits regardless of user speed.
The list of gaits to use at different speeds of user movement.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionAnimationFeetMetrics: The LocomotionAnimationFeetMetrics component is used to adjust the settings for an avatar's feet.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionAnimationFeetMetrics.S: What transform space the metric values are in for this component.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionAnimationFeetMetrics.F: How far apart the feet should be placed from each other.
How much the foot should be shifted forwards or backwards.
The distance from the floor to the foot bone for the whole foot.
An optional value to influence the foot front distance from floor value.
An optional value to influence the foot back distance from floor value.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionAnimationHandMetrics: The LocomotionAnimationHandMetrics component can be used to adjust the settings on the hands for an avatar for its rest state.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionAnimationHandMetrics.S: What transform space the metric values are in for this component.
An optional value to specify how apart the shoulders are in local space meters.
An optional value to specify how far the shoulders are from the ground in local space meters.
The offset of the shoulders forwards or backwards in meters.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionAnimationHandMetrics.H: An optional value to specify how far the hands should be offseted from the avatar center in local space meters.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionAnimationHandMetrics.A: An optional value to specify how long the arms are in local space meters.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionAnimationHandMetrics.H: An optional value to specify how long the palms are in local space meters.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionAnimationSuppressor: Used to disable the walking animations if placed under an avatar.
messages.Tooltip.AudioClipPlayer: Add an audio clip to the Clip field by holding a default audio clip player and clicking.
messages.Tooltip.AudioClipPlayerBase.playback: The playback object that includes data about playtime, duration, position, and playspeed of the audio.
messages.Tooltip.AudioClipPlayer.Clip: The AudioClip to play. This can be a StaticAudioClip.
messages.Tooltip.AudioDeviceIndexFinder: The AudioDeviceIndexFinder component only works in user space, and is used to find an audio device by name.
messages.Tooltip.AudioDeviceIndexFinder.DeviceNam: the name to use when looking for a device
messages.Tooltip.AudioDeviceIndexFinder.CaseSensi: Whether the matching with <mark=#ffffff10>DeviceName</mark> is case sensitive
messages.Tooltip.AudioDeviceIndexFinder.AllowPart: Whether the matching with <mark=#ffffff10>DeviceName</mark> allows a partial match.
messages.Tooltip.AudioFilterBlendWrapper: The Audio Filter Blend Wrapper component is used to wrap other filters and provides blending functionality. This is useful for audio reverb zones, allowing each listener with its own blend weight to share the same instance of AudioZitaReverb (and potentially other filters in the future)
messages.Tooltip.AudioFilterBlendWrapper.BlendWei: The amount of weight this filter has with other filters.
messages.Tooltip.AudioInputDeviceInfo: The AudioDeviceIndexFinder component only works in user space, and is used to find an audio device by name and get info about it.
messages.Tooltip.AudioInputDeviceInfo.DeviceName: the name to use when looking for a device
messages.Tooltip.AudioInputDeviceInfo.IsAppDefaul: Whether this device is resonite's current default
messages.Tooltip.AudioInputDeviceInfo.IsSystemDef: Whether this device is the user's OS current default
messages.Tooltip.AudioInputDeviceInfo.DeviceType: The type of audio device this is.
messages.Tooltip.AudioListener: The Audio Listener component can hear audio from the world around it and relay it back. It can also be used as a source for audio data for AudioOutput, working as a virtual microphone (This only works on listeners that aren't bound to specific user - ActiveUser must be null)
messages.Tooltip.AudioListener.ActiveUser: Position the audio source to this user.
messages.Tooltip.AudioListener.Effects: The list of affects to apply to the audio this recieves.
messages.Tooltip.AudioOutput: Audio Output is a component that is used to output sound from any kind of IAudioSource. This includes but is not limited to: Audio streams, audio clip players, and voices.
messages.Tooltip.AudioOutput.Volume: The volume to play the clip at, from 0 to 1.
messages.Tooltip.AudioOutput.Source: The source of audio. Can be an AudioClipPlayer, LerpingMultiClipPlayer, or MultiAudioClipPlayer.
messages.Tooltip.AudioOutput.Spatialize: Enables or disables rather it's 3D or 2D.
messages.Tooltip.AudioOutput.DopplerLevel: Simulates audio distortion when you or the object is moving.
messages.Tooltip.AudioOutput.RolloffMode: Switches between logarithmic and Linear audio falloff.
messages.Tooltip.AudioOutput.MinDistance: Minimum distance you need to be from the source to hear the audio.
messages.Tooltip.AudioOutput.MaxDistance: Maximum distance from the source until you no longer hear the audio.
messages.Tooltip.AudioOutput.AudioTypeGroup: Changes what track of Audio the source should be. SoundEffects, Multimedia, Voice, User Interface.
messages.Tooltip.AudioOutput.DistanceSpace: Chooses rather the audio should use it's local scale or it's global scale.
messages.Tooltip.AudioOutput.excludedUsers: User references placed in here will be excluded from hearing the audio.
messages.Tooltip.AudioOutputDeviceInfo: The AudioOutputDeviceInfo component only works in user space, and is used to find an audio device by name and get info about it.
messages.Tooltip.AudioOutputDeviceInfo.DeviceName: the name to use when looking for a device
messages.Tooltip.AudioZitaReverb: The Audio Zita Reverb component can be used to create echo effects (called ZitaParameters) onto audio clips, or on listeners in an area using the Awwdio system. To see how to use it with audio listeners, see AudioListener.
messages.Tooltip.AudioZitaReverb.InDelay: Delay in ms before reverberation begins.
messages.Tooltip.AudioZitaReverb.Crossover: Crossover frequency separating low and middle frequencies (Hz).
messages.Tooltip.AudioZitaReverb.RT60Low: Time (in seconds) to decay 60db in low-frequency band.
messages.Tooltip.AudioZitaReverb.RT60Mid: Time (in seconds) to decay 60db in mid-frequency band.
messages.Tooltip.AudioZitaReverb.HighFrequencyDam: Frequency (Hz) at which the high-frequency T60 is half the middle-band's T60.
messages.Tooltip.AudioZitaReverb.EQ1Frequency: Center frequency of second-order Regalia Mitra peaking equalizer section 1.
messages.Tooltip.AudioZitaReverb.EQ1Level: Peak level in dB of second-order Regalia-Mitra peaking equalizer section 1
messages.Tooltip.AudioZitaReverb.EQ2Frequency: Center frequency of second-order Regalia Mitra peaking equalizer section 2.
messages.Tooltip.AudioZitaReverb.EQ2Level: Peak level in dB of second-order Regalia-Mitra peaking equalizer section 2.
messages.Tooltip.AudioZitaReverb.Mix: 0 is all dry, 1 is all wet.
messages.Tooltip.AudioZitaReverb.LegacyRangeMappe: The Legacy Range Mapper component is used in old legacy content from before the Awwdio system.
The field with the legacy min distance.
The field with the legacy max distance.
The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>MinDistance</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>MaxDistance</mark> converted.
The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>MinDistance</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>MaxDistance</mark> converted.
messages.Tooltip.ChannelVolumeMeter: Channel Volume meter is a component that is used to read the volume of an audio source with multiple channels. The amount of channels can vary from 1, 2, and even to beyond 4. Usually this component is used to split the Left and Right audio volumes of an .OGG clip.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeMeterBase.Smoothing: How much to smooth the value changes of the outputs in <mark=#ffffff10>ChannelVolumes</mark>
messages.Tooltip.VolumeMeterBase.Power: Raise <mark=#ffffff10>ChannelVolumes</mark> to this exponent. 1 will leave <mark=#ffffff10>ChannelVolumes</mark> unchanged.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeMeterBase.Method: Whether the outputted value should be the current volume, or the change in volume over time (delta)
messages.Tooltip.VolumeMeterBase.Source: The audio source to read the volume of (as long as it's streaming or playing).
messages.Tooltip.ChannelVolumeMeter.ChannelVolume: The output volumes of each channel <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> has.
messages.Tooltip.ChannelVolumeMeter.DoNotRemoveEx: Don't remove excess fields whenever a new audio clip is inserted and the fields are outside the amount of channels the audio clip has.
messages.Tooltip.ChildParentAudioClipPlayer: The ChildParentAudioClipPlayer component plays specified audio clips when it's immediate children are added to or removed from.
messages.Tooltip.RandomAudioClipPlayerBase.Parent: Where to parent the slots to when playing sounds. (DON'T SET THIS TO THE SLOT THIS COMPONENT IS ON. else it will spam audio every game tick)
messages.Tooltip.RandomAudioClipPlayerBase.MinDis: the distance in meters in the audio's set transform space where the audio's perceived volume stops increasing and stays constant all the way to the center.
messages.Tooltip.RandomAudioClipPlayerBase.MaxDis: the maximum distance in meters in the audio's set transform space in meters before it cannot be heard.
messages.Tooltip.RandomAudioClipPlayerBase.Rollof: The override for all ClipDatas Rolloff Mode when they are played.
messages.Tooltip.ChildParentAudioClipPlayer.PlayP: Where to position audio clips when they are played.
messages.Tooltip.ChildParentAudioClipPlayer.Child: If the immediate children count of this component's slot is above this amount, it will not play sounds.
messages.Tooltip.ChildParentAudioClipPlayer.Filte: If an added or removed immediate child has a tag that matches this value, a sound will not be played.
messages.Tooltip.ChildParentAudioClipPlayer.Paren: A list of audio clips to play when a slot is added to the immediate children of this component's slot.
messages.Tooltip.ChildParentAudioClipPlayer.Unpar: A list of audio clips to play when a slot is removed from the immediate children of this component's slot.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbableUserVoiceModifier: Frooxius commented on May 27, 2020 Hmm, this component is technically deprecated with the voice switching system and it will have to be redesigned. It works by modifying the voice AudioOutput on the user, but with the introduction of the voice controls those now control the user's voice.
The field representing the SpatialBlend value field.
The field representing the Spatialize value field.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAudioOutputIgnoreReverbAda: The Legacy Audio Output Ignore Reverb Adapter component converts driven data from the old audio system to Awwdio to preserve legacy behavior.
The field that determined if the audio clip was spatialized in the old system.
messages.Tooltip.LerpingMultiClipPlayer: The LerpingMultiClipPlayer component is an IAudioSource type that transitions between different audio clips (tracks) by lerping the volume and speed from one audio to the other using Lerp.
messages.Tooltip.LerpingMultiClipPlayer.Lerp: The reference value to use to lerp between different tracks with different values. This does not have to be 0-1, and can be any value.
messages.Tooltip.LerpingMultiClipPlayer.Tracks: A list of track's to lerp between using <mark=#ffffff10>Lerp</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.LocalAudioDeviceStream: The LocalAudioDeviceStream component can be used as a source of streamed audio data kind of like a player or a video for use with audio output components. This component Outputs locally the audio for a specified audio input Device by index number.
messages.Tooltip.LocalAudioDeviceStream.AudioDevi: The audio device to gather audio samples from.
messages.Tooltip.LocalAudioDeviceStream.UseFilter: Whether to use raw audio samples or filter them.
messages.Tooltip.MultiAudioClipPlayer: TODO: instead of being written by me with 2 brain cells of math this should be rewritten with actual math terms.
messages.Tooltip.MultiAudioClipPlayer.Tracks: the different tracks this Multi Audio Clip is playing.
messages.Tooltip.RandomAudioClipPlayer: The RandomAudioClipPlayer component is able to play a random audio file all the way through from a list of Clips and parent the resulting one shots under a slot.
messages.Tooltip.RandomAudioClipPlayer.Clips: The list of ClipData to choose from, randomly with weights.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAudioRolloffCurveAdapter: The Legacy Audio Rolloff Curve Adapter component is used to convert driven values from the old audio system to Awwdio.
messages.Tooltip.VisemeAnalyzer: The VisemeAnalyzer can be used to analyze the sounds from a user's voice, a video, an audio clip player, or any other sound source. The component does analyzing of audio to discern Viseme sounds.
messages.Tooltip.VisemeAnalyzer.Source: The audio source to analyze the audio and extract live viseme data for.
messages.Tooltip.VisemeAnalyzer.RemoteSource: An alternative source of raw data for viseme data.
messages.Tooltip.VisemeAnalyzer.Smoothing: How much to smooth lerp all the outputs.
messages.Tooltip.VisemeAnalyzer.Silence: How quiet the audio is in general.
messages.Tooltip.VisemeAnalyzer.PP: Outputs the amount that the source audio is making a "Puh" sound.
messages.Tooltip.VisemeAnalyzer.FF: Outputs the amount that the source audio is making a "efff" sound.
messages.Tooltip.VisemeAnalyzer.TH: Outputs the amount that the source audio is making a "thuh" sound.
messages.Tooltip.VisemeAnalyzer.DD: Outputs the amount that the source audio is making a "deh" or "duh" sound.
messages.Tooltip.VisemeAnalyzer.kk: Outputs the amount that the source audio is making a "kay" sound.
messages.Tooltip.VisemeAnalyzer.CH: Outputs the amount that the source audio is making a "cuh" sound.
messages.Tooltip.VisemeAnalyzer.SS: Outputs the amount that the source audio is making a "ess" sound.
messages.Tooltip.VisemeAnalyzer.nn: Outputs the amount that the source audio is making a "enm" sound.
messages.Tooltip.VisemeAnalyzer.RR: Outputs the amount that the source audio is making a "erh" sound.
messages.Tooltip.VisemeAnalyzer.aa: Outputs the amount that the source audio is making a "ah" sound.
messages.Tooltip.VisemeAnalyzer.E: Outputs the amount that the source audio is making a "eee" sound.
messages.Tooltip.VisemeAnalyzer.ih: Outputs the amount that the source audio is making a "eh" sound.
messages.Tooltip.VisemeAnalyzer.oh: Outputs the amount that the source audio is making a "oah" sound.
messages.Tooltip.VisemeAnalyzer.ou: Outputs the amount that the source audio is making a "ooh" sound.
messages.Tooltip.VisemeAnalyzer.LaughterProbabili: How likely it is that the audio source is playing laughter sounds.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeMeter: The VolumeMeter component can be used to get the volume of an inputted audio source.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeMeter.Volume: The current volume of <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> raised to the exponent <mark=#ffffff10>Power</mark>, after smoothing factor of <mark=#ffffff10>Smoothing</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ViolentAprilFoolsExplosion: The ViolentAprilFoolsExplosion component, when deleted, causes an explosion animation to be played on any mesh below it in the hierarchy.
messages.Tooltip.ViolentAprilFoolsExplosion.Bloat: Intensity of the bloat, prior to explosion
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera: The DroneCamera is a component commonly used in 3rd person free cam desktop. It can also be influenced via Command Line Arguments to change it's behavior.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.CameraUser: The user that should be simulating this camera.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.SimulateOnHost: Whether to simulate the camera on the host.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.ManualControl: Whether the user is manually controlling the camera.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.SlowSpeed: meters per second to move when going slow.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.Speed: Meters per second when going normal speed.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.FastSpeed: meters per second when holding shift.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.FieldOfViewSource: The field to use as the field of view value.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.FieldOfView: The field of view in absence of a value for <mark=#ffffff10>FieldOfViewSource</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.AspectRatioSource: The field to use as the aspect ratio source.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.AspectRatio: The aspect ratio in absence of a value for <mark=#ffffff10>AspectRatioSource</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.ControllerRejectDist: Distance limit for rejecting a controller.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.GroupSearchRadius: The radius to search for groups of users.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.BiggestGroupSearchIn: The time to wait before checking for the largest group of users in the world to track.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.HeadForwardPointDist: The distance from a user's head in a group to stay back from when the camera in front of them.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.HeadBackwardPointDis: The distance from a user's head in a group to stay back from when the camera is behind them.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.HeadUpPointDistance: The distance from a user's head in a group to stay back from when the camera is above them.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.HeadDownPointDistanc: The distance from a user's head in a group to stay back from when the camera is below them.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.HeightOffset: How far up or down to look from a target user's view point.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.CircleOffset: The offset from the circle around a user to position this component
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.DistanceOffset: The distance offset to have from a target.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.CircleSpeed: How fast to travel a circle around a target.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.PositionSpeed: The speed of this component's position change.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.LookSpeed: The look around speed of the camera.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.DistanceSpeed: How fast to close the distance in seconds to a target position when focusing on something or a UI.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.UserInfluenceSpeed: How fast to restore user influence.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.HeightAmplitude: The height Amplitude for a target height.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.DistanceAmplitude: The distance Amplitude for a target distance from a subject.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.CircleAmplitude: The circle Amplitude for a circle distance.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.HeightPeriod: The period in which to change height.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.DistancePeriod: The period in which to change distance.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.CirclePeriod: The period in which to change circle distance.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.CirclePeriodNoiseSpe: The speed of noise influence on the circle period attribute.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.CirclePeriodNoiseInf: The influence of noise on the circle period attribute.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.CheckOcclusion: Whether to check if a subject has parts of them occluded.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.AdjustHeightOnOcclus: Whether to adjust camera height when the target is occluded.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.TeleportWaitTime: How long to wait before teleporting to a target position.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.TeleportTriggerRelat: How far of a distance is needed to teleport to a target position.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.TeleportTriggerAngle: How big of an angle needed to teleport to a target rotation.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.MinRandomizeFovInter: The minimum time to wait before randomly changing the camera FOV.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.MaxRandomizeFovInter: The maximum time to wait before randomly changing the camera FOV.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.MinFov: The minimum FOV this camera can randomly change to.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.MaxFov: The maximum FOV this camera can randomly change to.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.MinChangeFovTime: The minimum time it takes for the FOV on this camera to change randomly.
messages.Tooltip.DroneCamera.MaxChangeFovTime: The maximum time it takes for the FOV on this camera to change randomly.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationsList: Account Migrations List is a component that only works in user space. It checks the current Migrations being ran by the cloud and gives a running count every game update. It shows how many there are total including counts of different statuses the different migrations have.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationsList.TotalMigra: How many total migrations are currently happening on the cloud right now for the current user.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationsList.WaitingMig: How many migrations are currently waiting to start on the cloud right now for the current user.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationsList.RunningMig: How many migrations are currently happening on the cloud right now for the current user.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationsList.CompletedM: How many completed migrations have happened on the cloud before for the current user.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationsList.MigrationT: The task ID's of all the migrations for the current user.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus: This component is able to take a taskID of a migration on the cloud, and is able to display the information about said Migration. This info is grabbed from a AccountMigrationTask object internally which is returned by the SkyFrost API.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus.TaskId: A UUID of a task that has been done, waiting, or is being done on the SkyFrost cloud.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus.Exists: Whether <mark=#ffffff10>TaskId</mark> is a valid ID for a task on the cloud
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus.Name: The name of the found migration task.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus.Descripti: The description of the task. Usually starts with "Account Migration" or "Favorites migration"
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus.State: Also known as "Status", this will show whether the task has started, and if it's done or currently running.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus.Estimated: An estimate of the migration task's position on the list of migrations the SkyFrost servers are currently doing. This number can be high if a huge influx of migrations is happening at the current time.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus.CreatedOn: When the migration task was submitted to the list of migrations to be done on the cloud
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus.StartedOn: When the cloud started to migrate the items for this task.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus.Completed: When the Cloud completed this migration task.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus.RecordsPe: A calculation average of how fast the SkyFrost cloud is transfering items from the source platform to Resonite.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus.Currently: What type of thing the cloud is currently migrating?
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus.CurrentIt: What item the cloud is currently migrating for this task.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus.TotalReco: How many records this migration task is to migrate in total.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus.TotalMigr: How many records this migration has successfully migrated so far.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus.TotalFail: How many records this migration failed to migrate.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus.TotalMigr: How many Cloud Variables that the user has created themselves that this migration task has to migrate in total.
How many Cloud Variable Definitions this migration task has to migrate in total.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus.TotalCont: How many friend connections that this migration task has to migrate in total.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus.MigratedC: How many friend connections that this migration task has migrated so far.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus.MigratedM: How many messages sent by the user that this migration task has migrated so far.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus.TotalGrou: How many groups that the user has created that this migration task has to migrate in total.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus.MigratedG: How many groups that the user has created that the migration task has migrated so far.
messages.Tooltip.AccountMigrationStatus.TotalMigr: How many member names of groups that the user has created has been migrated by the migration task.
messages.Tooltip.CloudServerStatus: The CloudServerStatus component gets live data about the Resonite cloud by getting the ping data the game engine is sending and receiving from the cloud.
messages.Tooltip.CloudServerStatus.ResponseTimeMi: How long in milliseconds it took for the server to respond to the last ping.
messages.Tooltip.CloudServerStatus.LastServerUpda: The last time the server updated
messages.Tooltip.CloudServerStatus.LastServerStat: The last time the server status was fetched.
messages.Tooltip.CloudServerStatus.LastLocalServe: The last time the client got a response from the server.
messages.Tooltip.CloudUserInfo: The CloudUserInfo component can be used to retrieve information about a user from the Resonite cloud, such as their name or profile picture, based on their user ID.
messages.Tooltip.CloudUserInfo.UserId: The ID of the user that this component should display information about
messages.Tooltip.CloudUserInfo.IsLoaded: Whether or not the given user's information has been loaded
messages.Tooltip.CloudUserInfo.Username: The name of the given user
messages.Tooltip.CloudUserInfo.RegistrationDate: The time that the given user registered their account
messages.Tooltip.CloudUserInfo.OriginalRegistrati: The time that the given user registered their account which they migrated from.
messages.Tooltip.CloudUserInfo.IconURL: A URL, pointing to the user's profile picture
messages.Tooltip.CloudUserInfo.IsContact: Whether or not this user is one of your contacts (only works in userspace)
messages.Tooltip.CloudUserInfo._loadedUserId: The user ID corresponding to the information this component is currently outputting. This is an internal value and shouldn't be edited manually.
messages.Tooltip.FundingStatistics: The Funding Statistics component is used to get the different funding values for Resonite in United States Dollars from supporters.
messages.Tooltip.FundingStatistics.Timestamp: The last time the values were updated as a time stamp.
messages.Tooltip.FundingStatistics.Aggregate: The combination of all support channels.
messages.Tooltip.FundingStatistics.Patreon: The Monetary support from Patreon members.
messages.Tooltip.FundingStatistics.Stripe: The Monetary support from Stripe members.
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics: The OnlineStatistics component shows information about current session numbers and the number of users in them.
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.Timestamp: The last time the component got the online statistics successfully.
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.ActiveVisibleSe: Statistics of visible sessions with users active in them.
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.ActiveHiddenSes: Statistics of hidden sessions with users active in them.
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.RegisteredOnlin: How many users with a Resonite account are online
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.TotalOnlineUser: How many users are online in total
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.PresentUsers: How many users are at their keyboard or in headset.
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.AwayUsers: How many users have their status set to away?
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.UsersInVR: How many users are in VR?
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.UsersInScreen: How many users are in screen mode?
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.UsersOnDesktop: How many users are on desktop?
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.UsersOnMobile: How many users are using the mobile binary of the game?
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.UsersInVisibleP: How many users are in visible sessions?
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.UsersInVisibleS: How many users are in semi accessible sessions like contacts or contacts plus?
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.UsersInHiddenSe: How many users are in hidden sessions
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.UsersInPrivateS: How many users are in private sessions?
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.UsersInPrivate: How many users are in private?
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.UsersInLAN: How many users are in Local Access Network sessions?
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.UsersInContacts: How many users are in contacts sessions?
How many users are in contacts plus sessions?
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.UsersInRegister: How many users are in sessions that require a logged in user? (Not nessarily resonite account because SAML)
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.UsersInPublic: How many users are currently online in public sessions
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.GraphicalClient: How many users are not headlesses
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.ChatClientUsers: How many users are using chat clients? (Like recon)
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.HeadlessUsers: How many of the online users are Headless Accounts
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.BotUsers: How many users are bot accounts (occasionally 1 when the resonite bot is online)
messages.Tooltip.RecordSyncStatus: The RecordSyncStatus component reports the current status of syncing items like worlds, avatars, settings, and user generated content when present in the user space world where the dash is.
messages.Tooltip.RecordSyncStatus.RecordQueueCoun: How many items are left in the queue of items to Sync from the user's machine to the cloud.
messages.Tooltip.RecordSyncStatus.AssetVariantQue: How many asset variants are left to upload for the items being uploaded to the cloud.
messages.Tooltip.RecordSyncStatus.CurrentTaskProg: How far along the Asset uploader is to uploading the current item.
messages.Tooltip.RecordSyncStatus.LastError: The last error given by the cloud item uploader process.
messages.Tooltip.RecordSyncStatus.StatusMessage: The status message for the item uploader process.
messages.Tooltip.RecordSyncStatus.FullySyncedColo: The color the UI should be if it is fully done syncing.
messages.Tooltip.RecordSyncStatus.ErrorColor: The color the UI should be if there was an error (UH OH!!! Stuck Sync!!!)
messages.Tooltip.RecordSyncStatus.SyncingRecordsC: The color the UI should be when the item uploader task is syncing Record items.
messages.Tooltip.RecordSyncStatus.UploadingAssetV: The color the UI should be when the item uploader is uploading Asset variants for things like textures or skyboxes.
messages.Tooltip.StorageUsageStatus: Storage Usage Status is a component that is used to read how much storage a user has used, total, shared, and that has left to share.
messages.Tooltip.StorageUsageStatus.OwnerId: The id of the user this is reading data from
messages.Tooltip.StorageUsageStatus.GroupMemberQu: Whether to read group storage limits.
messages.Tooltip.StorageUsageStatus.HasValidData: Whether this component has successfully grabbed the data.
messages.Tooltip.StorageUsageStatus.StorageBytes: how many bytes of storage <mark=#ffffff10>OwnerId</mark> has total
messages.Tooltip.StorageUsageStatus.FullStorageBy: how many bytes of storage <mark=#ffffff10>OwnerId</mark> has used currently.
messages.Tooltip.StorageUsageStatus.ShareableStor: How many bytes of storage <mark=#ffffff10>OwnerId</mark> currently has ready to share.
messages.Tooltip.StorageUsageStatus.SharedStorage: How many bytes of storage <mark=#ffffff10>OwnerId</mark> has shared with others currently.
messages.Tooltip.StorageUsageStatus.UsageBytes: The total amount of bytes being used by <mark=#ffffff10>OwnerId</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.StorageUsageStatus.UsageRatio: the percentage of how full <mark=#ffffff10>OwnerId</mark>'s storage is from 0->1
messages.Tooltip.StorageUsageStatus.StorageString: String version with nice formatting applied of how much storage <mark=#ffffff10>OwnerId</mark> has.
messages.Tooltip.StorageUsageStatus.FullStorageSt: String version with nice formatting applied of
messages.Tooltip.StorageUsageStatus.ShareableStor: String version with nice formatting applied of how much storage <mark=#ffffff10>OwnerId</mark> has ready to share.
messages.Tooltip.StorageUsageStatus.SharedStorage: String version with nice formatting applied of how much storage <mark=#ffffff10>OwnerId</mark> has shared with others.
messages.Tooltip.StorageUsageStatus.UsageString: String version with nice formatting applied of how much storage <mark=#ffffff10>OwnerId</mark> has used.
messages.Tooltip.StorageUsageStatus.RatioString: String version with nice formatting applied of how full <mark=#ffffff10>OwnerId</mark>'s storage is.
messages.Tooltip.SupporterCreditList: The SupporterCreditList component is used to get a list of supporters currently supporting the game financially.
messages.Tooltip.SupporterCreditList.CreditType: What kind of supporter level/category to get names from.
messages.Tooltip.SupporterCreditList.Separator: How to separate the list of names.
messages.Tooltip.SupporterCreditList.Prefix: What to put at the beginning of each name (Usually a color tag)
messages.Tooltip.SupporterCreditList.Suffix: What to put at the end of each name (Usually a color tag ender.
messages.Tooltip.UniverseStatus: The UniverseStatus component provides information on the current universe.
messages.Tooltip.UniverseStatus.UniverseId: The ID of the universe current Universe.
messages.Tooltip.UniverseStatus.PrimaryGroupId: The ID of the primary group of the universe.
messages.Tooltip.UniverseStatus.InUniverse: Whether we are in a universe or not.
messages.Tooltip.UserLoginStatus: The UserLoginStatus component gets the current login status of the local user.
messages.Tooltip.UserLoginStatus.IsLoggedIn: Whether the local user is logged in.
messages.Tooltip.UserLoginStatus.LoggedUserId: The userID of the local user if they are logged in.
messages.Tooltip.UserLoginStatus.LoggedUsername: The username of the local user if they are logged in.
messages.Tooltip.UserOnlineStatusSync: Attach to a slot that will be in userspace (like a facet) and bring it into userspace in order for it to begin working.
messages.Tooltip.UserOnlineStatusSync.OnlineStatu: The online status of the local user.
messages.Tooltip.UserLoginManager: The UserLoginManager component is used to handle the user's login status.
messages.Tooltip.UserLoginManager.IsLoggedIn: Whether the current user is logged in or not.
messages.Tooltip.UserLoginManager.IsLoggingOut: Whether the local user is currently logging out or not.
messages.Tooltip.UserLoginManager.CurrentUsername: The username of the local user.
messages.Tooltip.UserLoginManager.CurrentUserId: The user ID of the local user.
messages.Tooltip.UserLoginManager.CurrentAccountT: The account type of the local user (patreon status or team member likewise)
messages.Tooltip.UserLoginManager.CurrentAccountC: The current user color override of the local user. Like nametag colors.
messages.Tooltip.UserLoginManager.CurrentProfileI: The user icon of the local user.
messages.Tooltip.UserLoginManager.LoginLogoutButt: Only works in userspace. Is the button for logging in or out.
messages.Tooltip.ActiveUserCloudField`1: The ActiveUserCloudField Component binds to the field specified by Target. If IsLinkedToCloud is true, it will write the value of the cloud variable defined by Path for the active user of the slot it is attached to.
messages.Tooltip.CloudVariableBase.Path: The  path of the variable this component will read, for the current active user
messages.Tooltip.CloudValueBase`1.IsLinkedToCloud: (Read Only)) Indicates whether this field was successfully bound to the target variable.
messages.Tooltip.ActiveUserCloudValueBase`1.Updat: Indicates that this variable should update the value in the cloud when it is written or driven to a different value.
messages.Tooltip.ActiveUserCloudValueBase`1.Fallb: Default value if there is no current active user. If the active user's value isn't set, the definition default will be used instead.
messages.Tooltip.ActiveUserCloudField`1.Target: The target  field to which the variable value will be written.
messages.Tooltip.ActiveUserCloudValueVariable`1: To use this component, simply add it to a slot underneath a user's hierarchy and set the Path to the path of a cloud variable.
messages.Tooltip.ActiveUserCloudValueVariable`1.V: The value from the cloud variable.
messages.Tooltip.CloudValueField`1: Put this component on a slot, and give it a valid cloud variable Path and VariableOwnerId.
messages.Tooltip.CloudValueOwnerBase`1.VariableOw: The UserID of the user that made a definition for the variable specified by <mark=#ffffff10>Path</mark>
messages.Tooltip.CloudValueField`1.Target: The target  field to which the variable value will be written.
messages.Tooltip.CloudValueVariable`1: Upon being given a valid cloud variable Path and VariableOwnerId, the value in the Value field will be updated to reflect the value of the cloud variable.
messages.Tooltip.CloudValueVariable`1.Value: The value of the read cloud variable.
messages.Tooltip.CloudValueVariableDriver`1: Upon being given a valid cloud variable Path, the component will drive the value of the Target with the value of the cloud variable owned by the local user.
messages.Tooltip.CloudValueVariableDriver`1.Targe: The target  field to which the variable value will be written.
messages.Tooltip.CloudValueVariableDriver`1.IsLin: (Read Only) Indicates whether this field was successfully bound to the target variable.
messages.Tooltip.CloudValueVariableDriver`1.Write: Updates the value in the cloud when the driven field is changed. See write backs.
messages.Tooltip.CloudValueVariableDriver`1.Fallb: Default value if the local user does not have a definition for the cloud variable or the local user does not match <mark=#ffffff10>OverrideOwner</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.CloudValueVariableDriver`1.Overr: The UserID of the user that made a definition for the variable specified by <mark=#ffffff10>Path</mark> or the local user if not specified.
messages.Tooltip.Comment: Comment is a component that allows you to store text for another person using an inspector, ex: another content creator. This is useful for explaining what a slot is for or does.
messages.Tooltip.Comment.Text: The text to store for others to see when in an inspector
messages.Tooltip.ButtonActionTrigger: This component allows you to trigger actions on some special components, such as the Random Object Spawner component, by dragging the reference to the target action into a trigger slot (such as OnPressed).
messages.Tooltip.ButtonActionTrigger.OnPressed: Triggered when the button is pressed
messages.Tooltip.ButtonActionTrigger.OnPressing: Triggered when the button is pressed, before the <mark=#ffffff10>OnPressed</mark> event
messages.Tooltip.ButtonActionTrigger.OnReleased: Triggered when the button is released
messages.Tooltip.ButtonValueActionTrigger`1: The ButtonValueActionTrigger component receives any Button Event and uses it to trigger a Sync Delegate and sends a value to it.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonValueActionTrigger`1.OnPre: triggers while sending <mark=#ffffff10>Value</mark> when this component receives a Button Event of type OnPressed.
triggers while sending <mark=#ffffff10>Value</mark> when this component receives a Button Event of type OnPressing.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonValueActionTrigger`1.OnRel: triggers while sending <mark=#ffffff10>Value</mark> when this component receives a Button Event of type OnReleased.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonValueActionTrigger`1.Value: The value to send to any <mark=#ffffff10>OnPressed</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>OnPressing</mark>, and/or <mark=#ffffff10>OnReleased</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonDestroy: This can be used in projects that need things destroyed by a button press.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonDestroy.FindObjectRoot: When this is interacted, this component will take the target and go up in the hierarchy and look for the first object root slot, then destroys it.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonDynamicImpulseTrigger: Useful for sending dynamic pulses cleanly instead of using flux.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonDynamicImpulseTrigger.Targ: The slot hierarchy to send this impulse through. If this is null, it will pulse through root instead.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonDynamicImpulseTrigger.Excl: If true, this pulse will ignore disabled slots.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonDynamicImpulseTrigger.Pres: When the button is pressed, send a pulse for receivers looking for this tag.
When the button is pressing, send a pulse for receivers looking for this tag.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonDynamicImpulseTrigger.Rele: When the button is released, send a pulse for receivers looking for this tag.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonDynamicImpulseTrigger.Hove: When the button just began to hover, send a pulse for receivers looking for this tag.
When the button is hovering, send a pulse for receivers looking for this tag.
When the button has stopped hovering, send a pulse for receivers looking for this tag.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonDynamicImpulseTriggerWithR: Useful for sending dynamic pulses cleanly instead of using flux.

The slot to target with impulses specified by <mark=#ffffff10>PressedData</mark>,<mark=#ffffff10>PressingData</mark>,<mark=#ffffff10>ReleasedData</mark>,<mark=#ffffff10>HoverEnterData</mark>,<mark=#ffffff10>HoverStayData</mark>,<mark=#ffffff10>HoverLeaveData</mark>.
Whether disabled slots and hierarchies should be affected by the dynamic impulses sent.

What value and tag type dynamic impulse to send when an IButton starts being pointed at on the same slot.
What value and tag type dynamic impulse to send when an IButton is currently pointed at on the same slot. Fires every update while the button is being hovered over.
What value and tag type dynamic impulse to send when an IButton stops being pointed at on the same slot.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonDynamicImpulseTriggerWithV: Useful for sending dynamic pulses cleanly instead of using flux.

The slot to target with impulses specified by <mark=#ffffff10>PressedData</mark>,<mark=#ffffff10>PressingData</mark>,<mark=#ffffff10>ReleasedData</mark>,<mark=#ffffff10>HoverEnterData</mark>,<mark=#ffffff10>HoverStayData</mark>,<mark=#ffffff10>HoverLeaveData</mark>.
Whether disabled slots and hierarchies should be affected by the dynamic impulses sent.
What value and tag type dynamic impulse to send when an IButton is pressed on the same slot.
What value and tag type dynamic impulse to send when an IButton is being pressed on the same slot. Fires every update while the button is being pressed.
What value and tag type dynamic impulse to send when an IButton stops being pressed on the same slot.
What value and tag type dynamic impulse to send when an IButton starts being pointed at on the same slot.
What value and tag type dynamic impulse to send when an IButton is currently pointed at on the same slot. Fires every update while the button is being hovered over.
What value and tag type dynamic impulse to send when an IButton stops being pointed at on the same slot.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonEditColorX: This is used to edit colors with a press of a button.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonEditColorX.Continuous: Makes the color editing change continuous.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonEditColorX.Alpha: Allows for use of the alpha channel.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonEditColorX.HDR: Allows the use for HDR in this color.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonEnumShift`1: This is used to shift enums by button press.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonHoverEventRelay: Useful for sending multiple signals from one hover event.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonHoverEventRelay.Target: The slot to send the signal to.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonParentUnderUser: Useful if you want certain slots to be parented to you when a button is pressed.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonParentUnderUser.Root: The slot to parent to the user that pressed the button.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonParentUnderUser.FindObject: Looks upwards in the slot heirarchy to find the slot with the Object Root component, and then parents that  slot instead to the user.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonParentUnderUser.UnparentWh: Makes this component's parenting effects togglable.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonParentUnderUser.PreserveOr: Keep track of the original space.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonParentUnderUser._originalS: The slot that represents the original space.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonPressEventRelay: Useful for sending multiple signals from one press event.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonPressEventRelay.Target: The slot to send the signal to.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonReferenceCycle`1: Useful for needing a way to cycle through references of any type.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonReferenceCycle`1.TargetRef: The reference data to send outwards.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonReferenceCycle`1.Targets: The list of references to cycle through.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonReferenceSet`1: This is similar to the Write ProtoFlux node but as a component instead.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonReferenceSet`1.TargetRefer: The target to send the reference outwards.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonReferenceSet`1.SetReferenc: The reference to set when the button is pressed.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonStringAppend: To function, the component simply needs to be attached to a slot that also has a button component attached to it.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonStringAppend.TargetString: The string to which <mark=#ffffff10>AppendString</mark> will be added.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonStringAppend.AppendInFront: Whether or not to add <mark=#ffffff10>AppendString</mark> to the beginning of the string. (This does not reverse <mark=#ffffff10>AppendString</mark>.)
messages.Tooltip.ButtonStringErase: To function, the component simply needs to be attached to a slot that also has a button component attached to it.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonStringErase.TargetString: The string from which characters will be removed.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonStringErase.EraseFromBegin: Whether or not to remove characters from the beginning of the string.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonToggle: To function, the component simply needs to be attached to a slot that also has a button component attached to it.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonToggle.TargetValue: The boolean to invert whenever the button is pressed.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonValueCycle`1: Useful for needing a way to cycle through values of any type.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonValueCycle`1.TargetValue: The value data to send outwards.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonValueCycle`1.Values: The list of values to cycle through.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonValueSet`1: This is similar to the Write ProtoFlux node but as a component instead.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonValueSet`1.TargetValue: The target to send the value outwards.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonValueSet`1.SetValue: The value to set when the button is pressed.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonValueShift`1: To function, the component simply needs to be attached to a slot that also has a button component attached to it.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonValueShift`1.Delta: By how much <mark=#ffffff10>TargetValue</mark> should be shifted on each press.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonValueShift`1.Min: The minimum value that can be shifted to.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonValueShift`1.Max: The maximum value that can be shifted to.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonValueShift`1.WrapAround: Whether or not the value should wrap around to the other extreme when reaching either <mark=#ffffff10>Min</mark> or <mark=#ffffff10>Max</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonValueShift`1.MaxIsExclusiv: When the current amount is at the value before max, it will stop there, unless wrap around is enabled, which then wraps around from the max value - 1.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonWorldLink: This allows a user to make customizable world loading buttons, either as a way to go to another world or to pre-load a world.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonWorldLink.LastOpened: Shows the last opened time (not seen in the component).
messages.Tooltip.ButtonWorldLink.WorldLink: The WorldLink component that contains the world information.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonAudioClipPlayer: This is a powerful component that allows for customizability for your many sounds in one slot, great for boopers and UIX buttons that need to have a variety of sounds.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonAudioClipPlayer.PressedCli: The list of pressed clip sounds.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonAudioClipPlayer.ReleasedCl: The list of released clip sounds.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonAudioClipPlayer.HoverEnter: The list of hover enter clip sounds.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonAudioClipPlayer.HoverLeave: The list of hover leave clip sounds.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonLoopSet: Basically a loop button that can be setup anywhere.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonLoopSet.OnPress: Sets the type of loop for this media when pressed.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonLoopSet.OnRelease: Sets the type of loop for this media when released.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonPlaybackAction: Basically your media buttons (Play, Pause, Resume, and Stop) that can be setup anywhere.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonPlaybackAction.OnHover: The action to take when the button is hovered over.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonPlaybackAction.OnLeave: The action to take when the button is left.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonPlaybackAction.OnPress: The action to take when the button is pressed.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonPlaybackAction.OnRelease: The action to take when the button is released.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonPlaybackSeeker: This just gives extra functionality or options to control your media.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonPlaybackSeeker.Vertical: If true, the button will interpret the seek bar as vertical instead of horizontal.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonPlaybackSeeker.Continuous: If true, allows the user to hold down the laser on the seek bar button and have it constantly change as the laser moves around.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonAudioDeviceSet: The ButtonAudioDeviceSet component sets the audio device a user is using, and can only be used in Userspace, as switching audio devices is a Settings thing. (TODO: double check this)
messages.Tooltip.ButtonClipboardCopyText: The ButtonClipboardCopyText component is triggered on Button Press and copies the text content of a field into the pressing user's clipboard.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonClipboardCopyText.Source: Specifies where the value is copied from.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonEquipFavoriteAvatar: Attach to a button, when pressed while in Userspace, your current favorite avatar will be equipped.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonOpenHome: This is an easy way to set up a way to get to your favorite home faster.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonOpenHome.LastOpened: Shows when the world was last opened (not shown in the component).
messages.Tooltip.ButtonOpenHome.GroupOwnerId: An optional string input for a group home.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonUserProfileIconSet: Put this beside a button component on an slot or facet and press the button to set your profile icon via the button.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonUserProfileIconSet.IsUpdat: Whether or not the component is sending to the cloud your new icon url and/or asset.
messages.Tooltip.ConfirmationHandler: The ConfirmationHandler component is used to handle confirming an action. (what the actions are is currently not documented)
messages.Tooltip.ConfirmationHandler.Label: The message of the Confirmation box
messages.Tooltip.ConfirmationHandler.Color: The color of the button before confirming is allowed.
messages.Tooltip.ConfirmationHandler.OriginalLabe: The original label of the conformation before allowing the user to continue.
messages.Tooltip.ConfirmationHandler.OriginalColo: The original color of the Confirmation before allowing the user to continue.
messages.Tooltip.ConfirmationHandler.RevertTime: The time to allow the user to click the button.
messages.Tooltip.GenericUIContainer: There is no known usage for this component to be used in projects, but only seen on certain panels and canvases.
messages.Tooltip.GenericUIContainer.Title: Changes the title for this panel.
messages.Tooltip.GenericUIContainer.CloseRequest: Becomes true when this container (UIX panel/canvas) is closing.
messages.Tooltip.PagingControl: Great for counting items, and for controlling UI that can switch pages.
messages.Tooltip.PagingControl.ItemsPerPage: The increment to count up by.
messages.Tooltip.PagingControl.HasMoreItems: Allows to count beyond the max set of items.
messages.Tooltip.PagingControl.SkipItems: Skips items that are listed here.
messages.Tooltip.PagingControl.NoItemsLabel: Shows the output as this when there are no items.
messages.Tooltip.PagingControl.PagingInfoLabel: The structure of how the items will be shown to the user.
messages.Tooltip.PagingControl.RemainingItems: Shows how many items are remaining.
messages.Tooltip.PagingControl._previousEnabled: Becomes false if the minimum is currently reached.
messages.Tooltip.PagingControl._nextEnabled: Becomes false if the maximum is currently reached.
messages.Tooltip.Perspective360Panner: Useful as a way of looking around any 360 type of image.
messages.Tooltip.Perspective360Panner.IdleFOV: The field of view used when not looking around.
messages.Tooltip.Perspective360Panner.HoverFOV: The field of view used when looking around.
messages.Tooltip.Perspective360Panner.HorizontalP: How fast to pick up speed.
messages.Tooltip.Perspective360Panner.VerticalRan: The vertical range of this field of view.
messages.Tooltip.Perspective360Panner.AngleOffset: The field of view angle offset.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceOptionDescriptionDriver: The Reference Option Description Driver component is used primarily to drive the parts that make up a Context Menu Item Source. This component along with ContextMenuItemSource is used in context menus for toggles where the toggle controls a reference like a slot or object.
The reference field to compare against
Forces any context menu item source to change regardless if it is deselected.
The string to set to the chosen label in the list of <mark=#ffffff10>Options</mark>
The color to set to the chosen color in the list of <mark=#ffffff10>Options</mark>
The Sprite to set to the chosen Sprite in the list of <mark=#ffffff10>Options</mark>

The option to set <mark=#ffffff10>Label</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>Color</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>Sprite</mark> to when <mark=#ffffff10>Reference</mark> doesn't match any <mark=#ffffff10>ReferenceTarget</mark>s under the list of <mark=#ffffff10>Options</mark>

The list to search for a <mark=#ffffff10>ReferenceTarget</mark> that matches <mark=#ffffff10>Reference</mark>. once a match is found, the option's <mark=#ffffff10>Label</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>Color</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>Sprite</mark> is used to drive this section's drive targets of <mark=#ffffff10>Label</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>Color</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>Sprite</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ValueOptionDescriptionDriver`1: This component can be more efficient than using a bunch of Value Equality Drivers and generating a number based on such, or using ProtoFlux for a context menu.
messages.Tooltip.ValueOptionDescriptionDriver`1.V: The value field to compare against.
messages.Tooltip.ValueOptionDescriptionDriver`1.F: Forces any context menu item source to change regardless if it is deselected.
messages.Tooltip.ValueOptionDescriptionDriver`1.L: The string to set to the chosen label in the list of <mark=#ffffff10>Options</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ValueOptionDescriptionDriver`1.C: The color to set to the chosen color in the list of <mark=#ffffff10>Options</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ValueOptionDescriptionDriver`1.S: The Sprite to set to the chosen Sprite in the list of <mark=#ffffff10>Options</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ValueOptionDescriptionDriver`1.D: The option to set <mark=#ffffff10>Label</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>Color</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>Sprite</mark> to when <mark=#ffffff10>Reference</mark> doesn't match any <mark=#ffffff10>ReferenceTarget</mark>s under the list of <mark=#ffffff10>Options</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ValueOptionDescriptionDriver`1.O: The list to search for a <mark=#ffffff10>ReferenceTarget</mark> that matches <mark=#ffffff10>Value</mark>. once a match is found, the option's <mark=#ffffff10>Label</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>Color</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>Sprite</mark> is used to drive this section's drive targets of <mark=#ffffff10>Label</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>Color</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>Sprite</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ValueOptionDescriptionDriver`1.S: Tints the entire sprite with a ColorX.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleBoolDriver`1: The ConvertibleBoolDriver component is used to convert a value into a Bool using the value's convert to Bool method.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleBoolDriver`1.Target: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> converted into a Bool.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleCharDriver`1: The ConvertibleCharDriver component is used to convert a value into a Char using the value's convert to Char method.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleCharDriver`1.Target: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> converted into a Char.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleByteDriver`1: The ConvertibleByteDriver component is used to convert a value into a Byte using the value's convert to Byte method.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleByteDriver`1.Target: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> converted into a Byte.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleUshortDriver`1: The ConvertibleUshortDriver component is used to convert a value into a Ushort using the value's convert to Ushort method.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleUshortDriver`1.Target: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> converted into a Ushort.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleUintDriver`1: The ConvertibleUintDriver component is used to convert a value into a Uint using the value's convert to Uint method.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleUintDriver`1.Target: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> converted into a Uint.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleUlongDriver`1: The ConvertibleUlongDriver component is used to convert a value into a Ulong using the value's convert to Ulong method.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleUlongDriver`1.Target: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> converted into a Ulong.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleSbyteDriver`1: The ConvertibleSbyteDriver component is used to convert a value into a Sbyte using the value's convert to Sbyte method.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleSbyteDriver`1.Target: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> converted into a Sbyte.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleShortDriver`1: The ConvertibleShortDriver component is used to convert a value into a Short using the value's convert to Short method.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleShortDriver`1.Target: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> converted into a Short.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleIntDriver`1: The ConvertibleIntDriver component is used to convert a value into a Int using the value's convert to Int method.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleIntDriver`1.Target: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> converted into a Int.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleLongDriver`1: The ConvertibleLongDriver component is used to convert a value into a Long using the value's convert to Long method.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleLongDriver`1.Target: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> converted into a Long.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleFloatDriver`1: The ConvertibleFloatDriver component is used to convert a value into a Float using the value's convert to Float method.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleFloatDriver`1.Target: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> converted into a Float.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleDoubleDriver`1: The ConvertibleDoubleDriver component is used to convert a value into a Double using the value's convert to Double method.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleDoubleDriver`1.Target: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> converted into a Double.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleDecimalDriver`1: The ConvertibleDecimalDriver component is used to convert a value into a Decimal using the value's convert to Decimal method.
messages.Tooltip.ConvertibleDecimalDriver`1.Targe: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> converted into a Decimal.
messages.Tooltip.LocalDateTimeConvertor: The LocalDateTimeConvertor converts an inputted time to the user's local timezone.
messages.Tooltip.LocalDateTimeConvertor.Source: The source field holding the date time to convert.
messages.Tooltip.LocalDateTimeConvertor.LocalDate: The value of the field targeted by <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> converted to the local user's datetime.
messages.Tooltip.LocalNullableDateTimeConvertor: The NullableLocalDateTimeConvertor converts a nullable inputted time to the user's local timezone.
messages.Tooltip.LocalNullableDateTimeConvertor.S: The source field holding the date time to convert.
messages.Tooltip.LocalNullableDateTimeConvertor.L: The value of the field targeted by <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> converted to the local user's datetime.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicField`1: The DynamicField<T> binds the field pointed to by TargetField to the dynamic variable referred to by VariableName. The field must be a value type.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicVariableBase`1.VariableNa: The Variable name that will be used to link this component's referenced value to the Dynamic Variables System.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicField`1.TargetField: The field that will be used as the dynamic variable's value.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicField`1.OverrideOnLink: If true, the value of the field will be written when this component is moved into a new space
messages.Tooltip.DynamicReference`1: The DynamicReference component allows marking of any SyncRef`1 (Reference holder) as part of the Dynamic Variables System. The value inside of the specified SyncRef can change and will be changed by the Dynamic Variables System. The variable name and the type of variable will determine how this will link to and be changed by the Dynamic Variables System. For more info on how Dynamic Variables work please check the Dynamic Variables page.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicReference`1.TargetReferen: The field to become synced with and be the definition of the reference dynamic variable.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicReference`1.OverrideOnLin: whether this component should write its value to a Dynamic Variable Space Component when it links to the space, updating all child variables with the same name and type.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicReferenceVariable`1: A dynamic reference variable is a component that allows storing any reference Type inside the component. The reference is then accessible and changeable via the Dynamic Variables system.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicReferenceVariable`1.Refer: The reference this variable holds. Can set and be changed by the Dynamic Variables system.
