messages.Tooltip.DynamicReferenceVariable`1.Overr: whether this component should write its value to a Dynamic Variable Space Component when it links to the space, updating all child variables with the same name and type.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicReferenceVariableDriver`1: The DynamicReferenceVariableDriver component can drive a SyncRef`1 (Reference Holder) to the value assigned to a dynamic variable with the name VariableName. if the specified variable cannot be found, the value will revert to DefaultTarget how this works is explained on Dynamic Variables page.
The field to drive with the variable definition.
The value/reference to revert to when this component cannot find the specified <mark=#ffffff10>VariableName</mark> dynamic variable.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicReferenceVariableReset`1: Dynamic Reference Variable Reset is a component that will reset the value for a Dynamic Variable to a specified Reference. This is useful for when you are messing with an item, but you want the values for it to clear themselves with it is saved, loaded, or duplicated. The variable space is determined by the searching algorithm explained on Dynamic Variables page from the slot this is on.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicVariableResetBase`1.Varia: The Variable name that will be used to link this component's referenced value to the Dynamic Variables System.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicVariableResetBase`1.Reset: Reset when this component is loaded with a world.
Reset when the object this is under is duplicated.
Reset when the object this is under is pasted from clipboard.
The reference value to reset to when any of the enabled reset events occur.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicTypeField: The DynamicTypeField Component is used to mark a field containing a Type as the value of a dynamic variable.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicTypeField.TargetField: The field to stay up to date with and change the value of the dynamic variable's value.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicTypeField.OverrideOnLink: Whether to write the current value of the dynamic variable in a space when it connects to it with the one this component is storing.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicTypeVariable: The DynamicTypeVariable Component is used to define a Type as the value of a dynamic variable.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicTypeVariable.Value: The value of the dynamic variable that keeps in sync with the variable's valye.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicTypeVariable.OverrideOnLi: Whether to write the current value of the dynamic variable in a space when it connects to it with the one this component is storing.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicValueVariable`1: A dynamic value variable is a component that allows storing any value Type inside the component. The value is then accessible and changeable via the Dynamic Variables system.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicValueVariable`1.Value: The value this variable holds. Can set and be changed by the Dynamic Variables system.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicValueVariable`1.OverrideO: whether this component should write its value to a Dynamic Variable Space Component when it links to the space, updating all child variables with the same name and type.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicValueVariableDriver`1: Dynamic Value Variable Driver is a component that can drive a IField`1 (Value Holder) to the value assigned to a dynamic variable with the name VariableName. if the specified variable cannot be found, the value will revert to DefaultTarget how this works is explained on Dynamic Variables page.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicValueVariableDriver`1.Tar: The value/reference to revert to when this component cannot find the specified <mark=#ffffff10>VariableName</mark> dynamic variable.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicValueVariableDriver`1.Def: The value to revert to when one doesn't exist.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicValueVariableReset`1: Dynamic Value Variable Reset is a component that will reset the value for a Dynamic Variable to a specified Value. This is useful for when you are messing with an item, but you want the values for it to clear themselves with it is saved, loaded, or duplicated. The variable space is determined by the searching algorithm explained on Dynamic Variables page from the slot this is on.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicValueVariableReset`1.Rese: The value to reset to when any of the enabled reset events occur.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicVariableSpace: The DynamicVariableSpace component is used to separate dynamic variables into namespaces. Since dynamic variables are, by default, created on the slot of the nearest parent DynamicVariableSpace, this component provides control over where the root of that space is, as well as part of the path name to be referenced when using a consumer of dynamic variables, such as DynamicValueVariableDriver`1. For more info see Dynamic Variables.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicVariableSpace.OnlyDirectB: When enabled, variables won't be bound to this space unless they specify it by name: <spacename>/<varname>.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicVariableStatus`1: Dynamic Variable Status is a component that allows for reading if a certain dynamic variable with a type and name exists and/or is linked to a space. Which space this component reads from is determined by the slot this component is attached to. How a slot finds a dynamic variable space is further explained on the Dynamic Variables page.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicVariableStatus`1.IsLinked: Checks if the variable with the <mark=#ffffff10>VariableName</mark> and with this component's sub type (the "<T>" of this component) is linked to the found variable space. How a slot finds a dynamic variable space is further explained on the Dynamic Variables page.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicVariableStatus`1.Variable: Checks if the variable with the <mark=#ffffff10>VariableName</mark> and with this component's sub type (the "<T>" of this component) exists in the found variable space. How a slot finds a dynamic variable space is further explained on the Dynamic Variables page.
messages.Tooltip.ParentReference`1: When this component is on a slot, all immediate children are part of its parent value system.
messages.Tooltip.ParentData`1.Tag: Tag of the parent value. Compatible ParentReferenceLink components on the slot's immediate children must have the same tag.
messages.Tooltip.ParentReference`1.Reference: The value of the parent value associated with the <mark=#ffffff10>Tag</mark> and type.
messages.Tooltip.ParentReferenceLink`1: Upon a change of parent, this component will search for ParentReference components on the slot's immediate parent that have a matching type and tag.
messages.Tooltip.ParentDataLink`1.MatchTag: The tag to search for on the slot's immediate parent. Compatible ParentReference components must have the same <mark=#ffffff10>Tag</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ParentReferenceLink`1.Target: The field to drive using the value of the linked parent.
messages.Tooltip.ParentReferenceLink`1.WriteBack: If <mark=#ffffff10>True</mark>, any writes to the driven field will be propagated back to the <mark=#ffffff10>Reference</mark> provided by the ParentReference. See write backs.
messages.Tooltip.ParentReferenceLink`1.DefaultRef: Default value to use if the component can not find a compatible ParentReference.
messages.Tooltip.ParentValue`1: When this component is on a slot, all immediate children are part of its parent value system.
messages.Tooltip.ParentValue`1.Value: The value of the parent value associated with the <mark=#ffffff10>Tag</mark> and type.
messages.Tooltip.ParentValueLink`1: Upon a change of parent, this component will search for ParentValue components on the slot's immediate parent that have a matching type and tag.
messages.Tooltip.ParentValueLink`1.Target: The field to drive using the value of the linked parent.
messages.Tooltip.ParentValueLink`1.WriteBack: If <mark=#ffffff10>True</mark>, any writes to the driven field will be propagated back to the <mark=#ffffff10>Value</mark> provided by the ParentValue. See write backs.
messages.Tooltip.ParentValueLink`1.DefaultValue: Default value to use if the component can not find a compatible ParentValue.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceTag`1.Reference: the object to store a reference of.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanSpatialVariableDriver: The Boolean Spatial Variable Driver component is specifically for boolean values, which combines all the found bool spatial values at given point
messages.Tooltip.BooleanSpatialVariableDriver.Dri: The field to drive with the combined values of all the bools at the sampled slot point.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanSpatialVariableDriver.Mod: How to aggrigate values to determine the final result.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanSpatialVariableDriver.Def: The value to default to when no value can be sampled.
messages.Tooltip.CloningReferenceSpatialVariableC: The Cloning Reference Spatial Variable Collector`1 component is part of the Spatial variables system. It works similarly to ReferenceSpatialVariableCollector, but specifically for components. For each component it finds at the point, it will create & maintain a duplicate. It can duplicate either just the component itself or the whole Slot it's on. The duplicates can be made Local only (it's your responsibility to make sure that the target list can reference local elements). It will never clone spatial variables present on the slot.
The slot to parent cloned elements to.
Whether cloned items should be on the local machine rather than networked.
The name of the variable being sampled.
How to clone elements this samples.
The list of references this component has sampled at the point of the slot it is on.
messages.Tooltip.MinMaxValueSpatialVariableDriver: The Min Max Value Spatial Variable Driver`1 component drives fields with the min and max possible values of the spatial variables it is currently sampling. If there's no spatial variables, Min/Max will be the Max/Min possible values for that datatype
The field to drive with the minimum possible value that could be sampled from all the spatial variables that this sampler is sampling from.
The field to drive with the maximum possible value that could be sampled from all the spatial variables that this sampler is sampling from.
The variable name to sample for from the point this component's slot is at.
messages.Tooltip.NumericValueSpatialVariableDrive: The Numeric Value Spatial Variable Driver`1 component works specifically for numeric values and allows for multiple sampling modes.
The field to drive with the resulting value.
The variable name to sample for.
How to interpret the sampled values.
The value to default to if no value can be sampled.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceSpatialVariableCollecto: The Reference Spatial Variable Collector`1 component is specifically for reference types This drives a list of references, filling it with all the found spatial variables. It does this by reading all spatial variables of the reference type matching this one, and in range, and puts them into the referenced list.
The list to populate with the spatial variables of all spatial fields intersecting the point that is the slot this component is on.
The value to default to when no references can be sampled at all.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceSpatialVariableDriver`1: The Reference Spatial Variable Driver`1 component gets the closest reference value in the Spatial variables system and drives a field with it.
The variable name to sample for.
The value to default to when no reference can be sampled.
messages.Tooltip.SpatialVariableBlendDriver: The Spatial Variable Blend Driver component combines with the CloningReferenceSpatialVariableCollector component. If this is on a field on a spatial variable component which is driven by this component, the cloned version will be driven with the current blend weight for that clone. This is used for the BlendWeight for the new reverb zones. For more info on making reverb zones, see Component:AudioListener
messages.Tooltip.SpatialVariableBlendDriver.Blend: used for proportional blending and priority computation
messages.Tooltip.TypeSpatialVariableDriver: The Type Spatial Variable Driver component is part of the Spatial variables system. It will drive a field with value sampled at the current global position of the slot it's on. The value used is one with highest priority
messages.Tooltip.TypeSpatialVariableDriver.Drive: The field to drive with the sampled value.
messages.Tooltip.TypeSpatialVariableDriver.Variab: The variable name to sample for.
messages.Tooltip.TypeSpatialVariableDriver.Defaul: The value to default to if no variable is found from sampling at the point of the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.ValueSpatialVariableDriver`1: The Value Spatial Variable Driver`1 component samples from Spatial variables intersecting the slot it is on and gets a value.
messages.Tooltip.ValueSpatialVariableDriver`1.Dri: The field to drive with the sampled variable.
messages.Tooltip.ValueSpatialVariableDriver`1.Var: the name of the variable to be sampling for.
messages.Tooltip.ValueSpatialVariableDriver`1.Def: The value to default to when no value can be sampled.
messages.Tooltip.ValueSpatialVariablePartialDeriv: The Value Spatial Variable Driver`1 component samples from Spatial variables intersecting the slot it is on and gets a value.
Field X to drive with the sampled variable.
Field Y to drive with the sampled variable.
Field Z to drive with the sampled variable.
the name of the variable to be sampling for.
How to interpret the sampled values.
The value to default to when no value can be sampled.
If the sampling distance will be scaled to the slots scale or revert to global 1,1,1 scale
messages.Tooltip.BoxConstantReferenceSpatialVaria: The Box Constant Reference Spatial Variable`1 component provides a constant value regardless of position inside of it as a contribution to a Spatial variables system.
messages.Tooltip.SpatialVariable`1.VariableName: The name of the variable this has within it's field of influence for the Spatial variables system.
messages.Tooltip.SpatialVariable`1.Priority: The priority this spatial variable has other others overlapping in the same space for the Spatial variables system.
messages.Tooltip.BoxSpatialVariable`1.Size: the size of the box in which variable samplers can sample from this within.
messages.Tooltip.BoxSpatialVariable`1.BlendDistan: How the gradient for this variable within it's volume determines the sampled value for the Spatial variables system.
The mode used for blending the value with other variables for the Spatial variables system.
messages.Tooltip.BoxConstantReferenceSpatialVaria: The value this spatial variable gives when sampled at 100%.
messages.Tooltip.BoxConstantValueSpatialVariable`: The Box Constant Value Spatial Variable`1 component is a Spatial variables variable where the value is the same throughout the whole volume.
The value this spatial variable gives when sampled at 100%.
messages.Tooltip.BoxGradientValueSpatialVariable`: The Box Gradient Value Spatial Variable`1 component is a Spatial variables variable where the value changes from Start to End value along a Direction. This works best when the direction is aligned with one of the cube's axes. Start value starts at the edge of the shape and goes inwards towards the center.
The value this spatial variable gives when sampled at 0%.
The value this spatial variable gives when sampled at 100%.
The direction the Gradient goes in local space to the Spatial variable.
messages.Tooltip.BoxTexture3D_SpatialVariable: The Box Texture 3D Spatial Variable component is specifically for colorX spatial variables. This is a box spatial variable that provides colorX spatial variables sampled from a Texture3D.
messages.Tooltip.BoxTexture3D_SpatialVariable.Tex: The texture to use and sample from in 3D space. Must have <mark=#ffffff10>IsReadable</mark> enabled!
messages.Tooltip.BoxTexture3D_SpatialVariable.Use: When enabled, this will squish/stretch the texture with the box.
messages.Tooltip.BoxTexture3D_SpatialVariable.Sca: The scale of the texture in 3D space.
messages.Tooltip.BoxTexture3D_SpatialVariable.Off: The offset of the texture in 3D space from the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.BoxTexture3D_SpatialVariable.Wra: How to wrap the texture along the X axis.
How to wrap the texture along the Y axis.
How to wrap the texture along the Z axis.
messages.Tooltip.BoxVertexValueSpatialVariable`1: The Box Vertex Value Spatial Variable`1 component makes a spatial variable that provides samplers with a different value depending on what vertex of the area a sampler is closest to. This is similar to the color properties of a box emitter.
The value sampled when near the 0th vertex.
The value sampled when near the 1st vertex.
The value sampled when near the 2nd vertex.
The value sampled when near the 3rd vertex.
The value sampled when near the 4th vertex.
The value sampled when near the 5th vertex.
The value sampled when near the 6th vertex.
The value sampled when near the 7th vertex.
messages.Tooltip.SphereConstantReferenceSpatialVa: The SphereConstantReferenceSpatialVariable component is used to define a reference value as part of the Spatial variables system without a gradient.
messages.Tooltip.SphereSpatialVariable`1.Radius: The priority this spatial variable has other others overlapping in the same space for the Spatial variables system.
messages.Tooltip.SphereSpatialVariable`1.BlendDis: How the gradient for this variable within it's volume determines the sampled value for the Spatial variables system.
The mode used for blending the value with other variables for the Spatial variables system.
messages.Tooltip.SphereConstantReferenceSpatialVa: The value this spatial variable gives when sampled at 100%.
messages.Tooltip.SphereConstantValueSpatialVariab: The Sphere Constant Value Spatial Variable`1 component is part of the Spatial variables system.
The value this spatial variable gives when sampled at 100%.
messages.Tooltip.SphereStartEndValueSpatialVariab: The Sphere Start End Value Spatial Variable`1 component is part of the Spatial variables system. The start value starts at the edge of the shape and goes inwards towards the center, or the other way around depending on provided values.
The value this spatial variable gives when sampled at 0%.
The value this spatial variable gives when sampled at 100%.
The distance at which the sampled spatial variable starts at <mark=#ffffff10>StartValue</mark>.
The distance at which the sampled spatial variable ends at <mark=#ffffff10>EndValue</mark>.
The mode to use when sampling the values based on distance.
messages.Tooltip.DataPreset: The DataPreset is a component that is useful for switching a list of values to a predetermined set.
messages.Tooltip.DataPreset.IsActive: Indicates that all the presets in Entries have been applied. Cannot be driven.
messages.Tooltip.DataPreset.Entries: A list of data preset references and values.
messages.Tooltip.DataPresetReference`1: Data Preset reference is a component that takes any reference type for PresetReference. When this is triggered by a DataPreset, this sets TargetReference to PresetReference.
messages.Tooltip.DataPresetReference`1.PresetRefe: The field reference for the preset. Since this is a generic component, it can be used with any type of reference.
messages.Tooltip.DataPresetReference`1.TargetRefe: A reference to the reference field of the same type as PresetReference.
messages.Tooltip.DataPresetValue`1: Data Preset reference is a component that takes any value type for PresetValue. When this is triggered by a DataPreset, this sets TargetField to PresetValue.
messages.Tooltip.DataPresetValue`1.PresetValue: The value for the preset. Float is used as an example. Since this is a generic component, it can be used with any value.
messages.Tooltip.DataPresetValue`1.TargetField: A reference to the field of the same type as PresetValue.
messages.Tooltip.ValueTag`1: The ValueTag component is a simplified version of the ValueField component. The value can be read and written.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopControlDialog: The DesktopControlDialog is used to change the settings for what desktop to control and how to control it.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopControlDialog.Index: The display to show in a multi monitor settup.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopControlDialog.FollowCurso: whether the desktop cursor should follow the Resonite dash cursor when not clicking.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopControlDialog.Brightness: how bright the desktop display should be in the dash.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopControlDialog.Opacity: how transparent the desktop display should be in the dash.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopControlDialog.LegacyInput: Whether to use the legacy input system for desktop interaction.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopViewSettings: The DesktopViewSettings component is used to adjust how viewing the local user's computer screen is done via the Dash Menu.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopViewSettings.FollowCursor: Whether the Desktop cursor should follow where the user's in game cursor is on the dash in real time.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopViewSettings.Brightness: How bright the display of the desktop is on the in game dash menu.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopViewSettings.Opacity: How transparent the in game view of the desktop is on the dash menu.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopController: The DesktopController component is used to control the desktop through the game dash.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopController.FollowCursor: whether the desktop cursor should follow the Resonite dash cursor when not clicking.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopController.LegacyInputMod: Whether to use the legacy input system for desktop interaction.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopController.Brightness: how bright the desktop display should be in the dash.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopController.Opacity: how transparent the desktop display should be in the dash.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopController._displayColor: The color of the display texture to drive
messages.Tooltip.DesktopController._displayRect: The UIX size rectangle for the display texture.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopController._interactionRe: The relay allowing for system Desktop interaction.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopController._currentContro: The current dialogue being used to change settings for this component.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopDisplayLayout: The DesktopDisplayLayout component displays a list of display selection buttons for a user's monitor settup. It is used in the dash menu and only works in userspace.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopDisplayLayout.User: The user to get displays for.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopDisplayLayout.DisplayTemp: the template to use when generating display selection buttons.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopDisplayLayout.ItemGenerat: usually the OnDisplayItemGenerated() sync delegate from DesktopControlDialog.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopInteractionRelay: The DesktopInteractionRelay component only works in user space. When part of a UIX, it sends events from user inputs (the position is determined by the RectTransform) to the desktop display at DisplayIndex
messages.Tooltip.DesktopInteractionRelay.DisplayI: The display to send Interactions to.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopInteractionRelay.UseLegac: Whether to use the legacy text input system.
messages.Tooltip.DisplayIndex: The DisplayIndex component when duplicated by a DesktopDisplayLayout by being part of the slot hiearchy specified by that component's DisplayTemplate, this component's Index field will be filled by the screen number that DesktopDisplayLayout was trying to spawn a template for.
messages.Tooltip.DisplayInfo: The DisplayInfo component is used to get information about the local user's display.
messages.Tooltip.DisplayInfo.DisplayIndex: The display index to get info on.
messages.Tooltip.DisplayInfo.Offset: The offset of pixels of display <mark=#ffffff10>DisplayIndex</mark>
messages.Tooltip.DisplayInfo.DPI: The Dots Per Inch of display <mark=#ffffff10>DisplayIndex</mark>
messages.Tooltip.DisplayInfo.RefreshRate: The refresh rate in Hertz of <mark=#ffffff10>DisplayIndex</mark>
messages.Tooltip.DisplayInfo.IsPrimary: Whether display <mark=#ffffff10>DisplayIndex</mark> is the main monitor in the OS.
messages.Tooltip.BlendshapeWeightListEditor: Weight list editor is a component that is used within inspectors and shouldn't need to be interacted with by the user. Although, it can be used in very niche circumstances.
messages.Tooltip.ListEditor._targetList: The list of blendshapes. This can be acquired through grabbing the "BlendShapes" title at the beginning of a blendshape list inside of a Skinned Mesh Renderer
messages.Tooltip.ListEditor._addNewButton: The button that when pressed, should add a new blendshape to the end of the <mark=#ffffff10>_targetList</mark>. Whether the new blendshape does anything or not depends on if the <mark=#ffffff10>Mesh</mark> data inside of <mark=#ffffff10>_targetSkin</mark> has a blendshape list that reaches as far as the new item.
messages.Tooltip.BlendshapeWeightListEditor._targ: The skinned Mesh renderer to add a new blendshape item to. Has to be the same component <mark=#ffffff10>_targetList</mark> comes from.
messages.Tooltip.RotationGizmo: The RotationGizmo component is used by dev tools to allow rotation of slots.
messages.Tooltip.RotationGizmo._xGizmo: The rotation gizmo for the x axis.
messages.Tooltip.RotationGizmo._yGizmo: The rotation gizmo for the y axis.
messages.Tooltip.RotationGizmo._zGizmo: The rotation gizmo for the z axis.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo: The SlotGizmo is used with the Dev tool to interact with slots when they are selected and their sub gizmos. This is rarely interacted directly with by the user, but is a very important component for interacting with the game.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo.GizmoReplaced: A sync delegate to call when the gizmo is replaced with another one. Usually located on the dev tool that created the gizmo.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo._isFolded: Whether the gizmo is folded up.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo._activeGizmo: Which gizmo is being shown right now via tool options.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo._positionDrive: The field to drive position with for this visual.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo._scaleDrive: The field to drive scale with for this visual.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo._boundsMesh: The mesh to use to show bounds.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo._boundsRoot: The slot to make bounds for.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo._boundsRotation: the field to drive for the bounds rotation.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo._boundsOffset: The field to drive for bounds offset.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo._boundsActive: The field to drive with whether the bounds are active or not.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo._nameText: The field to drive with the name of the target slot.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo._nameOffset: The position offset field for the visual of the name of targeted slot.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo._nameRotation: The rotation field for the visual of the name of targeted slot.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo._nameActive: The active field for the visual of the name of targeted slot.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo._xPosSegment: The segment used for the X position vs this slot's parent slot.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo._yPosSegment: The segment used for the Y position vs this slot's parent slot.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo._zPosSegment: The segment used for the Z position vs this slot's parent slot.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo._boundsAnchorPositions: A list of infos for anchors this slot gizmo has.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo._rootAnchor: The root point anchor for this gizmo.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo._translationGizmo: The gizmo used for changing this slot's position.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo._rotationGizmo: The gizmo used for changing this slot's rotation.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo._scaleGizmo: The gizmo used for changing this slot's scale.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo.IsLocalSpace: Whether this gizmo is oriented to local space or not.
messages.Tooltip.TranslationGizmo: Handles the translation of slots on the X, Y, Z directions or a combination thereof.
messages.Tooltip.TranslationGizmo._xyzGizmo: The gizmo that allows translations on xyz at the same time.
messages.Tooltip.TranslationGizmo._xyGizmo: The gizmo that allows translations on xy at the same time.
messages.Tooltip.TranslationGizmo._xzGizmo: The gizmo that allows translations on xz at the same time.
messages.Tooltip.TranslationGizmo._yzGizmo: The gizmo that allows translations on yz at the same time.
messages.Tooltip.TranslationGizmo._xGizmo: The gizmo that allows translation on x.
messages.Tooltip.TranslationGizmo._yGizmo: The gizmo that allows translation on y.
messages.Tooltip.TranslationGizmo._zGizmo: The gizmo that allows translation on z.
messages.Tooltip.GizmoLink: The GizmoLink component handles the Interaction and connection of every Gizmo with their respective target. This is usually invisible to users and their inspectors, but upon opening a new inspector on a target of a Gizmo it appears. It is unknown if this is a bug. This component along with Gizmos do not save with items but Gizmos can save with a world.
messages.Tooltip.GizmoLink._gizmo: The Gizmo this is making a Link for.
messages.Tooltip.GizmoLink._type: The C# Type of what this is targeting.
messages.Tooltip.AxisRotationGizmo: The AxisRotationGizmo component is used for one of the any 3 axies of rotation for a rotation gizmo that can be interacted with through the Dev Tool.
messages.Tooltip.Gizmo.TargetSlot: The slot to influence or a component this is on.
messages.Tooltip.Gizmo.AutoPositionAtTargetSlot: whether the gizmo should auto position at <mark=#ffffff10>TargetSlot</mark>
messages.Tooltip.Gizmo._interactingComponent: The component like a Dev tool that is interacting with this gizmo.
messages.Tooltip.Gizmo._material: The material that is being used for the visual.
messages.Tooltip.Gizmo._toolPoint: The slot to position at the interacting tool's tip slot.
messages.Tooltip.Gizmo._activePoint: The point for this gizmo when using tip instead of projection for the interacting tooltip.
messages.Tooltip.Gizmo._lineRoot: The root slot of the visual being used when doing tip instead of Projection for the interacting tooltip.
messages.Tooltip.Gizmo._lineSegment: The line segment for the line from gizmo center to <mark=#ffffff10>_toolPoint</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.Gizmo._snapHighlight: The slot for a snap point highlight like a selected cube corner.
messages.Tooltip.AxisRotationGizmo.Axis: The direction of the 2d axis this will rotate along.
messages.Tooltip.AxisRotationGizmo.RotationSpace: The coordinate space of the <mark=#ffffff10>TargetRotation</mark> drive target.
messages.Tooltip.AxisRotationGizmo.TargetRotation: A field to drive with the target rotation
messages.Tooltip.AxisRotationGizmo.CircleRadius: The radius of the rotation circle gizmo.
messages.Tooltip.AxisRotationGizmo.CircleThicknes: The thickness of the torus of the rotation gizmo.
messages.Tooltip.AxisRotationGizmo._visualRoot: The root slot of the visual for interaction.
messages.Tooltip.AxisRotationGizmo._visualRot: The rotation field of the visual.
messages.Tooltip.AxisRotationGizmo._circle: The torus mesh being used to make the rotation gizmo interact visual.
messages.Tooltip.AxisRotationGizmo._circleCollide: The collider for the circle rotation gizmo visual.
messages.Tooltip.AxisRotationGizmo._linesRoot: The root of the visuals for the lines.
messages.Tooltip.AxisRotationGizmo._line0: The line along the rotation plane to below the tooltip.
messages.Tooltip.AxisRotationGizmo._line1: The line from the end of <mark=#ffffff10>_line0</mark> to the tooltip.
messages.Tooltip.AxisTranslationGizmo: The AxisTranslationGizmo is the squares often seen on a Gizmo to translate it along 2 axies at the same time.
messages.Tooltip.AxisTranslationGizmo.Axis: The direction of the 2d plane this will translate along.
messages.Tooltip.AxisTranslationGizmo.PointSpace: The coordinate space of <mark=#ffffff10>TargetPoint</mark> drive value.
messages.Tooltip.AxisTranslationGizmo.TargetPoint: A field to drive with the target point position.
messages.Tooltip.AxisTranslationGizmo.UseCustomVi: Whether to use a custom visual rather than the default code generated one.
messages.Tooltip.AxisTranslationGizmo._customVisu: The root slot of the custom visual.
messages.Tooltip.AxisTranslationGizmo.ArrowLength: The length of the arrow this gizmo is attached to.
messages.Tooltip.AxisTranslationGizmo.CreateUndoS: Whether to make edits done with this component undoable.
messages.Tooltip.AxisTranslationGizmo._visualRoot: The root slot of the visual for interaction.
messages.Tooltip.AxisTranslationGizmo._visualRot: The rotation field of the visual.
messages.Tooltip.AxisTranslationGizmo._arrowVecto: The vector field of the arrow mesh visual.
messages.Tooltip.AxisTranslationGizmo._arrow: The arrow mesh of the visual.
messages.Tooltip.AxisTranslationGizmo._linesRoot: the root slot of the lines visual.
messages.Tooltip.AxisTranslationGizmo._line0: The segment mesh being used for the first line.
messages.Tooltip.AxisTranslationGizmo._line1: The segment mesh being used for the second line.
messages.Tooltip.BoxGizmo: The BoxGizmo component is used to make the handles and visual for editing a box mesh or collider, and also handles the interaction with the handles.
messages.Tooltip.BoxGizmo.TargetSlot: The slot of the box to edit.
messages.Tooltip.BoxGizmo.BoxCenterSpace: The coordinate space to transform by when making the result of <mark=#ffffff10>BoxCenter</mark>
messages.Tooltip.BoxGizmo.BoxSizeSpace: The coordinate space to transform by when making the result of <mark=#ffffff10>BoxSize</mark>
messages.Tooltip.BoxGizmo.BoxSize: The field to edit for the box size.
messages.Tooltip.BoxGizmo.BoxCenter: The field to drive for the box center.
messages.Tooltip.BoxGizmo.LockOffset: Whether to lock the offset of the box so it's center stays the same.
messages.Tooltip.BoxGizmo._vertices: A list of fields to drive for the vertex positions of the cube gizmo visual.
messages.Tooltip.BoxGizmo._edges: A list of fields to drive for the edge positions of the cube gizmo visual.
messages.Tooltip.BoxGizmo._faces: A list of fields to drive for the face positions of the cube gizmo visual.
messages.Tooltip.BoxGizmo._sphereColliderRadii: A list of fields to drive for the sphere rotations  of the cube gizmo visual.
messages.Tooltip.BoxGizmo._material: The material being used for the gizmo visual.
messages.Tooltip.BoxGizmo._handleSphere: The mesh being used for the handles of the gizmo like corners or faces.
messages.Tooltip.BoxGizmo._visualRoot: The slot of the visual root.
messages.Tooltip.BoxGizmo._visualPosition: The field to drive for the visual position.
messages.Tooltip.BoxGizmo._visualRotation: The field to drive for the visual rotation.
messages.Tooltip.BoxGizmo._visualScale: The field to drive for the visual scale.
messages.Tooltip.BoxGizmo._tubeBox: The tube box mesh being used for the gizmo visual.
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo: The ConeGizmo component is auto generated for interaction with a Component:ConeMesh or Component:ConeCollider via a Dev Tool.
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo.TargetAngle: The field being edited for the angle of the cone.
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo.TargetRadius: The field being edited for the radius of the cone base.
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo.TargetHeight: The field being edited for the height of the cone.
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo.TargetDirection: The field being edited for the cone direction.
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo.TargetRotation: The field being edited for the cone rotation.
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo.FixedAngle: The angle that the cone should be at (shape).
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo.FixedHeight: The height that the cone mesh should be.
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo.FixedDirection: The direction the cone should be facing.
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo.LineThickness: The thickness of the lines of the visual.
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo.MinHeight: The minimum height the cone can be set to.
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo.MaxHeight: The maximum height the cone can be set to.
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo.MinAngle: The minimum tip angle the cone can be set to (shape).
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo.MaxAngle: The maximum tip angle the cone can be set to (shape).
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo._visualRoot: The root slot of the visual for this gizmo.
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo._visualRot: The rotation field of the <mark=#ffffff10>_visualRoot</mark> slot.
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo._handles: A list of Handle objects this cone gizmo has.
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo._heightMesh: The mesh being used to signal the height of the cone.
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo._coneLineMesh: The line segment based mesh that shows the cone itself.
messages.Tooltip.PlaneTranslationGizmo: The PlaneTranslationGizmo component is used to make the 3 plane axes on a translation gizmo for moving a slot using the dev tip. Used to move objects along a flat plane. Auto generated by the game.
messages.Tooltip.PlaneTranslationGizmo.PointSpace: The transform space of the field this is modifying.
messages.Tooltip.PlaneTranslationGizmo.Normal: The normal of where this plane gizmo should be pointing for its normal.
messages.Tooltip.PlaneTranslationGizmo.NormalSpac: The transform space the normal is defined in.
messages.Tooltip.PlaneTranslationGizmo.UseCustomV: Whether to use a custom visual for this gizmo.
messages.Tooltip.PlaneTranslationGizmo._customVis: The root slot of the custom visual.
messages.Tooltip.PlaneTranslationGizmo.HandleSize: The size of the plane used for interacting with this component.
messages.Tooltip.PlaneTranslationGizmo.HandleOffs: The offset of the plane used for interacting with this component.
messages.Tooltip.PlaneTranslationGizmo.CreateUndo: Whether to create Undo steps for interacting with this gizmo.
messages.Tooltip.PlaneTranslationGizmo._visualRoo: The root slot of the visual of this gizmo.
messages.Tooltip.PlaneTranslationGizmo._visualRot: The rotation field of this Gizmos visual.
messages.Tooltip.PlaneTranslationGizmo._visualPos: The position field of this gizmo's visual.
messages.Tooltip.PlaneTranslationGizmo._boxSize: The size of the box mesh being used for this visual.
messages.Tooltip.PlaneTranslationGizmo._linesRoot: The root slot for the tip projection lines visual.
messages.Tooltip.PlaneTranslationGizmo._line0: The first line for this gizmo when interacting via tip instead of Projection with a dev tip.
messages.Tooltip.PlaneTranslationGizmo._line1: The second line for this gizmo when interacting via tip instead of Projection with a dev tip.
messages.Tooltip.PlaneTranslationGizmo._collider: The collider for interacting with this gizmo.
messages.Tooltip.PointAnchor: The PointAnchor component is used so that the dev tool can snap objects to it when they are being moved. Part of the dev tool's snapping functionality.
messages.Tooltip.PointAnchor.SnapParent: The thing objects should snap to when snapping to this point anchor.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleGizmo: The ScaleGizmo component is used to allow scaling of a slot it is targeting.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleGizmo.TargetScale: The scale field of what this is scaling.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleGizmo.HandleLength: The length of the scale gizmo handles.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleGizmo._xSlot: The scale gizmo for the x axis.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleGizmo._ySlot: The scale gizmo for the y axis.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleGizmo._zSlot: The scale gizmo for the z axis.
messages.Tooltip.SphereGizmo: The SphereGizmo component is used to control the radius of certain sphere elements.
messages.Tooltip.SphereGizmo.TargetRadius: The field this gizmo is editing (also known as the radius field)
messages.Tooltip.SphereGizmo.RadiusSpace: The transform space the radius is in.
messages.Tooltip.SphereGizmo.RimRadius: The radius of the rim of the target radius.
messages.Tooltip.SphereGizmo.RimRadiusDistanceSca: How to scale the radius for the rim by distance.
messages.Tooltip.SphereGizmo._handles: a list of handles for this gizmo. usually a set of 3 rings.
messages.Tooltip.VectorGizmo: The VectorGizmo component is used to allow editing a gizmo via the Dev tool.
messages.Tooltip.VectorGizmo.VectorSpace: The space to do edits to the vector in.
messages.Tooltip.VectorGizmo.TargetVector: The target vector field to modify.
messages.Tooltip.VectorGizmo.TargetRotation: The target vector rotation to modify.
messages.Tooltip.VectorGizmo.FixMagnitude: whether to fix a magnitude during/after editing.
messages.Tooltip.VectorGizmo.FixedMagnitude: The value to fix the vector magnitude to if <mark=#ffffff10>FixMagnitude</mark> is enabled.
messages.Tooltip.VectorGizmo.VisualMagnitudeScale: The magnitude scalar of the vector visual.
messages.Tooltip.VectorGizmo.VisualThickness: The thickness of the visual for the vector.
messages.Tooltip.VectorGizmo._colliderRotation: The rotation field of the visual collider.
messages.Tooltip.VectorGizmo._collider: The collider used for the vector.
messages.Tooltip.VectorGizmo._mesh: The arrow mesh being used to show the vector visual.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeTranslationGizmo: The VolumeTranslationGizmo component is used to move around and edit a volume object.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeTranslationGizmo.PointSpac: The space that the translation point is in.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeTranslationGizmo.TargetPoi: The field in which to drive for the target point editing.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeTranslationGizmo.UseCustom: Whether or not to use a custom visual.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeTranslationGizmo._customVi: The root of the custom visual element.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeTranslationGizmo._visualRo: The root of the default visual.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeTranslationGizmo.CubeSize: The size of the visual root.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeTranslationGizmo.CreateUnd: Whether or not to create Undo steps for this gizmo.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeTranslationGizmo._cubeSize: The field to drive with the cube size.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeTranslationGizmo._collider: The field to drive with the collider size.
messages.Tooltip.BagEditorItem: The BagEditorItem component is used in conjunction with a Component:BagEditor. This is a reference to an item in the bag. For more information, please see the page on Component:BagEditor.
messages.Tooltip.BagEditorItem.Item: The bag item this is referencing.
messages.Tooltip.BagEditor: When _targetBag receives a reference to a bag, the BagEditor's slot's children will be populated with items in the bag.
messages.Tooltip.BagEditor._addNewButton: Button to add a new item to the bag.
messages.Tooltip.ComponentSelector: The Component Selector is better understood on its page, Complex Types in Components.
messages.Tooltip.ComponentSelector.ComponentSelec: The Sync delegate to call when the component is attached.
messages.Tooltip.ComponentSelector.ComponentFilte: The type of Components to show in the attacher.
messages.Tooltip.ComponentSelector.GenericArgumen: A sync delegate that fills the list of generic arguments for a selected type so it doesn't only ask for a certain type.
messages.Tooltip.ComponentSelector._uiRoot: The root slot of the UI.
messages.Tooltip.ComponentSelector._rootPath: The path that this is navigated to in the component attacher.
messages.Tooltip.ComponentSelector._genericType: The generic type we are trying to make a generic fill for.
messages.Tooltip.ComponentSelector._customGeneric: The label for the generic type we are attaching.
The color for the generic type we are attaching.
The generic arguments being used for the current Component being attached. See Complex Types in Components.
messages.Tooltip.DelegateEditor: The DelegateEditor component is used in the Inspector window of Components where there is a field a sync delegate can be dropped into.
messages.Tooltip.DelegateEditor._textDrive: The text field to drive with the name of the Sync delegate method.
messages.Tooltip.DelegateEditor._button: The button to interface with the Sync delegate.
messages.Tooltip.DelegateProxy`1: The DelegateProxy is the Sync delegate version of a proxy used to store information about a world element in a user's hand before dropping it into a field. It's similar to grabbing a field name or a value.
messages.Tooltip.DelegateProxySource`1: The DelegateProxySource component is used to make a Button UIX element give a sync delegate proxy when grabbed. To use almost any sync delegate with this component, provide "Delegate" as the type.
messages.Tooltip.DelegateProxySource`1.Delegate: The field to proxy when grabbing.
messages.Tooltip.FieldDriveReceiver`1: The FieldDriveReceiver component is used to handle the dropping of field names onto other field names in order to drive one with the other in inspectors, otherwise known as Drives. This is used as an interactable UIX element.
messages.Tooltip.FieldDriveReceiver`1.Field: The field to expose to dropping other field names onto it for driving.
messages.Tooltip.FieldEditor: A FieldEditor component takes an IField of any type and deconstructs it's elements into a list of editor components.
messages.Tooltip.FieldEditor._targetField: The field to generate an editor list for.
messages.Tooltip.FieldEditor._textEditors: A list of text editors of the specified <mark=#ffffff10>_targetField</mark>'s components.
messages.Tooltip.FieldEditor._textDrives: A list of text value fields to drive with the contents of the components of the specified <mark=#ffffff10>_targetField</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.InspectorMemberActions: The InspectorMemberActions component is used in inspectors to make the clicking on the left hand side button behavior that brings up a context menu.
messages.Tooltip.InspectorMemberActions.Member: The member to bring up a context menu with opens to break drives and a few other things.
messages.Tooltip.ListEditor: When _targetList receives a reference to a list, the ListEditor's slot's children will be populated with items in the list.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanMemberEditor: The BooleanMemberEditor component is commonly used in Scene Inspectors to allow toggling of booleans even if they are a sub property of a type like a Bool3 Using _path. This can be utilized in Ref Hacking.
messages.Tooltip.MemberEditor.Continuous: Whether to update the target field while editing the text field. If disabled, it will only update at the end of editing.
messages.Tooltip.MemberEditor._path: see Ref Hacking. essentially accesses a member underneath another one. for example, "x" will access the x element of a Float3, Float2, Float4, or FloatQ.
messages.Tooltip.MemberEditor._target: The bool field or a field with a toggleable bool subfield.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanMemberEditor._stateDrive: The field to drive with the state of <mark=#ffffff10>_target</mark>-><mark=#ffffff10>_path</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ColorMemberEditor: The ColorMemberEditor component is used in inspectors and Ref Hacking to edit Color Fields and their elements.
messages.Tooltip.ColorMemberEditorBase.Vertical: Whether the inspector showing this component is showing it in a vertical fashion.
messages.Tooltip.ColorMemberEditorBase._colorDriv: The field to drive with the colorX color for the inspector visual.
The field to drive with the colorX target with no alpha applied for the inspector visual.
messages.Tooltip.ColorMemberEditorBase._colorDial: The color dialogue currently being used to edit the color.
messages.Tooltip.ColorXMemberEditor: The ColorXMemberEditor component is used in inspectors and Ref Hacking to edit ColorX Fields and their UIX elements.
messages.Tooltip.EnumMemberEditor: The EnumMemberEditor component is used in inspectors to edit enum values.
messages.Tooltip.EnumMemberEditor._textDrive: The text field to drive with the enum's contents.
messages.Tooltip.EnumMemberEditor._button: The button to edit the enum with.
messages.Tooltip.NullableMemberEditor: The Nullable Member Editor component is commonly used in Ref Hacking and is used to modify nullable values.
messages.Tooltip.NullableMemberEditor.NullableBas: The type of the value stored within the nullable (for "Nullable<Float>" aka "Float?" This is "Float")
messages.Tooltip.NullableMemberEditor._checkBox: Whether the nullable has a value.
messages.Tooltip.NullableMemberEditor._stateDrive: The field to drive with whether or not the nullable has a value.
messages.Tooltip.NullableMemberEditor._button: The button for editing the nullable.
messages.Tooltip.PrimitiveMemberEditor: This component needs, at minimum, _target to point to an element with fields, a _textDrive that points to the Content field of a component implementing IText, and a _textEditor that points to a TextEditor with its Text field pointing to the to the aforementioned IText component.
messages.Tooltip.PrimitiveMemberEditor.Format: The format for representing the target primitive. About the same as the Format field for the To String node, but only works on a limited set of primitives.
messages.Tooltip.PrimitiveMemberEditor._textEdito: The TextEditor that points to any component that implements the IText type used for <mark=#ffffff10>_textDrive</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.PrimitiveMemberEditor._textDrive: Text field used as an interface to the member. Should be the <mark=#ffffff10>Content</mark> field of a component implementing IText.
messages.Tooltip.PrimitiveMemberEditor._button: Button used for editing, like on a TextField.
messages.Tooltip.PrimitiveMemberEditor._resetButt: Button used to reset the value. Intended to only be used for strings.
messages.Tooltip.QuaternionMemberEditor: The QuaternionMemberEditor component is a low-level component for use in editing FloatQ elements as XYZ Euler angles in degrees. It is used, for example, in the ProtoFlux input node for FloatQ values.
messages.Tooltip.QuaternionMemberEditor.Vertical: Whether the target UI is a vertical layout, as opposed to a horizontal layout.
messages.Tooltip.QuaternionMemberEditor._xDrive: Field to be driven by the X angle. Should be the <mark=#ffffff10>Content</mark> field on the Text component that <mark=#ffffff10>_xEditor</mark> is pointing to.
messages.Tooltip.QuaternionMemberEditor._yDrive: Field to be driven by the Y angle.
messages.Tooltip.QuaternionMemberEditor._zDrive: Field to be driven by the Z angle.
messages.Tooltip.QuaternionMemberEditor._xButton: Button whose sync delegates are attached to the <mark=#ffffff10>_xEditor</mark>
messages.Tooltip.QuaternionMemberEditor._yButton: Button whose sync delegates are attached to the <mark=#ffffff10>_yEditor</mark>
messages.Tooltip.QuaternionMemberEditor._zButton: Button whose sync delegates are attached to the <mark=#ffffff10>_zEditor</mark>
messages.Tooltip.QuaternionMemberEditor._editingV: The value that the user is currently entering at it's current editing state. updates continuously.
messages.Tooltip.SliderMemberEditor: The SliderMemberEditor is commonly used in inspectors and can be used for Ref hacking.
messages.Tooltip.SliderMemberEditor._slider: The slider to trigger change events this component should use to influence <mark=#ffffff10>_target</mark>
messages.Tooltip.SliderMemberEditor._sliderValue: The value to be used for changing <mark=#ffffff10>_target</mark>
messages.Tooltip.SliderMemberEditor._textEditor: The text editor to change <mark=#ffffff10>_target</mark> as a number text field.
messages.Tooltip.SyncPlaybackEditor: The SyncPlaybackEditor component is used to interact with and modify the playback of a Sync playback like a video, animation, or a sound player.
messages.Tooltip.SyncPlaybackEditor._playback: The playback to edit and influence with this component.
messages.Tooltip.SyncPlaybackEditor._sliderValue: The field to drive with the current playback of <mark=#ffffff10>_playback</mark>
messages.Tooltip.SyncPlaybackEditor._loopToggleSp: The sprite URI field of the toggle loop button. Used to switch between a loop and a play once icon URI.
messages.Tooltip.SyncPlaybackEditor._speedField: The field used to change the speed of the playback of <mark=#ffffff10>_playback</mark>
messages.Tooltip.SyncPlaybackEditor._slider: The slider used to change the play head/position of <mark=#ffffff10>_playback</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.RefEditor: The RefEditor component is used internally by inspectors to show the human readable name of any IWorldElement. Whenever you are looking at a field and see a text like "Enabled on ObjectRoot on ComponentHolder", that description is generated by this component. This can be used to identify fields in the process of Ref Hacking which is one of the Things to Avoid
messages.Tooltip.RefEditor._targetRef: The reference to generate a short description for
messages.Tooltip.RefEditor._textDrive: The text to fill with the short description of <mark=#ffffff10>_targetRef</mark>
messages.Tooltip.RefEditor._button: The button that allows for setting the field of <mark=#ffffff10>_targetRef</mark> when clicked.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceDriveReceiver`1: The ReferenceDriveReceiver component is used to allow dropping a reference proxy via the user's hand onto a UIX element to trigger the prompt for driving a reference field. This requires a UIX button to work.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceDriveReceiver`1.Referen: The field this should allow an option to drive for when a reference is dropped onto the UIX element this is on.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceField`1: Reference Field is a component that is able to store a reference for later use, as long as the reference still exists by then.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceField`1.Reference: A field that can be used to store any reference type.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceList`1: The Reference List`1 component acts as a way to store a list of references as a stand alone component. like a ReferenceField, but for a list of items of the same type.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceProxy: The ReferenceProxy component is used to make a grabbed item this is on act as the reference this refers to. This is also generated when a reference in an Scene Inspector is grabbed in the form of a block of text showing the reference and its id.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceProxy.Reference: The reference this component should point to for grabbable objects, making them like inspector grab cards.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceProxy.SpawnInstanceOnTr: Whether to spawn an inspector when clicking primary with the grabbable this is on is being held.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceProxySource: This is used to carry references to other fields that are looking for it.
messages.Tooltip.SaveProxy: The Save Proxy component saves another slot instead of this one when the slot this component is on is saved.
messages.Tooltip.SaveProxy.Target: The slot to save the hiearchy of when the slot this component is on is saved instead of this slot.
messages.Tooltip.SceneInspector.Root: The root of the view of this inspector's hiearchy.
messages.Tooltip.SceneInspector.ComponentView: The currently selected slot to view components for.
messages.Tooltip.SceneInspector._rootText: The text field for the display of the root hiearchy slot for this inspector's view.
messages.Tooltip.SceneInspector._componentText: The text field for the display of the slot for this inspector's component view.
messages.Tooltip.SceneInspector._hierarchyContent: The root of the view for slots on the currently viewed slot hiearchy.
messages.Tooltip.SceneInspector._componentsConten: The root of the view for components on the currently selected slot.
messages.Tooltip.SceneInspector._currentComponent: The current component this is looking at.
messages.Tooltip.SceneInspector._currentRoot: The current root this is looking at.
messages.Tooltip.SlotComponentReceiver: The SlotComponentReceiver component is used in inspectors to allow for recieving components to copy them into a slot.
messages.Tooltip.SlotInspector: The Slot Inspector component controls the slot items shown in a Scene Inspector and their drop down menus.
messages.Tooltip.SlotInspector._rootSlot: The root of the visual for this item.
messages.Tooltip.SlotInspector._childContainer: The container for slot inspectors that are children of this one.
messages.Tooltip.SlotInspector._depth: How far down this slot inspector is from the root selected slot inspector in it's parent Scene Inspector.
messages.Tooltip.SlotInspector._expander: The component responsible for expanding the children slot inspector hierarchy of this item.
messages.Tooltip.SlotInspector._expanderIndicator: The indicator for whether the slot inspector children hierarchy is expanded or not.
messages.Tooltip.SlotInspector._slotNameText: The name of the slot this slot inspector is pointing to.
messages.Tooltip.SlotRecord: Used solely by the Inspector when regenerating/updating.
messages.Tooltip.SlotRecord.TargetSlot: The slot to make a record of.
messages.Tooltip.TextureRefEditor: The TextureRefEditor component is commonly used in inspectors for materials and other components that use images/textures.
messages.Tooltip.TextureRefEditor._targetRef: The field to make a visual for.
messages.Tooltip.TextureRefEditor._drive: The field to drive with the contents of <mark=#ffffff10>_targetRef</mark> or a placeholder texture if null.
messages.Tooltip.TextureRefEditor._openInspectorB: The button to open an inspector for <mark=#ffffff10>_targetRef</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.TextureRefEditor._copyTextureBut: The button to copy the texture in <mark=#ffffff10>_targetRef</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.TextureRefEditor._pasteTextureBu: The button to paste a texture into <mark=#ffffff10>_targetRef</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.TextureRefEditor._referenceText: The field to drive with the description of the contents of <mark=#ffffff10>_targetRef</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.TextureRefEditor._infoText: The field to drive with the info of <mark=#ffffff10>_targetRef</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.TextureRefEditor._isNormalMap: Whether <mark=#ffffff10>_targetRef</mark> should be interpreted as a normal map.
messages.Tooltip.TypeField: The TypeField component is used to store a Type which is usually a reference to a C# class.
messages.Tooltip.TypeField.Type: The Type this component is storing.
messages.Tooltip.UserInspector: The User Inspector component is better described on it's page, User Inspector.
messages.Tooltip.UserInspector.ViewUser: The user this inspector is currently updating through.
messages.Tooltip.UserInspector.ViewGroup: What kind of information group this is inspecting.
messages.Tooltip.UserInspector.ViewStreamGroup: The kind of value stream group this is viewing.
messages.Tooltip.UserInspector._currentUser: The current user this is viewing.
messages.Tooltip.UserInspector._currentViewGroup: What kind of information group this is currently inspecting.
messages.Tooltip.UserInspector._currentStreamGrou: The kind of value stream group this is currently viewing.
messages.Tooltip.UserInspector._userListContentRo: The slot to place user stream/properties content when viewing a user.
messages.Tooltip.UserInspector._workersContentRoo: The slot to place user worker content when viewing a user.
messages.Tooltip.UserInspector._userText: The string field that stores text on the user being viewed.
messages.Tooltip.UserInspectorItem: The UserInspectorItem component is used to display user items of the User Inspector.
messages.Tooltip.UserInspectorItem._user: The user this item points to.
messages.Tooltip.UserInspectorItem._userNameText: The text field to display the user's name.
messages.Tooltip.UserInspectorItem._expander: The component used to expand items under the user like stream groups.
messages.Tooltip.UserInspectorItem._expanderIndic: The indicator component used to show the expand/collapse arrow.
messages.Tooltip.UserInspectorItem._childContaine: The slot to display child items of this user item.
messages.Tooltip.ValueField`1: The ValueField component contains a single field of a specific datatype that is selected when the component is attached.
messages.Tooltip.ValueField`1.Value: The value this component is storing.
messages.Tooltip.ValueFieldProxy`1: The Value Field Proxy component is used to make a field accessible via grabbing when paired with a grabbable. See #Usage for more info.
messages.Tooltip.ValueFieldProxy`1.Source: The field to relay into the grabbable behavior this component has when paired with a grabbable.
messages.Tooltip.ValueFieldProxySource`1: This is used to carry values to other UIX elements that are looking for it.
messages.Tooltip.ValueFieldProxySource`1.Field: The field to grab a value from.
messages.Tooltip.ValueProxy`1.Value: The value this component should be for grabbable objects, making them like inspector grab cards.
messages.Tooltip.ValueProxySource`1: This is used to carry values to other UIX elements that are looking for it.
messages.Tooltip.WorkerInspector: A worker inspector (as opposed to Scene Inspector) is an inspector which only shows a single worker such as a component. When you grab a component reference and click Primary it will usually create one of these inspectors. You can grab sub-objects from some components which will create one of these inspectors when clicking Primary. Some workers are only obtainable via Ref Hacking which is one of the Things to Avoid.
messages.Tooltip.WorkerInspector._targetContainer: The container which holds this component or proto component (FrooxEngine.ComponentBase).
messages.Tooltip.WorkerInspector._workerFilter: A filter to apply to the workers displayed.
messages.Tooltip.WorkerInspector._targetWorker: The component or proto component (FrooxEngine.ComponentBase) to view.
messages.Tooltip.SnapCircle: A snap circle is a component that is able to generate a point on a circle along a plane given a point by another component. Usually this is used in components like the Multi User Avatar Anchor Component to determine where to place the anchors upon clicking (click point is used in such case) When provided a point, it finds the closest point on a circle and normal and returns that to the component that called it.
messages.Tooltip.SnapCircle.Radius: How big the circle should be
messages.Tooltip.SnapCircle.Normal: which direction the circle should face
messages.Tooltip.SnapCircle.SnapParent: Where to put slots if the component is being used for slot placement.
messages.Tooltip.SnapGrid: Used as an anchor point generator in the Multi User Avatar Anchor Component and drawing tools.
messages.Tooltip.SnapGrid.BoundsSize: How big the grid is in total, and determines the total size available for grid points to take up
messages.Tooltip.SnapGrid.GridSize: How big each grid point is, or in other words, how far apart they are.
messages.Tooltip.SnapGrid.SnapParent: Where to put slots if the component is being used for slot placement.
messages.Tooltip.SnapLine: A snap line is a component that is able to generate a point in a line given a point by another component. Usually this is used in components like the Multi User Avatar Anchor Component to determine where to place the anchors upon clicking (click point is used in such case) When provided a point, it finds the closest point on a line and returns that to the component that called it.
messages.Tooltip.SnapLine.Point0: The first point to use to define the line
messages.Tooltip.SnapLine.Point1: the second point to define the line
messages.Tooltip.SnapLine.Anchor0: an override to use instead of Point0 to define the line
messages.Tooltip.SnapLine.Anchor1: an override to use instead of Point1 to define the line
messages.Tooltip.SnapLine.SnapParent: Where to put slots if the component is being used for slot placement.
messages.Tooltip.SnapPlane: A snap plane is a component that is able to generate a point in a plane shape when given a point by another component. Usually this is used in components like the Multi User Avatar Anchor Component to determine where to place the anchors upon clicking (click point is used in such case). This component will take a given point by another component and find the closest surface point to a plane with a normal + with a center positioned at the slot it is on. This component will then return the point to the component that called it.
messages.Tooltip.SnapPlane.Normal: Determines which direction the plane is facing
messages.Tooltip.SnapPlane.SnapParent: Where to put slots if the component is being used for slot placement.
messages.Tooltip.SnapPoint: A snap point is a component that when called by another component always returns the point of the slot it is on (0,0,0). Usually this is used in components like the Multi User Avatar Anchor Component to determine where to place the anchors upon clicking (click point is used in such case). This component will take a given point by another component and return the position the slot is on, acting as a nullifier of incoming data.
messages.Tooltip.SnapPoint.SnapParent: Where to put slots if the component is being used for slot placement.
messages.Tooltip.SnapSphere: Used as an anchor point generator in the Multi User Avatar Anchor Component and drawing tools.
messages.Tooltip.SnapSphere.SnapParent: where the objects to be snapped should go, if they are slots.
messages.Tooltip.CameraControlTool: The CameraControlTool component makes a tool that changes the mode of a Component:DroneCamera when it is fired at one of the colliders that is part of that component's hiearchy.
messages.Tooltip.Tool._equipLink: This is Internal, and is used by the engine to retrieve the component this field is a part of. It cannot be assigned to.
messages.Tooltip.Tool.TipReference: The slot to use as the tool's tip, instead of the component's slot. (this heavily improves desktop compatibility)
messages.Tooltip.Tool.BlockGripEquip: Whether to prevent legacy double grip equipping from equipping this tooltip.
messages.Tooltip.Tool.BlockRemoteEquip: Whether to prevent equipping by clicking via laser
messages.Tooltip.Tool.EquipName: The name of the tool in the context menu when equipping via context menu.
messages.Tooltip.Tool._overrideActiveTool: The interaction handler to use instead of this tool as an interaction handler.
messages.Tooltip.Tool._gripPosesGenerated: Whether the Grip Pose Reference components and slots have been generated for this tool.
messages.Tooltip.CameraControlTool.Mode: The mode to switch a Component:DroneCamera to when firing at it with Primary.
messages.Tooltip.CameraControlTool._material: The material to use and update the color of for the tool's cone visual.
messages.Tooltip.DevCreateNewForm.OnCreated: The Sync delegate to call when an item is created.
messages.Tooltip.DevCreateNewForm.CategoryRoot: The current location in the create menu we are at.
messages.Tooltip.DevCreateNewForm._contentRoot: Where to list content like buttons and such.
messages.Tooltip.DevTool: Detailed information can be located at Dev Tool.
messages.Tooltip.DevTool._selectedAnchor: The point anchor which is part of a gizmo this tool is moving currently.
messages.Tooltip.DevTool._selectedAnchorHighlight: The slot being used to indicate what gizmo is currently being moved. Is an icosphere.
messages.Tooltip.DevTool._material: The visual material used for the default cone mesh.
messages.Tooltip.DevTool._currentGizmo: The gizmo that the dev tool is currently targeting for gizmo options.
messages.Tooltip.DevTool._previousGizmo: The previous gizmo that the dev tool targeted for gizmo options.
messages.Tooltip.AudioPlayerInterface: The AudioPlayerInterface component is used on AudioClip objects that are spawned in by the user to allow the engine to interface with the stored audio clip.
messages.Tooltip.EntityInterface.ItemName: The name of the stream like Frooxius's Stream
messages.Tooltip.EntityInterface.SpawningUser: The user that spawned this favoritable interface.
messages.Tooltip.EntityInterface.SpawningUserID: The field that contains the user ID of the user that spawned this document viewer.
messages.Tooltip.EntityInterface.IsInstance: Whether this audio clip interface is a spawned interface or one being edited currently by the user. Usually set to true by the game when loaded as a user's favorite audio clip player
messages.Tooltip.AudioPlayerInterface.URL: The field of the URI on the Type:AudioClip this component interfaces with.
messages.Tooltip.AudioPlayerInterface.Clip: The Type:AudioClip interfaced by this component.
messages.Tooltip.AudioPlayerInterface.Group: The audio group setting for this audio clip.
messages.Tooltip.AudioPlayerInterface.Volume: The volume setting of this audio clip.
messages.Tooltip.AudioPlayerInterface.Spatialize: The Spatialize setting of the audio clip.
messages.Tooltip.AudioPlayerInterface.Doppler: The Doppler setting of the audio clip.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamInterface: The AudioStreamInterface component is used on AudioStream objects that are spawned in by the user via the dash to allow the engine to interface with the stored audio stream.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamInterface.Source: The audio stream this interfaces with
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamInterface.Bitrate: The Bitrate setting of the spawned audiostream interface (like a UIX)
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamInterface.Volume: The Volume slider/setting of the spawned audio stream interface (like a UIX)
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamInterface.Spatialize: The Spatialize setting of the spawned audio stream interface (like a UIX)
messages.Tooltip.ColorDialogInterface: The ColorDialogInterface component is generated by the game when a user interacts with choosing a color.
messages.Tooltip.ColorDialogInterface.TargetColor: The color this color editor is editing.
messages.Tooltip.ColorDialogInterface.Continuous: Whether changes to the color should instantly update the target color.
messages.Tooltip.ColorDialogInterface.AllowAlpha: Whether the color editor should allow transparency modification
messages.Tooltip.ColorDialogInterface.AllowHDR: Whether this color editor should allow for making colors beyond 100%.
messages.Tooltip.DocumentInterface: The DocumentInterface component is a Favorites Type Component that is used in the default or custom document viewers.
messages.Tooltip.DocumentInterface.URL: The URL field of the document asset component this document viewer is showing.
messages.Tooltip.DocumentInterface.Page: The field that controls which page this document viewer is currently showing.
messages.Tooltip.HyperlinkDisplayInterface: The HyperlinkDisplayInterface component is spawned by the favorites system when a user pastes a link that is recognized as a website link rather than a specific media type.
messages.Tooltip.HyperlinkDisplayInterface.Url: The field to fill with the URL this hyperlink display should leas to.
messages.Tooltip.HyperlinkDisplayInterface.UrlStr: The field to fill with the URL as a string so the hyperlink display can show what it leads to.
messages.Tooltip.NamePlateInterface: The NamePlateInterface component is a favoritable type component that allows a user to set their custom nameplate for avatars they spawn into
messages.Tooltip.NamePlateInterface.Username: The field to set with the username of the user who this nameplate has been spawned onto.
messages.Tooltip.NamePlateInterface.UserID: The field to set with the UserID of the user who this nameplate has been spawned onto.
messages.Tooltip.NamePlateInterface.IconURL: The field to set with the User profile icon of the user who this nameplate has been spawned onto.
messages.Tooltip.NamePlateInterface.TargetUser: The field to set with the user this nameplate has been spawned onto.
messages.Tooltip.NamePlateInterface.TargetUserRef: A user reference block to store info about the user this nameplate has been spawned onto.
messages.Tooltip.NamePlateInterface.VoiceStream: The field to populate with the user voice stream of the user this nameplate has been spawned onto.
messages.Tooltip.NoticeDisplayInterface: The NoticeDisplayInterface component allows the user to make custom notification dialogues or notification banners by saving this component as part of an item's root and then setting it as the user's notification interface.
messages.Tooltip.NoticeDisplayInterface.Heading: The field to populate with what the notification header should be.
messages.Tooltip.NoticeDisplayInterface.Icon: The field to populate with what the icon url should be for the notification.
messages.Tooltip.NoticeDisplayInterface.Text: The field to populate with the notification text.
messages.Tooltip.NoticeDisplayInterface.Color: The field to populate with the color that the notification should be.
messages.Tooltip.ProgressBarInterface: The ProgressBarInterface component is a favoritable item that appears when the user imports a file into Resonite.
messages.Tooltip.ProgressBarInterface.ProgressKno: The field to drive with whether or not the progress is known of the importing item.
messages.Tooltip.ProgressBarInterface.Progress: The field to drive with the current progress import percentage from 0 to 1 of the importing item.
messages.Tooltip.ProgressBarInterface.ProcessedCo: The field to drive with whether or not the amount of processed items is known for the importing item.
messages.Tooltip.ProgressBarInterface.ProcessedIt: The field to drive with the number of processed items for the importing item.
messages.Tooltip.ProgressBarInterface.TotalItemCo: The field to drive with whether the total number of items that need to be processed for the importing item is known.
The field to drive with the total number of items that need to be processed for the importing item.
messages.Tooltip.ProgressBarInterface.PhaseName: The field to drive with the current phase name of the importing item being imported is.
messages.Tooltip.ProgressBarInterface.SubPhaseNam: The field to drive with the current sub phase name of the item being imported.
messages.Tooltip.ProgressBarInterface.Stage: The field to drive with the progress stage for the importing item.
messages.Tooltip.ProgressBarInterface.HasComplete: The field to drive with whether the item has finished importing.
messages.Tooltip.ProgressBarInterface.HasFailed: The field to drive with whether the item has failed to import.
messages.Tooltip.ProgressBarInterface.CompletionM: The field to drive with the message for when the item has finished importing.
messages.Tooltip.ProgressBarInterface.FailureReas: The field to drive with the failure reason for something to import.
messages.Tooltip.ProgressBarInterface.CanBeHidden: The field to drive with whether this import dialogue can be hidden by the user.
messages.Tooltip.TextDisplayInterface: The TextDisplayInterface component is used to specify what the user's favorite display of clipboard contents should be when pasting text not into a text field and the text is not a link.
messages.Tooltip.TextDisplayInterface.Heading: The field to fill with what the header text is upon spawn.
messages.Tooltip.TextDisplayInterface.Text: The field to fill with what the text the user pasted was upon spawn.
messages.Tooltip.TextDisplayInterface.RTF: The field to fill with whether the text should be interpreted as Rich Text Format. This is usually the RTF field on a text rendering Component for the UI.
messages.Tooltip.VideoPlayerInterface: The VideoPlayerInterface component is a favoritable item that is used to specify the UI elements of a custom Video Player.
messages.Tooltip.VideoPlayerInterface.URL: The field to fill with the URL of the imported video asset.
messages.Tooltip.VideoPlayerInterface.Stream: The field to fill with whether this video player is showing stream.
messages.Tooltip.VideoPlayerInterface.VideoClip: The field to fill with what the video being displayed is.
messages.Tooltip.VideoPlayerInterface.VideoClipTe: The field to fill (Usually a material texture field) with the showing video texture.
messages.Tooltip.VideoPlayerInterface.AspectRatio: The field to fill with what the video's aspect ratio is.
messages.Tooltip.VideoPlayerInterface.DefaultVide: The default video texture to use if one isn't filled in.
messages.Tooltip.VideoPlayerInterface.StereoRende: The field to fill with whether the video is a stero video (3D)
messages.Tooltip.VideoPlayerInterface.StereoLayou: The field to fill with the kind of stero image layout the image has.
messages.Tooltip.VideoPlayerInterface.StereoTrans: The field to fill with what the rectangle area for the left eye should be.
The field to fill with what the rectangle area for the right eye should be.
The field to fill with what the UV scale for the left eye should be.
The field to fill with what the UV offset for the left eye should be.
The field to fill with what the UV scale for the right eye should be.
The field to fill with what the UV offset for the right eye should be.
messages.Tooltip.VideoPlayerInterface.PanoramicRe: The field to fill with whether the video is a 360 video
messages.Tooltip.VideoPlayerInterface.PanoramicHo: The field to fill with the video's horizontal 360 video FOV.
messages.Tooltip.VideoPlayerInterface.PanoramicVe: The field to fill with the video's vertical 360 video FOV.
messages.Tooltip.VideoPlayerInterface.PanoramicPr: The field to fill with what the video's panoramic texture arrangement is.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface: The WorldLoadingProgressInterface component is the key component in the creation of a user-definable world loading progress bar. See Favorites for favoritable items like this one.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.Pr: Float field where 0.0 - 1.0 progress will be written.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.Wo: String field where the loading world's name will be written.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.St: The field to write with the current progress stage of the currently loading world. This is written by the world loading main process.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.Ph: String field where the current loading phase name will be written.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.Su: String field where the current loading sub-phase name will be written.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.Ha: Boolean field that will be set to true on loading completion.
Boolean field that will be set to true on loading failure.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.Co: String field where message indicating completion will be written.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.Fa: String field where message indicating reason for loading failure will be written.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.Lo: Boolean field that is set true while loading assets.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.To: Integer field counting total assets to be loaded.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.Lo: Integer field counting loaded ITexture2D assets.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.To: Integer field counting total ITexture2D assets to be loaded.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.Lo: Integer field counting loaded ITexture3D assets.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.To: Integer field counting total ITexture3D assets to be loaded.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.Lo: Integer field counting loaded Cubemap assets.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.To: Integer field counting total Cubemap assets to be loaded.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.Lo: Integer field counting loaded Video assets.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.To: Integer field counting total Video assets to be loaded.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.Lo: Integer field counting loaded Mesh assets.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.To: Integer field counting total Mesh assets to be loaded.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.Lo: Integer field counting loaded Audio assets.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.To: Integer field counting total Audio assets to be loaded.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.Lo: Integer field counting loaded Shader assets.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.To: Integer field counting total Shader assets to be loaded.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.Lo: Integer field counting loaded Font assets.
