messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.To: Integer field counting total Font assets to be loaded.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.By: Long field counting total bytes downloaded so far.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.To: Long field count of total bytes expected to be downloaded.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.By: Long field indicating current Bytes-per-Second download rate.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadingProgressInterface.Pr: Reference to a Component:ProgressBarInterface component to use as the progress Bae visual.
messages.Tooltip.FileVisual: The FileVisual component is used to handle the creation and interfacing with a file object, which is spawned into the world from inventory or using the "Raw" option when Importing.
messages.Tooltip.FileVisual.MetadataSource: The component that handles importing and storing the file data this component is representing the visual of.
messages.Tooltip.FileVisual.TypeLabel: The label that determines the file type/extension (aka the "png" part of "hello.png")
messages.Tooltip.FileVisual.NameLabel: The name of the file before the extension (aka the "hello" part of "hello.png")
messages.Tooltip.FileVisual.FillMaterial: The material being used for the file icon in the center.
messages.Tooltip.FileVisual.OutlineMaterial: The material being used to outline the file icon.
messages.Tooltip.FileVisual.TypeMaterial: The material being used to color the file type. this can be orange for models, and the color varies based on file type.
messages.Tooltip.MazeGenerator: Great for prototyping mazes, and can be baked.
messages.Tooltip.MazeGenerator.Seed: The random seed for generating this maze.
messages.Tooltip.MazeGenerator.Cells: The maze square sections for this generated maze.
messages.Tooltip.MazeGenerator.WallSize: X: how long the walls are, Y: how tall the walls are.
messages.Tooltip.MazeGenerator.Point0: The start position for this maze.
messages.Tooltip.MazeGenerator.Point1: The end position for this maze.
messages.Tooltip.MazeGenerator.Material: The material for the walls of the maze.
messages.Tooltip.BezierCurve: Bezier curve is used along with Bezier Tube Mesh to define the array of curve points that control the mesh's shape. See Bezier Tube Mesh for a full example.
messages.Tooltip.BezierCurve.Points: A list of controlling Curve Points.
messages.Tooltip.BezierCurve.CoordinateSpace: The Coordinate Space in which to do curve calculations in.
messages.Tooltip.BezierCurve.AssignCurveData: The list to fill with curve data, which is the <mark=#ffffff10>Points</mark> field of a Bezier Tube Mesh.
messages.Tooltip.CurvePoint: Curve point is used by being put into a Bezier Curve component to define the shape of a Bezier Tube Mesh. See Bezier Tube Mesh for a full example.
messages.Tooltip.CurvePoint.LeftTangentSource: The left handle of the bezier curve point
messages.Tooltip.CurvePoint.RightTangentSource: The right handle of the bezier curve point
messages.Tooltip.PointClusterMesh: The PointClusterMesh component generates a randomized cluster of points in a cloud. These points can be displayed via a Mesh Renderer using a Unlit Material with at least it's UseBillboardGeometry field set to true.
messages.Tooltip.PointClusterMesh.Seed: The number to use to seed the generation of random points.
messages.Tooltip.PointClusterMesh.Points: The number of points to generate.
messages.Tooltip.PointClusterMesh.Scale: The scale of the generated mesh.
messages.Tooltip.PointClusterMesh.JitterRange: The maximum value to randomly offset each point.
messages.Tooltip.PointClusterMesh.UniformSize: Whether all points should always be uniformly scaled.
messages.Tooltip.PointClusterMesh.MinSize: The minimum size of a point.
messages.Tooltip.PointClusterMesh.MaxSize: The maximum size of a point.
messages.Tooltip.PointClusterMesh.MinRotation: The minimum rotation of a point.
messages.Tooltip.PointClusterMesh.MaxRotation: The maximum rotation of a point.
messages.Tooltip.ClipboardImporter: Clipboard importer is used by the game as a manager for pasting items and text from the clipboard. Without it, pasting text isn't possible in a world until it is added back to the world.
messages.Tooltip.FolderImportDialog: The FolderImportDialog component is used to make and handle the importing of a folder with the specified path. Generates a UIX upon attach.
messages.Tooltip.FolderImportDialog.Path: The folder path to Import the contents of.
messages.Tooltip.WizardForm.path: A list of sync delegates used to generate the UI when at particular paths in the importer.
messages.Tooltip.WizardForm._contentRoot: The slot to put ui elements for import options in.
messages.Tooltip.ImageImportDialog._projection: What image projection to import the image as.
messages.Tooltip.ImageImportDialog._layout: The image stereo layout for left and right to use.
messages.Tooltip.ImageImportDialog._screenshot: whether to import the image as a screenshot.
messages.Tooltip.ImageImportDialog._pointFilterin: Whether to import the image as pixel art or not.
messages.Tooltip.ImageImportDialog._uncompressed: Whether to import the image as uncompressed or not.
messages.Tooltip.ImageImportDialog._alphaBleed: Whether to import the image with alpha bleed reduction or not.
messages.Tooltip.ImageImportDialog._lut: Whether this image is a Look Up Table.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog: The ModelImportDialog component is better explained at Importing.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._scale: The scale of the model being imported.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._autoScale: Whether to use auto scale to humanoid height
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._material: What kind of material to import the materials for the model as.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._preferSpecula: Whether the importer should prefer to use specular materials.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._rig: Whether the model has a rig or not.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._setupIK: Whether IK should be set up.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._debugRig: Whether to debug the rig bones after import.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._colliders: Whether to add auto generated colliders.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._snappable: Whether to import the model as separate snappable pieces
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._timelapse: Whether to import animations as timelapses
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._externalTextu: Whether to look for external textures when importing
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._grabbable: Whether to import the model as grabbable
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._scalable: Whether to import the model as scalable.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._importAtOrigi: Whether to import the model centered at the world origin
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._forceTpose: Whether to enforce a t-pose on an IK model when importing
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._assetsOnObjec: Whether to place the asset components on the avatar itself under a list of slots, or place them under the world assets and have them be optimized automatically.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._asPointCloud: Whether to import as a point cloud.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._importImagesB: Whether to import images by name.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._importImageAl: Which axis import the image objects along.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._calculateNorm: Whether to calculate new normals on import.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._calculateTang: Whether to calculate new tangents on import.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._calculateText: Whether to calculate new texture alpha/transparency on import.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._importVertexC: Whether to import vertex color data for the model.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._importAlbedoC: Whether to import material solid albedo color data on import
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._importEmissiv: Whether to import material solid emissive color data on import.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._importBones: Whether to import model rig bones on import.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._importLights: Whether to import model light objects on import.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._makeDualSided: Whether to import the model as dual sided geometry.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._makeFlatShade: Whether to import the model geometry as flat shaded rather than smooth shaded.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._deduplicateIn: Whether to deduplicate duplicate data on the mesh like cubes or models in the same place and size.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._optimizeModel: Whether to do some small optimizations to the model or not.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._splitSubmeshe: Whether to split the model by material or not.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._generateRando: Whether to generate random colors per material for identification or not.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._spawnMaterial: Whether to spawn material orb objects per material for easy access.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._maxTextureSiz: The max texture size to restrict textures to on the model during import.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._textureConver: What kind of texture format to convert imported textures to.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._forcePointFil: Whether to force point filtering on textures (Pixel art)
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._forceNoMipMap: Whether to disable texture resolution reduction by distance optimization.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._forceUncompre: Whether to disable compression on assets and textures or not.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog.ForceAsPointCl: Whether to enforce importing the model as a point cloud or not.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog.PotentialGauss: Whether to interpret the data as a gaussian splat.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._flipY: Whether to flip the gaussian splat on the Y axis.
messages.Tooltip.ModelImportDialog._encodeSPZ: Whether the gaussian splat is an SPZ format.
messages.Tooltip.VideoImportDialog: The VideoImportDialog component is better explained in the Importing page.
messages.Tooltip.VideoImportDialog._videoType: The type of video being imported.
messages.Tooltip.VideoImportDialog._stereoLayout: The kind of video stereo layout the video being imported has.
messages.Tooltip.VideoImportDialog._depthPreset: The kind of depth the video being imported has.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager: The PhotoCaptureManager component is used to manage the finger photo capture ability for users and what settings to use for it.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager.FingerGestur: Whether the finger photo gesture can be used or not.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager.MinDistance: The distance from the view to use for minimum FOV point.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager.MaxDistance: The distance from the view to use for maximum FOV point.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager.MinFOV: The minimum FOV that can be achieved by moving the finger photo closer to the viewpoint.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager.MaxFOV: The maximum FOV that can be achieved by moving the finger photo further from the viewpoint.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager.PreviewResol: The resolution of the texture shown on the preview graphic.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager.NormalResolu: The resolution of the picture taken normally.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager.TimerResolut: The resolution of the picture taken via camera.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager.CaptureStere: Whether the finger photo should capture a 3D photo.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager.StereoSepara: How much to separate the L and R "eye" positions for the camera capture.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager.TimerSeconds: How many seconds the finger photo timer should be.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager.HideAllNamep: Whether to hide all nameplates for taking the photo.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager.EncodeFormat: What format to encode the resulting picture in.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager.DebugGesture: Whether to debug the user's gesture angles/position for achieving a valid finger photo gesture position.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager._timerStart: What time the last timer started at.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager._timerActive: Whether the timer is currently going or not.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager._originalPar: The original parent of this manager.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager._originalPos: The original position of this manager.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager._originalRot: The original rotation of this manager.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager._originalSca: The original scale of this manager.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager._root: The root of this entire finger photo manager.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager._previewRoot: The slot that is the root of the preview graphic.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager._renderTex: The texture being used for the preview render graphic.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager._quad: The quad to render the preview with.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager._frame: The frame mesh being used for the preview graphic.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager._cameraRoot: The slot being used for the camera's transforms.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager._cameraPos: The position field of the camera.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager._cameraRot: The rotation field of the camera.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager._frameMateri: The material being used for the frame visual for the preview graphic.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager._timerTextRo: The root slot of the visual being used for the timer text.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager._timerText: The component being used to display the timer text.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager._shutterClip: The audio to play when a photo is taken.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager._timerStartC: The audio to play when the timer starts.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager._timerCountd: The audio to play when the timer has plenty of time left.
The audio to play when the timer is close to being finished.
The audio output component outputting the timer has plenty of time sound.
The audio output component outputting the timer is close to being done sound.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureManager._timerRoot: The root slot of the entire timer visual and mechanism.
messages.Tooltip.TrackedHand: The TrackedHand component controls the grabbing and updating of a hand including camera based hand tracking of a user.
messages.Tooltip.TrackedHand.User: The user this tracked hand belongs to.
messages.Tooltip.TrackedHand.HandChirality: The hand side this tracked hand corresponds to.
messages.Tooltip.TrackedHand._grabber: The grabber component this hand is controlling.
messages.Tooltip.ArmHapticPointMapper: The Arm Haptic Point Mapper works as part of the game's robust Haptics system to map haptics points in real life for the arm to the in game arms.
messages.Tooltip.HapticPointMapper.Priority: The priority of this over other haptic elements
messages.Tooltip.HapticPointMapper.ShowDebugVisua: Whether to show the area visual of the Mapper and the different haptics mapped to it.
messages.Tooltip.BoneChainHapticPointMapper.BoneC: A list of bones that make up the center spine of the avatar. (Hips, Spine(s), Chest, upper chest)
messages.Tooltip.BoneChainHapticPointMapper.Norma: The start of where haptics points should be placed based on a normalized position from 0<->1 along the length of the arm, if all the bones were rotated to be along a straight line.
The end of where haptics points should be placed based on a normalized position from 0<->1 along the length of the arm, if all the bones were rotated to be along a straight line.
messages.Tooltip.LimbHapticPointMapper`1.Side: Specifies which controller/hand this component gives haptic data to.
messages.Tooltip.LimbHapticPointMapper`1.UpAxis: The axis on the arm which determines which way is up per bone, which is used to map haptic points from real life to in game.
messages.Tooltip.LimbHapticPointMapper`1.ForwardA: The axis on the arm which determines which way is forward per bone, which is used to map haptic points from real life to in game.
messages.Tooltip.ArmHapticPointMapper.HandRadius: The radius of the hand in which to map haptics points for.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarHapticSourceManager: The AvatarHapticSourceManager component allows you to override the injected haptic points that Resonite puts on fullbody avatars and to specify custom Haptic Volumes on your body.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarHapticSourceManager.Haptic: Drives the boolean field put in the list to be disabled when noclipping usually
messages.Tooltip.ControllerHapticPointMapper: The ControllerHapticPointMapper component allows you to specify a point where you feel your controller haptics, this can be useful in cases where you don't feel touching haptic volumes like objects or other user's avatars line up right.
messages.Tooltip.ControllerHapticPointMapper.Side: Specifies which controller/hand this component gives haptic data to.
messages.Tooltip.DirectTagHapticSource: The DirectTagHapticsSource component allows driving haptic sensations for a given haptic point directly, rather than using the haptic volume system.
messages.Tooltip.DirectTagHapticSource.Force: The force addition for haptics with the <mark=#ffffff10>HapticTag</mark> tag.
messages.Tooltip.DirectTagHapticSource.Temperatur: The temperature addition for haptics with the <mark=#ffffff10>HapticTag</mark> tag.
messages.Tooltip.DirectTagHapticSource.Pain: The pain addition for haptics with the <mark=#ffffff10>HapticTag</mark> tag.
messages.Tooltip.DirectTagHapticSource.Vibration: The vibration addition for haptics with the <mark=#ffffff10>HapticTag</mark> tag.
messages.Tooltip.AxisDistanceHapticFilter: The AxisDistanceHapticFilter multiplicatively changes the intensity of a Component:HapticVolume depending on the dot product from the vector of the device's position in local space to this component and Axis. Essentially the smaller the angle between the device local position from 0 direction and the Axis direction the smaller the distance.
messages.Tooltip.AxisDistanceHapticFilter.Axis: The axis to check distance from.
messages.Tooltip.AxisDistanceHapticFilter.StartDi: The starting distance to map to <mark=#ffffff10>StartIntensity</mark> for the intensity distance range.
messages.Tooltip.AxisDistanceHapticFilter.EndDist: The ending distance to map to <mark=#ffffff10>EndIntensity</mark> for the intensity distance range.
messages.Tooltip.AxisDistanceHapticFilter.StartIn: When at <mark=#ffffff10>StartDistance</mark> map the device intensity to this.
messages.Tooltip.AxisDistanceHapticFilter.EndInte: When at <mark=#ffffff10>EndDistance</mark> map the device intensity to this.
messages.Tooltip.AxisDistanceHapticFilter.Power: How much to amplify the haptics effect in general.
messages.Tooltip.CylindricalDistanceHapticFilter: The CylindricalDistanceHapticFilter component filters haptic device influences multiplicatively depending on their position within a range of 2 cylinders. One being an inner and one being an outer. Both of them are invisible though. This component works when used with a Component:HapticVolume.
The starting radius of the intensity gradient based on distance from the cylinders center line from end to end.
The ending radius of the intensity gradient based on distance from the cylinders center line from end to end.
The starting intensity of the intensity gradient based on distance from the cylinders center line from end to end.
The ending intensity of the intensity gradient based on distance from the cylinders center line from end to end.
The amplification of the intensity gradient based on distance from the cylinders center line from end to end.
Used to define the distance from the cylinder end cap to start the intensity gradient.
Used to define the distance from the cylinder end cap to end the intensity gradient.
The starting intensity value for the intensity gradient based on the distance to the cylinder cap.
The ending intensity value for the intensity gradient based on the distance to the cylinder cap.
How much to amplify the intensity of the intensity gradient based on the distance to the cylinder cap.
messages.Tooltip.ImpactTimeHapticFilter: The ImpactTimeHapticFilter component is a haptics filter mainly for Component:HapticVolumes change their intensity based on how long the user's hand has been inside of the affecting area.
messages.Tooltip.ImpactTimeHapticFilter.UseGlobal: Whether to use global time or local time.
messages.Tooltip.ImpactTimeHapticFilter.StartTime: When the user's hand has been here for this amount of time, use <mark=#ffffff10>StartIntensity</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ImpactTimeHapticFilter.EndTime: When the user's hand has been here for this amount of time, use <mark=#ffffff10>EndIntensity</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ImpactTimeHapticFilter.StartInte: The intensity to use at the start of the time frame.
messages.Tooltip.ImpactTimeHapticFilter.EndIntens: The intensity to use at the end of the time frame.
messages.Tooltip.ImpactTimeHapticFilter.Power: How much to amplify the overall intensity effect of this component.
messages.Tooltip.RadialDistanceHapticFilter: The RadialDistanceHapticFilter controls haptic areas on the same slot so that haptics within their range are influenced differently depending on how far away they are from the slot center in local space. The start and end intensities when mapped to the haptic devices Distance from the slot, the power is unclamped.
messages.Tooltip.RadialDistanceHapticFilter.Start: The starting radius point (Usually 0 distance). When the haptic device point is this Distance away from the slot this component is on in local space, the haptic gets a signal of <mark=#ffffff10>Power</mark>*<mark=#ffffff10>StartIntensity</mark>
messages.Tooltip.RadialDistanceHapticFilter.EndRa: The ending radius point (Usually 0 distance). When the haptic device point is this Distance away from the slot this component is on in local space, the haptic gets a signal of <mark=#ffffff10>Power</mark>*<mark=#ffffff10>StartIntensity</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.RadialDistanceHapticFilter.Start: The intensity*<mark=#ffffff10>Power</mark> for the haptic device when it is <mark=#ffffff10>StartRadius</mark> from the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.RadialDistanceHapticFilter.EndIn: The intensity*<mark=#ffffff10>Power</mark> for the haptic device when it is <mark=#ffffff10>EndRadius</mark> from the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.RadialDistanceHapticFilter.Power: How much to amplify the intensity for the haptic device.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexNoiseHapticFilter: The SimplexNoiseHapticFilter makes the space within a collider with haptic volume act like a 3d noise kind of like moving your haptics device through clouds.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexNoiseHapticFilter.NoiseOf: The offset of the noise from 0.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexNoiseHapticFilter.MinValu: The minimum haptics power the noise can give based on the 3d noise algorithm.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexNoiseHapticFilter.MaxValu: The maximum haptics power the noise can give based on the 3d noise algorithm.
messages.Tooltip.SineHapticFilter: The SineHapticFilter component is used to make haptics triggered by Components on the same slot change in intensity over time like its riding waves.
messages.Tooltip.SineHapticFilter.UseGlobalTime: Whether to use the global time or the initial local time for initial time when touched.
messages.Tooltip.SineHapticFilter.DistanceScale: An optional value on how to add to the offset in time for the waves of intensity based on distance of the haptic device from the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.SineHapticFilter.AxisScale: An optional value on how to add to the offset in time for the waves of intensity based on how close it is to a line direction in local space.
messages.Tooltip.SineHapticFilter.MinIntensity: The minimum haptics intensity this component will send.
messages.Tooltip.SineHapticFilter.MaxIntensity: The maximum haptics intensity this component will send.
messages.Tooltip.SineHapticFilter.Frequency: The speed at which the sinewave goes up and down over time.
messages.Tooltip.ValueNoiseHapticFilter: The ValueNoiseHapticFilter component makes a random intensity value every update to a device touching a Component:HapticVolume.
messages.Tooltip.ValueNoiseHapticFilter.MinValue: The minimum haptic intensity to output in a game tick.
messages.Tooltip.ValueNoiseHapticFilter.MaxValue: The maximum haptic intensity to output in a game tick.
messages.Tooltip.VelocityHapticFilter: The VelocityHapticFilter component is used to influence the haptics a user feels on a haptics device as the device moves through a Component:HapticVolume at different speeds.
messages.Tooltip.VelocityHapticFilter.VelocitySmo: How much to smooth the changes in velocity this component works with.
messages.Tooltip.VelocityHapticFilter.StartIntens: The intensity to use when a haptics device is moving at <mark=#ffffff10>StartVelocity</mark> through a valid Component:HapticVolume on the same slot.
messages.Tooltip.VelocityHapticFilter.EndIntensit: The intensity to use when a haptics device is moving at <mark=#ffffff10>EndVelocity</mark> through a valid Component:HapticVolume on the same slot.
messages.Tooltip.VelocityHapticFilter.Power: The multiplier of the haptics intensity before applying it.
messages.Tooltip.HapticManager: This works as part of the game's robust Haptics system.
messages.Tooltip.HapticManager.InjectedBodyPartIn: Adjusts the haptic intensity of bodyparts that aren't the head and hands.
messages.Tooltip.HapticManager.InjectedHandIntens: Adjusts the haptic intensity of the hands.
messages.Tooltip.HapticManager.InjectedHeadIntens: Adjusts the haptic intensity of the head.
messages.Tooltip.HapticManager.InjectedRadiusStar: Adjusts the start radius of all injected haptics.
messages.Tooltip.HapticManager.InjectedRadiusPowe: Adjusts the overall radius of all injected haptics.
messages.Tooltip.HapticManager.ShowDebugVisuals: Locally shows injected haptics for each user, Rebuild Injected Sources needs to be checked for this to work.
messages.Tooltip.HapticPointData: The HapticPointData component gets data about a haptics device on a particular user.
messages.Tooltip.HapticPointData.Index: The device index to get info on.
messages.Tooltip.HapticPointData.User: The user to get a device of <mark=#ffffff10>Index</mark> from.
messages.Tooltip.HapticPointData.TotalActivationI: The total activation intensity of the haptic's device.
messages.Tooltip.HapticPointSampler: The HapticPointSampler component acts as the receiver of haptics events and the in game world point for a haptics device recognized by the Resonite Haptics system.
messages.Tooltip.HapticPointSamplerBase.Radius: How big the haptics sampling radius is.
messages.Tooltip.HapticPointSamplerBase.ShowDebug: Whether to show the debug visual for this sampler.
messages.Tooltip.HapticPointSamplerBase._debugVis: the material on the sphere of the current generated debug visual. Is null when <mark=#ffffff10>ShowDebugVisual</mark> is false.
messages.Tooltip.HapticPointSampler.HapticPointIn: The haptics device by index on <mark=#ffffff10>SamplingUser</mark>'s input interface to send haptics sampled from this device to.
messages.Tooltip.HapticVolume: Haptic Volumes allows a user to feel haptics through a collider set to Haptic Trigger shared on the same slot.
messages.Tooltip.HapticVolume.Sensation: The type of haptic you want to do, for controllers the haptic types are subtly different variations. Specifying this is useful for haptic suits that have more advanced haptic motors.
messages.Tooltip.HapticVolume.Intensity: The haptic intensity ranging from zero to one, zero being none one being max that the device allows.
messages.Tooltip.HapticVolume.SensationHints: Allows triggering specific predefined sensations with certain haptic devices. Only useful for OWO haptic vests currently.
messages.Tooltip.HeadHapticPointMapper: The HeadHapticPointMapper component handles mapping haptic points around and to the head area of a user.
messages.Tooltip.HeadHapticPointMapper.HeadCenter: The center offset of the head area compared to the slot this component is on (the head slot)
messages.Tooltip.HeadHapticPointMapper._debugVisu: The root slot of the current Debug visual.
messages.Tooltip.LegHapticPointMapper: The Leg Haptic Point Mapper works as part of the game's robust Haptics system to map haptics points in real life for the leg to the in game legs.
messages.Tooltip.LegHapticPointMapper.UpperLegRad: The radius of the upper leg, where haptics points should be placed on the surface, radii is lerped between the upper leg and knee.
messages.Tooltip.LegHapticPointMapper.KneeRadius: The radius of the knee, where haptics points should be placed on the surface, radii is lerped between the upper leg and knee.
messages.Tooltip.LegHapticPointMapper.AnkleRadius: The radius of the upper leg, where haptics points should be placed on the surface, radii is lerped between the knee and ankle.
messages.Tooltip.TagHapticPointMapper: The TagHapticPointMapper allows for defining custom mapping on avatars.
messages.Tooltip.TagHapticPointMapper.HapticPoint: A list of haptic points with tags and where they go.
messages.Tooltip.TorsoHapticPointMapper: The TorsoHapticPointMapper component is used to tell the game the size of an Avatar's torso and its bones in order for the game to know where to place haptic point samplers so they better match the size and positions of the body.
messages.Tooltip.TorsoHapticPointMapper.LowerWidt: The width of the lower spine.
messages.Tooltip.TorsoHapticPointMapper.UpperWidt: The width of the upper spine.
messages.Tooltip.TorsoHapticPointMapper.FrontOffs: How far the front side is from the spine bones.
messages.Tooltip.TorsoHapticPointMapper.BackOffse: How far the back side is from the spine bones.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualHapticPointSampler: Virtual Haptic Point sampler is a component that is used as a way of picking up haptic sensations from the virtual environment inside Resonite, without needing a haptic device.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualHapticPointSampler.Force: The force sensations picked up by this haptic point sampler.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualHapticPointSampler.Pain: The pain sensations picked up by this haptic point sampler.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualHapticPointSampler.Temper: The temperature sensations picked up by this haptic point sampler.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualHapticPointSampler.Vibrat: the vibration sensations picked up by this haptic point sampler.
messages.Tooltip.HierarchyHapticsSource: The HiearchyHapticsSource component every Interval seconds triggers a relative intensity haptics signal (Additive) of RelativeIntensity.
messages.Tooltip.HierarchyHapticsSource.TargetHie: The hiearchy of haptics to trigger. If targeting an arm slot on an avatar, triggers all haptics under the arm and hand. For a VR user this would get their controller.
messages.Tooltip.HierarchyHapticsSource.Interval: The amount of seconds to wait per trigger. Fires automatically every this many seconds.
messages.Tooltip.HierarchyHapticsSource.RelativeI: The relative haptics intensity amount to send each trigger.
messages.Tooltip.PointerInteractionController: The PointerInteractionController component is used by the game internally to handle tip touch source behavior on a user.
messages.Tooltip.TouchSource.AutoUpdateUser: which user should be updating this component's logic. Defaults to active user if not filled.
messages.Tooltip.TouchSource.OutOfSightAngle: disables the function of this component if the updating user is looking more than this many degrees away from this component's slot.
messages.Tooltip.TouchSource.MaxTouchPenetrationD: The max distance of penetration into an item like a physical button before it's not considered pressed anymore.
messages.Tooltip.PointTouchSource.Offset: The offset of where the tip touch sources touch effects start from the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.PointTouchSource.Direction: The direction in which to interact with touchable elements using this component.
messages.Tooltip.PointTouchSource.MaxDistance: the maximum distance before hovering doesn't register
messages.Tooltip.RelayTouchSource: The RelayTouchSource component is used to handle the interactions of tip touch sources on the user.
messages.Tooltip.RelayTouchSource.TipPositionGett: Refers to a InteractionLaser's sync delegate of the same name.
messages.Tooltip.RelayTouchSource.TipDirectionGet: Refers to a InteractionLaser's sync delegate of the same name.
messages.Tooltip.RelayTouchSource.TouchTypeGetter: Refers to a InteractionLaser's sync delegate of the same name.
messages.Tooltip.RelayTouchSource.TouchableGetter: Refers to a InteractionLaser's sync delegate of the same name.
messages.Tooltip.TipTouchSource: The TipTouchSource component is responsible for interaction with touchable objects like buttons and touch buttons. This component gains functionality after being activated by a Component:AvatarHandDataAssigner upon avatar equip.
messages.Tooltip.TipTouchSource.UseUserSpaceForDi: whether to use local space for distance or use absolute world distance for distance.
messages.Tooltip.TipTouchSource.TouchDistance: how far in meters that this can touch things
messages.Tooltip.TipTouchSource.MaxDistance: the maximum distance before hovering doesn't register
messages.Tooltip.TipTouchSource.Offset: the offset for everything this component does from the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.TipTouchSource.Direction: the direction this can touch things.
messages.Tooltip.VibrationDeviceRelay: The VibrationDeviceRelay component recieves haptics events and relays them to other vibration recievers.
messages.Tooltip.VibrationDeviceRelay.TargetCompo: The component to relay the vibration event to.
messages.Tooltip.VibrationDeviceRelay.DynamicLook: The slot to look for components in to send the vibration event to.
messages.Tooltip.TwitchInterface: Twitch interface is a component that is used to allow resonite to communicate to and recieve twitch API events from twitch for a given Channel through a User.
messages.Tooltip.TwitchInterface.TargetUser: The user to handle sending and receiving network packets to and from twitch. User will need to have agreed to communicate with Twitch for this component to work.
messages.Tooltip.TwitchInterface.Channel: The twitch channel to get communications from.
messages.Tooltip.TwitchInterface.Connected: Whether the connection to <mark=#ffffff10>Channel</mark> is a success and is communicating.
messages.Tooltip.TwitchInterface.ViewerCount: How many viewers are viewing <mark=#ffffff10>Channel</mark>'s stream.
messages.Tooltip.TwitchInterface.FollowTimeoutSec: How many seconds to wait before allowing another follow event through.
messages.Tooltip.DestroyBlock: The Destroy Block component blocks destroying a Slot tagged by this component via the Context Menu. Does not block destroying via Scene Inspector or ProtoFlux.
messages.Tooltip.DuplicateBlock: The Duplicate Block component blocks duplication of a Grabbable slot tagged with this component by using the Context menu, but not via the Scene Inspector or ProtoFlux.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbableReparentBlock: The GrabbableReparentBlock component makes it so that, if any slot that is a descendant (up to MaxDepth levels deep) of the current one is grabbed and then let go of, it won't be reparented to this slot.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbableReparentBlock.DontRepar: Don't allow this slot to be reparented to. Subject to the rules explained in Grabbable - Behavior
messages.Tooltip.GrabbableReparentBlock.MaxDepth: Objects beyond this level in the hierarchy will not be effected. Set to Int MaxValue by default
messages.Tooltip.GrabBlock: Used to prevent grabbing a Slot from a certain Collider or set of colliders. Place between a collider and a Grabbable component and the slot will no longer be able to be grabbed via that collider.
messages.Tooltip.GrabToolSnapper: The GrabToolSnapper component snaps a grabbable component's slot on the same slot as this component to the tip of a tool or your tooltip anchor when the Grabbable is grabbed.
messages.Tooltip.GrabToolSnapper.Offset: How far away from the tool the object should be upon grab.
messages.Tooltip.GripPoseReference: The GripPoseReference component is placed under Tools to tell them how they should be positioned when equipped.
messages.Tooltip.GripPoseReference.HandSide: The hand side this grip pose should
messages.Tooltip.GripPoseReference._rootPos: The root position of the tooltip.
messages.Tooltip.GripPoseReference.TipReference: the slot for the tool tips tip in question.
messages.Tooltip.GripPoseReference.ShowVisual: Whether to show the adjustment visual.
messages.Tooltip.GripPoseReference.DisableSlider: Whether to disable adjusting this grip pose.
messages.Tooltip.GripPoseReferenceEditor: The GripPoseReferenceEditor component handles the UI Edit Mode behavior of adjusting Raw Data Tools in the user's hand. See UI Edit Mode for more information.
messages.Tooltip.GripPoseReferenceEditor.Referenc: The root slot of the tool tip holder.
messages.Tooltip.GripPoseReferenceEditor.Item: The root slot of the tooltip being adjusted.
messages.Tooltip.GripPoseReferenceEditor.Slider: The slider to use to adjust the tool tip during grabbing.
messages.Tooltip.GripPoseReferenceEditor.PoseRefe: The grip pose reference to be modifying.
messages.Tooltip.GripPoseReferenceEditor.HideVisu: Hide the visual when it's not being grabbed.
messages.Tooltip.GripPoseReferenceEditor.RootPosi: The root position of the gripping pose reference.
messages.Tooltip.GripPoseReferenceEditor.RootRota: The root rotation of the gripping pose reference.
messages.Tooltip.GripPoseReferenceEditor.RootScal: The root scale of the gripping pose reference.
messages.Tooltip.KnobControl: As the local rotation of this component's slot changes, the knob's value is updated according to how much it has rotated about the RotationAxis.
messages.Tooltip.KnobControl.Target: A field to set with the knob's value.
messages.Tooltip.KnobControl.Callback: A delegate that is called with the knob's value change.
messages.Tooltip.KnobControl.RotationAxis: The axis around which to measure the slot's local rotation.
messages.Tooltip.KnobControl.Rate: The proportional effect of the knob, in values per full (360-degree) rotation.
messages.Tooltip.KnobControl.Min: The minimum value for the knob.
messages.Tooltip.KnobControl.Max: The maximum value for the knob.
messages.Tooltip.KnobControl._lastRotation: (internal, read-only) Used in determining the rotational change.
messages.Tooltip.KnobControl._lastAxis: (internal, read-only) Used if the RotationAxis changes.
messages.Tooltip.KnobControl._lastUser: (internal, read-only) The user who last rotated the slot.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton: The PhysicalButton component can be used to create buttons that move inward when pressed by a user, a press depth and threshold can be set to customize the physical feeling of the button.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.PressAxis: The direction in local space to go when pressing the button
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.AcceptPhysicalTou: Whether to allow Component:TipTouchSources to interact with/activate this component.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.AcceptRemoteTouch: Whether this component allows interaction via the user's interaction laser.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.AcceptOutOfSightT: Whether or not to allow interaction with this component if it is out of the user's view.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.EditModeOnly: Whether this button can only be pressed if the user is in Edit Mode
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.ActiveUserFilter: How to filter in or out the active user of this component.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.__legacyActiveUse: Whether to use the legacy active user root only option.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.Pressed: A sync delegate to call when the button is pressed.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.Pressing: A sync delegate to call when the button is being pressed. is called every game update while the button is held down.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.Released: A sync delegate to call when the button is released.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.PressDepth: The max distance the button can move in <mark=#ffffff10>PressAxis</mark> direction
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.PressThreshold: If pressed more than this amount, the button is considered pressed.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.ReleaseThreshold: If pressed less than this amount, the button is considered released.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.IsPressed: Whether the user has the button pressed.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.IsHovering: Whether the user is hovering their interaction source at the button (laser or tip touch source)
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.IsHolding: Whether the user is holding the button.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.IsPressedOrHoldin: Whether the button is being held down or being pressed.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.Hold: Whether the button can be held down.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.HoldDepthRatio: How far the button has to be pressed before it is considered being held down.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.BeginPressVibrati: How to vibrate a user's hand when they begin to press the button.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.PressVibration: How to vibrate a user's hand when they press the button.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.HoverVibration: How to vibrate a user's hand when they point at the button.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton.Label: The field to drive with a label describing this button.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton._currentPressingD: The current amount that the button is being pushed down.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton._buttonOffset: The initial position of the button's transforms in local space.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalButton._buttonPosition: the field to drive to influence the position of the button for pressing.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleElement: The ScaleElement component scales itself up or down depending on if it is the selected scale element in a Scale Group..
messages.Tooltip.ScaleElement.Group: The group this belongs to. If there is a group in this component's parents, the group is auto filled into this field on attach.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleElement.RespondToPhysicalTo: Whether to make this item selected upon physical touch.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleElement.RespondToRemoteTouc: Whether to make this item selected upon clicking via laser.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleElement._scaleTarget: The field to drive in order to influence the scale of this slot when selecting/deselecting.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleGroup: The ScaleGroup component is used to make a touchable selection system that can be used to select items like the Steam<sup>tm</sup> store and big picture menus.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleGroup.SelectedElement: The currently selected element for this group.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleGroup.IdleScale: The scale of elements when they aren't selected.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleGroup.BackgroundScale: The scale of elements when they go into the background.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleGroup.SelectedScale: The scale of any element when it is selected.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleGroup.SmoothSpeed: The speed at which elements change scale when they change state.
messages.Tooltip.TouchableData: The TouchableData component can be used to create simple touch based interactions.
messages.Tooltip.TouchableData.Hovering: Whether a user's laser is hovering over the component's collider
messages.Tooltip.TouchableData.Touching: Whether a user is clicking on the collider this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.TouchableData.AcceptOutOfSightTo: Whether or not to allow interaction with this component if it is out of the user's view.
messages.Tooltip.TouchableData.AcceptPhysicalTouc: Whether to allow Component:TipTouchSources to interact with/activate this component.
messages.Tooltip.TouchableData.AcceptRemoteTouch: Whether this component allows interaction via the user's interaction laser.
messages.Tooltip.TouchableData.OnHoverStartVibrat: How to vibrate the user's controller when they started hovering on the component.
messages.Tooltip.TouchableData.OnHoverStayVibrate: How to vibrate the user's controller every game update when they are hovering on the component.
messages.Tooltip.TouchableData.OnHoverEndVibrate: How to vibrate the user's controller when they stopped hovering on the component.
messages.Tooltip.TouchableData.OnTouchStartVibrat: How to vibrate the user's controller when they started primary pressing on the component.
messages.Tooltip.TouchableData.OnTouchStayVibrate: How to vibrate the user's controller every game update when they hold primary press on the component.
messages.Tooltip.TouchableData.OnTouchEndVibrate: How to vibrate the user's controller when they ended primary pressing on the component.
messages.Tooltip.TouchableData.EditModeOnly: Whether this component can only be interacted with by users in edit mode.
messages.Tooltip.TouchableData.ActiveUserFilter: How to handle which users can use this component based on the current active user.
messages.Tooltip.TouchButton: The TouchButton component is used to make a physical button with no press depth that is instantly pressable upon click or touch. For a button with press depth, please see PhysicalButton.
messages.Tooltip.TouchButton.IsPressed: Whether the button is pressed or not
messages.Tooltip.TouchButton.IsHovering: Whether the button is being pointed at with the laser in the case of a touch button
messages.Tooltip.TouchButton.AcceptPhysicalTouch: Whether to allow Component:TipTouchSources to interact with/activate this component.
messages.Tooltip.TouchButton.AcceptRemoteTouch: Whether this component allows interaction via the user's interaction laser.
messages.Tooltip.TouchButton.AcceptOutOfSightTouc: Whether or not to allow interaction with this component if it is out of the user's view.
messages.Tooltip.TouchButton.EditModeOnly: Whether this button will only work if the user is in Edit Mode
messages.Tooltip.TouchButton.ActiveUserFilter: How to handle user interaction depending on active user.
messages.Tooltip.TouchButton.Pressed: The Sync Delegate to call when the button is pressed.
messages.Tooltip.TouchButton.Pressing: The Sync Delegate to call when the button is being pressed every game tick.
messages.Tooltip.TouchButton.Released: The Sync Delegate to call when the button is released.
messages.Tooltip.TouchButton.BeginPressVibration: What vibration to send when this button is starting to be pressed.
messages.Tooltip.TouchButton.PressVibration: What vibration to send when this button is pressed.
messages.Tooltip.TouchButton.HoverVibration: What vibration to send when this button is hovered over via laser.
messages.Tooltip.TouchButton.Label: The field that represents this button's label.
messages.Tooltip.TouchEventRelay: The TouchEventRelay component is used to relay touch events recieved by a slot to other ITouchables.
messages.Tooltip.TouchEventRelay.Touched: The sync delegate to call when this receives a touch event.
messages.Tooltip.TouchEventRelay.AcceptOutOfSight: Whether this component allows touch events from a user not looking directly at the slot with this component.
messages.Tooltip.TouchEventRelay.TouchableTargets: Target touchable components to send the touch event to.
messages.Tooltip.TouchToggle: The TouchToggle component acts as a button that works as a check box, and the different Toggle on and off action can be mapped to certain Touch events.
messages.Tooltip.TouchToggle.State: The checkbox state of this touch toggle.
messages.Tooltip.TouchToggle.EventType: the type of touch event to listen for different event states from.
messages.Tooltip.TouchToggle.ToggleEvent: The event state needed when touching to Toggle the <mark=#ffffff10>State</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.TouchToggle.OnEvent: The event state needed when touching to switch to true the <mark=#ffffff10>State</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.TouchToggle.OffEvent: The event state needed when touching to switch to false the <mark=#ffffff10>State</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.TouchToggle.AcceptOutOfSightTouc: Whether or not to allow interaction with this component if it is out of the user's view.
messages.Tooltip.TouchToggle.AcceptPhysicalTouch: Whether to allow Component:TipTouchSources to interact with/activate this component.
messages.Tooltip.TouchToggle.AcceptRemoteTouch: Whether this component allows interaction via the user's interaction laser.
messages.Tooltip.TouchToggle.Vibrate: How to vibrate the user's hand when they touch the toggle.
messages.Tooltip.TouchToggle.EditModeOnly: Whether this is only available in edit mode.
messages.Tooltip.TouchToggle.ActiveUserFilter: how to filter users in the session who can interact with this component depending on the parent user.
messages.Tooltip.TouchToggle.__legacyActiveUserRo: Whether to use the legacy active user root. Internal.
messages.Tooltip.TouchValueOption`1: The TouchValueOption component allows you to set a field to a value on press, it can drive indicators for Active and Hovering.
messages.Tooltip.TouchValueOption`1.ActiveIndicat: The field to drive with whether T in Target equals Value.
messages.Tooltip.TouchValueOption`1.HoverIndicato: The field to drive with whether this button is being hovered over via laser.
messages.Tooltip.TouchValueOption`1.HoverVibrate: How to vibrate haptics on the hand that is hovering over this option.
messages.Tooltip.TouchValueOption`1.Vibrate: How to vibrate haptics on the hand that is pressing this option.
messages.Tooltip.TouchValueOption`1.SetOnTouchBeg: Whether to set the T in Target to Value upon start touching this component.
messages.Tooltip.TouchValueOption`1.SetOnTouchSta: Whether to set the T in Target to Value every frame while keeping touching this component.
messages.Tooltip.TouchValueOption`1.SetOnTouchEnd: Whether to set the T in Target to Value upon end touching this component.
messages.Tooltip.TouchValueOption`1.AcceptOutOfSi: Whether or not to allow interaction with this component if it is out of the user's view.
messages.Tooltip.TouchValueOption`1.AcceptPhysica: Whether to allow Component:TipTouchSources to interact with/activate this component.
messages.Tooltip.TouchValueOption`1.AcceptRemoteT: Whether this component allows interaction via the user's interaction laser.
messages.Tooltip.TouchValueOption`1.EditModeOnly: Whether this component can only be interacted with if the user is in edit mode.
messages.Tooltip.TouchValueOption`1.ActiveUserRoo: Whether only the active user of this component can interact with this component.
messages.Tooltip.MissingComponent: The MissingComponent component is used to signify that a component previously saved on a slot no longer exists inside of Resonite. seeing this component is rare due to the backwards compatibility of Resonite. This component does not have any active functionality.
messages.Tooltip.MissingComponent.Type: The string version of a Type of the removed or depreciated component.
messages.Tooltip.MissingComponent.Data: The data of the missing component.
messages.Tooltip.SavedReferenceTable: Used solely by Component:SpawnOrDestroy to store the data of a destroyed object to restore later.
messages.Tooltip.SavedReferenceTable.Table: The table to save destroyed object data.
messages.Tooltip.UnresolvedReferences: The UnresolvedReferences component is internal to FrooxEngine and is found usually on a world root. It is used to store references that are unresolved by the load controller of the game for that world.
messages.Tooltip.UnresolvedReferences.References: A list of references to keep a storage for.
messages.Tooltip.CurrentLocaleInfo: The CurrentLocaleInfo component gives localized values of the user's current locale.
messages.Tooltip.CurrentLocaleInfo.LocaleCode: The code of the culture of the current locale.
messages.Tooltip.CurrentLocaleInfo.LanguageCode: The code of the language of the current locale.
messages.Tooltip.CurrentLocaleInfo.NativeLocaleNa: The name of the current locale in native language (Ex: "Español")
messages.Tooltip.CurrentLocaleInfo.EnglishLocaleN: The name of the current locale in english (Ex: "Spanish")
messages.Tooltip.LocaleActiveDriver: The LocaleActiveDriver does a comparison against the local user's locale code and returns if it matches.
messages.Tooltip.LocaleActiveDriver.Target: The field to drive with whether or not <mark=#ffffff10>LocaleCode</mark> is the local user's active locale code.
messages.Tooltip.LocaleActiveDriver.LocaleCode: The locale code to check for ignoring cass.
messages.Tooltip.LocaleActiveDriver.MatchMainLang: Whether to match to the locale's main language.
messages.Tooltip.LocaleAuthorsInfo: The LocaleAuthorsInfo component gives the credits for a locale. Usually members of the community that helped with translations.
messages.Tooltip.LocaleAuthorsInfo.Locale: The locale source to get the credits for.
messages.Tooltip.LocaleStringDriver: The LocaleStringDriver component is used to turn a key into a translated string from a locale, and then fill its value arguments with field contents or values.
messages.Tooltip.LocaleStringDriver.Target: The string field to drive with the final locale translation.
messages.Tooltip.LocaleStringDriver.Key: The locale key to get a translation with.
messages.Tooltip.LocaleStringDriver.Locale: The locale resource to use <mark=#ffffff10>Key</mark> on to get the translated string for.
messages.Tooltip.LocaleStringDriver.ArgumentSourc: A list of argument strings in a locale translation and their fields to get the values for them from.
messages.Tooltip.LocaleStringDriver.ArgumentValue: A list of argument strings in a locale translation and their values.
messages.Tooltip.FootstepEventDebugVisualizer: The FootStepEventDebugVisualizer shows footstep collisions with a collider on the same Slot as the one it's attached on.
messages.Tooltip.FootstepEventDebugVisualizer.Dur: How long the debug step visual should display for in seconds.
messages.Tooltip.FootstepEventRelay: The FootstepEventRelay component can be put on a user's feet or an object with a collider to receive footstep events. The events are then sent to another slot or any number of ProtoFlux:FootstepEvents ProtoFlux nodes.
messages.Tooltip.FootstepEventRelay.Target: The slot to relay footstep events to.
messages.Tooltip.FootstepSoundDefinition: The FootstepSoundDefinition component can be referenced by other components which can trigger this to play noise during footstep events.
messages.Tooltip.FootstepSoundDefinition.ParentUn: The slot to parent played sounds under.
messages.Tooltip.FootstepSoundDefinition.MinDista: The minimum distance the audio can be heard from before playing at maximum amount.
messages.Tooltip.FootstepSoundDefinition.MaxDista: The max distance before the audio cannot be heard anymore.
messages.Tooltip.FootstepSoundDefinition.RolloffM: How played audio should fall off based on distance.
messages.Tooltip.FootstepSoundDefinition.Clips: Different footsteps that can play when this component is triggered
messages.Tooltip.FootstepSoundPlayer: The FootstepSoundPlayer component triggers a list of IFootstepSoundMaterials when a player walks on a collider on the same slot.
messages.Tooltip.FootstepSoundSplatmapSplitter: The FootstepSoundSplatmapSplitter component plays a different sound player depending on what part of a mesh collider on in the same slot a player walks on.
messages.Tooltip.FootstepSoundSplatmapSplitter.Sp: The texture to get channel data from for playing different sound materials.
messages.Tooltip.FootstepSoundSplatmapSplitter.R_: The sound material to play when the user walks on a part of the mesh collider that maps to the R channel of <mark=#ffffff10>SplatMap</mark>
messages.Tooltip.FootstepSoundSplatmapSplitter.G_: The sound material to play when the user walks on a part of the mesh collider that maps to the G channel of <mark=#ffffff10>SplatMap</mark>
messages.Tooltip.FootstepSoundSplatmapSplitter.B_: The sound material to play when the user walks on a part of the mesh collider that maps to the B channel of <mark=#ffffff10>SplatMap</mark>
messages.Tooltip.FootstepSoundSplatmapSplitter.A_: The sound material to play when the user walks on a part of the mesh collider that maps to the A channel of <mark=#ffffff10>SplatMap</mark>
messages.Tooltip.FootstepSoundSplatmapSplitter.Bl: Whether to blend sounds together based on what mix of channels the user is stepping on.
messages.Tooltip.FootstepSoundSplatmapSplitter.Mi: The minimum threshold needed for a channel to be for it to cound for a sound.
messages.Tooltip.FootstepSoundSplatmapSplitter.R_: The strength the sound for the R channel has, which can make it have more importance than other channel sounds on the same pixel.
messages.Tooltip.FootstepSoundSplatmapSplitter.G_: The strength the sound for the G channel has, which can make it have more importance than other channel sounds on the same pixel.
messages.Tooltip.FootstepSoundSplatmapSplitter.B_: The strength the sound for the B channel has, which can make it have more importance than other channel sounds on the same pixel.
messages.Tooltip.FootstepSoundSplatmapSplitter.A_: The strength the sound for the A channel has, which can make it have more importance than other channel sounds on the same pixel.
messages.Tooltip.SelfFootstepEventRelay: The SelfFootstepEventRelay can be put on a user's feet to recieve foot step events and send them to other slots.
messages.Tooltip.SelfFootstepEventRelay.Target: The slot to relay foot step events to.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionController: The LocomotionController component is used on user root slots to allow user inputs to control Locomotion modules and switch between them. It also handles disabling them when they should be restrained.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionController.ScalingEnab: Whether the user is allowed to scale or not.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionController.SupressSour: Sources of Locomotion suppression. This will usually be a AvatarAnchor.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionController.InputSupres: A list of Components that act as input control supressors.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionController.LocomotionM: A list of locomotions the user can switch between and use.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionController.ActiveModul: The current locomotion module being used.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionController.FindUserPre: Whether to automatically use the user's preferred locomotion in Settings.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionController._currentGro: the current object that the user is standing on (supporting ground or slope)
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionController._lastGround: The previous collider the user was standing on before they started nocliping or otherwise.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionController.OnInitializ: A sync delegate to call and pass this locomotion controller as a reference to when this component Initializes.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionController._parentHier: A list of slots that are parents of this component.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionController._dummyChara: The character controller to use when there is no locomotion. Is found on User root.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionGrip: The LocomotionGrip component controls if users can climb on a collider hierarchy.
messages.Tooltip.ConstantCharacterControllerModif: The ConstantCharacterController component modifies the properties of a Component:CharacterController that makes contact with a character collider on the same Slot as this component.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterControllerModifier.Para: The parameter of the contacting character controller to modify.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterControllerModifier.Modi: How to modify the parameter on the contacting character controller.
messages.Tooltip.ConstantCharacterControllerModif: the value to use in the modification.
messages.Tooltip.SubmeshCharacterControllerModifi: The SubmeshCharacterController is a component able to change properties of a Person or NPC locomotion (Character Controller) walking on a mesh based on what submesh of the mesh they are walking on.
What value to use when modifying for each sub mesh. In order of submesh number.
messages.Tooltip.TextureCharacterControllerModifi: The TextureCharacterControllerModifier component reacts to character controllers that collide with mesh colliders in its children hiearchy. It starts by finding what the contact triangle and point on the mesh collider a character controller hit was. That point is mapped to what part of a texture the character controller hit. Finally, the texture data in Texture is used to determine what the value should be used for Modifying the character controller that hit that mesh collider.
When the mapped value on <mark=#ffffff10>Texture</mark> maps to a value of 0, what should the value be for the character controller modification?
When the mapped value on <mark=#ffffff10>Texture</mark> maps to a value of 100% brightness or 1, what should the value be for the character controller modification?
The channel to use on <mark=#ffffff10>Texture</mark> when getting a value for UV contact points.
The texture to use for character controller modification data.
messages.Tooltip.GrabWorldLocomotion: The GrabWorldLocomotion component can be used to make a locomotion for a world or injected into a user from an avatar or tooltip.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionModule.Icon: The icon image visual for this locomotion in the context menu.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionModule.Color: The color of this locomotion's icon in the context menu.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionModule._currentControl: The current Locomotion controller which would be from a user controlling this locmotion.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionModule._lastDefaultIco: The last URI used for the default context menu icon for this locomotion.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionModule._lastDefaultCol: The last color used for the default version of this locomotion for the context menu.
messages.Tooltip.GrabWorldLocomotion.Turn: How turning should be handled for this locomotion.
messages.Tooltip.GrabWorldLocomotion.ActivationTh: The primary strength from the user before activation.
messages.Tooltip.GrabWorldLocomotion.Deactivation: The primary strength from the user before deactivation.
messages.Tooltip.GrabWorldLocomotion._visual: the slot of the cross mesh visual used when moving.
messages.Tooltip.GrabWorldLocomotion._crossMesh: The cross mesh that should appear at the user's hand whenever moving with this locomotion.
messages.Tooltip.GrabWorldLocomotion._material: The material applied to the cross mesh visual.
messages.Tooltip.NoclipLocomotion: The NoclipLocomotion component when being used as an active locomotion by the user allows for a user to go through walls.
messages.Tooltip.NoclipLocomotion.Turn: How turning should be handled for this locomotion.
messages.Tooltip.NoclipLocomotion.MaxSpeed: The fastest speed this locomotion can travel.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalLocomotion: The PhysicalLocomotion component is a versatile Locomotion Module that can be used for walking, climbing, flying, and Zero-G.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalLocomotion.Turn: How turning should be handled for this locomotion.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalLocomotion.Archetype: Used to specify what kind of locomotion this is, to help with identification in flux and compatibility with user settings.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalLocomotion.MinInitializa: The minimum time to wait for the locomotion to allow initalization.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalLocomotion.MaxInitializa: If the amount of time passed after this component was created is past this value, the locomotion is forced to initialize anyways.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalLocomotion.Initializatio: The slot which a collider in its hiearchy has to be raycastable (From the user's feet downward) in order for this locomotion to initalize.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalLocomotion.UseSpeedFromU: Whether to allow the speed in the user settings to influence this locomotion's speed.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalLocomotion.Description: The description of this locomotion in the context menu.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalLocomotion.Mode: How the enviroment/user influences how this locomotion can move.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalLocomotion.FallRespawnPo: How far down in global space the user needs to be to respawn.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalLocomotion.MakeGravityCh: Whether the gravity should be based on the user's parent space orientation (true) or global (false)
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalLocomotion.AutoAlignVert: How fast to rotate the player to orient them to the direction of gravity.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalLocomotion.ManualAlignVe: How fast the user aligns with gravity if 3 axis rotation is enabled and they're pressing the manual align button.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalLocomotion.AirDecelerati: How fast the user is slowed down while in air.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalLocomotion.GripScaleDela: How long the user has to wait after gripping a surface in order to be able to scale with both hands in VR.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalLocomotion.AllowCrouch: Whether this locomotion allows the user to crouch down.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalLocomotion.MaximumNormal: The fastest speed this locomotion is allowed to travel.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalLocomotion._defaultIcon: The default icon url for this kind of locomotion.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalLocomotion._defaultColor: The default color for this kind of locomotion.
messages.Tooltip.PhysicalLocomotion._characterCon: The character controller associated with this locomotion component.
messages.Tooltip.SlideLocomotion: The SlideLocomotion component acts the same as a Component:NoclipLocomotion except that this locomotion will attach the user to a raycasted surface below them regardless of collider type. When a surface is not found, Behavior compared to noclip is identical.
messages.Tooltip.SlideLocomotion.MaxSnapDistance: The maximum distance to check from the user's feet position plus <mark=#ffffff10>SnapCheckOffset</mark> downward.
messages.Tooltip.SlideLocomotion.SnapCheckOffset: anything from the user's feet within this range downward is not raycasted.
messages.Tooltip.SlideLocomotion.SnapMinObjectSiz: The minimum size of the object's max dimensions on any axis in global space compared to the user's max size on any axis needed for us to snap to it's surface below us.
messages.Tooltip.TeleportLocomotion: The TeleportLocomotion component is used to allow users to teleport to spots using an arc path as a location selector.
messages.Tooltip.TeleportLocomotion.Turn: How turning should be handled for this locomotion.
messages.Tooltip.TeleportLocomotion.BackstepDista: How far to go backwards when pressing the backwards movement direction.
messages.Tooltip.TeleportLocomotion.ActivationTim: How long it takes for the teleport visual to show and for the locomotion to allow teleport when pressing the forward movement direction.
messages.Tooltip.TeleportLocomotion.InitialForceM: The minimum inital arc force value for the destination selection arc.
The maximum inital arc force value for the destination selection arc.
messages.Tooltip.TeleportLocomotion.RangeExp: The amount to boost the teleport range.
messages.Tooltip.TeleportLocomotion.StepUnit: What to use for the step value in the teleport arc.
messages.Tooltip.TeleportLocomotion.Drag: What to use for the drag value in the teleport arc.
messages.Tooltip.TeleportLocomotion.MaxSmallObjec: The maximum size to allow before a small object is not ignored for teleport destinations.
messages.Tooltip.TeleportLocomotion.WallDistance: The depth a wall has to be compared to the user's size to be considered a wall.
messages.Tooltip.TeleportLocomotion._pathMesh: The path mesh being used to display this locomotion's teleport location selector.
messages.Tooltip.TeleportLocomotion._pathMaterial: The material to control the look of that is on <mark=#ffffff10>_pathMesh</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.TeleportLocomotion._pathRenderer: The renderer of this teleport locomotion's location selector visual.
messages.Tooltip.TeleportLocomotion._pathVisual: the root slot of this teleport locomotion's location selector visual
messages.Tooltip.TeleportLocomotion._targetPointV: the root of the hit point visual of this teleport locomotion's location selector visual
messages.Tooltip.TeleportLocomotion._characterCon: The character controller for this teleport locomotion.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterEventTrigger: The CharacterEventTrigger component invokes Action<Float3> triggers whenever a user enters or exits an attached collider.
messages.Tooltip.CollisionListenerComponent.Trigg: Whether to only allow collision events from trigger type colliders on this slot to trigger or all types of colliders to trigger this world link.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterEventTrigger.TriggerEnt: Triggered when a user enters a collider attached to the parent slot
messages.Tooltip.CharacterEventTrigger.TriggerLef: Triggered when a user exits a collider attached to the parent slot
messages.Tooltip.CharacterEventTrigger.IgnorePare: If this component is under a user in the hierarchy, events from that user are ignored.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterForceField: This component creates a force field that allows the setting of or applying force to the velocity of Character Controllers.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterForceField.Force: force direction and magnitude to apply a character controller within range with.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterForceField.ForceMode: Whether to use impulse, apply, or set velocity using <mark=#ffffff10>Force</mark>
messages.Tooltip.CharacterForceField.ForceSpace: Whether to do the force direction in local or global space (so should <mark=#ffffff10>Force</mark> be scaled and rotated by the slot this is on or not?)
messages.Tooltip.CharacterForceField.RadialForceR: The range of effect this force field has.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterForceField.ForceSlotSpa: Override the transform space for <mark=#ffffff10>Force</mark> with this if set.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterForceField.MinActivatio: The minimum velocity a character controller has to be going to trigger this force field.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterForceField.MaxForceVelo: How fast the character controller's velocity can be at max after being affected by this component when the user isn't holding Jump
messages.Tooltip.CharacterForceField.HoldJumpMaxF: How fast the character controller's velocity can be at max after being affected by this component when the user is holding Jump
messages.Tooltip.CharacterForceField.PreseveDirec: Calculates a plane that is perpendicular to the vector <mark=#ffffff10>Force</mark> (like a stick stuck into a piece of paper) and maintains the initial direction of the velocity of the character controller in either the forward or backwards facing direction of this plane.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterForceField.IgnoreParent: Whether to ignore the active user when finding character controllers within range of <mark=#ffffff10>RadialForceRadius</mark>. This does not ignore character controllers that are simulated by the user.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterForceField.NoJumpMultip: How much to multiply the effect of <mark=#ffffff10>Force</mark> when the user isn't holding Jump
messages.Tooltip.CharacterForceField.HoldJumpMult: How much to multiply the effect of <mark=#ffffff10>Force</mark> when the user is holding Jump
messages.Tooltip.CharacterForceField.MaxCharacter: The maximum velocity this component can set a character controller's speed to after setting a force on it.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterForceField.MinCharacter: The minimum velocity this component can set a character controller's speed to after setting a force on it.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterForceField.CharacterVel: How fast to slow the user down. this can be used to give the effect of jumping into molasses.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterParenter: Character Parenters are a component that allow for parenting or aligning the gravity of a user when they hit a collider.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterParenter.NestIntoSpace: Parent the user into the slot <mark=#ffffff10>ParentSpace</mark> or not.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterParenter.MustBeOnGround: The user must be on the ground according to the character controller before they will be aligned/parented.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterParenter.Filters: What directions a user must be aligned to relative to the slot this component is on for them to get parented.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterParenter.IgnoreParentUs: Ignore the active user of this component when attempting to parent a user.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterParenter.ParentSpace: The coordinate space that calculations should be done in.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterTeleporter: Place the component on a slot with a collider that has character collider field enabled.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterTeleporter.Exits: A list of slot exits to send the user to, which include rotation and individualized data.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterTeleporter.MinimumVeloc: the minimum velocity the user has to be traveling to enter the teleporter.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterTeleporter.DirectionRef: Which way is looking forward for this teleporter.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterTeleporter.MaxDirection: If the user's velocity direction has an angle greater than this compared to the <mark=#ffffff10>DirectionReference</mark>, then don't teleport the user.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterTeleporter.IgnoreParent: Whether to ignore the active user of this component when finding users that meet the criteria to go through the teleporter.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterTeleporter.TeleportEnte: Sync Delegates that take Float3 that should be called when the teleporter is entered. For example, RandomAudioClipPlayer and RandomObjectSpawner have sync delegates that can be used here.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterTeleporter.TeleportExit: Sync Delegates that take Float3 that should be called when the teleporter is exited anywhere. For example, RandomAudioClipPlayer and RandomObjectSpawner have sync delegates that can be used here.
messages.Tooltip.HoverPlayback: The HoverPlayback component plays or stops a Playable when on the root of a hiearchy that has colliders and is touched/pressed.
messages.Tooltip.HoverPlayback.FromBeginning: Whether to play from the beginning or not.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAudioPlayer: The LegacyAudioPlayer component is a leftover Component from content migrated from other platforms. It should not be used, and should be replaced whenever possible.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAudioPlayer.AudioClip: The audio clip for this audio player.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAudioPlayer._clipPlayer: The audio clip player that will play the audio clip.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAudioPlayer._audioOutput: The audio output for the audio clip.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAudioPlayer._waveformMater: The material that is used on the ring visual.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAudioPlayer._playbackMater: The material that is used for the playback visual.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAudioPlayer._waveformRingM: The mesh used for the waveform ring visual.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAudioPlayer._playbackRingM: The mesh used for the playback ring visual.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAudioPlayer._playbackRingA: The field to drive to make the visual for the playback ring.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAudioPlayer._volumeRingArc: The field to drive to make the visual for the audio arc.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAudioPlayer._playbackTimeT: The field to drive for the playback time text.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAudioPlayer._clipLengthTim: The field to drive for the audio clip length text.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAudioPlayer._iconTextureUR: The field to drive with the icon texture URI.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAudioPlayer._stopItem: The item that can be clicked to stop playback.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAudioPlayer._loopItem: The item that can be clicked to loop playback.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAudioPlayer._spatialItem: The item that can be clicked to spatialize or unspatialize the audio.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAudioPlayer._loopIconURL: The field to drive with the loop icon texture URI.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAudioPlayer._spatialIconUR: The field to drive with the spatialize icon URI.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAudioPlayer._exportable: The audio exportable component used to handle the export behavior of this audio player.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAudioPlayer._assetProxy: The asset proxy used to handle dropping this audio player into fields that accept audio clips.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer: The Legacy Video Player component is used to construct and control legacy video players.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer.StereoLayout: The layout of the stereo audio for the video player.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer.SizeCompensati: How to compensate for video size.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer.videoProvider: The video provider component giving the audio and visual data for the video.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._style: The legacy ui style component being used for this video player.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._indicatorText: The field to drive to control the video player's texture url
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._indicatorTint: The field to drive to control the video player's indictor's tint
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._colliderSize: The field to drive to control the video player's collider size
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._frameWidth: The field to drive to control the video player's border frame width
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._frameHeight: The field to drive to control the video player's border frame height
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._frameMaterial: The field to drive to control the video player's frame material
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._displayMateri: This stores the video player's display material.
The field to drive to control the video player's display material texture.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._displaySize: The field to drive to control the video player's display section size.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._mainAudioOutp: This stores the video player's main audio output.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._timelineSlide: This stores the video player's timeline slider.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._timelinePosit: The field to drive to control the video player's timeline playhead position.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._timelineWidth: The field to drive to control the video player's timeline ui width.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._positionDrive: The field to drive to control the video player's ui position.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._volumeSlider: This stores the video player's volume slider.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._volumePositio: The field to drive to control the video player's volume amount.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._volumeWidth: The field to drive to control the video player's volume ui width.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._volumeDrive: The field to drive to control the video player's volume for the audio output component.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._buttonsWidth: The field to drive to control the video player's button container widths.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._buttonsHeight: The field to drive to control the video player's button container height.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._buttonsPositi: The field to drive to control the video player's button container position.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._playButtonCol: The field to drive to control the video player's play button color.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._pauseButtonCo: The field to drive to control the video player's pause button color.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._stopButtonCol: The field to drive to control the video player's stop button color.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._loopButtonCol: The field to drive to control the video player's loop button color.
