messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._audio3DButton: The field to drive to control the video player's 3d audio button.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._exportable: The component that is used to make this video player exportable.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._assetProxy: The component that is used to allow this video to be dropped into asset fields for videos.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyVideoPlayer._referenceProx: The component that allows this video to be dropped into texture and audio fields or receivers.
messages.Tooltip.PlaybackPositionDriver: The PlaybackPositionDriver component is used to output and/or influence the playback position of an IPlayable.
messages.Tooltip.PlaybackPositionDriver.Target: The field to drive with the time position of <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> from. If <mark=#ffffff10>UseNormalizedPosition</mark> is true, this is 0<->1, else it's the amount of seconds from the start.
messages.Tooltip.PlaybackPositionDriver.Source: The playable audio to get the time from.
messages.Tooltip.PlaybackPositionDriver.UseNormal: Whether to output a 0<->1 or an amount of seconds
messages.Tooltip.PlaybackPositionDriver.WriteBack: Whether writing to the value of the field specified by <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> will set the playback position. See write backs.
messages.Tooltip.IsPlayingDriver: The IsPlayingDriver component checks if a particular SyncPlayback is currently playing, and drives a set of Bool to reflect this status.
messages.Tooltip.IsPlayingDriver.Playback: The playback to check if it is playing
messages.Tooltip.IsPlayingDriver.Targets: The set of booleans to drive to whether or not <mark=#ffffff10>Playback</mark> is playing or not.
messages.Tooltip.PlaybackStateDriver: The PlaybackStateDriver is a component that drives a set of booleans to whether or not certain states are currently active or not for a particular IPlayable.
messages.Tooltip.PlaybackStateDriver.IsPlaying: Will drive IField<bool> to true if playing, false if not playing
messages.Tooltip.PlaybackStateDriver.IsNotPlaying: Will drive IField<bool> to true is not playing, false if playing
messages.Tooltip.PlaybackStateDriver.IsPaused: Will drive IField<bool> to true if playable is paused
messages.Tooltip.PlaybackStateDriver.IsStopped: Will drive IField<bool> to true if playable is stopped
messages.Tooltip.PlaybackSynchronizer: The PlaybackSynchronizer component is used to make two Audio Clips play together in unison, play at the same time, or to synchronize.
messages.Tooltip.PlaybackSynchronizer.Target: The playable to synchronize the playback position with the position of <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.PlaybackSynchronizer.Source: The playable that will drive <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark>'s playback
messages.Tooltip.PlaybackSynchronizer.UseNormaliz: Whether to speed up or slow down the playback of <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> if the duration doesn't match.
messages.Tooltip.PlaybackSynchronizer.PositionOff: How many seconds/normalized position to offset the <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark>'s synchronization with <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark>'s position.
messages.Tooltip.PlaybackSynchronizer.PositionRat: How much to multiply <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark>'s position before driving <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark>'s position with it.
messages.Tooltip.AssetMetadata: This component is used as a base component for other components like PhotoMetadata
messages.Tooltip.AssetMetadata.LocationName: Name of the session the screenshot was taken in.
messages.Tooltip.AssetMetadata.LocationURL: The URI of the session's world that the photo was taken in. (basically a world link)
messages.Tooltip.AssetMetadata.LocationHost: The host user of the session the photo was taken in.
messages.Tooltip.AssetMetadata.LocationAccessLeve: The access level of the world the photo was taken in.
messages.Tooltip.AssetMetadata.LocationHiddenFrom: Whether the session was marked as hidden when the audio was recorded.
messages.Tooltip.AssetMetadata.TimeTaken: Exact timestamp that the audio was recorded at
messages.Tooltip.AssetMetadata.TakenBy: The user who recorded the audio
messages.Tooltip.AssetMetadata.TakenGlobalPositio: the position in global space the microphone tip was at the time of recording.
messages.Tooltip.AssetMetadata.TakenGlobalRotatio: the rotation in global space the microphone tip was at the time of recording.
messages.Tooltip.AssetMetadata.TakenGlobalScale: the scale in global space the microphone tip was at the time of recording.
messages.Tooltip.AssetMetadata.AppVersion: The Version of the game the photo was taken in.
messages.Tooltip.AssetMetadata.UserInfos: A list of users present in the session when the photo was taken.
messages.Tooltip.AssetMetadata.__legacyPresentUse: A list of users present in the session when the photo was taken. This is internal legacy data.
messages.Tooltip.AudioMetadata: The AudioMetadata component provides information about an audio clip. It is automatically added to audio clips recorded with the Microphone Tool and stores data about when, where, and by whom the audio was recorded.
messages.Tooltip.FileMetadata: File metadata is a component used in RawFile object imports and is often taken advantage of for Cloud Spawning.
messages.Tooltip.FileMetadata.Filename: The name of the file, usually followed by .brson or .7bson
messages.Tooltip.FileMetadata.IsProcessing: whether this component is currently processing an import or export action asynchronously.
messages.Tooltip.License: License is a legacy component, that has no functionality. We don't recommend using it. There is no standard for the formats, meaning or use of the properties within the component. Due to this different communities have used it for different items and purposes leading to a lot of confusion.
messages.Tooltip.License.RequireCredit: There is no common standard for this value's meaning
messages.Tooltip.License.CreditString: There is no common standard for this value's meaning
messages.Tooltip.License.CanExport: There is no common standard for this value's meaning
messages.Tooltip.PhotoMetadata: The PhotoMetadata component is used to contain the information on a photo usually taken within Resonite. It is automatically attached to every screenshot taken using the finger photo gesture. This component can also be attached to a photo imported with the "import as screenshot" option when importing an image.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoMetadata.CameraFOV: FOV of the camera at picture time.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoMetadata.Is360: Whether the image is a 360 degree image or not.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoMetadata.StereoLayout: The layout of the image's left and right sections if it is a stereoscopic image, also known as a 3D image.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoMetadata._exportedUsers: This is a list of users that exported the screenshot. If this component is part of a saved item, this is the list of users that exported the screenshot before the item was saved to the cloud.
messages.Tooltip.OSC_Field`1: The OSC_Field`1 Component is used to send and recieve OSC data.
messages.Tooltip.OSC_ValueHandler.Handler: The Component:OSC_Receiver or Component:OSC_Sender to be used with this component.
messages.Tooltip.OSC_ValueHandler.Path: The OSC Path for this value.
messages.Tooltip.OSC_ValueHandler.ArgumentIndex: For Multi-Dimensional OSC Values, this specifies which value to retrieve or send.
messages.Tooltip.OSC_Field`1.Field: The Field to store the value in.
messages.Tooltip.OSC_Receiver: The OSC_Reciever component creates an OSC Server within Resonite to receive OSC messages.
messages.Tooltip.OSC_Handler.HandlingUser: The OSC Reciever creates the OSC server on this User's machine.
messages.Tooltip.OSC_Handler.AccessReason: Displayed in the Host Access Dialog when a connection is trying to be established.
messages.Tooltip.OSC_Receiver.Port: Network port to listen on for OSC connections
messages.Tooltip.OSC_Receiver.IsListening: This value is set to true when Resonite is successful in opening a network connection to receive data on.
messages.Tooltip.OSC_Sender: The OSC_Sender component will try to connect to the OSC Server at the URL provided, and send data to it.
messages.Tooltip.OSC_Sender.URL: Uri of the OSC Server to connect to, must be in the format <mark=#ffffff10>osc://<ip>:<port><mark=#ffffff10>. E.g. <mark=#ffffff10>osc://127.0.0.1:3001</mark>
messages.Tooltip.OSC_Sender.LocalPort: Sets the local port. Defaults on 0 which is random.
messages.Tooltip.OSC_Sender.IsSending: Set to true if this OSC Sender is sending data.
messages.Tooltip.OSC_Sender.SendMode: The Mode in which data is sent to the OSC Server. See #Usage
messages.Tooltip.OSC_Sender.AutoResendInterval: Data will automatically be resent at this interval if it is set. In Seconds. See #Behavior
messages.Tooltip.OSC_Value`1: The OSC_Value`1 Component is used to send and recieve OSC data.
messages.Tooltip.OSC_Value`1.Value: The recieved OSC Value or the default value of the Type if there is no OSC data.
messages.Tooltip.WebsocketClient: This component must be used in conjunction with the Websocket Protoflux Nodes - it only exists to store state, and does not function on its own.
messages.Tooltip.WebsocketClient.URL: The Websocket server to target - either secure (<mark=#ffffff10>wss://</mark>) or insecure (<mark=#ffffff10>ws://</mark>)
messages.Tooltip.WebsocketClient.HandlingUser: The user who will be responsible for establishing the connection, and transmitting data.
messages.Tooltip.WebsocketClient.AccessReason: A descriptive string explaining why this connection request should be granted
messages.Tooltip.WebsocketClient.ConnectRetryInte: How often to retry the connection, if it fails for reasons other than being rejected by the user.
messages.Tooltip.WebsocketClient.IsConnected: A read-only value indicating if this client has successfully connected to the target specified in <mark=#ffffff10>URL</mark>
messages.Tooltip.BooleanUserOverrideGather: The BooleanUserOverrideGather Component allows storing Boolean "override" values for each user listed under _overrides and driving the Target field. The behavior is similar to ValueUserOverride<Bool>, except this component also reports aggregate information about the overrides.
messages.Tooltip.ValueOverrideBase`1.Default: The default value to drive <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> with on the user's local machine if they don't have an override in the list of <mark=#ffffff10>_overrides</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ValueOverrideBase`1.CreateOverri: Whether an entry in <mark=#ffffff10>_overrides</mark> should be created when <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> is written back.
messages.Tooltip.ValueOverrideBase`1.PersistentOv: Whether the <mark=#ffffff10>_overrides</mark> list contents are saved when this component is saved.
messages.Tooltip.ValueOverrideBase`1._overrides: A Bag of the Boolean Overrides and the corresponding UserRef they apply to.
messages.Tooltip.ValueUserOverride`1.Target: The field to drive with different values for each user's local machine using the <mark=#ffffff10>_overrides</mark> list.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanUserOverrideGather.Any: Reports true when any user in the session would have their override be true (either from the default value or an override).
messages.Tooltip.BooleanUserOverrideGather.All: Reports true when all users in the session would have their override be true (either from the default value or an override).
messages.Tooltip.BooleanUserOverrideGather.None: Reports true when none of the users in the session would have their override be true (either from the default value or an override).
messages.Tooltip.BooleanUserOverrideGather.TrueCo: Reports the total count of <mark=#ffffff10>true</mark> values for all users in the session.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanUserOverrideGather.FalseC: Reports the total count of <mark=#ffffff10>false</mark> values for all users in the session.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanUserOverrideGather.Exclud: Whether to exclude a headless user when doing aggregates and overrides.
messages.Tooltip.NumericUserOverrideGather`1: The NumericOverrideGather component acts the same as a Component:ValueUserOverride`1 with the key difference being it can get the average, minimum, maximum, and sum of all the values users picked that are still in the session.
messages.Tooltip.NumericUserOverrideGather`1.Min: The minimum value of all the <mark=#ffffff10>_overrides</mark> for currently in session users. If a user doesnt have an entry, they are considered to have an entry of <mark=#ffffff10>Default</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.NumericUserOverrideGather`1.Max: The maximum value of all the <mark=#ffffff10>_overrides</mark> for currently in session users. If a user doesnt have an entry, they are considered to have an entry of <mark=#ffffff10>Default</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.NumericUserOverrideGather`1.Sum: The value for if of all the <mark=#ffffff10>_overrides</mark> were added together for currently in session users. If a user doesnt have an entry, they are considered to have an entry of <mark=#ffffff10>Default</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.NumericUserOverrideGather`1.Aver: The average value of all the <mark=#ffffff10>_overrides</mark> for currently in session users. If a user doesnt have an entry, they are considered to have an entry of <mark=#ffffff10>Default</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.NumericUserOverrideGather`1.Excl: Whether headless client values should be excluded from calculations.
messages.Tooltip.ValueUserOverride`1: The ValueUserOverride Component allows storing "override" values for each user listed under _overrides and driving the Target field with them.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionPermissions: The LocomotionPermissions component allows you to control what locomotions users can use in your world.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionPermissions.Locomotion: Whether to blacklist or whitelist the <mark=#ffffff10>Locomotions</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionPermissions.Scaling: Whether self scale is Disallowed, Allowed, or Edit Mode Only.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionPermissions.JumpToUser: Whether jumping to users is Disallowed, Allowed, or Edit Mode Only.
messages.Tooltip.TouchablePermissions: The TouchablePermissions component is used in worlds to manage what certain roles that users get assigned to can and cannot do.
messages.Tooltip.TouchablePermissions.Tags: Slot tag object to determine the kinds of tags to filter for the selected roles.
messages.Tooltip.TouchablePermissions.Components: A component filter that applies to the selected roles.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController: This component is usually used internally by Resonite to simulate user movement physics like walking and climbing.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.SimulatingUs: The user who is currently simulating and networking this Character Controller.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.CharacterRoo: The slot to simulate, can be any inputted slot.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.HeadReferenc: Allow the object simulated by this object to rotate, essentially making it a rigid body. See warning.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.MassScaling: How to scale the mass based on this component's slot scale.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.ForceScaling: How to scale the force based on this component's slot scale.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.SpeedScaling: How to scale the speed based on this component's slot scale.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.JumpScaling: How to scale the jump force based on this component's slot scale.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.GravityScali: How to scale the gravity effect based on this component's slot scale.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.LinearDampin: how fast the character controller slows down when no other forces are being applied to it
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.AngularDampi: how fast thus character slows down its rotation when no other forces are being applied to it.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.StepUpHeight: How big of a ledge the character controller can step up
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.StepUpCheckD: determines the distance the shape is swept in the movement direction to determine if it's free of other obstacles
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.KillVertical: will convert any vertical velocity into directional one in the current planar direction of the movement and push the player back down, preventing the player from getting too much air when running off a ramp or stairs.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.EdgeDetectio: the smallest size an edge can be that the character can stand on while against a wall
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.Speed: How fast this character controller can move.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.SlidingSpeed: how fast the character controller moves when sliding down a slope
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.AirSpeed: how fast the character moves in the air
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.TractionForc: How much force is needed to overcome standing friction.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.SlidingForce: How much force is needed to stay moving
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.AirForce: the force needed to move in the air
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.MaximumGlueF: Reduces the velocity of breaking contact from a surface vertically be under this speed. This is useful for simulating sticky surfaces, where the user cannot jump as high.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.MaximumTract: Maximum Traction angle (degrees) before user slides down a slope, even if they are actively trying to walk up it.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.MaximumSuppo: Maximum Support angle (degrees) before user slides down a slope, when not working against the slope.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.JumpSpeed: The speed of the velocity applied when jumping on a slope with an angle less than or equal to <mark=#ffffff10>MaximumTractionSlope</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.SlidingJumpS: The speed of the velocity applied when jumping on a slope with an angle greater than <mark=#ffffff10>MaximuSupportSlope</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.Gravity: Force of Gravity for this character controller
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.GravitySpace: The coordinate space in which <mark=#ffffff10>Gravity</mark> is applied in. applying gravity to world space using this field, can allow a user to stay upright despite a floor they're parented under is being rotated. which generates interesting effects.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.DebugVisualD: Allows for the component to visualize the force vectors acting on the character simulator if set above 0.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.__height: Height of the driven CapsuleCollider (Component) Currently does nothing.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.__radius: Radius of the driven CapsuleCollider (Component) Currently does nothing.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterController.__mass: Mass of the of the driven CapsuleCollider (Component) Currently does nothing.
messages.Tooltip.SingleShapeCharacterControllerMa: The SingleShapeCharacterControllerManager component is used by locomotions to change the shape of a Capsule Collider based on a user's standing height and current height.
Whether to use the user's head height to do calculations when possible.
How much to add/subtract from the user's head to make the top of the capsule.
How wide the capsule should become when crouching.
The maximum height the user's head has to be for them to be crouching.
The minimum height the user's head has to be for them to be crouching.
Whether the bottom end of the capsule should be at the slot rather than the slot being at the middle.
The slot to drive to influence the capsule collider's height.
The slot to drive to influence the capsule collider's radius.
The slot to drive to influence the capsule collider's center offset.
messages.Tooltip.ColliderEventRelay: The ColliderEventRelay takes a collider to monitor and sends ContactEvent messages when certain collisions happen.
messages.Tooltip.ColliderEventRelay.Collider: The collider to monitor events for.
messages.Tooltip.ColliderEventRelay.ContactStart: The Sync delegate of a certain definition to call when <mark=#ffffff10>Collider</mark> starts contact with another collider.
messages.Tooltip.ColliderEventRelay.ContactStay: The Sync delegate of a certain definition to call when <mark=#ffffff10>Collider</mark> stays in contact with another collider. Is called every frame.
messages.Tooltip.ColliderEventRelay.ContactEnd: The Sync delegate of a certain definition to call when <mark=#ffffff10>Collider</mark> ends contact with another collider.
messages.Tooltip.BoxCollider: Representing a simple box, this collider is the second most performant collider in the game, beaten by Sphere Colliders
messages.Tooltip.Collider.Offset: how much to add to points <mark=#ffffff10>A</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>B</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>C</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.Collider.Type: The type of collider. See the enum page for details.
messages.Tooltip.Collider.Mass: How heavy this collider is in total. [https://discord.com/channels/1040316820650991766/1154514007479287942/1212229273738412082 according to ProbablePrime] this is in 1 KG per cubic meter.
messages.Tooltip.Collider.CharacterCollider: Whether an avatar should be prevented from moving into the collider's volume.
messages.Tooltip.Collider.IgnoreRaycasts: Whether an avatar's laser should be prevented from hitting the collider's volume. Also this applies to raycasting nodes or components.
messages.Tooltip.BoxCollider.Size: How big locally this collider is in the x, y, and z directions.
messages.Tooltip.CapsuleCollider: The CapsuleCollider component creates a simple capsule collision shape from the defined height and radius.
messages.Tooltip.CapsuleCollider.Height: The height of the capsule. Does not include the added height from the rounded ends
messages.Tooltip.CapsuleCollider.Radius: the radius of the body of the capsule and it's rounded ends.
messages.Tooltip.ConeCollider: This component represents a pointed circular cone collider given a height and a base radius.
messages.Tooltip.ConeCollider.Height: The height of the cone collider from base to tip.
messages.Tooltip.ConeCollider.Radius: The radius of the base of the cone collider.
messages.Tooltip.ConvexHullCollider: Is a [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Convex_hull convex hull] (a shape closely wrapping the object, but with no indentations) over the mesh of the object.
messages.Tooltip.MeshBasedCollider`2.Mesh: The mesh to wrap (kind of like using plastic wrap) and use the wrapped result as the collider this component represents.
messages.Tooltip.CylinderCollider: Is like a Capsule Collider except instead of rounded ends the ends are flat.
messages.Tooltip.CylinderCollider.Height: Height of the cylinder shape of the collider
messages.Tooltip.CylinderCollider.Radius: The radius of the cylinder shape of the collider from center to edge.
messages.Tooltip.MeshCollider: A mesh collider allows for making a 1:1 collider of the given Mesh.
messages.Tooltip.MeshCollider.ActualSpeculativeMa: Essentially the room for error on the collisions for this collider.
messages.Tooltip.SphereCollider: The SphereCollider generates a spherical collision shape based on an inputted radius.
messages.Tooltip.SphereCollider.Radius: How big the sphere is from center to outside edge.
messages.Tooltip.TriangleCollider: Triangle collider is a component that acts as a single polygon version of a Mesh Collider
messages.Tooltip.TriangleCollider.A: The position of the first vertex of the triangle.
messages.Tooltip.TriangleCollider.B: The position of the second vertex of the triangle.
messages.Tooltip.TriangleCollider.C: The position of the third vertex of the triangle.
messages.Tooltip.CollisionListenerComponent: This component listens to collider events and sends a collider event. See Collider Events for how these work.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain: Dynamic Bone Chain is a component that allows you to add rigidbody physics to the bones of a rigged model or avatar.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.Inertia: Controls the amount of inertia a bone experiences. Does not affect acceleration of bones.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.InertiaForce: Controls the amount of inertia a bone experiences. Does affect acceleration of bones.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.Damping: Controls the amount of damping a bone experiences. A high damping value will cause bones to decelerate quickly.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.Elasticity: Controls the elasticity of bone joints. A high elasticity value will cause bones to accelerate toward their starting rotation more quickly.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.Stiffness: Controls the stiffness of bone joints.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.SimulateTermina: Whether or not to use the last bone in the chain. If your armature has end bones that do not control anything, you probably want this off.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.BaseBoneRadius: The default size of the sphere that allows a bone to be interacted with.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.DynamicPlayerCo: Whether or not to allow this bone chain to collide with players.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.CollideWithOwnB: Whether or not to allow this bone chain to collide with the active user of the dynamic bone chain.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.HandCollisionVi: The vibration intensity when touching the chain colliders with a hand.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.CollideWithHead: Whether or not to allow this bone chain to collide with player heads.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.CollideWithBody: Whether or not to allow this bone chain to collide with player body parts.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.CollideWithLeft: Whether or not to allow this bone chain to collide with a player's left hand.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.CollideWithRigh: Whether or not to allow this bone chain to collide with a player's right hand.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.Gravity: A force like gravity in <mark=#ffffff10>GravitySpace</mark> space that should be applied to the bone chain at all times.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.GravitySpace: The coordinate space to apply <mark=#ffffff10>Gravity</mark> in.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.UseUserGravityD: Whether or not to use the active user of the dynamic bone chain's character controller gravity for the gravity direction
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.LocalForce: Constant force being applied to the bone in the dynamic bone chain's local space.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.GlobalStretch: How stretched out the bone chain should be at rest.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.MaxStretchRatio: The maximum amount of stretching that can be caused by pulling on the bones.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.CurrentStretchR: The current amount of stretching applied to the bones.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.UseLocalUserSpa: Whether or not to make the dynamic bone only respond to movements generated from the user slot's local position changing.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.SimulationSpace: The coordinate space that movement in should affect this dynamic bone chain.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.StaticColliders: A list of dynamic bone collider components to be used.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.VisualizeCollid: Causes all bone colliders colliding with this bone to be displayed.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.VisualizeBones: Causes the bones, their colliders, and the connections between them to be displayed.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.IsGrabbable: Allows you to grab bones by touching them and grabbing. Requires DynamicPlayerCollision to be checked.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.ActiveUserRootO: Allows the active user of the dynamic bone chain to be the only user that can grab it.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.AllowSteal: Whether or not a second user can grab the chain while someone is holding it.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.GrabPriority: Determines what gets grabbed if several grabbable objects are touching someone's grab sphere. The IGrabbable with the highest priority will be grabbed first. If the highest priority ties with another IGrabbable, it will grab both of those and ignore any lower priority IGrabbables.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.IgnoreGrabOnFir: Prevents EffectorBoneIndex from being 0 while being grabbed.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.GrabRadiusToler: how much further a user can grab a bone with their grab sphere despite the radius of the bone not being in contact with their grab sphere.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.GrabReleaseDist: How far a bone can be dragged before it automatically releases.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.GrabSlipping: Whether or not to make the EffectorBoneIndex increase as the hand moves along the bone chain away from the first bone in the chain. Does not let EffectorBoneIndex decrease unless re-grabbed.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.GrabTerminalBon: Whether or not the final bone in the chain can be grabbed.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.GrabVibration: The vibration intensity when grabbing the chain with a hand.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.IgnoreOwnLeftHa: Whether or not to allow this bone chain to be grabbed with the left hand of the active user of the dynamic bone chain.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.IgnoreOwnRightH: Whether or not to allow this bone chain to be grabbed with the right hand of the active user of the dynamic bone chain.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.EffectorTarget: The chain tries to position one if its bones at this slot (used for grabbing)
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.EffectorBoneInd: Which bone is being positioned, every other bone after this acts as if it is not being grabbed
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.EffectorBoneOff: acts as an offset from the grabbing slot in the grabbing slot's local space at the moment the bone was grabbed. Essentially prevents the bone from snapping to the center of the hand's grab sphere and keeps an offset for a smoother grabbing experience. Is automatically set every grab.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain._activeGrabber: The grabber component that is grabbing this bone chain currently
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.Bones: The bones that should be controlled by this dynamic bone chain.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBonePlayerColliders: This component is used in the Resonite hair dryer, and allows for dynamic bone colliders specified in the Colliders list to automatically collide with Dynamic Bone Chains.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBonePlayerColliders.Visua: Visualize all the colliders in <mark=#ffffff10>Colliders</mark> that are currently colliding against a Dynamic Bone Chain. Automatically updates the collider visuals every frame server side.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBonePlayerColliders.Disab: Whether to not use the default head collider made by the engine.
Whether to not use the body colliders made by the engine.
Whether to not use the left hand collider made by the engine.
Whether to not use the right hand collider made by the engine.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBonePlayerColliders.Colli: A list of colliders to enable collision for all Dynamic bone chains in a world.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneSphereCollider: The dynamic bone sphere collider is a component that allows for making a static sphere collider by adding it to the list of StaticColliders on a DynamicBoneChain or make it dynamic by making it part of the Colliders list on a DynamicBonePlayerColliders Component
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneSphereCollider.Radius: The radius of the sphere created by this component in local space of the Slot it is made on.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneSphereColliderGizmo: The DynamicBoneSphereColliderGizmo allows for interacting with Dynamic Bone Sphere Colliders via a Sphere Gizmo. these are usually created by activating the gizmo option using the context menu while having a slot with a Dynamic Bone Sphere Collider selected.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneSphereColliderGizmo._: The collider to make a visual/gizmo for.
The gizmo being used to control <mark=#ffffff10>_target</mark> through this component.
messages.Tooltip.MeshUVRaycastPortal: This component also known as a raycast portal allows users to interact with objects through a camera.
messages.Tooltip.MeshUVRaycastPortal.Offset: How far back from the camera's actual view direction to project from
messages.Tooltip.MeshUVRaycastPortal.UVOffset: The added offset to the UV point you hit on the mesh before it's projected out; best to keep this (0,0) unless needed
messages.Tooltip.MeshUVRaycastPortal.UVScale: The multiplied offset to the UV point you hit on the mesh before it's projected out; best to keep this (1,1) unless needed
messages.Tooltip.MeshUVRaycastPortal.RepeatUV: Whether to repeat between the 0-1 range when the laser on the source hits a UV beyond 0-1
messages.Tooltip.MeshUVRaycastPortal.OverrideHitT: Whether to hit colliders, requires Filter to have a sync delegate, but doesn't need to be enabled for this component to work
messages.Tooltip.MeshUVRaycastPortal.Filter: This is a field usually used by the dash manager in local space. Very hard to use, and is not required at all
messages.Tooltip.MeshUVRaycastPortal.FilterMode: how to use the <mark=#ffffff10>Filter</mark> if provided.
messages.Tooltip.SlotRaycastTransferPortal: The SlotRaycastTransferPortal acts very similarly to the Component:MeshUVRaycastPortal except that it works on colliders hitting a slot hiearchy to another slot rather than just a mesh and a camera.
messages.Tooltip.SlotRaycastTransferPortal.Exit: The slot to send the raycast through if it hits this slot's hierarchy, converting the hit point / direction in this component slot's local space to the space of the slot specified here.
messages.Tooltip.SlotRaycastTransferPortal.Overri: Whether to override if a laser coming through will hit triggers and what to override it with.
messages.Tooltip.SlotRaycastTransferPortal.Filter: The filter we should apply if a raycast comes through this portal?
If a raycast being transfered has its own filter, how should we combine it with <mark=#ffffff10>Filter</mark> if it exists?.
messages.Tooltip.BoxColliderGizmo: The BoxColliderGizmo component is used to resize and move Component:BoxColliders in a visual manner using the DevTip.
messages.Tooltip.BoxColliderGizmo._target: The box collider this component is currently editing
messages.Tooltip.BoxColliderGizmo._cubeGizmo: The visual for this component which is used to edit <mark=#ffffff10>_target</mark> in a visual manner.
messages.Tooltip.SphereColliderGizmo: The SphereColliderGizmo component is used to adjust the radius of a sphere collider.
messages.Tooltip.SphereColliderGizmo._sphereGizmo: The gizmo to use to edit the sphere collider size.
messages.Tooltip.ColliderUserTracker: Attach this component to a slot, and add a Collider to the slot.
messages.Tooltip.ColliderUserTracker.IsLocalUserI: Becomes set for the local user if the local user is within the collider of the object that this component is attached to.
messages.Tooltip.ColliderUserTracker.IsAnyUserIns: Becomes set for all users if any user is within the collider of the object that this component is attached to.
messages.Tooltip.ColliderUserTracker.NumberOfUser: Tracks the number of users inside the collider of the object that this component is attached to.
messages.Tooltip.ColliderUserTracker._usersInside: Data on the users that are inside the collider of the object that this component is attached to.
messages.Tooltip.AlwaysOnFacetModule: The AlwaysOnFacetModule component allows apart of a facet to remain active while the dashboard is closed.
messages.Tooltip.AlwaysOnFacetModule._targets: A list of Slots that should remain active when the dash is closed.
messages.Tooltip.GridContainer: The GridContainer component allows for placing facets in a grid format and storing them under a container.
messages.Tooltip.FacetContainer.EditMode: Whether this grid container is currently being edited (UI edit mode)
messages.Tooltip.FacetContainer.FacetsRoot: Where to put facets when they are placed on the grid.
messages.Tooltip.FacetContainer._background: The UIX rectangle that specifies what the background is.
messages.Tooltip.FacetContainer._content: The UIX rectangle for the content like facets.
messages.Tooltip.FacetContainer._overlay: The UIX rectangle for the grid Overlay when editing.
messages.Tooltip.GridContainer.RecalculateOnSizeC: Whether to recalculate the facets on size change of the canvas.
messages.Tooltip.GridContainer.CellCount: The forced cell count of this grid container.
messages.Tooltip.GridContainer.CellSize: How big each cell is in pixels.
messages.Tooltip.GridContainer.Padding: How much padding between facets there should be.
messages.Tooltip.GridContainer._lastCalculatedCen: The last centering offset calculated by this component, if at all.
messages.Tooltip.GridContainer._lastCalculatedCel: The last cell size calculated by this component, if at all. Created when <mark=#ffffff10>CellCount</mark> is specified.
messages.Tooltip.GridContainer._lastCalculatedPad: The last calculated padding between facets if at all.
messages.Tooltip.GridContainerPreset: The GridContainerPreset component generates a Component:GridContainerScreen tab and set it up when not initialized before.
messages.Tooltip.GridContainerPreset._initializer: The C# Type of the object that initialized this preset.
messages.Tooltip.GridContainerPreset._initialized: The version that this preset initialized. Used to reinitialize the Grid Container Screen if it has new updates.
messages.Tooltip.GridContainerPreset._isModified: Whether the Grid Container Screen has new content like facets.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu: The ContextMenu component is the main component that handles the functionality of every user's Context Menu.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu.Owner: The user that is using this context menu
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu.Pointer: the slot that represents the pointer the user is interacting with the menu with.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu.Separation: How many degrees of separation each arc button has.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu.LabelSize: The size of image labels on the buttons
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu.RadiusRatio: The ratio of the button arcs vs the size of the circle in the middle.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._currentSummoner: The thing opening the menu currently
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._canvas: The UIX canvas rendering the buttons.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._arcLayout: The layout being used to arrange the buttons
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._canvasActive: The active field of the canvas this context menu's visuals are being drawn on.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._colliderEnabled: The active field of the collider of the context menu to allow interaction with it.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._iconImage: The icon of the center circle button. Used to display the image of the hovered button option.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._separation: The field used to control the separation of button options in the context menu.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._offsetMin: The offset min field of the radial menu section
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._offsetMax: The offset max field of the radial menu section.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._innerCircle: The graphic of the inner circle for the context menu
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._innerCircleButton: The button part of the inner circle of the context menu.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._innerCircleAnchorMi: The anchor min field of the rectangle transform of the inner circle graphic. Used to control how it looks when flicking and changing its size.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._innerCircleAnchorMa: The anchor max field of the rectangle transform of the inner circle graphic. Used to control how it looks when flicking and changing its size.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._itemsRoot: the slot that contains all the arc items that makes up the context menu options.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._arcMaterial: The material used to draw the menu options in the context meny.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._fontMaterial: The material used to render the text for the context menu options
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._spriteMaterial: The material used to render the image sprites on the buttons in the context menu.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._arcOverlay: The enable Overlay mode on the arc material. Used to control if the context menu should appear ontop of everything else.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._fontOverlay: The enable Overlay mode on the font material. Used to control if the context menu should appear ontop of everything else.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._spriteOverlay: The enable Overlay mode on the sprite material. Used to control if the context menu should appear ontop of everything else.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._arcZTest: the Ztest field on the arc material. Used to control if the context menu should appear ontop of everything else.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._fontZTest: the Ztest field on the font material. Used to control if the context menu should appear ontop of everything else.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._spriteZTest: the Ztest field on the spite material. Used to control if the context menu should appear ontop of everything else.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._zwriteArc: the Zwrite field on the arc material. Used to control if the context menu should appear ontop of everything else.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._zwriteText: the Zwrite field on the font material. Used to control if the context menu should appear ontop of everything else.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._arcRenderQueue: the render queue field on the arc material. Used to control if the context menu should appear ontop of everything else.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._fontRenderQueue: the render queue field on the font material. Used to control if the context menu should appear ontop of everything else.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._spriteRenderQueue: the render queue field on the sprite material. Used to control if the context menu should appear ontop of everything else.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._canvasOffset: Used to control if the context menu should appear ontop of everything else.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._lerp: Lerps between 0 and 1. 0 for context menu closed and 1 for context menu open.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._state: Communicates what opening stage the context menu is in.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._flickModeActive: Whether the context menu currently is being flicked.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._flickEnabled: Whether the context menu currently allows flicking.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._hidden: Whether the context menu is currently hidden from every user except <mark=#ffffff10>Owner</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenu._selectedItem: The item currently being hovered over by the user's pointer.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenuItem: A component that is used internally within context menus. If you want to create a custom context menu, you should look at ContextMenuItemSource.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenuItem.Highlight: Set and unset whether the item is being hovered over and should be highlighted.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenuItem.Label: The label element of this item.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenuItem._highlighted: The item is being hovered over.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenuItem._arc: The arc that makes up this item.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenuItem._outerRadius: The field that specifies the outer radius of this item.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenuItem._button: The button that makes up this item.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenuItemSource: Attach to a slot, and provide a name and color.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenuItemSource.Label: The text label next to the item.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenuItemSource.Color: The background/border color of the item.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenuItemSource.Sprite: The icon that is displayed on the item.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenuItemSource.ButtonEnab: Whether or not the button is active. If false, it will be greyed out and unclickable.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenuItemSource.AllowDrag: Whether or not the user can drag onto the button from the center of the context menu to click it.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenuItemSource.CloseMenuO: Whether or not the context menu should be closed after the item is pressed.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenuSubmenu: Attach this component to a slot and attach a Context Menu Item Source along side it.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenuSubmenu.ItemsRoot: All Context Menu Item Sources on slots immediately under this one will be included in the submenu.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenuSubmenu.SearchWholeHi: Display all items under <mark=#ffffff10>ItemsRoot</mark> including sub slots, not just ones immediately under.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenuSubmenu.DisableFlick: Whether to allow opening of the sub menu by clicking on the context menu center and moving it to this component's Context Menu Item Source on the same slot.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenuSubmenu.SpeedOverride: Override the speed at which the new context menu will open.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenuSubmenu.CounterClockw: Whether the items should be arranged clockwise or counter-clockwise in the submenu.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenuSubmenu.KeepPosition: When set, the context menu will stay in the same place as the submenu opens. Otherwise, the context menu will recenter on the user's laser.
messages.Tooltip.ContextMenuSubmenu.Hidden: If set, the submenu is only visible to the user that opened it.
messages.Tooltip.RootContextMenuItem: Root context menu item is a component that makes a new context menu item appear for the active user for when they open their context menu.
messages.Tooltip.RootContextMenuItem.OnlyForSide: When set, the item will only be inserted when the context menu is opened on the specified hand.
messages.Tooltip.RootContextMenuItem.ExcludeOnToo: Determines whether or not the item shows up when opening the context menu while having a tool equipped on the same hand.
messages.Tooltip.RootContextMenuItem.ExcludePrima: Makes the item not show up when the context menu is open on the user's primary hand.
messages.Tooltip.RootContextMenuItem.ExcludeSecon: Makes the item not show up when the context menu is open on the user's secondary hand.
messages.Tooltip.RootContextMenuItem.Item: The ContextMenuItemSource that will be inserted into the context menu.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash: RadiantDash controls the opening and closing animations of the dash menu. When this component is created, it automatically creates child slots and the components it references.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash.CurrentScreen: The current screen this is showing.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash.ScreenProjection: Whether this should be rendering in desktop mode.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash.AspectRatioCompensat: How much to compensate the animation for curvature
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash.ScreenSwitchingEnabl: Whether to allow user screen switching.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._screensContainer: The slot to store different screens.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._camera: The camera being used to render the screen onto the curved surface.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._renderTexture: the texture to render to the curved surface.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._topContainer: The slot to store top elements.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._topMesh: The mesh to show the top elements.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._screenMesh: The mesh to show the screen elements.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._buttonsMesh: The mesh to show the button elements.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._topMaterial: The material to use for the top element.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._screenMaterial: The material to use for the screen element.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._buttonsMaterial: The material to use for the buttons element.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._overlayEffectMateri: The material to use for an overlay effect.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._topBorderMaterial: The material to use for the top border.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._screenBorderMateria: The material to use for the screen border.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._buttonsBorderMateri: The material to use for the buttons border.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._renderRoot: The slot to render with the camera to create the visual for the curved screen.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._topRoot: The root of the top element renderable.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._screenRoot: The root of the screen element renderable.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._buttonsRoot: The root of the button element renderable.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._topCanvas: The UIX canvas of the top element.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._buttonsUIroot: The root slot of the buttons element.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._buttonsCanvas: The UIX canvas of the buttons element.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._style: The style of the particle system creating the screen animation opening and closing edge.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._particleColors: The color of the particles being spawned in when the dash is opening and closing.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._emitter: The emitter for the opening and closing animation.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._topCollider: The collider for the top element.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._screenCollider: The collider for the screens element.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDash._buttonsCollider: The collider for the buttons element.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashButton.Dash: The dash this is a button to.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashButton.Screen: The screen this button triggers a switch to for.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashButton._switchingEnab: The field to drive with whether switching is enabled.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashButton._screenEnabled: The field to drive with whether the screen is enabled.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashButton._button: The button to trigger switching to the target screen.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashButton._layout: The layout UIX element of the button.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashButton._rootRect: The root Rect UIX element for this button.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashButton._iconRect: The root Rect UIX element for the button's icon.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashProjectionMapper.Dash: The Dash this is pointing to.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashProjectionMapper.Root: The root of the thing this is trying to project from.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashProjectionMapper.Mesh: The mesh this is trying to project to.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashProjectionMapper.UVSc: The scale field to drive in order to do the remapping.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashProjectionMapper.UVOf: The offset field to drive in order to do the remapping.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashScreen.Icon: The icon for this dialogue screen.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashScreen.ActiveColor: The color when this dash menu screen tab is selected.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashScreen.Label: The text for this dash menu Screen tab.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashScreen.ScreenEnabled: Whether this dash screen is enabled or not.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashScreen.BaseResolution: The default resolution of the tab screen.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashScreen._screenRoot: The root slot of this screen's visual elements.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashScreen._screenCanvas: The canvas Component used to render this dash screen.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashScreen._modalOverlayM: This is the Overlay manager that handles editing mode on this screen.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashScreen._button: This is the button to switch to viewing this screen.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashScreen._iconTexture: This is the texture Component showing the icon for the tab of this screen.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashStateSync: Best used when attached to an item that will exist on the dash, like a facet.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashStateSync.IsFreeform: Whether the userspace dash is set to freeform movement.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashStateSync.IsOpen: Whether the userspace dash is currently open.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantDashStateSync.Curvature: What the curvature of the dash is currently.
messages.Tooltip.MoveUpDataFeedCategory: The MoveUpDataFeedCategory is a component that receives Button Events and goes to the previous parent path in a Data Feed view.
messages.Tooltip.MoveUpDataFeedCategory.View: The view to go up in category for.
messages.Tooltip.OpenDataFeedCategory: The OpenDataFeedCategory component receives Button Events and will open the Category with the path defined by the list CategorySubpath.
messages.Tooltip.OpenDataFeedCategory.View: The view to change the directory for.
messages.Tooltip.OpenDataFeedCategory.CategorySub: A list of path directories (Like a folder path split by the slashes) to define the category to go to starting from root for <mark=#ffffff10>View</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.SetDataFeedCategory: The SetDataFeedCategory sets the Path field for View when it receives a button event from a button on the same slot or somewhere else.
messages.Tooltip.SetDataFeedCategory.View: The view to change the Path for.
messages.Tooltip.SetDataFeedCategory.CategoryPath: What to set the path to for <mark=#ffffff10>View</mark>
messages.Tooltip.SetDataFeedCategory.IsInsideCate: Whether the View is already under <mark=#ffffff10>CategoryPath</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.DebugFeed: The DebugFeed component sends a list of Categories, then Labels, then finally String field values which are driven by the generated templates by a feed Mapper.
messages.Tooltip.DebugFeed.CategoryCount: The amount of categories to put at the beginning of the feed.
messages.Tooltip.DebugFeed.LabelItemCount: The amount of label Feed items to put at the beginning of the feed.
messages.Tooltip.DebugFeed.StringItemCount: The amount of String fields generated by the feed.
messages.Tooltip.DebugFeed.StringValues: Is automatically filled with values driven by the content of Feed value fields generated by the feed mapper creating templates from the data in this feed.
messages.Tooltip.EnumFeed`1: The EnumFeed component acts like a kind of data feed that provides a list of enums that can be further used by feeds with filtering and searching.
messages.Tooltip.EnumFeed`1.DisplayOrder: Whether to feed the enums in order.
messages.Tooltip.EnumFeed`1.Distinct: Whether the enums should be distinct when gathering.
messages.Tooltip.EnumFeed`1.IncludeObsolete: Whether the enums should be gathered despite being tagged as obsolete.
messages.Tooltip.WorldUsersFeed: The WorldUsersFeed can be used to build systems that need a list of users currently in the session.
messages.Tooltip.FeedActionInterface: The FeedActionInterface component is used as a template type item in data feed mappers in the Data Feeds system.
messages.Tooltip.FeedItemInterface.HasData: The field to set when this interface is templated by a data feed for whether it has data or not.
messages.Tooltip.FeedItemInterface.ItemName: The field to fill with the name of the interface item when it is templated via a data feed. For settings toggles, this would be the toggle name.
messages.Tooltip.FeedItemInterface.ItemKey: The field to fill with the interfaces key if it is a dictionary type item.
messages.Tooltip.FeedItemInterface.ItemDescriptio: The field to fill with the template description when template via a data feed.
messages.Tooltip.FeedItemInterface.HasDescription: Whether the item when templated via a data feed has a description.
messages.Tooltip.FeedItemInterface.DescriptionCle: If this interface doesn't have a description during templating via a data feed, then delete the slot specified.
messages.Tooltip.FeedItemInterface.ItemIcon: The resource URL to fill with an image link/URI if the data feed item has an icon.
messages.Tooltip.FeedItemInterface.HasIcon: Whether this template has an icon. This is set during duplication as a template via a data feed.
messages.Tooltip.FeedItemInterface.IconCleanup: The slot to delete when this interface item doesn't have an icon when being duplicated as a template item by a data feed.
messages.Tooltip.FeedItemInterface.View: The data feed view that this template was instantiated by. Like a SingleFeedView.
messages.Tooltip.FeedItemInterface.ParentContaine: This template's parent container. If that template also has a parent and a child, then it recursively goes up the containers till it finds the root interface with a parent and a child interface, then instantiates that interfaces child interface and it's Nested items instead of just this interface.
messages.Tooltip.FeedItemInterface.ChildContainer: This template's child template which it instanciates if it's part of a parent child structure of interfaces.
messages.Tooltip.FeedItemInterface.NestedItems: A list of extra data feed items to instantiate the same as this one with the same arguments, except each one can be Nested under this item.
messages.Tooltip.FeedItemInterface.EnabledState: The enabled state of this feed interface item UI element.
messages.Tooltip.FeedActionInterface.Action: The field to fill with the Action Sync delegate when this is set up as a template via a data feed.
messages.Tooltip.FeedActionInterface.Highlight: The bool to toggle when this item is highlighted.
messages.Tooltip.FeedValueActionInterface`1: The FeedValueActionInterface component is used as a template type item in data feed mappers in the Data Feeds system. This component specifically passes a value to an action that requires a value argument to be passed.
messages.Tooltip.FeedValueActionInterface`1.Actio: The field to fill with the Action to be triggered.
messages.Tooltip.FeedValueActionInterface`1.Value: The  field to fill with the value that should be passed.
messages.Tooltip.FeedValueActionInterface`1.Highl: Whether this item should be highlighted or is being highlighted.
messages.Tooltip.FeedCategoryInterface: The FeedCategoryInterface component is used as a template type item in data feed mappers in the Data Feeds system.
messages.Tooltip.FeedCategoryInterface.Path: A list of strings specifying this categories specific sub path.
messages.Tooltip.FeedClampedValueFieldInterface`1: The FeedClampedValueFieldInterface component is used as a template type item in data feed mappers in the Data Feeds system. This component allows for making a UI Template for changing a value with a minimum and maximum value.
messages.Tooltip.FeedValueItemInterface`1.Value: The field that represents the data that should be read from and written to the source data feed.
messages.Tooltip.FeedValueItemInterface`1.Formatt: The field to populate with the format string that should be used when displaying the value.
messages.Tooltip.FeedClampedValueFieldInterface`1: The value Field to fill with the minimum value this feed number can be.
The value field to fill with the maximum value this feed number can be.
messages.Tooltip.FeedEntityInterface`1: The FeedEntityInterface component is used as a template type item in data feed mappers in the Data Feeds system. This is most commonly used with the WorldUsersFeed feed to template user items from the feed.
messages.Tooltip.FeedEntityInterface`1.Reference: The field to fill with the reference value from the source data feed.
messages.Tooltip.FeedGridInterface: The FeedGridInterface component is used as a template type item in data feed mappers in the Data Feeds system.
messages.Tooltip.FeedGroupInterface: The FeedGroupInterface component is used as a template type item in data feed mappers in the Data Feeds system.
messages.Tooltip.FeedIndicatorInterface`1: The FeedIndicatorInterface component is used as a template type item in data feed mappers in the Data Feeds system. This works as a value viewing module for Feed items, as well as providing the wanted format for the item.
messages.Tooltip.FeedIndicatorInterface`1.Value: The value field to fill and update from the data feed with.
messages.Tooltip.FeedIndicatorInterface`1.Format: The field to fill with the format string the source data feed has specified should be used with this value when displaying it.
messages.Tooltip.FeedItemInterface: The FeedItemInterface component is used as a template type item in data feed mappers in the Data Feeds system.
messages.Tooltip.FeedOrderedItemInterface`1: The FeedOrderedItemInterface component is used as a template type item in data feed mappers in the Data Feeds system. This acts as an item in a list of items that can be reordered and moved around.
messages.Tooltip.FeedOrderedItemInterface`1.IsFir: The field to set to whether this item is currently first
messages.Tooltip.FeedOrderedItemInterface`1.IsLas: The field to set to whether this item is currently last.
messages.Tooltip.FeedOrderedItemInterface`1.MoveU: The field that represents this item's move up label.
messages.Tooltip.FeedOrderedItemInterface`1.MoveD: The field that represents this item's move down label.
messages.Tooltip.FeedOrderedItemInterface`1.MakeF: The field that represents this item's make first label.
messages.Tooltip.FeedOrderedItemInterface`1.MakeL: The field that represents this item's make last label.
messages.Tooltip.FeedOrderedItemInterface`1.MoveU: This field is populated with the Action that can be called to move the item up.
messages.Tooltip.FeedOrderedItemInterface`1.MoveD: This field is populated with the Action that can be called to move the item down.
messages.Tooltip.FeedOrderedItemInterface`1.MakeF: This field is populated with the Action that can be called to make the item first.
messages.Tooltip.FeedOrderedItemInterface`1.MakeL: This field is populated with the Action that can be called to make the item last.
messages.Tooltip.FeedOrderedItemInterface`1.HasMo: The field to set to whether this item can currently be moved further up.
The field to set to whether this item can currently be moved down.
messages.Tooltip.FeedOrderedItemInterface`1.HasMa: The field to set to whether this item can currently be made first.
The field to set to whether this item can currently be made last.
messages.Tooltip.FeedQuantityFieldInterface`2: The FeedQuantityFieldInterface component is used as a template type item in data feed mappers in the Data Feeds system.
messages.Tooltip.FeedQuantityFieldInterface`2.Def: The field to fill with the denoter for the default unit for this quantity type.
messages.Tooltip.FeedQuantityFieldInterface`2.For: Usually points to the FormatUnit on a QuantityTextFormatDriver.
Usually points to the FormatNumber on a QuantityTextFormatDriver.
messages.Tooltip.FeedQuantityFieldInterface`2.Com: Usually points to the CompoundFormatUnits on a QuantityTextFormatDriver.
Usually points to the CompoundOverrideNames on a QuantityTextFormatDriver.
Usually points to the CompoundUseLongNames on a QuantityTextFormatDriver.
Usually points to the CompoundSeparator on a QuantityTextFormatDriver.
messages.Tooltip.FeedResettableGroupInterface.Res: The action that can be used to reset the child settings items.
messages.Tooltip.FeedSliderInterface`1: The FeedSliderInterface component is used as a template type item in data feed mappers in the Data Feeds system. This component in particular is used to make sliders for stuff like volume or motion blur.
messages.Tooltip.FeedSliderInterface`1.ReferenceV: The default reference value field for this slider (ex: this is a value that the slider should have by default)
The slot to delete if a reference value is not needed.
messages.Tooltip.FeedToggleInterface: The FeedToggleInterface component is used as a template type item in data feed mappers in the Data Feeds system. This component specifically is used for toggle things like check boxes.
messages.Tooltip.FeedValueFieldInterface`1: The FeedValueFieldInterface component is used as a template type item in data feed mappers in the Data Feeds system. This component allows for making a UI Template for changing a value.
messages.Tooltip.DataFeedItemMapper: The DataFeedItemMapper allows you to define which data feed items map to which UI templates. It does this via the list Mappings, which take classes that extend DataFeedItem and If they match a mapping in the list it Duplicates that item's template and adds it into the list.
messages.Tooltip.DataFeedItemMapper.Mappings: A list of item Mappings to map data feed item types to Feed Templates.
messages.Tooltip.DebugDataFeedView: The DebugDataFeedView component shows the data from a feed including calls to update already provided data from the feed.
messages.Tooltip.DataFeedViewBase.Feed: The Feed to view items for in this data feed view.
messages.Tooltip.DataFeedViewBase.Path: The path within the feed to view.
messages.Tooltip.DataFeedViewBase.GroupingKeys: A list of grouping keys to group alike data feed items together from the incoming data.
messages.Tooltip.DataFeedViewBase.SearchPhrase: The phrase to search for within the current list of feed items  under <mark=#ffffff10>Path</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.DataFeedViewBase.UpdatingUser: The user handling the logic for this data feed view.
messages.Tooltip.DataFeedViewBase.ResetViewOnSave: Whether to clear the view upon saving this data feed view.
messages.Tooltip.DebugDataFeedView.TargetOutput: The field (usually the text on a text renderer) to update with the debug feed data.
messages.Tooltip.RootCategoryView: The RootCategoryView component is commonly used in Settings, and Worlds Menu view to show the list of root view categories while still showing the current Path like audio device lists, trackers, worlds, or anything else feeds might have.
messages.Tooltip.RootCategoryView.CategoryManager: The Mapper to handle showing categories in the root regardless of what <mark=#ffffff10>Path</mark> is.
messages.Tooltip.RootCategoryView.ItemsManager: The Mapper to handle showing items within a selected <mark=#ffffff10>Path</mark>, no matter how deep of a <mark=#ffffff10>Path</mark> this is viewing.
messages.Tooltip.SingleFeedView: The SingleFeedView is the simplest and most commonly used data feed viewer used to usually view users in a session from a WorldUsersFeed.
messages.Tooltip.SingleFeedView.ItemsManager: The Mapper to handle showing items within a selected <mark=#ffffff10>Path</mark>, no matter how deep of a <mark=#ffffff10>Path</mark> this is viewing.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreatorFacetPreset: The AvatarCreatorFacetPreset component loads the Avatar Creator facet when it's not _fullyLoaded. It does this by loading the facet from the cloud, and using that loaded data to make the preset
messages.Tooltip.TemplateFacetPreset._fullyLoaded: Whether or not the facet has been fully generated and doesn't need to load again.
messages.Tooltip.ClipboardPasteFacetPreset: The ClipboardPasteFacetPreset component loads the Clipboard Paste facet when it's not _fullyLoaded. It does this by loading the facet from the cloud, and using that loaded data to make the preset
messages.Tooltip.ControllerDiagramFacetPreset: The ControllerDiagramFacetPreset component loads the Controller Diagram facet and controls the texture URI of _texture
messages.Tooltip.ControllerDiagramFacetPreset._te: The texture to change the URI to the user's controller scheme (VR controller of their type or desktop controls)

messages.Tooltip.NewWorldFacetPreset: The NewWorldFacetPreset component loads the New World facet when it's not _fullyLoaded. It does this by loading the facet from the cloud, and using that loaded data to make the preset
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatusFacetPreset: The OnlineStatusFacetPreset component loads the Online Status facet when it's not _fullyLoaded. It does this by loading the facet from the cloud, and using that loaded data to make the preset
messages.Tooltip.OnlineUserCountFacetPreset: The OnlineUserCountFacetPreset component loads the Online User Count facet when it's not _fullyLoaded. It does this by loading the facet from the cloud, and using that loaded data to make the preset
messages.Tooltip.OpenDebugFacetPreset: The OpenDebugFacetPreset component loads the Open Debug facet when it's not _fullyLoaded. It does this by loading the facet from the cloud, and using that loaded data to make the preset
messages.Tooltip.OpenHomeFacetPreset: The OpenHomeFacetPreset component loads the Open Home facet when it's not _fullyLoaded. It does this by loading the facet from the cloud, and using that loaded data to make the preset
messages.Tooltip.PagingControlFacetPreset: The PagingControlFacetPreset component makes a container that stores a PagingControl component on the dash and tells that component to build a UIX for itself inside of this container.
messages.Tooltip.PagingControlFacetPreset._paging: The paging control component for this facet
messages.Tooltip.SearchBarFacetPreset: The SearchBarFacetPreset component is used to control search functions in different parts of the game
messages.Tooltip.SearchBarFacetPreset._searchBar: The searching component this should be controlling
messages.Tooltip.SeatedModeFacetPreset: The SeatedModeFacetPreset component loads the Seated Mode facet when it's not _fullyLoaded. It does this by loading the facet from the cloud, and using that loaded data to make the preset
messages.Tooltip.ServerStatusFacetPreset: The ServerStatusFacetPreset component loads the Server Status facet when it's not _fullyLoaded.
messages.Tooltip.SettingsFacetPreset: The SettingsFacetPreset component loads the Settings facet when it's not _fullyLoaded. It does this by loading the facet from the cloud, and using that loaded data to make the preset
messages.Tooltip.Setup2FAFacetPreset: The Setup2FAFacetPreset component loads the Setup 2FA facet when it's not _fullyLoaded. It does this by loading the facet from the cloud, and using that loaded data to make the preset
messages.Tooltip.SimpleProfileFacetPreset: The SimpleProfileFacetPreset component loads the Simple Profile facet when it's not _fullyLoaded. It does this by loading the facet from the cloud, and using that loaded data to make the preset
messages.Tooltip.StorageIndicatorFacetPreset: The StorageIndicatorFacetPreset component loads the Storage Indicator facet when it's not _fullyLoaded. It does this by loading the facet from the cloud, and using that loaded data to make the preset
messages.Tooltip.StreamAudioFacetPreset: The StreamAudioFacetPreset component loads the Stream Audio facet when it's not _fullyLoaded. It does this by loading the facet from the cloud, and using that loaded data to make the preset
messages.Tooltip.StreamingCameraFacetPreset: The StreamingCameraFacetPreset component loads the Streaming Camera facet when it's not _fullyLoaded. It does this by loading the facet from the cloud, and using that loaded data to make the preset
messages.Tooltip.SyncStatusFacetPreset: The SyncStatusFacetPreset component loads the Sync Status facet when it's not _fullyLoaded. It does this by loading the facet from the cloud, and using that loaded data to make the preset
messages.Tooltip.TimeFacetPreset: The TimeFacetPreset component loads the Time facet when it's not _fullyLoaded. It does this by loading the facet from the cloud, and using that loaded data to make the preset

messages.Tooltip.Facet: The Facet Component is the component that drives every user made facet in the game. This component specifically handles the sizes in which a facet can/can't, and prefers to be placed. It also reports the last size a facet was placed, the aspect ratio sizes it can be placed, and the Canvas that makes up the facet's contents.
messages.Tooltip.Facet.MinWidth: The minimum width this facet can be.
messages.Tooltip.Facet.MaxWidth: The maximum width this facet can be.
messages.Tooltip.Facet.MinHeight: The minimum height this facet can be.
messages.Tooltip.Facet.MaxHeight: The maximum height this facet can be.
messages.Tooltip.Facet.LastPlacedSize: The last size that this Facet was placed as.
messages.Tooltip.Facet.PreferredSizes: A list of sizes this canvas prefers to be placed when being dropped onto a facet holder.
messages.Tooltip.Facet.AllowedAspectRatios: A list of allowed ratios between width and height this facet allows when being placed, lest the placement guide turns red and prevents placement.
messages.Tooltip.Facet.IsStandalone: Whether this facet has not been placed in a dash or otherwise and is out in free form space.
messages.Tooltip.Facet.Canvas: The canvas being used for the visuals of this Facet.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantSearchBar: The RadiantSearchBar component is most commonly used and found in the World Browser.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantSearchBar.SearchTerm: The term this is searching for.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantSearchBar.IsSearching: Whether this component is currently searching.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantSearchBar.SearchAnimTileS: How fast the search animation scrolling speed should be.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantSearchBar.SearchAnimColor: What the color of the search bar should be while searching.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantSearchBar._textField: The text field for entering a search.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantSearchBar._searchText: The text UIX Component for searching
messages.Tooltip.RadiantSearchBar._defaultText: The text UIX Component that should be visible if a search term has not yet been entered.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantSearchBar._searchingVisua: The visual to show while searching
messages.Tooltip.RadiantSearchBar._cancelButton: The button that can be used to cancel a currently running search.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantSearchBar._defaultVisible: The field to drive with what should be visible by default.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantSearchBar._searchingAnima: The field to drive for the color of the searching animation visual.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantSearchBar._searchingTextu: The offset field to drive to create the searching animation scrolling visual.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionLaser: The InteractionLaser component is part of the user to world interaction interfacing. This component is entirely regenerated separate from the avatar per respawn, making it un-reference-able.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionLaser.SmoothSpeed: How much to smooth laser movement for this laser.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionLaser.SmoothModulateS: Angle at which the laser smoothing speed will start modulating to catch up to your movements. Increasing this value will require larger movements for the laser to start catching up.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionLaser.SmoothModulateE: Related to the modulate start angle, this indicates when the modulation will reach maximum speed. By changing the start and end angles, you can control how responsive the laser is the more you move your hand.

Controls the response curve of the laser modulation between the start and end angles. You can use this to either make the laser respond more sluggishly at first or to start responding quickly and then tapering off.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionLaser.SmoothModulateM: The laser smoothing speed will be multiplied by this value once you have reached the modulate end angle. Increasing this value will make the laser catch up quicker once you move your hand far enough. Lowering the value will make it take longer to catch up.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionLaser.StickThreshold: Controls how much the laser will stick to interactive items. Larger values will make it stick more. Setting this to zero will stop the stickiness completely.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionLaser.ShowInDesktop: When on, the laser visual will be rendered to you fully when in first person view in desktop mode.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionLaser.StickPointSpace: The slot the laser is sticking to.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionLaser.StickPointPosit: The position the laser is sticking to.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionLaser._handler: The interaction handler used to handle behavior for this component.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionLaser._lastHit: The last slot hit by the laser.

messages.Tooltip.BreadcrumbInterface: The BreadcrumbInterface component is used by data feeds to get the translated name of one directory in a list of directories that makes a path. This is most commonly used in the Settings at the top to show a user where in the settings they are.

messages.Tooltip.Workspace: The Workspace component allows for doing work inside of a containerized slot, where the user can add or remove items which gets saved every interval specified. These workspaces can be public or private, and work similarly in concept to Cloud Variables except greatly simplified and store an entire hierarchy instead of a single value. Workspaces have a path to them, which allow for having multiple different workspaces per user. Workspaces take up storage as a saved item would, and automatically synchronize in real time. Multiple users working on workspaces at the same time can cause issues however, since workspaces save their changes before loading from the cloud unless ReadOnly is enabled.
messages.Tooltip.Workspace.User: The user to manage reading and writing the contents of this workspace to the cloud for.
messages.Tooltip.Workspace.OverrideOwnerId: The override for the owner of this workspace to read and write the data for, which allows for reading public workspaces from others.
messages.Tooltip.Workspace.WorkspacePath: The path of the workspace on the Cloud. Adding "public/" (case ignored) to the start of the path allows for public workspaces anyone can edit. Public workspaces must exist first before another user than the owner can save to them.
messages.Tooltip.Workspace.InitializeWorkspace: A Sync Delegate to call that takes a slot for when this component generates the work space. the slot is the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.Workspace.ReadOnly: Whether to only read the contents of this workspace from the cloud and not save it.
messages.Tooltip.Workspace.AutosaveDelay: How long to wait between save attempts before trying to save again. saves only happen if there have been changes.
messages.Tooltip.Workspace.SuspendUpdates: Whether to pause the loading and saving of this workspace to the cloud.
messages.Tooltip.Workspace._unsavedChanges: Whether this workspace has changes that should be saved to the cloud upon the next save.
messages.Tooltip.Workspace._lastModification: The last time the workspace was changed.
