messages.Tooltip.Byte4ToColor32SwizzleDriver.Sour: The field which to take values from it's components and drive <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark>'s components with them.
messages.Tooltip.Byte4ToColor32SwizzleDriver.Targ: The field which to drive the components of.
messages.Tooltip.Tween`1: The Tween component is usually auto generated by ProtoFlux or by holding an object and hitting primary to align the object. The purpose of this component is to handle transitions in a value to value manner and only exists temporarily. This is not used for animations, only for a transition from one value to another. For animations, see either Component:ValueGradientDriver or Component:Animator
messages.Tooltip.Tween`1.User: The user handling the processing for this component.
messages.Tooltip.Tween`1.Target: The field to drive and Tween.
messages.Tooltip.Tween`1.OnlyUnderParent: Only run the component if it exists under the hierarchy of this slot, else stop the tween.
messages.Tooltip.Tween`1.StartTime: The world time this Tween should start. Usually is creation time.
messages.Tooltip.Tween`1.Duration: How long from <mark=#ffffff10>StartTime</mark> the Tween should go for.
messages.Tooltip.Tween`1.Curve: How <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> should transition from <mark=#ffffff10>From</mark> to <mark=#ffffff10>To</mark>. See CurvePreset
messages.Tooltip.Tween`1.LocalCallback: Whether the Callback should be executed by <mark=#ffffff10>User</mark>
messages.Tooltip.Tween`1.OnDone: The Sync Method to call when this is done tweening.
messages.Tooltip.ValueDriver`1: This component ensures that the value in the DriveTarget is equal to the value in the ValueSource field.
messages.Tooltip.ValueDriver`1.ValueSource: The value field to get a value from.
messages.Tooltip.ValueDriver`1.DriveTarget: The value field to set to the value of <mark=#ffffff10>ValueSource</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ValueMultiDriver`1: The ValueMultiDriver`1 component allows you to drive multiple fields from a single value.
messages.Tooltip.ValueMultiDriver`1.Value: Value of the specified type, which will be driven to all fields listed in <mark=#ffffff10>Drives</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ValueMultiDriver`1.Drives: A list of fields to be driven by this component
messages.Tooltip.AmbientLightSH2: The Ambient Light SH2 component creates ambient lighting data that creates global colored light using sampling from a Level 2 Spherical Harmonic type ColorX.
messages.Tooltip.AmbientLightSH2.AmbientLight: The spherical harmonic to sample for ambient lighting.
messages.Tooltip.AnimationTrack`1: Animation track seems to be a placeholder for future content and is unused. It does not fit into anything in the game internally and it's methods go unused (According to DNSpy 07/26/2024 (mm/dd/yyyy))
messages.Tooltip.AnimationTrack`1._node: Designed to be used to specify the object being controlled by the animation. Can be used with the Find Animation Track Index ProtoFlux node.
messages.Tooltip.AnimationTrack`1._component: Designed to be used to specify the field of the object that is being controlled by the animation. Can be used with the Find Animation Track Index ProtoFlux node.
messages.Tooltip.AnimationTrack`1._property: The property on an animation asset
messages.Tooltip.AnimationTrack`1.Data: A list of values at specific time intervals to determine what the whole track's value should be at specific times.
messages.Tooltip.Animator: Animator is a component that's used to play Animation and drive a list of fields using the contained animation data. The order and type of said fields is determined by the Animation in the Clip field, and the value sampled from the Animation for any field (Fields) is determined by the Animation's sampled value at the position the _playback is currently at. Sampling is determined by the type of Animation Clip is. You should be able to drive field by assigning ValueField's Value onto Fields.
messages.Tooltip.Animator._playback: Is populated by the length and properties of <mark=#ffffff10>Clip</mark> and is used to determine what point at which to sample the <mark=#ffffff10>Clip</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.Animator.Clip: An Animation used to determine the list of <mark=#ffffff10>Fields</mark> on this component and their value/ref types.
messages.Tooltip.Animator.Fields: the list of values stored in this animation. Each field and it's type/animation data is determined by track index order.
messages.Tooltip.AtlasInfo: Atlas info is used in the Component:UVAtlasAnimator to provide the animator with information on format of an image that is an atlas of all the frames in an animation.
messages.Tooltip.AtlasInfo.GridSize: The size of the grid that the image frames take up on the atlas (Ex: 4 by 8 grid of frames for a max of 32 total frames)
messages.Tooltip.AtlasInfo.GridFrames: The amount of frames on the animation atlas image. If the amount of frames is less than the max (the two <mark=#ffffff10>GridSize</mark> numbers multiplied together) then it will stop on frame <mark=#ffffff10>GridFrames</mark> when this component is used by UVAtlasAnimator.
messages.Tooltip.BillboardRenderBufferRenderer: The BillboardRenderBufferRenderer component is used as a debug component to determine the states of Photon Dust buffers during the simulation process.
messages.Tooltip.BillboardRenderBufferRenderer.Bu: The renderer to render point buffers.
messages.Tooltip.BillboardRenderBufferRenderer.Ma: The material to render this with.
messages.Tooltip.BillboardRenderBufferRenderer.Mo: How to render the object motion vectors.
messages.Tooltip.BillboardRenderBufferRenderer.Mi: The minimum size of particles rendered.
messages.Tooltip.BillboardRenderBufferRenderer.Ma: The maximum size of particles rendered.
messages.Tooltip.BlitToDisplay: BlitToDisplay is a component that is able to make a user's game window display a 2d texture of any kind (including render textures) and is also able to create new game windows to display images as well.
messages.Tooltip.BlitToDisplay.TargetUser: The user to display the image on their screen for. Using the local user does work.
messages.Tooltip.BlitToDisplay.Texture: The texture to display to the screen. A render texture may be used here.
messages.Tooltip.BlitToDisplay.DisplayIndex: which game window to display <mark=#ffffff10>Texture</mark> to. If the window number doesn't exist, one is created automatically. If 0 is used, the main game window will be used.
messages.Tooltip.BlitToDisplay.BackgroundColor: The color to display behind the image.
messages.Tooltip.BlitToDisplay.FlipHorizontally: flip the image horizontally before displaying to the screen.
messages.Tooltip.BlitToDisplay.FlipVertically: flip the image vertically before displaying to the screen.
messages.Tooltip.Bounds: Bounds is a component that is used by the Legacy Radio Component When being generated by a UIBuilder, such as when being a legacy radio is created by the DevTip. This component implements Type:IBounded so it can be used with anything that accepts a bounds source such as Component:BoundingBoxDriver.
messages.Tooltip.Bounds.Available: Says whether or not this component is ready. Used internally.
messages.Tooltip.Bounds.LocalBounds: The Bounding Box that this component represents
messages.Tooltip.Camera: Put the component onto a slot, and position the slot.
messages.Tooltip.Camera.DoubleBuffered: Determines if [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Multiple_buffering#Double_buffering_in_computer_graphics DoubleBuffering] is applied or not.
messages.Tooltip.Camera.ForwardOnly: Determine whether the render technique is only forward. This can badly effect performance.
messages.Tooltip.Camera.Projection: Determines whether it's [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Perspective_(graphical) perspective] or [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Orthographic_projection orthographic]
messages.Tooltip.Camera.OrthographicSize: The size of the render-output in orthographic view. (Only active when <mark=#ffffff10>Projection</mark> is set to <mark=#ffffff10>Orthographic</mark>
messages.Tooltip.Camera.FieldOfView: The vertical field of view of the camera. (Only active when <mark=#ffffff10>Projection</mark> is set to <mark=#ffffff10>Perspective</mark>
messages.Tooltip.Camera.NearClipping: The point in meters where the camera ignores near objects.
messages.Tooltip.Camera.FarClipping: The point in meters where the camera ignores far objects.
messages.Tooltip.Camera.UseTransformScale: Determines if the scale of the camera should be taken into account.
messages.Tooltip.Camera.Clear: See Camera Clear Mode for what this does.
messages.Tooltip.Camera.Viewport: 2D rectangular where the camera is allowed to render
messages.Tooltip.Camera.Depth: Whether this camera should be rendered before or after other cameras render.
messages.Tooltip.Camera.RenderTexture: The RenderTextureProvider input in order to get a ITexture2D
messages.Tooltip.Camera.Postprocessing: Determines if post processing is allowed.
messages.Tooltip.Camera.MotionBlur: Determines if MotionBlur is rendered or not.
messages.Tooltip.Camera.RenderShadows: Determines if shadows are rendered or not.
messages.Tooltip.Camera.SelectiveRender: A list of SyncReferences to slots the camera is allowed to render.
messages.Tooltip.Camera.ExcludeRender: A list of SyncReferences to slots the camera is NOT allowed to render.
messages.Tooltip.CameraPortal: Camera Portal, Also known as: Mirror, Portal, Gateway, Non Euclidean, and Bigger on the inside thing. This component allows for making a flat surface appear to have something on the other side.
messages.Tooltip.CameraPortal.Renderer: The renderer that the geometry is being used for the effect. This really only works best with flat surfaces, so use a quad in the target mesh renderer.
messages.Tooltip.CameraPortal.ReflectionTexture: The render texture that this component should render what it wants to display to. This texture only works with a Reflection Material that goes on the <mark=#ffffff10>Render</mark>'s mesh.
messages.Tooltip.CameraPortal.PlaneOffset: How far away from the surface the mirror or portal should render from. This doesn't need to be touched for a mirror, and causes some weird effects.
messages.Tooltip.CameraPortal.PlaneNormal: Rotates what the mirror is rendering. this can can cause some very strange effects for Mirrors and Portals, and is best understood by playing with it.
messages.Tooltip.CameraPortal.RenderMode: Whether this CameraPortal should act as a Mirror (Silver mirror) or as a Portal (Gateway, Non Euclidean, Tardis, ETC)
messages.Tooltip.CameraPortal.PortalTarget: The slot that should be used as a position to render from. When using Mirror <mark=#ffffff10>RenderMode</mark>, keep this as the same slot as <mark=#ffffff10>Renderer</mark>. When using Portal <mark=#ffffff10>RenderMode</mark>, set the slot as the thing you're trying to make a gateway to visually (for a tardis, this would be the interior entrance somewhere else in the world)
messages.Tooltip.CameraPortal.OverrideClear: The type of color to clear when rendering. Functionally identical to the OverrideClear on a Camera
messages.Tooltip.CameraPortal.ClearColor: If using "Color" for <mark=#ffffff10>OverrideClear</mark> use this color.
messages.Tooltip.CameraPortal.DisablePerPixelLigh: Disables lighting from points or spots in the projected image, which helps performance.
messages.Tooltip.CameraPortal.DisableShadows: Disable lights casting shadows in the projected image, which helps performance.
messages.Tooltip.CameraPortal.OverrideFarClip: Override the default farclip from 4096 to this value when enabled.
messages.Tooltip.CameraPortal.OverrideNearClip: Override the default nearclip from ~0 to this value when enabled.
messages.Tooltip.DirectVisemeDriver: The DirectVisemeDriver component takes a viseme analyser source and a few modifying sources in order to drive a list of Visemes.
messages.Tooltip.DirectVisemeDriver.Source: The viseme analyser used as a source of data.
messages.Tooltip.DirectVisemeDriver.MouthTracking: A source of mouth tracking that influences final results. Use a Component:AvatarMouthDataSourceAssigner To assign to this automatically.
messages.Tooltip.DirectVisemeDriver.StrengthMulti: How much to multiply the values from <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> before sending them to the drives on this component.
messages.Tooltip.DirectVisemeDriver.VoiceMouthSup: How much visemes should be affected by <mark=#ffffff10>MouthTrackingSource</mark> if the user is not talking. Lower values mean the affect from <mark=#ffffff10>MouthTrackingSource</mark> is greater.
messages.Tooltip.DirectVisemeDriver.LaughThreshol: How much probability for laughter is needed before it sends it to <mark=#ffffff10>Laugh</mark>
messages.Tooltip.DirectVisemeDriver.Silence: How quiet the source data is overall.
messages.Tooltip.DirectVisemeDriver.PP: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>PP</mark> from <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> after applying filters from this component.
messages.Tooltip.DirectVisemeDriver.FF: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>FF</mark> from <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> after applying filters from this component.
messages.Tooltip.DirectVisemeDriver.TH: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>TH</mark> from <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> after applying filters from this component.
messages.Tooltip.DirectVisemeDriver.DD: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>DD</mark> from <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> after applying filters from this component.
messages.Tooltip.DirectVisemeDriver.kk: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>kk</mark> from <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> after applying filters from this component.
messages.Tooltip.DirectVisemeDriver.CH: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>CH</mark> from <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> after applying filters from this component.
messages.Tooltip.DirectVisemeDriver.SS: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>SS</mark> from <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> after applying filters from this component.
messages.Tooltip.DirectVisemeDriver.nn: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>nn</mark> from <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> after applying filters from this component.
messages.Tooltip.DirectVisemeDriver.RR: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>RR</mark> from <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> after applying filters from this component.
messages.Tooltip.DirectVisemeDriver.aa: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>aa</mark> from <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> after applying filters from this component.
messages.Tooltip.DirectVisemeDriver.E: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>E</mark> from <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> after applying filters from this component.
messages.Tooltip.DirectVisemeDriver.ih: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>ih</mark> from <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> after applying filters from this component.
messages.Tooltip.DirectVisemeDriver.oh: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>oh</mark> from <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> after applying filters from this component.
messages.Tooltip.DirectVisemeDriver.ou: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>ou</mark> from <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> after applying filters from this component.
messages.Tooltip.DirectVisemeDriver.Laugh: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>Laugh</mark> from <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> after applying filters from this component.
messages.Tooltip.MaterialSet: Material set allows for switching the list of materials being used on a mesh dynamically at any given moment.
messages.Tooltip.MaterialSet.ActiveSetIndex: The index into the Sets list of the materials to apply to the target renderer.
messages.Tooltip.MaterialSet.Target: The list of materials in a renderer to swap out.
messages.Tooltip.MaterialSet.Sets: The list of lists of materials in a renderer to swap out.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicVisemeDriver: The DynamicVisemeDriver component allows using a viseme analyser to drive Float values. It allows for combinations of visemes with weights to drive a Float, and said Float may be a shapekey.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicVisemeDriver.MouthTrackin: The source of mouth tracking to influence viseme data.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicVisemeDriver.VoiceMouthSu: How much audio supresses the effect of the <mark=#ffffff10>MouthTrackingSource</mark> data.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicVisemeDriver.Drivers: A list of drivers that use the <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>MouthTrackingSource</mark> data to drive their targets.
messages.Tooltip.FontMaterial: The FontMaterial component is a Legacy component that was used for rendering text.
messages.Tooltip.FontMaterial.OutlineThickness: The thickness of the outer border of the characters.
messages.Tooltip.FontMaterial.OutlineColor: The color of the outer border on characters.
messages.Tooltip.FontMaterial.FaceSoftness: How much to soften the edges of the characters to make them not so sharp.
messages.Tooltip.FontMaterial.FaceDilate: How much to puff up the characters and make them more bold.
messages.Tooltip.FontMaterial.Culling: Whether to show the material on the front, back, or both sides
messages.Tooltip.FontMaterial.ZTest: Determines whether this object should render when it is in front of or behind other objects that respect depth from the camera
messages.Tooltip.FontMaterial.RenderQueue: changes at which point a material renders on the render stack
messages.Tooltip.FontMaterial._convertedMaterial: The converted material version of this material.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatRenderer: The GaussianSplatRenderer component renders Gaussian Splats into the world.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatRenderer.SizeScale: How much to scale up or down the gaussian splat.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatRenderer.OpacitySca: How transparent the Gaussian Splat is.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatRenderer.MaxSHOrder: The max Spherical Harmonics level to render with.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatRenderer.SphericalH: Whether to use only Spherical Harmonics.
messages.Tooltip.GenericSlicer: The GenericSlicer component is used to drive and handle the slicing of materials like volumes and PBS Slice.
messages.Tooltip.GenericSlicer.Targets: The Slicer Fields on materials to drive.
messages.Tooltip.GenericSlicer.Space: The transform space to do slicing in.
messages.Tooltip.GenericSlicer._grabbedVisual: The visual to show and hide when this Slicer is grabbed.
messages.Tooltip.LightGizmo: The Light Gizmo is used to control lights, and is generated by the Dev Tool.
messages.Tooltip.LightGizmo._pointIconActive: The field to drive to control the enabled of the point light icon.
messages.Tooltip.LightGizmo._spotIconActive: The field to drive to control the enabled of the spot light icon.
messages.Tooltip.LightGizmo._directionalIconActiv: The field to drive to control the enabled of the directional light icon.
messages.Tooltip.LightGizmo._pointActive: The field to drive to control the enabled of the point light controls.
messages.Tooltip.LightGizmo._spotActive: The field to drive to control the enabled of the spot light controls.
messages.Tooltip.LightGizmo._directionalActive: The field to drive to control the enabled of the directional light controls.
messages.Tooltip.LightGizmo._pointGizmo: Stores the point light gizmo component.
messages.Tooltip.LightGizmo._spotGizmo: Stores the spot light gizmo component.
messages.Tooltip.LightGizmo._dirGizmo: Stores the directional light gizmo component.
messages.Tooltip.LightGizmo._iconMaterial: The field to drive to control the icon material.
messages.Tooltip.MaterialGizmo: The MaterialGizmo component is used to set up and manage Material orbs and their behavior for snapping to snapper components.
messages.Tooltip.MaterialGizmo._target: The material this Gizmo was made for.
messages.Tooltip.MaterialGizmo._inspectorRoot: The inspector made in order to view <mark=#ffffff10>_target</mark>
messages.Tooltip.MeshGizmo: The Mesh Gizmo component is used to set up and generate the visuals/behavior of mesh orbs which are physical item/references to mesh data.
messages.Tooltip.MeshGizmo._target: The static mesh that this is referencing and displaying.
messages.Tooltip.MeshGizmo._inspectorRoot: The inspector this component generated when it was made.
messages.Tooltip.MeshRendererGizmo: The MeshRenderer Gizmo is used to change the Bounding properties to a mesh object using the dev tool.
messages.Tooltip.MeshRendererGizmo._meshCollider: The mesh collider to edit along with <mark=#ffffff10>_target</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.TextGizmo: The TextGizmo component is commonly found on newly spawned text elements and is used to quickly edit the text of a spawned text element. This component and it's visuals delete themselves after the user selects the text element using a development tip or inspector.
messages.Tooltip.TextGizmo._target: The text renderer to edit the text of.
messages.Tooltip.TextGizmo._editor: The text editor Component that targets <mark=#ffffff10>_target</mark> for its editing.
messages.Tooltip.TextGizmo._editIconPosition: The position field of the start editing icon.
messages.Tooltip.TextGizmo._editIconScale: The scale field of the start editing icon.
messages.Tooltip.LightIntensityFieldAdapter: The Light Intensity Field Adapter component is used to convert the light intensity from migrated content to the new color space system.
messages.Tooltip.LightIntensityFieldAdapter.Profi: Which color profile to convert to when converting <mark=#ffffff10>Value</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.DirectionalLightIntensityFieldAd: The DirectionalLightIntensityFieldAdapter component is used to convert intensity in a directional light from the old SRGB color space into linear when converting worlds.
messages.Tooltip.Light: The Light component is used to illuminate the render scene beyond skybox illumination. This component can be used for suns, spots, and points, but not area lights.
messages.Tooltip.Light.LightType: The type of light. Can be Point, Directional, or Spot.
messages.Tooltip.Light.Intensity: How intense the light is. This is a multiplier.
messages.Tooltip.Light.Color: The color of the light this source should project.
messages.Tooltip.Light.ShadowType: The type of shadow this light should use. Can be None, Hard, or Soft.
messages.Tooltip.Light.ShadowNearPlane: How far away shadows will render for this point light.
messages.Tooltip.Light.ShadowMapResolution: How detailed the resolution for shadows is in pixels.
messages.Tooltip.Light.ShadowBias: added to the distance in the shadow map to ensure that pixels on the borderline definitely pass the comparison as they should.
messages.Tooltip.Light.ShadowNormalBias: Makes surrounding lit areas encroach upon the center shadow, making the encroached area lit too.
messages.Tooltip.Light.Range: How far for the light to shine in meters, where the range represents the point where the falloff stops.
messages.Tooltip.Light.SpotAngle: The angle from 0-180 when on spot light mode to project the light at. 0 is no light, 60 is like car headlights, and 180 is extreme and unrealistic.
messages.Tooltip.Light.Cookie: The cookie texture is used to limit the light area to add texture and occlusion.
messages.Tooltip.LocalLightsBufferRenderer: The Local Lights Buffer Renderer component is used to render the lights for a particle system.
messages.Tooltip.LocalLightsBufferRenderer.LightT: The kind of light to use.
messages.Tooltip.LocalLightsBufferRenderer.Cookie: The texture used for the spotlight cutout texture.
messages.Tooltip.LODGroup: A LOD (Level Of Detail) Group allows you to define behaviors to load alternate meshes for objects based on their relative size on a user's screen. This is commonly used to replace complex and finely detailed meshes with simplified versions based on a user's distance from the object.
messages.Tooltip.LODGroup.LODs: A list of different LOD stages with renderers that should appear for each stage.
messages.Tooltip.LODGroup.CrossFade: Cross fade on the Unity LOD component.
messages.Tooltip.LODGroup.AnimateCrossFading: Animate Cross fade on the Unity LOD component.
messages.Tooltip.MeshRenderBufferRenderer: The MeshRenderBufferRenderer component is used to render point buffers for normal particles as meshes.
messages.Tooltip.MeshRenderBufferRenderer.Buffer: The Buffer to render as meshes.
messages.Tooltip.MeshRenderBufferRenderer.Materia: The material to render on the meshes.
messages.Tooltip.MeshRenderBufferRenderer.Alignme: How to align the meshes rendered using orientation and camera position.
messages.Tooltip.MeshRenderer: The MeshRenderer component is used for rendering static 3D meshes in the world, and applying materials to that mesh.
messages.Tooltip.MeshRenderer.Mesh: The mesh to be rendered. Can be a StaticMesh or a Procedural Mesh
messages.Tooltip.MeshRenderer.Materials: A list of materials to be applied to the mesh
messages.Tooltip.MeshRenderer.MaterialPropertyBlo: A list of material property blocks to apply to the materials on this mesh. Usually used for performance reasons where using multiple similar materials would take more resources.
messages.Tooltip.MeshRenderer.ShadowCastMode: How this object will cast shadows onto the world, or if it only draws a shadow.
messages.Tooltip.MeshRenderer.SortingOrder: Whether to render this before or after other renderers with geometry in the same location.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbeMetadata.Probe: The probe to read data from.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbeMetadata.OnChange: The amount of times the probe has had to re-render the enviroment due to changes from it's internal OnChanges list changing.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbe: The ReflectionProbe component is used to make points in space that makes reflective surfaces within a box around the point show a different reflection map than the skybox. This is useful for controlling the lighting and look of a scene.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbe.ProbeType: What mode this reflection probe uses.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbe.Importance: How much priority this probe takes over another probe when two probes overlap
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbe.Intensity: how strong or bright the reflection is
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbe.BlendDistance: how far the probe will slowly take over the reflection map of objects as an object goes towards the center of influence
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbe.BoxSize: the area that this reflection probe will affect the reflection maps of objects
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbe.BoxProjection: project the reflection map in a box shape from the reflection probe rather than a sphere.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbe.BakedCubemap: The cube map to replace the refection maps of reflective objects with in the influence area of this reflection probe
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbe.ChangesSources: A list of elements that will prompt this reflection probe to render a new map whenever they change.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbe.TimeSlicing: How updates to this reflection probe should happen in updates if this probe is a realtime probe.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbe.Resolution: the forced resolution of the reflection map from this reflection probe shown in the reflections of shiny objects.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbe.HDR: Whether the color encoding should be in HDR.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbe.ShadowDistance: how far to render shadows from the reflection probe
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbe.ClearFlags: Whether to use the skybox or color when backing transparent pixels
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbe.BackgroundColor: The color to clear with when <mark=#ffffff10>ClearFlags</mark> is set to "Color"
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbe.NearClip: The closest distance to render from the reflection probe center when using "Realtime" on <mark=#ffffff10>ProbeType</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbe.FarClip: The furthest distance to render from the reflection probe center when using "Realtime" on <mark=#ffffff10>ProbeType</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbe.SkyboxOnly: Whether to render only the skybox or not.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbe.ShowDebugVisuals: Shows the box influence area and the center point of this reflection probe
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbe._debugVisual: The current debug visual that this reflection probe is showing, if applicable.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbeSH2: The Reflection Probe SH2 component is used to turn what a ReflectionProbe sees into a spherical harmonic for use in ambient lighting like a AmbientLightSH2 component.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbeSH2.Probe: The probe to get color data from.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbeSH2.AmbientLight: <mark=#ffffff10>Probe</mark>'s sampled data as SH2 color data for ambient lighting usage.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbeSH2.Order0Scale: The scale factor for Order 0 for the spherical harmonics.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbeSH2.Order1Scale: The scale factor for Order 1 for the spherical harmonics.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbeSH2.Order2Scale: The scale factor for Order 2 for the spherical harmonics.
messages.Tooltip.RenderMaterialOverride: The RenderMaterialOverride Component allows for overriding materials on a renderer when it is rendered in certain contexts.
messages.Tooltip.RenderContextOverride.Context: The context in which to override the materials of <mark=#ffffff10>Renderer</mark> with the list of <mark=#ffffff10>Overrides</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.RenderMaterialOverride.Renderer: The renderer to override materials for.
messages.Tooltip.RenderMaterialOverride.Overrides: A list of materials to override during rendering in a <mark=#ffffff10>Context</mark> context.
messages.Tooltip.RenderTransformOverride: The RenderTransformOverride component is used to transform, rotate or scale a Slot following a specific rendering context.
messages.Tooltip.RenderTransformOverride.Position: If not null, will override the position of the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.RenderTransformOverride.Rotation: If not null, will override the rotation of the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.RenderTransformOverride.ScaleOve: If not null, will override the scale of the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.RenderTransformOverride.SkinnedM: List of SkinnedMeshRenderers that need to be recalculated and overridden.
messages.Tooltip.Rig: The Rig component is used to inform what bones are "Rig" bones for components like Dynamic Bone Chains.
messages.Tooltip.Rig.Bones: The bones that are a part of the item this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.SkinnedMeshRenderer: While it can be used for rendering static meshes, it is not recommended as there is a slight performance penalty for using SkinnedMeshRenderer, even if the animation features aren't used — Please try to use MeshRenderer where possible.
messages.Tooltip.SkinnedMeshRenderer.BoundsComput: How the bounds of this mesh will be calculated. Should be left as Static if possible, for performance reasons.
messages.Tooltip.SkinnedMeshRenderer.ProxyBoundsS: A SkinnedMeshRenderer to use the <mark=#ffffff10>ExplicitLocalBounds</mark> of rather than the <mark=#ffffff10>ExplicitLocalBounds</mark> on this component.
messages.Tooltip.SkinnedMeshRenderer.ExplicitLoca: A box that within view will render the mesh, and when out of view will cull the mesh and stop rendering it. Is used when <mark=#ffffff10>ProxyBoundsSource</mark> is null.
messages.Tooltip.SkinnedMeshRenderer.Bones: Automatically Assigned — List of bones present in this mesh
messages.Tooltip.SkinnedMeshRenderer.BlendShapeWe: Automatically Assigned — List of blendshapes present in this mesh, and their respective weights.
messages.Tooltip.Skybox: Can be used to specify the current material for the skybox and set the current skybox for the current world.
messages.Tooltip.Skybox.Material: The material to use for the skybox.
messages.Tooltip.ColorSky: The ColorSky component creates a skybox with a color. Currently when this component is added to a slot, it will be immediately replaced with a Skybox and a material component automatically. It exists as a stub to maintain compatibility with legacy content.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralSky: The ProceduralSky texture is used to dynamically create a skybox using a sun rotation and intensity including sun color.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralSky.SunQuality: The quality preset of the sun in the sky.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralSky.SunSize: size of the sun in the sky
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralSky.Sun: The light to use for rotation, intensity, and color of the sun on this material.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralSky.AtmosphereThicknes: How much the atmosphere will light up the scene. If this is low, the <mark=#ffffff10>SkyTint</mark> color coming up from the lower region of the world won't make it up to the top of the world as well, and will gradient into black. good for thin atmosphere planets, or 0 for no atmosphere.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralSky.SkyTint: The tint of the atmosphere of the world.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralSky.GroundColor: The tint of the sky below the horizon line.
messages.Tooltip.ProceduralSky.Exposure: How much to light up or darken the sky color overall.
messages.Tooltip.FontChain: The FontChain component provides an easy way to use "Fallback" fonts for a main font, usually in the event that the main font does not contain certain Unicode characters. Any place where a StaticFont can be used, a FontChain can be used.
messages.Tooltip.FontCollection: The FontCollection component acts as a set of fonts that can be used to make one mega font. When a font in the set does not have a character for a unicode point, another font can take over.
messages.Tooltip.FontCollection.FontSets: The list of fonts to congregate into one huge font.
messages.Tooltip.FontDataManager: The FontDataManager component handles the mapping of Font data and parameters to font based materials during rendering.
messages.Tooltip.TextRenderer: The TextRenderer component shows Text in the world. An example can be created from the Create New menu, Text, then Basic or Outline.
messages.Tooltip.TextRenderer.Font: The font set to use to render the text. For example, Times new Roman, or Avali Scratch.
messages.Tooltip.TextRenderer.Text: The text to display. For example, "Hello world!"
messages.Tooltip.TextRenderer.ParseRichText: Whether or not to interpret text formatting tags like "" for bold and "" for itallic.
messages.Tooltip.TextRenderer.NullText: the text to display if <mark=#ffffff10>Text</mark> is null. (Empty text doesn't count)
messages.Tooltip.TextRenderer.Size: how big to render the text as.
messages.Tooltip.TextRenderer.HorizontalAlign: How to align the text horizontally.
messages.Tooltip.TextRenderer.VerticalAlign: how to align the text vertically.
messages.Tooltip.TextRenderer.AlignmentMode: how to align the text within the bounding box
messages.Tooltip.TextRenderer.Color: what color to render the text in.
messages.Tooltip.TextRenderer.Materials: what materials to render the text as. Really only works with Unlit Text Material type.
messages.Tooltip.TextRenderer.LineHeight: how much space each text line should take up
messages.Tooltip.TextRenderer.Bounded: whether to restrict the text within a certain area
messages.Tooltip.TextRenderer.BoundsSize: the area to use if <mark=#ffffff10>Bounded</mark> is enabled.
messages.Tooltip.TextRenderer.BoundsAlignment: how to align the text to the bounds and therefore how to handle text that spills out.
messages.Tooltip.TextRenderer.MaskPattern: a string to replace every character in <mark=#ffffff10>Text</mark> with when rendering. Useful for password fields.
messages.Tooltip.TextRenderer.HorizontalAutoSize: whether to scale the text to fit the bounds horizontally
messages.Tooltip.TextRenderer.VerticalAutoSize: whether to scale the text to fit the bounds vertically
messages.Tooltip.TextRenderer.CaretPosition: Where the typing cursor position is within the text by character index.
messages.Tooltip.TextRenderer.SelectionStart: where the selection starts if the text is being edited currently.
messages.Tooltip.TextRenderer.CaretColor: the color of the typing cursor
messages.Tooltip.TextRenderer.SelectionColor: the color of the typing selection.
messages.Tooltip.TrailsRenderBufferRenderer: The TrailsRenderBufferRenderer component is mainly a debug component to show the allocation of rendering buffers for particles in the form of colors. This relies on the specific implementation of Photon Dust.
messages.Tooltip.TrailsRenderBufferRenderer.Buffe: The trails renderer module to get buffer data from.
messages.Tooltip.TrailsRenderBufferRenderer.Mater: The material to use for rendering.
messages.Tooltip.TrailsRenderBufferRenderer.Textu: The texture mode to use for rendering.
messages.Tooltip.TrailsRenderBufferRenderer.Motio: The motion vector mode for rendering from a camera.
messages.Tooltip.TrailsRenderBufferRenderer.Gener: Whether or not to generate lighting data for trail rendering.
messages.Tooltip.SlicingVolumeVisualizer: The SlicingVolumeVisualizer component is used to visualize the slicer imported with new volumes imported into the game.
messages.Tooltip.SlicingVolumeVisualizer.AutoScal: Whether to auto scale the visual to fit the object it is slicing.
messages.Tooltip.SlicingVolumeVisualizer._rendere: The renderer component which this is slicing a mesh for.
messages.Tooltip.SlicingVolumeVisualizer._mesh: The mesh this visual is slicing.
messages.Tooltip.SlicingVolumeVisualizer._collide: The box collider that is used to interact with the slicer this is attached to.
messages.Tooltip.SlicingVolumeVisualizer._scale: The scale field of the slicer visual element.
messages.Tooltip.SlicingVolumeVisualizer.Slicers: A list of slicers that are slicing the object.
messages.Tooltip.SlicingVolumeVisualizer._slicePl: A list of slice planes that are slicing the object.
messages.Tooltip.SlicingVolumeVisualizer._highlig: a list of highlight components for slicing the object.
messages.Tooltip.VolumePlaneSlicer: See Volume Unlit Material Examples section for better information on how slicers work.
messages.Tooltip.VolumePlaneSlicer.Mode: The kind of slicer mode this slicer is doing.
messages.Tooltip.VolumePlaneSlicer.HighlightColor: The color highlight to highlight the slicer with. please see Volume Unlit Material Examples section for better info.
messages.Tooltip.VolumePlaneSlicer.HighlightRange: The color highlight range to use. please see Volume Unlit Material Examples section for better info.
messages.Tooltip.VolumePlaneSlicer._colorDialog: The dialog ui currently being used to choose a color to display for the color highlight.
messages.Tooltip.VolumePlaneSlicer._grabGrid: The slot to enable when grabbing the slicer to show the grid visual.
messages.Tooltip.BipedRig: This component is used by ProtoFlux and other Resonite systems to read the different parts of an anthro/humanoid avatar by Body Node. This is created and assigned to by the Assimp import system that FrooxEngine uses internally, and cannot be edited in game without mods. If this component is deleted, internal code or ProtoFlux might not be able to read what parts of your body are which Body Node designation, and will cause issues in user creations and some FrooxEngine functions. If the Biped Rig is not present or it does not contain the slot for the Body Node, the Body Node search will fallback to looking for AvatarObjectSlot components on the user instead.
messages.Tooltip.BipedRig.ForwardAxis: The forward direction the VRIK should use when setting up the VRIK in the game internally. Is not used after the avatar is generated by Assimp. Mostly for debugging.
messages.Tooltip.BipedRig.Bones: A list of bones this component has stored and mapped in the form of body nodes to slot assignments.
messages.Tooltip.FingerPoseLerp: The FingerPoseLerp component allows for a linear blending between two different Finger pose sources. The resulting data becomes the pose source data of this component. This component in itself is a IFingerPoseSourceComponent.
messages.Tooltip.FingerPoseLerp.A: The pose to start at when interpolating.
messages.Tooltip.FingerPoseLerp.B: The pose to end at when interpolating.
messages.Tooltip.FingerPoseLerp.Lerp: The value to use 0<->1 when interpolating from <mark=#ffffff10>A</mark><-><mark=#ffffff10>B</mark>
messages.Tooltip.FingerPoseModifier: The FingerPoseModifier component takes a Source and makes a copy of the finger pose data from it. It then modifies the copied data before making it the finger pose data of this component. This component in itself is a IFingerPoseSourceComponent.
messages.Tooltip.FingerPoseModifier.Source: The component to take finger pose data from and make a copy to modify and make this component's finger pose data.
messages.Tooltip.FingerPoseModifier.LeftOffsets: How to modify the left finger pose data before making it this component's finger pose data.
messages.Tooltip.FingerPoseModifier.RightOffsets: How to modify the right finger pose data before making it this component's finger pose data.
messages.Tooltip.FingerPoseMultiplexer: The FingerPoseMultiplexer component can be used to get data from multiple finger pose sources and make that data its own finger pose source data. This component in itself is a IFingerPoseSourceComponent.
messages.Tooltip.FingerPoseMultiplexer.Index: What Component in <mark=#ffffff10>Sources</mark> to copy data from for the finger pose data for this component.
messages.Tooltip.FingerPoseMultiplexer.Interpolat: How fast the Multiplexer transitions what data this component is copying for this component's finger pose data.
messages.Tooltip.FingerPoseMultiplexer.Sources: The list if Finger pose sources to copy data from for the data for this component's Finger pose data.
messages.Tooltip.FingerPosePreset: The FingerPosePreset component is itself a IFingerPoseSourceComponent type component that it's finger pose data is determined by the preset selected in PresetPose.
messages.Tooltip.FingerPosePreset.PresetPose: The Finger pose data this component's finger pose data should be.
messages.Tooltip.FingerReferencePoseSource: The FingerReferencePoseSource component is used to read and generate Pose data from the user or its already created bone hiearchy Template. The Slots used in the Bones list need to not be null. Every time the Pose data is read from this component as a Finger Pose Source, the current positions and rotations of the slots in Bones are used. This component in itself is a IFingerPoseSourceComponent.
messages.Tooltip.FingerReferencePoseSource.Bones: A list of bones mapped to Fingers to use as the Finger pose source data for this component. The Fingers need to be in the default hand structure.
messages.Tooltip.FingerSplayModifier: The FingerSplayModifier takes data from a Source, adding splay data to a copy of that data. The resulting data becomes the pose source data of this component. This component in itself is a IFingerPoseSourceComponent.
messages.Tooltip.FingerSplayModifier.Source: The source data to copy to this component's Finger pose source data.
messages.Tooltip.FingerSplayModifier.LeftSplayMag: How much splay data to the left hand to add to the data copied data from <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> during copying.
messages.Tooltip.FingerSplayModifier.RightSplayMa: How much splay data on the right hand to add to the data copied data from <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> during copying.
messages.Tooltip.HandPoser: This component modifies the rotation of the fingers of an avatar's hand based on a pose source. The HandPoser works with whatever data is avilable, either precise finger tracking like a LeapMotion, or course grained like a vive wand, where grip and trigger affect the main finger positions.
messages.Tooltip.HandPoser.PoseSource: What the finger transform data is derived from.
messages.Tooltip.HandPoser.Side: Which hand this poser is representing
messages.Tooltip.HandPoser.PoseMetacarpals: If metacarpals should be posed (this should normally be left as True)
messages.Tooltip.HandPoser.HandRoot: The root of the hand (if null the slot the component is attached to is used)
messages.Tooltip.HandPoser.HandForward: A unit vector pointing forward from the wrist to the fingers.
messages.Tooltip.HandPoser.HandUp: A unit vector pointing from the back of the hand outwards (alternatively phrased, this is pointing into the palm)
messages.Tooltip.HandPoser.HandRight: A unit vector pointing to the right of the hand, if the hand is facing down. This is roughly the direction of the thumb on the left hand, and the other side of the hand from the thumb on the right hand.
messages.Tooltip.HandPoser.Thumb: The Thumb on an anthro hand.
messages.Tooltip.HandPoser.Index: The Index on an anthro hand.
messages.Tooltip.HandPoser.Middle: The Middle on an anthro hand.
messages.Tooltip.HandPoser.Ring: The Ring on an anthro hand.
messages.Tooltip.HandPoser.Pinky: The Pinky on an anthro hand.
messages.Tooltip.HandPoser.DebugFingers: Whether to show visuals for debugging the fingers.
messages.Tooltip.HandSimulator: The HandSimulator component is used to control the logic for the transforms of a hand when overridden or interacting with something.
messages.Tooltip.HandSimulator.Side: The hand side to simulate for.
messages.Tooltip.HeadSimulator: The HeadSimulator used to control the movement of the user's head.
messages.Tooltip.ScreenController: The ScreenController component is used mainly in desktop and is found on user roots. It is used to control the screen controls like third person in the game.
messages.Tooltip.ScreenController.TransitionSpeed: How fast to transition the camera between different modes.
messages.Tooltip.ScreenController.PointerControll: The pointer component handling inspector interactions.
messages.Tooltip.ScreenController.ActiveTargettin: The view controller targeting mechanism for stuff like UIX targeting.
messages.Tooltip.ScreenController.Head: The component handling head control.
messages.Tooltip.ScreenController.LeftHand: The component handing the left hand control.
messages.Tooltip.ScreenController.RightHand: The component handling the right hand control.
messages.Tooltip.ScreenController._firstPerson: The component to handle first person object targeting.
messages.Tooltip.ScreenController._thirdPerson: The component to handle third person object targeting.
messages.Tooltip.ScreenController._uiCamera: The targetting controller used for UIX targeting.
messages.Tooltip.ScreenController._freeformCamera: The targetting controller used for third person targetting like freeform camera mode in desktop.
messages.Tooltip.FirstPersonTargettingController: The FirstPersonTargettingController component is used to control the head movement of a desktop user when they look around.
The furthest down angle from 0 being forward the user can look in desktop.
The furthest up angle from 0 being forward the user can look in desktop.
messages.Tooltip.FreeformTargettingController: The FreeformTargettingController component is used to handle the 3rd person targeting onto UIX in desktop mode.
messages.Tooltip.FreeformTargettingController.Foc: The center focus position. Acts as an offset.
messages.Tooltip.ThirdPersonTargettingController: Is used to control the F5 camera in desktop.
The minimum pitch angle in desktop third person.
The maximum pitch angle in desktop third person.
messages.Tooltip.UI_TargettingController: The UI_TargettingController component is used to control the desktop view which can target onto UIs like inspectors if in builder or videos.
messages.Tooltip.UI_TargettingController.Target: The current UIX target element to look at.
messages.Tooltip.UserspaceTargettingController: Used as a screen controller component for userspace actions.
messages.Tooltip.RepulsionTreeItem: Repulsion tree item is used along side the Repulsion Tree Simulator Component to make a live repulsion tree simulation. Check the simulator's page for a more detailed explanation.
messages.Tooltip.RepulsionTreeItem.Force: How much force to apply to other RepulsionTreeItems within range.
messages.Tooltip.RepulsionTreeItem.Radius: The radius of influence this item has when interacting with other items.
messages.Tooltip.RepulsionTreeSimulator: The RepulsionTreeSimulator can be used alongside the RepulsionTreeItem component on it's children slots to create a repulsion tree simulation. A repulsion tree is a simulation that keeps a bunch of RepulsionTreeItems on the outside shell of a sphere. The different slots of the tree will move around as they push or pull each other in real time.
messages.Tooltip.RepulsionTreeSimulator.FalloffPo: how quickly the force power diminishes by distance.
messages.Tooltip.RepulsionTreeSimulator.Radius: The radius of this repulsion tree's sphere.
messages.Tooltip.RepulsionTreeSimulator.Force: how much to multiply the forces of the children objects.
messages.Tooltip.AppVersionDriver: App version driver is a component that drives the content of the string field provided to Text to Resonite's version.
messages.Tooltip.AppVersionDriver.Text: Drives the provided text field's content to Resonite's version. The text is formatted starting with "Beta", "Alpha", or "Release", followed by the game's compile date. The date is formatted <mark=#ffffff10>year.day.month.minuteOfDay</mark>. Minute of day is the minutes since midnight. The time is in UTC.
messages.Tooltip.DebugBoxDistance: The Debug Box Distance component allows for debugging the distance from a box object, and generates 32 random points on attach to do so.
messages.Tooltip.DebugBoxDistance.Size: The size of the box to debug
messages.Tooltip.DebugBoxDistance.Points: The points to use to visualize the distances from the box.
messages.Tooltip.DebugConeDistance: The DebugConeDistance component creates a Debug cone visual that draws lines from the list of Points to the surface of the cone. If the point is inside the cone, the point visual will be Cyan, if it is outside it will be red. The lines from the point to the cone are Yellow. When attached, the component will add 32 random points to Points that are 4 units away.
messages.Tooltip.DebugConeDistance.Radius: The radius of the cone base.
messages.Tooltip.DebugConeDistance.Points: Points that the Debug visual will draw lines to from the cone surface to the point.
messages.Tooltip.DebugFeatureUpgrade: The DebugFeatureUpgrade is used to test upgrading features which is an Elements.Core.dll C# tag function.
messages.Tooltip.DebugFeatureReplacement: The DebugFeatureReplacement component is used to test upgrading features which is an Elements.Core.dll C# tag function.
messages.Tooltip.DebugFeatureReplacement.Test2: A test value to replace with.
messages.Tooltip.DebugFieldAdapter: The DebugFieldAdapter component just Inverts Value's rgb colors and drives Target's Target with it. Used with Component:DebugFieldAdapterTest.
messages.Tooltip.DebugFieldAdapterTest: The DebugFieldAdapterTest is used with Component:DebugFieldAdapter to test the accuracy. If set properly, the InvertedColor should flash between inverted and not inverted every frame.
messages.Tooltip.DebugFieldAdapterTest.InvertedCo: <mark=#ffffff10>SourceColor</mark> inverted. Doesn't do this when this component is by itself. Is set to <mark=#ffffff10>SourceColor</mark> every time this component changes in any way.
messages.Tooltip.DebugFieldAdapterTest.SourceColo: The source color. Doesn't affect or is changed by anything. Except for sets or drives.
messages.Tooltip.DebugHandOffsetCompensation: The DebugHandOffsetCompensation component constantly changes the mapping offsets of the left and right controllers with the values in its fields. These offsets determine how a controller is kept in a consistent orientation to other controllers a user can have.
messages.Tooltip.DebugHandOffsetCompensation.Left: The position offset of the left hand.
The rotation Offset of the left hand.
messages.Tooltip.DebugHandOffsetCompensation.Righ: The position offset of the right hand.
The rotation offset of the right hand.
messages.Tooltip.DebugHands: The DebugHands component shows the vector axes and finger axes for a particular hand on a particular user.
messages.Tooltip.DebugHands.User: The user to make the debug for.
messages.Tooltip.DebugHands.Chirality: The hand to make the debug visual for.
messages.Tooltip.DebugHapticPoint: The DebugHapticPoint component sends Force sensations to a haptic device as well as gets the name of the haptic device.
messages.Tooltip.DebugHapticPoint.Intensity: Sends a sensation of force of this intensity to the Haptic device at <mark=#ffffff10>Index</mark>
messages.Tooltip.DebugHapticPoint.Index: The haptic device to send Intensity to and get a name for.
messages.Tooltip.DebugHapticPoint.Position: The name of the haptic device at <mark=#ffffff10>Index</mark>
messages.Tooltip.DebugLinePoint: The DebugLinePoint component draws a line from LinePoint0 to LinePoint1 which is the central line. When attached, it generates 128 random Float3's which get added to Points. Every point in said list have a line drawn from them to the closest point on the central line.
messages.Tooltip.DebugLinePoint.LinePoint0: The starting point of the central line.
messages.Tooltip.DebugLinePoint.LinePoint1: the ending point of the central line.
messages.Tooltip.DebugLinePoint.Points: A list of points to draw lines from to the closest point on the central line.
messages.Tooltip.DebugLocomotionSimulator: The DebugLocomotionSimulator component is used to draw Debug visuals for a virtual locomotion simulator that drives the procedural animation system.
messages.Tooltip.DebugLocomotionSimulator.TrackPo: Whether to keep track of this Debug locomotion's progressively moving position.
messages.Tooltip.DebugLocomotionSimulator.State: The kind of locomotion this is debugging
messages.Tooltip.DebugLocomotionSimulator.Movemen: How fast the locomotion should be moving.
messages.Tooltip.DebugLocomotionSimulator.Angular: How fast this locomotion should be turning.
messages.Tooltip.DebugLocomotionSimulator.RotateD: Whether the locomotion should rotate its movement direction.
messages.Tooltip.DebugLocomotionSimulator.OffsetR: Whether this locomotion should be offsetting from root as a starting position
messages.Tooltip.DebugSphereRaycast: The DebugSphereRaycast component is used to show the multiple hit normals (1 per collider) of a sweep raycast of a sphere. This essentially acts as a fat raycast, which has thickness. Unfortunately this raycast is only for Debug purposes. Is simulated on the host machine.
messages.Tooltip.DebugSphereRaycast.Direction: the direction and distance the raycast should go.
messages.Tooltip.DebugSphereRaycast.Radius: How far the raycasts created should go.
messages.Tooltip.DebugSphereSectorDistance: The DebugSphereSectorDistance component makes a bunch of spazzy lines all over the place that vaguely resembles two spheres having a shoot out. This is only used for Debug.
messages.Tooltip.DebugSphereSectorDistance.Angle: The angle of the sphere to debug
messages.Tooltip.DebugTangentSpace: This component was added in Beta 2023.12.15.22 to diagnose tangent space on meshes.
messages.Tooltip.DebugTangentSpace.Mesh: The mesh renderer to Debug tangents for.
messages.Tooltip.DebugTangentSpace.Triangle: The triangle index to Debug tangents for.
messages.Tooltip.DebugTangentSpace.BaryCoord: Used as an interpolator for the tangents and normals of the target triangle.
messages.Tooltip.DebugTransform: The DebugTransform component is used to show the parent hiearchy of a slot in the form of text to a specific ShowToUser.
messages.Tooltip.DebugTransform.ShowToUser: Shows the debug visual to this user only.
messages.Tooltip.DebugUsers: The DebugUsers component drives get contents of a string field to show statistics of each user in the current world.
messages.Tooltip.DebugUsers.text: The field to populate with world users statistics.
messages.Tooltip.DebugValueStreamRange: The DebugValueStreamRange component is used to debug the range of a Float3 stream for debugging. Like a graph of sorts.
messages.Tooltip.DebugValueStreamRange.positionDr: The position field to drive with the stream position.
messages.Tooltip.DebugValueStreamRange.sizeDrive: The field to drive with the overall stream range.
messages.Tooltip.DebugVector: The DebugVector component shows a vector using a thin line.
messages.Tooltip.DebugVector.PositionOffset: the position offset for the entire Debug visual.
messages.Tooltip.DebugVector.RotationOffset: The rotation Offset for the entire Debug visual.
messages.Tooltip.DebugVector.Color: The color of the debug vector line.
messages.Tooltip.DebugVector.UseGlobalSpace: Whether the vector is in global space or not.
messages.Tooltip.DebugWorld: The DebugWorld component renders out a block of text that displays information about the current world the component is in.
messages.Tooltip.DebugWorld.text: The text field to drive with all the debug data in a world.
messages.Tooltip.SubmeshDebug.Offset: How much to space all the generated triangles.
messages.Tooltip.SubmeshDebug.SubmeshCount: How many submesh triangles to generate.
messages.Tooltip.TalkVisualizer: This component takes a float value and a colorX value, and multiplies the float value by the colorX value, and outputs a colorX.
messages.Tooltip.TalkVisualizer.Input: A value that constantly changes to visualize the color for. For example, the <mark=#ffffff10>Volume</mark> field of a Volume Meter.
messages.Tooltip.TalkVisualizer.MaterialColor: The color to drive with an amplified <mark=#ffffff10>BaseColor</mark> value based on <mark=#ffffff10>Input</mark>
messages.Tooltip.TalkVisualizer.LightIntensity: How bright the output color is beyond 1 gain.
messages.Tooltip.TestFakeViveTracker: The TestFakeViveTracker component simulates a vive Tracker for User that has the position and rotation of the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.TestFakeViveTracker.Id: Upon attaching is a random GUID. Can be changed which makes a new Tracker object. If identical to another older tracker the user has used before, will take on the old tracker's identity and settings.
messages.Tooltip.TestFakeViveTracker.IsTracking: Controls whether the tracker has lost tracking.
messages.Tooltip.TestSineWaveSource: The TestSineWaveSource component acts the same as a SineWaveClip if it were continuously playing as an AudioClipPlayer. The main difference though is it allows constant and quick changes in Frequency and Amplitude without needing time to recalculate.
messages.Tooltip.TestSineWaveSource.Frequency: The pitch of the audio streamed whistle tone.
messages.Tooltip.TestSineWaveSource.Amplitude: The loudness of the streamed whistle tone.
messages.Tooltip.TimeDiagnosis: The TimeDiagnosis component is used to check diagnostic info for user time.
messages.Tooltip.TimeDiagnosis.FormatTime: Whether to format the time shown.
messages.Tooltip.TimeDiagnosis.text: The text field to drive with time info.
messages.Tooltip.BillboardBrushTool: The BillboardBrushTool is a Brush Tool that draws ribbons.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.FixedMinimumPointDista: Stroke point distances within a stroke cannot be less than this value.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.PositionSmoothing: How much to smooth the brush tip position when drawing.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.RotationSmoothing: How much to smooth the rotation of the brush tip when drawing
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.PressureSmoothing: How much to smooth the brush pressure being applied
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.MaxStrokeLength: The longest a Brush stroke can be before being ended forcibly.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.StrokeFadeInLength: The length along the stroke that the stroke fades in.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.StrokeFadeOutLength: The length along the stroke that the stroke fades out.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.StrokeGroupFinishWaitT: Finishes the group when drawing strokes for more than this many seconds regardless of how full the group is.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.ActivationThreshold: When pressure/strength is above this value, the tool starts drawing.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.DeactivationThresholdR: When pressure/strength is below this value, the tool stops drawing.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.MenuSizeChange: How much to increment or decrement the brush size when using the context menu controls.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.SnapTip: Whether the brush tip should snap to snappers like planes or snap spheres.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.SnapLine: Whether to snap new lines to snappers like snap planes or snap spheres.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.StrokesSpace: The space to create new strokes into.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.PositionStrokesToTip: Position the slots of new stroke groups to the brush tip.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.OrientStrokesToTip: Make the rotation of new stroke group slots oriented to the brush tip.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.ScaleStrokesToUser: Whether the brush strokes should be scaled up or down to match the user's scale.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.PickMaterials: Whether this brush can pick a material by dipping the brush tip in a material orb.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.PickColors: Whether this tool can pick colors by dipping the brush tip into a material orb.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.CurrentMaterial: The current material the brush is using to draw.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.ColorMappings: Associates materials with their color fields.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.ParticleSystem: The Particle system to Template when making Particle strokes.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.MeshEmitterTemplate: The template component to use as the mesh emitter of new particle emitting strokes.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.ParticleTemplateHandli: How to handle the use of the Particle system template per new stroke group.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.EmissionRatePerUnitLen: The rate of particle emission per unit length section of the stroke mesh.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool._colorPicker: The current color picker dialouge being used to pick a color for this brush.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool._pickedColor: The color that the tool has picked by being dipped in a material orb or picked via a color picker.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool._hideOnStroke: The list of slots to hide while making a Brush stroke.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool._lastUsedMaterial: The last used material by this brush.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool._lastCreatedMaterial: The last material generated by this brush.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.Pressure: The pressure being applied to the brush.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.Position: The position of the brush tip after smoothing.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.Rotation: The rotation of the brush tip after smoothing.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.LastPointDelta: The difference between the last drawing position and its current position.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.Velocity: The velocity of the tool tips position.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.RawDelta: The raw delta of the tool tips position.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.RawVelocity: The raw velocity of the tool tip.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.StrokeLength: The current stroke length in meters in <mark=#ffffff10>StrokesSpace</mark>
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.NormalizedStrokeLength: The length of the current stroke from 0->1 where one is maximum length set for this brush for strokes.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.StrokeFadeMultiplier: The multiplier to the fade in and out effects of the stroke. Making the fade in and out sharper or more gradual.
messages.Tooltip.BrushTool.StrokeGroupIndex: The running index of strokes being drawn as part of a stroke group.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleBrushTool.TipAnchor: The slot used as the source of strokes being drawn.

How far a user needs to move from a group before they are removed as being part of a group.
