messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatTool: The GaussianSplatTool component can be applied to a slot to create a Gaussian Splat Tool, used to manipulate a Gaussian Splat in 3D space.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatTool.Mode: The selected mode for this Gaussian Splat Tool.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatTool.BoxSelectionTe: The templete used to show the user what is being cropped when using the box.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatTool.SphereSelectio: The templete used to show the user what is being cropped when using the sphere.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatTool.CylinderSelect: The templete used to show the user what is being cropped when using the cylinder.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatTool.BoxInterface: The Box Interface component is an abstracted type that doesn't do anything.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatTool.BoxInterface.S: The size of the box area.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatTool.SphereInterfac: The Sphere Interface component is an abstracted type that doesn't do anything.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatTool.CylinderInterf: The Cylinder Interface component is an abstracted type that doesn't do anything.
messages.Tooltip.MaterialRelay: The MaterialRelay component is used to specify to a Material Tool when hitting a slot tagged with this component what materials to grab/apply to when using the tool.
messages.Tooltip.MaterialRelay.MaterialRefs: A list of material storage fields that a material tool when applying should apply to. The first non null item in the list is the one a material tool will pick up when using the secondary action.
messages.Tooltip.MeshRendererMaterialRelay: Attach to a slot and provide the mesh renderers that should have materials applied to them with the material Tool in Renderers.
messages.Tooltip.MeshRendererMaterialRelay.Render: The list of renderers to relay to the material tool.
messages.Tooltip.HierarchyMaterialTarget: The HierarchyMaterialTarget component signals that a slot tagged with this component you are hitting with the Material Tool is an appropriate target to look for materials in.
messages.Tooltip.MaterialApplyPolicy: The MaterialApplyPolicy component allows for controlling whether a Material Tool is able to apply a material to the slot hierarchy tagged with this component onto its SkinnedMeshRenderer/MeshRenderer components.
messages.Tooltip.MaterialApplyPolicy.CanApply: Setting this to false will prevent the Material Tool from applying materials to any SkinnedMeshRenderer/MeshRenderer components in the slot or child slots.
messages.Tooltip.OrbCartridgeTool._orbSlot: The slot to store the material orb when an orb is placed into the tool or picked from an object.
messages.Tooltip.MaterialTool.ReplacementMode: How to replace materials using the tool.
messages.Tooltip.MaterialTool.AreaRadius: The radius to use when <mark=#ffffff10>ReplacementMode</mark> is set to Area.
messages.Tooltip.MaterialTool._areaIndicator: The slot that is the root of the area indicator visual.
messages.Tooltip.MaterialTool._areaIndicatorActiv: The area indicator's active field, used to disable the visual when <mark=#ffffff10>ReplacementMode</mark> is not set to Area.
messages.Tooltip.MaterialTool._knobControlActive: The active field of the knob control visual/control.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectOrb: According to Frooxius this was originally made during the infancy stages of FrooxEngine to serve as an item the user would stick into an "Object Placement Tool" where users could easily place objects into the world in the form of duplicates and make it easier to create worlds. However this never came to fruition and the concept lives on as this remnant in the game code and Component set.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectOrb.PreviewRoot: The slot storing the preview visual.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectOrb.TemplateRoot: The slot to duplicate when placing the object.
messages.Tooltip.BuilderActionTool.ApplyToObjectR: whether this should apply to all colliders under a found object root from the hit collider rather than just the hit collider.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterColliderSetterTool.Mark: whether to mark it as  being able to be climbed.
messages.Tooltip.ColorTool: Color tool is a component that drives the color tool from Tools. Pressing Secondary allows for picking a color via raycast, and hitting Primary allows for setting the color of a material.
messages.Tooltip.ColorTool.Color: The selected color for this tool.
messages.Tooltip.ColorTool.EnsureUniqueMaterial: Creates a new material rather than changing the color of the material of a target object when hitting Primary.
messages.Tooltip.ColorTool.ColorIndicators: The field to drive to the current picked color. used for an indicator.
messages.Tooltip.ColorTool._colorDialog: The currently open color picker dialogue.
messages.Tooltip.ComponentCloneTool.EnsureSingleI: Whether the tool should delete existing components of the same type before adding the ones from the template.
messages.Tooltip.ComponentCloneTool.AllowPickup: Whether the tool can add components to its template list with secondary.
messages.Tooltip.ComponentCloneTool._label: The label giving information on this tool.
messages.Tooltip.ComponentCloneTool._templateRoot: The slot this tool is using to store its list of components to place onto other slots.
messages.Tooltip.ControllerDiagnosticTool: The ControllerDiagnosticTool component is a tool that gives information on controller info for the hand its equipped on. For more info see Controller Diagnostic Tool.
messages.Tooltip.ControllerDiagnosticTool._text: The text renderer to fill with controller diagnostic info to display.
messages.Tooltip.DuplicatorTool: Duplicates objects grabbed with the tool when pressing primary, as if it was done with the Context menu
messages.Tooltip.GlueTool.GlueMode: The mode that newly created Glue spheres should be set to.
messages.Tooltip.GlueTool._indicatorColor: The field to drive with the current indicator color.
messages.Tooltip.GlueTool._activeGlues: List of glue AOE fields to glue when hitting secondary.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbableSetterTool.SetScalable: Whether the applied grababble should be scalable.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbableSetterTool._indicatorCo: The color to change for indicating this tool's settings. Used for the default cone model usually.
messages.Tooltip.Grabber: Grabber is a Component that is used in a User's hiearchy to allow the user to grab objects. This component is generated internally usually. Interacting with this component dynamically is done through Grabbable ProtoFlux Nodes.
messages.Tooltip.Grabber.AutoUpdateUser: The user handling updates for this Grabber.
messages.Tooltip.Grabber.ReleaseCheckRadius: How far to check before releasing a grabbable from the <mark=#ffffff10>_baseDistance</mark>. Usually used for dynamic bones.
messages.Tooltip.Grabber.CorrespondingBodyNode: The node this hand is associated with.
messages.Tooltip.Grabber._scaleReference: The grabber used as a scale reference, like the opposite hand.
messages.Tooltip.Grabber._baseScale: The default scale for the grabber.
messages.Tooltip.Grabber._baseDistance: The default distance for the grabber.
messages.Tooltip.Grabber._holderSlot: The slot used to store objects being grabbed by the user.
messages.Tooltip.InspectorTool: Opens an inspector when hitting primary.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler: The Interaction Handler component is used to control and handle the user interactions like grabbing, tooltips, and all general controls for the user regarding their hands.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler.LocomotionCon: The locomotion controller the user is using
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler.GrabSmoothing: How much to smooth grabbing interactions
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._streamDriver: The stream providing info from the user regarding interaction using hands.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._undoItem: The context menu item used to undo actions for this user.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._redoItem: The context menu item used to redo actions for this user.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler.ContextMenu: The context menu for the user.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler.EquippingEnab: Whether the user can equip stuff.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler.MenuEnabled: Whether the user can use their menu.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler.UserScalingEn: Whether the user can scale themselves.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler.VisualEnabled: Whether the user's visual is enabled.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler.PointingGrab: Whether the user is grabbing with this hand via the pointer. (remote grabbing)
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler.PointingTouch: Whether the user is physical grabbing with this hand.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._toolRoot: The root of the tool equipped to this hand currently.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._laserSlot: The laser object slot of this hand.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._laserPositio: The laser position of this hand.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._laserRotatio: The laser rotation of this hand.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._interactionL: The interaction laser component of this hand.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._laserEnabled: Whether the user's laser is enabled for this hand.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._handGrabType: The hand grab type being used for this hand.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._grabToggle: Whether the user's grabbing is enabled for this hand.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._holderPos: The holder position field for this hand.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._holderRot: The holder rotation field for this hand.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._laserRotatio: The rotation type for this hand's laser.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._holderAxisOf: The holder axis offset for this hand.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._holderRotati: The holder rotation offset for this hand. used for laser rotation.
the reference value for the holder rotation for this hand. used for laser rotation.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._originalTwis: the original twist value for this holder. used for laser rotation.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler.ToolHolder: The slot that holds tooltips for this hand.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler.ShowInteracti: Whether to show tooltip interaction hints for this hand for the user.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._grabberSpher: The field to drive with whether the grab sphere visual should be visible.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._grabIgnoreRo: The slot which to ignore grabbable objects for this hand.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._grabber: The grabber component for this hand to handle grabbing objects.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._currentGrabT: The current grab type selected for this hand.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler.ActiveToolLin: The link that references to the active tool for this hand. Points to the <mark=#ffffff10>_equipLink</mark> on the equipped tool for this hand.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._activeToolGr: The grip pose reference to use for positioning the active tool for the user in this hand.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._toolLocked: Whether the tool is equipped and locked into place.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._grabMaterial: The material used for the grab sphere visual for this hand.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._itemShelfSlo: The slot for the current item shelf on the user.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandler._itemShelf: The component for the current item shelf on the user.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandlerPermissions: The type in each list item corrisponds to a tool in the game.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandlerPermissions.Al: Whether only tools which are set to <mark=#ffffff10>Allow</mark> are are allowed to be equipped.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandlerRelay: The InteractionHandlerRelay component is used internally by the game engine to find the source handler of a tool or slot.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandlerRelay.CommonTo: The handler this is a relay for.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandlerStreamDriver.S: The side to get the data.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandlerStreamDriver.P: The value interaction data currently being blocked. (Primary)
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandlerStreamDriver.S: The value interaction data currently being blocked. (Secondary)
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandlerStreamDriver.L: The streamed data for this interaction.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandlerStreamDriver.S: Shows the laser data for the other stream.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandlerStreamDriver.L: Shows the value for the target of this interaction.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionHandlerStreamDriver.G: Shows the data of the distance of this interaction.
messages.Tooltip.InteractiveCameraObjectExcludeTo: The InteractiveCameraObjectExludeTool component can add or remove objects from the exclude render list of the user's currently active InteractiveCamera.
messages.Tooltip.Laser: The Laser component creates a simple red laser when attached that raycasts the enviroment.
messages.Tooltip.Laser._material: The material to use for the laser.
messages.Tooltip.Laser._rayDriver: The ray driver driving the generated segment mesh.
messages.Tooltip.Laser._meshRenderer: The mesh renderer rendering the segment mesh for this laser pointer type component.
messages.Tooltip.LightOrb: The Light Orb component is used as a visual for lights created by the Light Tool.
messages.Tooltip.LightOrb.FlipOnTouch: Whether this can be toggled by touching a collider on the same slot.
messages.Tooltip.LightOrb.EmissionColor: The field to drive to control the emission color of the light.
messages.Tooltip.LightOrb.AcceptPhysicalTouch: Whether to allow Component:TipTouchSources to interact with/activate this component.
messages.Tooltip.LightOrb.AcceptRemoteTouch: Whether this component allows interaction via the user's interaction laser.
messages.Tooltip.LightTool: The LightTool component can be best learned through its page, the Light Tool for non power users.
messages.Tooltip.LightTool.Color: The color to make new lights.
messages.Tooltip.LightTool.Intensity: The intensity to make new lights.
messages.Tooltip.LightTool.ShadowType: The shadow type to make new lights.
messages.Tooltip.LightTool.ShadowStrength: The strength for the shadows new lights should have.
messages.Tooltip.LightTool.SpotAngle: The spot angle of new lights.
messages.Tooltip.LightTool.PointLightVisual: The current point light visual root.
messages.Tooltip.LightTool.SpotlightVisual: The current spot light visual root.
messages.Tooltip.LightTool.DirectionalLightVisual: The current directional light visual root.
messages.Tooltip.LightTool.ColorIndicators: A list of color fields to drive with the indication color.
messages.Tooltip.LightTool.ShowGizmo: Whether to show the Gizmo of new lights.
messages.Tooltip.LightTool._colorPicker: The current color picker being used to pick the color for <mark=#ffffff10>Color</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.MeshVisibilityToggleTool.SetShad: Whether to set the meshes shadow mode and what to set it to.
messages.Tooltip.MicrophoneTool.Format: The type of audio file to make.
messages.Tooltip.MicrophoneTool.Quality: The quality of the resulting audio clip file. (only relevant for <mark=#ffffff10><noparse>Format=OGG_Vorbis</noparse></mark>)
messages.Tooltip.MicrophoneTool.Mode: Whether the user has to hold or it's a toggle.
messages.Tooltip.MicrophoneTool.Source: The way to record the audio.
messages.Tooltip.MicrophoneTool.RecordingUser: The user that is currently recording using the Tool.
messages.Tooltip.MicrophoneTool.RecordingStartTim: The time that the user started recording in world time.
messages.Tooltip.MicrophoneTool.IsRecording: Whether the tool is currently being used to record.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectSlicerTool.EdgeColor: what the edge color for new generated slice materials should be.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectSlicerTool.EdgeStart: The start of the range of the edge color from the slicer.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectSlicerTool.EdgeEnd: The end of the range of the edge color from the slicer.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectSlicerTool._slicers: The list of slicers this tool has generated and/or is controlling.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectSlicerTool._sliceMaterials: The list of slice materials this tool has generated and/or is controlling.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleSpray: The ParticleSpray component is a tool that shoots particles when the trigger is pulled.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleSpray.Rate: Ths rate at which the spray tip sprays at max (primary strength is raised to <mark=#ffffff10>RateExp</mark> first before multiplying with this)
messages.Tooltip.ParticleSpray.RateExp: primary strength of the tool is raised to this number.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleSpray.MinSpeed: The min speed the particles travel at.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleSpray.MaxSpeed: The max speed the particles travel at.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleSpray.partSpeed: The min/max speed the particles travel at, is synced with <mark=#ffffff10>MinSpeed</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>MaxSpeed</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ParticleSpray.partEmitter: the Particle emitter this particle spray is controlling.
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool: The RawDataTool component is used in many different tool creations to allow for getting tool related actions on the hand its equipped in.
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool.LocalTipOffset: How much to offset the tool and its laser
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool.LocalTipReference: The slot to use for the laser source and tip location.
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool.UseLaser: Whether to enable the use of and visual of the Laser.
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool.BlockPrimaryWhenTouc: blocks use of primary when the tool is touching a wall.
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool.UseSecondary: Allow for use of secondary. When disabled on quest this disables the Tank Controls mode which many quest users in the resonite community dislike.
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool.AllowUseWhenHolding: Allow use of the tool when the user is holding onto the tool rather than having it equipped.
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool.Equipped: Whether the tool is equipped or not. This will still return true if the user is holding onto the tip. Commonly referred to as "Soft Equipping" or "Pseudo Equipping".
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool.ControllerType: The Type (C# class) of the controller holding this tool.
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool.ControllerSide: Whether the left, right, or none controller is holding this tool.
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool.PrimaryStrength: The live updated value of the user's primary strength on this hand. Applies to VRcontrollers or game pad type controllers.
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool.SecondaryAxis: The position of the thumb on the track pad used for secondary on this hand.
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool.Primary: Whether the user is pressing primary on this hand. See Controls
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool.Secondary: Whether the user is pressing secondary on this hand. See Controls.
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool._primaryStrengthStre: The value stream of the primary strength value. This comes from a user's value streams which can be viewed through a User Inspector.
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool._secondaryAxisStream: The value stream of the secondary strength value. This comes from a user's value streams which can be viewed through a User Inspector.
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool._primaryStream: The value stream of the primary pressed value. This comes from a user's value streams which can be viewed through a User Inspector.
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool._secondaryStream: The value stream of the secondary pressed value. This comes from a user's value streams which can be viewed through a User Inspector.
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool._rawStrength: The Raw unfiltered value of the primary strength value.
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool._rawAxis: the Raw unfiltered value of the secondary axis value.
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool._rawPrimary: the Raw unfiltered value of the primary pressed value.
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool._rawSecondary: the Raw unfiltered value of the secondary pressed value.
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool.PrimaryActionDescrip: sets the label that tells the user what primary on this tool does.
messages.Tooltip.RawDataTool.SecondaryActionDescr: sets the label that tells the user what secondary on this tool does.
messages.Tooltip.RigMeshTransferTool.SourceRig: The original rig Component to get data from.
messages.Tooltip.RigMeshTransferTool._label: The indication label for this tool.
messages.Tooltip.ShapeTool.shapes: A list of shapes to switch between depending on <mark=#ffffff10>activeShape</mark> for the tool tips visual.
messages.Tooltip.ShapeTool.Material: The material to use when drawing shapes.
messages.Tooltip.SpotLightTool: The SpotLightTool component is a tool that when pressed primary with turns on and off a spotlight. This tool is best described as a flashlight.
messages.Tooltip.SpotLightTool.Material: The material for the default cone visual.
messages.Tooltip.SpotLightTool.Light: The light attached to this tool that turns on and off
messages.Tooltip.SunMover: The SunMover Tool is a tool that can move the sun by simply pointing at the sky and pressing primary.
messages.Tooltip.ToolMultiplexer: A Tool Multiplexer is a key component in almost every multitool to ever exist. It handles the switching and usage of multiple tools, and allows all of them to act as one tool tip the user can equip, making multiple tool usage easier. Multitools can be assigned to the Tools list of other multitools.
messages.Tooltip.ToolMultiplexer.ActiveToolIndex: The tool that this tool tip is currently using
messages.Tooltip.ToolMultiplexer.Tools: A list of tools that this tool tip is to switch between using <mark=#ffffff10>ActiveToolIndex</mark> as the one it should be currently using.
messages.Tooltip.ToolSimulator: The ToolSimulator Component allows a tool to be used without needing it to be equipped by a User.
messages.Tooltip.ToolSimulator.Tool: The tool to simulate the use of.
messages.Tooltip.ToolSimulator.SimulatingUser: The user that should simulate the tool.
messages.Tooltip.ToolSimulator.Primary: Whether <mark=#ffffff10>Tool</mark> should be simulated as holding primary.
messages.Tooltip.ToolSimulator.Secondary: Whether <mark=#ffffff10>Tool</mark> should be simulated as holding secondary.
messages.Tooltip.ToolSimulator.Strength: The primary strength the <mark=#ffffff10>Tool</mark> should be simulated as using.
messages.Tooltip.ToolSimulator.Axis: The position axis (like trackpad) the <mark=#ffffff10>Tool</mark> should be simulated as using.
messages.Tooltip.WigglerApplier: Applies a Wiggler Component to a slot to make it rotate randomly.

messages.Tooltip.WorldGrabMover._referencePositio: the position this visual is trying to stay and anchor at.
messages.Tooltip.LabelerTool: See Tools for where to get this tool and what it does.

messages.Tooltip.TwitchChatDialog: Twitch chat dialogue is a component used to control and manage a live stream of chat messages sent by users at Twitch from a specific channel on Twitch.
messages.Tooltip.TwitchChatDialog.MaxMessages: The maximum amount of messages to keep in the message feed before deleting old ones.
messages.Tooltip.TwitchChatDialog.Interface: The twitch interface that is providing and receiving events to allow this component to function.
messages.Tooltip.TwitchChatDialog._channelName: The text field that holds the name of the channel this component is displaying chat messages for.
messages.Tooltip.TwitchChatDialog._viewerCount: The text object displaying the amount of users watching <mark=#ffffff10>_channelName</mark>
messages.Tooltip.TwitchChatDialog._messagesRoot: the slot to put UIX text components under which are messages sent into <mark=#ffffff10>_channelName</mark>'s chat.
messages.Tooltip.TwitchChatDialog._messagesScroll: The scroll rectangle that is controlled to make sure it stays scrolled to the bottom as chat messages appear.
messages.Tooltip.TwitchChatDialog._highlightPanel: The image used for when the panel is highlighted.
messages.Tooltip.TwitchChatDialog._highlightText: The text used for when the panel is highlighted.
messages.Tooltip.TwitchChatDialog._spriteSheet: The dynamically changing sprite font for text characters like custom twitch emojis.
messages.Tooltip.TwitchChatDialog._fontCollection: A list of fonts used by chat messages from twitch.
messages.Tooltip.ItemShelf: The Item Shelf component, often referred to as the tool shelf, is found on the wrists of avatars and is used to place items or tools onto the user's hands for later retrieval or use. It is sometimes used by creations to keep an item on the user's hand.
messages.Tooltip.ItemShelf.GrowDirection: the direction the shelf should grow as the item area grows.
messages.Tooltip.ItemShelf.IgnoreGrabber: The grabber that when items are released from it, the items should be ignored by this.
messages.Tooltip.ItemShelf.MinLength: The minimum length the shelf visual can be.
messages.Tooltip.ItemShelf.Width: The width of the shelf visual.
messages.Tooltip.ItemShelf.Thickness: The thickness of the shelf visual.
messages.Tooltip.ItemShelf.MaxItemSize: The maximum size before items will not stick to this shelf.
messages.Tooltip.ItemShelf.MaxPlaneDistance: The max plane distance items can be before they will not stick to this shelf.
messages.Tooltip.ItemShelf.MaxHeightDistance: The max height distance items can be before they will not stick to this shelf.
messages.Tooltip.ItemShelf._targetLength: The length this item shelf is tweening towards.
messages.Tooltip.ItemShelf._visual: The root of the shelf visual.
messages.Tooltip.ItemShelf._content: The slot containing items for this item shelf.
messages.Tooltip.ItemShelf._material: The material being used for the item shelf.
messages.Tooltip.ItemShelf._visualOffset: The field being used to offset the visual position.
messages.Tooltip.ItemShelf._shelfMesh: The mesh being used for the shelf visual.
messages.Tooltip.ItemShelf._collider: The collider being used to receive items for this shelf.
messages.Tooltip.AuthorityTimeBase: The output of the component is the current world time (as a Double) multiplied by the BaseSpeed, minus the _actualOffset.
messages.Tooltip.AuthorityTimeBase._actualSpeed: Actual speed of the time base. Should not be written to or driven.
messages.Tooltip.AuthorityTimeBase._actualOffset: Offset of the time base from the current world time. Can be written to or driven perfectly fine.
messages.Tooltip.AutoLookAtUser: The AutoLookAtUser component can be used to make a slot rotate towards a given user or some user that is close to it.
messages.Tooltip.AutoLookAtUser.RetargetDistance: The distance that the current target needs to be from the slot for it to pick a new target.
messages.Tooltip.AutoLookAtUser.LerpSpeed: The speed at which the slot rotates towards its target.
messages.Tooltip.AutoLookAtUser.TargetedUser: The user that the slot is currently looking at.
messages.Tooltip.AutoLookAtUser.AutoTarget: Whether or not the component should pick its own target.
messages.Tooltip.AutoLookAtUser.ExcludeActiveUser: If this is true and the slot with this component is parented to a user, it won't look at that user.
messages.Tooltip.AutoLookAtUser.SwingReference: The first axis in which to restrict rotation essentially.
messages.Tooltip.AutoLookAtUser.TwistReference: The second axis in which to restrict rotation essentially, for best results, make this axis perpendicular to <mark=#ffffff10>SwingReference</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.AutoLookAtUser.MaxSwing: How much this can rotate on the <mark=#ffffff10>SwingReference</mark> axis.
messages.Tooltip.AutoLookAtUser.MaxTwist: How much this can rotate on the <mark=#ffffff10>TwistReference</mark> axis.
messages.Tooltip.AutoLookAtUser._rotation: The rotation field to drive with this component's calculations. Usually this is the slot that this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.AxisAligner: The AxisAligner component is used to Align elements along a single line axis in local space under a slot based on their Bounding boxes. It also allows for excluding bounded elements from calculations, and for aligning elements themselves so that they can be centered like varied sized beads on a string or like boxes into the corner edge between a wall and floor. These calculations are heavy, and this component is best used lightly. Remove this component after using it to Align world elements, as it will be constantly active and will behave violently if used to Align shelves for example in a world and left unremoved.
messages.Tooltip.AutoAddChildrenBase.AutoAddChild: Controls whether slots below this component's slot in the hierarchy are automatically added to <mark=#ffffff10>_targets</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.AutoAddChildrenBase.AutoAddIgnor: if <mark=#ffffff10>AutoAddChildren</mark> is enabled. A slot will not be added to <mark=#ffffff10>_targets</mark> if it's tag is in this list.
messages.Tooltip.AxisAligner.Direction: The axis and direction the items will be aligned on
messages.Tooltip.AxisAligner.GlobalAxisXAlign: Controls how items will be aligned globally, with each other in the X Axis
messages.Tooltip.AxisAligner.GlobalAxisYAlign: Controls how items will be aligned globally, with each other in the Y Axis
messages.Tooltip.AxisAligner.GlobalAxisZAlign: Controls how items will be aligned globally, with each other in the Z Axis
messages.Tooltip.AxisAligner.ElementAxisXAlign: Controls how each item aligns itself, within the align, in the X Axis
messages.Tooltip.AxisAligner.ElementAxisYAlign: Controls how each item aligns itself, within the align, in the Y Axis
messages.Tooltip.AxisAligner.ElementAxisZAlign: Controls how each item aligns itself, within the align, in the Z Axis
messages.Tooltip.AxisAligner.Separation: The spacing between each item in <mark=#ffffff10>_targets</mark> along the specified axis
messages.Tooltip.AxisAligner.ExcludeList: A list of Bounded elements under target slots which are excluded from the aligners bounds calculations
messages.Tooltip.AxisAligner._targets: A list of slots which will be aligned. This will be automatically generated if <mark=#ffffff10>AutoAddChildren</mark> is enabled.
messages.Tooltip.AxisPanner: The AxisPanner component is used to move an object along a single axis linearly. This component can affect position and scale.
messages.Tooltip.AxisPanner.TimeBase: Reference to the timebase or "clock" that the component will use for moving an object. Possible components that can be used as an input include AuthorityTimeBase.
messages.Tooltip.AxisPanner.Speed: The speed at which the object will move.
messages.Tooltip.AxisPanner.Range: How far the object will move.
messages.Tooltip.AxisPanner.Offset: Where the object starts its move.
messages.Tooltip.AxisPanner.Axis: The direction that the object will move in.
messages.Tooltip.AxisPanner.ReferenceScale: Sets the maximum scale that the object will become at the halfway point. Starting at 0 the object will grow to this size and then back to 0.
messages.Tooltip.AxisPanner.FullScaleRangeRatio: Changes the duration that the object scale will remain at the ReferenceScale size.
messages.Tooltip.AxisPanner._position: Reference to the position that is being driven. By default, set the to object that this component is first attached to.
messages.Tooltip.AxisPanner._scale: Reference to the scale that is being driven. By default, set to null.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanReferenceDriver`1: Attach this component to a slot by first choosing what kind of value you want to boolean switch.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanReferenceDriver`1.State: Determines whether TargetReference should be driven to TrueTarget or to FalseTarget.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanReferenceDriver`1.TargetR: The field that should be driven.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanReferenceDriver`1.FalseTa: The value that the field will be driven to if <mark=#ffffff10>State</mark> is false.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanReferenceDriver`1.TrueTar: The value that the field will be driven to if <mark=#ffffff10>State</mark> is true.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanSwitcher: The BooleanSwitcher component can be used to enable and disable a list of Slots depending on an index in the list.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanSwitcher.Targets: The list of slots of which one will be enabled.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanSwitcher.ActiveIndex: The index of the currently active slot.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanSwitcher.ActivationMode: Allows for enabling items in <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> based on <mark=#ffffff10>ActiveIndex</mark> number.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanValueDriver`1: The BooleanValueDriver component drives the Field in TargetField to the value specified in TrueValue or FalseValue based on State.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanValueDriver`1.State: The state of this component. Chooses which value to use when driving <mark=#ffffff10>TargetField</mark>
messages.Tooltip.BooleanValueDriver`1.FalseValue: The value that is used to drive <mark=#ffffff10>TargetField</mark> when <mark=#ffffff10>State</mark> is false.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanValueDriver`1.TrueValue: The value that is used to drive <mark=#ffffff10>TargetField</mark> when <mark=#ffffff10>State</mark> is true.
messages.Tooltip.CircleAligner: The CircleAligner component is used to position a list of slots in a circle.
messages.Tooltip.CircleAligner.Axis: The axis around which the slots will be aligned.
messages.Tooltip.CircleAligner.Radius: The radius of the circle that the slots will be positioned on.
messages.Tooltip.CircleAligner.Offset: Offsets the starting position of the circle.
messages.Tooltip.CircleAligner.Arc: 360 by default. If smaller, the slots will only fill that section of a circle. Likewise, if it is larger than 360, the slots will wrap around the circle multiple times.
messages.Tooltip.CircleAligner.FillWholeArc: If true, the last slot will be at the very end of the arch, otherwise there will be a gap after the last slots.
messages.Tooltip.CircleAligner.RotationOffset: An offset, in degrees, that is applied to the rotation of the target slots.
messages.Tooltip.CircleAligner.Items: The list of slots to be aligned.
messages.Tooltip.ColorToColorX: The ColorToColorX component uses data from a Color and a ColorProfile to drive the value of a ColorX field with optional write back.
messages.Tooltip.ColorToColorX.SourceColor: The color to convert into a ColorX for <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ColorToColorX.SourceProfile: The color profile to use for the resulting <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ColorToColorX.DefaultProfile: the ColorProfile to use for <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> if <mark=#ffffff10>SourceProfile</mark> is empty.
messages.Tooltip.ColorToColorX.Target: The field to drive with the converted <mark=#ffffff10>SourceColor</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ColorToColorX.WriteBack: Whether to allow changes to the field specified by <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> to go backwards and affect <mark=#ffffff10>DefaultProfile</mark> or <mark=#ffffff10>SourceProfile</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>SourceColor</mark>. See write backs.
messages.Tooltip.DoubleToFloatCopy.Source: The source Double field to copy from.
messages.Tooltip.DoubleToFloatCopy.Target: The field to drive with the value of a Float representation of <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark>
messages.Tooltip.DoubleToFloatCopy.WriteBack: Whether changes to <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> get written back to <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark>
messages.Tooltip.CopyGlobalScale: The CopyGlobalScale component is used to ensure that one object has the exact same global scale as another object.
messages.Tooltip.CopyGlobalScale.Source: The object that serves as scale reference.
messages.Tooltip.CopyGlobalScale.NonUniform: If true, non-uniform scale is preserved, otherwise the X scale of the source serves as X, Y and Z of the target.
messages.Tooltip.CopyGlobalScale._scaleDrive: The field that is driven to match the global scale of the source. This automatically gets populated with the scale field of the slot that this component is added to.
messages.Tooltip.CopyGlobalTransform: The CopyGlobalTransform component is used to ensure that one object has the exact same global transform (position and rotation) as another object.
messages.Tooltip.CopyGlobalTransform.Source: The object that serves as the transform reference.
messages.Tooltip.CopyGlobalTransform._posDrive: The field that is driven to match the global position of the source. This automatically gets populated with the position field of the slot that this component is added to.
messages.Tooltip.CopyGlobalTransform._rotDrive: The field that is driven to match the global rotation of the source. This automatically gets populated with the rotation field of the slot that this component is added to.
messages.Tooltip.DistanceMeter: The DistanceMeter component is used by the Meter Tool to display the distance between slots placed by it.
messages.Tooltip.DistanceMeter.Anchors: List of points being used to measure right now.
messages.Tooltip.DistanceMeter._legacyAnchor0: Legacy, unused. For when the tool only measured two points.
messages.Tooltip.DistanceMeter._legacyAnchor1: Legacy, unused. For when the tool only measured two points.
messages.Tooltip.DistanceMeter.FormatUnit: The formatting string to use when formatting the distance number
messages.Tooltip.DistanceMeter.FormatNumber: How to format the distance number
messages.Tooltip.DistanceMeter.CompoundFormatUnit: The format string to format compound measurement units with.
messages.Tooltip.DistanceMeter.CompoundUseLongNam: Whether to use long name of distance (Ex: cm vs Centimeter)
messages.Tooltip.DistanceMeter.CompoundOverrideNa: Whether to use compound measurements or not.
messages.Tooltip.DistanceMeter.CompoundDiscardLas: Whether to discard small distances like Millimeters.
messages.Tooltip.DistanceMeter.CompoundSeparator: What to separate compound measurements with (like spaces or commas)
messages.Tooltip.DistanceMeter.CompoundZeroHandli: How to handle compound measurements (1 meter 1cm 10mm)
messages.Tooltip.DistanceMeter.MeasurementSpace: The transform space to measure in.
messages.Tooltip.DistanceMeter.DistanceText: The field to drive when displaying the total amount of distance.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbableParenter: Grabbable Parenter is a component that when paired with a collider that is not set to a Trigger or StaticTrigger type, it will receive objects that are released within it's collider. This is a volume based alternative to Grabbable Receiver Surface.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbableParenter.ParentUnder: The slot to parent objects under instead of the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbableParenter.Filter: A Sync Delegate that takes an IGrabbable and returns a boolean. If a grabbable returns false when fed into this function, that IGrabbable will not be parented by this component.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbableReceiverSurface: The GrabbableReceiverSurface component allows you to set up an object so that Grabbable objects snap to its surface when a user releases the Grabbable object within range of it. For a volume based alternative, see Grabbable Parenter.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbableReceiverSurface.ParentP: Whether the Grabbable object gets reparented to this object's Slot or the Slot specified in OverrideParent.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbableReceiverSurface.Overrid: If not null, the slot that the Grabbable object gets reparented to, if ParentPlaced is true.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbableReceiverSurface.TweenTi: The amount of time in seconds for the animation effect when the object snaps to the surface.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbableReceiverSurface.MaxDist: How far away in meters something released gets grabbed.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbableReceiverSurface.Offset: The distance from the surface that the Grabbable object will stop at.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbableReceiverSurface.CheckOf: How far away from the surface that the object ignores before it starts looking for released objects.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbableReceiverSurface.Directi: List of directions a Grabbable object can be received from.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbableReceiverSurface.TagFilt: A filter that limits what can be picked up by this surface.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper1D: Linear Mapper 1D is a component that live maps one range to another, allowing for a reverse mapping of the target range value back to the source range when it is written to.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper1D.Source: The value to map from <mark=#ffffff10>SourceMin</mark> to <mark=#ffffff10>SourceMax</mark> using <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper1D.Target: The value to map from <mark=#ffffff10>TargetMin</mark> to <mark=#ffffff10>TargetMax</mark> using <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper1D.SourceMin: The minimum of the range of the <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper1D.SourceMax: The maximum of the range of the <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper1D.TargetMin: The minimum of the range of the <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper1D.TargetMax: The maximum of the range of the <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper1D.AllowReverseMappi: Allow <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> to map it's value to <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark>'s ranged value when written to. See write backs.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper1D.Clamp: Whether to prevent <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> from going outside their defined ranges.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper2D: Linear Mapper 2D is a component that draws a line from TargetMin and TargetMax and uses a linear range between the two with Target to map Source to a range between SourceMin and SourceMax.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper2D.Source: The value to map from <mark=#ffffff10>SourceMin</mark> to <mark=#ffffff10>SourceMax</mark> using <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper2D.Target: The value to map from <mark=#ffffff10>TargetMin</mark> to <mark=#ffffff10>TargetMax</mark> using <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper2D.SourceMin: The minimum of the range of the <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper2D.SourceMax: The maximum of the range of the <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper2D.TargetMin: The minimum of the range of the <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper2D.TargetMax: The maximum of the range of the <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper2D.AllowReverseMappi: Allow <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> to map it's value to <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark>'s ranged value when written to.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper2D.Clamp: Whether to prevent <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> from going outside their defined ranges.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper3D: Linear Mapper 3D is a component that draws a line from TargetMin and TargetMax and uses a linear range between the two with Target to map Source to a range between SourceMin and SourceMax.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper3D.Source: The value to map from <mark=#ffffff10>SourceMin</mark> to <mark=#ffffff10>SourceMax</mark> using <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper3D.Target: The value to map from <mark=#ffffff10>TargetMin</mark> to <mark=#ffffff10>TargetMax</mark> using <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper3D.SourceMin: The minimum of the range of the <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper3D.SourceMax: The maximum of the range of the <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper3D.TargetMin: The minimum of the range of the <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper3D.TargetMax: The maximum of the range of the <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper3D.AllowReverseMappi: Allow <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> to map it's value to <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark>'s ranged value when written to. See write backs.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper3D.Clamp: Whether to prevent <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> from going outside their defined ranges.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper4D: Linear Mapper 4D is a component that draws a line from TargetMin and TargetMax and uses a linear range between the two with Target to map Source to a range between SourceMin and SourceMax.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper4D.Source: The value to map from <mark=#ffffff10>SourceMin</mark> to <mark=#ffffff10>SourceMax</mark> using <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper4D.Target: The value to map from <mark=#ffffff10>TargetMin</mark> to <mark=#ffffff10>TargetMax</mark> using <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper4D.SourceMin: The minimum of the range of the <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper4D.SourceMax: The maximum of the range of the <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper4D.TargetMin: The minimum of the range of the <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper4D.TargetMax: The maximum of the range of the <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper4D.AllowReverseMappi: Allow <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> to map it's value to <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark>'s ranged value when written to. See write backs.
messages.Tooltip.LinearMapper4D.Clamp: Whether to prevent <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> from going outside their defined ranges.
messages.Tooltip.LinearColorMapper: The LinearColorMapper allows for mapping a float to a color range, and optionally do so in reverse.
messages.Tooltip.LinearColorMapper.Source: The value to map from <mark=#ffffff10>SourceMin</mark> to <mark=#ffffff10>SourceMax</mark> using <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.LinearColorMapper.Target: The value to map from <mark=#ffffff10>TargetMin</mark> to <mark=#ffffff10>TargetMax</mark> using <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.LinearColorMapper.SourceMin: The minimum of the range of the <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearColorMapper.SourceMax: The maximum of the range of the <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearColorMapper.TargetMin: The minimum of the range of the <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearColorMapper.TargetMax: The maximum of the range of the <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearColorMapper.AllowReverseMa: Allow <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> to map it's value to <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark>'s ranged value when written to. See write backs.
messages.Tooltip.LinearColorMapper.Clamp: Whether to prevent <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> from going outside their defined ranges.
messages.Tooltip.LinearRotationMapper: The LinearRotationMapper component is used to map a float range to a rotation range using lerp.
messages.Tooltip.LinearRotationMapper.Source: The source value field to get the source mapped value from.
messages.Tooltip.LinearRotationMapper.Target: The rotation to drive with the mapped rotation value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearRotationMapper.SourceMin: The minimum value for <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> for mapping.
messages.Tooltip.LinearRotationMapper.SourceMax: The maximum value for <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> for mapping.
messages.Tooltip.LinearRotationMapper.TargetMin: The minumum rotation value when <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> is at <mark=#ffffff10>SourceMin</mark>'s value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearRotationMapper.TargetMax: The maximum rotation value when <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> is at <mark=#ffffff10>SourceMax</mark>'s value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearRotationMapper.AllowRevers: Whether changes to <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> map in the reverse direction and get applied to target. See write backs.
messages.Tooltip.LinearRotationMapper.Clamp: Whether to allow extrapolation of the lerped data or to keep it within the values specified.
messages.Tooltip.LinearAngleMapper: The LinearAngleMapper component is used to map a float range to a rotation range using lerp.
messages.Tooltip.LinearAngleMapper.Source: The source value field to get the source mapped value from.
messages.Tooltip.LinearAngleMapper.Target: The rotation to drive with the mapped rotation value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearAngleMapper.SourceMin: The minimum value for <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> for mapping.
messages.Tooltip.LinearAngleMapper.SourceMax: The maximum value for <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> for mapping.
messages.Tooltip.LinearAngleMapper.TargetMin: The minumum rotation value when <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> is at <mark=#ffffff10>SourceMin</mark>'s value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearAngleMapper.TargetMax: The maximum rotation value when <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> is at <mark=#ffffff10>SourceMax</mark>'s value.
messages.Tooltip.LinearAngleMapper.AllowReverseMa: Whether changes to <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> map in the reverse direction and get applied to target. See write backs.
messages.Tooltip.LinearAngleMapper.Clamp: Whether to allow extrapolation of the lerped data or to keep it within the values specified.
messages.Tooltip.LookAt: Attach the component to a slot and provide a Target or TargetPoint to make it look at or point at a position.
messages.Tooltip.LookAt.Target: The slot to try to look at.
messages.Tooltip.LookAt.TargetPoint: The 3D point to look at when <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> is not provided.
messages.Tooltip.LookAt.Up: The up axis of this slot, usually best to keep at (0,1,0)
messages.Tooltip.LookAt.RotationOffset: How much to rotate the slot by after applying the look at (quaternion multiplying)
messages.Tooltip.LookAt.SwingReference: The axis to apply "Swing" rotation to.
messages.Tooltip.LookAt.TwistReference: The axis to apply "Twist" rotation to.
messages.Tooltip.LookAt.MaxSwing: The maximum (pos/neg) amount that the slot can rotate on <mark=#ffffff10>SwingReference</mark>
messages.Tooltip.LookAt.MaxTwist: The maximum (pos/neg) amount that the slot can rotate on <mark=#ffffff10>TwistReference</mark>
messages.Tooltip.LookAt._target: The field to apply the rotation result to.
messages.Tooltip.LookAtUser: The LookAtUser component drives the rotation specified by _rotationDrive to point towards the user specified in TargetUser, or the Local User if TargetAtLocalUser is checked.
messages.Tooltip.LookAtUser.TargetUser: The user to look at, unless TargetAtLocalUser is checked.
messages.Tooltip.LookAtUser.TargetAtLocalUser: Targets the Local User in each client.
messages.Tooltip.LookAtUser.SourcePositionOffset: Offset from which to calculate the look vector.
messages.Tooltip.LookAtUser.Invert: The rotated item faces away from the user, instead of towards.
messages.Tooltip.LookAtUser.RotationOffset: The degree to which the item is rotated away from the look vector.
messages.Tooltip.LookAtUser.PositionSource: The UserNode to calculate the look vector from
messages.Tooltip.LookAtUser.AroundAxis: The rotation is around an arbitrary axis defined in <mark=#ffffff10>Axis</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.LookAtUser.Axis: A unit vector specifying the arbitrary axis to rotate around
messages.Tooltip.LookAtUser._rotationDrive: Automatically Assigned - The FloatQ to be driven by this component.
messages.Tooltip.MirrorTransform: The Mirror Transform drives the position of the slot to mirror the position of an inputted slot across the normal of a plane.
messages.Tooltip.MirrorTransform.MirrorSource: The slot to get the mirror data from.
messages.Tooltip.MirrorTransform.MirrorPlane: The slot to use as the position to mirror across.
messages.Tooltip.MirrorTransform.MirrorOffset: An offset from the <mark=#ffffff10>MirrorPlane</mark> to mirror across.
messages.Tooltip.MirrorTransform.AllowWriteBack: Whether to allow changing the target fields of <mark=#ffffff10>_position</mark> or <mark=#ffffff10>_rotation</mark> to change the position and rotation of <mark=#ffffff10>MirrorSource</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.MirrorTransform._position: The position field to drive with the mirrored mirror data. Usually the Position field of the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.MirrorTransform._rotation: The rotation field to drive with the mirrored mirror data. Usually the Rotation field of the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.MultiBoolConditionDriver: The MultiBoolConditionDriver drives Target depending on the state of the Conditions list, and the ConditionMode selected.
messages.Tooltip.MultiBoolConditionDriver.Target: The field to drive to true if the bools in <mark=#ffffff10>Conditions</mark> meet the conditions set by <mark=#ffffff10>ConditionMode</mark>
messages.Tooltip.MultiBoolConditionDriver.Mode: What state the bools in <mark=#ffffff10>Conditions</mark> must be in, to drive <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> to true.
messages.Tooltip.MultiBoolConditionDriver.Conditi: A list of bool fields to compare against the <mark=#ffffff10>ConditionMode</mark>
messages.Tooltip.NullableSourceDriver`1: The NullableSourceDriver component allows for getting info from a Source nullable and driving a set of split fields with content from the nullable. Changes to driven fields by this component can also be reverse written back to Source.
messages.Tooltip.NullableSourceDriver`1.Source: The field to get nullable data from.
messages.Tooltip.NullableSourceDriver`1.Value: The field to drive with the value for <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> if it has a value, or instead drive with <mark=#ffffff10>DefaultValue</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.NullableSourceDriver`1.HasValue: The field to drive with whether <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> has a value.
messages.Tooltip.NullableSourceDriver`1.DefaultVa: The default value if <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> doesn't have a value.
messages.Tooltip.NullableSourceDriver`1.WriteBack: Whether writes to the fields driven by this component will update the value of <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark>. See write backs.
messages.Tooltip.NullableSourceDriver`1.UpdateDef: Whether the default value gets updated when using write back and <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> has no value.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectScroller: The ObjectScroller component acts as a way to scroll a list of items with sizes by offsetting them within a region they should appear within. Objects outside of the region get disabled until the Offset allows them to appear within it again.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectScroller.Items: A list of items to move and activate/deactivate if they are within/outside of the <mark=#ffffff10>RegionSize</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ObjectScroller.Offset: How much to offset the positions of <mark=#ffffff10>Items</mark> in order to scroll them.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectScroller.RegionSize: The size area objects have to be touching from their current position and size after <mark=#ffffff10>Offset</mark> is applied in order to stay visible.
messages.Tooltip.PositionAtUser: The PositionAtUser component drives the values in PositionDrive and RotationDrive to match the user's position and rotation as determined by the settings in PositionSource and RotationSource respectively.
messages.Tooltip.PositionAtUser.TargetUser: The user that will be used to calculate the  parent slot position, unless <mark=#ffffff10>PositionAtLocalUser</mark> is selected.
messages.Tooltip.PositionAtUser.PositionAtLocalUs: Position the parent slot at the Local User of each client, ignoring the value in <mark=#ffffff10>TargetUser</mark>
messages.Tooltip.PositionAtUser.TargetPositionOff: Position offset in the user's local space units. Respects the user's current scale and transforms.
messages.Tooltip.PositionAtUser.TargetRotationOff: Rotation offset in the user's local space unit. Respects the user's current scale and transforms.
messages.Tooltip.PositionAtUser.PositionOffset: Position offset in global space units
messages.Tooltip.PositionAtUser.RotationOffset: Rotation offset in global space units
messages.Tooltip.PositionAtUser.PositionSource: The user node that the position will be calculated from
messages.Tooltip.PositionAtUser.RotationSource: The user node that the rotation will be calculated from
messages.Tooltip.PositionAtUser.PositionDrive: Target Float3 to drive with the calculated position value
messages.Tooltip.PositionAtUser.RotationDrive: Target FloatQ to drive with the calculated rotation value.
messages.Tooltip.RayDriver: A ray driver is a component that takes a start origin and a direction and drives point A and B via a RayCast, keeping the distance between the two below MaxDistance.
messages.Tooltip.RayDriver.MaxDistance: the max distance that A and B can be from each other
messages.Tooltip.RayDriver.PointA: what to drive with the local space start position of the Raycast
messages.Tooltip.RayDriver.PointB: what to drive with the local space end point of the raycast
messages.Tooltip.RayDriver.LocalOrigin: the local space start point of the raycast
messages.Tooltip.RayDriver.LocalDirection: the direction in local space to fire the raycast in.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceCast`2: The ReferenceCast component can be used to try to take a type and change it to another type. This performs a C# cast which requires the types to be compatible for casting to make a result.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceCast`2.Target: The target field. Casts <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> to this fields type, and then uses the result to drive this field.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceCast`2.WriteBack: whether to allow changes to the <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> to update <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> instead of discarding the change. See write backs.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceCopy`1: The ReferenceCopy component is used to copy one reference to another place, driving the target with an write back option.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceCopy`1.Source: The source to copy the Reference from
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceCopy`1.Target: The target to drive to the value of <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceCopy`1.WriteBack: whether to allow changes done to <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> to change <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark>. See write backs.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceEqualityDriver`1: The ReferenceEqualityDriver component checks the equality of two objects and says whether they are equal. For objects that define their own behavior for checking Equality (Like Bounding boxes) this will check based on that logic (for Bounding boxes they only need to be numerically equal, like being the same size and center).
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceEqualityDriver`1.Refere: The reference to use as a thing to compare against.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceEqualityDriver`1.Target: The boolean field to drive to whether <mark=#ffffff10>TargetReference</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>Reference</mark> are equal. Doesn't always mean they are the exact same object.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceEqualityDriver`1.Invert: Whether to invert the result sent to <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceUserOverride`1: This is a component that allows you to locally change a reference on a value. Changing the value locally for yourself or others so that an item or world element is different depending on who's viewing it.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceUserOverride`1.Target: The value to drive locally for every user
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceUserOverride`1.Default: The content of this value will be inside of <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> for every user locally unless otherwise specified by an Override inside of the <mark=#ffffff10>_overrides</mark> bag.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceUserOverride`1.CreateOv: When flux or any other means tries to set the value of target from someone's machine, create an Override for them in <mark=#ffffff10>_overrides</mark> bag.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceUserOverride`1.Persiste: whether to save the overrides or not when the component is part of a hierarchy being saved. This is useful to turn on when an item should have overrides
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceUserOverride`1._overrid: A bag of Overrides that are read to make local changes to the value referenced by <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark>
messages.Tooltip.RelativePositioner: The RelativePositioner component puts the slot its attached to in a position based on the Bounding box and position of Reference. Optionally deleting the component on an update when the slot is positioned successfully.
messages.Tooltip.RelativePositioner.Reference: The slot to Relative position to.
messages.Tooltip.RelativePositioner.ReferenceBoun: The space to calculate the Bounding box of <mark=#ffffff10>Reference</mark> in.
messages.Tooltip.RelativePositioner.ReferenceAnch: How far away from the center of the <mark=#ffffff10>Reference</mark>'s Bounding box based on size. (1,1,1) puts this slot at the positive corner in global space of <mark=#ffffff10>Reference</mark>'s Bounding box and (0,0,0) put it at <mark=#ffffff10>Reference</mark>'s center Bounding box point.
messages.Tooltip.RelativePositioner.ReferenceOffs: How much to add to the slot position after Bounding box positioning.
messages.Tooltip.RelativePositioner.DestroyAfterD: Whether to destroy  this component after update and positioning was successfully done due to needed fields not being null.
messages.Tooltip.RelativePositioner._target: The position field of this slot to drive.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleAligner: The ScaleAligner component scales a list of slots based on bounding box, each one being bigger/smaller than the last.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleAligner.BaseSize: The size the first item should be in local space based on Bounding box.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleAligner.Increment: How much to make the next item in the list bigger/smaller than the last by adding to its Bounding box size.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleAligner.Multiplier: How much to make the next item in the list bigger/smaller than the last by multiplying its Bounding box size after adding <mark=#ffffff10>Increment</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ScaleAligner.NonUniform: Whether scaling can be non uniform for Bounding box Scale calculations.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleAligner._targets: A list of slots and their scales to influence.
messages.Tooltip.Scaler: The Scaler component scales a target scale based on the distance of the slot this component is on from ""Point"" = (ScaleOffset in ScaleSource's transform space) with the distance of both points calculated in ScaleSpace.
messages.Tooltip.Scaler.ScaleMultiplier: How much to multiply the strength of the distance from A to B after conversion has on scale.
messages.Tooltip.Scaler.ScaleMode: How to restrict the distance calculations for scaling certain axies.
messages.Tooltip.Scaler.ScaleSpace: The space to convert A and B to for distance measurement.
messages.Tooltip.Scaler.scaleTarget: The field to drive to the distance from A to B after conversion to space ScaleSpace and applying ScaleMode restrictions, and then muliplying by 2 * ScaleMultiplier.
messages.Tooltip.SphereAligner: Sphere aligner is a component that has a list of child slots under Items and aligns those items into a spherical shape.
messages.Tooltip.SphereAligner.Radius: The radius of the sphere to align items into
messages.Tooltip.SphereAligner.DistributionOffset: Shift the positions of the items along the sphere surface, redistributes.
messages.Tooltip.SphereAligner.AlignToNormal: Whether to rotate the items to align them to the surface of the sphere
messages.Tooltip.SphereAligner.RotationOffset: How much to rotate the sphere of aligned items by their individual origins
messages.Tooltip.SphereAligner.NormalizedStart: What point to start the alignment of items from top to bottom of the sphere.
messages.Tooltip.SphereAligner.NormalizedEnd: What point to end the alignment of items from top to bottom of the sphere.
messages.Tooltip.SphereAligner.HorizontalStart: What point to start the alignment of items along the equator of the sphere.
messages.Tooltip.SphereAligner.HorizontalEnd: What point to end the alignment of items along the equator of the sphere.
messages.Tooltip.SphereAligner.Items: A list of items to align into the sphere shape.
messages.Tooltip.Spinner: The Spinner component is used to make something rotate on 1 or more axis at a constant rate.
messages.Tooltip.Spinner.Range: how much each axis can go up to from 0 before wrapping around.
messages.Tooltip.Spinner._offset: the offset rotation to start at world start from.
messages.Tooltip.Spinner._speed: how fast to rotate on each axis, in Degrees per Second. Negative values go in reverse.
messages.Tooltip.TextureSizeDriver: The TextureSizeDriver component drives a field with the size of a texture in pixels.
messages.Tooltip.TextureSizeDriver.Texture: The texture to get a size from.
messages.Tooltip.TextureSizeDriver.Target: The value field to drive with the final result value.
messages.Tooltip.TextureSizeDriver.DriveMode: The way of restricting <mark=#ffffff10>Texture</mark> size values as second step.
messages.Tooltip.TextureSizeDriver.Premultiply: The value to multiply the <mark=#ffffff10>Texture</mark> size by as first step.
messages.Tooltip.TextureSizeDriver.Ratio: The value to multiply the <mark=#ffffff10>Texture</mark> size by as the third step.
messages.Tooltip.TextureSizeDriver.MaxSize: The value to restrict the output value to be less than this as the final step.
messages.Tooltip.TimeIntDriver: The TimeIntDriver Component drives the Int value specified in Target based on the current time, modified by the specified parameters.
messages.Tooltip.TimeIntDriver.Scale: Time scale in relation to time in seconds, where 1.0 means <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> will increment once per second.
messages.Tooltip.TimeIntDriver.Repeat: Once <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> reaches this value, it will reset to 0 and restart.
messages.Tooltip.TimeIntDriver.PingPong: Causes <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> to reverse direction when it reaches 0 or the value in <mark=#ffffff10>Repeat</mark>, instead of restarting.
messages.Tooltip.UserDistanceValueDriver`1: The UserDistanceValueDriver switches or flip flops or flips between two different values like a conditional or ternary operator when a user is close to or far away from the slot this component is on. The switch is local.
messages.Tooltip.UserDistanceValueDriver`1.Node: The user node to check distance from.
messages.Tooltip.UserDistanceValueDriver`1.Distan: The distance to use when checking near vs far.
messages.Tooltip.UserDistanceValueDriver`1.Target: The field to drive with either <mark=#ffffff10>NearValue</mark> or flip to <mark=#ffffff10>FarValue</mark> depending on distance.
messages.Tooltip.UserDistanceValueDriver`1.NearVa: The value to use to drive <mark=#ffffff10>TargetField</mark> with when the local user <mark=#ffffff10>Node</mark> under <mark=#ffffff10>Distance</mark> away from this component's slot in global space.
messages.Tooltip.UserDistanceValueDriver`1.FarVal: The value to use to drive <mark=#ffffff10>TargetField</mark> with when the local user <mark=#ffffff10>Node</mark> over <mark=#ffffff10>Distance</mark> away from this component's slot in global space.
messages.Tooltip.UserRefToSyncRefAdapter: The UserRefToSyncRefAdapter component takes a UserRef type and drives a user ref field with what it has inside of it.
messages.Tooltip.UserRefToSyncRefAdapter.User: The ref to get a user from.
messages.Tooltip.UserRefToSyncRefAdapter.TargetRe: The field to drive with what user <mark=#ffffff10>User</mark> refers to.
messages.Tooltip.UserRefToSyncRefAdapter.WriteBac: Whether changes to the field <mark=#ffffff10>TargetReference</mark> will update the value of <mark=#ffffff10>User</mark>. See write backs.
messages.Tooltip.UVAtlasAnimator: The UVAtlasAnimator component is used primarily to drive texture animations on materials like GIFs. It can also be used as a sprite sheet to animate things like 2D mouths.
messages.Tooltip.UVAtlasAnimator.ScaleField: The texture's <mark=#ffffff10>TextureScale</mark> field that should be driven
messages.Tooltip.UVAtlasAnimator.OffsetField: The texture's <mark=#ffffff10>TextureOffset</mark> field that should be driven
messages.Tooltip.UVAtlasAnimator.AtlasInfo: The AtlasInfo component that provides the size and frame amount for the atlas
messages.Tooltip.UVAtlasAnimator.Frame: Which frame of the atlas the texture scale and offset should be driven to
messages.Tooltip.ValueCopy`1: This component ensures that the value in the Target field is equal to the value in the Source field.
messages.Tooltip.ValueCopy`1.Source: The source to copy the value from.
messages.Tooltip.ValueCopy`1.Target: The target to copy the value to.
messages.Tooltip.ValueCopy`1.WriteBack: Allow Target to write back to Source. See write backs.
messages.Tooltip.ValueEqualityDriver`1: The ValueEqualityDriver component lets you drive a boolean to whether or not one value is equal to another.
messages.Tooltip.ValueEqualityDriver`1.Reference: The value that <mark=#ffffff10>TargetValue</mark> is being compared to.
messages.Tooltip.ValueEqualityDriver`1.Target: The boolean that is driven to true if <mark=#ffffff10>TargetValue</mark> is equal to <mark=#ffffff10>Reference</mark> and false if it isn't.
messages.Tooltip.ValueEqualityDriver`1.Invert: Whether to invert the result of <mark=#ffffff10>TargetValue</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ValueEqualityDriver`1.UseApproxi: Whether or not to use approximate comparison for types such as float, where values that seem identical can be very slightly different.
messages.Tooltip.ValueGradientDriver`1: Each point in the Points list has a Position field and Value field.
messages.Tooltip.ValueGradientDriver`1.Progress: Controls which items from the <mark=#ffffff10>Points</mark> list will be used to drive the value of <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ValueGradientDriver`1.Target: The field that this component will drive the value of
messages.Tooltip.ValueGradientDriver`1.Interpolat: Controls whether or not this component will interpolate (or blend) between the nearest two <mark=#ffffff10>Points</mark> to the current <mark=#ffffff10>Progress</mark> value.
messages.Tooltip.ValueGradientDriver`1.Points: A list of items indicating their <mark=#ffffff10>Position</mark> (in relation to <mark=#ffffff10>Progress</mark>), and a <mark=#ffffff10>Value</mark>
messages.Tooltip.Float2Driver: The Float2Driver component can be used to drive a foat2 value, based on an X and a Y value.
messages.Tooltip.Float2Driver.X: The field that holds the X value for the float2. If null, the X component of the float2 will be 0.
messages.Tooltip.Float2Driver.Y: The field that holds the Y value for the float2. If null, the Y component of the float2 will be 0.
messages.Tooltip.Float2Driver.Target: The field that is driven to the constructed float2.
messages.Tooltip.Float3Driver: The Float3Driver component can be used to drive a foat3 value, based on an X, Y and a Z value.
messages.Tooltip.Float3Driver.X: The field that holds the X value for the float3. If null, the X component of the float3 will be 0.
messages.Tooltip.Float3Driver.Y: The field that holds the Y value for the float3. If null, the Y component of the float3 will be 0.
messages.Tooltip.Float3Driver.Z: The field that holds the Z value for the float3. If null, the Z component of the float3 will be 0.
messages.Tooltip.Float3Driver.Target: The field that is driven to the constructed float3.
messages.Tooltip.Float4Driver: The Float4Driver component can be used to drive a foat4 value, based on an X, Y, Z and a W value.
messages.Tooltip.Float4Driver.X: The field that holds the X value for the float4. If null, the X component of the float4 will be 0.
messages.Tooltip.Float4Driver.Y: The field that holds the Y value for the float4. If null, the Y component of the float4 will be 0.
messages.Tooltip.Float4Driver.Z: The field that holds the Z value for the float4. If null, the Z component of the float4 will be 0.
messages.Tooltip.Float4Driver.W: The field that holds the W value for the float4. If null, the W component of the float4 will be 0.
messages.Tooltip.Float4Driver.Target: The field that is driven to the constructed float4.
messages.Tooltip.Wiggler: The Wiggler component is used to make a FloatQ rotate randomly. Usually used on a slot's rotation.
messages.Tooltip.Wiggler._offset: The base value to "add" to when rotating.
messages.Tooltip.Wiggler._speed: How fast to wiggle on each axis.
messages.Tooltip.Wiggler._magnitude: How much to wiggle on each axis
messages.Tooltip.Wiggler._seed: the seed for the randomness of the wiggle.
messages.Tooltip.Wobbler1D: The Wobbler1D component can be used to make a value randomly change in a smooth fashion.
messages.Tooltip.Wobbler1D.Offset: The starting point of the wobble.
messages.Tooltip.Wobbler1D.Seed: the value to use as a seed for the randomness.
messages.Tooltip.Wobbler2D: The Wobbler2D component can be used to make a value randomly change in a smooth fashion.
messages.Tooltip.Wobbler2D.Offset: The starting point of the wobble.
messages.Tooltip.Wobbler2D.Seed: the value to use as a seed for the randomness.
messages.Tooltip.Wobbler3D: The Wobbler3D component can be used to make a value randomly change in a smooth fashion.
messages.Tooltip.Wobbler3D.Offset: The starting point of the wobble.
messages.Tooltip.Wobbler3D.Seed: the value to use as a seed for the randomness.
messages.Tooltip.Wobbler4D: The Wobbler4D component can be used to make a value randomly change in a smooth fashion.
messages.Tooltip.Wobbler4D.Offset: The starting point of the wobble.
messages.Tooltip.Wobbler4D.Seed: the value to use as a seed for the randomness.
messages.Tooltip.AI_GeneratedContent.Source: The AI used to make the content
messages.Tooltip.AxisRotationAligner: The AxisRotationAligner can be used to keep a slot facing a direction relative to a space.
messages.Tooltip.AxisRotationAligner.LocalDirecti: A vector direction in this slot's local space to align to <mark=#ffffff10>TargetDirection</mark> via rotating this slot.
messages.Tooltip.AxisRotationAligner.TargetDirect: The direction vector to align to.
messages.Tooltip.AxisRotationAligner.DirectionSpa: The coordinate space that <mark=#ffffff10>TargetDirection</mark> is in.
messages.Tooltip.AxisRotationAligner.LocalRotatio: A rotation offset to the alignment.
messages.Tooltip.AxisRotationAligner._rotation: The rotation field of the slot this component is on. Is driven to do the rotation alignment.
messages.Tooltip.ConstantLerpValue`1: The ConstantLerpValue component will make the Value reach TargetValue, making Speed progress per second. it can also be known as a linear lerp.
messages.Tooltip.ConstantLerpValue`1.Speed: The amount to change <mark=#ffffff10>Value</mark> by per second to reach <mark=#ffffff10>TargetValue</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ConstantLerpValue`1.WriteBack: Whether changes to <mark=#ffffff10>Value</mark> will be written to <mark=#ffffff10>TargetValue</mark>.  See write backs.
messages.Tooltip.ConstantLerpValue`1.Value: The value to constantly progress towards <mark=#ffffff10>TargetValue</mark>
messages.Tooltip.DestroyOnUserLeave: The DestroyOnUserLeave component causes the parent slot to be deleted when the user specified in User leaves the world.
messages.Tooltip.DestroyOnUserLeave.TargetUser: The user that will cause the parent slot to be deleted upon leaving
messages.Tooltip.DestroyRoot: The Destroy Root component marks the slot tagged with the component where an object's root is for destroying grabbed objects if the game cannot find an object root component above the grabbed object.
messages.Tooltip.DestroyWithoutChildren: The DestroyWithoutChildren component is used to make a slot self-destruct when it has no children.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBlendShapeDriver: This component is used to map values to Blendshapes by name.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBlendShapeDriver.Renderer: The renderer to use to search for shapekeys with
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBlendShapeDriver.BlendSha: A list of BlendShape that are searched for in order on the <mark=#ffffff10>Renderer</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBlendShapeDriver._lastRen: The last renderer that was searched. This value is automatically filled and is read only.
messages.Tooltip.Consumable: This component allows the item to be consumed by users.
messages.Tooltip.Consumable.MustBeHolding: Only allow component to be triggered when held.  Needs to be paired by a grabbable in the same slot.
messages.Tooltip.FlipAtUser: The FlipAtUser component can be used to make an object face towards positive or negative Z, depending on what side of it the local user is. <!--T:2-->
messages.Tooltip.FlipAtUser.UpSpace: the coordinate space that is used when calculating what is "up". If set to local user space, then the up direction of this component is the up direction of the slot the user is parented under, which is usually up for that user. This can be useful if a user is in a gravity direction which doesn't match the world's up direction, and will make this orient to that user's direction for a better reading experience for example.
messages.Tooltip.FlipAtUser._rotation: The field that is driven to make the object rotate to the user. This is automatically set to the Rotation field of the slot this component is added to.
messages.Tooltip.TimeSineDriver: The TimeSineDriver component outputs a value over time that matches a Sine Wave of the provided values.
messages.Tooltip.TimeSineDriver.Target: The field to drive with a constantly changing Sine wave.
messages.Tooltip.TimeSineDriver.Speed: The frequency/speed of the Sine Wave waves.
messages.Tooltip.TimeSineDriver.Min: The minimum value to drive <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> to.
messages.Tooltip.TimeSineDriver.Max: The maximum value to drive <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> to.
messages.Tooltip.Glue: The Glue component is generated by the GlueTool when the user press and drags primary with the tool equipped. The mode on that tool determines what this component's GlueMode will be.
messages.Tooltip.Glue.GlueMode: How to Glue the objects in the area or effect.
messages.Tooltip.Glue.Active: Whether this component is ready to glue.
messages.Tooltip.Glue.DryTime: How long it takes for this Glue to dry.
messages.Tooltip.Glue.Expire: When this component should delete itself in world time.
messages.Tooltip.Glue.GluingUser: The user that is gluing this glue.
messages.Tooltip.Glue._forceDry: Whether to force dry this glue.
messages.Tooltip.Glue.isDrying: Whether this Glue is currently drying or not.
messages.Tooltip.Glue.dryStartTime: When the user triggerd this Glue to start drying in world time.
messages.Tooltip.GlueVisualizer: The GlueVisualizer component is used by the Glue Tool to visualize glue objects that are to potentially gluing objects together.
messages.Tooltip.GlueVisualizer.Material: The material being used to visualize the area of effect of <mark=#ffffff10>Glue</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.GlueVisualizer.Scale: The scale field of the visual so it wobbles.
messages.Tooltip.GlueVisualizer.BaseScale: The default or base scale of the visual.
messages.Tooltip.GrabReferenceSet`1: The GrabReferenceSet can be used to write a reference to a field on grab and release.
messages.Tooltip.GrabReferenceSet`1.Target: The field in which to change on grab and release.
messages.Tooltip.GrabReferenceSet`1.GrabbedTarget: The value to set the contained data of the SyncRef referenced in <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> when the item is grabbed.
messages.Tooltip.GrabReferenceSet`1.ReleasedTarge: The value to set the contained data of the SyncRef referenced in <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> when the item is released.
messages.Tooltip.GrabReferenceSet`1.SetOnGrabbed: Whether to set the contained data of the SyncRef referenced in <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> when the item is grabbed.
messages.Tooltip.GrabReferenceSet`1.SetOnReleased: Whether to set the contained data of the SyncRef referenced in <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> when the item is released.
messages.Tooltip.GrabTransformReset: The GrabTransformReset component resets transforms of a grabbable on the same slot upon grab and/or release.
