messages.Tooltip.GrabTransformReset.ResetPosition: Whether to set the grabbable position to 0 on grab.
messages.Tooltip.GrabTransformReset.ResetRotation: Whether to set the grabbable rotation to 0 on grab.
messages.Tooltip.GrabTransformReset.ResetScaleOnG: Whether to set the grabbable scale to 1 on grab.
messages.Tooltip.GrabTransformReset.ResetPosition: Whether to set the grabbable position to 0 on release.
messages.Tooltip.GrabTransformReset.ResetRotation: Whether to set the grabbable rotation to 0 on release.
messages.Tooltip.GrabTransformReset.ResetScaleOnR: Whether to set the grabbable scale to 1 on release.
messages.Tooltip.GrabValueSet`1: The GrabValueSet component has options to set the value within a value field upon release and/or grabbing of a grabbable Component on the same slot.
messages.Tooltip.GrabValueSet`1.Target: The field to set the contained value of during grabbing events.
messages.Tooltip.GrabValueSet`1.GrabbedValue: The value to set the contained data of the Field referenced in <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> when the item is grabbed.
messages.Tooltip.GrabValueSet`1.ReleasedValue: The value to set the contained data of the Field referenced in <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> when the item is let go of.
messages.Tooltip.GrabValueSet`1.SetOnGrabbed: Whether to set the contained value of the field referenced by <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> on grabbed.
messages.Tooltip.GrabValueSet`1.SetOnReleased: Whether to set the contained value of the field referenced by <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> on letting go.
messages.Tooltip.Grabbable: The Grabbable component allows you to grab any slot it is attached to, provided it has some type of Collider.
messages.Tooltip.Grabbable.ReparentOnRelease: The slot is reparented to the value of _lastParent, instead of root when dropped *
messages.Tooltip.Grabbable.PreserveUserSpace: The slot is reparented to Local User Space *
messages.Tooltip.Grabbable.DestroyOnRelease: The slot this component is attached to is destroyed upon release
messages.Tooltip.Grabbable.GrabPriority: Determines what gets grabbed if several grabbable objects are touching someone's grab sphere. The IGrabbable with the highest priority will be grabbed first. If the highest priority ties with another IGrabbable, it will grab both of those and ignore any lower priority IGrabbables.
messages.Tooltip.Grabbable.GrabPriorityWhenGrabbe: Optionally changes the grab priority of the object when it is already being grabbed. Useful for different behavior when it can be grab stolen by another player.
messages.Tooltip.Grabbable.CustomCanGrabCheck: Not Usable inside Resonite Requires a mod for interacting with Sync Delegates
messages.Tooltip.Grabbable.EditModeOnly: Determines if this grabbable is effective only in Edit Mode
messages.Tooltip.Grabbable.AllowSteal: Other users can grab the slot this component is attached to. Like a flag in capture the flag.
messages.Tooltip.Grabbable.DropOnDisable: The parent slot will be dropped when this component is disabled.
messages.Tooltip.Grabbable.ActiveUserFilter: Changes if this component can be grabbed based on who is the active user if any.
messages.Tooltip.Grabbable.OnlyUsers: A list of users that are allowed to grab this slot.
messages.Tooltip.Grabbable.Scalable: Scalable determines if the grabbable's slot can be scaled.
messages.Tooltip.Grabbable.Receivable: Receivable determines if a grabbable can be dropped onto a GrabbableReceiverSurface when let go of. The receivable has to be close enough for the receiver to receive it.
messages.Tooltip.Grabbable.AllowOnlyPhysicalGrab: Only allow grab an object with a physical interaction - remote grabs are not allowed
messages.Tooltip.Grabbable._grabber: Automatically Assigned, the grabber that is grabbing this component.
messages.Tooltip.Grabbable._lastParent: Automatically Assigned, the last parent this slot had. Is used to parent the slot back (if allowed) when let go of.
messages.Tooltip.Grabbable._lastParentIsUserSpace: Automatically Assigned Whether the last parent slot is the user space slot of the user grabbing this.
messages.Tooltip.Grabbable.__legacyActiveUserRoot: Automatically Assigned Legacy do not use. Used to handle whether only the active user can grab. Use ActiveUserFilter instead!
messages.Tooltip.GrabbableAvatarPoseFilter: The GrabbableAvatarPoseFilter Component is used to apply pose filters to the user's hand body node that grabbed the Type:IGrabbable on the same Slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbableAvatarPoseFilter.Filter: The filter to apply when a user grabs the Type:IGrabbable this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbableAvatarPoseFilter.Priori: Whether this pose filter should be applied before other pose filters currently affecting the user.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbablePermissions: The GrabbablePermissions component is used to control what roles can pick up what items.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbablePermissions.Tags: A tag filter to filter what tags can or can't be grabbed by the selected roles.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbablePermissions.ValidateTyp: how to use the <mark=#ffffff10>ValidateTypes</mark> list for the selected roles.
A list of C# types that will have <mark=#ffffff10>ValidateTypeListMode</mark> applied to them.
messages.Tooltip.GrabInstancer: The GrabInstancer component spawns a new copy of Template and attaches it to the user's hand whenever the slot it is attached to is grabbed.
messages.Tooltip.GrabInstancerBase.Template: The slot that gets duplicated and grabbed when the GrabInstancer is grabbed. (Like a figurine)
messages.Tooltip.GrabInstancerBase.ContainerTempl: The slot to duplicate and act as a container for the grabbed object (Like a box for a figurine)
The override on where to place the duplicated grabbed object (Where to put the figurine in the box hiearchy).
messages.Tooltip.GrabInstancerBase.ActivateRoot: Whether or not the GrabInstancer should activate the newly duplicated slot.
messages.Tooltip.GrabInstancerBase.EnableGrabbabl: Whether or not the GrabInstancer should activate any Grabbable component on the newly duplicated slot.
messages.Tooltip.GrabInstancerBase.SetInstancePer: Whether to set the duplicated object's persistence field, and what to set it to.
messages.Tooltip.GrabInstancerBase.ExcludedParts: slots will be skipped while duplicating
messages.Tooltip.GrabInstancer.Physical: If true, the GrabInstancer only reacts to physical grab.
messages.Tooltip.GrabInstancer.GrabPriority: Determines what gets grabbed if several grabbable objects are touching someone's grab sphere. The IGrabbable with the highest priority will be grabbed first. If the highest priority ties with another IGrabbable, it will grab both of those and ignore any lower priority IGrabbables.
messages.Tooltip.Snapper: The Snapper component allows a slot to parent to Snap Target slots.
messages.Tooltip.Snapper.UseBoundingBoxCenter: Causes the Snapper to use the center of its Bounding Box rather than its Origin Point.
messages.Tooltip.Snapper.CheckStaticColliders: Allows the  Snapper to look for colliders on the Snap Target's slot that are set to <mark=#ffffff10>Static</mark> in addition to colliders set to <mark=#ffffff10>Trigger</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.Snapper.SnapTargetWhitelist: List of  Snap Targets that the slot can attach to
messages.Tooltip.Snapper.Keywords: List of keywords to match against a corresponding  Snap Targets. Can have multiple keywords to snap to multiple  Snap Targets
messages.Tooltip.SnapTarget: The SnapTarget component allows a slot with a Snapper Component to attach to this slot.
messages.Tooltip.SnapTarget.SnapperWhitelist: List of Snappers that are allowed to attach to this component
messages.Tooltip.SnapTarget.SnapperKeywordWhiteli: List of keywords to match against a corresponding Snapper
messages.Tooltip.SnapTarget.MaximumSnapDistance: The maximum distance the Snapper can snap with the Target. This value drives the SphereCollider radius
messages.Tooltip.SnapTarget.AnimationTime: The time it takes for the Snapper to lerp to the Target
messages.Tooltip.SnapTarget._snapColliderRadius: The radius drive reference of the SphereCollider
messages.Tooltip.SnapTarget.proxySphere: This must refer to a SphereCollider on the same slot as the SnapTarget. Usually refers to the one generated automatically when the SnapTarget was added.
messages.Tooltip.FABRIK_Chain: FABRIK Chain is the core of Resonite's in house IK system
messages.Tooltip.Joint: The Joint component allows for a slot or object to rotate around a single point, in any direction, or around an axis. Common uses are doors, helmet tops, and dials.
messages.Tooltip.Draggable.EditModeOnly: Determines if this grabbable is effective only in Edit Mode
messages.Tooltip.Draggable.AllowSteal: Other users can grab the slot this component is attached to. Like a flag in capture the flag.
messages.Tooltip.Draggable.DropOnDisable: Controls whether the user drops the joint if the object has been disabled
messages.Tooltip.Draggable.DontDrive: Write the transforms every game tick, rather than driving it on the local machine and then sending the final value to the host.
messages.Tooltip.Draggable.AllowOnlyPhysicalGrab: Only allow grabbing the joint with a physical interaction - remote grabs are not allowed
messages.Tooltip.Draggable.ActiveUserFilter: How to allow or deny users from interacting with this component depending on the active user.
messages.Tooltip.Draggable.CustomGrabCheck: A sync delegate that controls whether this object can be grabbed in certain instances.
messages.Tooltip.Draggable._grabber: Automatically Assigned, the grabber that is grabbing this component.
messages.Tooltip.Draggable._holdSlot: The slot that is "holding" this slot, but not actually acting as this slider's parent.
messages.Tooltip.Draggable._pos: The current position field this is driving or influencing.
messages.Tooltip.Draggable._rot: The current rotation field this is driving or influencing.
messages.Tooltip.Draggable._scl: The current scale field this is driving or influencing.
messages.Tooltip.Draggable.__legacyActiveUserRoot: Automatically Assigned Legacy do not use. Used to handle whether only the active user can grab. Use ActiveUserFilter instead!
messages.Tooltip.Draggable.GrabPriority: Determines what gets grabbed if several grabbable objects are touching someone's grab sphere. The IGrabbable with the highest priority will be grabbed first. If the highest priority ties with another IGrabbable, it will grab both of those and ignore any lower priority IGrabbables.
messages.Tooltip.Joint.MaxSwing: Maximum amount the joint will Twist during rotation.
messages.Tooltip.Joint.MaxTwist: Maximum amount the joint will rotate in the given axis
messages.Tooltip.Joint.Axis: Determines which axis the joint rotates on. Is used like a vector.
messages.Tooltip.Joint.PositionTwistThresholdAngl: The angle at which twist will start affecting position of the slot via position twisting.
messages.Tooltip.Joint.VibrationAngle: The angle at which to trigger vibration.
messages.Tooltip.Joint.VibrationPreset: The kind and intensity of vibration to send to the grabbing controller.
messages.Tooltip.Joint.SnapIncrement: Snap the joint to a multiple of this angle during grabbing. Can be used to simulate "detents" on a rotary switch.
messages.Tooltip.Joint.SnapTime: The time it takes for the joint to lerp towards given <mark=#ffffff10>SnapOrientations</mark>. Higher the value the slower it takes
messages.Tooltip.Joint.SnapOnRelease: Controls whether the join snaps to given SnapOrientations
messages.Tooltip.Joint.SnapOrientations: A list of snap orientations that the joint can snap to.
messages.Tooltip.Joint.origRotation: The resting rotation of the joint.
messages.Tooltip.Joint.rotReference: The reference rotation, which is where the joint started.
messages.Tooltip.Joint.twistReference: The position to use for twist calculations
messages.Tooltip.Joint.usePositionTwist: Whether twist can influence the position of the joint.
messages.Tooltip.LabelContentDriver: The LabelContentDriver Component is used to handle driving and updating the visual of labels placed by a Labeler Tool.
messages.Tooltip.LabelContentDriver.AutoUpdate: Automatically update the visual per tick
messages.Tooltip.LabelContentDriver.Padding: How much padding this label should have from the label's text content.
messages.Tooltip.LabelContentDriver.BaseWidth: The width of the underline for the text attached to the label.
messages.Tooltip.LabelContentDriver.ContentOrient: How to orient the text content of this label.
messages.Tooltip.LabelContentDriver.OrientAtLocal: Whether to look at the local user (usually true)
messages.Tooltip.LabelContentDriver._contentRoot: The root of the text content for the label this is driving.
messages.Tooltip.LabelContentDriver._targetPoint: The target point of the line coming from the underline for the label this is driving.
messages.Tooltip.LabelContentDriver._contentRotat: The rotation of the content of the label this is driving.
The rotation field of the content.
messages.Tooltip.LabelContentDriver._labelPositio: The position field of the label.
messages.Tooltip.LabelContentDriver._labelWidth: The length field of the label underline.
messages.Tooltip.LabelContentDriver._lineWidth: The width field of the line for the label.
messages.Tooltip.LabelContentDriver._labelRotatio: The rotation field of the label.
messages.Tooltip.LabelContentDriver._pointPositio: The target point field for what the label is pointing to.
messages.Tooltip.LineSegment: The Line Segment component is used to create a line between two slots or two local points in space.
messages.Tooltip.LineSegment.Radius: The radius of the generated line
messages.Tooltip.LineSegment.Sides: How many sides along the radius circle this cylinder should have.
messages.Tooltip.LineSegment.Point0: Point 1 as a number in local space to place point 1 of the line visual.
messages.Tooltip.LineSegment.Point1: Point 2 as a number in local space to place point 2 of the line visual.
messages.Tooltip.LineSegment.Anchor0: Point 1 as a slot to place point 1 of the line visual.
messages.Tooltip.LineSegment.Anchor1: Point 2 as a slot to place point 2 of the line visual.
messages.Tooltip.LineSegment._collider: The collider for the line visual.
messages.Tooltip.LineSegment._offset: The field to drive for the position of the visual + collider slot.
messages.Tooltip.LineSegment._rotation: The field to drive for the rotation of the visual + collider slot.
messages.Tooltip.LineSegment._visualScale: The field to drive for the scale of the visual + collider slot.
messages.Tooltip.LineTransform: The LineTransform component is a transform driver component used to position an object between two points or slots.
messages.Tooltip.LineTransform.Point0: The point in local space to transform from.
messages.Tooltip.LineTransform.Point1: The point in local space to transform to.
messages.Tooltip.LineTransform.Point0Anchor: acts as a slot to offset <mark=#ffffff10>Point0</mark> in global space.
messages.Tooltip.LineTransform.Point1Anchor: acts as a slot to offset <mark=#ffffff10>Point1</mark> in global space.
messages.Tooltip.LineTransform.LinePositionType: How to calculate the position on the line using <mark=#ffffff10>LinePoint</mark> for the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.LineTransform.PlanePositionType: How to calculate the position on the line using <mark=#ffffff10>LinePoint</mark> for the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.LineTransform.LinePoint: Where on the line specified by <mark=#ffffff10>Point0</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>Point1</mark> in percentage or relative distance that the slot this component is on should be positioned.
messages.Tooltip.LineTransform.OffsetPoint: The offset from the point found by <mark=#ffffff10>LinePoint</mark> slot this component is on should be.
messages.Tooltip.LineTransform.RotationOffset: The rotation offset from looking along the line.
messages.Tooltip.LineTransform._position: drives the target field with the position found by <mark=#ffffff10>LinePoint</mark> plus <mark=#ffffff10>OffsetPoint</mark> rotated by <mark=#ffffff10>RotationOffset</mark> and "looking down the line rotation" around the point found by <mark=#ffffff10>LinePoint</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.LineTransform._rotation: drives the target field with <mark=#ffffff10>RotationOffset</mark> and "looking down the line rotation"
messages.Tooltip.LineTransform.AllowRepositioning: Allow grabbing of the slot this component is on to move the object and change <mark=#ffffff10>LinePoint</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ObjectGridAligner: The ObjectGridAligner component aligns objects into a grid of items with x number of items per row.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectGridAligner.LerpSpeed: How fast the items should move to align themselves in the specified grid arrangement.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectGridAligner.HorizontalAlig: How to align the items horizontally.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectGridAligner.VerticalAlignm: How to align the items vertically.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectGridAligner.RowAxis: The axis that items should be aligned on for the horizontal axis of the grid.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectGridAligner.ColumnAxis: The axis that items should be aligned on for the vertical axis of the grid.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectGridAligner.Items: A list of items who's positions should be driven to place them into a grid alignment using this component's calculations.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectRoot: The ObjectRoot component marks a slot as the root of an object, where an object is the slot itself plus all of its child slots, recursively.
messages.Tooltip.BoxPointGenerator: A generator for points arranged in a box with a given length, width, and height.
messages.Tooltip.BoxPointGenerator.Size: How big the box should be for generating points.
messages.Tooltip.BoxPointGenerator.Shell: Whether points should only be spawned on the surface of the box or not.
messages.Tooltip.CirclePointGenerator: A generator for points arranged in a circle with a given radius.
messages.Tooltip.CirclePointGenerator.Radius: The distance from center to outside that this point generator should make points.
messages.Tooltip.CirclePointGenerator.Shell: Generate the points for this generator only along the outside edge.
messages.Tooltip.PointGenerator: Generates points only at a single point which is the position of the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.SpherePointGenerator: A generator for points arranged in a sphere with a given radius. Used in Common Spawn Areas usually.
messages.Tooltip.SpherePointGenerator.Radius: How big the sphere should be for generating points in
messages.Tooltip.SpherePointGenerator.Shell: Whether or not to generate points on the outside edge rather than inside the sphere too.
messages.Tooltip.SquarePointGenerator: A generator for points arranged in a rectangle with a given length and width.
messages.Tooltip.SquarePointGenerator.Size: A flat rectangle size for generating points in
messages.Tooltip.SquarePointGenerator.Shell: Whether to spawn users on only the perimeter or not.
messages.Tooltip.PositionDeltaDriver: The PositionDeltaDriver component drives a field with a vector drawn from Origin to Target in global space transformed (optionally normalized) into space VectorSpace.
messages.Tooltip.PositionDeltaDriver.Origin: The Origin starting point for the vector, defaults to root if null.
messages.Tooltip.PositionDeltaDriver.Target: The target for the vector. Defaults to root if null.
messages.Tooltip.PositionDeltaDriver.VectorSpace: The coordinate space to calculate the vector result in for <mark=#ffffff10>Origin</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark>
messages.Tooltip.PositionDeltaDriver.Normalized: Whether the vector from <mark=#ffffff10>Origin</mark> to <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> in <mark=#ffffff10>VectorSpace</mark> should be normalized.
messages.Tooltip.PositionDeltaDriver.Vector: The field to drive with the vector from <mark=#ffffff10>Origin</mark> to <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> in the coordinate space <mark=#ffffff10>VectorSpace</mark>
messages.Tooltip.RandomObjectSpawner: The RandomObjectSpawner component selects a slot from the Templates list, and spawns it either at this component's parent (in the case of Spawn()), or at the point specified in an argument (in the case of SpawnAtPoint())
messages.Tooltip.RandomObjectSpawner.Templates: List of slots to be selected from, when a Trigger is invoked
messages.Tooltip.RandomObjectSpawner.SpawnSpace: The coordinate space in which the template item will be spawned.
messages.Tooltip.RaycastDriver: When placed on a slot, _positionDrive and _rotationDrive are automatically filled to the slot's position and rotation.
messages.Tooltip.RaycastDriver.IgnoreHierarchy: a hiearchy of slots which to ignore the colliders for
messages.Tooltip.RaycastDriver.FilterDistance: ignore hit detections before this distance.
messages.Tooltip.RaycastDriver.Origin: The slot to start the raycast from.
messages.Tooltip.RaycastDriver.Offset: The offset from <mark=#ffffff10>Origin</mark> before starting the raycast.
messages.Tooltip.RaycastDriver.Direction: The direction in <mark=#ffffff10>Origin</mark>'s local space to shoot in.
messages.Tooltip.RaycastDriver.MaxDistance: The maximum distance to raycast for.
messages.Tooltip.RaycastDriver.NoHitDistance: How far to raycast shoot for in meters till reporting no hit.
messages.Tooltip.RaycastDriver.Filter: A sync delegate that takes a collider and returns a bool. if a collider returns false from this sync delegate, it is ignored when raycasting for objects.
messages.Tooltip.RaycastDriver._positionDrive: The position field to drive. This does not actually affect the field that is driven, but if it is not filled, the component will not function.
messages.Tooltip.RaycastDriver._rotationDrive: The rotation field to drive. This does not actually affect the field that is driven, but if it is not filled, the rotation of the slot is not changed. Aligns itself to the normal of the surface this raycast hit, or <mark=#ffffff10>(0,0,0)</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.RemoteConnectionPointDriver: RemoteConnectionPointDriver allows driving local position, vector, orientation and size from a target Slot. It can be used with TubeWireMesh and StripeWireMesh to drive the other point from another slot.
messages.Tooltip.RemoteConnectionPointDriver.Filt: The threshold before recalculating the target values.
messages.Tooltip.RemoteConnectionPointDriver.Targ: The target vector as a local transform under <mark=#ffffff10>TargetPoint</mark>
The target scale as a local transform offset under <mark=#ffffff10>TargetPoint</mark>
The target rotation as a local transform offset under <mark=#ffffff10>TargetPoint</mark>
messages.Tooltip.RemoteConnectionPointDriver.Loca: The field to drive with the position of <mark=#ffffff10>TargetPoint</mark> with an offset of <mark=#ffffff10>TargetVector</mark> converted to this slot's local space.
The field to drive with a vector of <mark=#ffffff10>TargetVector</mark> in the transform space of <mark=#ffffff10>TargetPoint</mark> converted to this slot's local space.
The field to drive with the rotation of <mark=#ffffff10>TargetPoint</mark> with an offset of <mark=#ffffff10>TargetVector</mark> converted to this slot's local space.
The field to drive with the scale of <mark=#ffffff10>TargetPoint</mark> with an offset of <mark=#ffffff10>TargetSize</mark> converted to this slot's local space.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleTransition: The ScaleTransition component makes a slot scale from one scale state to another, disabling when fully reaching its disabled scale point. The component's scale transition state is controlled via ShowField.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleTransition.ShowField: Whether to be transitioning to hidden (false) or shown (true)
messages.Tooltip.ScaleTransition.TransitionTimeFi: How long the transition to shown or hidden should take.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleTransition.ShowScaleField: What scale the object should transition to from <mark=#ffffff10>HiddenScaleField</mark> when <mark=#ffffff10>ShowField</mark> is true.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleTransition.HiddenScaleField: What scale the object should transition to from <mark=#ffffff10>ShowScaleField</mark> when <mark=#ffffff10>ShowField</mark> is false.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleTransition.CurveField: How the object should transition scale over time.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleTransition._scaleDrive: The field to drive to make this slot scale up and down. Usually the slot's scale field.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleTransition._enabledDrive: The field to drive to false when the object is done scaling to hidden and drive to true when transitioning away from hidden.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleObject: The ScaleObject component can be used to position and scale a slot relative to a ScaleObjectManager.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleObjectCreator: Scale Object Creator component as seen in the Scene Inspector
messages.Tooltip.ScaleObjectManager: The ScaleObjectManager component is used to create a field of objects with different relative sizes. This is used most prominently in the "Size of the Universe Adventure" world.
messages.Tooltip.SearchBlock: The Search Block component, slots tagged with this component can be used to prevent either: * A user from grabbing an item from contacting certain colliders. * A user from equipping an avatar when clicking on a limb or collider attached to it. * A user from equipping a tool tip by clicking on a certain part of it. * A user from clicking a physical button when clicking on a collider parented to the physical button further down the hierarchy, or any button type not just physical buttons.
messages.Tooltip.SkinnedMeshPositioner: Attaches an object to a specific part of a SkinnedMesh that is affected by multiple bones and/or blendshapes
messages.Tooltip.SkinnedMeshPositioner.Skin: The skinned mesh renderer to attach this object to
messages.Tooltip.SkinnedMeshPositioner.TriangleIn: The index of the triangle in the mesh to attach this object to
messages.Tooltip.SkinnedMeshPositioner.Barycentri: How close the attachment point should be to each vertex of the triangle (barycentric coordinate system).
messages.Tooltip.SkinnedMeshPositioner.LocalPosit: An offset to apply to the position of the object
messages.Tooltip.SkinnedMeshPositioner.LocalRotat: An offset to apply to the rotation of the object
messages.Tooltip.SkinnedMeshPositioner.LocalScale: A multiplier to apply to the scale of the object
messages.Tooltip.SkinnedMeshPositioner.PositionDr: The position field of the slot to be driven with the position of the triangle in the SkinnedMeshRenderer
messages.Tooltip.SkinnedMeshPositioner.RotationDr: The rotation field of the slot to be driven with the rotation of the triangle in the SkinnedMeshRenderer
messages.Tooltip.SkinnedMeshPositioner.ScaleDrive: The scale field of the slot to be driven with the scale of the SkinnedMeshRenderer
messages.Tooltip.Slider: The Slider component allows for an object to be grabbed and moved. See Usage for more information.
messages.Tooltip.Slider.Rotatable: Whether the slider object is rotatable.
messages.Tooltip.Slider.Range: The range that the object is allowed to move position wise.
messages.Tooltip.Slider.Origin: The origin of the range that the object is allowed to move.
messages.Tooltip.Slider.MinScale: The minimum scale the object can be scaled to.
messages.Tooltip.Slider.MaxScale: The maximum scale the object can be scaled to.
messages.Tooltip.Slider.VibrationOffset: The offset of the vibration intensity.
messages.Tooltip.Slider.VibrationPreset: The preset for triggering vibration with this object.
messages.Tooltip.Slider.SnapIncrement: What increment the position of this object should snap to, instead of allowing smooth movement.
messages.Tooltip.Slider.SnapTime: The time the object will take to move to a snap position when it snaps.
messages.Tooltip.Slider.SnapOnRelease: Only snap to positions in <mark=#ffffff10>SnapPositions</mark> when released rather than always.
messages.Tooltip.Slider.SnapPositions: Positions to snap to while grabbing or when released.
messages.Tooltip.Slider.posOffset: How much to offset the slider's position.
messages.Tooltip.Slider.rotOffset: How much to offset the slider's rotation.
messages.Tooltip.Slider.referenceParent: The slot that is supposed to be this slider's parent.
messages.Tooltip.SmoothTransform: Smooth transform is a component that is used to create a "lag behind" or "trailing" effect on slots or objects that follow another object. The slot this controls will try to return to it's rest position, iteratively getting closer and closer until in eventuality reaching it's target location at rest.
messages.Tooltip.SmoothTransform.InterpolationSpa: The coordinate space that should be used to check the delta of the transforms in. If set to user space and parented under the user's hand, this slot will only lag behind (or smooth) when the user moves their hand, but not when they walk around. This can also be set to a slot for similar effects without a user.
messages.Tooltip.SmoothTransform.TargetPosition: The local transform position that this slot is trying to get to.
messages.Tooltip.SmoothTransform.TargetRotation: The local transform rotation that this slot is trying to get to.
messages.Tooltip.SmoothTransform.TargetScale: The local transform scale that this slot is trying to get to.
messages.Tooltip.SmoothTransform.Position: The position field to drive with the smoothed value (auto filled with the position field of the slot this is on).
messages.Tooltip.SmoothTransform.Rotation: The rotation field to drive with the smoothed value (auto filled with the rotation field of the slot this is on).
messages.Tooltip.SmoothTransform.Scale: The scale field to drive with the smoothed value (auto filled with the scale field of the slot this is on).
messages.Tooltip.SmoothTransform.SmoothSpeed: How fast or slow this slot is at catching up to its target location. Higher numbers are faster.
messages.Tooltip.SmoothTransform._updateIndex: Whether to do this smooth transform before or after other smooth transforms in the same slot hiearchy.
messages.Tooltip.SmoothValue`1: The SmoothValue component smoothly interpolates a given value towards a target value at a set speed. To do this it drives the value it is interpolating.
messages.Tooltip.SmoothValue`1.TargetValue: The value that is being interpolated towards
messages.Tooltip.SmoothValue`1.Speed: The speed at which it is interpolated
messages.Tooltip.SmoothValue`1.WriteBack: If WriteBack is enabled, writing to the driven value will also set TargetValue. If not, the driven value cannot be written to. See write backs.
messages.Tooltip.SmoothValue`1.Value: The value that is being driven
messages.Tooltip.StringConcatenationDriver: The StringConcatenationDriver component constructs a string from the inputted list of strings.
messages.Tooltip.StringConcatenationDriver.Target: The field to drive with the resulting completed string.
messages.Tooltip.StringConcatenationDriver.Separa: What text to put between each pair of strings when building the string. This can be something like a comma, a space, or anything else the user desires.
messages.Tooltip.StringConcatenationDriver.String: A list of strings to join together separated by <mark=#ffffff10>Seperator</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.StringConcatenationDriver.NullOu: Whether to null the entire string when all <mark=#ffffff10>Strings</mark> fields are null.
messages.Tooltip.TeleportBlock: The Teleport Block component is a component that is used to Tag colliders like platforms and walls as a place where a user cannot teleport to via the Teleport Locomotion Mode.
messages.Tooltip.TeleportPassthrough: The Teleport Passthrough component makes a collider on the same slot tagged with this component act as if the collider doesn't exist to the teleport selector when teleporting via the Teleport Locomotion Mode.
messages.Tooltip.TeleportSurface: Attach this component to the same slot as a collider to mark that collider as a spot a user can specifically teleport to.
messages.Tooltip.ToolAvatarPoseFilter: The ToolAvatarPoseFilter component is used to assign a Pose Filter to the hand you have a Tool equipped on.
messages.Tooltip.ToolAvatarPoseFilter.Priority: Whether this pose filter should be applied before other filters.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver: The UnwrappableBoxDriver component makes a 6 sided cube made of individual quads that unwrap themselves into a flat area like a paper box.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver.SideSize: The size of each side (a default Quad is 1)
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver.ScaleConten: Whether to scale the side slots and align them to fit as <mark=#ffffff10>SideSize</mark>'s value increases.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver._side0rotat: The rotation field of side 0 of the box.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver._side1rotat: The rotation field of side 1 of the box.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver._side2rotat: The rotation field of side 2 of the box.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver._side3rotat: The rotation field of side 3 of the box.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver._topRotatio: The rotation field of the top side of the box.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver._side0offse: The position field of side 0 of the box.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver._side1offse: The position field of side 1 of the box.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver._side2offse: The position field of side 2 of the box.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver._side3offse: The position field of side 3 of the box.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver._topOffset: The position field of the top side of the box.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver._side0conte: The position field of the content of side 0 of the box.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver._side1conte: The position field of the content of side 1 of the box.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver._side2conte: The position field of the content of side 2 of the box.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver._side3conte: The position field of the content of side 3 of the box.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver._topContent: The position field of the content of the top side of the box.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver._side0conte: The scale field of the content of side 0 of the box.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver._side1conte: The scale field of the content of side 1 of the box.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver._side2conte: The scale field of the content of side 2 of the box.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver._side3conte: The scale field of the content of side 3 of the box.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver._bottomCont: The scale field of the content of the bottom side of the box.
messages.Tooltip.UnwrappableBoxDriver._topContent: The scale field of the content of the top side of the box.
messages.Tooltip.UserInterfacePositioner: The UserInterfacePositioner component causes the slot to which it is attached to track the user's position and rotation (optionally head). This is a somewhat nicer and cleaner alternative than parenting a slot directly to a user's avatar node.
messages.Tooltip.UserInterfacePositioner.UseHead: Whether to track to the user's head.
messages.Tooltip.UserInterfacePositioner.RotateVe: Whether to act like the freeform dash or not.
messages.Tooltip.UserInterfacePositioner.Position: The speed at which the object will track the user's position.
messages.Tooltip.UserInterfacePositioner.Rotation: The speed at which the object will track the user's rotation.
messages.Tooltip.UserInterfacePositioner.Activati: The distance from the user to the object at which the object will start tracking the user's position and rotation.  Zero means "always track".
The angle between the user and the object at which the object will start tracking the user's position and rotation. Zero means "always track".
messages.Tooltip.UserInterfacePositioner.Deactiva: How far away the user has to be from the component for it to to stop following.
How much of an angle deviation it has to be for it to stop following.
messages.Tooltip.UserInterfacePositioner.TargetPo: The target position this UI is lerping to.
messages.Tooltip.UserInterfacePositioner.TargetRo: The target rotation this UI is lerping to.
messages.Tooltip.UserInterfacePositioner._positio: The field to drive to set the position of this UI.
messages.Tooltip.UserInterfacePositioner._rotatio: The field to drive to set the rotation of this UI.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualParent: The Virtual Parent Component is used to make a Slot act as the child of another Slot without needing to be in the normal hierarchy order. This can be useful when you need it to respond in a certain way using transforms of the overriding parent.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualParent.OverrideParent: If not null, the slot that the object gets reparented to
messages.Tooltip.VirtualParent._targetPos: Position to be driven by parent's position.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualParent._targetRot: Rotation to be driven by parent's rotation.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualParent._targetScl: Scale to be driven by parent's scale.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonHoverRelay: This can send a signal internally.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonHoverRelay.OnHoverEnter: Sends a signal when hovering begins.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonHoverRelay.OnHoverLeave: Sends a signal when hovering ends.
messages.Tooltip.NestedCanvas: This component is used mainly for consistency with other canvas components, no matter where in the world hierarchy.
messages.Tooltip.NestedCanvas.TargetCanvas: The target canvas to nest as
messages.Tooltip.NestedCanvas._renderOffset: The render offset reference from the target canvas to be applied to the new nested canvas
messages.Tooltip.NestedCanvas._maskDepth: The mask depth reference from the target canvas to be applied to the new nested canvas
messages.Tooltip.ModalOverlayManager: The ModalOverlayManager component allows for the creation of ModalOverlay slots. Using the Template field to create Modal Overlays duplicates.
messages.Tooltip.ModalOverlayManager.Constructor: The constructor to make the ModalOverlay.
messages.Tooltip.ModalOverlayManager.SpawnRoot: The slot hierarchy to spawn this modal overlay on.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantModalOverlay: The RadiantModalOverlay component is similar to the ModalOverlay, but with the dash menu. This also can be duplicated as a template in the ModalOverlayManager component.
messages.Tooltip.ModalOverlay.ShowLerp: The lerp amount for this modal.
messages.Tooltip.ModalOverlay.AnimationTime: The lerp time for this modal.
messages.Tooltip.ModalOverlay.ContentRoot: The center root of this modal.
messages.Tooltip.ModalOverlay.CloseOnContextMenuA: Closes the modal when the user's context menu closes.
messages.Tooltip.ModalOverlay.CloseOnClick: Closes this modal when it is clicked on.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantModalOverlay.BackgroundCo: The background color of this RadiantModalOverlay.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantModalOverlay.ContentAnima: Offests the content animation for this RadiantModalOverlay.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantModalOverlay.HeaderSize: The header size of this RadiantModalOverlay.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantModalOverlay.Padding: The padding amount for this RadiantModalOverlay.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantModalOverlay._blurGraphic: The blur graphic of this RadiantModalOverlay.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantModalOverlay._blurSpread: The blur speed of this RadiantModalOverlay.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantModalOverlay._backgroundC: The background color of this RadiantModalOverlay.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantModalOverlay._maskRect: The mask rect of this RadiantModalOverlay.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantModalOverlay._headerOffse: The header offset min of this RadiantModalOverlay.
The header offset max of this RadiantModalOverlay.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantModalOverlay._contentOffs: The content offset min of this RadiantModalOverlay.
The content offset max of this RadiantModalOverlay.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantModalOverlay._titleOffset: The title offset min of this RadiantModalOverlay.
The title offset max of this RadiantModalOverlay.
messages.Tooltip.RadiantModalOverlay._closeOffset: The close offset min of this RadiantModalOverlay.
The close offset max of this RadiantModalOverlay.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioConfiguration: Avatar Audio Configuration is a component that allows for overriding Avatar Audio Output Manager voice configs in worlds using user injection.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioConfiguration.Normal: The settings to override <mark=#ffffff10>NormalConfig</mark> on an Avatar Audio Output Manager with.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioConfiguration.Shout: The settings to override <mark=#ffffff10>ShoutConfig</mark> on an Avatar Audio Output Manager with.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioConfiguration.Broadca: The settings to override <mark=#ffffff10>BroadcastConfig</mark> on an Avatar Audio Output Manager with.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarFacetAnchor: Avatar Facet Anchor is a component that is used to position a userspace facet anchor to the slot this is on.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarFacetAnchor.FacetAnchorPoi: Which facet anchor should be positioned at the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarFacetAnchor.OverrideState: Whether to force this anchor to stay on or off.
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource: The MockupMouthTrackingSource component can be used to simulate the tracking data of a Mouth Tracker.
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.Tongue: The tongue roll into a burrito shape.
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.LipUpp: The upper left lip raise amount.
The upper right lip raise amount.
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.LipLow: The lower left lip raise amount.
The lower right lip raise amount.
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.LipUpp: The shift side to side of the upper lip.
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.LipLow: The shift side to side of the lower lip. (negative to positive 1)
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.MouthL: The left frown/smile amount. (negative to positive 1)
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.MouthR: The right frown/smile amount. (negative to positive 1)
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.MouthL: push the area closest to the corners of the lips inwards on the left.
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.MouthR: push the area closest to the corners of the lips inwards on the right.
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.MouthP: The mouth kissy amount on the left.
The mouth kissy amount on the right.
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.LipTop: The amount The top lip is flipping upwards on the left.
The amount The top lip is flipping upwards on the right.
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.LipBot: The amount The top lip is flipping upwards
how much the bottom right side of the lips is pushing outwards like duck lips.
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.LipTop: The amount the top lip is going down to cover the bottom lip.
How much the bottom right part of the lips is going behind the top right lip.
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.LipBot: The amount the bottom lip is going up to cover the top lip left.
The amount the bottom lip is going up to cover the top lip right.
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.LipLef: The amount the left side of the lips are pulling towards the outside.
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.LipRig: The amount the right side of the lips are pulling towards the outside.
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.LipsLe: The amount the left side of the lips are pulling towards the inside.
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.LipsRi: The amount the right side of the lips are pulling towards the inside.
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.CheekL: The amount the left cheek is sucking inwards or being puffed out. (negative to positive 1)
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.CheekR: The amount the right cheek is sucking inwards or being puffed out. (negative to positive 1)
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.CheekL: How much the cheek area of the left side of the face is being pushed upwards (Usually caused by smile) (negative to positive one)
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.CheekR: How much the cheek area of the right side of the face is being pushed upwards (Usually caused by smile) (negative to positive one)
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.NoseWr: How much the left side of the nose is being pushed up.
How much the right side of the nose is being pushed up.
messages.Tooltip.MockupMouthTrackingSource.ChinRa: How much the bottom chin is being pulled up.
How much the top chin is being pulled up.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarNameplateVisibilityDriver: AvatarNameplateVisibilityDriver is used to drive the visibility of nameplates during certain scenarios.
Show to contacts regardless of whether it should be hidden or not.
The boolean to drive for nameplate visiblity (like the active of the nameplate slot)
messages.Tooltip.VoicePermission: The VoicePermission component changes the allowed voice modes for users with the selected roles in a world.
messages.Tooltip.VoicePermission.MaxAllowedVoiceM: The maximum allowed voice mode allowed for the selected roles.
messages.Tooltip.VoicePermission.AllowChangeOther: Whether the selected roles are allowed to change the voice mode of other users mainly through the User Inspector.
messages.Tooltip.CommonAvatarBuilder: Common Avatar Builder is a component that is part of every world that defines the spawning behavior and routines to do for all users when they spawn into a world. This can be used to add world settings menus, change what parts of a user get automatically set up, and so on.
messages.Tooltip.CommonAvatarBuilder.LoadCloudAva: Whether to load the User's set avatar, or load the default avatar instead.
messages.Tooltip.CommonAvatarBuilder.CustomAvatar: Avatars that user's will spawn into if they don't have a cloud avatar, or optionally force them into one of these templates.
messages.Tooltip.CommonAvatarBuilder.AutoInject: A slot that will automatically be duplicated and added as a child of a user when they join the world. Certain components will use this to override user behavior when part of this slot's hierarchy, like Avatar Audio Configuration to name one.
messages.Tooltip.CommonAvatarBuilder.SetupNameBad: Whether to set up the user's default name badge when they spawn
messages.Tooltip.CommonAvatarBuilder.SetupIconBad: Whether to set up the user's default badges when they spawn.
messages.Tooltip.CommonAvatarBuilder.SetupLocomot: Whether to give the user the default locomotion modules when they spawn.
messages.Tooltip.CommonAvatarBuilder.AllowLocomot: Whether to allow the user to move using locomotion when they spawn.
messages.Tooltip.CommonAvatarBuilder.DefaultHostS: Whether the user should be default muted to the host when they spawn.
messages.Tooltip.CommonAvatarBuilder.DefaultClien: Whether the user should be default muted on their end when they spawn.
messages.Tooltip.CommonAvatarBuilder.LocomotionMo: The slot full of locomotion modules that the user will have by default after they spawn.
messages.Tooltip.CommonAvatarBuilder.ForceDefault: The locomotion to enable for the user by default when spawning rather than the one in that user's locomotion settings set in their dash.
messages.Tooltip.CommonAvatarBuilder.FindUserPref: Whether to try to spawn the user into their preferred locomotion module they set in settings.
messages.Tooltip.CommonAvatarBuilder.SetupItemShe: Whether to add item shelves to the user when they spawn.
messages.Tooltip.CommonAvatarBuilder.ParentClient: Parents users that join under the host user's slot (yes this is what it does)
messages.Tooltip.CommonAvatarBuilder.AlignTrackin: used in Kiosk mode in dash space to show Debug tracking visuals
messages.Tooltip.CommonAvatarBuilder.EmptyAvatarS: Uses this component to handle empty slots like head and hands on a spawning avatar.
messages.Tooltip.CommonAvatarBuilder.FillEmptySlo: Whether to fill empty AvatarObjectSlots with default head and hand objects if they are missing on their loaded avatar.
messages.Tooltip.CommonSpawnArea: alt=A photo of the common spawn area component and it's fields in the inspector.|thumb|The CommonSpawnArea component as seen in the Scene Inspector
messages.Tooltip.CommonSpawnArea.SpawnPointGenera: A Point Generator Type component. This determines which points the spawn area will use for spawning new users.
messages.Tooltip.CommonSpawnArea.FloorPointRay: Shoots a ray from the slot containing this component and trys to detect a floor
messages.Tooltip.CommonSpawnArea.OtherUserCheckRa: A minimum of how far away other users must be before a spawn point generated by SpawnPointGenerator is valid.
messages.Tooltip.CommonSpawnArea.ParentUser: If true, newly-spawned users will be parented to the slot holding this component.
messages.Tooltip.CommonSpawnArea.OrientUser: If true, newly-spawned users will be oriented towards the Z- axis that the slot holding this component is facing.
messages.Tooltip.CommonSpawnArea.ScaleUser: If true, newly-spawned users will be scaled to be the same scale as the slot holding this component. This can cause issues with parenting, so take care.
messages.Tooltip.CommonSpawnArea.Capacity: The maximum number of users that can be spawned in the slot holding this component at any given time. A value of -1 makes the maximum unlimited.
messages.Tooltip.CommonSpawnArea.BaseWeight: The chance this spawner will be used to spawn users. The higher the number, the more likely the chance.
messages.Tooltip.CommonSpawnArea.PositionNode: Positions the user based on this body node.
messages.Tooltip.CommonSpawnArea.RotationNode: Rotates the user based on this body node.
messages.Tooltip.CommonSpawnArea.ParentOverride: Where to place users instead of under this component's Slot.
messages.Tooltip.EyeTrackingStreamManager.User: The focused user to get streaming data from.
messages.Tooltip.EyeTrackingStreamManager.Converg: The eye distance data from the headset.
messages.Tooltip.EyeTrackingStreamManager.IsEyeTr: Returns true if the eyes are being tracked from the headset.
messages.Tooltip.EyeTrackingStreamManager.LeftEye: A set of fields for the left eye.
messages.Tooltip.EyeTrackingStreamManager.RightEy: A set of fields for the right eye.
messages.Tooltip.FingerPoseStreamManager.User: The referenced user to get tracking data from.
messages.Tooltip.FingerPoseStreamManager.LeftIsTr: The stream data for the left hand.
messages.Tooltip.FingerPoseStreamManager.RightIsT: The stream data for the right hand.
messages.Tooltip.FingerPoseStreamManager.Stream: The stream data from all the tracking.
messages.Tooltip.FingerPoseStreamManager.PoseCont: The pose controller from the user.
messages.Tooltip.MouthTrackingStreamManager.User: The referenced user to get the streamed data.
messages.Tooltip.MouthTrackingStreamManager.Jaw: The streamed data for the Jaw.
messages.Tooltip.MouthTrackingStreamManager.JawOp: The streamed data for the Jaw opening.
messages.Tooltip.MouthTrackingStreamManager.Tongu: The streamed data for the tongue.
The streamed data for the tongue rolling.
messages.Tooltip.MouthTrackingStreamManager.LipUp: The streamed data for the lip (upper-left raised).
The streamed data for the lip (upper-right raised).
messages.Tooltip.MouthTrackingStreamManager.LipLo: The streamed data for the lip (lower-left raised).
The streamed data for the lip (lower-right raised).
messages.Tooltip.MouthTrackingStreamManager.LipUp: The streamed data for the lip (upper-horizontal).
messages.Tooltip.MouthTrackingStreamManager.LipLo: The streamed data for the lip (lower-horizontal).
messages.Tooltip.MouthTrackingStreamManager.Mouth: The streamed data for the mouth (left frown).
The streamed data for the mouth (right frown).
The stream for pushing the area closest to the corners of the lips inwards on the left.
The stream for pushing the area closest to the corners of the lips inwards on the right.
The stream for the mouth kissy amount on the left.
The stream for the mouth kissy amount on the right.
messages.Tooltip.MouthTrackingStreamManager.LipTo: The stream for the amount The top lip is flipping outwards on the left.
The stream for the amount The top lip is flipping outwards on the right.
messages.Tooltip.MouthTrackingStreamManager.LipBo: The stream for the amount The bottom lip is flipping outwards on the left.
The stream for The amount The bottom lip is flipping outwards on the right.
messages.Tooltip.MouthTrackingStreamManager.LipTo: The stream for the amount the top lip is going up to cover the bottom lip left.
The stream for the amount the top lip is going up to cover the bottom lip right.
messages.Tooltip.MouthTrackingStreamManager.LipBo: The stream for the amount the bottom lip is going up to cover the top lip left.
The stream for the amount the bottom lip is going up to cover the top lip right.
messages.Tooltip.MouthTrackingStreamManager.LipLe: The stream for the amount the left side of the lips are pulling towards the outside.
messages.Tooltip.MouthTrackingStreamManager.LipRi: The stream for the amount the right side of the lips are pulling towards the outside.
messages.Tooltip.MouthTrackingStreamManager.LipsL: The stream for the amount the left side of the lips are pulling towards the inside.
messages.Tooltip.MouthTrackingStreamManager.LipsR: The stream for the amount the right side of the lips are pulling towards the inside.
messages.Tooltip.MouthTrackingStreamManager.Cheek: The streamed data for the cheek (left puff).
The streamed data for the cheek (right puff).
The stream for how much the cheek area of the left side of the face is being pushed upwards (Usually caused by smile) (negative to positive one)
The stream for how much the cheek area of the right side of the face is being pushed upwards (Usually caused by smile) (negative to positive one)
messages.Tooltip.MouthTrackingStreamManager.NoseW: The stream for how much the left side of the nose is being pushed up.
The stream for how much the right side of the nose is being pushed up.
messages.Tooltip.MouthTrackingStreamManager.ChinR: The stream for how much the bottom chin is being pulled up.
The stream for how much the top chin is being pulled up.
messages.Tooltip.SimpleAwayIndicator: This is used to make a customized or fancier way of stating that you are focused away into another world.
messages.Tooltip.SimpleAwayIndicator.User: User whose away state will be watched. On avatars, may be set by an AvatarUserReferenceAssigner component.
messages.Tooltip.SimpleAwayIndicator.AwayMaterial: Material to display when the user is away.
messages.Tooltip.SimpleAwayIndicator.Renderer: Mesh renderer component whose materials should be replaced
messages.Tooltip.SimpleAwayIndicator._oldMaterial: Backup of the renderer's original material list. Written automatically when "Away" state is triggered.
messages.Tooltip.SimpleUserSpawn: A SimpleUserSpawn defines a simple spawn point.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnArc: Used to help shape how you want users to spawn in a world.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnArc.Weight: The likelihood of this spawner being chosen for a spawning user.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnArc.Capacity: How many users this spawner can have spawned at a time.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnArc.Radius: The range of how far this spawner can spawn this user.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnArc.Arc: The shape of how the spawner will place the user.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnArc.UsersPerArc: The segment area for the users to spawn at in this arc.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnArc.CenterArcOffset: The offset rotation of the center of the arc circle for this spawner.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnArc.GrowBothSides: The option to extend both sides for the spawn arc, fanning out from a center point.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnArc.RowHeightOffset: The hieght where the users will spawn along the circle.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnArc.OrientUser: Positions and rotates the user towards the center.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnArc.ParentUser: Keeps the user parented onto this specific spawner slot.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnArc.TiltUsers: Tilts the user when spawning in.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnArc.PositionNode: Takes the position of the user's body node for spawning.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnArc.RotationNode: Takes the rotation of the user's body node for spawning.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnArc._testSlots: Internal: Shows the number of test slots on this spawning arc.
messages.Tooltip.TrackedDevicePositioner: The TrackedDevicePositioner component is used to get information from the user's input system to get positioning data for tracked objects like headset, hands, and full body trackers.
messages.Tooltip.TrackedDevicePositioner.DeviceIn: The input Device by index to track.
messages.Tooltip.TrackedDevicePositioner.Correspo: The body node of the found input Device tracker.
messages.Tooltip.TrackedDevicePositioner.AutoBody: If present, use this to find the input Device to track rather than <mark=#ffffff10>DeviceIndex</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.TrackedDevicePositioner.AlwaysRe: Whether to always render the tracker Debug model regardless of if its mapped to a 3d model.
messages.Tooltip.TrackedDevicePositioner.Referenc: The slot of the debug model visual.
messages.Tooltip.TrackedDevicePositioner.BodyNode: acts as an offset for the actual position of a Tracked object.
messages.Tooltip.TrackedDevicePositioner.ObjectSl: The avatar object slot this component is driving.
messages.Tooltip.TrackedDevicePositioner.IsTracki: Whether the input Device is tracking or lost its tracking.
messages.Tooltip.TrackedDevicePositioner.IsActive: Whether the input Device is enabled.
messages.Tooltip.TrackedDevicePositioner.IsSimula: Whether the device is being simulated instead by the locomotion animation system.
messages.Tooltip.TrackedDevicePositioner.CreateAv: Whether to create an avatar object slot component on a slot named "BodyNode" under this component slot if <mark=#ffffff10>BodyNodeRoot</mark> is null.
messages.Tooltip.TrackedDevicePositioner.PoseFilt: The pose controller for this component to filter its position and movement based on if this component should be filtered.
messages.Tooltip.TrackerController: The TrackerController component is an internally used component that triggers a pair of sync Delegates when a device is added to the user's list of tracked devices with position and rotation.
messages.Tooltip.TrackerController.PositionStream: The Sync delegate to call with the created tracked device's position stream and "2" as a number when a device is added.
messages.Tooltip.TrackerController.RotationStream: The Sync delegate to call with the created tracked device's rotation stream and "2" as a number when a device is added.
messages.Tooltip.TrackingOffsetCompensator: The TrackingOffsetCompensator counteracts changes to tracking offset to all trackers at once, so this one doesn't move when all others do. usually due to a tracking offset component or seated mode.
messages.Tooltip.TransformStreamDriver: The TransformStreamDriver is used to convert a stream of position rotation and scale values into drives which can drive slot transforms. It is used internally by the user positioning system.
messages.Tooltip.TransformStreamDriver.PositionSt: The source of float3 values used to position the slot.
messages.Tooltip.TransformStreamDriver.RotationSt: The source of floatQ values used to rotate the slot.
messages.Tooltip.TransformStreamDriver.ScaleStrea: The source of float3 values used to scale the slot.
messages.Tooltip.TransformStreamDriver.RootSpaceS: The slot being used for the root space.
messages.Tooltip.TransformStreamDriver.Position: The field to drive with the streamed in position values.
messages.Tooltip.TransformStreamDriver.Rotation: The field to drive with the streamed in rotation values.
messages.Tooltip.TransformStreamDriver.Scale: The field to drive with the streamed in scale values.
messages.Tooltip.TransformStreamDriver.AllowOffse: Whether to allow offsets to the affected slot/object.
messages.Tooltip.TransformStreamDriver.ResetStrea: Whether to reset the incoming streams upon destroying this slot.
messages.Tooltip.TransformStreamDriver.PositionOf: Offset to the resulting position value this is driving.
messages.Tooltip.TransformStreamDriver.RotationOf: Offset to the resulting rotation value this is driving.
messages.Tooltip.TransformStreamDriver.ScaleOffse: Offset to the resulting scale value this is driving.
messages.Tooltip.TransformStreamDriver.RunBeforeP: Whether or not to run before physics. can fix some issues where users fly off of a platform being driven by this component.
messages.Tooltip.UserAudioStream`1: The UserAudioStream component writes Audio from a user Audio device to a specified stream. The audio device used is set internally and such methods are not accessible to the user.
messages.Tooltip.UserAudioStream`1.Stream: The stream to write audio to.
messages.Tooltip.UserAudioStream`1.UseFilteredDat: Whether to filter the audio data being written to <mark=#ffffff10>Stream</mark> from the user.
messages.Tooltip.UserInfo: The UserInfo component handles keeping certain bits of data about a player spawned physically in the world. It's stored under the user root slot.
messages.Tooltip.UserInfo.Controllers: The slots of the controllers this user is using.
messages.Tooltip.UserInfo.Hands: The slots of the hands this user.
messages.Tooltip.UserJoinAudioIndicator: Attach to a slot and provide some audio clips to play.
messages.Tooltip.UserJoinAudioIndicator.JoinedCli: A list of random audio clips to choose from when a user joins the session. Each one will have a different weight.
messages.Tooltip.UserJoinAudioIndicator.LeftClips: A list of random audio clips to choose from when a user leaves the session. Each one will have a different weight.
messages.Tooltip.UserJoinAudioIndicator.Spatializ: Whether to play the sounds from the slot this component is on or globally.
messages.Tooltip.UserJoinAudioIndicator.Volume: How loud the audio clips should be by default.
messages.Tooltip.UserPoseController: The UserPoseController component is used to control the posing of the user.
messages.Tooltip.UserPoseController.RenderDebugVi: Whether or not to render the debug visuals.
messages.Tooltip.UserPoseController.PauseLocomoti: Whether or not to pause the current locomotion animation.
messages.Tooltip.UserPoseController.OverrideLocom: What to override the current locomotion animation with.
What to override the current locomotion animation angular velocity with.
messages.Tooltip.UserPoseController._defaultConfi: What the default locomotion animation for all avatars that don't specify it should be.
messages.Tooltip.UserRoot: Usually users find the slot containing this component by using the ProtoFlux node User Root Slot.
messages.Tooltip.UserRoot.RenderSettings: The user's render settings. Usually part of an avatar.
messages.Tooltip.UserRoot.ScreenController: The screen controller that the user is using to control the game.
messages.Tooltip.UserRoot.OverrideRoot: The slot currently overriding the user's root position locally for rendering.
messages.Tooltip.UserRoot.OverrideView: The slot currently overriding the user's view position locally for rendering.
messages.Tooltip.ViewReferenceController: The ViewReferenceController component is a view controller type component mainly used in desktop mode.
messages.Tooltip.ViewReferenceController.Position: The source for position values for this component.
messages.Tooltip.ViewReferenceController.Rotation: The source for rotation values for this component.
messages.Tooltip.ViewReferenceController.ShouldVo: The field to drive with whether the user's voice should be transmitting.
messages.Tooltip.ViewReferenceController._objectS: The field to drive with the object scale value.
The field to drive with whether the object slot should be active.
messages.Tooltip.AppVersion: App version is a component that updates it's fields with the live information of Resonite's version.
messages.Tooltip.AppVersion.VersionNumber: The version number of Resonite. usually formatted year.month.day.minute. The minute part of the version is the minute from midnight UTC the version was compiled.
messages.Tooltip.AppVersion.VersionName: The name of the version of Resonite. Usually this is "Beta", "Alpha" or "Release" followed by <mark=#ffffff10>VersionNumber</mark>
messages.Tooltip.AppVersion.BuildTimeOfDay: The amount of minutes since midnight UTC.
messages.Tooltip.AssetMultiplexer`1: Add the list of assets you want to use through the SyncAssetList<IAsset`1> area, then add in your assets into the items.
messages.Tooltip.AssetMultiplexer`1.Index: Which element in <mark=#ffffff10>Assets</mark> to drive to <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark>
messages.Tooltip.AssetMultiplexer`1.Assets: The list of assets to multiplex between.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanAssetDriver`1: This component is functionally identical to Boolean Reference Driver. Except it only accepts IAssets and acts as a user for assets in FalseTarget and TrueTarget meaning assets assigned to this component won't get cleaned up by the asset optimizer world processes.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanAssetDriver`1.Target: The field to drive the value of.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanAssetDriver`1.FalseTarget: The asset to drive <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> to when <mark=#ffffff10>State</mark> is false.
messages.Tooltip.BooleanAssetDriver`1.TrueTarget: The asset to drive <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> to when <mark=#ffffff10>State</mark> is true.
messages.Tooltip.CallbackValueArgument`1: This isn't very often used by the user, since there aren't many Sync Delegates in the game that match this component's requirements.
messages.Tooltip.CallbackValueArgument`1.Value: The value to give <mark=#ffffff10>Callback</mark> when <mark=#ffffff10>Call()</mark> or <mark=#ffffff10>CallAndDestroy()</mark> is called.
messages.Tooltip.CallbackValueArgument`1.Callback: The Sync Delegate to call and send a value to.
messages.Tooltip.CallbackRefArgument`1: Similar to CallbackValueArgument but instead of a value it's a reference for an action.
messages.Tooltip.CallbackRefArgument`1.Callback: The Sync Delegate to call and provide <mark=#ffffff10>Reference</mark> to.
messages.Tooltip.ChildrenSaveBlocker: When attached to a slot, the children of the slot will not be saved when the slot is part of an item being saved.
messages.Tooltip.CurrentDateTimeTextDriver: The CurrentDateTimeTextDriver component takes the current time and formats it using C# DateTime to string using format as an argument.
messages.Tooltip.CurrentDateTimeTextDriver.Target: What to drive the contents to be the current time formatted.
messages.Tooltip.CurrentDateTimeTextDriver.Patter: The pattern to use when rendering the date time, EX: 24 vs 12 hr.
messages.Tooltip.CurrentDateTimeTextDriver.UseUTC: Whether the current time outputted should be UTC time zone.
messages.Tooltip.CurrentDateTimeTextDriver.Overri: How to format the outputted current time.
messages.Tooltip.DestroyProxy: The DestroyProxy component is used to automatically destroy a specified DestroyTarget when this component itself or it's parent slot is destroyed.
messages.Tooltip.ExternalSlotDuplicator: The ExternalSlotDuplicator component parents TargetSlot under itself when this component is duplicated. If this component is parented under a larger hiearchy, the slot will only be parented if it's not anywhere under this larger hiearchy or this component's hiearchy. When the slot is parented back under this component, any non null specified transform values for this component are applied to the slot.
messages.Tooltip.ExternalSlotDuplicator.TargetSlo: The slot to parent and write values for during duplication.
messages.Tooltip.ExternalSlotDuplicator.LocalPosi: The local position to apply to <mark=#ffffff10>TargetSlot</mark> when parenting under this slot during duplication if this is not null.
messages.Tooltip.ExternalSlotDuplicator.LocalRota: The local rotation to apply to <mark=#ffffff10>TargetSlot</mark> when parenting under this component's slot during duplication if this is not null.
messages.Tooltip.ExternalSlotDuplicator.LocalScal: The local scale to apply to <mark=#ffffff10>TargetSlot</mark> when parenting under this component's slot during duplication if this is not null.
messages.Tooltip.ExternalSlotDuplicator.ActiveSel: The active state to apply to <mark=#ffffff10>TargetSlot</mark>'s active field when parenting under this component's slot during duplication if this is not null.
messages.Tooltip.ExternalSlotDuplicator.DoNotRest: Whether to parent the slot back where it came from after duplication, Undoing all overrides and setting all values back to their originals.
messages.Tooltip.ExternalSlotSaver: The ExternalSlotSaver will ensure that the TargetSlot is saved as a child of the slot this component is on if the slot isn't already in the hierarchy.
messages.Tooltip.ExternalSlotSaver.TargetSlot: The slot to target when saving.
messages.Tooltip.ExternalSlotSaver.SaveActiveSelf: When not null, save this rather than <mark=#ffffff10>TargetSlot</mark>'s current active self value.
messages.Tooltip.ExternalSlotSaver.SaveLocalPosit: When not null, save this rather than <mark=#ffffff10>TargetSlot</mark>'s current position value.
messages.Tooltip.ExternalSlotSaver.SaveLocalRotat: When not null, save this rather than <mark=#ffffff10>TargetSlot</mark>'s current rotation value.
messages.Tooltip.ExternalSlotSaver.SaveLocalScale: When not null, save this rather than <mark=#ffffff10>TargetSlot</mark>'s current scale value.
messages.Tooltip.ExternalSlotSaver.IgnoreWhenNonP: Whether to not save the external slot if we are non persistent ourselves.
messages.Tooltip.Hyperlink: The Hyperlink component allows you to turn an object with a collider into a clickable link that will prompt the user in Userspace to open the provided link in their default web browser.
messages.Tooltip.Hyperlink.OpenOnce: Prevents the user from clicking on the URL more than once and bringing up multiple confirmation dialouges
messages.Tooltip.Hyperlink.Reason: The reason that the hyperlink is being opened. Displayed to the user when they click it in the security dialog.
messages.Tooltip.MultiTextureFader`1: The MultiTextureFader component can be used with the Component:PBSLerpMetallic or Component:PBSLerpSpecular to lerp multiple textures rather than just one.
messages.Tooltip.MultiTextureFader`1.FirstTexture: Usually set to the Texture set 0 of a lerping material.
messages.Tooltip.MultiTextureFader`1.SecondTextur: Usually set to the Texture set 1 of a lerping material. The type of texture set like albedo or emission this one is driving should be the same as <mark=#ffffff10>FirstTexture</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.MultiTextureFader`1.Lerp: The lerp field of the material. Can be left blank if using multiple of this component on the same material.
messages.Tooltip.MultiTextureFader`1.Position: The position in the list of <mark=#ffffff10>Textures</mark> to fade between. If this is a decimal, the <mark=#ffffff10>FirstTexture</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>SecondTexture</mark> will be set to textures in <mark=#ffffff10>Textures</mark> that are at the positon of floor and ceiling of this value.
messages.Tooltip.MultiTextureFader`1.Textures: A list of textures to lerp between and set <mark=#ffffff10>FirstTexture</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>SecondTexture</mark> to using <mark=#ffffff10>Position</mark> to determine the texture from this list.
messages.Tooltip.MultiValueTextFormatDriver: The ValueTextFormatDriver applies the value of a multiple values to a [https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/api/system.string.format?view=net-5.0#get-started-with-the-stringformat-method text format string] and drives the target string field. Elements are defined by putting {} brackets around a number to specify an element in Sources.
messages.Tooltip.MultiValueTextFormatDriver.Forma: The format string, which has a list of "{X}" where "X" is a number that refers to an item number in <mark=#ffffff10>Sources</mark>. The entire "{X}" gets replaced by a source with that number.
messages.Tooltip.PlatformColorPalette: The PlatformColorPalette component is a quick way of getting the colors of Resonite's Branding. This also provides you with both the color values in ColorX form and [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Web_colors Hex] form.
messages.Tooltip.PlatformColorPalette.Neutrals: A list of neutral color and hexes used for UI backgrounds in resonite.
messages.Tooltip.PlatformColorPalette.Hero: A list of color and hexes used for bright colored buttons and text like yellow for selected inspector slots or used in other cases.
messages.Tooltip.PlatformColorPalette.Sub: A darker color and hexes set used for buttons or UI that are darker due to being selected or used in other cases.
messages.Tooltip.PlatformColorPalette.Dark: A dark color and hexes set used for buttons or UI that is disabled or used in other cases.
messages.Tooltip.PlatformInfo: The PlatformInfo component is used where data about the platform that FrooxEngine is running under (In this case, Resonite) for the purpose of providing info to users like social links and language like Abbreviations and ways to refer to the platform.
messages.Tooltip.PlatformInfo.ShortNamePrefix: The short name of the platform, used in record strings.
messages.Tooltip.PlatformInfo.Abbreviation: The abreviation of the platform, useful for referring to it in casual conversation.
messages.Tooltip.PlatformInfo.DiscordInviteUrl: The URL to get to the platform's Discord.
messages.Tooltip.PlatformInfo.PatreonUrl: The Patreon URL of the platform.
messages.Tooltip.PlatformInfo.GitHubProfile: The Github profile of the platform.
messages.Tooltip.PlatformInfo.GitHubIssuesReposit: The Github issues page of the platform.
messages.Tooltip.PlatformInfo.AuthScheme: The authorization scheme of the platform.
messages.Tooltip.PlatformInfo.AppScheme: The app scheme of the platform.
messages.Tooltip.PlatformInfo.DBScheme: The data base scheme of the platform. used in asset urls.
messages.Tooltip.PlatformInfo.SessionScheme: The session scheme of the platform. used in session links.
messages.Tooltip.PlatformInfo.RecordScheme: The record scheme of the platform. used in item link urls.
messages.Tooltip.PlatformInfo.UserSessionScheme: The user session scheme of the platform.
messages.Tooltip.QuantityTextFormatDriver`1: The QuantityTextFormatDriver allows driving a string with an auto formatted quantity.
messages.Tooltip.QuantityTextFormatDriver`1.Targe: The field to drive with the formatted Quantity string.
messages.Tooltip.QuantityTextFormatDriver`1.BaseV: Value to get converted into string
messages.Tooltip.QuantityTextFormatDriver`1.Forma: How to format the number when making it part of the string (So if it should remove decimals.
messages.Tooltip.QuantityTextFormatDriver`1.Compo: A list of format Units this can use to format the base value (common ones are "ft" as an entry followed by another entry with "in" or "m" as an entry followed by another entry with "cm" ).
Show the long names of compound units.
Whether the compound format unit names should be used rather than their usual name.
Whether to discard the decimal of the smallest unit being displayed in the output or not. (so "3m 2.5cm" will become 3m 2cm")
String to put between compound units.
Remove or trim compound units with a value of zero.
messages.Tooltip.RandomEventGenerator: Random event generator is a component that is used to generate random events and point events given a minimum and maximum delay between events.
messages.Tooltip.RandomEventGenerator.MinInterval: The minimum time in seconds before another event is generated
messages.Tooltip.RandomEventGenerator.MaxInterval: The maximum time in seconds before another event is generated
messages.Tooltip.RandomEventGenerator.RandomPoint: a point generator component that is used as the limiter on where random point events can be located.
messages.Tooltip.RandomEventGenerator.Events: Random events that when calling a Sync Delegate provide no data to it's arguments.
messages.Tooltip.RandomEventGenerator.PointEvents: Random events that when calling a Sync Delegate provide a Float3 to it's arguments.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceMultiplexer`1: ReferenceMultiplexers allow to curate a list of references and drive a target with one of them.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceMultiplexer`1.Target: A SyncRef which is driven with the currently selected reference
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceMultiplexer`1.Index: 0-based index that determines which reference of <mark=#ffffff10>References</mark> has been selected; values outside the range <mark=#ffffff10>[0;length-1]</mark> are wrapped around internally.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceMultiplexer`1.Reference: A list of references which can also individually be driven or written to
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceMultiplexer`1.AllowWrit: Setting this to <mark=#ffffff10>true</mark> redirects writes from <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> to the currently indexed list entry.
messages.Tooltip.StoppedPlayableCleaner: Stopped playable cleaner is used on play one shots in order to delete or clean them once they have finished playing.
messages.Tooltip.StoppedPlayableCleaner.Playable: the audio player that is playing audio
messages.Tooltip.StoppedPlayableCleaner.GracePeri: How long to wait before deleting the component after it has finished playing.
messages.Tooltip.StoppedPlayableCleaner.CheckingU: The user that is monitoring the <mark=#ffffff10>Playable</mark> on their machine to check when it has stopped playing
messages.Tooltip.TextCountdownClock: The TextCountdownClock component is able to make a countdown till a certain value of T (world time) since a session started. This drives a text field to do so.
messages.Tooltip.TextCountdownClock.CountdownTime: The world time we are counting down to (The world time is zero when a session starts)
messages.Tooltip.TextCountdownClock.AllowNegative: Whether negative time can be displayed after the time for the world has been reached. Or to display 0 if false.
messages.Tooltip.TextCountdownClock.TextTarget: Text String field to drive with the form of HH:MM:SS based on the value in <mark=#ffffff10>CountdownTime</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ValueGraphRecorder: The ValueGraphRecorder component is used to show a graph of values over time with a specified number of points worth of history. As new points are added, the graph is shifted sideways and old points are deleted.
messages.Tooltip.ValueGraphRecorder.RecordingUser: The user handling updates for this component.
messages.Tooltip.ValueGraphRecorder.SourceValue: The field that will be sampled for values.
messages.Tooltip.ValueGraphRecorder.UpdateInterva: The interval, in seconds, that the source field will be sampled at.
messages.Tooltip.ValueGraphRecorder.Points: The number of samples to retain.
messages.Tooltip.ValueGraphRecorder.TargetArray: The array to store samples in. Typically this will be the Values field in a StandaloneRectMesh<LineGraphMesh>
messages.Tooltip.ValueGraphRecorder.TargetArrayOf: The integer field to set with the offset of where in the TargetArray to start showing samples at.
messages.Tooltip.ValueGraphRecorder.MinRangeAdjus: If a value in the graph goes beyond this value for currently shown samples, adjust the graph minimum value.
How much to increase the graph size downwards when adjusting for min value (1 is exactly to where the value edge is, 2 is add 2 times the needed value to show min on the graph.
messages.Tooltip.ValueGraphRecorder.MaxRangeAdjus: If a value in the graph goes beyond this value for currently shown samples, adjust the graph maximum value.
How much to increase the graph size upwards when adjusting for min value (1 is exactly to where the value edge is, 2 is add 2 times the needed value to show min on the graph.
messages.Tooltip.ValueGraphRecorder.RangeMin: The field to drive with what the minimum range of the graph all points being shown is currently.
