messages.Tooltip.ValueGraphRecorder.RangeMax: The field to drive with what the maximum range of the graph all points being shown is currently.
messages.Tooltip.ValueGraphRecorder.Drive: Whether to use drive fields to drive target values rather than field pointers. Makes the calculations local.
messages.Tooltip.ValueGraphRecorder._arrayDrive: The field to drive with the set of samples. Typically this will be the Values field in a StandaloneRectMesh<LineGraphMesh>
messages.Tooltip.ValueGraphRecorder._arrayOffsetD: The integer field to drive with the offset in the TargetArray to start showing samples at.
messages.Tooltip.ValueGraphRecorder._rangeMinDriv: The field to drive with what the minimum range of the graph all points being shown is currently.
messages.Tooltip.ValueGraphRecorder._rangeMaxDriv: The field to drive with what the maximum range of the graph all points being shown is currently.
messages.Tooltip.ValueMultiplexer`1: The component behaves similar to a ValueCopy with the list entry indicated by Index as the value to output.
messages.Tooltip.ValueMultiplexer`1.Target: A field which is driven with the currently selected list value
messages.Tooltip.ValueMultiplexer`1.Index: 0-based index that determines which value of <mark=#ffffff10>Values</mark> has been selected; values outside the range <mark=#ffffff10>[0;length-1]</mark> are wrapped around internally.
messages.Tooltip.ValueMultiplexer`1.Values: A list of values which can also individually be driven or written to
messages.Tooltip.ValueMultiplexer`1.AllowWriteBac: Setting this to <mark=#ffffff10>true</mark> redirects writes from <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> or from a <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark> representing the selected value to the currently indexed list entry.
messages.Tooltip.ValueTextFormatDriver`1: ValueTextFormatDriver applies the value of a single source field and drives the target string field using a format string.
messages.Tooltip.ValueTextFormatDriver`1.Source: The source field for the value.
messages.Tooltip.ValueTextFormatDriver`1.Format: The string format with "{0}" in it somewhere
messages.Tooltip.AssetOptimizationWizard: The component that drives the Asset Optimization Wizard.
messages.Tooltip.AssetOptimizationWizard.Root: The slot specified in the generated UI for the wizard. If this is null, the wizard will use the Root slot.
messages.Tooltip.AssetOptimizationWizard.IgnoreNo: Ignore the contents of the slots of users in the session or not.
messages.Tooltip.AssetOptimizationWizard._maxReso: The max resolution field that's part of the generated UI.
messages.Tooltip.AssetOptimizationWizard._message: The message for the current progress and statuses of this wizard's work.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator: The avatar creator component is the component that drives the functionality of the avatar creator object. See Avatar Creation for a much less technical page on this, and for information on how to actually use the avatar creator.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._headsetPoint: the headset point that marks the head proxy target slot position.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._leftPoint: the point that marks the left hand's target slot position.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._rightPoint: the point that marks the right hand's target slot position.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._leftFootPoint: the point that marks the foot's target slot position.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._rightFootPoint: the point that marks the right foot target slot position.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._pelvisPoint: the pelvis guide object that marks the pelvis proxy position.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._headsetReference: the headset guide that marks the headset proxy position (eyeballs/view position)
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._pelvisReference: the pelvis guide object that marks the pelvis proxy position.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._leftReference: the left hand guide object that marks the left hand proxy.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._rightReference: the right hand guide object that marks the right hand proxy.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._initialized: whether this avatar creator is ready to be used.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._showAnchors: whether to show the tool anchors on the hands.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._useSymmetry: whether to enforce symmetry of the objects across the headset location during settup.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._setupVolumeMeter: Whether to set up a volume meter component on the avatar when the avatar is created.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._setupProtection: Whether to set up avatar protection components on the root and meshes of the avatar upon creation.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._setupEyes: Whether to set up the eye bones and eye manager upon avatar creation
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._setupFaceTracking: Whether to set up a face tracking component for the mesh with the most shapekey matches for visemes.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._calibrateFeet: Whether to show the feet calibration guide objects.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._calibratePelvis: whether to show the pelvis calibration guide object.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._canProtect: Whether the user is allowed to protect. This is false if in local.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._symmetrySetup: Whether to enforce symmetry on the avatar when creating.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._anchors: A list of Anchor that are the different parts for avatar markers the user can move when using this creator.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._scale: the scale of all the <mark=#ffffff10>_anchors</mark> of this avatar creator.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._protectAvatarEnab: The protect avatar checkbox button enabled field. The checkbox disables when in user space
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator._createEnabled: The field that controls the enabled state of the create avatar button.
messages.Tooltip.CubemapCreator.TopBottomRotation: How to Rotate the top and bottom images to fit.
messages.Tooltip.CubemapCreator.PosX: What to use for the positive X side.
messages.Tooltip.CubemapCreator.NegX: What to use for the negative X side.
messages.Tooltip.CubemapCreator.PosY: What to use for the positive Y side.
messages.Tooltip.CubemapCreator.NegY: What to use for the negative Y side.
messages.Tooltip.CubemapCreator.PosZ: What to use for the top side.
messages.Tooltip.CubemapCreator.NegZ: What to use for the bottom side.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbeWizard: See Reflection Probe Wizard for more up to date info
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbeWizard.Root: The root slot in which all probes in it's hierarchy should be baked.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbeWizard.ProcessDis: Whether to bake probes in disabled hierarchies.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbeWizard.TeleportUs: Whether to teleport the user to the probe before baking.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbeWizard._tag: Probes only with this tag will be baked.
messages.Tooltip.ReflectionProbeWizard._delayBetw: How long to wait between baking probes.
messages.Tooltip.VHACD_Dialog: The VHACD_Dialog component or V-HACD 2.0 dialog, is used to do a convex hull decomposition on a mesh to make the collision simpler and more performant.
messages.Tooltip.VHACD_Dialog.Resolution: maximum number of voxels generated during the voxelization stage
messages.Tooltip.VHACD_Dialog.Depth: maximum number of clipping stages. During each split stage, all the model parts (with a concavity higher than the user defined threshold) are clipped according the "best" clipping plane
messages.Tooltip.VHACD_Dialog.PlaneDownsampling: controls the granularity of the search for the "best" clipping plane
messages.Tooltip.VHACD_Dialog.ConvexHullDownsampl: controls the precision of the convex-hull generation process during the clipping plane selection stage
messages.Tooltip.VHACD_Dialog.Alpha: controls the bias toward clipping along symmetry planes
messages.Tooltip.VHACD_Dialog.Beta: controls the bias toward clipping along revolution axes
messages.Tooltip.VHACD_Dialog.Gamma: maximum allowed concavity during the merge stage
messages.Tooltip.VHACD_Dialog.Delta: Controls the bias toward maximizing local concavity
messages.Tooltip.VHACD_Dialog.PCA: enable/disable normalizing the mesh before applying the convex decomposition
messages.Tooltip.VHACD_Dialog.Mode: how to base the approximate convex decomposition
messages.Tooltip.VHACD_Dialog.MaxVerticesPerHull: controls the maximum number of vertices per convex-hull
messages.Tooltip.VHACD_Dialog.MinVolumePerHull: controls the adaptive sampling of the generated convex-hulls
messages.Tooltip.WorldLightSourcesWizard.Root: The root directory in which all lights underneath it will be affected.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLightSourcesWizard.ProcessD: Whether to process lights that are not part of an active hiearchy or the component itself is disabled.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLightSourcesWizard.TargetSh: The shadow type to set all lights to.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLightSourcesWizard._tag: The slot tag that lights have to have under <mark=#ffffff10>Root</mark> to be affected.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLightSourcesWizard._intensi: The intensity to set all lights under <mark=#ffffff10>Root</mark> to.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLightSourcesWizard._rangeFi: The distance range to set all lights under <mark=#ffffff10>Root</mark> to.
messages.Tooltip.WorldTextRendererWizard.Root: The root to do processing for.
messages.Tooltip.WorldTextRendererWizard.Material: The material to replace text renderer materials with.
messages.Tooltip.WorldTextRendererWizard.Font: The font to override texts with.
messages.Tooltip.WorldTextRendererWizard.ProcessD: Whether to process disabled texts or not.
messages.Tooltip.WorldTextRendererWizard.Color: What color to change the texts to.
messages.Tooltip.WorldTextRendererWizard._tag: Only process texts with this tag.
messages.Tooltip.AssetLoadStatus: This is a component that tracks the load status of a list of assets locally.
messages.Tooltip.AssetLoadStatus.Assets: List of assets that the component should listen to for loading status
messages.Tooltip.AssetLoadStatus.IsLoaded: Are all the assets in Assets list loaded?
messages.Tooltip.AssetLoadStatus.LoadProgress: The ratio of "how many assets are loaded" to the total asset count.
messages.Tooltip.AssetLoadStatus.ProgressWeight: The total count of assets in <mark=#ffffff10>Assets</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterColliderWorldLink: Character collider world link is a component that allows for having a user trigger a world link when they collide against a collider or go into a trigger zone.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterColliderWorldLink.OpenO: Use world link on contact start.
Use world link on contact stay.
Use world link on contact end.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterColliderWorldLink.World: The world to open and optionally go to to on contact.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterColliderWorldLink.LastO: Last time this world link was used.
messages.Tooltip.FocusedWorldStatus: FocusedWorldStatus is a component that displays information about the currently focused world. It's used in the session tab in the dash.
messages.Tooltip.FocusedWorldStatus.WorldName: The name of the currently focused world.
messages.Tooltip.FocusedWorldStatus.RawWorldName: The raw unfiltered name of the currently focused world.
messages.Tooltip.FocusedWorldStatus.SessionId: The session ID of the currently focused world.
messages.Tooltip.FocusedWorldStatus.IsHost: Whether the user is the host of the focused world.
messages.Tooltip.FocusedWorldStatus.CanSave: Whether the user is allowed to save the focused world.
messages.Tooltip.FocusedWorldStatus.ShouldSave: Whether the focused world has unsaved changes or not.
messages.Tooltip.FocusedWorldStatus.CanClose: Whether the focused world can be closed. False for the Local Home.
messages.Tooltip.FocusedWorldStatus.RoleName: The name of the role that the user currently has in the focused world.
messages.Tooltip.FocusedWorldStatus.UserCount: The amount of users currently in the focused world.
messages.Tooltip.FocusedWorldStatus.ActiveUserCou: How many of the users in the focused world aren't AFK.
messages.Tooltip.FocusedWorldStatus.MaxUserCount: The maximum amount of users that can be in the focused world.
messages.Tooltip.FocusedWorldStatus.AccessLevel: The access level of the focused world.
messages.Tooltip.FocusedWorldStatus.HideFromListi: Whether or not the focused world should not be shown in world listings or shown to other users.
messages.Tooltip.FocusedWorldStatus.AwayKickEnabl: Whether the focused world has AFK kick enabled.
messages.Tooltip.FocusedWorldStatus.AwayKickMinut: How many minutes a user can be AFK before being kicked from the focused world.
messages.Tooltip.FocusedWorldStatus.UnsafeMode: Whether the focused world is in unsafe mode, which allows for some features to work. Can only be true in private worlds.
messages.Tooltip.MeshRendererLoadStatus: The MeshRendererLoadStatus component keeps track of how many meshes in the list of meshes have been loaded for the local user. The meshes can be skinned mesh renderers or mesh renderers. The loaded status checks for if the mesh is loaded, including for any materials on those mesh/skinned mesh renderers.
messages.Tooltip.MeshRendererLoadStatus.Renderers: A list of skinned mesh renderers or mesh renderers to keep track of the load progress for.
messages.Tooltip.MeshRendererLoadStatus.IsLoaded: Whether all meshes are finished loading
messages.Tooltip.MeshRendererLoadStatus.LoadProgr: The percentage of meshes that have loaded.
messages.Tooltip.MeshRendererLoadStatus.ProgressW: The number of not null meshes that exist in <mark=#ffffff10>Renderers</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.RecordEditForm: The RecordEditForm component is used to edit the details and info/metadata for a world.
messages.Tooltip.RecordEditForm._worldOrb: The world orb that created this dialog.
messages.Tooltip.RecordEditForm._name: The field to edit the name of the world.
messages.Tooltip.RecordEditForm._description: The field to edit the description of the world.
messages.Tooltip.RecordEditForm._path: The field to edit the path of the world.
messages.Tooltip.RecordEditForm._tags: The field to edit the tags of the world.
messages.Tooltip.RecordEditForm._publicSetting: The field to edit the  of the world.
messages.Tooltip.RecordEditForm._readonly: The checkbox to edit the read only property of the world.
messages.Tooltip.RecordEditForm._ownerUser: The field to show the owner of the world.
messages.Tooltip.RecordEditForm._privateOptionTex: The field to show the private options of the world.
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource: The SessionInfoSource component can be used to receive an assortment of useful information from an inputted SessionId.
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource.SessionId: The session ID to get information on.
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource.LastUpdated: The last time the info on this component was updated.
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource.Name: The name of the session
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource.Tags: Tags that the session world has.
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource.CorrespondingR: The record ID of the world of the session
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource.CorrespondingO: The owner UserID of the session's world.
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource.HostUserId: The UserID of the session host.
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource.HostUsername: The username of the session host.
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource.SanitizedHostU: The username of the session host which escapes special characters.
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource.AppVersion: The App version of the client of the session being hosted.
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource.HeadlessHost: Whether the session is being hosted by a headless client.
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource.SessionURLs: A list of other session URLs using the same world.
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource.Thumbnail: The link for the session thumbnail in text form.
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource.JoinedUsers: How many users are in the world.
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource.ActiveUsers: Of the people joined, how many are active/focused?
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource.TotalJoinedUse: How many users have ever joined the world?
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource.MaximumUsers: The maximum users that can be in the session.
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource.MobileFriendly: Whether the session is compatible with a mobile/quest build of the game.
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource.AccessLevel: The sessions level of access to users joining.
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource.IsOnLAN: Whether the session is set to Local Access Network.
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource.AwayKickEnable: Whether away kick is enabled or not
messages.Tooltip.SessionInfoSource.AwayKickInterv: How long till a user is kicked for being away in a session.
messages.Tooltip.SessionCaptureThumbnailSource: Place this component on a slot.
messages.Tooltip.CaptureThumbnailSource.Overlay: An overlay image to superimpose over the normal session capture. This image must have an average alpha channel value under 25% or else it will not be applied.
messages.Tooltip.WorldCaptureThumbnailSource: The WorldCaptureThumbnailSource component is used as a point in space to capture the preview of a world in the world browser minus users in the session when a world is saved.
messages.Tooltip.WorldCaptureThumbnailSource.Excl: Whether to exclude users from the thumbnail.
messages.Tooltip.WorldTextureThumbnailSource: The WorldTextureThumbnailSource specifies a completely customized texture to use for the world thumbnail.
messages.Tooltip.TouchWorldLink: The TouchWorldLink component when touched allows a user to go to the world of the given link.
messages.Tooltip.TouchWorldLink.AcceptPhysicalTou: Whether to allow Component:TipTouchSources to interact with/activate this component.
messages.Tooltip.TouchWorldLink.AcceptRemoteTouch: Whether this component allows interaction via the user's interaction laser.
messages.Tooltip.TouchWorldLink.AcceptOutOfSightT: Whether or not to allow interaction with this component if it is out of the user's view.
messages.Tooltip.TouchWorldLink.Vibrate: How to vibrate the haptics of the hand that touched this component.
messages.Tooltip.TouchWorldLink.LastOpened: The last world time this component was pressed.
messages.Tooltip.TouchWorldLink.WorldLink: The world link to open upon touch.
messages.Tooltip.UsersAssetLoadProgress`1: The UsersAssetLoadProgress component is commonly used with ProtoFlux to tell if a particular User has loaded an asset. The node in particular is the Asset Load Progress ProtoFlux node. This component takes any IAsset as the generic argument for the component.
messages.Tooltip.UsersAssetLoadProgress`1.Asset: The Asset to monitor loading progress for.
messages.Tooltip.UsersAssetLoadProgress`1.Progres: A list of users in the session and their status on loading the specified <mark=#ffffff10>Asset</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.UsersAssetLoadProgress`1.TotalUs: The total users in the session that see <mark=#ffffff10>Asset</mark> exists?
messages.Tooltip.UsersAssetLoadProgress`1.UsersNo: How many users haven't fully loaded <mark=#ffffff10>Asset</mark>?
messages.Tooltip.UsersAssetLoadProgress`1.UsersLo: How many users are trying to load <mark=#ffffff10>Asset</mark> currently?
messages.Tooltip.UsersAssetLoadProgress`1.UsersPa: How many users are currently in the process of downloading <mark=#ffffff10>Asset</mark>?
messages.Tooltip.UsersAssetLoadProgress`1.UsersFu: How many users have finished loading <mark=#ffffff10>Asset</mark>?
messages.Tooltip.UsersAssetLoadProgress`1.UsersFa: How many users failed to load <mark=#ffffff10>Asset</mark> due to an error?
messages.Tooltip.WorldCloseAction: The WorldCloseAction component is used to handle the leaving of a world after the user confirms leaving. This component only works on user space.
messages.Tooltip.WorldCloseAction.Action: The kind of action this Button Events recieving Component is doing to a world.
messages.Tooltip.WorldCloseAction.WaitingConfirm: Whether the game is waiting on confirmation via double click, which is 2 clicks within a 2 second time frame.
messages.Tooltip.WorldCloseAction.OnClosed: What Action Sync delegate to call when the world finishes closing.
messages.Tooltip.WorldCloseDialog: The WorldCloseDialog component is used to confirm closing a world that the user is currently trying to close.
messages.Tooltip.WorldCloseDialog._worldName: The text field showing the name of the world being closed.
messages.Tooltip.WorldCloseDialog._saveButton: The button used to save the world being closed.
messages.Tooltip.WorldCloseDialog._saveAsButton: The button used to save the world as something for the world being closed.
messages.Tooltip.WorldCloseDialog._discardButton: The button used to discard changes for the world being closed.
messages.Tooltip.WorldCloseDialog._saveAction: The component to trigger with the request to save the world being closed.
messages.Tooltip.WorldCloseDialog._saveAsAction: The component to trigger with the request to save the world as something for the world being closed.
messages.Tooltip.WorldCloseDialog._discardAction: The component to trigger with the request to discard changes for the world being closed.
messages.Tooltip.WorldCloseScreen: The World Close Screen component is used in the dash and appears when the user asks to close a world. This acts as a conformation. This is also an internally used component.
messages.Tooltip.WorldCloseScreen._closeDialog: The close dialog this is controlling.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLink: The WorldLink component is used to set the world information, either to use as a way to jump to different worlds or to pre-load worlds.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLink.CreateIfNotExists: If the world we are going to does not exist, then make one.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLink.AutoFocus: After the world loads, focus to this world automatically.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLink.GetExisting: If a world we are looking for already exists, then don't make a new one and then optionally focus onto it.
messages.Tooltip.WorldOrb: The WorldOrb component is a Grabbable component that allows users to grab and throw it to open a world.
messages.Tooltip.WorldOrb.SessionStartingUser: The user that started the session if this is a session orb.
messages.Tooltip.WorldOrb.OpenActionOverride: The Sync delegate to call as an override for this world orb's open Action. Passes this component as an argument.
messages.Tooltip.WorldOrb.Visit: Whether the local user has visted the target world.
messages.Tooltip.WorldOrb.ActiveUsers: How many active users are in the target world.
messages.Tooltip.WorldOrb.RecordStateUpdated: Whether the world orb has up to date info after spawn.
messages.Tooltip.WorldOrb.CanModify: Whether the world can be modified and saved.
messages.Tooltip.WorldOrb.LongPressIndicatorColor: The color to use when the world orb is long pressed.
messages.Tooltip.WorldOrb.LongPressTime: How long the user has to click to trigger a long press.

messages.Tooltip.WorldOrb._longPressIndicatorMate: The material used to indicate a long press.

messages.Tooltip.LegacyPanel._pinButton: The title button object for the pin to user button.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyPanel.CloseOverride: A sync delegate that takes a LegacyPanel that overrides the handling of closing this panel.
messages.Tooltip.LegacySwapCanvasPanel: The LegacySwapCanvasPanel was used to swap between different panels using some buttons.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyUIStyle: The LegacyUIStyle component is used in older content to specify what colors a UI should be depending on where it is. This should not be used in new content, and should be removed/replaced whenever possible.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyUIStyle.UserParentedColor: The color a UI should have when parented under the user.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyUIStyle.PrivateColor: The color a UI should have when it's in userspace.
messages.Tooltip.BevelPlaneMesh: Used in old Legacy content like old inspectors and panels.
messages.Tooltip.BevelRingMesh: Bevel Ring Mesh is a component that can create an circle or part of a circle in the form of a 3D strip that can have beveled edges. The geometry made by this component can be viewed by inserting the component into a MeshRenderer.
messages.Tooltip.BevelRingMesh.Arc: How long the strip continues from the starting point defined by <mark=#ffffff10>ArcOffset</mark>
messages.Tooltip.BevelRingMesh.ArcOffset: The degree that the start point of the strip is at.
messages.Tooltip.BevelRingMesh.Radius: The distance the middle of the strip is from the curvature center.
messages.Tooltip.BevelRingMesh.Width: How wide the curved strip is
messages.Tooltip.BevelRingMesh.Thickness: The thickness of the curved strip.
messages.Tooltip.BevelRingMesh.Tilt: How much to bend the strip from a flat orientation to inwards/outwards slop from/towards the center. Is in degrees.
messages.Tooltip.BevelRingMesh.Segments: how many bends are generated in the curve. Bigger values result in smoother curve in exchange for performance.
messages.Tooltip.BevelStripeMesh: The geometry this generates can be viewed by inserting the component into a Mesh Renderer component.
messages.Tooltip.MultiBevelStripeMesh: The MultiBevelStripeMesh component can be used to instanciate multiple Component:BevelStripeMeshs as part of the same geometry.
messages.Tooltip.MultiBevelStripeMesh.Stripes: A list of stripes to render.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyGlowCircle: The GlowCircle component is used to make the circle that surrounds default spawn areas.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyGlowCircle.Radius: The radius of the circle visual.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyGlowCircle.Height: The height of the walls for the glow visual.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyGlowCircle.Color: The color of the glow visual.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyGlowCircle._cylinderOffset: The field to drive with the offset of the cylinder used for this visual.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyGlowCircle._cylinderRadius: The field to drive with the radius of the cylinder used for this visual.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyGlowCircle._cylinderHeight: The field to drive with the height of the cylinder used for this visual.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyGlowCircle._circleQuadSize: The field to drive with the size of the cylinder faces used for this visual.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyGlowCircle._circleTint: The field to drive with the color of the circle used for this visual.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyGlowCircle._cylinderTint: The field to drive with the color of the cylinder used for this visual.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyLabel: The LegacyLabel component is a leftover Component from content migrated from other platforms. It should not be used, and should be replaced whenever possible.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyLabel.TextRenderer: The text renderer for the label visual.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyLabel.BaseColor: The base color of the label visual.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyLabel.BaseColorElement: The UI element for the base color.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyLabel.Brightness: The brightness of the label visual.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyLabel.LerpRatio: A value from 0<->1 to lerp <mark=#ffffff10>BaseColor</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>LerpColor</mark> with.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyLabel._textColor: The field to drive with a color between <mark=#ffffff10>BaseColor</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>LerpColor</mark> using <mark=#ffffff10>LerpRatio</mark> as the specifier for the in between color.
messages.Tooltip.AssetFrameSlot`1: An asset frame slot is a component that takes any Asset Type as an attach component type argument, and allows for snapping assets to a frame. The asset in the frame can be used as a source to drive a list of asset fields which is defined in Targets. Optionally it calls a sync delegate that takes an argument of the chosen asset type for this component.
messages.Tooltip.AssetFrameSlot`1.FrameSize: how thick the frame should be.
messages.Tooltip.AssetFrameSlot`1.FrameAnimSpeed: how fast the frame should fit to the asset's bounding box in seconds
messages.Tooltip.AssetFrameSlot`1.SnapAnimTime: How long the animation should be for the asset going into the frame when snapped in seconds.
messages.Tooltip.AssetFrameSlot`1.Targets: The items to drive with the snapped object's asset.
messages.Tooltip.AssetFrameSlot`1.Callback: A sync delegate that takes an argument of the chosen asset type for this component. The sync delegate is called when the asset is snapped.
messages.Tooltip.AssetFrameSlot`1._current: The slot of the asset item inside of the frame.
messages.Tooltip.AssetFrameSlot`1._currentRatio: The ratio of the asset's bounding box held in the frame
messages.Tooltip.AssetFrameSlot`1._frameSize: The field to drive with what the size of the frame should be for the held asset's bounding box.
messages.Tooltip.AssetFrameSlot`1._collider: The collider to drive the values of the size of the held asset's bounding box.
messages.Tooltip.IntTextEditorParser: Place on the same Slot as a TextEditor and anything entered into the text editor will be attempted to be parsed as a Int and output via the ParsedValue field.
messages.Tooltip.TextEditorParser`1.ParseContinuo: If true, makes the value always update and parse. If false, it only updates when submitted.
messages.Tooltip.TextEditorParser`1.UpdateStringF: If true, will update the string value from this parsed value.
messages.Tooltip.TextEditorParser`1.ParsedValue: The value that was read from the text field.
messages.Tooltip.IntTextEditorParser.Min: The minimum value that can be read.
messages.Tooltip.IntTextEditorParser.Max: The maximum value that can be read.
messages.Tooltip.IntTextEditorParser.Increments: The output value will round down to the nearest increment multiple of this value.
messages.Tooltip.IntTextEditorParser.StringFormat: Lets you format the string for the text output.
messages.Tooltip.FloatTextEditorParser: Place on the same Slot as a TextEditor and anything entered into the text editor will be attempted to be parsed as a Float and output via the ParsedValue field.
messages.Tooltip.FloatTextEditorParser.Min: The minimum value that can be read.
messages.Tooltip.FloatTextEditorParser.Max: The maximum value that can be read.
messages.Tooltip.FloatTextEditorParser.DecimalPla: The amount of decimal places that can be read.
messages.Tooltip.FloatTextEditorParser.StringForm: Lets you format the string for the text output.
messages.Tooltip.QuantityTextEditorParser`2: Place on the same Slot as a TextEditor and anything entered into the text editor will be attempted to be parsed as a quantity and output via the ParsedValue field.
messages.Tooltip.QuantityTextEditorParser`2.MinVa: The minimum value that can be read.
messages.Tooltip.QuantityTextEditorParser`2.MaxVa: The maximum value that can be read.
messages.Tooltip.QuantityTextEditorParser`2.Ignor: Ignores any value that is out of range from the min and max.
messages.Tooltip.QuantityTextEditorParser`2.Defau: Changes the default unit to show in the text (especially if the <mark=#ffffff10>FormatUnit</mark> is incorrect).
messages.Tooltip.QuantityTextEditorParser`2.Forma: The unit to show in the text.
messages.Tooltip.QuantityTextEditorParser`2.Compo: Compounds units together (example: feet and inches for height).
Use the long version of names.
messages.Tooltip.QuantityTextEditorParser`1: Place on the same Slot as a TextEditor and anything entered into the text editor will be attempted to be parsed as a quantity and output via the ParsedValue field.
messages.Tooltip.TextEditor: This component is used in many places, including: * Primitive Member Editor for directly editing TextFields (and Ref Hacking) * UIX and TextFields * Parsing floats and parsing ints
messages.Tooltip.TextEditor.Text: The output of where the text is going to be placed at.
messages.Tooltip.TextEditor.Undo: If true, the setting of the text is undoable.
messages.Tooltip.TextEditor.UndoDescription: This gets shown on the undo button on the Context menu.
messages.Tooltip.TextEditor.FinishHandling: Lets the Text editor know what to do when the user leaves a text input.
messages.Tooltip.TextEditor.AutoCaretColorField: Automatically sets the color of the caret (based on text color).
messages.Tooltip.TextEditor.SelectionColorField: Sets the selection color for this caret.
messages.Tooltip.TextEditor.EditingStarted: Gives the TextEditor an action when the editing has started.
messages.Tooltip.TextEditor.EditingChanged: Gives the TextEditor an action when the editing has changed.
messages.Tooltip.TextEditor.EditingFinished: Gives the TextEditor an action when the editing has finished.
messages.Tooltip.TextEditor.SubmitPressed: Gives the TextEditor an action when the editing has been submitted.
messages.Tooltip.TouchableTextField: Great for when you don't want to be bound by UIX to make editable texts.
messages.Tooltip.TouchableTextField.TextEditor: The TextField to allow for bi-directional editing.
messages.Tooltip.TouchableTextField.AcceptOutOfSi: Whether or not to allow interaction with this component if it is out of the user's view.
messages.Tooltip.TouchableTextField.AcceptPhysica: Whether to allow Component:TipTouchSources to interact with/activate this component.
messages.Tooltip.TouchableTextField.AcceptRemoteT: Whether this component allows interaction via the user's interaction laser.
messages.Tooltip.TouchableTextField.EditModeOnly: Allows for the user to only edit this text in Edit Mode.
messages.Tooltip.TouchableTextField.ActiveUserRoo: Allows to only be editable when under the active user.
messages.Tooltip.UnitSystemSwitcher.DefaultUnit: Changes the default unit to show in the text (especially if the <mark=#ffffff10>FormatUnit</mark> is incorrect).
messages.Tooltip.UnitSystemSwitcher.FormatUnit: The unit to show in the text.
messages.Tooltip.UnitSystemSwitcher.CompoundForma: Compounds units together (example: feet and inches for height).
messages.Tooltip.UnitSystemSwitcher.CompoundUseLo: The field to drive with whether to use long names like "meters" instead of "m".
messages.Tooltip.BabbleSettings.OSC_DataPort: The port to listen for Babble Face Data from.
messages.Tooltip.SteamLinkSettings.OSC_DataPort: the port to use to communicate steam link info via OSC.
messages.Tooltip.GiggleTechSettings: The GiggleTechSettings Component is used to control different aspects of [https://giggletech.io/products/giggle_puck giggle pucks] for use in Resonite.
messages.Tooltip.GiggleTechSettings.Devices: A list of giggle pucks with settings for them.
messages.Tooltip.GiggleTechSettings.GigglePuckIP: the IP address of the giggle puck.
messages.Tooltip.GiggleTechSettings.IsGigglePuckV: Whether the giggle puck is valid and tracking.
messages.Tooltip.AssetOptimizationBlock: The AssetOptimizationBlock component is used to prevent the asset optimization tasks in the game from slots tagged by this component. Currently according to decompiled code as of 13/10/2024, asset optimization block just prevents materials on the same slot that are the same value wise from being merged.
messages.Tooltip.CameraPermissions: The Camera Permissions component allows you to set up who can use certain camera modes on the streaming camera. Currently, the component allows setting permissions for camera modes per role.
messages.Tooltip.CameraPermissions.CameraModeFilt: Whether to allow or disallow the <mark=#ffffff10>CameraModes</mark>
messages.Tooltip.CameraPermissions.CameraModes: List of Camera modes which decide how a stream camera can look at users and/or the world.
messages.Tooltip.CameraPermissions.AllowFramingOt: Determines whether focusing the camera on other user is allowed.
messages.Tooltip.ScreenViewPermissions: The ScreenViewPermissions component is a Permissions type component that can apply rules for what certain roles in a session can do with views like third person, free cam, or UI targetting.
messages.Tooltip.ScreenViewPermissions.ListMode: How to filter and handle the list of <mark=#ffffff10>ViewFilters</mark> for the selected roles.
messages.Tooltip.ScreenViewPermissions.ViewFilter: A list of targetting views to check a user with the selected role(s) with and act accordingly on if they are using one in the list.
messages.Tooltip.WorldPermissions: The WorldPermissions component controls various actions the selected roles can do in the world.
messages.Tooltip.WorldPermissions.AllowSavingItem: Whether the selected roles can save items.
messages.Tooltip.WorldPermissions.AllowTransferin: Whether the selected roles can smuggle items out.
messages.Tooltip.WorldPermissions.AllowSpawningOb: Whether the selected roles can spawn objects.
messages.Tooltip.WorldPermissions.AllowSwappingAv: Whether the selected roles can swap into a different avatar.
messages.Tooltip.WorldPermissions.SaveCopyPermiss: When can the selected roles save the world?
messages.Tooltip.HiddenLayer: The HiddenLayer component prevents rendering of the hierarchy it is applied to unless disabled or viewed through a camera that is explicitly set to render it. The Hidden Layer component is used for the Dash Menu's curved ui.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayLayer: The OverlayLayer component instructs the renderer to render the hierarchy it is attached to on top of everything else. In desktop mode the component also causes the hierarchy to render directly in front of the camera, mimicking a 2D user interface.
messages.Tooltip.ResoniteLinkPermissions.AllowRea: Allows ResoniteLink to read data from the world.
messages.Tooltip.ResoniteLinkPermissions.AllowWri: Allows ResoniteLink to write data to the world.
messages.Tooltip.HostAccessDialog: See Request Host Access ProtoFlux Node.
messages.Tooltip.HostAccessDialog.Host: The host destination string being accessed.
messages.Tooltip.HostAccessDialog.Port: The port being asked for access.
messages.Tooltip.HostAccessDialog.AccessType: The access type wanted for the port.
messages.Tooltip.HostAccessDialog._hostText: The text field used to show the server wanting access for.
messages.Tooltip.HostAccessDialog._reasonText: The reason for accessing the server.
messages.Tooltip.HostAccessDialog._allowButton: The button to allow the server to be accessed through your client.
messages.Tooltip.LegacySettingsData: The Legacy Settings Data component was used in the old settings menu before the settings were updated in the settings update series.
messages.Tooltip.LegacySettingsData._settings: A list of setting keys and their values.
messages.Tooltip.SettingManager: The Setting Manager component is internally used by the settings system to handle settings changes.
messages.Tooltip.SettingManager.OwnerId: The owner of the settings that this manages.
messages.Tooltip.SettingManager.SupressedBy: Whether changes in this are suppressed by another setting manager.
messages.Tooltip.AudioAccessibilitySettings.Whisp: Controls the volume of sounds outside of your whisper sphere when using whisper mode.
messages.Tooltip.AudioAccessibilitySettings.Voice: Controls the volume of sounds while you are recording a voice message.
messages.Tooltip.AudioInputDeviceSettings: The AudioInputDeviceSettings Component is used to set the priority order of input devices for the local user. It also can be used to disable priority and use the system default instead.
messages.Tooltip.AudioInputDeviceSettings.UseSyst: Whether or not to use the user's default device on their OS or not.
messages.Tooltip.AudioInputDeviceSettings.DeviceP: A list of devices and their priority which is the order to use them in.
Whether the "Use Default Device" setting is turned on or off
messages.Tooltip.AudioInputFilteringSettings.UseV: Whether to normalize the incoming voice data.
messages.Tooltip.AudioInputFilteringSettings.Norm: When below this volume, boost so the voice is louder.
messages.Tooltip.AudioInputFilteringSettings.Nois: The threshold that audio needs to be above for the voice to get through.

This indicates how fast the noise gate responds to an incoming sound that is louder than the threshold volume. Larger values will result in a slower volume fade in, while smaller ones will make the volume ramp up quicker.
This controls how long it takes for the noise gate to begin to close after the microphone input volume falls below the threshold volume.
This setting controls how fast the noise gate closes after the incoming audio has quieted again.
messages.Tooltip.AudioOutputDeviceSettings: The AudioOutputDeviceSettings Component is used to set the priority order of output devices for the local user. It also can be used to disable priority and use the system default instead.
messages.Tooltip.AudioOutputDeviceSettings.UseSys: Whether or not to use the user's default device on their OS or not.
messages.Tooltip.AudioOutputDeviceSettings.Device: A list of devices and their priority which is the order to use them in.
Whether the "Use Default Device" setting is turned on or off
messages.Tooltip.AudioPerformanceSettings: The Audio Performance Settings component is part of the Settings that control audio buffers.
messages.Tooltip.AudioPerformanceSettings.MaxVoic: The maximum voices the user is allowed to hear.
messages.Tooltip.AudioPerformanceSettings.OutputB: The output buffer size in samples.
messages.Tooltip.AudioPerformanceSettings.Simulat: The simulation buffer size in samples.
messages.Tooltip.AudioVolumeSettings: The AudioVolumeSettings is a Legacy component previously used for settings in the game.
messages.Tooltip.AudioVolumeSettings.MasterVolume: The volume setting for everything in the game.
messages.Tooltip.AudioVolumeSettings.SoundEffectV: The volume setting for sound effects in the game.
messages.Tooltip.AudioVolumeSettings.MultimediaVo: The volume setting for multimedia in the game.
messages.Tooltip.AudioVolumeSettings.VoiceVolume: The volume setting for voices in the game.
messages.Tooltip.AudioVolumeSettings.UserInterfac: The volume setting for the user interface in the game.
messages.Tooltip.EarmuffSettings: The EarmuffSettings component is used to allow a user to change the settings for the Ear muffs, which reduce the noise outside of a cone emitted from the user's head. This feature is useful in crowds or noisy places
messages.Tooltip.EarmuffSettings.EarmuffEnabled: Whether to have the earmuffs mode enabled for the local user
messages.Tooltip.EarmuffSettings.VolumeAttenuatio: How much to reduce volume based on its distance from the cone.
messages.Tooltip.EarmuffSettings.Directionality: How much the audio is affected by how in front the audio is.
messages.Tooltip.EarmuffSettings.Distance: How far forward from the user's head the cone should stretch
messages.Tooltip.EarmuffSettings.TransitionStart: The start of audio reducing outside the cone.
messages.Tooltip.EarmuffSettings.TransitionLength: The distance outside the cone that audio becomes quiet over distance from the cone.
messages.Tooltip.FullBodyTrackingSettings: The FullBodyTrackingSettings component is used to control full body behavior in the game. It's part of the Settings menu.
messages.Tooltip.FullBodyTrackingSettings.BodyHor: The max horizontal angle before the feet no longer attempt to do walking animations.
messages.Tooltip.GamepadSettings.ThumbstickLookSp: How sensitive the thumbstick is when looking around. higher values mean faster look speed with the joystick at max.
messages.Tooltip.GamepadSettings.ThumbstickLookEx: How much faster the joystick makes the user look around as it approaches the edge, becoming exponentially fast at the very edge. This value increases and can decrease the power of this effect.
messages.Tooltip.GamepadSettings.UseGamepadWhenUn: Whether to allow usage of the connected gamepad when unfocused from the game.
messages.Tooltip.GeneralControlsSettings.PrimaryH: Adapts the game to your dominant hand. Some items and tools will adapt to this.
messages.Tooltip.GeneralControlsSettings.DoubleCl: Used to adjust the time window for any actions which require a double click.
messages.Tooltip.GrabbingSettings.DefaultHandGrab: The hand grabbing mode a use has by default for their hands.
messages.Tooltip.KeyboardLookSettings: The KeyboardLookSettings component is part of the Settings menu.
messages.Tooltip.KeyboardLookSettings.HorizontalS: The speed of the keyboard looking on the horizontal axis.
messages.Tooltip.KeyboardLookSettings.VerticalSpe: The speed of the keyboard looking on the vertical axis.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionSettings: The LocomotionSettings component is better described on the settings page.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionSettings.LocomotionPre: A list of locomotion entries that the user prefers when entering a world.
messages.Tooltip.MouseSettings.MouseSensitivity: How sensitive the game head movement is to mouse movement.
messages.Tooltip.MouseSettings.ScrollWheelGrabMov: How far items move to and from the player while being grabbed and the scroll wheel is moved 1 unit.
messages.Tooltip.MouseSettings.MouseLookSpeed: How fast the mouse moves the camera.
messages.Tooltip.MouseSettings.MousePanSpeed: How fast the mouse moves the camera sideways and up/down when focused on an inspector.
messages.Tooltip.MouseSettings.MouseRotateSpeed: How fast the mouse can be used to rotate objects.
messages.Tooltip.MouseSettings.MouseFreeformRotat: How fast the mouse can be used to free form look in 3rd person desktop mode.
messages.Tooltip.MovementSettings.LeftSidewaysMod: How the left controller should be used to move the user.
messages.Tooltip.MovementSettings.RightSidewaysMo: How the right controller should be used to move the user.
messages.Tooltip.MovementSettings.UseHeadDirectio: Should head direction or controller direction in VR for movement direction.
messages.Tooltip.MovementSettings.UseSmoothTurn: Whether smooth turning using joystick should be used.
messages.Tooltip.MovementSettings.SmoothTurnExclu: Whether to use only smooth turning in all cases.
messages.Tooltip.MovementSettings.SnapTurnAngle: How many degrees to change by when using snap turn mode.
messages.Tooltip.MovementSettings.NoClipSpeed: How fast the user's noclip speed should be.
messages.Tooltip.MovementSettings.MovementDeadzon: Any 0 to 1 value less than this is ignored for movement. (this is 0 to 1 range)
messages.Tooltip.MovementSettings.TurningDeadzone: Any 0 to 1 value less than this is ignored for turning.(this is 0 to 1 range)
messages.Tooltip.MovementSettings.MovementExponen: Pushes values from a 0 to 1 linear value to a quick ramp up effect still within a 0 to 1 range for movement speed input on the joysticks.
messages.Tooltip.GeneralHapticsSettings.EnableCon: Enables or disables controller vibration as a whole.
messages.Tooltip.GeneralHapticsSettings.EnableHap: Enables or disables haptic feedback from avatars and items in the world or other haptic devices.
messages.Tooltip.GeneralVRSettings.UseVRHotswitch: Whether to allow hot swapping to VR and desktop when taking on or off a VR headset.
messages.Tooltip.TrackerSettings.UseTrackers: If all body trackers should be enabled.
messages.Tooltip.TrackerSettings.Trackers: View all names, assignments, ids, and statuses for previously used trackers.
messages.Tooltip.TrackingSmoothingSettings: The TrackingSmoothingSettings component is better explained on the Settings page.
messages.Tooltip.TrackingSmoothingSettings.HandPo: How much to smooth hand positioning, if not null.
messages.Tooltip.TrackingSmoothingSettings.HandRo: How much to smooth hand rotation, if not null.
messages.Tooltip.TrackingSmoothingSettings.FeetPo: How much to smooth feet positioning, if not null.
messages.Tooltip.TrackingSmoothingSettings.FeetRo: How much to smooth feet rotation, if not null.
messages.Tooltip.TrackingSmoothingSettings.HipsPo: How much to smooth hips positioning, if not null.
messages.Tooltip.TrackingSmoothingSettings.HipsRo: How much to smooth hips rotation, if not null.
messages.Tooltip.CursorSettings: The CursorSettings component is used in the settings menu to allow the user to change the size of their cursor when it is under specific circumstances.
messages.Tooltip.CursorSettings.GrabMultiplier: The amount to multiply the cursor size by when grabbing something
messages.Tooltip.CursorSettings.InteractionMultip: The amount to multiply the cursor size by when pressing a button or interacting with something.
messages.Tooltip.CursorSettings.TextMultiplier: How much to multiply the cursor size when highlighting an editable text field.
messages.Tooltip.CursorSettings.SliderMultiplier: The amount to multiply the cursor size when sliding a slider.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopRenderSettings: The DesktopRenderSettings component is used to set Settings used in Desktop mode.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopRenderSettings.FieldOfVie: The fov of the desktop client.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopRenderSettings.SprintFiel: Whether to multiply the FOV when sprinting
messages.Tooltip.DesktopRenderSettings.LimitFrame: Whether to limit framerate when the game is unfocused.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopRenderSettings.MaximumBac: How much to limit the framerate when unfocused and <mark=#ffffff10>LimitFramerateWhenUnfocused</mark> is true.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopRenderSettings.FramePacin: Whether to pace frames due to options like <mark=#ffffff10>MaximumBackgroundFramerate</mark> is being used.
messages.Tooltip.DesktopRenderSettings.Background: Whether to render new frames when in the background.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatQualitySettings: The Gaussian Splat Quality Settings component is used to control the quality of Gaussian Splats in a world.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatQualitySettings.Qua: The preset to use for quality of Gaussian splats.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatQualitySettings.Min: The preset to use for the minimum quality of Gaussian splats.
messages.Tooltip.GaussianSplatQualitySettings.Sor: The amount of sorting to do in different cameras rendering Gaussian splats.
messages.Tooltip.PostProcessingSettings.MotionBlu: Controls the strength of motion blur, disabled entirely if set to 0%.
messages.Tooltip.PostProcessingSettings.BloomInte: The Bloom effect causes a glow to appear around bright objects in the scene, disabled entirely if set to 0%.
messages.Tooltip.PostProcessingSettings.AmbientOc: Adds shading in corners and area that'd receive less light. Controls the strength of [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ambient_occlusion Ambient Occlusion], disabled entirely if set to 0%.
messages.Tooltip.PostProcessingSettings.ScreenSpa: When enabled, any parts of the scene currently visible to the camera will reflect off shiny surfaces in realtime.
messages.Tooltip.PostProcessingSettings.Antialias: Smooths out jagged edges on objects. Some may not work or be suitable for VR.
messages.Tooltip.RendererDecouplingSettings.Activ: How low the framerate from frooxengine needs to be to activate a decoupled frooxengine state
messages.Tooltip.RendererDecouplingSettings.Deact: How high the framerate from frooxengine needs to be to deactivate a decoupled frooxengine state
messages.Tooltip.RendererDecouplingSettings.Force: Whether or not to wait for frooxengine at all, and to just runaway render frames.
messages.Tooltip.RendererDecouplingSettings.Asset: How long to wait to process assets before continuing on the next frame anyways.
messages.Tooltip.RenderingQualitySettings.PerPixe: How many lights can affect a single pixel on screen.
messages.Tooltip.RenderingQualitySettings.ShadowC: How many shadows can be affecting an area.
messages.Tooltip.RenderingQualitySettings.ShadowR: The max resolution allowed for shadows.
messages.Tooltip.RenderingQualitySettings.ShadowD: The max distance to render shadows from the camera.
messages.Tooltip.RenderingQualitySettings.SkinWei: How many skin weights to allow for skinned mesh renderers.
messages.Tooltip.ResolutionSettings: The ResolutionSettings component is a Settings component.
messages.Tooltip.ResolutionSettings.Fullscreen: Whether or not the game should change to fullscreen mode.
messages.Tooltip.ResolutionSettings.WindowResolut: The resolution in pixels the main window should render at while in windowed mode.
messages.Tooltip.ResolutionSettings.FullscreenRes: The resolution in pixels the main window should render at while in full screen.
messages.Tooltip.ResolutionSettings.CommitedWindo: The resolution in pixels the main window is currently at while in windowed mode.
messages.Tooltip.ResolutionSettings.CommitedFulls: The resolution in pixels the main window is currently at while in full screen.
messages.Tooltip.ResolutionSettings.NeedsToApplyR: Whether or not the resolution settings need to be applied.
messages.Tooltip.TextureQualitySettings: The TextureQualitySettings component is part of the Settings menu and should be interacted with via such menu instead of through this component.
messages.Tooltip.TextureQualitySettings.TextureSi: The ratio at which textures should be loaded locally versus their original size on the cloud.
The maximum Texture Size that textures should be loaded at.
messages.Tooltip.TextureQualitySettings.MinimumTe: The minimum size textures loaded can be locally.
messages.Tooltip.TextureQualitySettings.DefaultFi: The default Texture mode that textures will be if they don't manually specify their Texture mode.
messages.Tooltip.TextureQualitySettings.Anisotrop: The Anisotropic levels textures should have.
messages.Tooltip.TextureQualitySettings.UseAnisot: Whether the Anisotropic levels should be used.
messages.Tooltip.ImportSettings: The Import Settings component handles how world links being pasted into the game should be imported.
messages.Tooltip.ImportSettings.SessionUrls: How to handle pasted session URLs.
messages.Tooltip.ImportSettings.WorldUrls: How to handle pasted world URLs.
messages.Tooltip.ImportSettings.NetworkUrls: How to handle pasted network URLs.
messages.Tooltip.DiscordIntegrationSettings: The DiscordIntegrationSettings component handles discord rich presence info communicated from Resonite. This is interacted with via the Settings system.
messages.Tooltip.DiscordIntegrationSettings.RichP: The level of information to communicate via discord rich presence.
messages.Tooltip.SteamIntegrationSettings.RichPre: The setting to use when doing steam presence.
messages.Tooltip.SteamIntegrationSettings.SaveScr: Whether to save screenshots to steam.
messages.Tooltip.SteamIntegrationSettings.ForceSt: Whether steam should force voice on remote playing via internet over connection to a pc remotely.
messages.Tooltip.CutenessSettings: This component is solely a joke, and is supposed to make a setting that marks U-GlitchFur as cute and cannot be changed. It only appears for that user.
messages.Tooltip.CutenessSettings.AmCute: An unchangeable bool that stays true for U-GlitchFur. The description reads: "This setting indicates if the current user is very cute. When this setting is on, the current user makes a legally binding admission of their own cuteness."
messages.Tooltip.DebugSettings: The DebugSettings component is a component updated during Resonite updates to contain bleeding edge settings for the game.
messages.Tooltip.DebugSettings.DebugInputBindings: Whether to display the user's controller inputs on their hands in userspace.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyFeatureSettings: The LegacyFeatureSettings component is best described on it's page, Settings.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyFeatureSettings.UseLegacyG: Toggles the ability to quick equip tools.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyFeatureSettings.UseLegacyW: Toggles the old way of switching between sessions.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyFeatureSettings.UseLegacyI: Toggles the Legacy Inventory window. (Use left grip + dash menu button in VR, or use Ctrl + i in Desktop).
messages.Tooltip.LegacyFeatureSettings.SuppressFe: This is to make certain features like posers that hijack feet proxies work, without having to modify them.
messages.Tooltip.MysterySettings: The Mystery Settings component is a component which was added in the April Fools update 2025.4.1.438 which contains multiple harmless jokes.
messages.Tooltip.MysterySettings.Difficulty: This controls Resonite gameplay difficulty. If you want more challenge when playing Resonite, you can turn on the Hard difficulty.
messages.Tooltip.MysterySettings.MirrorMirrors: When enabled, mirrors are mirrored, so when mirrors mirror the mirroring cancels out and mirrors just become rorrs.
messages.Tooltip.MysterySettings.PreciousToggleDu: How long the previous precious little toggle was held.
messages.Tooltip.MysterySettings.MysteriousMessag: Message for posterity: Something will appear here at some point.
messages.Tooltip.AssetGatherSettings: Allows the user to set and adjust the asset download settings within dash space.
messages.Tooltip.AssetGatherSettings.MaxConcurren: How many assets can be sent to or received from other players in parallel for the client
How many assets can be downloaded from the cloud at the same time.
messages.Tooltip.RealtimeNetworkingSettings.Disab: Whether or not to disable LAN
messages.Tooltip.RealtimeNetworkingSettings.LNL_W: The size of the LNL data stream.
messages.Tooltip.RelaySettings.AlwaysUseRelay: Relays are typically used as a fallback when a direct connection to the host cannot be established. By enabling this option, you will force the connection to always happen through a relay. This can be useful in cases of connection issues to particular hosts. It is NOT recommended to have this option permanently on.
messages.Tooltip.RelaySettings.UseClosestAvailabl: When this option is enabled, the closest available relay will be automatically selected. If you want to connect through a specific relay (e.g. in case of long distance connections to avoid packet queuing) you can disable this option and configure the preferred relays below.
messages.Tooltip.RelaySettings.RelayPriorities: A list of relays to use in order when trying to connect to a host.
messages.Tooltip.RelaySettings.RelayPrioritiesEna: Whether or not to use the relay priorities list.
messages.Tooltip.VideoStreamingServicesSettings: The VideoStreamingServicesSettings component handles settings for streaming service content downloading like allowing usage of browser cookies to get around the issue of "login to confirm you're not a bot" issue.
messages.Tooltip.VideoStreamingServicesSettings.U: allows usage of browser cookies to get around the issue of "login to confirm you're not a bot" issue.
messages.Tooltip.MediaPrivacySettings.MediaMetada: Requests for the user to opt out of being in media metadata like photos or microphone recordings when they are captured in world.
messages.Tooltip.MediaPrivacySettings.HideInScree: Requests the user be not rendered in photos when they are taken in world, including world preview thumbnails.
messages.Tooltip.MediaPrivacySettings.StripImageF: Strips the metadata from images that the user themselves brings into a scene.
messages.Tooltip.MessagingPrivacySettings.DoNotSe: Doesn't tell the cloud whether or not the user has read a message when enabled. Can prevent users from knowing you are online from the act of you reading a message even when appearing as offline.
messages.Tooltip.FavoritesSettings: The FavoritesSettings component is used to handle favorite settings in the settings tab inside the Dash Menu.
messages.Tooltip.FavoritesSettings.AutoLoadCloudH: Whether to automatically load the cloud home the user has set.
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatusSettings: The OnlineStatusSettings component is used to define how online status for the user should be handled.
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatusSettings.DefaultStat: The default status for the user when they log in.
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatusSettings.RememberMod: Whether the status the user had when they were last on should be remembered.
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatusSettings.RememberTim: How long to remember the last status from the previous login before setting back to default again.
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatusSettings.InvisibleRe: How to remember the previous status from the last login.
How long to remember a status if it's set to invisible.
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatusSettings.AutoAwayTim: How long to wait when the user is AFK before enabling Away.
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatusSettings.EnableDefau: Whether to enable the default status behavior.
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatusSettings.ShowRemembe: Whether to show the remember timespan control UI.
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatusSettings.ShowInvisib: Whether to show the remember invisible timespan control UI.
messages.Tooltip.UserMetricsSettings.UserHeight: The height of the user in meters.
messages.Tooltip.HostAccessSettings.Entries: A list of allowed and denied IP address locations.
messages.Tooltip.UserRestrictionsSettings: The UserRestrictionsSettings component is found in the user space and contains all blocked user entries. this is a good way to handle blocked users until they fix the regression of no longer having a blocked users list introduced in the settings update.
messages.Tooltip.SettingActionProxy`1: The SettingActionProxy component is used to trigger an action on a subsetting under the component of type S which would be globally registered in the world.
messages.Tooltip.SettingActionProxyBase`2.ActionN: The name of the action being triggered.
messages.Tooltip.SettingActionProxyBase`2.Subsett: The name of the sync delegate method being triggered.
The key being used to find the sub setting being triggered.
messages.Tooltip.SettingValueActionProxy`2: The SettingValueActionProxy component is used to trigger an action while passing a value to it on a subsetting under the component of type S which would be globally registered in the world.
messages.Tooltip.SettingManagersManager: The Setting Managers Manager component is used to manage the changing from cloud to local setting sets.
messages.Tooltip.SettingManagersManager.LocalSett: The manager handling locally stored settings.
messages.Tooltip.SettingManagersManager.CloudSett: The manager handling cloud stored settings.
messages.Tooltip.SettingValueSync`2: The SettingValueSync component allows for reading (And sometimes writing) a setting from a particular SyncingUser for the purpose of changing an item/world's appearance or behavior depending on that settings value.
messages.Tooltip.SettingValueSync`2.SyncingUser: The user to get the setting from.
messages.Tooltip.SettingValueSync`2.SettingName: The setting or setting group to get a value for. This is a path separated by ".".
messages.Tooltip.SettingValueSync`2.TargetField: The field to put the setting value into, can also write back to the setting if the world is unsafe or the setting allows for the world to write it.
messages.Tooltip.SettingValueSync`2.SubsettingGet: The subsetting method name to use to get a subsetting.
messages.Tooltip.SettingValueSync`2.SubsettingKey: The key to pass to the subsetting getter method found under the name specified by <mark=#ffffff10>SubsettingGetter</mark> in order to get a subsetting.
messages.Tooltip.CustomizationSettings: The CustomizationSettings component adds an option to the Settings menu to enable and disable UI Edit Mode.
messages.Tooltip.CustomizationSettings.UserInterf: Whether UI Edit Mode is enabled or not.
messages.Tooltip.DashSettings: The DashSettings component is part of the Settings menu and is used to change the settings of how the Dash works.
messages.Tooltip.DashSettings.DashCurvature: How much to curve the dash in VR.
messages.Tooltip.DashSettings.OpenCloseSpeed: How fast the animation should play when opening and closing the dash.
messages.Tooltip.DashSettings.AllowReplacingSetti: Allows you to remove panels from the settings menu, for usage as individual facets.
messages.Tooltip.EditSettings: The Edit Settings component is used to control Development tooling in world.
messages.Tooltip.EditSettings.ConfirmComponentDes: Whether the user gets a confirm dialog when destroying a component.
messages.Tooltip.EditSettings.ConfirmSlotDestroy: Whether the user gets a confirm dialog when destroying a slot.
messages.Tooltip.LaserSettings: The LaserSettings component is used in the Settings.
messages.Tooltip.LaserSettings.SmoothSpeed: Controls how fast the laser responds to your movements. Lower values will make the laser more sluggish, but better at filtering tremors. Higher values will be more responsive, but also transfer more controller shake.
messages.Tooltip.LaserSettings.ModulateStartAngle: Angle at which the laser smoothing speed will start modulating to catch up to your movements.
messages.Tooltip.LaserSettings.ModulateEndAngle: Related to the modulate start angle, this indicates when the modulation will reach maximum speed. By changing the start and end angles, you can control how responsive the laser is the more you move your hand.
messages.Tooltip.LaserSettings.ModulateExponent: Controls the response curve of the laser modulation between the start and end angles. You can use this to either make the laser respond more sluggishly at first or to start responding quickly and then tapering off.
messages.Tooltip.LaserSettings.ModulateSpeedMulti: The laser smoothing speed will be multiplied by this value once you have reached the modulate end angle. Increasing this value will make the laser catch up quicker once you move your hand far enough. Lowering the value will make it take longer to catch up.
messages.Tooltip.LaserSettings.StickThreshold: Controls how much the laser will stick to interactive items. Larger values will make it stick more. Setting this to zero will stop the stickiness completely.
messages.Tooltip.LaserSettings.ShowInDesktop: When on, the laser visual will be rendered to you fully when in first person view in desktop mode.
messages.Tooltip.LocaleSettings: The Locale Settings component is part of the Settings system and controls the platform's language.
messages.Tooltip.LocaleSettings.PrimaryInterfaceL: The primary language for the UI.
messages.Tooltip.LocaleSettings.CultureLocaleCode: The locale code for the primary language, Ex: "en_us", "ru", or "pl_PL"
messages.Tooltip.LocaleSettings.UseImperialUnits: Whether to use Imperial or Metric units regardless of language selection.
messages.Tooltip.NamePlateSettings.NameplateVisib: can be used to control what nameplates the user sees.
messages.Tooltip.NamePlateSettings.UseCustomNamep: Whether to render the default nameplates or user made ones.
messages.Tooltip.NotificationSettings.UserOnline: Whether to show notifications for users coming online.
messages.Tooltip.NotificationSettings.UserOnlineO: Whether to show notifications for users coming online while they are on a different version than you.
messages.Tooltip.NotificationSettings.UserSociabl: How to handle notifications for users turning to sociable mode.
messages.Tooltip.NotificationSettings.Message: How to handle notifications for messages.
messages.Tooltip.NotificationSettings.Invite: How to handle notifications for invites.
messages.Tooltip.NotificationSettings.ContactRequ: How to handle notifications for contact requests.
messages.Tooltip.NotificationSettings.InviteReque: How to handle notifications for invite requests.
messages.Tooltip.NotificationSettings.ContactSess: How to handle notifications for contact session startings.
messages.Tooltip.NotificationSettings.PublicSessi: How to handle notifications for public session startings.
messages.Tooltip.NotificationSettings.UserJoinAnd: How to handle notifications for users joining and leaving sessions.
messages.Tooltip.NotificationSettings.PermissionC: How to handle notifications for your permissions changing in a world.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureSettings: The PhotoCaptureSettings component is used to control the behavior of the PhotoCaptureManager and what it does.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureSettings.FingerGestu: Whether the finger photos should even render.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureSettings.NormalCaptu: The resolution of pictures taken without timer.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureSettings.TimerCaptur: The resolution of pictures taken with timer.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureSettings.TimerSecond: How long the timer should be for timed photos.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureSettings.CapturePriv: Whether finger photos should capture private ui elements like the dash.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureSettings.CaptureSter: Whether photos should be 3D with a left and right "eye".
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureSettings.StereoSepar: how far apart the left and right "eye" are when taking stereo photos.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureSettings.AlwaysHideN: Whether to always hide nameplates in taken photos.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureSettings.EncodeForma: The format taken photos should output as.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureSettings.PhotoAutosa: Where to auto save photos in the inventory to.
messages.Tooltip.PhotoCaptureSettings.AutosaveAct: Whether to use the auto save photos to inventory feature.
messages.Tooltip.SettingsDataFeed: The SettingsDataFeed component is a data feed that generates a list of settings and their categories.
messages.Tooltip.DebugSettingSwitchSource: The DebugSettingSwitchSource component is used to test settings profiles for switching between different profiles like desktop and VR switching.
messages.Tooltip.DebugSettingSwitchSource.Current: The profile of settings to use.
messages.Tooltip.DebugSettingSwitch: The DebugSettingSwitch component is used to Switch profiles as a way of debugging. Same thing switching between VR and desktop does. Only works in user space.
messages.Tooltip.DeviceIDSettingSwitchSource: The DeviceIDSettingSwitchSource component is used to switch setting sets when a different device is used in conjunction with a Component:DeviceIDSettingSwitch
messages.Tooltip.DeviceIDSettingSwitch: The DeviceIDSettingSwitch component is used to switch setting sets when a different device is used in conjunction with a Component:DeviceIDSettingSwitchSource
messages.Tooltip.DeviceIDSettingSwitch.DeviceID: The device ID currently active for the set of settings for the user.
messages.Tooltip.VR_ActiveSetttingSwitchSource: The VR_ActiveSetttingSwitchSource component works in tandem with the VR Active Setting Switch component. this component specifically acts as the source of enforcing a setting set switch from VR settings to desktop settings.
messages.Tooltip.VR_ActiveSettingSwitch: The VR_ActiveSettingSwitch component handles when the user switches between vr and deskop in order to switch to different setting sets.
messages.Tooltip.VR_ActiveSettingSwitch.VR_Active: Whether VR is currently active or not.
messages.Tooltip.UserPermissions: The UserPermissions component is used to control the permissions of users in a world for the selected roles.
messages.Tooltip.UserPermissions.AllowEnableEditM: Whether the selected roles can enable their Edit Mode.
messages.Tooltip.UserPermissions.AllowRespawn: Whether the selected roles can respawn other users.
messages.Tooltip.UserPermissions.AllowSilence: Whether the selected roles can silence other users.
messages.Tooltip.UserPermissions.AllowKick: Whether the selected roles can kick other users from the session.
messages.Tooltip.UserPermissions.AllowBan: Whether the selected roles can ban other users from this session.
messages.Tooltip.UserPermissions.AllowAssignRoles: Whether the selected roles can assign roles for other users that have a role less than them.
messages.Tooltip.UserPermissions.AllowSeeOtherRol: Whether the selected roles can see what roles other users have.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyCloudStorageSpaceIndicator: The LegacyCloudStorageSpaceIndicator component was used in old migrated legacy content that showed the cloud storage usage for a user. This component should not be used in new content and should be replaced when possible.
The source of the cloud usage data.
The color of the UI container.
The normal color when storage has been used.
The color to use when space is low.
The color to use when space is almost filled or beyond filled.
The percentage 0 to 1 for cloud storage to be considered low space.
The percentage from 0 to 1 for cloud storage to be considered critically full.
The text renderer that shows the owner of this cloud storage indicator.
The text renderer that shows the usage of the cloud storage for the user.
The text renderer that shows the percentage full for the cloud storage of the user.
The UI used to show the amount of storage used as a progress bar.
The legacy owner ID for what user this component was supposed to show the cloud storage for.
Whether this component was supposed to show the amount of storage a user was using for a group shared storage that they were allocated.
messages.Tooltip.GridContainerAnimatedEditVisual: The GridContainerAnimatedEditVisual component is used to handle the animation of turning on and off UI Edit Mode.
The image being used to show the grid alignment.
The position shift of the grid from default.
The color tint of the grid.
messages.Tooltip.EngineDebugDialog: The EngineDebugDialog component is used in the debug screen of the dash to show debug information about the game at the current moment.
messages.Tooltip.EngineDebugDialog._text: The text displaying the engine debug info.
messages.Tooltip.ContactsDialog: The ContactsDialog component is used in the contacts screen of the Dash Menu to search, view, and talk to contacts.
messages.Tooltip.ContactsDialog._searchBar: The search bar to search for users.
messages.Tooltip.ContactsDialog._sessionsRoot: The slot where the list of sessions from the selected user will be.
messages.Tooltip.ContactsDialog._messagesRoot: The slot where the list of messages from the selected user will be.
messages.Tooltip.ContactsDialog._status: The image icon being used for the selected user's status.
messages.Tooltip.ContactsDialog._avatar: The image icon being used for the selected user's avatar image.
messages.Tooltip.ContactsDialog._username: The text for the selected user's username.
messages.Tooltip.ContactsDialog._userActionsRoot: The root slot to store buttons for performing actions on the selected user.
messages.Tooltip.ContactsDialog._sendMessageButto: The button for sending the currently typed message to the selected user.
messages.Tooltip.ContactsDialog._sendVoiceMessage: The button for sending a voice message to the currently selected user.
messages.Tooltip.ContactsDialog._sendMessageTextF: The text field to type messages to send to users.
messages.Tooltip.ContactsDialog._messagesScrollRe: The scroll rectangle component for the scroll area for messages to and from the selected user.
messages.Tooltip.ContactsDialog._inviteButton: Invite the selected user to the currently focused session if possible.
messages.Tooltip.ContactsDialog._requestInviteBut: Ask for an invite from the selected user.
messages.Tooltip.ContactsDialog._banAllButton: Ban the selected user from all sessions that the current local user will host.
messages.Tooltip.ContactsDialog._banSessionButton: Ban the selected user from the current focused session.
messages.Tooltip.ContactsDialog._unblockedButton: The button to unblock the currently selected user.
messages.Tooltip.ContactsDialog._avatarBlockButto: The button to block the currently selected user's avatar.
messages.Tooltip.ContactsDialog._mutualBlockButto: The button to do a mutual block with the currently selected user.
messages.Tooltip.ContactItem: The ContactItem component is used in the contacts tab of the Dash Menu to be a contact in the list of contacts on that screen.
messages.Tooltip.CircleThumbnailItem._background: The UIX image handling the background for this session item.
messages.Tooltip.CircleThumbnailItem._statusIndic: The UIX image used to handle showing the status for this world like access level.
messages.Tooltip.CircleThumbnailItem._thumbnail: The thumbnail Component of that user's profile picture.
messages.Tooltip.CircleThumbnailItem._thumbnailTe: The image handling the session preview thumbnail image data.
messages.Tooltip.ContactItem._username: The text showing the user's username.
messages.Tooltip.ContactItem._status: The text showing the user's status.
messages.Tooltip.ContactItem._unreadCount: The text showing the number of unread messages from the user.
messages.Tooltip.ContactItem._joinButton: The button that can be used to join the user.
messages.Tooltip.ContactItem._rawUsername: The raw string of the user's username without ignore tags.
messages.Tooltip.ContactItem._alternateNames: A list of names the user may have had before.
messages.Tooltip.ContactLink: Add the ContactLink component to an object, clicking on that object will open up the Contacts Menu and will show the User page of the user defined in the UserId field.
messages.Tooltip.ContactLink.UserId: The User ID of the User whose contact page will be opened upon clicking the object.
messages.Tooltip.InviteRequestItem: The Invite Request Item component is used to handle the visual and behavior of invite requests received in chats from other users.
messages.Tooltip.InviteRequestItem.ContactDialog: The dialog component that this is under.
messages.Tooltip.InviteRequestItem.HeaderText: The text field that makes up the header for this request in the chat.
messages.Tooltip.InviteRequestItem.InviteButton: The button to invite the user or forward the invite to the next in command.
messages.Tooltip.InviteRequestItem.AddContactButt: The button to add the user who is asking for an invite as a contact.
messages.Tooltip.NotificationPanel: The NotificationPanel component is used in user space to show the User their notifications.
messages.Tooltip.NotificationPanel.DisplayDuratio: How long to show notifications for.
messages.Tooltip.NotificationPanel.Dash: The component for the dash in the same world.
messages.Tooltip.NotificationPanel._notificationC: The audio to play on a new notification made.
messages.Tooltip.NotificationPanel._contactReques: The audio to play on a new contact request being made.
messages.Tooltip.NotificationPanel._inviteClip: The audio to play on an invite clip being made.

messages.Tooltip.NotificationPanel._userLeaveClip: The audio to play when a user leaves.
messages.Tooltip.SessionItem: The SessionItem component is used to handle the session items in a world menu.

messages.Tooltip.SessionItem._sessionHost: The text visual being used to show the session host's name.
messages.Tooltip.SessionItem._userCount: The text visual being used to show the session user count.
messages.Tooltip.SessionItem._joinButton: The button that can be used to join the specified session.
messages.Tooltip.HyperlinkOpenDialog: The HyperlinkOpenDialog component shows in user space as a UIX when the user clicks on a HyperLink component.
messages.Tooltip.HyperlinkOpenDialog._hyperlinkTe: The text to fill with what is being accessed.
messages.Tooltip.HyperlinkOpenDialog._reasonText: The text to show the reason for the link dialog showing.
messages.Tooltip.HyperlinkOpenDialog._openButton: The button to open the link in <mark=#ffffff10>URL</mark> using the user's OS default browser.
messages.Tooltip.InventoryBrowser: The InventoryBrowser Component is the component that handles the Inventory screen.
messages.Tooltip.BrowserDialog.SelectedItem: The currently selected item being highlighted.
messages.Tooltip.BrowserDialog.AllowSelect: Whether this file browser allows selecting item elements.
messages.Tooltip.BrowserDialog.ItemSize: How big the items are on the view.
messages.Tooltip.BrowserDialog._selectedText: The text to fill with the name of the selected item.
messages.Tooltip.BrowserDialog._pathRoot: The root of the area being used to display the current path.

messages.Tooltip.BrowserDialog._itemGrid: The grid layout Component being used to align the files in the directory.

messages.Tooltip.BrowserDialog._loadingIndicator: The slot that stores the loading indicator for a newly opened directory.
messages.Tooltip.BrowserDialog._swapper: The component to handle the slide animation when opening a different directory.

messages.Tooltip.InventoryBrowser._lastSpecialIte: The last special item type that was selected in this inventory.
