messages.Tooltip.BrowserCreateDirectoryDialog._ca: A sync delegate to call when the Create Folder dialogue is confirmed.
messages.Tooltip.BrowserCreateDirectoryDialog._te: The text for the Create Directory dialouge box.
The text field that is the name of the new directory.
messages.Tooltip.InventoryItem: The InventoryItem component is used in the inventory browser to act as the spawnable items in the user's inventory.
messages.Tooltip.InventoryItem.RelativeToUserRoot: Spawn in item relative to user root for the user who is spawning it.
messages.Tooltip.InventoryItem.SavedRotation: The rotation of the saved item.
messages.Tooltip.InventoryItem.SavedScale: The scale of the saved item.
messages.Tooltip.InventoryItemUI: The InventoryItemUI component is used to represent an item or folder in the Inventory.
messages.Tooltip.BrowserItem.Browser: The source browser this item came from.
messages.Tooltip.BrowserItem.SelectedColor: The color this item's background should be when selected.
messages.Tooltip.BrowserItem.SelectedText: The color this item's text should be when selected.
messages.Tooltip.BrowserItem.NormalColor: The color this item's background should be at rest state
messages.Tooltip.BrowserItem.NormalText: The color this item's text would be at rest state.

messages.Tooltip.InventoryLink.TargetName: The name of the folder this component is linking to. This will change the display name of the folder when saved, but if shared by the same user again from their inventory, this will become what the cloud says the folder's name is and not the custom name it was when saved.

messages.Tooltip.SessionPermissionController: The SessionPermissionController component is used to change the roles or view the roles of a specific user in the current session.

messages.Tooltip.SessionUserController._respawn: The button used to respawn the target  user.
messages.Tooltip.SessionUserController._silence: The button used to silence the target user.
messages.Tooltip.SessionUserController._kick: The button used to kick the target user.
messages.Tooltip.SessionUserController._ban: The button used to ban the target user.
messages.Tooltip.UserspaceFacetAnchor: The UserspaceFacetAnchor component is used to control the user facet anchors on the head, hands, and arms of the user in user space.

messages.Tooltip.UserspaceFacetAnchor.BackgroundM: The material used for the canvas background.

messages.Tooltip.FacetAnchorsSettings.ShowContain: Whether to show the background visual of facet anchors.
messages.Tooltip.UserspaceFacetAnchorsManager: To know what this controls, see Facet Anchors.

messages.Tooltip.UserspaceFacetAnchorsManager.Use: Whether to allow the use of facet anchors.

messages.Tooltip.UserspaceFacetAnchorsManager.Ani: How fast the facet anchors should open or close.
messages.Tooltip.UserspaceFacetAnchorsManager.Das: The dash that is in the world this component is managing facet anchors for.
messages.Tooltip.UserspaceFacetAnchorsManager.Pro: The name of the profile to use when loading the facet anchors.
messages.Tooltip.UserspaceFacetAnchorsManager.Anc: A list of facet anchors and their data.
messages.Tooltip.UserspaceRadiantDash: The UserspaceRadiantDash is better described on the dedicated page, Dash Menu.

messages.Tooltip.UserspaceRadiantDash.Freeform: Whether the dash can be grabbed around in freeform style.

messages.Tooltip.UserspaceRadiantDash._dashVisual: The root slot for the visuals of the radiant dash.
messages.Tooltip.UserspaceRadiantDash._positioner: Handles positioning the dash in front of the user while it is in user space.
messages.Tooltip.UserspaceRadiantDash._modalOverl: Handles managing the different overlays on different screens like the home tab.
messages.Tooltip.UserspaceRadiantDash._legacyInve: Legacy. not used in newer versions. From a time before time when UIX didn't exist.
Legacy. not used in newer versions. From a time before time when UIX didn't exist.
messages.Tooltip.UserspaceRadiantDash._slider: The slider used to move the dash around via grabbing it.
messages.Tooltip.UserspaceRadiantDash._lookat: The lookat component that handles keeping the dash facing the user.

messages.Tooltip.UserspaceRadiantDash._alwaysOnFa: This slot stores elements of the dash that were made on a facet that are marked as always on. Things that end up here will have a Always On Facet Module Component so they always stay enabled even when the dash closes.
messages.Tooltip.UserspaceRadiantDash._screensWor: The workspace for screens that should be saved when the user's dash gets saved. helps preserve dash customization between machines.
messages.Tooltip.UserspaceRadiantDash._topWorkspa: The workspace for top area elements that should be saved when the user's dash gets saved. helps preserve dash customization between machines.
messages.Tooltip.UserspaceRadiantDash._notificati: The component that handles notifications for the game for the current session and people coming online.
The slot that contains the entirety of the notifications stack and logic.
The slot at which notification elements should appear like users joining and role changing.
messages.Tooltip.UserspaceScreensManager: The UserspaceScreensManager component is used in the user space and handles login status and unread notifications.
messages.Tooltip.UserspaceScreensManager._loginSt: The current login status of the user.
messages.Tooltip.UserspaceScreensManager._unreadI: How many unread notifications the user has, which is driven to the specified text element.
messages.Tooltip.SimpleVirtualKeyboard: The SimpleVirtualKeyboard component makes a simple keyboard UI that can be used.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualCloseKey: Closes the Keyboard in Keyboard field when it receives a button event. Using a button on the same slot of this component and pressing it will make this component receive said event.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualKey: Virtual key is a component that turns a button event into a button press on the keyboard in game as if it came from the physical keyboard.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualKey.AppendString: What text to append if the user is focused into a text field
messages.Tooltip.VirtualKey.ShiftTargetKey: What key to use if the Virtual Shift component is taking effect, or shift is held on the physical keyboard.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualKey.ShiftAppendString: What text to append if the user is focused into a text field and if the Virtual Shift component is taking effect, or shift is held on the physical keyboard.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualKey.IgnoreShift: Whether to ignore shift and virtual shift keys entirely.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualKey.ModifierKey: A key that can act as a special modifier (for example, if you wanted a key on your VR keyboard that appended "Cat!!" every time  you type a letter)
messages.Tooltip.VirtualKey.ModifiedTargetKey: The key to use instead of <mark=#ffffff10>TargetKey</mark> when <mark=#ffffff10>ModifiedTargetKey</mark> is pressed down.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualKey.ModifiedAppendString: hat text to append if the user is focused into a text field and the key specified by <mark=#ffffff10>ModifiedTargetKey</mark> is pressed down.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualKey.Keyboard: The keyboard object this key is a part of.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualKeyboard: The Virtual Keyboard is a component that groups many Keys, Modifier Keys, and Multi Keys together.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualKeyboard.ShiftActive: Whether the Virtual Shift key is toggled to pressed.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualKeyboard.HoldShift: Whether the Virtual Shift key is held.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualKeyboard.TextPreviewActiv: drives the visibility of the preview text display.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualKeyboard.TextPreview: The text preview object that shows what the user is typing.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualKeyTextDrive: This component takes a VirtualKey component and uses it's settings to drive a textfield's content to match what that key would type when pressed.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualKeyTextDrive.Key: The virtual key to source the text from (so if the virtual key is set to "d" it will drive the target of <mark=#ffffff10>Text</mark> to "d".
messages.Tooltip.VirtualModifierKey: Virtual Modifier Key is a way of making Virtual Keys change what they type when pressed.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualModifierKey.Keyboard: The keyboard this key is a part of.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualModifierKey.State: Whether this modifier key is active or not, kind of like if CAPSLOCK was active.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualMultiKey: This component when triggered by a button event will make the user press all the keys in the multikey at the same time.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualMultiKey.Keys: A list of keys that should be simulated being pressed when receiving a button event and they will be simulated by the source user of the button event.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualShift: Simulates pressing or holding onto the shift key on a normal physical keyboard.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualShift.Keyboard: The keyboard which the keys should be set or unset from the holding shift state.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualShift.HoldPressInterval: what value <mark=#ffffff10>_lastPress</mark> has to be below when the button is pressed again to enable <mark=#ffffff10>AlwaysHold</mark> field.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualShift.AlwaysHold: Whether the user has double clicked shift and enabled a constant hold shift state.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualShift._lastPress: Internal, how many seconds has passed since this button was last pressed. is used to handle enabling/disabling <mark=#ffffff10>AlwaysHold</mark> field.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualShiftColorDriver: The VirtualShiftColorDriver component is used to change a color field whenever a keyboard specified has its Shift key state changed.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualShiftColorDriver.Keyboard: The keyboard to monitor the shirt state for.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualShiftColorDriver.ColorTar: The field to set the color of depending on keyboard state.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualShiftColorDriver.NormalCo: The color to set the field in <mark=#ffffff10>ColorTarget</mark> to when <mark=#ffffff10>Keyboard</mark> is in default state.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualShiftColorDriver.ShiftCol: The color to set the field in <mark=#ffffff10>ColorTarget</mark> to when <mark=#ffffff10>Keyboard</mark>'s Shift key is toggled on like capslock.
messages.Tooltip.VirtualShiftColorDriver.HoldColo: The color to set the field in <mark=#ffffff10>ColorTarget</mark> to when <mark=#ffffff10>Keyboard</mark>'s Shift key is being held down.
messages.Tooltip.LegacySessionOrbsController: The LegacySessionOrbsController component displays session orbs of current sessions into a slot. This component was used in what some players called the "Session plate" which would have many world orbs of active sessions.

The height of spawning orbs in a cylinder shape.
The offset of the spawn cylinder shape in local space vs the center on <mark=#ffffff10>_root</mark>

messages.Tooltip.LegacySessionOrbsController.MaxO: How many orbs can be under <mark=#ffffff10>_root</mark> at any one time.

messages.Tooltip.WorldSwitcher._visualRoot: The root slot for all the visuals of the switcher.

messages.Tooltip.WorldOrbSaver.interactive: Whether this item is interactive or not.
messages.Tooltip.WorldOrbSaver.saving: Whether this item is currently saving to the user's inventory.

messages.Tooltip.MigrationDialog._migrateFavorite: Whether to migrate favorites from the account or not.

messages.Tooltip.MigrationDialog._migrateMessageH: Whether to migrate message history from the account or not.

messages.Tooltip.MigrationDialog._swapRegion: Thw region used for animation when switching between different screens in the migration dialog

messages.Tooltip.TOTP_Dialog._state: The screen this is currently showing.
messages.Tooltip.TOTP_Dialog._codeField: The text field the accepts codes in the TOTP protocol.
messages.Tooltip.TOTP_Dialog._messageText: The text element showing messages to the user.

messages.Tooltip.TwoFactorCodeDialog._code: The text field used to enter the 2FA code.
messages.Tooltip.UserspaceLaserPriority: The UserspaceLaserPriority component is used in dash space only.
messages.Tooltip.LocalVariableSync`1: The LocalVariableSync component is used to store a value to the local dB for the user space and load it up again.
messages.Tooltip.LocalVariableSync`1.OwnerUser: The user that this variable is associated with in the world.
messages.Tooltip.LocalVariableSync`1.Value: The value to Sync with the local DB variable.
messages.Tooltip.LocalVariableSync`1.Variable: The name of the local DB variable.
messages.Tooltip.LoadingSphereThumbnail: The Loading Sphere Thumbnail component is used to provide a texture for when the thumbnail for the preview of a world is not yet made or is still loading.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadProgress: The WorldLoadProgress component is the component that controls the WorldLoadingProgressInterface behavior.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadProgress._visual: The root slot for the progress indicator and visual.
messages.Tooltip.WorldLoadProgress.ProgressIndica: The loading indicator for this component.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayManager: The OverlayManager component is used to handle the Overlay for edit mode on userspace dash screens.
messages.Tooltip.OverlayManager._overlayRoot: The slot that has the Overlay visuals.
messages.Tooltip.ScreenModeController: The ScreenModeController component is used to control the interactions of the user within user space also known as the dash world.
messages.Tooltip.ScreenModeController._dash: The dash of the dash world.
messages.Tooltip.ScreenModeController._muteSound: The audio clip to play when the user mutes themselves.
messages.Tooltip.ScreenModeController._unmuteSoun: The audio clip to play when the user unmutes themselves.
messages.Tooltip.ScreenModeController._startTalkS: The audio clip to play when the user starts to record a voice message.
messages.Tooltip.ScreenModeController._stopTalkSo: The audio clip to play when the user stops recording a voice message.
messages.Tooltip.TransferGrabbable: Transfer Grabbable is a component that is used to mark objects that undergo Transfer grabbing, but only while in Userspace. The grabber during this process saves the object into memory including materials, then simulates a paste as it puts the object into the target world.
messages.Tooltip.TransferGrabbable.PasteOnGrab: Whether to trigger a paste event in userspace when transfering the object to user space. does not affect when releasing.
messages.Tooltip.TransferGrabbable._indicatorMate: The indicator material to use when transfering to worlds.
messages.Tooltip.UserInterfaceEditModeSync: Allows a world to tell if a user is currently in UI edit mode.
messages.Tooltip.UserInterfaceEditModeSync.Target: The user to check UI edit mode for.
messages.Tooltip.UserInterfaceEditModeSync.EditMo: Whether <mark=#ffffff10>TargetUser</mark> is in UI edit mode.
messages.Tooltip.Userspace: Userspace is a component that manages some of the tasks like exiting, saving, and emergency desktop shortcuts.
messages.Tooltip.UserspacePointer: The UserspacePointer component is used in user space and is used to control the behavior of the user space lasers for interacting with user space elements like the dash menu.
messages.Tooltip.AudioDeviceVolume: The AudioDeviceVolume component only functions in dash space. If AudioDeviceIndex is negative, then it uses the user's set default audio device. This component is used to manage the volume of an input device like a microphone for one example. This is a settings component. <!--(confirm is this a setting component?)-->
messages.Tooltip.AudioDeviceVolume.AudioDeviceInd: The audio input device by OS audio manager index to modify the volume for.
messages.Tooltip.AudioDeviceVolume.Volume: The volume the input device should have.
messages.Tooltip.AudioDeviceVolume.NormalizedVolu: The component uses this value to boost the output noise to a higher volume when it is too quiet or reduce the volume when it is too loud.
messages.Tooltip.AudioDeviceVolume.Smoothing: How fast to reduce or increase the volume when it gets too loud or quiet
messages.Tooltip.AudioDeviceVolume.Power: The amount to boost the volume overall before normalization.
messages.Tooltip.AudioSettingSync: The AudioSettingSync component only works in dash space. This handles the settings of the game when it's values are changed.
messages.Tooltip.AudioSettingSync.DefaultAudioInp: The audio device that should be the default
messages.Tooltip.AudioSettingSync.MasterVolume: Controls the volume of everything in Resonite
messages.Tooltip.AudioSettingSync.WhisperVoiceVol: Controls the volume of sounds outside of your whisper sphere when using whisper mode.
messages.Tooltip.AudioSettingSync.NoiseGateThresh: Noise gate prevents background noises from being transmitted to other users. This setting indicates the maximum volume at which sounds will be filtered out. If too much noise is making it in, increase this setting, if your voice is not getting through, lower it.
messages.Tooltip.AudioSettingSync.NoiseGateAttack: This indicates how fast the noise gate responds to an incoming sound that is louder than the threshold volume. Larger values will result in a slower volume fade in, while smaller ones will make the volume ramp up quicker.
messages.Tooltip.AudioSettingSync.NoiseGateHold: This controls how long it takes for the noise gate to begin to close after the microphone input volume falls below the threshold volume.
messages.Tooltip.AudioSettingSync.NoiseGateReleas: This setting controls how fast the noise gate closes after the incoming audio has quieted again.
messages.Tooltip.AudioSettingSync.NormalizationTh: Using this setting you can control the threshold when the incoming audio will become amplified. If your normal speech is too quiet and it's not getting amplified, lower this setting. If random quiet noises are being amplified, increase it.
messages.Tooltip.AudioSettingSync.VoiceNormalizat: Whether to normalize a user's voice or not.
messages.Tooltip.AudioSettingSync.NoiseSupression: This feature processes the incoming audio to filter out various undesirable noises. This can clean up audio from noisy microphones considerably, producing a cleaner voice. We strongly recommend keeping this setting on. However, it can also filter out certain noises that do not get identified as speech.
messages.Tooltip.FileBrowser: Not used directly by the user.
messages.Tooltip.FileBrowser._user: The user using this file browser.
messages.Tooltip.FileBrowser._importButton: The button to import a selected folder or file.
messages.Tooltip.FileBrowser._rawImportButton: The button to import a selected file as a raw file object.
messages.Tooltip.FileBrowser._createNewButton: The button to create a new file directory.
messages.Tooltip.FileBrowser._reloadButton: The button used to refresh the folder and file list.
messages.Tooltip.FileSystemItem: The FileSystemItem component is a type of Type:BrowserItem used for system files or inventory items being viewed on the dash menu.
messages.Tooltip.FileSystemItem.Name: The name of the
messages.Tooltip.FileSystemItem.BasePath: The base directory of the item.
messages.Tooltip.FileSystemItem.Type: Whether if this is a file or folder.
messages.Tooltip.LocalUserLiveStatus: The LocalUserLiveStatus component outputs whether the local user is live or not.
messages.Tooltip.LocalUserLiveStatus.IsLive: Outputs whether the local user is live or not.
messages.Tooltip.PerformanceMetrics: The PerformanceMetrics component is used to show performance for a particular User at the present moment.
messages.Tooltip.PerformanceMetrics.User: The user we are measuring performance for.
messages.Tooltip.PerformanceMetrics.RenderTime: <mark=#ffffff10>User</mark>'s time it takes to render the scene in seconds.
messages.Tooltip.PerformanceMetrics.TotalEngineUp: <mark=#ffffff10>User</mark>'s time it takes to update the engine in seconds.
messages.Tooltip.UserTrackingSpaceSync: User Tracking Space Sync is a component that provides the local user's height, and whether they are currently using seated mode.
messages.Tooltip.UserTrackingSpaceSync.SeatedMode: whether the local user is using seated mode.
messages.Tooltip.UserTrackingSpaceSync.UserHeight: the local user's height in meters.
messages.Tooltip.VoiceModeSync: Attach to a slot that would exist in userspace, such as a facet.
messages.Tooltip.VoiceModeSync.FocusedWorldVoiceM: The voice mode set for the current world.
messages.Tooltip.VoiceModeSync.GlobalMute: Whether the user is muted for everyone.
messages.Tooltip.VoiceModeSync.FocusedWorldMaxAll: The maximum voice mode their allowed by the role the user has in the current focused world.
messages.Tooltip.VoiceModeSync.BroadcastAllowed: Whether broadcast is allowed for the user voice modes for the user in the currently focused world.
messages.Tooltip.VoiceModeSync.ShoutAllowed: Whether shout is allowed for the user voice modes for the user in the currently focused world.
messages.Tooltip.VoiceModeSync.NormalAllowed: Whether normal is allowed for the user voice modes for the user in the currently focused world.
messages.Tooltip.VoiceModeSync.WhisperAllowed: Whether whisper is allowed for the user voice modes for the user in the currently focused world.
messages.Tooltip.WorldValueSync`1: The WorldValueSync component is used to sync values from world space to user space.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamController: The AudioStreamController component is an old legacy component.

messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamController.AudioOutpu: The audio output being used to play the stream.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamController.IsPlayingF: Whether the audio stream is playing for the user who spawned it.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamSettings: The AudioStreamSettings component is a Legacy component used for the old settings menu.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamSettings.DefaultBitra: The default bit rate for this Audio stream when spawned by the user.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamSpawner: The AudioStreamSpawner component is used as part of the dash to spawn a selected audio stream.
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamSpawner.BitrateKbps: The bitrate the audio stream should be
messages.Tooltip.AudioStreamSpawner.DeviceID: the device name of the stream we are spawning
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreatorSpawner: Avatar Creator Spawner is a component that works in either userspace or real space. This component recieves button events, which means it works when on the same slot of any button. When it recieves a button press event, this component spawns a fully set up Avatar Creator in the world.
messages.Tooltip.FullBodyCalibratorSpawner: The FullBodyCalibratorSpawner when on the same Slot as a button, spawns a full body calibrator UI in front of the user that pressed the button.
messages.Tooltip.GenericModalDialogSpawner`1: The GenericModalDialogSpawer component works in world space. The Generic type it takes has to be a component type. This component activates when on the same slot as a button. When activated, this component attaches a new instance of T to the world, triggers Initializer with the new Component as an argument, and then positions it in front of the user.
messages.Tooltip.GenericModalDialogSpawner`1.Init: The Sync delegate to call with a
messages.Tooltip.GenericModalDialogSpawner`1.Clos: Whether to close the Dialog on click.
Whether to close the Dialog when opening the context menu.
messages.Tooltip.GenericUserspaceDialogSpawner`1: The GenericUserspaceDialogSpawner only works in dash space. The Generic type it takes has to be a component type. This component activates when on the slot as a button. When activated, this component attaches a new instance of T to the world, triggers Initializer with the new Component as an argument, and then positions it in front of the user in dash space. It needs a Component:ModalOverlayManager above it in order to work.
An action that takes a Component type.
messages.Tooltip.InteractiveCameraSpawner: The InteractiveCameraSpawner can receive Button Events, including ones from a button on the same slot. When receiving a pressed event, this component will spawn an InteractiveCamera in the currently focused world.
messages.Tooltip.DebugTrackingAligner: The DebugTrackingAligner calls the align tracking for all users in the world every 0.5 seconds if they have a slot called "Bases" with bases "Base 0" and "Base 1" under them.
messages.Tooltip.WorldConfigurationPermissions: The WorldConfigurationPermissions component is used to handle the Permissions of roles to change world configuration values like max user count and world name.
messages.Tooltip.WorldConfigurationPermissions.Al: Whether changes are allowed for the selected roles.
messages.Tooltip.Driver`1: The Driver component drives a value field via a protoflux output. This is used in ProtoFlux to utilize output calculations.
messages.Tooltip.Driver`1.Source: The node output to get a value from.
messages.Tooltip.Driver`1.Target: The value to drive with the output acquired from <mark=#ffffff10>Source</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.GlobalValue`1: When not using ProtoFlux, this component has no use over more idiomatic components such as a ValueField.
messages.Tooltip.GlobalValue`1.Value: The underlying value of the global.
messages.Tooltip.GlobalReference`1: When not using ProtoFlux, This component has no use over more idiomatic components such as a ReferenceField.
messages.Tooltip.GlobalReference`1.Reference: The underlying reference of the global.
messages.Tooltip.GlobalDelegate`1: When not using ProtoFlux, This component has no use over more idiomatic components such as a ReferenceField<SyncField<WorldDelegate>>.
messages.Tooltip.GlobalDelegate`1.Delegate: The underlying delegate of the global.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeDebugInfo: The ProtoFluxNodeDebugInfo component is used to gather Debug Info on a particular ProtoFlux node and it's group.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeDebugInfo.Node: The ProtoFlux node to get Debug Info on.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeDebugInfo.IsBuilt: Whether the protoflux node group this is in is built.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeDebugInfo.IndexInGr: The node's position in its group.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeDebugInfo.Allocatio: The allocation index of this node in its group.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeDebugInfo.GroupName: The autogenerated name of the node group.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeDebugInfo.GroupIsVa: Whether the group this node is a part of is valid.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeDebugInfo.GroupNode: The number of nodes in the group this node is a part of.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeDebugInfo.NodeInsta: The hash of this node instance.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeDebugInfo.GroupRegi: Whether the group this node is a part of is registered for continuous changes. See ContinuouslyChanging
Whether this group is registered for updates.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeDebugInfo.NodeConti: Whether the node this component is debugging is continously changing itself. See ContinuouslyChanging.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxTool: For more info on the protoflux tool in general, see ProtoFlux and ProtoFlux Tool.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxTool.SpawnNodeType: The type of the ProtoFlux node to spawn.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxTool.WirePoint: The slot that the tool is currently pulling a wire out of.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxTool.MaxConnectDistance: The distance this protoflux tool will search for nodes from it's tip before stopping. it's usually 20 meters in local space.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxTool.SelectionProgress: How far the user is to selecting nodes by holding Secondary
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxTool.HoveringElementNam: The name of the protoflux element this tool is currently hovering over
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxTool.HoveringElementCon: The type of the protoflux element this tool is currently hovering over
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxTool._selectedNodes: The list of nodes the protoflux nodes that the tool currently has selected.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxTool._wirePointPosition: What field to drive on a StripeMesh for a currently pulled wire.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxTool._text: The text to drive with information from this tool.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxTool._currentProxy: What element the protoflux tool is currently pulling in order to connect to another element via the UI.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxTool._currentTempWire: The current wire that this tool is pulling from a protoflux connector.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxTool._currentCutLine: The current wire that this tool is trying to cut.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxTool._cutLineScale: The current wire that this tool is trying to cut's scale.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxTool._cutLineOrientatio: The orientation of the current wire that this tool is trying to cut.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxTool._currentHighlighte: The current node this tool is looking at.
messages.Tooltip.ProtofluxArrowManager: The ProtofluxArrowManager component is used to drive and create the visuals for arrows pointing from/to variables and where they are being used.
messages.Tooltip.ProtofluxArrowManager.ConnectNod: The node the arrow should point to.
messages.Tooltip.ProtofluxArrowManager.TargetSize: The size field of the target.
messages.Tooltip.ProtofluxArrowManager.TargetOffs: The offset field of the target.
messages.Tooltip.ProtofluxArrowManager._arrowMesh: The arrow mesh this is modifying.
messages.Tooltip.ProtofluxArrowManager._targetVec: The field for the target vector on the arrow mesh.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeVisual: The ProtoFluxNodeVisual component is used to manage the visuals and interaction with ProtoFlux nodes when they are unpacked.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeVisual.Node: The protoflux node this is a visual for.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeVisual.IsSelected: Whether or not this node is selected.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeVisual.IsHighlighte: Whether or not this node is highlighted.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeVisual._nodeHoverAr: The Hover interaction area for this visual.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeVisual._bgImage: The background image for tye protoflux visual.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeVisual._inputsRoot: The root of the different slots for node inputs.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeVisual._outputsRoot: The root of the different slots for node outputs.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeVisual._referencesR: The slot for references to other nodes like arrows for protoflux variables/globals.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeVisual._overviewVis: The field for enabling the overview alt version of this node.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeVisual._overviewBg: The BG color of the overview version of the visual.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeVisual._labelBg: The enabled field of the label BG.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxNodeVisual._labelText: The enabled field of the label text.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxWireManager: The ProtoFluxWireManager component is used to drive the visual elements of protoflux wires which connect protoflux nodes.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxWireManager.ConnectPoin: The point this is supposed to be pointing to.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxWireManager.Type: The type of wire this is supposed to be showing.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxWireManager.Width: The width this wire should be.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxWireManager.StartColor: The color this wire should start at. usually used for async impulse wires.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxWireManager.EndColor: The end color this wire should end at. usually used for async impulse wires.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxWireManager.DeleteHighl: Whether to delete the highlight visual.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxWireManager._wireMesh: The stripe mesh this is modifying.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxWireManager._renderer: The renderer of the stripe mesh this is modifying.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxWireManager._collider: The collider of this stripe mesh visual.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxWireManager._targetPosi: The field to drive with the target position for the stripe mesh.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxWireManager._targetTang: The field to drive with the target tangent for the stripe mesh.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxWireManager._targetOrie: The field to drive with the target orientation for the stripe mesh.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxWireManager._targetWidt: The field to drive with the target width for the stripe mesh.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxGlobalRefProxy: The ProtoFluxGlobalRefProxy component allows for a piece of UI to act as a relay for interacting with global value component.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxRefProxy.Node: The node this is making a visual for.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxRefProxy.ElementName: the name of the target component.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxRefProxy.ValueType: the type of the value input this is pointing to.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxRefProxy._label: The field to drive with global value properties to make a name.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxRefProxy._proxyVisual: The Button used for the proxy surrounding visual.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxRefProxy._refProxySourc: the component that handles grabbing the value of the field this points to via UIX.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxGlobalRefProxy.TargetGl: The target global reference this visual has been made for.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxGlobalRefProxy._current: The current proxy this is using.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxImpulseProxy: The ProtoFluxImpulseProxy component interacts with the ProtoFlux tool and ProtoFlux in general to act as the relay for attaching impulses to ProtoFlux nodes.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxElementProxy.Node: The ProtoFlux component whose output you wish to proxy.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxElementProxy.ElementNam: The name that appears when you hover this output with a Flux Tool.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxElementProxy.IsDynamic: Whether this output can be converted by inputting a different type.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxElementProxy.Index: The index in a list if this points to a list of inputs/outputs.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxElementProxy.ConnectPoi: the slot to attach the wire end being connected.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxWireProxy`1.Wire: The wire Manager handling the visual for the connection to this connector.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxImpulseProxy.NodeImpuls: The impulse value field to target.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxImpulseProxy.ImpulseTyp: the type of impulse this is for.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxInputProxy: The ProtoFluxInputProxy component is used to manage the input sockets of protoflux nodes, making components become the actual visuals the user uses to interact with protoflux with. Otherwise, users would have to use purely the developer tool.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxInputProxy.NodeInput: The field on a protoflux node Component this connector is being used as a way to connect to.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxInputProxy.InputType: The type of the input this proxy is for.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxOperationProxy: The ProtoFluxOperationProxy component is used to allow plugging impulse wires into protoflux visuals.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxOperationProxy.NodeOper: The node operation target (impulse target) this is a connector for.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxOperationProxy.IsAsync: Whether the impulse input type is async.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxOutputProxy: The ProtoFluxOutputProxy component is used to manage the output sockets of protoflux nodes, making components become the actual visuals the user uses to interact with protoflux with. Otherwise, users would have to use purely the developer tool.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxOutputProxy.NodeOutput: The field on a protoflux node Component this connector is being used as a way to connect to.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxOutputProxy.OutputType: The type of the output this proxy is for.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxReferenceProxy: The ProtoFluxReferenceProxy component is used to usually manage global references like on write node write targets and the monitoring slot on the slot events node.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxReferenceProxy.NodeRefe: The node the value field points to, if it should point to a protoflux node like for a write node.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxReferenceProxy.Arrow: the arrow pointing to a protoflux node if the value field's value points to a protoflux node.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxReferenceProxy.ConnectP: The slot that should act as the connector point.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxReferenceProxy._current: The field to drive with the current name of the value that this component's value field is pointing to.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxReferenceProxy._selfHov: The field to drive with whether or not this element is being hovered over.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxReferenceProxy._targetH: The field to drive with whether or not the element this is targeting is a node and is being hovered over.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxReferenceProxy._arrowMa: The field to drive in order to enable/disable the arrow manager.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxReferenceProxy._arrowRe: The field to drive in order to enable/disable the arrow manager's arrow visual.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxImpulseListManager: The ProtoFluxImpulseListManager component is used to manage lists and their visuals like in the sequence ProtoFlux node.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxDynamicElementManager.V: The visual of the protoflux node this is making a list for.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxDynamicElementManager.L: The list this is managing and making a UI for.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxDynamicElementManager.M: The minimum number of items allowed in the list.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxDynamicElementManager.A: The enabled field of the add elements button.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxDynamicElementManager.R: The enabled field of the remove elements button.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxDynamicElementManager._: The list of UI elements being used to display the elements in <mark=#ffffff10>List</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxImpulseListManager.Impu: The type of impulse this is using (ex: Async or normal)
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxInputListManager: The ProtoFluxInputListManager component is used to manage a list of inputs on a ProtoFlux visual.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxInputListManager.InputT: The C# Type of the list of inputs for the list this is managing.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxOperationListManager: The ProtoFluxOperationListManager component is used for the visuals in the ProtoFlux:Sequence and ProtoFlux:Async Sequence nodes.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxOperationListManager.Su: Whether the elements support Async input/output.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxOutputListManager: The ProtoFluxOutputListManager component is used to manage a list of outputs on a ProtoFlux visual.
messages.Tooltip.ProtoFluxOutputListManager.Outpu: The C# Type of the list of outputs for the list this is managing.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchor: An anchor allows for an avatar to be attached to a slot.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchor.Highlight: Whether a user is pointing at this anchor and it is possible for them to activate the enter dialogue by pressing Primary at this moment.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchor.ParentSpace: The coordinate space in which to work with the avatar.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchor.MinScale: If an avatar has a scale below the MinScale, it will be scaled up to that size.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchor.MaxScale: If an avatar has a scale above the MaxScale, it will be scaled down to that size.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchor.PositionNode: Which part of the avatar to use to position the avatar.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchor.PositionReference: Where to position the avatar to.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchor.RotationNode: Which part of the avatar to use to rotate the avatar.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchor.RotationReference: Where to rotate the avatar to.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchor.PreserveUpOnEnter: Whether to keep the user's root slot's up direction pointing the same direction it was in global space before entering, or to align them to the up of the slot of the anchor. This is disabled when <mark=#ffffff10>RotationNode</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>RotationReference</mark> are set to not none.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchor.PreserveUpOnExit: Whether to keep the user's root slot's up direction pointing the same direction it was in global space before exiting, or to align them to the up of the slot they get parented back under. This is disabled when <mark=#ffffff10>TransformRestoreMode</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>RestoreNode</mark> are set to not none.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchor.UnparentEverythingO: Unparent all slots under <mark=#ffffff10>ParentSpace</mark>'s Space slot and parent them under the parent of the nearest ObjectRoot (doesn't need an explicit ObjectRoot component). If <mark=#ffffff10>ParentSpace</mark>'s Space slot is null, or the ObjectRoot parent is null, then all children are deleted anyway.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchor.TransformRestoreMod: What slot or space to use when setting the user's transforms to what they were before they entered the anchor.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchor.RestoreNode: The part of the user's avatar that the transforms should be changed to <mark=#ffffff10>_originalPosition</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>_originalRotation</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>_originalScale</mark> when the user exits the anchor. Which space the transforms refer to (aka what it would be parented to) depends on what <mark=#ffffff10>TransformRestoreMode</mark> is set to.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchor.RestoreReference: This will be used from when <mark=#ffffff10>TransformRestoreMode</mark> is set to "Reference". The specified <mark=#ffffff10>RestoreNode</mark>'s transform will be set to this slot's space using <mark=#ffffff10>_originalPosition</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>_originalRotation</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>_originalScale</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchor.Filters: A list of pose filters to use. Each one can control the position, rotation, and/or speed of a specific body node
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchor.UserFilters: Nothing currently implements IAvatarAnchorUserFilter so this currently does nothing.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchorDestroyOnRelease: When a user is released from an anchor on the same slot as this component, DestroyRoot is destroyed.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchorDestroyOnRelease.Des: The slot to destroy when a user is released from an anchor on the same slot as this component.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchorLocomotionRelease: The AvatarAnchorLocomotionRelease Component is used so a user can get out of an anchor with their controller when they get into an anchor.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchorLocomotionRelease.Re: Whether to allow the user to jump out of the anchor
The amount of pressing strength on the jump button needed to get out if not null.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchorTouchTrigger: Used often for a way of entering and exiting an Avatar Anchor when the user wants to.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchorTouchTrigger.Anchor: The anchor to enter after confirming the enter anchor.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchorTouchTrigger.EnterTe: The text for when the user is being prompted to enter the anchor.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchorTouchTrigger.ExitTex: The text for when the user is being prompted to exit the anchor.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchorTouchTrigger.Enter: Whether to allow this component to be used for entering the anchor.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchorTouchTrigger.Exit: Whether to allow this component to be used for exiting the anchor.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchorTouchTrigger.AcceptO: Whether or not to allow interaction with this component if it is out of the user's view.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchorTouchTrigger.AcceptP: Whether to allow Component:TipTouchSources to interact with/activate this component.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchorTouchTrigger.AcceptR: Whether this component allows interaction via the user's interaction laser.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchorTouchTrigger.Vibrate: What kind of haptics vibration feedback to send to the user's controller when bringing up the enter or exiting prompt.
messages.Tooltip.MultiUserAvatarAnchor: Multi User Avatar anchor allows you to auto generate anchors, but only if you have a collider on the same slot as the multi anchor. It uses an IAvatarAnchor type component as a template, and generates them in a shape based on a Snapper (Snap Circle Component or Snap Sphere Component to name a couple).
messages.Tooltip.MultiUserAvatarAnchor.MaxUsers: the maximum users that can be in this multi anchor
messages.Tooltip.MultiUserAvatarAnchor.MinDistanc: How close a new anchor can be to another when a user clicks on the anchor before it denies it. The location of the anchor before this check is done is determined by the component in AnchorPointSnap.
messages.Tooltip.MultiUserAvatarAnchor.Template: The IAvatarAnchor to use as a template for making anchors. will duplicate the slot the anchor is on that this is referencing
messages.Tooltip.MultiUserAvatarAnchor.AnchorsRoo: what shape to put the anchors when they are generated. The part of the shape the anchor is placed on is determined by the point the user clicked on. If user clicks on left side of a cube, user is positioned on the left face.
messages.Tooltip.MultiUserAvatarAnchor.AcceptOutO: Whether or not to allow interaction with this component if it is out of the user's view.
messages.Tooltip.MultiUserAvatarAnchor.AcceptPhys: Whether to allow Component:TipTouchSources to interact with/activate this component.
messages.Tooltip.MultiUserAvatarAnchor.AcceptRemo: Whether this component allows interaction via the user's interaction laser.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioOutputManager: Avatar Audio Output Manager is the component that handles changing the audio output values when a user changes their voice mode or mutes.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioOutputManager.AudioOu: The audio output this component is controlling and targeting that has the <mark=#ffffff10>_activeUser</mark>'s voice.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioOutputManager.IsViewV: Whether or not this component is the voice of the <mark=#ffffff10>_activeUser</mark>'s head or the <mark=#ffffff10>_activeUser</mark>'s free cam.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioOutputManager.Whisper: What <mark=#ffffff10>AudioOutput</mark> should be set to when <mark=#ffffff10>_activeUser</mark> is in Whisper voice mode.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioOutputManager.NormalC: What <mark=#ffffff10>AudioOutput</mark> should be set to when <mark=#ffffff10>_activeUser</mark> is in Normal voice mode.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioOutputManager.ShoutCo: What <mark=#ffffff10>AudioOutput</mark> should be set to when <mark=#ffffff10>_activeUser</mark> is in Shout voice mode.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioOutputManager.Broadca: What <mark=#ffffff10>AudioOutput</mark> should be set to when the user is in Broadcast mode.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioOutputManager._active: The user that this component is supposed to be managing an Audio Output Component for.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioOutputManager._enable: The enabled field of the Audio Output Component controlled by this component.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioOutputManager._volume: The volume field of the Audio Output Component controlled by this component.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioOutputManager._dopple: The doppler field of the Audio Output Component controlled by this component.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioOutputManager._spatia: The spatialize field of the Audio Output Component controlled by this component.
The spatialBlend field of the Audio Output Component controlled by this component.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioOutputManager._minDis: The minDistance field of the Audio Output Component controlled by this component.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioOutputManager._maxDis: The maxDistance field of the Audio Output Component controlled by this component.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioOutputManager._rollOf: The rollOffMode field of the Audio Output Component controlled by this component.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioOutputManager._distan: The distance space field of the Audio Output Component controlled by this component.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioOutputManager._minSca: The minScale field of the Audio Output Component controlled by this component.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioOutputManager._maxSca: The maxScale field of the Audio Output Component controlled by this component.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioOutputManager._scaleC: How much to scale up the volume range of the user so a small <mark=#ffffff10>_activeUser</mark> can still be heard by everyone else.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAudioOutputManager._audioC: A field automatically filled with an Avatar Audio Configuration when the user spawns with an injected Avatar Audio Configuration courtesy of the world they spawned in. When not null, the specified component overrides <mark=#ffffff10>WhisperConfig</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>NormalConfig</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>ShoutConfig</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>BroadcastConfig</mark> voice settings.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarControllerInfo: Reads the controller FrooxEngine class type and it's model identifier for a User for a hand Side.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarControllerInfo.TargetUser: The user this component is reading controller info from
messages.Tooltip.AvatarControllerInfo.ControllerS: The side (left or right) controller this component is reading info from.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarControllerInfo.ControllerT: The type of the controller <mark=#ffffff10>TargetUser</mark> is using on their <mark=#ffffff10>ControllerSide</mark> hand.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarControllerInfo.ControllerD: The device model of the controller <mark=#ffffff10>TargetUser</mark> is using on their <mark=#ffffff10>ControllerSide</mark> hand.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarControllerSpawner: The AvatarControllerSpawner component will spawn the corrosponding controller for the user when placed on an avatar under either the right or left hand.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarControllerSpawner.SpawnRoo: Where to spawn the model. (The Component attaches a slot called "root" under this when spawning the model, that becomes the model root)
messages.Tooltip.AvatarControllerSpawner.Material: What material to replace the spawned model's materials with upon spawning.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarControllerSpawner.Controll: The component calls this method when it has finished spawning the model with the user that has this controller model, and the root slot of the spawned model.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarControllerSpawner._sourceC: The controller info this component grabbed in order to find and generate the right model.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarControllerSpawner._lastSpa: The froox engine type of the last controller type this component generated.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarDebugHand: Debug hand is a component that is used to show a debug visual when the Biped Rig Component has its "Debug Hand" sync delegate pressed.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarDebugHand._objects: The slots of the mesh renderers that are being used to debug the hand.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarDebugHand._materialTarget: The component used as a reference to apply <mark=#ffffff10>_material</mark> to the debug when <mark=#ffffff10>_material</mark> changes.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarDebugHand._material: The material this component is using to render the visual
messages.Tooltip.AvatarDestroyOnDequip: The AvatarDestroyOnDequip component destroys DestroyRoot when the avatar it's in the hierarchy of is unequipped, or the slot the component is attached to if DestroyRoot is null.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarDestroyOnDequip.DestroyRoo: The slot to destroy when the avatar this component is a part of is dequipped, or null to destroy the slot this component is attached to.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarEquipBlock: AvatarEquipBlock prevents anyone from equipping an avatar.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarFingerPoseInfo: The AvatarFingerPoseInfo is used to override the assigning behavior from a Component:AvatarHandDataAssigner on the avatar when it assigns a target.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarFingerPoseInfo.FingerPoseS: The data source to override the assigning behavior of a Component:AvatarHandDataAssigner on the avatar with.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarGroup: For detailed information on how this functions for mix and match body parts. Please also see Equipping Multiple Avatars.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarGroup.OriginalParent: The slot that this avatar was parented under before being equipped.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarHandDataAssigner: The AvatarHandDataAssigner component is usually located on avatar hands, and is used to assign key objects that make a hand work like tip touch sources and the avatar object controlling it.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarHandDataAssigner.TargetRef: A field to assign a finger pose source to when the avatar above this component is equipped. Usually the <mark=#ffffff10>PoseSource</mark> on a Component:HandPoser is specified here.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarHandDataAssigner.TouchSour: A tip touch source component to put data into when the avatar above this component is equipped.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarHandDataAssigner.Vibration: A vibration device relay component to put data into when the avatar above this component is equipped.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarHandDataAssigner.Chirality: Whether this component should assign data from the left or right hand data sets.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarHandDataAssigner._equippin: This field is auto filled with the avatar object slot that is controlling the tip touch source and other components specified by this component.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarManager: For detailed information on how this functions for mix and match body parts. Please also see Equipping Multiple Avatars.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarManager._objectGroups: A list of objects currently equipped by the user as part of an avatar.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarManager._currentAnchor: The anchor the user associated with this component is currently sitting in.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarManager.AutoAddNameBadge: Whether to auto add the name badge component if it doesn't exist on an avatar when equipping it. Usually managed by the world through the Component:CommonAvatarBuilder
messages.Tooltip.AvatarManager.AutoAddIconBadge: Whether to auto add the icon badges if it doesn't exist on an avatar when equipping it. Usually managed by the world through the Component:CommonAvatarBuilder
messages.Tooltip.AvatarManager.AutoAddLiveIndicat: Whether to auto add the live indicator component if it doesn't exist on an avatar when equipping it. Usually managed by the world through the Component:CommonAvatarBuilder
messages.Tooltip.AvatarManager.EmptySlotHandler: The object to handle empty avatar slots. Usually defined by the world through the Component:CommonAvatarBuilder.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarManager.DefaultScale: The default scale multiplier of the avatar currently equipped
messages.Tooltip.AvatarManager.NameTagText: The text that should be assigned to the avatar nametag's text field list
messages.Tooltip.AvatarManager.NameTagColor: The color that should be assigned to the avatar nametag's color list.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarManager.NameTagOutline: The color that should be assigned to the avatar nametag's color1 list.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarManager.NameTagBackground: The color.that should be assigned to the avatar nametag's color background list.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarManager._badgeTemplates: the slot that was made for the user for storage of their badges.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarManager._autoNameBadge: the slot that was made for the user for their name badge.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarManager._autoIconBadge: the slot that was made for the user for their Icon.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarManager._autoLiveIndicator: The slot that was made for the user for their live indicator
messages.Tooltip.AvatarNeckOffset: Avatar Neck Offset is a component that allows for dynamically lengthening and shortening the neck of an avatar.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarNeckOffset.Priority: How much priority this component takes over other components that constrain or change Avatar Pose Nodes
messages.Tooltip.AvatarNeckOffset.Offset: How much to offset the user's neck from it's original position in local space.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarNeckOffset._activeUser: Internal. Automatically updates to active user of this component that's being affected by it.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarNodeAssigner: The AvatarNodeAssigner component only functions on an avatar being equipped or de-equipped by a user. When equipped, the component finds the first Component:AvatarObjectSlot with a body node value that equals Node. if found, it assigns all slot storage fields in Targets to the slot the Component:AvatarObjectSlot is on. When de-equipped, all slot storage fields in Targets have their stored slot cleared.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarNodeAssigner.Node: The body node to search for when the avatar this is on is equipped.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarNodeAssigner.Targets: The slot storage fields to fill when an Component:AvatarObjectSlot of node type <mark=#ffffff10>Node</mark> is found.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarObjectComponentProxy: The AvatarObjectComponentProxy component receives equipping, dequipping, and preequipping events and sends them to every IAvatarObjectComponent component under the Target
messages.Tooltip.AvatarObjectComponentProxy.Targe: The slot with components to send Equipping, Deequipping, and prequipping events to.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarObjectPermissions: The AvatarObjectPermission component is a Permissions component which determines which avatars users are allowed to equip if the avatar is tagged properly.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarObjectPermissions.Tags: The list of tags and whether they are white listed or black listed
messages.Tooltip.AvatarObjectScale: The AvatarObjectScale component allows setting it's slot scale relative to the UserRoot slot when equipped under an avatar.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarObjectScale.UserSpaceScale: The desired scale relative to the UserRoot slot.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarObjectSlot: For detailed information on how this functions for mix and match body parts. Please also see Equipping Multiple Avatars.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarObjectSlot.Priority: Whether to do the equip checking on this AvatarObjectSlot before other AvatarObjectSlots
messages.Tooltip.AvatarObjectSlot.Equipped: Usually an Avatar Pose Node that is on one of the proxies on an avatar.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarObjectSlot.Node: the body node of the avatar this component's slot is. Unless already specified by a Biped Rig Component higher up in the hierarchy.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarObjectSlot.IsTracking: Whether this component is currently being simulated by the procedural animation system.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarObjectSlot.IsActive: Whether this component is currently active and controlling an avatar.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarObjectSlot.IsSimulated: Whether this object slot is being controlled by the procedural animation system.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarObjectSlot.DriveActive: Whether to drive the active of this slot when hooked into by an Avatar Pose Node.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarObjectSlot.DriveScale: Whether to drive the scale of this slot when hooked into by an Avatar Pose Node.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarObjectSlot.DoNotSimulate: Whether to not control this with the procedural locomotion system
messages.Tooltip.AvatarObjectSlot.Filters: Filters that are currently effecting this and their priorities. See Avatar Pose Filters
messages.Tooltip.AvatarObjectSlot._autoSmoothing: The Component currently smoothing the transform changes of this Avatar Object slot.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarParentNode: This component is used to parent and pose the avatar in a certain way. When using this component now, it will attach then remove itself from the slot.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseFilterInstaller: The AvatarPoseFilterInstaller component can be placed into slots below or on the same Slot as a Proxy on an avatar in order to auto install a pose filter on those parts upon equipping the avatar.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseFilterInstaller.Filter: the Pose Filter to install on an Avatar's pose node like the head, hands, arms, or feet.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseFilterInstaller.Priori: If this installer should run before other IAvatarObjectComponents.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseNode: For detailed information on how this functions for mix and match body parts. Please also see Equipping Multiple Avatars.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseNode.Node: the node this component is  copying.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseNode.EquipOrderPriorit: The priority of this node being updated in the list of Pose Nodes on an Avatar.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseNode.RunAfterInputUpda: Whether to run this component's copy Behavior after the engine's input received stage.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseNode.MutuallyExclusive: When equipped as part of an avatar, if the user equipping has an AvatarObjectSlot with a BodyNode value from this list, it is forced to dequip the Type:IAvatarObjects bound to it, kicking them off the user.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseNode.SourceIsTracking: Whether the source <mark=#ffffff10>_objectSlot</mark> is currently tracking an inputting object like a controller or FBT device
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseNode.SourceIsSimulated: Whether the source <mark=#ffffff10>_objectSlot</mark> is being simulated by the procedural animation system.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseNode._objectSlot: The object slot this component is getting pose data and tracking data from.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseNode._position: The position field to drive to the pose data. Usually the position field of the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseNode._rotation: The rotation field to drive to the pose data. Usually the rotation field of the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseNode._scale: The scale to drive to the scale of <mark=#ffffff10>_source</mark> if drive scale is enabled on the <mark=#ffffff10>_objectSlot</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseNode._active: Whether this component is currently active and setting pose data.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawToolData: The AvatarRawToolData component is used to read controller data associated with RawDataTools from.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawToolData.ControllerSide: The side to read data from the user's controller for.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawToolData.PressingPrimar: Whether the user is pressing Primary
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawToolData.PressingSecond: Whether the user is pressing Secondary.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawToolData.PressingGrab: Whether the user is pressing the grab button
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawToolData.PrimaryStrengt: The strength the user is pressing Primary.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawToolData.SecondaryAxis: What direction the user is moving their secondary button joystick
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawToolData._activeUser: The user this is reading data and getting streams from.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawToolData._strengthStrea: The stream to get <mark=#ffffff10>PrimaryStrength</mark>'s value from
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawToolData._axisStream: The stream to get <mark=#ffffff10>SecondaryAxis</mark>'s value from.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawToolData._primaryStream: The stream to get <mark=#ffffff10>PressingPrimary</mark>'s value from.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawToolData._secondaryStre: The stream to get <mark=#ffffff10>PressingSecondary</mark>'s value from.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawToolData._grabStream: The stream to get <mark=#ffffff10>PressingGrab</mark>'s value from.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRenderSettings: This component changes internal camera settings for the first person view by optionally changing the far and/or near clip values.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRenderSettings.NearClip: If not null, changes the NearClip of the user's POV camera when this component is under the user's avatar hierarchy.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRenderSettings.FarClip: If not null, changes the FarClip of the user's POV camera when this component is under the user's avatar hierarchy.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRoot: For detailed information on how this functions for mix and match body parts. Please also see Equipping Multiple Avatars.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRoot.Scale: What to set the local scale of the slot this component is on to when being equipped by a user.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRoot._originalParent: The slot this avatar was parented to before equipping.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarSlotEquippedToggle: The AvatarSlotEquippedToggle component drives one or two booleans to a state depending on if an Component:AvatarObjectSlot is equipped or not.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarSlotEquippedToggle.ObjectS: The Avatar Object Slot to monitor for equipping status.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarSlotEquippedToggle.Equippe: Drives the target bool to true when <mark=#ffffff10>ObjectSlot</mark> is equipped. Otherwise false.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarSlotEquippedToggle.Dequipp: Drives the target bool to false when <mark=#ffffff10>ObjectSlot</mark> is equipped. Otherwise true.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarToolAnchor: Avatar Tool anchor is a component that defines where to place either the tool, toolshelf, or grab area anchors when the avatar is equipped. Or where to position the context menu when it is opened.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarToolAnchor.AnchorPoint: The type of tool anchor to place under the slot this component is on for this hand.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarTrackingOffset: AvatarTrackingOffset can be used to shift the position of your trackers (including headset and hands) by a certain amount for an avatar. To work, the component needs to be in the avatar's hierarchy.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarTrackingOffset.Offset: The amount that your tracking position should be offset when in the avatar
messages.Tooltip.AvatarTrackingOffset._user: The user to which the offset is applied. This is set automatically when equipping an avatar with this component
messages.Tooltip.AvatarUserMeshHider: The AvatarUserMeshHider component makes non Skinned meshes invisible or only cast shadows according to the active user's perspective. It does this by making value User overrides for all MeshRenderers except for those in Exclude. It restores the fields back to normal after dequipping.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarUserMeshHider.Method: How to hide the meshes for the active user under the avatar.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarUserMeshHider.Exclude: Which meshes to not hide for the active user.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarUserPositioner: The AvatarUserPositioner component is used to position a user to the avatar upon equipping the avatar.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarUserPositioner.PositionNod: What node to use to match the position of the user.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarUserPositioner.RotationNod: What node to use to match the rotation of the user.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarUserPositioner.PositionRef: The slot to position the user to instead of the slot this component is on when they equip the user.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarUserPositioner.RotationRef: The slot to rotate the user to instead of the slot this component is on when they equip the user.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarUserPositioner.PreserveUp: Whether to upright the user when they equip the avatar, or set them to the rotation this avatar is in when equipping.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarUserPositioner.OnManualEqu: Whether to trigger this positioner only when the user clicks on the avatar to equip it rather than also triggering when the avatar is equipped through flux.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarUserReferenceAssigner: This is often used with assigning the user reference to nameplates, and the components within.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarUserReferenceAssigner.Assi: How to use the list when assigning users to User storage fields.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarUserReferenceAssigner.Refe: The references to scan through when assigning.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarUserRootOverrideAssigner: Avatar User Root Override Assigner is a component that allows for changing the user's viewpoint, the user's first person standing location, or the user's ears location.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarUserRootOverrideAssigner.O: The slot to relocate the user's eyes or ears to.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarUserRootOverrideAssigner.N: Whether to make the user's eyes, Standing position, or ears located at <mark=#ffffff10>Slot</mark> without changing any slot transforms.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarUserRootOverrideAssigner._: an Avatar Object Slot to use for handling this node.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarUserViewHeadOverride: This component is automatically added to Avatars to make the first person viewpoint not move around in desktop mode from the idle head animation.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarUserViewHeadOverride.Rende: The render transform override made by this component.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarUserViewHeadOverride.PoseF: This sync delegate is part of the VRIK Avatar Component's sync delegates.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarUserViewHeadOverride._equi: The avatar object slot that is controlling the head.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarVoiceInfo: The AvatarVoiceInfo is used to override the assigning behavior from a AvatarVoiceSourceAssigner component on the avatar when it assigns a target.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarVoiceInfo.AudioSource: The audio source to override the assigning behavior of a Component:AvatarVoiceSourceAssigner on the avatar with.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarVoiceRangeVisualizer: The AvatarVoiceRangeVisualizer component is used to generate and maintain the whisper bubble that appears when a user enables their whisper bubble mode.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarVoiceRangeVisualizer.Volum: A float representing audio change. Usually the <mark=#ffffff10>Volume</mark> field from a Component:VolumeMeter
messages.Tooltip.AvatarVoiceRangeVisualizer.Audio: An audio output to read info from to make the bubble size
messages.Tooltip.AvatarVoiceRangeVisualizer.Whisp: The color the bubble should glow at when the user is not speaking during whisper mode.
The color the bubble should glow at when the user is speaking at max volume during whisper mode.
The color the bubble should be when the user is recording a message.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarVoiceRangeVisualizer.Visua: The root slot of the visual generated for this component to function
messages.Tooltip.AvatarVoiceRangeVisualizer._acti: The user controlling and updating this component's logic.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarVoiceRangeVisualizer._visu: The field to use when driving the size of this visual to match the range of the user's whisper mode. Uses <mark=#ffffff10>AudioOutput</mark>'s range for the source of the data.
The field to drive to a color in an inclusive range of <mark=#ffffff10>WhisperColorMin</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>WhisperColorMax</mark> depending on volume output.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarVoiceSourceAssigner: The AvatarVoiceSourceAssigner sets the IAudioSource field referenced by TargetReference to the voice stream of the active user when the avatar this component is attached to is equipped.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarVoiceSourceAssigner.Target: Target <mark=#ffffff10>IAudioSource</mark> that will be assigned the user's audio stream
messages.Tooltip.DefaultUserScale: This component sets the default scale of the avatar it is attached to, which also affects the scale of the user wearing said avatar.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarExpressionDriver: Allows the assignment of the IMouthTrackingSourceComponent to blendshapes.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarExpressionDriver.DataSourc: The component that is providing raw mouth tracking data from a physical or virtual device on a machine.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarExpressionDriver.VolumeSou: A volume source like a Volume Meter that is used to determine facial tracking better.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarExpressionDriver.SilenceSo: A silence source like the target value of the <mark=#ffffff10>Silence</mark> field on a Direct Viseme Driver
messages.Tooltip.AvatarExpressionDriver.Expressio: A list of Expression Drivers that take facial data and drive a float or blendshape with the data.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarEyeDataSourceAssigner: The AvatarEyeDataSourceAssigner component manages assigning eye tracking sources to fields
messages.Tooltip.AvatarEyeDataSourceAssigner.Targ: The field to assign an eye tracking source to, unless it finds a Component:AvatarEyeTrackingInfo first, it assigns from that instead of the user's one.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarEyeTrackingInfo: The AvatarEyeTrackingInfo is used to override the assigning behavior from a Component:AvatarEyeDataSourceAssigner on the avatar when it assigns a target.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarEyeTrackingInfo.EyeDataSou: The data source to override the assigning behavior of a Component:AvatarEyeDataSourceAssigner on the avatar with.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarMouthDataSourceAssigner: Avatar Mouth Data Source Assigner is a component that is able to assign a mouth tracking source to a field that accepts it.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarMouthDataSourceAssigner.Ta: A field that accepts a mouth tracking source (like <mark=#ffffff10>DataSource</mark> on the Avatar Expression Driver Component) to assign a tracking source to.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarMouthTrackingInfo: The AvatarMouthTrackingInfo is used to override the assigning behavior from a Component:AvatarMouthDataSourceAssigner on the avatar when it assigns a target. or when a Component:AvatarExpressionDriver on the avatar assigns it's own mouth data source.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarMouthTrackingInfo.MouthDat: The data source to override the assigning behavior of a Component:AvatarMouthDataSourceAssigner on the avatar with. or to override what a Component:AvatarExpressionDriver on the avatar assigns it's own mouth data source with.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawEyeData: The AvatarRawEyeData component provides the eye tracking data on a particular User. This component links to a user upon being part of an Avatar that a user equips.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawEyeData.LeftEye: Data about the left eye from the user's eye tracking device
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawEyeData.RightEye: Data about the right eye from the user's eye tracking device
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawEyeData.CombinedEye: Data about combined eye Data from the user's tracking device
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawEyeData.ConvergenceDist: The User's eye tracking convergence distance data
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawEyeData.Timestamp: The timestamp value gotten directly from the eye tracking device.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawEyeData._activeUser: The user that the component is reading eye tracking data from.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawMouthData: The AvatarRawMouthData component reads the tracking values from any given DataSource and turns it into in game usable data.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawMouthData.DataSource: The place to get tracking data from.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawMouthData.StrengthMulti: How much to amplify the incoming data.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver: The EyeLinearDriver component is used to drive the shapekeys on eyes. It can also do eye look direction shapekeys if each eye is provided a projection plane slot.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.EyeManager: The eye manager to get the eye data from.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.ProjectionPlaneS: The scaling of the inital hit point before it is clamped. See How Projection Plane Works.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.ProjectionPointD: The distance from the offseted plane to <mark=#ffffff10>ProjectionPlanePoint</mark> on all the eyes in <mark=#ffffff10>Eyes</mark> list. See How Projection Plane Works.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.PositionOffsetCe: How much to add to the inital plane projection hit point after clamping. See How Projection Plane Works.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.PositionOffsetRa: how much to scale the inital plane projection hit after clamping. See How Projection Plane Works.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.MinimumTargetPoi: The minimum distance for every eye in <mark=#ffffff10>Eyes</mark> a look target point from that eye's projection plane slot can be. Points closer than that are set to be further than this value during calculation. See How Projection Plane Works.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.PositionClampMod: How to clamp projection plane projection range when doing projection calculations. See How Projection Plane Works.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.LeftCloseSubtrac: The component finds the EyeCloseLimit with the maximum influence in this list. If the EyeCloseLimit with the maximum influence in <mark=#ffffff10>CombinedEyeCloseSubtractLimits</mark> is higher, then that is used instead. Then this subtracts the result from the Left Eye close amount (essentially prying the eye open) to get the eye closed value to use with every left eye.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.RightCloseSubtra: The component finds the EyeCloseLimit with the maximum influence in this list. If the EyeCloseLimit with the maximum influence in <mark=#ffffff10>CombinedEyeCloseSubtractLimits</mark> is higher, then that is used instead. Then this subtracts the result from the Left Eye close amount (essentially prying the eye open) to get the eye closed value to use with every right eye.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.Eyes: The list of eyes this component is doing calculations and shapekey driving for.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager: The Eye Manager Component is a component that is used for configuring the Eyes of an avatar, Both supporting simulated eye-movement, and real eye tracking using a headset with built in eye tracking sensors such as the HTC Vive pro eye.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.TargetPoint: The average point The eyes are trying to look at currently. Simulated or controlled.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.LeftEyeTargetPoint: The point The left eye specifically is looking at. Simulated or controlled. This can be different from <mark=#ffffff10>TargetPoint</mark> if <mark=#ffffff10>LeftEyeTargetOffset</mark> has a non zero value.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.RightEyeTargetPoint: The point The right eye specifically is looking at. Simulated or controlled. This can be different from <mark=#ffffff10>TargetPoint</mark> if <mark=#ffffff10>RightEyeTargetOffset</mark> has a non zero value.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.LeftEyeClose: How much the left eye is closed currently. Simulated or controlled.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.RightEyeClose: How much the right eye is closed currently. Simulated or controlled.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.CombinedEyeClose: an average of how much both eyes are closed.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.LeftEyePupilSizeMilli: how big the left eye's pupil is in millimeters currently. Simulated or controlled.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.RightEyePupilSizeMill: How big the right eye's pupil is currently. Simulated or controlled.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.CombinedEyePupilSizeM: The average pupil size of both eyes. Simulated or controlled.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.LeftEyeWiden: A 0-1 value representing how much the left eye is being wider than rest. Simulated or controlled.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.RightEyeWiden: A 0-1 value representing how much the right eye is being wider than rest. Simulated or controlled.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.CombinedEyeWiden: An average of the eyes being widened. Simulated or controlled.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.LeftEyeSqueeze: A 0-1 value of how scrunched the upper and lower lids are on the left eye. Simulated or controlled.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.RightEyeSqueeze: A 0-1 value of how scrunched the upper and lower lids are on the right eye. Simulated or controlled.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.CombinedEyeSqueeze: An average of the squinting of both eyes. Simulated or controlled.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.LeftEyeFrown: The amount that the left eye is frowning according to the eye tracker if it exists.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.RightEyeFrown: The amount that the right eye is frowning according to the eye tracker if it exists.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.CombinedEyeFrown: The average amount of both eyes frowning according to the eye tracker if it exists.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.LeftEyeInnerBrowVerti: Eye tracking left inner brow vertical value.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.RightEyeInnerBrowVert: Eye tracking right inner brow vertical value.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.CombinedEyeInnerBrowV: Eye tracking average inner brow vertical value.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.LeftEyeOuterBrowVerti: Eye tracking left outer brow vertical value.
