messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.RightEyeOuterBrowVert: Eye tracking right outer brow vertical value.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.CombinedEyeOuterBrowV: Eye tracking average outer brow vertical value.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.SimulatingUser: Determines the user simulating the eye manager
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.SimulateOnHost: Determines if the eye manager is simulated by the host
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.IgnoreLocalUserHead: Determines if the eye manager should ignore the local user's head
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.UserHeadWeight: Weight for looking at other user heads
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.GrippingHandWeight: Weight for looking at hands that are gripping
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.ForcedCameraWeight: Weight for looking at cameras that are forcing eyes to look at it
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.EyeReference: Slot that is used for referencing the origin of eye tracking data
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.SaccadeSpeed: Speed the eyes move during saccades (does not affect eye tracking)
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.LookTargetRoot: Slot that the eyes should look at
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.LookTargetLocalPoint: The point that the eyes are looking at in local space.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.LeftEyeTargetOffset: The point that the left eye is looking at in global space.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.RightEyeTargetOffset: The point that the right eye is looking at in global space.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.LookTargetOffset: The point between the points both eyes are looking at in global space.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.MinRandomSaccadeInter: Minimum time in seconds between random saccades
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.MaxRandomSaccadeInter: Maximum time in seconds between random saccades
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.MinTargetSaccadeInter: Minimum time in seconds between Targeting saccades
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.MaxTargetSaccadeInter: Maximum time in seconds between Targeting saccades
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.MaxRandomSaccadeOffse: Maximum distance the eyes can randomly saccade off of the center of a target
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.DefaultAcceptAngle: Maximum acceptance angle for random objects
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.DefaultBreakAngle: Maximum angle before breaking eye contact with random objects
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.HeadBreakAngle: Maximum angle before breaking eye contact with Players
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.CameraBreakAngle: Maximum angle before breaking eye contact with cameras
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.HandBreakAngle: Maximum angle before breaking eye contact with hands
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.DistanceCompensationE: At higher values, objects further away become easier to be within angle accept. Equation is <noparse>[(ObjectAngleToEyeBall) * (Object Distance)^</noparse><mark=#ffffff10>DistanceCompensationExp</mark><noparse> == new (ObjectAngleToEyeBall)]</noparse>
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.LeftEyeCloseOverride: Overrides the left eyelid when open
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.RightEyeCloseOverride: Overrides the right eyelid when open
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.MinBlinkInterval: Minimum interval between blinks in seconds
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.MaxBlinkInterval: Maximum interval between blinks in seconds
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.BlinkSpeedSpread: How much to scale the randomness of blink speed. Basically to make more extreme values more common.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.PupilSizeNoiseSpeed: How fast the random pupil noise will move
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.PupilSizeNoiseOffset: How much extra random noise the right eye pupil size will have vs the left one.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.EyeTrackingPupilSizeS: Smooth speed for tracked pupil dilation
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.MiniExpressionProbabi: chance for a miniexpression to occur
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.MiniExpressionInterva: Interval between attempting to do a miniexpression
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.MiniExpressions: A list of mini expressions to use when attempting a mini expression.
messages.Tooltip.EyeRotationDriver: Set up automatically on avatars that are made using the Avatar Creator.
messages.Tooltip.EyeRotationDriver.EyeManager: The source of the eye rotation data.
messages.Tooltip.EyeRotationDriver.EyeMotionScale: How much to multiply the target rotation by before applying it to an Eye.
messages.Tooltip.EyeRotationDriver.EyeMotionExp: The exponent. This makes the eye rotate more or less as it reaches higher rotation target values.
messages.Tooltip.EyeRotationDriver.MaxSwing: The maximum amount the eyes can rotate in degrees from forward facing.
messages.Tooltip.EyeRotationDriver.Eyes: A list of eyes to drive the rotation of.
messages.Tooltip.EyeRotationDriverGizmo: The EyeRotationDriverGizmo component is used to edit the Eye forwards of an Eye Rotation Driver.
messages.Tooltip.EyeRotationDriverGizmo._target: The eye rotation driver we are editing.
messages.Tooltip.EyeRotationDriverGizmo._vectorGi: The forward vectors of the eyes.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseBoxConstraint: The AvatarPoseBoxConstraint component is a BodyPoseFilter that can be used in the construction of tool tips, anchors, grabbable items, and more.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseBoxConstraint.BoxSize: the size of the box that a user's body node should be constrained within. (0,0,0) is valid for this field.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseBoxConstraint.DefaultP: the slot which the box will be positioned, rotated, and scaled by. If null, this is the slot this component is on.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseBoxConstraint.ProcessS: Whether to affect simulated poses like from the animation system.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseOffset: The Avatar Pose Offset Component is used to offset a user's limb while they use a tool tip or sit in an anchor.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseOffset.PositionOffset: How much position to offset a body node by after previous pose constraints have been applied.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseOffset.RotationOffset: How much rotation to offset a body node by after previous pose constraints have been applied.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseRotationConstraint: The AvatarPoseRotationConstraint component is used to either restrict the rotation direction of the user's arm when using a tooltip, or to restrict the rotation of a particular BodyNode.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseRotationConstraint.Max: the max twist in degrees allowed for the object being filtered.
the max swing in degrees allowed for the object being filtered.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseRotationConstraint.Axi: swing axis direction for the object being filtered.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseRotationConstraint.Twi: The twist axis direction for the object being filtered.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseSmoothLerp: The AvatarPoseSmoothLerp component changes the smoothing speed of the position and rotation of a BodyNode.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseSmoothLerp.PositionSmo: how fast the body node should move. Lower values mean slower speed.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseSmoothLerp.RotationSmo: how fast the body node should rotate. Lower values mean slower speed.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarPoseSmoothLerp.SmoothSimul: Whether this filter should smooth limbs affected by the procedural animation system.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarBadgeManager: This is used to help with organizing badges on a user's Nameplate, including custom badges.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarBadgeManager.BadgeSize: The size of the generated badges in local space.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarBadgeManager.BadgeSeparati: The separation in meters between the badges in local space
messages.Tooltip.AvatarBadgeManager.MaxRowSize: The maximum amount of badges on one row before moving to the next.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarBadgeManager._badgesRoot: The slot to put the badges under.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarBadgeManager._badgesOffset: The position of the <mark=#ffffff10>_badgesRoot</mark> slot, which is used to move the badges away from the user's name tag and keep them positioned properly.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarLiveIndicator: Usually this is used with the Camera, especially when streaming.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarLiveIndicator._activeUser: The user that this component is monitoring
messages.Tooltip.AvatarNameTagAssigner: The AvatarNameTagAssigner component is responsible for setting the nametag's text, color, and the ContactLink UserId. It basically defines what a nametag is.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarNameTagAssigner.LabelTarge: A list of string fields that should be set to the user's display name when this component is under an avatar and the avatar is equipped.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarNameTagAssigner.UserIdTarg: A list of string fields that should be set to the user's UUID (aka UserID) when this component is under an avatar and the avatar is equipped.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarNameTagAssigner.ColorTarge: A list of colorX fields that should be set to the user's special color when this component is under an avatar and the avatar is equipped. Supporters, Staff, Mentors, and Moderators are some of the many users that get custom name plate colors through this component.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarNameTagAssigner.OutlineTar: Similar to <mark=#ffffff10>ColorTargets</mark> but for the outline.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarNameTagAssigner.Background: Similar to <mark=#ffffff10>ColorTargets</mark> but for the background color.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarNameTagAssigner.DequippedL: What text should display when the avatar this component belongs to is dequipped. You can be creative with this!
messages.Tooltip.SimpleAvatarProtection: SimpleAvatarProtection prevents other users from wearing an avatar or saving it.
messages.Tooltip.SimpleAvatarProtection.User: The user who is permitted to use the avatar.
messages.Tooltip.NoDestroyUndo: The NoDestroyUndo component prevents an Undo step from being created when destroying the slot that the component is attached to.
messages.Tooltip.SetAssetReference`1: The Set Asset Reference component is used to store an undo step for setting a reference field for assets to another asset.
messages.Tooltip.SetAssetReference`1.TargetBefore: The asset value before the change
messages.Tooltip.SetAssetReference`1.TargetAfter: The asset value after the change.
messages.Tooltip.SetAssetReference`1._performed: Whether the undo step was done or undone.
messages.Tooltip.SetAssetReference`1._description: The description of the change that was made and what to for the context menu label text when undoing/redoing.
messages.Tooltip.SetReference`1: The Set Reference component is internally used by the Undo System for setting and reverting a Reference.
messages.Tooltip.SetReference`1.TargetBefore: The reference value before the change.
messages.Tooltip.SetReference`1.TargetAfter: The reference value after the change.
messages.Tooltip.SetReference`1._performed: Whether the undo step was done or undone.
messages.Tooltip.SetReference`1._description: The description of the change that was made and what to for the context menu label text when undoing/redoing.
messages.Tooltip.SetType: Set type is a component that is part of the undo system. This stores an undo step for setting a type field.
messages.Tooltip.SetType.Target: The value field for a type value that was changed.
messages.Tooltip.SetType.ValueBefore: The value it was before edit.
messages.Tooltip.SetType.ValueAfter: The value it became after edit.
messages.Tooltip.SetType._performed: Whether this was done (true) or can be redone (false)
messages.Tooltip.SetType._description: The description of this undo step for changing a type field.
messages.Tooltip.SetValue`1: Set Value is a component that is part of the undo system.
messages.Tooltip.SetValue`1.Target: The field edited by the user.
messages.Tooltip.SetValue`1.ValueBefore: The value before it was edited by the user.
messages.Tooltip.SetValue`1.ValueAfter: The value after it was edited by the user.
messages.Tooltip.SetValue`1._performed: Whether this has been done or has been undone instead.
messages.Tooltip.SetValue`1._description: The description of this undo step.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnOrDestroy: The SpawnOrDestroy component is part of the Undo system, and handles spawning and destruction of objects in the Undo system.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnOrDestroy.Target: The target worker to use when executing.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnOrDestroy.TargetParent: The parent of this object to restore it to.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnOrDestroy._onRestored: The sync delegate with this compoonent's worker passed as an object to trigger when the object is restored.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnOrDestroy._preserveAssets: Whether the destruction should preserve assets like meshes.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnOrDestroy._sendDestroyingEv: Whether to send destruction events when destroying.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnOrDestroy._savedObject: The URI of the saved object.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnOrDestroy._isComponent: Whether this is in component form or internal to the engine.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnOrDestroy._referenceTable: The place to store destroyed object data in order to restore the object later.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnOrDestroy._isSaving: Whether this component is saving object data.
messages.Tooltip.SpawnOrDestroy._performed: Whether this component is finished executing it's function.
messages.Tooltip.UpdateTransform: Update transform is part of the Undo system, and is used in the Undo Manager to allow undoing and redoing of moving an object.
messages.Tooltip.UpdateTransform.RestoreParent: Whether or not to restore the parent object.
messages.Tooltip.UpdateTransform.RestorePosition: Whether or not to restore the position of the object.
messages.Tooltip.UpdateTransform.RestoreRotation: Whether or not to restore the rotation of the object.
messages.Tooltip.UpdateTransform.RestoreScale: Whether or not to restore the scale of the object.
messages.Tooltip.UpdateTransform.ParentBefore: The parent slot that the object had before the move.
messages.Tooltip.UpdateTransform.ParentAfter: The parent slot the object had after the move.
messages.Tooltip.UpdateTransform.LocalPositionBef: The Local Position that the object had before the move.
messages.Tooltip.UpdateTransform.LocalRotationBef: The Local Rotation that the object had before the move.
messages.Tooltip.UpdateTransform.LocalScaleBefore: The Local Scale that the object had before the move.
messages.Tooltip.UpdateTransform.GlobalPositionBe: The Global Position that the object had before the move.
messages.Tooltip.UpdateTransform.GlobalRotationBe: The Global Rotation that the object had before the move.
messages.Tooltip.UpdateTransform.GlobalScaleBefor: The Global Scale that the object had before the move.
messages.Tooltip.UpdateTransform.LocalPositionAft: The Local Position the object had after the move.
messages.Tooltip.UpdateTransform.LocalRotationAft: The Local Rotation the object had after the move.
messages.Tooltip.UpdateTransform.LocalScaleAfter: The Local Scale the object had after the move.
messages.Tooltip.UpdateTransform.GlobalPositionAf: The Global Position the object had after the move.
messages.Tooltip.UpdateTransform.GlobalRotationAf: The Global Rotation the object had after the move.
messages.Tooltip.UpdateTransform.GlobalScaleAfter: The Global Scale the object had after the move.
messages.Tooltip.UpdateTransform._performed: Whether the action is performed or it is in the future (due to undoing by the user.
messages.Tooltip.UpdateTransform._description: The description of this move action.
messages.Tooltip.BatchAction: Batch action is a component that is used internally by the Undo Manager to handle keeping data that the user has done so that same user can Undo it later via the control menu(s).
messages.Tooltip.BatchAction._description: a short description of what this group of items is undoing. Can be filled automatically with custom content when generated by a begin and end Undo Batch protoflux node
messages.Tooltip.UndoListRoot: The UndoListRoot component acts as a marker for a particular user's undo actions list. For more information on undos, see Undo.
messages.Tooltip.UndoListRoot.OwnerUser: the owner of this undo list.
messages.Tooltip.UndoManager: The UndoManager component keeps track of all user's actions in the world, allowing users to Undo or revert back to a previous step or action. Undoing can be controlled in ProtoFlux within the Undo Category, where users create undo batches and steps that this component can utilize. Undoing can be disabled by setting max steps to 0.
messages.Tooltip.UndoManager.MaxUndoSteps: The maximum memory for Undoable actions per user for the current world.
messages.Tooltip.UndoManager.UnsavedChanges: Whether the user has done anything that makes an undo step, which tells the world it has unsaved changes.
messages.Tooltip.BoxEmitter: Box emitter is a component that is used in conjunction with Particle System and Particle Style. It allows for adding particles into a Particle system with a rate, and does them inside or along the outside of the box area specified. The particle starting colors are lerped based on what corner of the cube the particle generated is closest to.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleEmitter.System: The particle system to get data from like style and particle count limits.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleEmitter.Rate: how fast to emit particles into the system.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleEmitter.BurstOnActivated: The minimum value of the range of particle count to be emitted when activated as a burst.
The maximum value of the range of particle count to be emitted when activated as a burst.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleEmitter.BurstOnStart: Whether or not particle bursts should be done on spawn.
messages.Tooltip.BoxEmitter.Size: The size of this box emitter.
messages.Tooltip.BoxEmitter.EmitFromShell: Enable emitting particles on the outside of the shape instead of from any point within the volume.
messages.Tooltip.BoxEmitter.DirectionMode: What direction to shoot the particles in from the box.
messages.Tooltip.BoxEmitter.Direction: Which direction in local space to shoot particles emitted by this emitter.
messages.Tooltip.BoxEmitter.DirectionTransformMod: How to determine the transform of the particles made by this emitter at start.
messages.Tooltip.BoxEmitter.RandomDirectionWeight: How much to randomize the velocity of new particles (this is a magnitude)
messages.Tooltip.BoxEmitter.DirectionPostTransfor: The direction the particles should face as an extra matrix transform after the final particle starting direction has been calculated.
messages.Tooltip.BoxEmitter.Color0: Color of the cube on corner (-x,-z,-y)
messages.Tooltip.BoxEmitter.Color1: Color of the cube on corner (+x,-z,-y)
messages.Tooltip.BoxEmitter.Color2: Color of the cube on corner (+x,+z,-y)
messages.Tooltip.BoxEmitter.Color3: Color of the cube on corner (-x,+z,-y)
messages.Tooltip.BoxEmitter.Color4: Color of the cube on corner (-x,-z,+y)
messages.Tooltip.BoxEmitter.Color5: Color of the cube on corner (+x,-z,+y)
messages.Tooltip.BoxEmitter.Color6: Color of the cube on corner (+x,+z,+y)
messages.Tooltip.BoxEmitter.Color7: Color of the cube on corner (-x,+z,+y)
messages.Tooltip.BoxEmitter.LegacyDirectionConver: The LegacyDirectionConverter is used in converted legacy particle systems that were converted to PhotonDust as part of The Performance Updates.
messages.Tooltip.FieldConverter`2.Target: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>Value</mark> converted.
messages.Tooltip.CircleEmitter: Circle emitters are used with Particle Systems to emit new particles into the system from a circle shaped source.
messages.Tooltip.CircleEmitter.Scale: The size of the circle on the x and y separately. used to make elipses.
messages.Tooltip.CircleEmitter.EmitFromShell: Enable emitting particles on the outside of the shape instead of from any point within the volume.
messages.Tooltip.CircleEmitter.CircleAlignment: How to align the particles via the circle on start.
messages.Tooltip.CircleEmitter.DirectionMode: How to emit particles from the circle.
messages.Tooltip.CircleEmitter.Direction: Which direction in local space to shoot particles emitted by this emitter.
messages.Tooltip.CircleEmitter.DirectionTransform: How to determine the transform of the particles made by this emitter at start.
messages.Tooltip.CircleEmitter.RandomDirectionWei: How much to randomize the velocity of new particles (this is a magnitude)
messages.Tooltip.CircleEmitter.DirectionPostTrans: The direction the particles should face as an extra matrix transform after the final particle starting direction has been calculated.
messages.Tooltip.CircleEmitter.LegacyDirectionCon: The LegacyDirectionConverter is used in converted legacy particle systems that were converted to PhotonDust as part of The Performance Updates.
messages.Tooltip.ConeEmitter: The ConeEmitter component is used to create particles via a particle system (see Photon Dust). This Emitter does this in a cone shape.
messages.Tooltip.ConeEmitter.BaseRadius: The radius of the cone to emit from.
messages.Tooltip.ConeEmitter.Height: The height of the cone to emit from.
messages.Tooltip.ConeEmitter.EmitFromShell: Enable emitting particles on the outside of the shape instead of from any point within the volume.
messages.Tooltip.ConeEmitter.DirectionMode: How particles are emitted from the cone.
messages.Tooltip.ConeEmitter.RandomDirectionWeigh: How much to randomize the velocity of new particles (this is a magnitude)
messages.Tooltip.ConeEmitter.Direction: Which direction in local space to shoot particles emitted by this emitter.
messages.Tooltip.ConeEmitter.DirectionTransformMo: How to determine the transform of the particles made by this emitter at start.
messages.Tooltip.ConeEmitter.DirectionPostTransfo: The direction the particles should face as an extra matrix transform after the final particle starting direction has been calculated.
messages.Tooltip.ConeEmitter.LegacyDirectionConve: The LegacyDirectionConverter is used in converted legacy particle systems that were converted to PhotonDust as part of The Performance Updates.
messages.Tooltip.CylinderEmitter: The CylinderEmitter component is used to emit particles in PhotonDust.
messages.Tooltip.CylinderEmitter.Radius: The radius of the emitting cylinder shape
messages.Tooltip.CylinderEmitter.Height: The height of the emitting cylinder shape.
messages.Tooltip.CylinderEmitter.EmitFromShell: Enable emitting particles on the outside of the shape instead of from any point within the volume.
messages.Tooltip.CylinderEmitter.ExcludeCaps: Whether to exclude the ends of the cylinder when emitting particles.
messages.Tooltip.CylinderEmitter.DirectionMode: How to emit particles from the cylinder.
messages.Tooltip.CylinderEmitter.CapsDirectionMod: how to emit particles from the cylinder caps.
messages.Tooltip.CylinderEmitter.RandomDirectionW: How much to randomize the velocity of new particles (this is a magnitude)
messages.Tooltip.CylinderEmitter.Direction: Which direction in local space to shoot particles emitted by this emitter.
messages.Tooltip.CylinderEmitter.DirectionTransfo: How to handle the direction when emitting particles.
messages.Tooltip.CylinderEmitter.DirectionPostTra: The direction particles should be when spawned post transform using a matrix.
messages.Tooltip.CylinderEmitter.LegacyDirectionA: The LegacyDirectionAdapter is used in converted legacy particle systems that were converted to PhotonDust as part of The Performance Updates.
Whether to force direction on the particles.
Whether the particles should emit from the shell of the cylinder.
The direction the particles should come from the cylinder.
The direction the particles should come from the caps of the cylinder.
messages.Tooltip.LineEmitter: The LineEmitter component is used with particle systems (see Photon Dust) to create particles from a straight 3d line made from two points.
messages.Tooltip.LineEmitter.Point0: Point 1 of the line for this Emitter drawn between two points.
messages.Tooltip.LineEmitter.Point1: Point 2 of the line for this Emitter drawn between two points.
messages.Tooltip.LineEmitter.Color0: The starting Color multiplier for particles emitted from this Emitter when being emitted nearest to <mark=#ffffff10>Point0</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.LineEmitter.Color1: The starting Color multiplier for particles emitted from this Emitter when being emitted nearest to <mark=#ffffff10>Point1</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.LineEmitter.DirectionMode: How to handle the direction when emitting particles from the line.
messages.Tooltip.LineEmitter.Direction0: The direction particles should go when being emitted nearest to <mark=#ffffff10>Point0</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.LineEmitter.Direction1: The direction particles should go when being emitted nearest to <mark=#ffffff10>Point1</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.LineEmitter.UpDirection: The direction that should be considered up for this line.
messages.Tooltip.LineEmitter.RandomDirectionWeigh: How much to randomize the velocity of new particles (this is a magnitude)
messages.Tooltip.LineEmitter.DirectionPostTransfo: The direction the particles should face as an extra matrix transform after the final particle starting direction has been calculated.
messages.Tooltip.LineEmitter.LegacyAdapter: The Legacy Adapter component is used in converted legacy particle systems that were converted to PhotonDust as part of The Performance Updates.
messages.Tooltip.LineEmitter.LegacyAdapter.Direct: <mark=#ffffff10>ForceDirection</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>ForcedDirection</mark> converted to PhotonDust direction mode.
<mark=#ffffff10>ForceDirection</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>ForcedDirection</mark> converted to PhotonDust direction vector.
<mark=#ffffff10>ForceDirection</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>ForcedDirection</mark> converted to PhotonDust direction vector.
messages.Tooltip.MeshEmitter: The MeshEmitter component allows for emitting particles from a mesh.
messages.Tooltip.MeshEmitterBase`1.EmitFrom: What parts of the mesh to emit from for the specified mesh.
messages.Tooltip.MeshEmitterBase`1.UseVertexColor: Whether or not to use interpolated vertex colors of the mesh this is emitting from.
messages.Tooltip.MeshEmitterBase`1.DirectionMode: How to determine the emission direction of particles emitted from this mesh.
messages.Tooltip.MeshEmitterBase`1.Direction: Which direction in local space to shoot particles emitted by this emitter.
messages.Tooltip.MeshEmitterBase`1.RandomDirectio: How much to randomize the velocity of new particles (this is a magnitude)
messages.Tooltip.MeshEmitterBase`1.ColorTexture: The texture to sample from for the particle starting color, which is sampled from the texture via the uv coordinate of the mesh surface the particle was emitted from.
messages.Tooltip.MeshEmitterBase`1.WrapMode: How to wrap the texture sampling for the particle color when the uvs are beyond a 0 to 1 range.
messages.Tooltip.MeshEmitterBase`1.UVOffset: How much to offset the UV sample position of <mark=#ffffff10>ColorTexture</mark>
messages.Tooltip.MeshEmitterBase`1.UVScale: How much to scale the UV sample position of <mark=#ffffff10>ColorTexture</mark>
messages.Tooltip.MeshEmitterBase`1.ClipRect: The rectangle to discard UV coordinates if they end up outside this range. Kind of like a UV discard.
messages.Tooltip.MeshEmitter.Mesh: The mesh to use for particle emission.
messages.Tooltip.PointEmitter: The PointEmitter component is used to make particles using particle systems (see Photon Dust) from a single point.
messages.Tooltip.PointEmitter.Direction: Which direction in local space to shoot particles emitted by this emitter.
messages.Tooltip.PointEmitter.RandomDirectionWeig: How much to randomize the velocity of new particles (this is a magnitude)
messages.Tooltip.PointEmitter.Color: Color multiplier for starting color of particles emitted from this point.
messages.Tooltip.PointEmitter.LegacyDirectionConv: The LegacyDirectionConverter is used in converted legacy particle systems that were converted to PhotonDust as part of The Performance Updates.
messages.Tooltip.SkinnedMeshEmitter: A skinned mesh renderer can be used to emit particles from either the vertices, edges, or faces of a SkinnedMeshRenderer. The colors of the particles can be made to be the colors of certain points of a texture, which is sampled via which point on the skinned mesh renderer the particle is emitted from.
messages.Tooltip.SkinnedMeshEmitter.Skin: The skinned mesh renderer to emit from.
messages.Tooltip.SphereEmitter: The SphereEmitter component is used to create particles in a particle system (See Photon Dust). This Emitter emits particles from a sphere shape.
messages.Tooltip.SphereEmitter.Radius: The radius of the sphere shape this is emitting from.
messages.Tooltip.SphereEmitter.EmitFromShell: Enable emitting particles on the outside of the shape instead of from any point within the volume.
messages.Tooltip.SphereEmitter.DirectionMode: How particles should be oriented when emitting from the Sphere shape.
messages.Tooltip.SphereEmitter.RandomDirectionWei: How much to randomize the velocity of new particles (this is a magnitude)
messages.Tooltip.SphereEmitter.ForcedDirection: Which direction in local space to shoot particles emitted by this emitter.
messages.Tooltip.SphereEmitter.DirectionTransform: How to determine the transform of the particles made by this emitter at start.
messages.Tooltip.SphereEmitter.DirectionReference: The point to use as a reference point when orienting particles upon emission.
messages.Tooltip.SphereEmitter.DirectionPostTrans: The direction the particles should face as an extra matrix transform after the final particle starting direction has been calculated.
messages.Tooltip.SphereEmitter.LegacyDirectionCon: The LegacyDirectionConverter is used in converted legacy particle systems that were converted to PhotonDust as part of The Performance Updates.
messages.Tooltip.SphereEmitter.LegacyTransformCon: The Legacy Transform Converter component converts transforms for legacy content to the new Photon Dust system.
The transform field to drive with a converted value.
The emitter to make conversions with.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAlignmentSizingAdapter: The LegacyAlignmentSizingAdapter component is used in converted legacy particle systems that were converted to PhotonDust as part of The Performance Updates.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAlignmentSizingAdapter.Len: The original length scale from legacy content.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAlignmentSizingAdapter.Vel: The original velocity scale from legacy content.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAlignmentSizingAdapter.Ali: The original alignment from legacy content.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAlignmentSizingAdapter.Par: The particle mesh this is using.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAlignmentSizingAdapter.Usi: <mark=#ffffff10>LengthScale</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>VelocityScale</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>Alignment</mark> are used to generate this value, and can be used in PhotonDust.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAlignmentSizingAdapter.Rot: <mark=#ffffff10>LengthScale</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>VelocityScale</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>Alignment</mark> are used to generate the drive value for the target field specified in this, and is used to drive a field of the same name on another component in PhotonDust.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAlignmentSizingAdapter.Ori: <mark=#ffffff10>LengthScale</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>VelocityScale</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>Alignment</mark> are used to generate the drive value for the target field specified in this, and is used to drive a field of the same name on another component in PhotonDust.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAlignmentSizingAdapter.Piv: <mark=#ffffff10>LengthScale</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>VelocityScale</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>Alignment</mark> are used to generate the drive value for the target field specified in this, and is used to drive a field of the same name on another component in PhotonDust.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAlignmentSizingAdapter.Use: <mark=#ffffff10>LengthScale</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>VelocityScale</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>Alignment</mark> are used to generate the drive value for the target field specified in this, and is used to drive a field of the same name on another component in PhotonDust.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAlignmentSizingAdapter.Siz: <mark=#ffffff10>LengthScale</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>VelocityScale</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>Alignment</mark> are used to generate the drive value for the target field specified in this, and is used to drive a field of the same name on another component in PhotonDust.

<mark=#ffffff10>LengthScale</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>VelocityScale</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>Alignment</mark> are used to generate the drive value for the target field specified in this, and is used to drive a field of the same name on another component in PhotonDust.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAlignmentSizingAdapter.Ori: <mark=#ffffff10>LengthScale</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>VelocityScale</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>Alignment</mark> are used to generate the drive value for the target field specified in this, and is used to drive a field of the same name on another component in PhotonDust.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAlignmentSizingAdapter.Len: <mark=#ffffff10>LengthScale</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>VelocityScale</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>Alignment</mark> are used to generate the drive value for the target field specified in this, and is used to drive a field of the same name on another component in PhotonDust.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAlignmentSizingAdapter.Vel: <mark=#ffffff10>LengthScale</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>VelocityScale</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>Alignment</mark> are used to generate the drive value for the target field specified in this, and is used to drive a field of the same name on another component in PhotonDust.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAlignmentSizingAdapter.Piv: <mark=#ffffff10>LengthScale</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>VelocityScale</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>Alignment</mark> are used to generate the drive value for the target field specified in this, and is used to drive a field of the same name on another component in PhotonDust.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAlignmentSizingAdapter.Bil: <mark=#ffffff10>LengthScale</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>VelocityScale</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>Alignment</mark> are used to generate the drive value for the target field specified in this, and is used to drive a field of the same name on another component in PhotonDust.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAlignmentSizingAdapter.Mes: <mark=#ffffff10>LengthScale</mark>, <mark=#ffffff10>VelocityScale</mark>, and <mark=#ffffff10>Alignment</mark> are used to generate the drive value for the target field specified in this, and is used to drive a field of the same name on another component in PhotonDust.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAnimationTypeAdapter: The LegacyAnimationTypeAdapter component is used in converted legacy particle systems that were converted to PhotonDust as part of The Performance Updates.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAnimationTypeAdapter.Sourc: The original animation type from legacy content.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAnimationTypeAdapter.Targe: The target field to drive with the legacy animation values.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyAnimationTypeAdapter.Anima: The field to set to the amount of animation tiles.
The field to drive with whether animation should be enabled.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyDistributionAdapter: The LegacyDistributionAdapter is used in converted legacy particle systems that were converted to PhotonDust as part of The Performance Updates.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyDistributionAdapter.Distri: The field to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>UseRandomDistibution</mark> converted.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyGravityAdapter: The LegacyGravityAdapter component is used in converted legacy particle systems that were converted to PhotonDust as part of The Performance Updates.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyLightsModuleAdapter: The LegacyLightsModuleAdapter component is used in converted legacy particle systems that were converted to PhotonDust as part of The Performance Updates.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyLightsModuleAdapter.Refere: The light to use as a target for converted values.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyLightsModuleAdapter.Lights: The field to drive with whether the lights module is enabled.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyRendererSwitcher: The LegacyRendererSwitcher component is used to switch between different rendering types depending on meshes.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyRendererSwitcher.ParticleM: The particle mesh object when not null will use <mark=#ffffff10>MeshRenderer</mark> for the renderer.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyRendererSwitcher.Billboard: Can be switched to in order to render the particles as squares.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyRendererSwitcher.MeshRende: Can be switched to in order to render the particles as meshes.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyRendererSwitcher.RendererD: Drives the target field with <mark=#ffffff10>BillboardRenderer</mark> or <mark=#ffffff10>MeshRenderer</mark> depending on if <mark=#ffffff10>ParticleMesh</mark> is null.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyRotationAdapter: The LegacyRotationAdapter component can be used to convert legacy rotation values to the new particle system values.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyRotationAdapter.ParticleMe: The mesh particle that was used with the original if any.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyRotationAdapter.UsingStret: Whether or not stretch was used with the particle.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyRotation3DAdapter: The LegacyRotation3DAdapter component can be used to convert legacy rotation values to the new particle system values.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyRotation3DAdapter.Particle: The mesh particle that was used with the original if any.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyRotation3DAdapter.UsingStr: Whether or not stretch was used with the particle.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyTrailsRibbonAdapter: The Legacy Trails Ribbon Adapter component is used to convert legacy content from the old particle system driven by Unity to Photon Dust.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyTrailsRibbonAdapter.Trails: The enum used to control the conversion from legacy trails to Photon Dust.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyTrailsRibbonAdapter.Partic: Whether the particle size should affect the trail width.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyTrailsRibbonAdapter.Inheri: Whether the trail should inherit color from the particle it is following.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyTrailsRibbonAdapter.TrailW: Whether the trail is in world or local space.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyTrailsRibbonAdapter.Trails: The enabled field on a trails module to drive.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyTrailsRibbonAdapter.Ribbon: The enabled field on a ribbons module to drive.
The use particle size field to drive on a ribbons module.
The use particle color field to drive on a ribbons module.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyTrailsRibbonAdapter.TrailS: The use particle size field to drive on a trails module.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyTrailsRibbonAdapter.TrailC: The use particle color field to drive on a trails module.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyTrailsRibbonAdapter.Trails: The trails space field to drive on a trails module.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleLightsModule: The ParticleLightsModule makes a particle system create lights every so often for emitted particles that follow the particle they're assigned to.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleLightsModule.TemplateLig: The light to use as a template.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleLightsModule.LightsRatio: How many lights as a percentage there should be from a 0 to 1 range.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleLightsModule.Distributio: How to distribute the lights among the particles being emitted.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleLightsModule.MaxLights: The maximum number of lights that the particle system can have.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleLightsModule.MultiplyCol: Whether to multiply the color of the lights by the color of the particle it is attached to.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleLightsModule.MultiplyInt: Whether to multiply the intensity of the lights by the alpha channel of the color of the particle it is attached to.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleLightsModule.MultiplyRan: Whether to multiply the range of the light based on the size of the particle it is attached to.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleLightsModule.AngleMultip: How to influence the angle of the light based on the angle of the particle it is following.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleLightsModule.RangeMultip: How much to amplify the range of the light.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleLightsModule.IntensityMu: How much to amplify the intensity of the light.
messages.Tooltip.RibbonFollowerWidthConstantIniti: The RibbonFollowerWidthConstantInitializer component makes all ribbon followers in a particle system have a starting width of Value.
messages.Tooltip.ConstantValueInitializer`2.Value: The starting rotation speed all particles should have along the X, Y, and Z axies.
messages.Tooltip.RibbonFollowerWidthRangeInitiali: The RibbonFollowerWidthRangeInitializer component gives ribbon followers in a particle system a random width in a range.
messages.Tooltip.RangeValueInitializer`2.MinValue: The minimum value a particle can have for rotation speed along the X, Y, and Z axies.
messages.Tooltip.RangeValueInitializer`2.MaxValue: The maximum value a particle can have for rotation speed along the X, Y, and Z axies.
messages.Tooltip.RibbonFollowerColorConstantIniti: The RibbonColorConstantInitializer gives all ribbon followers a color of Value upon start in a particle system.
messages.Tooltip.RibbonFollowerColorRangeInitiali: The RibbonFollowerColorRangeInitializer component gives ribbon followers in a particle system a random color in a range upon start.
messages.Tooltip.RibbonWidthConstantInitializer: The RibbonWidthConstantInitializer component makes all ribbons in a particle system have a starting width of Value.
messages.Tooltip.RibbonWidthRangeInitializer: The RibbonWidthRangeInitializer component gives ribbons in a particle system a random width in a range.
messages.Tooltip.RibbonColorConstantInitializer: The RibbonColorConstantInitializer gives all ribbons a color of Value upon start in a particle system.
messages.Tooltip.RibbonColorRangeInitializer: The RibbonColorRangeInitializer component gives ribbons in a particle system a random color in a range upon start.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleRibbonsModule: The ParticleRibbonsModule component makes it to where a particle system will have ribbons that connect particles based on distance and other factors.
messages.Tooltip.TrailRendererModule`1.Material: The material the particle trails should have.
messages.Tooltip.TrailRendererModule`1.TextureMod: How to handle viewing the texture specified on the ribbons.
messages.Tooltip.TrailRendererModule`1.MotionVect: How to handle the rendering of ribbons in regards to motion vectors.
messages.Tooltip.TrailRendererModule`1.GenerateLi: Whether to generate lighting data for the ribbons.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleRibbonsModule.RibbonPoin: How many of the particles should be ribbons.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleRibbonsModule.Distributi: How to distribute the ribbon followers.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleRibbonsModule.MaxRibbonP: The max amount of ribbon points there should be.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleRibbonsModule.Interweave: The amount of long particle-interconnected ribbons there can be, essentially multiple ribbons rather than one ribbon line.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleRibbonsModule.UseParticl: Whether to use the color of particles the ribbons are following.
Whether to use the size of particles the ribbons should follow.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleRibbonsModule.ShuffleInt: Randomly moves interweaved ribbon groups to new particles when they are generated.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleRibbonsModule.SizeInheri: How to inherit the size of the particles the ribbons are following.
messages.Tooltip.TrailLifetimeConstantInitializer: The TrailLifetimeConstantInitializer component gives trails in a particle system a constant starting Lifetime when born.
messages.Tooltip.TrailLifetimeRangeInitializer: The TrailLifetimeRangeInitializer component makes trails in a particle syste m be born with a random lifetime a point generated by them will exist before disappearing at the end of the trail.
messages.Tooltip.TrailWidthConstantInitializer: The TrailWidthConstantInitializer component makes all trails have the same width when born in a particle system.
messages.Tooltip.TrailWidthRangeInitializer: The TrailWidthRangeInitializer component makes trails in a particle system start with a random width within a range when born.
messages.Tooltip.TrailColorConstantInitializer: The TrailColorConstantInitializer component makes all trails in a particle system be born with or start with a color.
messages.Tooltip.TrailColorRangeInitializer: The TrailColorRangeInitializer component makes trails in a particle system be born or start with a random lerped color in a range when.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleTrailsModule: The ParticleTrailsModule component allows particles to have trails with different properties. This can be added multiple times for different trail types.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleTrailsModule.TrailsRatio: How many of the particles as a 0<->1 percentage should have trails
messages.Tooltip.ParticleTrailsModule.Distributio: How to distribute the followers for this module.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleTrailsModule.MaxTrails: The maximum amount of trails the system can have of this particular trail type.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleTrailsModule.SimulationS: What Transform space to simulate the trails for this module in?
messages.Tooltip.ParticleTrailsModule.MinVertexDi: The minimum vertex distance between trail segments when generating trail geometry.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleTrailsModule.TrailDiesWi: Whether the trail disappears instantly when the particle dies or not.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleTrailsModule.ParticleCol: How the trail inherits the color of the particle it's following.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleTrailsModule.ParticleSiz: How the trail inherits the size of the particle it's following.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleTrailsModule.SizeInherit: How the size the trail inherited from the particle it's followinh should be applied.
messages.Tooltip.SequenceRibbonSplitter: The SequenceRibbonSplitter splits ribbons based on how long they are at random.
messages.Tooltip.SequenceRibbonSplitter.MinSequen: The minimum ribbon sequence length a ribbon has to be before it is split.
messages.Tooltip.SequenceRibbonSplitter.MaxSequen: The maximum ribbon sequence length a ribbon can be before it is split forcibly.
messages.Tooltip.TimeProximityRibbonSplitter: The TimeProximityRibbonSplitter component splits ribbons based on how long the ribbons have been living for.
messages.Tooltip.TimeProximityRibbonSplitter.Inte: The time interval to split acords
messages.Tooltip.TimeProximityRibbonSplitter.Mode: How to split the ribbons based on the lifetime of Ribbon segments.
messages.Tooltip.TrailLifetimeFromSizeInitializer: The TrailLifetimeFromSizeInitializer component will give trails a lifetime depending on how much more or less size they have compared to a ref size.
The size to reference a trail size against when making a trail's lifetime.
messages.Tooltip.ColorHSV_RangeInitializer: The Color HSV Range Initializer component initializes a random range of HSV between two different sets for particles.
messages.Tooltip.ColorHSV_RangeInitializer.HueMin: The minimum Hue the particle can have.
messages.Tooltip.ColorHSV_RangeInitializer.HueMax: The maximum Hue the particle can have.
messages.Tooltip.ColorHSV_RangeInitializer.Satura: The minimum Saturation the particle can have.
The maximum Saturation the particle can have.
messages.Tooltip.ColorHSV_RangeInitializer.ValueM: The minimum Value the particle can have.
The maximum Value the particle can have.
messages.Tooltip.ColorListInitializer.Colors: A list of colors to use.
messages.Tooltip.LifetimeConstantInitializer: The LifeTimeConstantInitializer component makes all particles in a particle system start with a lifetime/duration of Value.
messages.Tooltip.SizeConstantInitializer: The SizeConstantInitializer component makes all particles start with with a scale of Value.
messages.Tooltip.UniformSizeConstantInitializer: The UniformSizeConstantInitalizer component makes particles in a particle system have a starting size of Value on all 3 axies.
messages.Tooltip.RotationConstantInitializer: The RotationConstantInitializer component gives all particles a starting rotation of Value along their orientation axis.
messages.Tooltip.Rotation3DConstantInitializer: The Rotation3DConstantInitializer component gives all particles a starting rotation of Value upon creation.
messages.Tooltip.ColorConstantInitializer: The ColorConstantInitializer component makes all particles start with the same color when they are born.
messages.Tooltip.SpeedConstantInitializer: The SpeedConstantInitializer component makes all particles in a particle system start with a speed of Value.
messages.Tooltip.AngularVelocityConstantInitializ: The AngularVelocityConstantInitializer component makes particles have a constant starting rotation speed within a range along their orientation axis when born.
messages.Tooltip.AngularVelocity3DConstantInitial: The AngularVelocity3DRangeInitializer component makes particles have a random starting rotation angular speed along the X, Y, and Z axies.
messages.Tooltip.LifetimeRangeInitializer: The LifetimeRangeInitializer component makes particles in a particle system start with a random lifetime/duration within a range.
messages.Tooltip.SizeRangeInitializer: The SizeRangeInitializer component make particles in a particle system start with a random size within a range.
messages.Tooltip.UniformSizeRangeInitializer: The UniformSizeRangeInitializer component makes particles in a particle system have a random size in a range.
messages.Tooltip.ColorRangeInitializer: The ColorRangeInitializer component gives particles a random color lerped between a min and Max color when they are born.
messages.Tooltip.RotationRangeInitializer: The RotationRangeInitializer component gives particles a random starting rotation within a range along it's orientation axis.
messages.Tooltip.Rotation3DEulerRangeInitializer: The Rotation3DEulerRangeInitializer component makes particles have a random rotation between a pair of Euler angles.
messages.Tooltip.SpeedRangeInitializer: The SpeedRangeInitializer component makes particles in a particle system start with a random speed in a range when initialized.
messages.Tooltip.AngularVelocityRangeInitializer: The AngularVelocityRangeInitializer component makes particles have a random rotation speed within a range along their orientation axis when born.
messages.Tooltip.AngularVelocity3DRangeInitialize: The AngularVelocity3DRangeInitializer component makes particles have a random starting rotation angular speed along the X, Y, and Z axies.
messages.Tooltip.AngularVelocityDrag: The Angular Velocity Drag component reduces the angular momentum of particles in a particle system over time.
messages.Tooltip.AngularVelocityDrag.Drag: How much force to apply to slow down. like resistance.
messages.Tooltip.ConstantAngularVelocityForce: The Constant Angular Velocity Force component applies constant angular velocity in euler angles to all particles in a particle system.
messages.Tooltip.ConstantAngularVelocityForce.For: The euler angles force to apply to particles.
messages.Tooltip.AlphaOverLifetimeLinearGradient: The AlphaOverLifetimeLinearGradient component implements the older alpha over lifetime system that came from Unity. It allows for particles to change alpha depending on how long they have existed for.
The Linear Gradient of alpha values to use over lifetime.
messages.Tooltip.ColorByOrientationDirection: The ColorByOrientationDirection component is used to make particles change color depending on their dot product of their orientation to a reference orientation. Dot product essentially means how close in terms of angle is one direction to another. The colors when in-between are lerped.
messages.Tooltip.ColorByOrientationDirection.Refe: The direction in local space that is being used as a reference.
messages.Tooltip.ColorByOrientationDirection.Alig: The color to use if the particle's orientation is exactly the same direction as <mark=#ffffff10>ReferenceDirection</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ColorByOrientationDirection.Orth: The color to use if the particle's orientation is exactly 90 degrees to <mark=#ffffff10>ReferenceDirection</mark>. This 90 degrees falls onto any particle direction that is along the surface of a plane facing the same direction as <mark=#ffffff10>ReferenceDirection</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ColorByOrientationDirection.Oppo: The color if the particle's orientation is exactly opposite of <mark=#ffffff10>ReferenceDirection</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ColorByOrientationDirectionSH1: The Color By Orientation Direction SH1 component samples a ColorX spherical harmonic based on the orientation vector of a particle in it's simulation space. that sampled color becomes the particle's color.
messages.Tooltip.ColorByOrientationDirectionSH2: The Color By Orientation Direction SH2 component samples a ColorX spherical harmonic based on the orientation vector of a particle in it's simulation space. that sampled color becomes the particle's color.
messages.Tooltip.ColorByOrientationDirectionSH3: The Color By Orientation Direction SH3 component samples a ColorX spherical harmonic based on the orientation vector of a particle in it's simulation space. that sampled color becomes the particle's color.
messages.Tooltip.ColorByOrientationDirectionSH4: The Color By Orientation Direction SH4 component samples a ColorX spherical harmonic based on the orientation vector of a particle in it's simulation space. that sampled color becomes the particle's color.
messages.Tooltip.ColorBySpeedMinMax: The Color By Speed Min Max component makes particles in a particle system change their color based on their speed using a minimum and maximum speed value. Lerps between min and max colors.
messages.Tooltip.ColorBySpeedMinMax.MinSpeed: The speed the particle has to be be traveling at or under to become <mark=#ffffff10>MinColor</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ColorBySpeedMinMax.MaxSpeed: The speed the particle has to be be traveling at or above to become <mark=#ffffff10>MaxColor</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ColorBySpeedMinMax.MinColor: The color to display when traveling at or below <mark=#ffffff10>MinSpeed</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ColorBySpeedMinMax.MaxColor: The color to display when traveling at or above <mark=#ffffff10>MaxSpeed</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ColorBySpeedMinMaxHSV: The Color By Speed Min Max HSV component changes the HSV values of particles in a particle system depending on their speed.
messages.Tooltip.ColorBySpeedMinMaxHSV.MinSpeed: The speed the particle has to be be traveling at or under to be considered minimum speed.
messages.Tooltip.ColorBySpeedMinMaxHSV.MaxSpeed: The speed the particle has to be be traveling at or above to be considered maximum speed.
messages.Tooltip.ColorBySpeedMinMaxHSV.MinHue: The Hue to display when traveling at or below <mark=#ffffff10>MinSpeed</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ColorBySpeedMinMaxHSV.MaxHue: The Hue to display when traveling at or above <mark=#ffffff10>MaxSpeed</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ColorBySpeedMinMaxHSV.MinSaturat: The Saturation to display when traveling at or below <mark=#ffffff10>MinSpeed</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ColorBySpeedMinMaxHSV.MaxSaturat: The Saturation to display when traveling at or above <mark=#ffffff10>MaxSpeed</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ColorBySpeedMinMaxHSV.MinValue: The Value to display when traveling at or below <mark=#ffffff10>MinSpeed</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ColorBySpeedMinMaxHSV.MaxValue: The Value to display when traveling at or above <mark=#ffffff10>MaxSpeed</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ColorBySpeedTexture: The Color By Speed Texture component samples from the x axis of the UV depending on the min and max speeds being a 0-1 range and values beyond the range being extrapolated.
messages.Tooltip.ColorBySpeedTexture.MinSpeed: The speed the particle has to be be traveling at or under to be considered minimum speed.
messages.Tooltip.ColorBySpeedTexture.MaxSpeed: The speed the particle has to be be traveling at or above to be considered maximum speed.
messages.Tooltip.ColorBySpeedTexture.U_WrapMode: How to wrap around the X axis.
messages.Tooltip.ColorBySpeedTexture.Texture: The texture to sample color from.
messages.Tooltip.ColorByThreadDiagnosticModule: The ColorByThreadDiagnosticModule component makes particles colored based on what the thread is doing and/or what thread they are a part of as part of the Photon Dust Simulation.
messages.Tooltip.ColorByVelocityDirection: The Color By Velocity Direction component changes the color of a particle depending on the dot product of it's velocity vs a specified axis.
messages.Tooltip.ColorByVelocityDirection.Referen: The direction to make a dot product with the particle's velocity.
messages.Tooltip.ColorByVelocityDirection.Aligned: The color the particle should be when it has velocity that gives dot product of 1 with <mark=#ffffff10>ReferenceDirection</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ColorByVelocityDirection.Orthogo: The color the particle should be when it has velocity that gives dot product of 0 with <mark=#ffffff10>ReferenceDirection</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ColorByVelocityDirection.Opposit: The color the particle should be when it has velocity that gives dot product of -1 with <mark=#ffffff10>ReferenceDirection</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ColorByVelocityDirectionSH1: The Color By Velocity Direction SH1 component samples a ColorX spherical harmonic based on the velocity vector of a particle in it's simulation space. that sampled color becomes the particle's color.
messages.Tooltip.ColorByVelocityDirectionSH2: The Color By Velocity Direction SH2 component samples a ColorX spherical harmonic based on the velocity vector of a particle in it's simulation space. that sampled color becomes the particle's color.
messages.Tooltip.ColorByVelocityDirectionSH3: The Color By Velocity Direction SH3 component samples a ColorX spherical harmonic based on the velocity vector of a particle in it's simulation space. that sampled color becomes the particle's color.
messages.Tooltip.ColorByVelocityDirectionSH4: The Color By Velocity Direction SH4 component samples a ColorX spherical harmonic based on the velocity vector of a particle in it's simulation space. that sampled color becomes the particle's color.
messages.Tooltip.ColorFromTexture3D: The ColorFromTexture3D component makes particles sample their color from the color in their same position within a Texture3D.
messages.Tooltip.ColorFromTexture3D.Scale: How much bias to choose higher axis values. The default is 1 which is no bias.
messages.Tooltip.ColorFromTexture3D.Offset: How much to add to the chosen point in the 3D texture, which can cut off lower or higher values from ever being chosen.
messages.Tooltip.ColorHSV_OverLifetimeStartEnd: The ColorHSV_OverLifetimeStartEnd component makes particles have an HSV value that lerps from start to end over the particle's lifetime.
messages.Tooltip.ColorHSV_OverLifetimeStartEnd.St: The starting hue at birth of a particle.
The starting saturation at birth of a particle.
The starting value at birth of a particle.
messages.Tooltip.ColorHSV_OverLifetimeStartEnd.En: The ending hue at death of a particle.
The ending staturation at death of a particle.
The ending value at death of a particle.
messages.Tooltip.ColorOverLifetimeLinearGradient: The ColorOverLifetimeLinearGradient component implements the older color over lifetime system that came from Unity. It allows for particles to change color depending on how long they have existed for.
A Linear Gradient of colors and points to use to give particles color over lifetime.
messages.Tooltip.ColorOverLifetimeStartEnd: The ColorOverLifetimeStartEnd component makes particles in a Particle system change color linearly over the duration of their lifetime.
messages.Tooltip.ColorOverLifetimeStartEnd.StartC: The color particles should be when emitted.
messages.Tooltip.ColorOverLifetimeStartEnd.EndCol: The color particles should lerp towards over their lifetime and they will have this color right before they die.
messages.Tooltip.ColorOverLifetimeTexture: The ColorOverLifetimeTexture component samples a texture during life time along the X axis to get the color that a Particle should be.
messages.Tooltip.ColorOverLifetimeTexture.Texture: The texture to sample along the X axis based on Lifetime normalized to get a color for particles.
messages.Tooltip.PivotFromVelocityMagnitude: The Pivot From Velocity Magnitude component is used in Photon Dust to make particles rotate based on their velocity direction on a pivot.
messages.Tooltip.PositionSimulatorModule: The PositionSimulatorModule component is the most essential particle simulator module. Without it, particles will not move, and won't have velocity.
messages.Tooltip.PositionSimulatorModule.Collisio: How much lifetime percentage from 0<->1 should particles loose when hitting a surface?
How much velocity should particles retain as a percentage from 0<->1 when bouncing off of a surface?
messages.Tooltip.OrientAtPoint: The Orient At Point component makes particles in a particle system orient at a point in a specified transform space.
messages.Tooltip.OrientAtPoint.PointSpace: The tranform space <mark=#ffffff10>TargetPoint</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>Up</mark> are in.
messages.Tooltip.OrientAtPoint.TargetPoint: The point that particles should point at.
messages.Tooltip.OrientAtPoint.Up: The direction that should be up locally for each particle.
messages.Tooltip.OrientByVelocity: The OrientByVelocity component makes particles orient the Up axis of the particle in the direction they are moving.
messages.Tooltip.OrientByVelocity.Up: What axis of the particle to orient in the direction the particle is moving.
messages.Tooltip.RotationSimulatorModule: The RotationSimulatorModule component is the second most essential particle simulator module. Without it, particles will not have orientation, or have Angular velocity.
messages.Tooltip.SizeModifier: The SizeModifier component modifies the size of particles as an extra effect on top of existing size modifiers.
messages.Tooltip.SizeModifier.Multiplier: How much to multiply the size of all particles.
messages.Tooltip.SizeModifier.Offset: How much to add to the size of all particles.
messages.Tooltip.SizeModifier.SizeClampMin: The minimum size particles can be.
messages.Tooltip.SizeModifier.SizeClampMax: The maximum size particles can be.
messages.Tooltip.SizeMultiplierByVelocity: The Size Multiplier By Velocity component is used in Photon Dust systems to make particles change size on any given axies based on velocity.
messages.Tooltip.SizeMultiplierByVelocity.Velocit: The amount to multiply velocity of a particle by before using it as a multiplier.
Which axies should be included in or excluded from this scaling effect.
messages.Tooltip.SizeMultiplierByVelocity.Multipl: the minimum value to clamp the current velocity of a particle to before it is used as a scale multiplier.
the maximum value to clamp the current velocity of a particle to before it is used as a scale multiplier.
messages.Tooltip.SizeMultiplierByVelocity.ResultC: the minimum scale the particle is allowed to be as a final scale.
the maximum scale the particle is allowed to be as a final scale.
messages.Tooltip.SizeOffsetByVelocity: The SizeOffsetByVelocity component changes the size of particles in a particle system based on their velocity.
messages.Tooltip.SizeOffsetByVelocity.VelocityMul: How much velocity should multiply the size of the particle.
messages.Tooltip.SizeOffsetByVelocity.OffsetClamp: The minimum value for the clamping of the offset.
The maximum value for the clamping of the offset.
messages.Tooltip.SizeOffsetByVelocity.ResultClamp: The minimum value for the clamping of the result.
The maximum value for the clamping of the result.
messages.Tooltip.SizeOverLifetimeStartEnd: The SizeOverLifetimeStartEnd component makes particles in a particle system change size as their lifetime duration progresses.
messages.Tooltip.SizeOverLifetimeStartEnd.StartSi: The size particles will have at the beginning of their lifetime.
messages.Tooltip.SizeOverLifetimeStartEnd.EndSize: The size particles will have at the end or their lifetime.
messages.Tooltip.UniformSizeOverLifetimeStartEnd: The UniformSizeOverLifetimeStartEnd component makes particles in a particle system start with a size and lerp towards an end size over their lifetime.
The size that particles have when starting or being born.
The size that particles go towards as their lifetime decreases.
messages.Tooltip.SizeOverLifetimeTexture.Texture: The texture to sample for size over lifetime.
messages.Tooltip.SizeSineMultiplier: The SizeSineMultiplier component makes particles multiply their size on start with a value sampled from a Sine Wave based on the time they were emitted in a particle system.
messages.Tooltip.SizeSineMultiplier.Speed: The speed that the Sine Wave changes.
messages.Tooltip.SizeSineMultiplier.Offset: The initial offset of the Sine Wave function.
messages.Tooltip.SizeSineMultiplier.MinMultiplier: The minimum value outputted by the sinewave function.
messages.Tooltip.SizeSineMultiplier.MaxMultiplier: The maximum value outputted by the sinewave function.
messages.Tooltip.UniformSizeSineMultiplier: The UniformSizeSineMultiplier component makes particles start their size multipled by the current point in a constantly changing Sine wave.
messages.Tooltip.UniformSizeSineMultiplier.Speed: The speed of the Sine Wave change.
messages.Tooltip.UniformSizeSineMultiplier.Offset: The initial offset of the Sine Wave.
messages.Tooltip.UniformSizeSineMultiplier.MinMul: The minimum Sine Wave value that can be outputted and multipled with the particle's starting size.
messages.Tooltip.UniformSizeSineMultiplier.MaxMul: The maximum Sine Wave value that can be outputted and multipled with the particle's starting size.
messages.Tooltip.TextureSheetAnimator: The TextureSheetAnimator component makes particles animate their texture over lifetime or duration.
messages.Tooltip.TextureSheetAnimator.TileGridSiz: The amount of cells and rows that the texture atlas sheet has.
messages.Tooltip.TextureSheetAnimator.AnimationCy: How many times to cycle the animation during the specified time frame.
messages.Tooltip.TextureSheetAnimator.AnimationTy: The animation type to use for the texture sheet animation.
messages.Tooltip.TextureSheetAnimator.RowIndex: The row index to start on.
messages.Tooltip.TextureSheetAnimator.UseRandomRo: Whether to start the texture sheet animation in a random row.
messages.Tooltip.TextureSheetAnimator.StartWithRa: Start the texture sheet animation in a random column.
messages.Tooltip.GravityForce: The GravityForce component acts as a global force for particles that makes them fall either up or down along the Up axis.
messages.Tooltip.GravityForce.Gravity: The force to apply. Negative values are down and positive values are up.
messages.Tooltip.GravityForce.OverrideForceSpace: The space to calculate gravity force in. Is converted into the particle system's space.
messages.Tooltip.LinearForce: The LinearForce component applies force to all particles within a system towards a direction regardless of particle position.
messages.Tooltip.LinearForce.Force: The direction to force particles in.
messages.Tooltip.LinearForce.OverrideForceSpace: What Transform space the force direction is in rather than in the particle system's local space.
messages.Tooltip.OriginRadialForce: The Origin Radial Force component is used with Photon Dust to make particles have force applied on them to make them return to their original starting point over time. This was a personal addition by Frooxius.
messages.Tooltip.RadialForceBase`1.Force: The constant force applied to particles to make them return to where they started.
messages.Tooltip.RadialForceBase`1.Mode: how the Radial area and effect for this component is defined.
messages.Tooltip.RadialForceBase`1.MinDistance: The minimum distance for the force gradient, aka force gradient start point, the gradient does not stop here.
messages.Tooltip.RadialForceBase`1.MaxDistance: The maximum distance for the force gradient, aka force gradient start point, the gradient does not stop here.
messages.Tooltip.RadialForceBase`1.MinForce: how much force should be applied at <mark=#ffffff10>MinDistance</mark> modified by <mark=#ffffff10>Force</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.RadialForceBase`1.MaxForce: how much force should be applied at <mark=#ffffff10>MaxDistance</mark> modified by <mark=#ffffff10>Force</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.RadialForceBase`1.OverrideForceS: The space to calculate this component's values in. The values get converted to the particle system's space.
messages.Tooltip.RadialForce: The RadialForce component makes particles attracted or repelled from a point in 3D space when they get closer or further away. The gradient for the force does not cut off at MinDistance and/or MaxDistance. instead the gradient continues into infinity at the value it was when it hits these values. The Force determines the distance truely if MinDistance is at 0 and MaxDistance is at infinity.
messages.Tooltip.RadialForce.Center: The center point of the force point.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTurbulentForce: The SimplexTurbulentForce component is used to make random noise throughout an entire particle system that affects particles moving through it by changing their movement.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTurbulentForce.Mode: How to affect Particles in terms of force.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTurbulentForce.Strength: How strong is the force effect.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTurbulentForce.GlobalNois: The 4th dimensional offset of the noise. Essentially a seed.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTurbulentForce.Scale: How much to scale up or down the noise from it's center.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTurbulentForce.Offset: How much to shift the noise on its axies.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTurbulentForce.X_NoiseOff: How much to offset the noise on the X axis.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTurbulentForce.Y_NoiseOff: How much to offset the noise on the Y axis.
messages.Tooltip.SimplexTurbulentForce.Z_NoiseOff: How much to offset the noise on the Z axis.
messages.Tooltip.Texture3D_Force: The Texture3DForce component makes a 3 dimensional field that affects the movement of particles in a particle system based on the colors of a 3D texture. The colors map from RGB->XYZ in that order. The XYZ mapped value is used to affect the movement of particles.
messages.Tooltip.Texture3D_Force.Texture3D: The 3D texture to act as the force volume and to sample colors from in 3D.
messages.Tooltip.Texture3D_Force.Mode: How to affect Particles with the force this component exerts on them.
messages.Tooltip.Texture3D_Force.Strength: The strength of this component's forvce.
messages.Tooltip.Texture3D_Force.Scale: How much to scale the 3D texture in physical space along it's axies.
messages.Tooltip.Texture3D_Force.Offset: How much to shift the 3D texture in physical space on it's axies.
messages.Tooltip.Texture3D_Force.ColorBias: The bias of colors being sampled from the 3D texture that are being mapped into XYZ values.
messages.Tooltip.Texture3D_Force.ColorScale: How much to scale the color of pixels in the 3D texture, to change how the force works.
messages.Tooltip.VelocityDrag: The VelocityDrag component makes particles in a particle system slow down towards 0 Velocity when not acted upon as if moving through a substance.
messages.Tooltip.VelocityDrag.Drag: How much to slow down particles to 0 velocity.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleStyle: A Particle Style defines the visual appearance and behavior for a ParticleSystem.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleStyle.Renderer: How to render the particles for systems this is assigned to.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleStyle.Modules: A list of modules to influence how the particle system looks and behaves.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleStyle.UseSystemLocalScal: Whether particles should rely on the particle system's simulation space for scale.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleStyle.ParticleScaleMode: How to scale particles during simulation for this style.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleStyle.UseSystemLocalRota: Whether particles should rely on the particle system's simulation space for rotation.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleSystem: With a particle system, multiple emitters can use the same source ParticleStyle.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleSystem.Style: The ParticleStyle component that this component is using.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleSystem.SimulationSpace: The coordinate space the emitted particles will do calculations and physics under. (They are not actually placed under this slot, but they visually behave as though they are).
messages.Tooltip.ParticleSystemMetadata: The Particle System Metadata component gives running status numbers for a given ParticleSystem.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleSystemMetadata.System: the particle system to get data from.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleSystemMetadata.LastSimul: The seconds needed to do the last simulation of <mark=#ffffff10>System</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleSystemMetadata.LastSubmi: the seconds needed to do the last submission of data to the renderer for <mark=#ffffff10>System</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleSystemMetadata.RenderDat: The amount of reallocation needed for the render data for <mark=#ffffff10>System</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleSystemMetadata.TrailCoun: The amount of current trails in <mark=#ffffff10>System</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleSystemMetadata.TrailCapa: The trail capacity for trail point groups of the current trail buffer pool of <mark=#ffffff10>System</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleSystemMetadata.TrailPoin: The trail capacity for trail points of the current trail buffer pool of <mark=#ffffff10>System</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ParticleSystemMetadata.TrailsDat: The amount of reallocation needed for the render data for trails of <mark=#ffffff10>System</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.BillboardParticleRenderer: The BillboardParticleRenderer component is a rendering style component for Particle systems that give them the default square tile based particles.
messages.Tooltip.BillboardParticleRenderer.Materi: The material to use to render the particles.
messages.Tooltip.BillboardParticleRenderer.MinBil: The minimum size a particle can be on the screen.
messages.Tooltip.BillboardParticleRenderer.MaxBil: The maximum size a particle can be on the screen.
messages.Tooltip.BillboardParticleRenderer.Motion: How to handle rendering particles when it comes to their motion vectors.
messages.Tooltip.DebugQuadMeshRenderer: The DebugQuadMeshRenderer component renders particles via a Component:QuadArrayMesh.
messages.Tooltip.DebugQuadMeshRenderer.Material: The material to use for the mesh.
messages.Tooltip.MeshParticleRenderer: The MeshParticleRenderer component is a particle renderer that makes all particle instances in a particle system render as a specific mesh.
messages.Tooltip.MeshParticleRenderer.Material: The material the particles should have.
messages.Tooltip.MeshParticleRenderer.Mesh: The mesh object to GPU instance to all particle points and render.
messages.Tooltip.MeshParticleRenderer.Alignment: How to align the meshes with the particle transforms.
messages.Tooltip.NullParticleRenderer: The Null Particle Renderer component is used with Photon Dust to render zero geometry particles.
messages.Tooltip.PointCloudParticleRenderer: The PointCloudParticleRenderer component is used to render particles as dots with size using a material
messages.Tooltip.IKDraggableOffset: The IKDraggableOffset component is generated as part of newly imported humanoid avatars. It allows users to grab (meaning it's an IGrabbable) and move parts of a model to test how it moves before making it an avatar.
messages.Tooltip.IKDraggableOffset.Solver: The IK solver that this is currently controlling. See IKSolver for what this could be.
messages.Tooltip.IKDraggableOffset.PositionTarget: The position field to influence when grabbing.
messages.Tooltip.IKDraggableOffset.RotationTarget: The rotation field to influence when grabbing.
messages.Tooltip.IKDraggableOffset.Weight: The weight field to influence when grabbing.
messages.Tooltip.IKDraggableOffset._grabber: The grabber component currently grabbing this.
messages.Tooltip.IKDraggableOffset._holdSlot: The slot currently holding this component's slot for grabbing.
messages.Tooltip.IKDraggableOffset._posOffset: The position offset this component is applying to <mark=#ffffff10>Solver</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.IKDraggableOffset._rotOffset: The rotation offset this component is applying to <mark=#ffffff10>Solver</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.VRIK: All weight values are 1-0, excluding clamps which are inverted 0-1.
messages.Tooltip.VRIK.AutoUpdate: Whether to update the VRIK regardless of whether or not the sync delegates are called by a VRIK avatar or similar sources. this can break systems still using a VRIKAvatar but can fix ones with a lack of such component.
messages.Tooltip.VRIK.FixTransformsEnabled: Clamps IK transforms to reasonable values and Resets IK every update.
messages.Tooltip.VRIK.Solver: The actual solver that contains most of the fields of this component.
messages.Tooltip.VRIK.componentInitiated: Marks this component as fully started and ready to solve IK transforms.
messages.Tooltip.VRIK._drives: A list of fields to drive with <mark=#ffffff10>defaultLocalPositions</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>defaultLocalRotations</mark> plus transforms solved by the IK using <mark=#ffffff10>defaultLocalPositions</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>defaultLocalRotations</mark>  as a basis.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar: The VRIKAvatar component is used to drive and control the VRIK component on an Avatar.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.HeightCompensation: How much to scale up or down the user's avatar compared to the tracking points. Think a multiplier to avatar scale vs user root.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.AvatarHeight: The height of the avatar from floor to eyes in meters.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.UserResizeThreshold: How much the user has to be different from original scale in percentage before prompting to reset scale.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.FeetIgnoreOtherPlayer: Whether feet raycasts ignore other users.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.FeetCollisionListMode: The mode to use for feet raycasting whitelist/blacklist.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.FeetCollisionList: The list of items to filter to/from raycasting according to <mark=#ffffff10>FeetCollisionListMode</mark>
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.HeadMaxFixDistance: The maximum distance to allow fixing head position due to bad IK.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.ForceUseFeetProxies: Whether to force the use of Feet proxy/tracker objects.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.ForceUsePelvisProxy: Whether to force the use of Pelvis proxy/tracker objects.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.ForceUseChestProxy: Whether to force the use of Chest proxy/tracker objects.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.ForceUseElbowProxies: Whether to force the use of Elbow proxy/tracker objects.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.ForceUseKneeProxies: Whether to force the use of Knee proxy/tracker objects.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.FeetCalibrated: Whether or not the feet are calibrated.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.PelvisCalibrated: Whether or not the pelvis is calibrated.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.GroundCheckHeightRati: How far to check compared to user height for the floor (unused)
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.FeetHoverHeight: The height at which feet should hover in either up or down from rest position when off the floor.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.FeetHoverSmoothSpeed: How much to smooth the feet movement when hovering.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.MinFeetTransitionSpee: The minimum speed at random to transition to different modes.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.MaxFeetTransitionSpee: The maximum speed at random to transition to different modes.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.GaitFeetTransitionSpe: How much to multiply the user's speed to determine the gait speed.
