messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.FeetHoverTilt: How much to tilt the feet when hovering.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.LeftFootFloatOffset: How much to offset the left foot when starting hovering.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.RightFootFloatOffset: How much to offset the right foot when starting hovering.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.LeftFootRootHeight: How much to move up the left foot when starting hovering.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.RightFootRootHeight: How much to move up the right foot when starting hovering.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.FootFloatSpeed: How fast the feet should move when hovering.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.FootFloatAngleMagnitu: How much the feet angle should change when hovering.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.FootFloatOffsetMagnit: How much the feet should wander from avatar center when hovering.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.FeetFloatVelocityForc: How much velocity to apply to the feet hovering simulation when moving.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.FeetFloatVelocityDamp: How much to dampen velocity over time for the feet hovering simulation.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.MaxFeetVelocityOffset: The maximum amount that the feet can wander from avatar center.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.VelocityAverageRate: The average amount of velocity the user has while moving in user transform space.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.HoverVelocityThreshol: The threshold for velocity the user needs to be to be hovering.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.HorizontalBodyAngle: The angle required for the user to be considered horizontal for their hips.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.SupressWalkAnimationW: Whether or not to suppress the walking animation when the user is horizontal.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.AlwaysUseTrackersWhen: Whether or not to always override and use trackers when the user is horizontal.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.Gaits: A list of gaits to use when moving (Now is unused).
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.GaitTransitionSpeed: How fast to transition between different gaits (Now is unused).
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.GaitMovementDirection: How much to smooth the gait feet movement direction (Now is unused).
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.RigCollidersRadiusRat: The ratio between the avatar limb length and collider radius sizes.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.LeftHandRotationOffse: The offset for the left hand rotation versus controller rotation.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.RightHandRotationOffs: The offset for the right hand rotation versus controller rotation.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.CurrentAverageVelocit: The average amount of velocity the user has while moving in user transform space.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.CurrentOnGround: Whether or not the user is currently is on the ground.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.CurrentGaitIndex: The current walk animation the user is using (Now is unused).
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar._locomotionController: The current locomotion module the user is using.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar._leftHandNode: The avatar pose node for posing/IK for the left hand.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar._rightHandNode: The avatar pose node for posing/IK for the right hand.

messages.Tooltip.ReferenceReceiver: The ReferenceReceiver component can receive a reference dropped onto a UIX element Button by a user. The reference dropped is then stored into the field specified by TargetReference (an example would be to use the ReferenceField).
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceReceiver.TargetReferenc: The target field to fill when a reference is received.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceReceiver.Undoable: Whether dropping elements onto this UIX interactable is undoable.
messages.Tooltip.Canvas: This component is needed for UIX, without it, your other components that relay on UIX, canvas, elements, and rects will not work as expected.
messages.Tooltip.Canvas.Size: The dimensions of the canvas. At normal scale, this is equivalent to meters
messages.Tooltip.Canvas.EditModeOnly: This makes this component only editable in Edit Mode
messages.Tooltip.Canvas.AcceptRemoteTouch: Whether this component allows interaction via the user's interaction laser.
messages.Tooltip.Canvas.AcceptPhysicalTouch: Whether to allow Component:TipTouchSources to interact with/activate this component.
messages.Tooltip.Canvas.AcceptExistingTouch: If this canvas is already being touched (physically or remotely), accept the input
messages.Tooltip.Canvas.HighPriorityIntegration: If true, integrating this asset (e.g. processing procedural assets) gets higher priority than assets with this flag off. An example is user laser procedural meshes.
messages.Tooltip.Canvas.IgnoreTouchesFromBehind: Makes the canvas ignore all touches from behind
messages.Tooltip.Canvas.LaserPassThrough: Allows the laser to go through the canvas
messages.Tooltip.Canvas.PixelScale: Sets the pixel scale for this canvas and its contents
messages.Tooltip.Canvas.UnitScale: Scales the contents of this canvas, higher number makes the contents smaller
messages.Tooltip.Canvas._rootRect: Internal - The root rect of this canvas
messages.Tooltip.Canvas.Collider: The collider that receives touches from this canvas
messages.Tooltip.Canvas._colliderSize: Internal - Takes in a box collider and uses it for the canvas
messages.Tooltip.Canvas._colliderOffset: Internal - Offsets this box collider for this canvas
messages.Tooltip.Canvas.StartingOffset: The visibility order of rendering this canvas (lower number gets drawn over higher numbers)
messages.Tooltip.ButtonRelay: This can send a signal internally.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonRelayBase.DoublePressDelay: how long it takes before it is to late to count as a double press.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonRelayBase.ReleasePressInte: The amount of time it takes to release the button.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonRelay.ButtonPressed: The place to send the signal.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonRelay`1: This can send a signal internally.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonRelay`1.ButtonPressed: The place to send the signal.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonRefRelay`1: This can send a signal internally.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonRefRelay`1.ButtonPressed: The place to send the signal.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonDelegateRelay`1: This can send a signal internally.
messages.Tooltip.ButtonDelegateRelay`1.ButtonPres: The place to send the signal.
messages.Tooltip.DualColorImage: The DualColorImage component takes in a SpriteProvider or Material and controls the tint and secondary tint of a UIX image. There are optional placement parameters allowing the user to adjust the offset of this image using FillRect.
messages.Tooltip.ImageBase.Sprite: The sprite to use as the image.
messages.Tooltip.ImageBase.Material: The material to render with. is not required.
messages.Tooltip.ImageBase.PreserveAspect: Preserves the aspect ratio of this image provided.
messages.Tooltip.ImageBase.NineSliceSizing: Tells how the image gets 9-sliced on this UIX.
messages.Tooltip.ImageBase.FlipHorizontally: Flips the image horizontally (but not the color).
messages.Tooltip.ImageBase.FlipVertically: Flips the image vertically (but not the color).
messages.Tooltip.ImageBase.InteractionTarget: Makes this image as the interaction target for this UIX.
messages.Tooltip.ImageBase.FillRect: The filling rect for this image.
messages.Tooltip.ImageBase.__legacyZWrite: Internal - The legacy Z writing for this image.
messages.Tooltip.Image.Tint: A color that is multiplied with the material's color.
messages.Tooltip.DualColorImage.SecondaryTint: The secondary tint color for this image.
messages.Tooltip.GradientImage: You can make fancy effects and icons using this component, especially if your hue shifting the different color corners.
messages.Tooltip.GradientImage.TintTopLeft: The color of this top left corner.
messages.Tooltip.GradientImage.TintTopRight: The color of this top right corner.
messages.Tooltip.GradientImage.TintBottomRight: The color of this bottom right corner.
messages.Tooltip.GradientImage.TintBottomLeft: The color of this bottom left corner.
messages.Tooltip.Image: A user can use this to just display an image with an optional tint color, or have this as a background image for other images to be on top of.
messages.Tooltip.Mask: This can be used for fancy cutout effects.
messages.Tooltip.Mask.ShowMaskGraphic: Shows the graphic being used to do the masking for debug purposes.
messages.Tooltip.OutlinedArc: A user could make fancy effects like a [https://en.
messages.Tooltip.OutlinedArc.Arc: The amount to arc around the center.
messages.Tooltip.OutlinedArc.Offset: The amount to rotate around the center.
messages.Tooltip.OutlinedArc.OuterRadiusRatio: The outer distance for this arc from the center.
messages.Tooltip.OutlinedArc.InnerRadiusRatio: The inner distance for this arc from the center.
messages.Tooltip.OutlinedArc.RoundedCornerRadius: The amount of how rounded the edges of the arc end points are.
messages.Tooltip.OutlinedArc.FillColor: The inner color (filled in color) of this arc.

messages.Tooltip.OutlinedArc.OutlineThickness: The amount to thicken for the outline.
messages.Tooltip.OutlinedArc.Material: Uses a material for the arc.
messages.Tooltip.RawGraphic: Use this component when the Image component does not have what your looking for in your UIX design.

messages.Tooltip.RawGraphic.PropertyBlock: The image within a literal image block.
messages.Tooltip.RawGraphic.FillRect: The filling rect for this image.
messages.Tooltip.RawGraphic.Color: Changes the color of the image.
messages.Tooltip.RawGraphic.UVRect: Shifts the UV of the raw image.
messages.Tooltip.RawGraphic.Orientation: Rotates the raw image and respects aspect ratio.
messages.Tooltip.RawGraphic.Normal: The normal for this raw graphic.
messages.Tooltip.RawGraphic.Tangent: The tangent for this raw graphic.

messages.Tooltip.RawGraphic.PreserveUVAspectRatio: If this raw graphic should preserve its aspect ratio.
messages.Tooltip.RawGraphic.InteractionTarget: Makes this image as the interaction target for this UIX.
messages.Tooltip.RawImage: This component acts more like a holder for a texture image to be used by other components.

messages.Tooltip.RawImage.Tint: The tint color for this raw image.

messages.Tooltip.RawImage.PreserveAspect: If this raw graphic should preserve its aspect ratio.

messages.Tooltip.RectMesh`1: This can be used to make fancy audio visuals from a user's Audio Stream Controller.

messages.Tooltip.RectMesh`1.Materials: The material to render for this rect mesh.
messages.Tooltip.Text: Text can be used to explain something, to be descriptive, to notify, and many other things text can be used for.
messages.Tooltip.Text.Content: What string of text to display
messages.Tooltip.Text.ParseRichText: Whether or not to interpret text formatting
messages.Tooltip.Text.NullContent: What to display if Content is empty
messages.Tooltip.Text.Size: The size to render text at, in display units

messages.Tooltip.Text.VerticalAlign: How to align the text, vertically
messages.Tooltip.Text.Color: The color to render the text with
messages.Tooltip.Text.LineHeight: How high each line of text is

messages.Tooltip.Text.VerticalAutoSize: Automatically adjusts the text's size to fit in its vertical space

messages.Tooltip.Button: The Button component is an interactive UIX element that allows for users to click on its RectTransform. It can provide visual feedback by altering the colors of graphics on the RectTransform Slot, such as the Image component. This is able to trigger several other components, such as Text Field, by using the Button Events system.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionElement.BaseColor: The color that all other tints will be based on
messages.Tooltip.InteractionElement.ColorDrivers: A list of sets of colors. Each one points at another component's color, and determines how it looks normally, when highlighted, when pressed, and when disabled
messages.Tooltip.InteractionElement.IsPressed: Check if the user is pressing on the slider handle.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionElement.IsHovering: Check if the user is hovering on the slider handle.
messages.Tooltip.Button.HoverVibrate: How a controller should vibrate when hovering over this button
messages.Tooltip.Button.PressVibrate: How a controller should vibrate when pressing this button
messages.Tooltip.Button.ClearFocusOnPress: If set, will defocus any currently focused TextEditor, DesktopInteractionRelay, or any other IFocusable when this button is pressed. Defaults to true.
messages.Tooltip.Button.PassThroughHorizontalMove: Whether or not press-and-drag movement should be passed to higher components
messages.Tooltip.Button.PassThroughVerticalMoveme: Whether or not press-and-drag movement should be passed to higher components
messages.Tooltip.Button.RequireInitialPress: Internal - Check for the initial press of the button. Defaults to true.
messages.Tooltip.Button.PressPoint: The (x,y) coordinate where the button is being pressed
messages.Tooltip.Button.SendSlotEvents: If set, all Pressing, Pressed, Released, HoverEnter, HoverStay, and HoverLeave events are sent to all IButtonPressReceiver components within this component's slot. Defaults to true.
messages.Tooltip.Button.Pressed: Delegate. If set, this function is called with the Pressed event. Defaults to unset.
messages.Tooltip.Button.Pressing: Delegate. If set, this function is called with the Pressing event. Defaults to unset.
messages.Tooltip.Button.Released: Delegate. If set, this function is called with the Released event. Defaults to unset.
messages.Tooltip.Button.HoverEnter: Delegate. If set, this function is called with the HoverEnter event. Defaults to unset.
messages.Tooltip.Button.HoverStay: Delegate. If set, this function is called with the HoverStay event. Defaults to unset.
messages.Tooltip.Button.HoverLeave: Delegate. If set, this function is called with the HoverLeave event. Defaults to unset.
messages.Tooltip.Checkbox: Checkboxes are great for settings and options in your designs.
messages.Tooltip.Checkbox.State: The current state of thie check box.
messages.Tooltip.Checkbox.TargetState: The Bool field to drive with the state of the checkbox.
messages.Tooltip.Checkbox.CheckVisual: The Bool field controlling visibility of the visual to display when the checkbox is in the checked state.
messages.Tooltip.DebugInteractable: The DebugInteractable component is a UIX interactable that changes the color of an image on the same slot when the component area is interacted with.
messages.Tooltip.DebugInteractable.TouchExitLock: Whether the component can have an exit event or not when touching the component is stopped.
messages.Tooltip.DebugInteractable.TouchEnterLock: Whether the component can have an enter event or not when touching the component is started.
messages.Tooltip.Expander: This component is combined with the Button component to trigger this expander component.
messages.Tooltip.Expander.SectionRoot: The slot to expend with, by activating the slot that is referenced when toggled.
messages.Tooltip.HoverArea: The HoverArea component listens for a user's hover event on a UIX element. This component only works when it has an interactable component on the same Slot, such as a Button.
messages.Tooltip.HoverArea.IsHovering: Updates when a user's hover event is detected.
messages.Tooltip.HoverDynamicValueSet`1: The HoverDynamicValueSet component listens for a user's hover event on a UIX element and sets a value.
messages.Tooltip.HoverDynamicValueSet`1.VariableN: The dynamic variable name to find these values.
messages.Tooltip.HoverDynamicValueSet`1.ValueOnBe: The value to set when a hover event is detected.
messages.Tooltip.HoverDynamicValueSet`1.ValueOnEn: The value to set when a hover event has ended.
messages.Tooltip.HoverDynamicValueSet`1.SetValueO: Sets the value when a hover event is detected.
Sets the value when a hover event has ended.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionBlock: Any time you want to stop any UIX element from interacting (like having a popup menu to be on top of your main UIX), you can use this component to make the main UIX elements not respond to the user, making them focus on the newly spawned UIX.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionBlock.TouchExitLock: Blocks interactions when an interaction ends.
messages.Tooltip.InteractionBlock.TouchEnterLock: Blocks interactions when an interaction starts.
messages.Tooltip.ProgressBar: The ProgressBar component is used to show the user the normalized input of a float value on the UIX element.
messages.Tooltip.ProgressBar.Progress: The normalized progression the of value
messages.Tooltip.ProgressBar.AnchorMin: UIX Anchor minimum offset reference input
messages.Tooltip.ProgressBar.AnchorMax: UIX Anchor maximum offset reference input
messages.Tooltip.ProgressBar.Power: Modifies the Power of the progress bar
messages.Tooltip.ValueRadio`1: To function, the component simply needs to be attached to a slot that also has a button component attached to it.
messages.Tooltip.Radio.CheckVisual: The boolean that is driven to true whenever the <mark=#ffffff10>TargetValue</mark> is equal to the <mark=#ffffff10>OptionValue</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ValueRadio`1.OptionValue: What to set the <mark=#ffffff10>TargetValue</mark> to when the button is pressed.
messages.Tooltip.ValueRadio`1.TargetValue: The value to set when the button is pressed.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceRadio`1: The ReferenceRadio component is a listener component that activates or deactivates slots using the CheckVisual field, based if a TargetReference matches the OptionReference field. When a match is found, the CheckVisual field will be set to true.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceRadio`1.OptionReference: The reference we are checking a match for, when found, it will trigger this option.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceRadio`1.TargetReference: The thing we are using to check.
messages.Tooltip.ScrollRect: This is useful when you have a lot of text or other UIX elements that you want to be scrollable, much like how a webpage has a lot of content that can't fit in just one screen.
messages.Tooltip.ScrollRect.NormalizedPosition: The scroll position between (0,0) and (1,1) with (0,0) being the lower left corner.
messages.Tooltip.ScrollRect.ViewportOverride: Overrides the viewport of this scrollrect.
messages.Tooltip.ScrollRect.__legacyContent: Internal - Used for the content of the scrollrect.
messages.Tooltip.Slider`1.Value: The position this slider is at (normalized between <mark=#ffffff10>0</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>1</mark>)
messages.Tooltip.Slider`1.Min: The minimum value of this slider.
messages.Tooltip.Slider`1.Max: The maximum value of this slider.
messages.Tooltip.Slider`1.Integers: Changes the way this slider counts along the slider line. Setting to integers makes it step along whole numbers.
messages.Tooltip.Slider`1.Power: Changes the way this slider determines its position. Power represents a shifted curve of values that is heavier at the ends of the slider.
messages.Tooltip.Slider`1.Clamp: Enforces the min and max values of this slider.
messages.Tooltip.Slider`1.SlideDirection: The orientation of this slider's direction.
messages.Tooltip.Slider`1.AnchorOffset: The offset for the handle of this slider.
messages.Tooltip.Slider`1.HandleAnchorMinDrive: The min offset for the handle drive.
messages.Tooltip.Slider`1.HandleAnchorMaxDrive: The man offset for the handle drive.
messages.Tooltip.Slider`1.FillLineDrive: Fill line for this slider, fills in one side of it.
messages.Tooltip.Slider`1.RequireLockInToInteract: Checks to see if a user has locked in a press on this slider.
messages.Tooltip.Slider`1.RequireInitialPress: Check for a initial press from a user.
messages.Tooltip.TextField: This component is useful for forms, user input, and other text based projects.
messages.Tooltip.TextField.Editor: A reference to the text editor component.
messages.Tooltip.TextField.__text: Internal - The text for this field.
messages.Tooltip.UIGrabInstancer: Useful for making copies of items you referenced, but specifically from the UIX panels you have.
messages.Tooltip.ArcLayout: The ArcLayout is a component primarily used in a user's context menu. It requires a set of slots under the slot the component is attached to, and each slot needs an OutlinedArc Component and an ArcSegmentLayout Component.
messages.Tooltip.ArcLayout.Arc: The amount of the circle in degrees to cover with the arc elements.
messages.Tooltip.ArcLayout.Offset: the amount to rotate the arc elements around the center in degrees from the default position.
messages.Tooltip.ArcLayout.Separation: how much to separate the elements from each other.
messages.Tooltip.ArcLayout.CenterAtSeparation: Centers the separation point of this layout.
messages.Tooltip.ArcLayout.ProportionalSize: Keep all segments of the arc proportional in size.
messages.Tooltip.ArcLayout.ItemDirection: How to arrange the elements in order from the initial position.
messages.Tooltip.ArcSegmentLayout: This is used for the individual arc segment selection of the context menu.
messages.Tooltip.ArcSegmentLayout.Nested: The rect transform to hold the segments of the arc layout.
messages.Tooltip.ArcSegmentLayout.NestedSizeRatio: The size ratio for this arc segment.
messages.Tooltip.ArcSegmentLayout.Label: The label text for the arc segment.
messages.Tooltip.ArcSegmentLayout.LabelSize: The label text size for the arc segment.
messages.Tooltip.ArcSegmentLayout.LabelDistance: The label text distance from the arc segment.
messages.Tooltip.AspectRatioFitter: Great for keeping things more uniform by a ratio.
messages.Tooltip.AspectRatioFitter.AspectRatio: The aspect ratio of this UIX element. <mark=#ffffff10>1</mark> is a perfect square, less than <mark=#ffffff10>1</mark> is a vertical ratio, greater than <mark=#ffffff10>1</mark> is a horizontal ratio.
messages.Tooltip.ContentSizeFitter: This is used for a polished look of keeping your contents in the bounds of a UIX element.
messages.Tooltip.ContentSizeFitter.HorizontalFit: The fit mode to use to determine the width.
messages.Tooltip.ContentSizeFitter.VerticalFit: The fit mode to use to determine the height.
messages.Tooltip.FixedRectFitterLayout: The FixedRectFitterLayout component computes the bounds of child elements using their computed OffsetMax values and attempts to to fit the child elements into the FixedRectFitterLayout's Component:RectTransform according to the mode.
messages.Tooltip.FixedRectFitterLayout.VerticalAl: The alignment of the content vertically.
messages.Tooltip.FixedRectFitterLayout.Mode: How the child elements should attempt to fit into this RectTransform
messages.Tooltip.FixedRectFitterLayout.AllowShrin: Should the child elements grow to fill the space if needed.
messages.Tooltip.GraphicChunkRoot: The GraphicChunkRoot component is an internal component that is used for graphics, marking them as the root of a graphical image or process on UIX elements. Users don't need to worry as it does not have a clear use at this time (until one is dicovered).
messages.Tooltip.GridLayout: This is used to make a nice grid layout of a user's content.
messages.Tooltip.GridLayout.PaddingTop: Makes padding on the top of this grid, separating it from the top.
messages.Tooltip.GridLayout.PaddingRight: Makes padding on the right of this grid, separating it from the right.
messages.Tooltip.GridLayout.PaddingBottom: Makes padding on the bottom of this grid, separating it from the bottom.
messages.Tooltip.GridLayout.PaddingLeft: Makes padding on the left of this grid, separating it from the left.
messages.Tooltip.GridLayout.CellSize: The size of each content cell.
messages.Tooltip.GridLayout.Spacing: Makes padding between the content cells of this grid, separating it evenly.
messages.Tooltip.GridLayout.HorizontalAlign: how this grid should be aligned horizontally.
messages.Tooltip.GridLayout.VerticalAlign: how this grid should be aligned vertically.
messages.Tooltip.GridLayout.ExpandWidthToFit: Extends this grid to fit the entire UIX element that it is on.
messages.Tooltip.GridLayout.PreserveAspectOnExpan: Preserves the aspect ratio when expanded to fit.
messages.Tooltip.GridLayout.AlignLastRowIndividua: Alighns the last row for this grid seprately.
messages.Tooltip.HorizontalLayout: The HorizontalLayout component places its child layout elements next to each other, side by side.
messages.Tooltip.DirectionalLayout.PaddingTop: The padding to add, in pixels, at the top of the entire layout.
messages.Tooltip.DirectionalLayout.PaddingRight: The padding to add, in pixels, at the right of the entire layout.
messages.Tooltip.DirectionalLayout.PaddingBottom: The padding to add, in pixels, at the bottom of the entire layout.
messages.Tooltip.DirectionalLayout.PaddingLeft: The padding to add, in pixels, at the left of the entire layout.
messages.Tooltip.DirectionalLayout.Spacing: The padding to add, in pixels, between each object.
messages.Tooltip.DirectionalLayout.HorizontalAlig: The horizontal alignment to use for the child objects in the layout.
messages.Tooltip.DirectionalLayout.VerticalAlign: The vertical alignment to use for the child objects in the layout.
messages.Tooltip.DirectionalLayout.ForceExpandWid: Whether to force the children to expand to fill the available horizontal space.
messages.Tooltip.DirectionalLayout.ForceExpandHei: Whether to force the children to expand to fill the available vertical space.
messages.Tooltip.IgnoreLayout: Attach to a slot which the elements within should ignore the layout of the parent slot.
messages.Tooltip.LayoutElement: LayoutElement is used to inform UIX Layout Components in the layout containers and slots above it within the hierarchy about what size it should be.
messages.Tooltip.LayoutElement.MinWidth: The minimum width for this LayoutElement.
messages.Tooltip.LayoutElement.PreferredWidth: The preferred width for this LayoutElement.
messages.Tooltip.LayoutElement.FlexibleWidth: If there is any space left over after layouting, the width is used as a weight to decide what width should given to this LayoutElement.
messages.Tooltip.LayoutElement.MinHeight: The minimum height for this LayoutElement.
messages.Tooltip.LayoutElement.PreferredHeight: The preferred height for this LayoutElement.
messages.Tooltip.LayoutElement.FlexibleHeight: If there is any space left over after layouting, the height is used as a weight to decide what height should be given to this LayoutElement.
messages.Tooltip.LayoutElement.UseZeroMetrics: If checked, it allows "0" to be used in the other properties within this component.
messages.Tooltip.OverlappingLayout: The OverlappingLayout is best explained on the UIX page. The description for such is as follows:
messages.Tooltip.OverlappingLayout.PaddingTop: The padding from the center content to the top side.
messages.Tooltip.OverlappingLayout.PaddingRight: The padding from the center content to the right side.
messages.Tooltip.OverlappingLayout.PaddingBottom: The padding from the center content to the bottom side.
messages.Tooltip.OverlappingLayout.PaddingLeft: The padding from the center content to the left side.
messages.Tooltip.OverlappingLayout.HorizontalAlig: The alignment of the content horizontally.
messages.Tooltip.OverlappingLayout.VerticalAlign: The alignment of the content vertically.
messages.Tooltip.OverlappingLayout.ForceExpandWid: Whether content inside this layout going beyond the normal container size of this layout should be forced to go beyond the container so it fits width wise.
messages.Tooltip.OverlappingLayout.ForceExpandHei: Whether content inside this layout going beyond the normal container size of this layout should be forced to go beyond the container so it fits height wise.
messages.Tooltip.VerticalLayout: The VerticalLayout component places its child layout elements on top of each other.
messages.Tooltip.AssetReceiver`1: Asset Receiver is a useful component for getting asset data from a user through a UIX. When this item is placed on a UIX that allows receiving (like a Button) and it's Reference field is filled, it will be active. When an item is pushed through the canvas where the button is so the interaction laser is hitting it and the item is let go; the value within the field inside of Reference is set to the first available IAsset which is exposed by an Asset Proxy.
messages.Tooltip.AssetReceiver`1.Reference: The field to fill with a value when a grabbed item with an Asset Proxy is dropped onto the canvas region with this component.
messages.Tooltip.AssetReceiver`1.Undoable: Whether to add changes to this value via grab receiving to the undo stack of the user that changed it.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceReceiver`1: The ReferenceReceiver component can receive a reference dropped onto a UIX element Button by a user. The reference dropped is then stored into the field specified by TargetReference (an example would be to use the ReferenceField).
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceReceiver`1.Undoable: Whether dropping elements onto this UIX interactable is undoable.
messages.Tooltip.ValueReceiver`1: The ValueReceiver component is used in UIX elements to allow dropping a value property into a Component:Button or interactable UIX section to change a value field to the dropped value.
messages.Tooltip.ValueReceiver`1.Field: The field to drop the value into.
messages.Tooltip.ValueReceiver`1.Undoable: Whether dropping the value is undo able.
messages.Tooltip.ValueReceiver`1.TryConvertValues: Tries to change a dropped value which is possibly a string into the proper format by parsing.
messages.Tooltip.RectTransform: This can be used for placements inside your canvas or other upper elements, for your images or other lower elements for this container component.
messages.Tooltip.RectTransform.AnchorMin: The normalized position (0 to 1) in the parent object that the lower left corner is anchored to.
messages.Tooltip.RectTransform.AnchorMax: The normalized position (0 to 1) in the parent object that the upper right corner is anchored to.
messages.Tooltip.RectTransform.OffsetMin: The offset of the lower left corner of the rectangle relative to the lower left anchor, in pixels.
messages.Tooltip.RectTransform.OffsetMax: The offset of the upper right corner of the rectangle relative to the upper right anchor, in pixels.
messages.Tooltip.RectTransform.Pivot: The normalized position in this rectangle that it rotates around.
messages.Tooltip.AxisMultiViewportPanner: This is useful if you want to make a slideshow, tab pages for UIX, or anything that you want to have pan across for multiple pages in your design.
messages.Tooltip.AxisMultiViewportPanner.Viewport: The current focused viewport UIX element.
messages.Tooltip.AxisMultiViewportPanner.Animatio: The time it takes to pan across to get to the selected viewport index.
messages.Tooltip.AxisMultiViewportPanner.Directio: The direction this animates to get to the selected viewport index.
messages.Tooltip.AxisMultiViewportPanner.Viewport: The list of UIX element viewports to pan to.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.Slicer.Position: The position of the Slicer in global or local space.
messages.Tooltip.PBS_Slice.Slicer.Normal: The direction of the slicing plane front side in global or local space.
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionAnimationGlobalParamet: The amount of velocity needed before the user is considered to be moving.
How far the foot can step forward while sitting still.
The amount compared to forward the feet can step sidewya while still.
How much the feet can tilt before being picked up.
The minimum time a user can put their foot down before they can pick it up again while standing still.
The fastest the hands can move.
How much the hands are affected by drag when not touching anything.
How much stopping force is applied to the hands
messages.Tooltip.RandomAudioClipPlayerBase.ClipDa: The AudioClip to play. This can be a StaticAudioClip.
How likely it is for this audio clip to play
The minimum playback speed this audio should be played at
The maximum playback speed this audio should be played at
Whether this audio plays in 2d or 3d
The distance in meters in the audio's set transform space where the audio's perceived volume stops increasing and stays constant all the way to the center.
The maximum distance in meters in the audio's set transform space in meters before it cannot be heard.
messages.Tooltip.RootSpace.LocalSpace: This is the slot that the transform data is a coordinate space of (Local Space). If it isn't specified, then the transform data is the space specified by <mark=#ffffff10>Default</mark> instead.
messages.Tooltip.RootSpace.UseParentSpace: If this is turned on, then the transform data will be part of the coordinate space of the slot above <mark=#ffffff10>LocalSpace</mark>. if the slot above is Root, then the space is automatically Global Space.
messages.Tooltip.RootSpace.Default: The space to use if there is no specified <mark=#ffffff10>LocalSpace</mark> slot.
messages.Tooltip.RootSpace.OverrideRootSpace: Override all the settings of this RootSpace to match the target <mark=#ffffff10>OverrideRootSpace</mark> instead.
messages.Tooltip.AssetMetadata.UserInfo.User: User that this is containing info on.
messages.Tooltip.AssetMetadata.UserInfo.IsInVR: Whether the <mark=#ffffff10>User</mark> was in VR or not when this photo was taken.
messages.Tooltip.AssetMetadata.UserInfo.IsPresent: Whether the <mark=#ffffff10>User</mark> was focused or not when this photo was taken.
messages.Tooltip.AssetMetadata.UserInfo.HeadPosit: Where the <mark=#ffffff10>User</mark>'s head position was when this photo was taken.
messages.Tooltip.AssetMetadata.UserInfo.HeadOrien: How the <mark=#ffffff10>User</mark>'s head was oriented was when this photo was taken.
messages.Tooltip.AssetMetadata.UserInfo.SessionJo: When the <mark=#ffffff10>User</mark> joined the session this photo was taken.
messages.Tooltip.ValueOverrideBase`1.Override.Use: The user this override value is for.
messages.Tooltip.ValueOverrideBase`1.Override.Val: The value that the user will use for the localized driven value.
messages.Tooltip.GradientSkyMaterial.Gradient: The GradientSkyMaterial component is used in Component:Skybox to make a gradient type sky for use in worlds.
messages.Tooltip.GradientSkyMaterial.Gradient.Dir: The direction this gradient should point in.
messages.Tooltip.GradientSkyMaterial.Gradient.Fro: The color to start at for this gradient.
messages.Tooltip.GradientSkyMaterial.Gradient.ToC: The color to end at for this gradient.
messages.Tooltip.GradientSkyMaterial.Gradient.Spr: How much to extend the gradient from it's <mark=#ffffff10>From</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>To</mark> limits beyond the transitioning section.
messages.Tooltip.GradientSkyMaterial.Gradient.Fro: Where this gradient should start from in a range from 0<->1
messages.Tooltip.GradientSkyMaterial.Gradient.To: Where this gradient should end at in a range from 0<->1
messages.Tooltip.GradientSkyMaterial.Gradient.Exp: The sharpness of the transition of the gradient
messages.Tooltip.VolumeUnlitMaterial.SlicePlane: The VolumeUnlitMaterial component is used to display 3D Textures like Xrays, sunbeams, MRIs, CT scans, or any other 3D texture in a volumetric 3D cube that can optionally be sliced. Can also be used for volumetric lighting, but high texture resolutions can be large and cause considerable render lag.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeUnlitMaterial.SlicePlane.N: The normal direction of the slice in local space.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeUnlitMaterial.SlicePlane.O: The position of the slice in local space.
messages.Tooltip.VolumeUnlitMaterial.Highlight: The VolumeUnlitMaterial component is used to display 3D Textures like Xrays, sunbeams, MRIs, CT scans, or any other 3D texture in a volumetric 3D cube that can optionally be sliced. Can also be used for volumetric lighting, but high texture resolutions can be large and cause considerable render lag.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicSpriteFont.SpriteGlyph: The DynamicSpriteFont component is used to construct a font asset that can be used to create custom fonts character by character.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicSpriteFont.SpriteGlyph.Sp: The resource URI of the Font character.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicSpriteFont.SpriteGlyph.Un: the Unicode number of this Font character. See [https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Unicode_characters Wikipedia list of unicode characters].
messages.Tooltip.DynamicSpriteFont.SpriteGlyph.Ti: Whether this glyph is affectable by Font color.
messages.Tooltip.DiagnosticGaussianSplat.SplatDat: The Diagnostic Gaussian Splat component is used to render diagnostic info on Gaussian Splats.
messages.Tooltip.ConstrainedDelaunayMesh.Hole.Hol: The vertices that make up this Hole.
messages.Tooltip.LightningMesh.StrikeProperties: The LightingMesh component generates geometry of a procedural lighting bolt for use with a Component:MeshRenderer.
messages.Tooltip.LightningMesh.StrikeProperties.S: How many angled segments makes up this group.
the width of the start of the forks of this fork segment.
messages.Tooltip.LightningMesh.StrikeProperties.E: the width of the end of the forks of this fork segment.
messages.Tooltip.LightningMesh.StrikeProperties.M: the minimum offset from the start of the lighting bolt for this fork group
The amount of forks in this fork group
the minimum distance of the children of this fork group from the center bolt.
the maximum distance of the children of the fork group from the center bolt.
The minimum target offset for children of this fork group.
the maximum target offset for children of this fork group.
messages.Tooltip.MultiLineMesh.Line: The MultiLineMesh component generates geometry for use with a Component:MeshRenderer. For now, this component is unusable without Mods due to having a list type object.
messages.Tooltip.MultiLineMesh.Line.Scale: The radius of the line. This is a multiplier.
messages.Tooltip.MultiLineMesh.Line.Color: The vertex color of the line
messages.Tooltip.MultiLineMesh.Line.Points: How many points are in this line.
messages.Tooltip.MultiLineMesh.Line.Topology: the kind of Topology this line has.
messages.Tooltip.MultiLineMesh.Line.Shading: How to handle the Shading of this line.
messages.Tooltip.MultiLineMesh.Line.Ends: How to handle the caps at the start and end of this line
messages.Tooltip.MultiLineMesh.Line.DualSided: Whether the line can be seen from the inside.
messages.Tooltip.MultiLineMesh.Line.AbsolutePoint: Whether each position is relative or not from the last point
messages.Tooltip.MultiLineMesh.Line.UVScale: the scale of the material detail on the surface of this line.
messages.Tooltip.MultiLineMesh.Line.ScaleUVByCirc: Whether to keep the material detail per square millimeter consistent regardless of the value used for <mark=#ffffff10>Scale</mark>
messages.Tooltip.MultiLineMesh.Line.PreciseUV: Whether to make a more accurate UV for the line.
messages.Tooltip.MultiLineMesh.Line.Positions: A list of positions to use for making the line
messages.Tooltip.MultiLineMesh.Line.Scales: The scale of each line segment. Each one is multipled by <mark=#ffffff10>Scales</mark> for the final geometry.
messages.Tooltip.MultiLineMesh.Line.Orientations: The rotation of each segment of the line
messages.Tooltip.MultiLineMesh.Line.Colors: The vertex colors of each segment of the line
messages.Tooltip.MultiLineMesh.Line.Profile: The color profile used for the vertex colors of this line.
messages.Tooltip.MultiLineMesh.Line.PointOffsets: The offset of each point of the line. Used in tandem with <mark=#ffffff10>Orientations</mark> to handle line shape.
messages.Tooltip.MultiSegmentMesh.SegmentInfo: Multi line segment mesh is a component that generates mesh data for use with a SkinnedMeshRenderer. It uses a list of lines to generate lines of geometry with varying thickness.
messages.Tooltip.MultiSegmentMesh.SegmentInfo.Rad: how thick the line is in local space
messages.Tooltip.MultiSegmentMesh.SegmentInfo.Poi: The position of the start of the line in local space
The position of the end of the line in local space
messages.Tooltip.MultiChannelSimplexTexture3D.Cha: The MultiChannelSimplexTexture3D component creates a monochrome noise channel for each RGBA channel with their own settings. It is also considered a ITexture3D element.
How much to shift the noise.
How much to scale up the noise to make it more gentle, or down to make it more noisy.
Whether to use the 4D sample Map or a 1D sample map.
How much to offset on the W axis to change the seed.
The smallest value that can be sampled for this channel.
The biggest value that can be sampled for this channel.
How fast/slow to make output values approach 1 as you get to 100% noise value sampled. (Sharpness)
messages.Tooltip.GridTexture.Grid: The GridTexture component is used to generate a grid of lines with a background color to use in materials.
messages.Tooltip.GridTexture.Grid.Spacing: The distance between lines in pixels for this grid
messages.Tooltip.GridTexture.Grid.Offset: The amount to shift this grid around in pixels. can be used to animate movement.
messages.Tooltip.GridTexture.Grid.Width: The width of the grid lines in pixels for this grid.
messages.Tooltip.GridTexture.Grid.LineColor: The color of the lines for this grid.
messages.Tooltip.ExportDialog.ExportOption.Export: The thing to Export. Usually in Export Category
What kind of Export to do to the exportable.
messages.Tooltip.LerpingMultiClipPlayer.Track: The LerpingMultiClipPlayer component is an IAudioSource type that transitions between different audio clips (tracks) by lerping the volume and speed from one audio to the other using Lerp.
messages.Tooltip.LerpingMultiClipPlayer.Track.Cli: The audio clip to play if <mark=#ffffff10>Lerp</mark> is within <mark=#ffffff10>StartPosition</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>EndPosition</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.LerpingMultiClipPlayer.Track.Sta: What the starting point of this track's range is.
messages.Tooltip.LerpingMultiClipPlayer.Track.End: What the ending point of this track's range is.
messages.Tooltip.LerpingMultiClipPlayer.Track.Sta: What the speed should be at the start of this track's range, lerped.
messages.Tooltip.LerpingMultiClipPlayer.Track.End: What the speed should be at the end of this track's range, lerped.
messages.Tooltip.LerpingMultiClipPlayer.Track.Sta: How much of this track's position range to fade the audio in for.
messages.Tooltip.LerpingMultiClipPlayer.Track.End: How much of this track's position range to fade the audio out for.
messages.Tooltip.MultiAudioClipPlayer.Track: TODO: instead of being written by me with 2 brain cells of math this should be rewritten with actual math terms.
messages.Tooltip.MultiAudioClipPlayer.Track.Clip: The Clip to play for this track.
messages.Tooltip.MultiAudioClipPlayer.Track.Volum: How loud the track should be.
messages.Tooltip.MultiAudioClipPlayer.Track.Relat: How fast the Clip is compared to the speed of <mark=#ffffff10>playback</mark>
messages.Tooltip.FundingStatistics.ServiceStats: The Funding Statistics component is used to get the different funding values for Resonite in United States Dollars from supporters.
messages.Tooltip.FundingStatistics.ServiceStats.C: The current money being supported via this medium.
messages.Tooltip.FundingStatistics.ServiceStats.H: The historical money that was given via this medium.
messages.Tooltip.FundingStatistics.FundingStats: The Funding Statistics component is used to get the different funding values for Resonite in United States Dollars from supporters.
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.SessionStats: The OnlineStatistics component shows information about current session numbers and the number of users in them.
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.SessionStats.Pu: How many public sessions are in this category?
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.SessionStats.Re: How many sessions open only to registered users are in this category?
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.SessionStats.Co: How many sessions open only to contacts of contacts are in this category?
How many sessions open to only contacts of the host are in this category?
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.SessionStats.LA: How many sessions that are Local access network sessions are in this category?
messages.Tooltip.OnlineStatistics.SessionStats.Pr: How many sessions that are invite only are in this category?
messages.Tooltip.ButtonDynamicImpulseTriggerWithR: Useful for sending dynamic pulses cleanly instead of using flux.
The Tag of the sent impulse
The reference to send with the Dynamic Impulse
messages.Tooltip.ButtonDynamicImpulseTriggerWithV: Useful for sending dynamic pulses cleanly instead of using flux.
The Tag of the sent impulse
The value to send with the Dynamic Impulse
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceOptionDescriptionDriver: The Reference Option Description Driver component is used primarily to drive the parts that make up a Context Menu Item Source. This component along with ContextMenuItemSource is used in context menus for toggles where the toggle controls a reference like a slot or object.
The values below should be used if <mark=#ffffff10>Reference</mark> is equal to this.
The label to use if <mark=#ffffff10>ReferenceTarget</mark> matches <mark=#ffffff10>Reference</mark>
The color to use if <mark=#ffffff10>ReferenceTarget</mark> matches <mark=#ffffff10>Reference</mark>
The sprite to use if <mark=#ffffff10>ReferenceTarget</mark> matches <mark=#ffffff10>Reference</mark>
messages.Tooltip.ValueOptionDescriptionDriver`1.O: This component can be more efficient than using a bunch of Value Equality Drivers and generating a number based on such, or using ProtoFlux for a context menu.
The values below should be used if <mark=#ffffff10>Value</mark> is equal to this.
The label to use if <mark=#ffffff10>ReferenceValue</mark> matches <mark=#ffffff10>Value</mark>
The color to use if <mark=#ffffff10>ReferenceValue</mark> matches <mark=#ffffff10>Value</mark>
The sprite to use if <mark=#ffffff10>ReferenceValue</mark> matches <mark=#ffffff10>Value</mark>
The tint color for this sprite.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo.AnchorInfo: The SlotGizmo is used with the Dev tool to interact with slots when they are selected and their sub gizmos. This is rarely interacted directly with by the user, but is a very important component for interacting with the game.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo.AnchorInfo.anchor: The point anchor to snap to for other gizmos.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo.AnchorInfo.posDrive: The position to drive for this point anchor.
messages.Tooltip.SlotGizmo.AnchorInfo.scaleDrive: The scale to drive for this point anchor.
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo.Handle: The ConeGizmo component is auto generated for interaction with a Component:ConeMesh or Component:ConeCollider via a Dev Tool.
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo.Handle.RelativeHeight: The relative height of this handle compared to the cone.
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo.Handle.root: The root slot of the visual and interface of this handle.
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo.Handle.offset: The field to drive with the offset of the handle.
messages.Tooltip.ConeGizmo.Handle.mesh: The mesh being used for this handle.
messages.Tooltip.SphereGizmo.Handle: The SphereGizmo component is used to control the radius of certain sphere elements.
messages.Tooltip.SphereGizmo.Handle.RelativeRadiu: The relative radius between the gizmo and this mesh.
messages.Tooltip.SphereGizmo.Handle.root: The root of this handle visual.
messages.Tooltip.SphereGizmo.Handle.mesh: The torus mesh used for this visual.
messages.Tooltip.MazeGenerator.Area: Great for prototyping mazes, and can be baked.
messages.Tooltip.TagHapticPointMapper.TaggedHapti: The TagHapticPointMapper allows for defining custom mapping on avatars.
The tag the haptic device needs to be put here.
Where to put the haptic sampler for the device.
messages.Tooltip.UnresolvedReferences.UnresolvedR: The UnresolvedReferences component is internal to FrooxEngine and is found usually on a world root. It is used to store references that are unresolved by the load controller of the game for that world.
A stored RefID of an unresolved reference.
messages.Tooltip.FootstepSoundDefinition.Footstep: The FootstepSoundDefinition component can be referenced by other components which can trigger this to play noise during footstep events.
The audioclip to play for this foot step.
How likely it is for this foot step sound to play.
The minimum player velocity required for the sound to play.
The maximum player velocity allowed for the sound to play.
Should this audio clip be played when the user is walking?
Should this audio clip be played when the user is landing from a jump?
Whether this sound should be played for the left foot.
Whether this sound should be played for the right foot.
The minimum pitch multiplier that can be used with this foot step sound.
The maximum pitch multiplier that can be used with this foot step sound.
The velocity the player should be moving to map to the minimum pitch for this sound.
The velocity the player should be moving to map to the maximum pitch for this sound.
The minimum random amount of pitch to add to the footstep.
The maximum random amount of pitch to add to the footstep.
The minimum volume that can be used with this foot step sound.
The maximum volume that can be used with this foot step sound.
The velocity the player should be moving to map to the minimum volume for this sound.
The velocity the player should be moving to map to the maximum volume for this sound.
The minimum random amount of volume to add to the footstep.
The maximum random amount of volume to add to the footstep.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterParenter.AlignmentFilte: Character Parenters are a component that allow for parenting or aligning the gravity of a user when they hit a collider.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterTeleporter.Exit: Place the component on a slot with a collider that has character collider field enabled.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterTeleporter.Exit.Weight: The probability of coming out of this exit.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterTeleporter.Exit.Telepor: The slot this exit will send the user to when this exit is chosen.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterTeleporter.Exit.ParentA: Whether to parent the character controller slot under <mark=#ffffff10>TeleportExit</mark>
messages.Tooltip.CharacterTeleporter.Exit.Transfo: Whether to rotate the user to align this user to be pointing the same direction relative to this as they were pointing relative to the entrance.
Whether to scale the character controller to <mark=#ffffff10>TeleportExit</mark>'s scale relative to the entrance.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterTeleporter.Exit.Relativ: Whether this exit will put the user at the same relative position to it as the user was when they went into the entrance.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterTeleporter.Exit.RandomE: A generator for points the user should end up when exiting out of this exit. for example, teleporting users into a circle area after they exit here. Only works if <mark=#ffffff10>RelativeExitPoint</mark> is false.
messages.Tooltip.CharacterTeleporter.Exit.Telepor: What velocity to set the user to when they exit out of this exit.
Sync Delegates that take Float3 that should be called when the teleporter is exited here. For example, RandomAudioClipPlayer and RandomObjectSpawner have sync delegates that can be used here.
messages.Tooltip.AssetMetadata.UserInfo: This component is used as a base component for other components like PhotoMetadata
messages.Tooltip.LocomotionPermissions.Locomotion: The LocomotionPermissions component allows you to control what locomotions users can use in your world.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.Bone: Dynamic Bone Chain is a component that allows you to add rigidbody physics to the bones of a rigged model or avatar.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.Bone.BoneSlot: The slot of the dynamic bone this bone is referring to.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.Bone.RadiusModi: How much to multiply the default radius specified by the dynamic bone chain to make this bone's radius.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.Bone.GrabOverri: A Bone the same type as this Bone that will be grabbed instead of this bone when this bone is grabbed.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.Bone._posDrive: The position of <mark=#ffffff10>BoneSlot</mark> which this should be driving to make the dynamic bone movement effects.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBoneChain.Bone._rotDrive: The rotation <mark=#ffffff10>BoneSlot</mark> which this should be driving to make the dynamic bone movement effects.
messages.Tooltip.FeedItemInterface.NestedItem: The FeedItemInterface component is used as a template type item in data feed mappers in the Data Feeds system.
messages.Tooltip.FeedItemInterface.NestedItem.Int: The template to instance when templated by a data feed as part of this Nested item.
messages.Tooltip.FeedItemInterface.NestedItem.Con: Where to place this Nested item when it is generated.
messages.Tooltip.FeedItemInterface.NestedItem.Ign: Whether to ignore instantiating the parent container of <mark=#ffffff10>Interface</mark> when this is instanciated.
messages.Tooltip.DataFeedItemMapper.ItemMapping: The DataFeedItemMapper allows you to define which data feed items map to which UI templates. It does this via the list Mappings, which take classes that extend DataFeedItem and If they match a mapping in the list it Duplicates that item's template and adds it into the list.
messages.Tooltip.DataFeedItemMapper.ItemMapping.G: If <mark=#ffffff10>ItemType</mark> is a type that has generic single letter arguments, the type won't be accepted if it's arguments in order don't match the arguments in this list in order.
messages.Tooltip.DataFeedItemMapper.ItemMapping.T: The template to duplicate when matched with this item mapping.
messages.Tooltip.DataFeedItemMappingManager.Conta: The place to put the items after generating a template
messages.Tooltip.DataFeedItemMappingManager.Templ: The Mapper used to map certain DataFeedItems to templates which get generated and put into <mark=#ffffff10>ContainerRoot</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.DataFeedItemMappingManager.OnGen: Actions to call when this type has been called to start generating items received by a data feed.
messages.Tooltip.LegacyWorldListManager.SortPrope: The LegacyWorldListManager component is used in the old dash to manage the world list before the WorldsDataFeed component was implemented.
The direction to use when applying <mark=#ffffff10>Parameter</mark>
messages.Tooltip.DynamicVisemeDriver.VisemeSource: The DynamicVisemeDriver component allows using a viseme analyser to drive Float values. It allows for combinations of visemes with weights to drive a Float, and said Float may be a shapekey.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicVisemeDriver.TargetDriver: The DynamicVisemeDriver component allows using a viseme analyser to drive Float values. It allows for combinations of visemes with weights to drive a Float, and said Float may be a shapekey.
The float value to drive. May be a shapekey.
A list of sources to use to drive <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark>
messages.Tooltip.GenericSlicer.SliceDrive: The GenericSlicer component is used to drive and handle the slicing of materials like volumes and PBS Slice.
messages.Tooltip.GenericSlicer.SliceDrive.Positio: The position of a material slice entry to drive
messages.Tooltip.GenericSlicer.SliceDrive.Normal: The Normal of a material slice entry to drive
messages.Tooltip.GenericSlicer.SliceDrive.Destroy: The Sync delegate to call when this is destroyed, passing this entry as an argument.
messages.Tooltip.LODGroup.LOD: A LOD (Level Of Detail) Group allows you to define behaviors to load alternate meshes for objects based on their relative size on a user's screen. This is commonly used to replace complex and finely detailed meshes with simplified versions based on a user's distance from the object.
messages.Tooltip.LODGroup.LOD.ScreenRelativeTrans: This defines the minimum size of the object to use this LOD stage.
messages.Tooltip.LODGroup.LOD.FadeTransitionWidth: FadeTransitionWidth on the Unity LOD component.
messages.Tooltip.LODGroup.LOD.Renderers: List of mesh renderers that should belong to this component, which become Mesh Renderers from the UnityEngine when they are injected by FrooxEngine into the unity scene forcibly via it's rendering pipeline.
messages.Tooltip.RenderMaterialOverride.MaterialO: The RenderMaterialOverride Component allows for overriding materials on a renderer when it is rendered in certain contexts.
The material index on <mark=#ffffff10>Renderer</mark> to override.
The material to override <mark=#ffffff10>Renderer</mark>'s materials for material <mark=#ffffff10>Index</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ColorSky.Gradient: The ColorSky component creates a skybox with a color. Currently when this component is added to a slot, it will be immediately replaced with a Skybox and a material component automatically. It exists as a stub to maintain compatibility with legacy content.
messages.Tooltip.FingerPoseModifier.FingerOffsets: The FingerPoseModifier component takes a Source and makes a copy of the finger pose data from it. It then modifies the copied data before making it the finger pose data of this component. This component in itself is a IFingerPoseSourceComponent.
How much curl to add to the thumb.
How much curl to add to the index finger.
How much curl to add to the middle finger.
How much curl to add to the ring finger.
How much curl to add to the pinky finger.
How much splaying to add to the thumb.
How much splaying to add to the index finger.
How much splaying to add to the middle finger.
How much splaying to add to the ring finger.
How much splaying to add to the pinky finger.
messages.Tooltip.HandPoser.FingerSegment: This component modifies the rotation of the fingers of an avatar's hand based on a pose source. The HandPoser works with whatever data is avilable, either precise finger tracking like a LeapMotion, or course grained like a vive wand, where grip and trigger affect the main finger positions.
messages.Tooltip.HandPoser.Finger: This component modifies the rotation of the fingers of an avatar's hand based on a pose source. The HandPoser works with whatever data is avilable, either precise finger tracking like a LeapMotion, or course grained like a vive wand, where grip and trigger affect the main finger positions.
messages.Tooltip.HandPoser.Finger.Metacarpal: Usually inside of the palm and not actually inside the finger itself.
messages.Tooltip.GeometryLineBrushTool.Line: The GeometryLineBrushTool component is further explained on the Geometry Line Brush page.
messages.Tooltip.GeometryLineBrushTool.Line.Topol: The kind of Topology the line should have.
messages.Tooltip.GeometryLineBrushTool.Line.Ends: How the beginning and the ends of the line should be capped.
messages.Tooltip.GeometryLineBrushTool.Line.Shadi: how the lines should be shaded.
messages.Tooltip.GeometryLineBrushTool.Line.Point: how many divisions the line should have circumference wise.
messages.Tooltip.GeometryLineBrushTool.Line.DualS: Whether the line should be dual sided.
messages.Tooltip.GeometryLineBrushTool.Line.Absol: Whether the points in the line are absolute rather than offsets from the previous point.
messages.Tooltip.GeometryLineBrushTool.Line.UVSca: the scale of the UVs of the line.
messages.Tooltip.GeometryLineBrushTool.Line.Scale: Whether the detail per square unit should not change when the line has a bigger circumference.
messages.Tooltip.GeometryLineBrushTool.Line.Preci: Whether the UV for each line should be precise.
messages.Tooltip.GeometryLineBrushTool.Line.Color: How to apply <mark=#ffffff10>Color</mark> for this line.

messages.Tooltip.GeometryLineBrushTool.Line.UseTi: Whether this line should be affected by Tool tip rotation.
messages.Tooltip.GeometryLineBrushTool.Line.MaxSi: The max radius this line can be at any given point.
messages.Tooltip.GeometryLineBrushTool.Line.Overr: The slot to use as an override when drawing this line.
the slot to use as a rotation override when drawing this line.
messages.Tooltip.GeometryLineBrushTool.Line.Offse: The space to put <mark=#ffffff10>PointOffsets</mark> in for this line.
messages.Tooltip.GeometryLineBrushTool.Line.Rotat: The space to use when applying rotation to this line.

messages.Tooltip.AxisAligner.Target: The AxisAligner component is used to Align elements along a single line axis in local space under a slot based on their Bounding boxes. It also allows for excluding bounded elements from calculations, and for aligning elements themselves so that they can be centered like varied sized beads on a string or like boxes into the corner edge between a wall and floor. These calculations are heavy, and this component is best used lightly. Remove this component after using it to Align world elements, as it will be constantly active and will behave violently if used to Align shelves for example in a world and left unremoved.
messages.Tooltip.AxisAligner.Target.TargetSlot: The slot to read bounds data from.
messages.Tooltip.AxisAligner.Target.PositionDrive: The position of <mark=#ffffff10>TargetSlot</mark> to drive in order to align it.
messages.Tooltip.CircleAligner.Item: The CircleAligner component is used to position a list of slots in a circle.
messages.Tooltip.CircleAligner.Item.AngleOffset: The offset amount of degrees the object should rotate from the default of pointing towards the center.
messages.Tooltip.MultiBoolConditionDriver.Conditi: The MultiBoolConditionDriver drives Target depending on the state of the Conditions list, and the ConditionMode selected.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectScroller.Item: The ObjectScroller component acts as a way to scroll a list of items with sizes by offsetting them within a region they should appear within. Objects outside of the region get disabled until the Offset allows them to appear within it again.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectScroller.Item.Position: Original position of the item before <mark=#ffffff10>Offset</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectScroller.Item.PositionDriv: Drives the item's position so <mark=#ffffff10>Offset</mark> can affect it.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectScroller.Item.ScaleDrive: Used to scale the item down if the item cannot fit within <mark=#ffffff10>RegionSize</mark> at its current position.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectScroller.Item.ActiveDrive: Used to enable/disable the item if it's inside/outside of <mark=#ffffff10>RegionSize</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.ReferenceUserOverride`1.Override: This is a component that allows you to locally change a reference on a value. Changing the value locally for yourself or others so that an item or world element is different depending on who's viewing it.
The User that should see <mark=#ffffff10>Value</mark>'s content inside of <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> locally instead of <mark=#ffffff10>Default</mark>'s content
The value that <mark=#ffffff10>User</mark> should see target driven to locally.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleAligner.Target: The ScaleAligner component scales a list of slots based on bounding box, each one being bigger/smaller than the last.
messages.Tooltip.ScaleAligner.Target.TargetSlot: The slot this Target entry is targeting.
messages.Tooltip.SphereAligner.Item: Sphere aligner is a component that has a list of child slots under Items and aligns those items into a spherical shape.
messages.Tooltip.SphereAligner.Item.Root: The slot this is pointing to.
messages.Tooltip.ValueGradientDriver`1.Point: Each point in the Points list has a Position field and Value field.
messages.Tooltip.DynamicBlendShapeDriver.BlendSha: This component is used to map values to Blendshapes by name.
messages.Tooltip.Consumable.Stage: This component allows the item to be consumed by users.
messages.Tooltip.FABRIK_Chain.ChainBone: FABRIK Chain is the core of Resonite's in house IK system
messages.Tooltip.Joint.SnapOrientation: The Joint component allows for a slot or object to rotate around a single point, in any direction, or around an axis. Common uses are doors, helmet tops, and dials.
messages.Tooltip.ObjectGridAligner.Item: The ObjectGridAligner component aligns objects into a grid of items with x number of items per row.
messages.Tooltip.RandomObjectSpawner.SpawnData: The RandomObjectSpawner component selects a slot from the Templates list, and spawns it either at this component's parent (in the case of Spawn()), or at the point specified in an argument (in the case of SpawnAtPoint())
messages.Tooltip.Slider.SnapPosition: The Slider component allows for an object to be grabbed and moved. See Usage for more information.
messages.Tooltip.Slider.SnapPosition.Position: The position to snap to and measure distance from.
messages.Tooltip.Slider.SnapPosition.MaxDistance: The distance the slider needs to be under from <mark=#ffffff10>Position</mark> in local transforms to snap to it.
messages.Tooltip.VoiceModeConfig.Volume: What to set the Volume field of an Audio Output Component to.
messages.Tooltip.VoiceModeConfig.MinDistance: What to set the MinDistance field of an Audio Output Component to.
messages.Tooltip.VoiceModeConfig.MaxDistance: What to set the MaxDistance field of an Audio Output Component to.
messages.Tooltip.VoiceModeConfig.RollOffMode: What to set the RollOffMode field of an Audio Output Component to.
messages.Tooltip.VoiceModeConfig.DistanceSpace: What to set the DistanceSpace field of an Audio Output Component to.
messages.Tooltip.VoiceModeConfig.MinScale: What to set the MinScale field of an Audio Output Component to.
messages.Tooltip.VoiceModeConfig.MaxScale: What to set the MaxScale field of an Audio Output Component to.
messages.Tooltip.VoiceModeConfig.Spatialize: What to set the Spatialize field of an Audio Output Component to.
messages.Tooltip.VoiceModeConfig.SpatialBlend: What to set the SpatialBlend field of an Audio Output Component to.
messages.Tooltip.VoiceModeConfig.Doppler: What to set the Doppler field of an Audio Output Component to.
messages.Tooltip.CommonAvatarBuilder.AvatarTempla: Common Avatar Builder is a component that is part of every world that defines the spawning behavior and routines to do for all users when they spawn into a world. This can be used to add world settings menus, change what parts of a user get automatically set up, and so on.
The avatar to use if this is chosen.
What role this choice should apply to.
The likelyness compared to other choices to choose this.
How many users at maximum could be assigned this avatar at any given moment.
Whether the user's Favorited avatar should be ignored.

messages.Tooltip.PlatformColorPalette.Grayscale: The PlatformColorPalette component is a quick way of getting the colors of Resonite's Branding. This also provides you with both the color values in ColorX form and [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Web_colors Hex] form.
messages.Tooltip.PlatformColorPalette.Grayscale.D: The dark hex code for this color type group
messages.Tooltip.PlatformColorPalette.Grayscale.M: The mid hex code for this color type group

messages.Tooltip.PlatformColorPalette.Grayscale.M: The mid color for this color type group.
messages.Tooltip.PlatformColorPalette.Grayscale.L: The light color for this color type group.
messages.Tooltip.PlatformColorPalette.Colors: The PlatformColorPalette component is a quick way of getting the colors of Resonite's Branding. This also provides you with both the color values in ColorX form and [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Web_colors Hex] form.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator.Anchor: The avatar creator component is the component that drives the functionality of the avatar creator object. See Avatar Creation for a much less technical page on this, and for information on how to actually use the avatar creator.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator.Anchor.ScaleDrive: The scale field of this guide object.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator.Anchor.AnchorName: The human readable name of this guide object.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator.Anchor.IsRight: Whether this guide object is on the right side of the headset. (So is it a right or left hand for example?)
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator.Anchor.Slot: The root slot of this guide object.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarCreator.Anchor.Slider: The slider that can be used to move this guide object around via grabbing.
messages.Tooltip.UsersAssetLoadProgress`1.LoadPro: The UsersAssetLoadProgress component is commonly used with ProtoFlux to tell if a particular User has loaded an asset. The node in particular is the Asset Load Progress ProtoFlux node. This component takes any IAsset as the generic argument for the component.
The user this Load Progress is tracking loading for.
If the user had begun downloading, how much have they downloaded from 0<->1?
