messages.Tooltip.UnitSystemSwitcher.Preset.Format: The unit to show in the text.
messages.Tooltip.UnitSystemSwitcher.Preset.Compou: A list of compound format units from a unit configuration.
The Separator for compound units for this preset.
Whether compound units should use long names (So millimeters vs mm)
messages.Tooltip.UserRef.User: The user that matches the <mark=#ffffff10>_machineId</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>_userId</mark>.
messages.Tooltip.UserRef._machineId: The machine id last known for a User. Is used to try to identify the user and fill <mark=#ffffff10>User</mark> user ref field the next time they're in a world with this component.
messages.Tooltip.UserRef._userId: The user's account ID last known for a User. Is used to try to identify the user and fill <mark=#ffffff10>User</mark> user ref field the next time they're in a world with this component.
messages.Tooltip.GiggleTechSettings.Device: The GiggleTechSettings Component is used to control different aspects of [https://giggletech.io/products/giggle_puck giggle pucks] for use in Resonite.
messages.Tooltip.GiggleTechSettings.Device.IP: The IP address of this device.
messages.Tooltip.GiggleTechSettings.Device.UseDev: Whether to allow use and registration of this device.
messages.Tooltip.GiggleTechSettings.Device.Always: Whether this puck should always be treated as connected even if it looses connection.
messages.Tooltip.GiggleTechSettings.Device.Custom: A user defined name for this giggle puck.
messages.Tooltip.GiggleTechSettings.Device.Minimu: The minimum intensity needed to activate this.
messages.Tooltip.GiggleTechSettings.Device.Initia: How strong the inital activation will be.
messages.Tooltip.GiggleTechSettings.Device.Haptic: The index of this device in the list of haptic devices.
messages.Tooltip.ComponentFilter.BlockedTypes: All components need to not be in this list to pass this filter.
messages.Tooltip.ComponentFilter.RequiredTypes: There needs to be at least one Component of each type in this list to pass this filter.
messages.Tooltip.ScreenViewPermissions.ViewTarget: The ScreenViewPermissions component is a Permissions type component that can apply rules for what certain roles in a session can do with views like third person, free cam, or UI targetting.
The C# Type a View target has to be in order to be matched to this list item.
This tag is required for a View targeting mode to be matched to this list item.
The user has to be in edit mode for a View targeting mode to be matched to this list item.
messages.Tooltip.TagFilter.Mode: Controls what filtering mode is applied to <mark=#ffffff10>List</mark>
messages.Tooltip.TagFilter.List: Tags which determine the allowed or disallowed usability dependant on the <mark=#ffffff10>Mode</mark>
messages.Tooltip.PriorityEntry.Priority: The numerical priority of this device. Higher numbers come first.
messages.Tooltip.DevicePriorityEntry.IsConnected: Whether the audio device is available and is not disconnected.
messages.Tooltip.TrackerSettings.Tracker.UseTrack: Whether to use the tracker right now.
messages.Tooltip.TrackerSettings.Tracker.FreezeTr: Freeze the tracking into it's last reported position
messages.Tooltip.TrackerSettings.Tracker.IsConnec: Whether this tracker is connected to the game or not, usually means it's not connected to steamvr if this is false.
messages.Tooltip.TrackerSettings.Tracker.CustomNa: A custom name set by the user to identify the tracker.
messages.Tooltip.TrackerSettings.Tracker.MappedBo: The node which this tracker should be used to track.
messages.Tooltip.TrackerSettings.Tracker.MappedPo: The offset position for mapping to the body
messages.Tooltip.TrackerSettings.Tracker.MappedRo: The offset rotation  for mapping to the body
messages.Tooltip.TrackerSettings.Tracker.UseInVR: Whether to use this tracker while the user is in VR mode.
messages.Tooltip.TrackerSettings.Tracker.UseInDes: Whether to use this tracker while the user is in desktop mode.
messages.Tooltip.TrackerSettings.Tracker.TrackerI: The ID of the tracker, usually designated by the manufacturer of the tracker.
messages.Tooltip.TrackerSettings.Tracker.TrackerB: How much battery the tracker has left.
messages.Tooltip.TrackerSettings.Tracker.TrackerI: Whether the tracker is currently charging.
messages.Tooltip.HostAccessSettings.Entry.AllowHT: This indicayes of HTTP(S) requests are allowed for this host. These are typically REST API requests to communicate with third party services.
messages.Tooltip.HostAccessSettings.Entry.AllowWe: This indicates if realtime Websocket connections are allowed for this host. These are often used for more realtime bi-directional communications and data streams.
messages.Tooltip.HostAccessSettings.Entry.AllowOS: This indicates if Resonite can receive and process OSC data
This controls if Resonite is allowed to send data via OSC protocol to given host
messages.Tooltip.HostAccessSettings.Entry.LastHyp: The last reason given for accessing a website.
messages.Tooltip.HostAccessSettings.Entry.LastWeb: The last reason given for accessing a web socket.
messages.Tooltip.HostAccessSettings.Entry.LastOSC: The last reason given for sending OSC data.
The last reason given for receiving OSC data.
messages.Tooltip.HostAccessSettings.Entry.Allowed: The number ports allowed from this entry address.
messages.Tooltip.HostAccessSettings.Entry.Blocked: The number ports blocked from this entry address.
messages.Tooltip.UserRestrictionsSettings.Entry: The UserRestrictionsSettings component is found in the user space and contains all blocked user entries. this is a good way to handle blocked users until they fix the regression of no longer having a blocked users list introduced in the settings update.
messages.Tooltip.UserRestrictionsSettings.Entry.B: The kind of block type you did against this user.
messages.Tooltip.UserRestrictionsSettings.Entry.I: Whether the user is fully banned by you or not from your sessions and worlds.
messages.Tooltip.UserRestrictionsSettings.Entry.U: The user ID of the user.
messages.Tooltip.UserRestrictionsSettings.Entry.M: A list of machine ids this user may have.
messages.Tooltip.UserRestrictionsSettings.Entry.E: A list of extra machine ids for this user.
messages.Tooltip.UserRestrictionsSettings.Entry.B: A list of worlds of yours the user is banned from.
messages.Tooltip.UserspaceFacetAnchorsManager.Dat: To know what this controls, see Facet Anchors.
The root of this facet anchor element.
The workspace component that controls saving and loading of this facet's data.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchor.BodyNodeFilters: An anchor allows for an avatar to be attached to a slot.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchor.BodyNodeFilters.Nod: The node on the anchored user to apply filters to.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchor.BodyNodeFilters.Req: Whether full body users can override this node and move the body part anyway.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchor.BodyNodeFilters.Fil: The list of filters that should be applied to <mark=#ffffff10>Node</mark> when the user is anchored.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarAnchor.PoseFilter: An anchor allows for an avatar to be attached to a slot.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarManager.EquippedGroup: For detailed information on how this functions for mix and match body parts. Please also see Equipping Multiple Avatars.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarExpressionDriver.Expressio: Allows the assignment of the IMouthTrackingSourceComponent to blendshapes.
The blendshape or float to drive with this type of expression
The expression to take from the parent AvatarExpressionDriver and drive <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> with it.
Whether to take a guess to this expression's value if it is not tracked
The minimum value to drive <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> to.
The maximum value to drive <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> to.
The amount to reduce the effect on <mark=#ffffff10>Target</mark> when the user is speaking.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawEyeData.EyeData: The AvatarRawEyeData component provides the eye tracking data on a particular User. This component links to a user upon being part of an Avatar that a user equips.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawEyeData.EyeData.IsTrack: Whether the device is tracking this eye.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawEyeData.EyeData.Directi: the direction the eye is looking
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawEyeData.EyeData.PupilDi: The diameter of the eyes in mm
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawEyeData.EyeData.Opennes: Whether the eyes are open or closed.
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawEyeData.EyeData.Widen: how wide the eyes are in surprise
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawEyeData.EyeData.Squeeze: how much the eyes are squinting
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawEyeData.EyeData.Frown: how much the eyes are affected by the face stretching into a frown
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawEyeData.EyeData.InnerBr: The vertical movement of the inner edge of the eyebrow
messages.Tooltip.AvatarRawEyeData.EyeData.OuterBr: The vertical movement of the outer edge of the eyebrow
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.EyeCloseLimit: The EyeLinearDriver component is used to drive the shapekeys on eyes. It can also do eye look direction shapekeys if each eye is provided a projection plane slot.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.Eye: The EyeLinearDriver component is used to drive the shapekeys on eyes. It can also do eye look direction shapekeys if each eye is provided a projection plane slot.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.Eye.Side: The side to get data from for this Eye like target look point and
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.Eye.ProjectionPl: The slot to use as a reference for doing Projection with. See How Projection Plane Works.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.Eye.PositionOffs: The field to drive with the final value from the Projection system for fields like slot position or a 2D texture offset on a material.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.Eye.LookLeft: Used to drive the surrounding eye surface shifting when the user looks left for this Eye. See Eye Look Calculation.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.Eye.LookUp: Used to drive the surrounding eye surface shifting when the user looks up for this Eye. See Eye Look Calculation.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.Eye.LookRight: Used to drive the surrounding eye surface shifting when the user looks right for this Eye. See Eye Look Calculation.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.Eye.LookDown: Used to drive the surrounding eye surface shifting when the user looks down for this Eye. See Eye Look Calculation.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.Eye.OpenCloseTar: The shapekey or value to use when this eye is closed.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.Eye.PupilSizeTar: The shapekey or value to use when the pupil needs to grow.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.Eye.WidenTarget: The shapekey to use to make this eye widen like in shock.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.Eye.SqueezeTarge: The shapekey or value to use when this eye is squinting.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.Eye.FrownTarget: The shapekey or value to use when this eye is making a frown.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.Eye.InnerBrowRai: The shapekey or value to use for the inner brow raising for this eye.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.Eye.InnerBrowLow: The shapekey or value to use for the inner brow lowering for this eye.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.Eye.OuterBrowRai: The shapekey or value to use for the outer brow raising for this eye.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.Eye.OuterBrowLow: The shapekey or value to use for the outer brow lowering for this eye.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.Eye.MinInputClos: eye shut amount The subtract limits are applied before the value from the eye tracker makes it to this filter.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.Eye.MaxInputClos: eye shut amount The subtract limits are applied before the value from the eye tracker makes it to this filter.
messages.Tooltip.EyeLinearDriver.Eye.OpenState: OpenCloseTarget The subtract limits are applied before the value from the eye tracker makes it to this filter.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.MiniExpression: The Eye Manager Component is a component that is used for configuring the Eyes of an avatar, Both supporting simulated eye-movement, and real eye tracking using a headset with built in eye tracking sensors such as the HTC Vive pro eye.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.MiniExpression.Weight: The likelihood of this mini expression of being picked.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.MiniExpression.Direct: The direction to look for this eye expression.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.MiniExpression.MinDur: The minimum amount of seconds this mini expression should last.
messages.Tooltip.EyeManager.MiniExpression.MaxDur: The maximum amount of seconds this mini expression should last.
messages.Tooltip.EyeRotationDriver.Eye: Set up automatically on avatars that are made using the Avatar Creator.
messages.Tooltip.EyeRotationDriver.Eye.Root: the slot of this eye that should be rotated
messages.Tooltip.EyeRotationDriver.Eye.Up: The up direction of <mark=#ffffff10>Root</mark> in local space.
messages.Tooltip.EyeRotationDriver.Eye.Rotation: The rotation field of <mark=#ffffff10>Root</mark> to drive.
The color choice this element represents.
How likely a particle will be created with this color.
messages.Tooltip.IKSolverVR: All weight values are 1-0, excluding clamps which are inverted 0-1.
messages.Tooltip.IKSolver.IKPositionWeight: Weight of the IK. Makes the bones go back to <mark=#ffffff10>defaultLocalPositions</mark> and <mark=#ffffff10>defaultLocalRotations</mark> as this goes to 0. Also known as default pose.
messages.Tooltip.IKSolver.SimulationSpace: The top most hiearchy this solver should simulate in. When on an Avatar this is usually the user's slot when equipped and the avatar root when unequipped.
messages.Tooltip.IKSolver.OffsetSpace: Is used to determine if there is movement happening in user space. for example shuffling around in the player's physical room. This also tells the feet to try to find new footing, and to tell the feet the user is moving.
messages.Tooltip.IKSolver._initiated: Whether or not this component is simulating and driving it's fields.
messages.Tooltip.IKSolver.OnPreInitiate: Currently unused. Fires the SyncDelegate inside when this component is started initializing(usually during loading)
messages.Tooltip.IKSolver.OnPostInitiate: Currently unused. Fires the SyncDelegate inside when this component is done initializing (usually during loading)
messages.Tooltip.IKSolver.OnPreUpdate: Sync Delegate usually filled with calls to the VRIKAvatar component.
messages.Tooltip.IKSolver.OnPostUpdate: Sync Delegate usually filled with calls to the VRIKAvatar component.
messages.Tooltip.IKSolver.root: The root of the solver simulation, on an avatar this will be the avatar root when equipped and nothing when dequipped.
messages.Tooltip.IKSolverVR.BoneReferences: The set of slots are what parts of the VRIK.
messages.Tooltip.IKSolverVR.defaultLocalPositions: This is internal, and stores the positions the bones should go to as IKPositionWeight goes to 0.
messages.Tooltip.IKSolverVR.defaultLocalRotations: This is internal, and stores the rotations the bones should go to as IKPositionWeight goes to 0.
messages.Tooltip.IKSolverVR.PlantFeet: When enabled, feet positions will be prioritized over anything else including HeadTarget. (Causes issues with walking) If true, will keep the toes planted even if the HeadTarget is out of reach, so this can cause the camera to exit the head if it is too high for the model to reach.
messages.Tooltip.IKSolverVR.spine: The spine section of the VRIK solver.
messages.Tooltip.IKSolverVR.leftArm: The left arm section of the VRIK solver.
messages.Tooltip.IKSolverVR.rightArm: The right arm section of the VRIK solver.
messages.Tooltip.IKSolverVR.leftLeg: The left leg section of the VRIK solver.
messages.Tooltip.IKSolverVR.rightLeg: The right leg section of the VRIK solver.
messages.Tooltip.IKSolverVR.locomotion: The locomotion section of the VRIK solver.
messages.Tooltip.IKSolverVR.ForwardFlipped: Swaps the feet and turns the whole IK around.
messages.Tooltip.IKSolverVR.ForceRootHeight: Foot planting height override (Negative numbers cause issues with walking)
messages.Tooltip.IKSolverVR.LocomotionPositionOff: Handles how the IK should be offset when calculating locomotion.
messages.Tooltip.IKSolverVR.DefaultRootPosition: The default position of the centered root.
messages.Tooltip.IKSolverVR.DefaultRootRotation: The default rotation of the centered root.
messages.Tooltip.VRIK.BoneDrive: All weight values are 1-0, excluding clamps which are inverted 0-1.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.Gait: The VRIKAvatar component is used to drive and control the VRIK component on an Avatar.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.Gait.MinimumVelocity: The minimum velocity required for this gait to take effect.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.Gait.FeetRange: The range in which the feet will attempt steps.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.Gait.CycleHeightBias: The amount the feet will be biased to step upwards for step height.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.Gait.CycleFeetSeparat: How much to separate the feet from each other during the cycle.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.Gait.CycleGroundRatio: The amount the feet will take into account the ground for the step height.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.Gait.FootRaiseBeginHe: What point the feet should start at for raising for a step.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.Gait.FootRaiseEndHeig: What point the feet should end at for raising for a step.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.Gait.FootRestAngle: The angle the feet should be at for rest.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.Gait.FootRaiseAngle: The angle a foot should be at when making a step.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.Gait.FeetAlignmentOff: How much to offset for feet alignment.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.Gait.SpeedRatio: How much to speed up the foot at the peak of the step.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.Gait.FeetSlipRatio: How much the feet will slip before taking a step.
messages.Tooltip.VRIKAvatar.Gait.ForwardOffset: The offset of the feet forwards or backwards.
messages.Tooltip.AxisMultiViewportPanner.Viewport: This is useful if you want to make a slideshow, tab pages for UIX, or anything that you want to have pan across for multiple pages in your design.
messages.Tooltip.ComponentBase`1.Enabled:
Controls whether this component is "enabled". Generally, components that are disabled (this field set to false) should do nothing.

Not all components respect this property. This field has no effect on such components.
